eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Di saha ka Farisii dimseeba, ni Saddusii dima daa ti kyɛ u saani na. Ba daa boori la ba bɛri u, lala zugu ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, u niŋma makkri tumni ka ba nya, ka di wulisi, ni, Naawunni mi u yɛla.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya bu nya? - zaanoori saha ka ya gɔsiri saazugu n‑yɛtti, ni, sangbana moaaya, saa bu ya mi:.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: asusuba muna ka ya yɛtti, ni, saa ku n‑sɔbgi vilim, saa nin mi. Tɔ - sangbana gɔsibu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ka ya mi suŋŋa, ka mun na zi pampaŋŋɔ saha ŋɔn wulisiri makkri yɛla seem. Ka wula nla?.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Zuna niriba wa n nyɛ ninvugbeeri ban boori zina. Ba kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku ti nya sɛseelli m‑paasi la anabi Gyoona alahazibu yɛla maa. Ka u daa bakki ka kyɛ ba.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Di saha ka ba daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni ni ba ti lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa tam ka bu mari boroboro.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni Saddusii dima dinni gba.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri ni taaba, ni, U yɛtti la lala tin ka boroboro la zugu.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Ka Yisa daa mi ban tɔ'asiri seelli maa, ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, Ya yɛddakɔŋŋniba wa! Wula ka ya tɔ'asiri taaba, ni, yan ka boroboro zugu?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Ya na bu mi, bee, bu laa n‑teaai boroboro anu an tɔri n‑ti niriba tusa anu la yɛla? Ka an daa kpalim la peeri ala ka ya daa vaai?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Ka boroboro ayɔpɔi n‑tisi niriba tusa anaasi la yɛla muna, peeri ala ka ya daa vaai, an daa kpalim la?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna wula zugu ka ya bu mi, ni, pa boroboro yɛla ka n tɔ'asi ka yɛli, ni, ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni Saddusii dima dinni gba.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ka lala saha maa ka ba daa baŋŋi, ni, pa boroboro dabina yɛla ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba, amaa wulisigu yɛla, Farisii dima ni Saddusii dima ban daa wulisiri seem la, ka u tɔ'asira.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti paai tinkpanseesi an pɔri Filipi Sizarii la ni. Ka u daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Niriba maa yɛtti, ni, man Ninsaala Bii maa nyɛ la ŋɔni.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Ka ba yɛli u, ni, Baseeba ban yɛtti, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura n nyɛ i; seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya; ka seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Gyɛrimaaya, bee, kurimbuni sa anabisɔa.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ka yarima muna? Ya yɛtti la wula.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Ka Saimoon Piita daa labsiya, ni, I nyɛ la Masiiya Kirisita, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tumni na nti tiligi ti: Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana Bii n nyɛ i.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Mboo! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti nyini, Saimoon, nyin Gyoona bii wa! Dama pa ninsaala n wulisi i lala maa, amaa m Ba ŋɔn bɛ la arizana ni maa - ŋɔna n wulisi i.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Man mun yɛtti i wa, ni, i yuuri boonni la Piita, ka di gbinni nyɛ la tampiiya: tɔ, n niriba nin la'am n‑tɔm tampii wa zugu, ka Sintaani suulinsi ku toaai n‑zabi ba n‑nyaŋŋi.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N nin zaŋŋi Arizana naam saafibiisi n‑ti i, ka nyin kpari bunseelli duniya wa ni, lala bunni nin ti kpari arizana ni gba, halli ka nyin kparigi bunseelli duniya wa ni, lala bunni nin ti kparigi arizana ni gba.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Di saha maa ka u daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di yɛli sɔa, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Ka lala saha maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka Yisa Kirisita Masiiya daa piili n‑wulisiri u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, u nin namsi pam kpamma maa nuusi ni, ban ni zaaligu karimbadima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima zaa. Ni, ba nin ku u, amaa dabsa ata daari u nin yisigi n‑labi u nyɔvuri ni.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Ka Piita daa mari u n‑labi kpaŋŋa m‑piili n‑kpaamni u, ni, Ay, n dugma! Di ku toaai n‑niŋi i lala.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Yisa mun daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli Piita, ni, Bakkma ka kyɛ ma, nyin Sintaani wa! I ta'ari la n soori. ni, I teesa wa bu yi Naawunni saani na, amaa a kur nyɛ la ninsaala teesa.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa zaa, ni, Ŋɔnkam boori u kyɛ n saani na, see ka u za'asi u mammaŋŋa, ka kpikki u kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti deaai duniya wa zaasa ka kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, nyɔbɔ ka u nya? Seelli mun ka ni ka ninsaala nin zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri n‑takki ka.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Ya bu nya? - Ninsaala Bii maa siri nin zaŋŋi u Ba Naawunni gyirima ka kyɛ ka Naawunni malaakadima dɔli u ka u kyɛ na: lala saha maa ka u nin yɔ sɔkam samni hoo di daama tuma ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ninvugseeba m bɛ kpa, ban ku kpi halli ka ti nya ka Ninsaala Bii maa kyɛ na ni u naam yiiko.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yisa Masiiya daa yi la NaaDauda naam tuŋŋu za'anɔri ni na, ŋɔn NaaDauda muna n daa nyɛ ti zaa yaaba Ibrahim ya'aŋŋa: ka Yisa yaadima yɛla nŋɔa :-.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Ibrahim daa dɔ'ai biiya, ka u bii mun yuuri Yisahaaku; ka Yisahaaku bii n daa nyɛ Yaakubu, ka Yaakubu mun biisi daa nyɛ Gyuuda ni u tunzɔppa.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Gyuuda biisi daa nyɛ Pɛrɛzi ni Zeera, ba ma Tamaara ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai seeba n‑ti u la, ka Pɛrɛzi mun bii daa nyɛ Hɛzrɔn, ka Hɛzrɔn bii daa nyɛ Raam.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yi zoori zugu n‑sigri na, niriba pam daa dɔli u.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Raam bii n daa nyɛ Aminadaba, ka Aminadaba mun bii daa nyɛ Neeseoon, ka Neeseoon bii daa nyɛ Salimoon.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Di saha ka kɔnsɔ daa bɛ ni, ka u kyɛ Yisa saani na n‑sɔmni u tooni n‑yɛli u, N daana, i yi da sakki, i naa kyɛ ka n kɔmni wa naai ka n kpɛ n‑taabi ni.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Salimoon bii n daa nyɛ Bɔ'azi, Reehabu ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai n‑ti u la, ka Bɔ'azi ni u pɔ'a Ruuta bii mun daa nyɛ Oobɛdi, ka Oobɛdi bii daa nyɛ Gyɛsi.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Ka Yisa daa teaai u nuu n‑siisi u, ka yɛli, N sakkiya, di naama! ka di saha maa ka u kɔmni maa daa basi u ka u daa niŋi kasi.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: ka Gyɛsi mun bii daa nyɛ NaaDauda. Tɔ; NaaDauda bii n daa nyɛ NaaSulɛmaana - pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi Yuraaya pɔ'a la daa dɔ'ai u n‑ti u.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Di yɛli sɔ zaa. Kur dɔli Musa ŋɔn daa zaali piiniseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tippu ni maa n‑kyaŋŋi n‑wulisi i maŋŋa n‑ti maalimaanna la, ka di nin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, i siri nya tippu.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Ka NaaSulɛmaana bii daa nyɛ NaaRɛhɔbɔ'am, ka NaaRɛhɔbɔ'am mun bii daa nyɛ NaaAbaigya, ka NaaAbaigya bii daa nyɛ NaaEesa;.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Sahanseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni la Kapirinyum tiŋŋa, ka soogyadima toondaansɔ daa tuusi u soori ni n‑susiri u.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: NaaEesa bii daa nyɛ NaaGyihoosafatti, ka NaaGyihoosafatti mun bii daa nyɛ NaaGyooram, ka NaaGyooram bii daa nyɛ NaaYuzaaya.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni, N daabri n beera; u dɔ la yiri ni n‑kɔŋŋi doaari, ka di mugsi u pam.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: NaaYuzaaya bii daa nyɛ NaaGyootam, ka NaaGyootam mun bii daa nyɛ NaaEehazi, ka NaaEehazi bii daa nyɛ NaaHɛzikaaya;.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Tɔ! N nin kyɛ i yiri maa ni na n‑tibi u.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: NaaHɛzikaaya bii daa nyɛ NaaManasa, ka NaaManasa mun bii daa nyɛ NaaEemɔn, ka NaaEemɔn bii daa nyɛ NaaGyoosaaya.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay, n Daana! Man poora ni i kyɛ n yiri ni na; nyin kur yɛlima yɛliseesi nyin ya yɛli la, ka n baara maa kpaŋŋi.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Ka ŋɔn NaaGyoosaaya biisi n daa nyɛ NaaGyɛkɔnaaya ni u tunzɔppa: lala saha ka ba daa gbaasi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Babɛlɔn.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Ni, dama ŋɔn gba nyɛ la ban su ninvugsɔ, ka u mun gba su soogyaseeba fukumsi ka nin toaai n‑yɛli ba yinni, ni, u kyɛm luginseaa ka u kyaŋŋi, bee n‑yɛli sɔ, ni, u kyɛm na ka u kyɛ na, ka nin toaai n‑yɛli u daabri, ni, u tumma nŋɔ ka u tum di.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Tɔ; ka Babɛlɔn kyɛnni saha la nyaaŋŋa, ka NaaGyɛkɔnaaya daa dɔ'ai biiya, ka u bii mun yuuri Seaalitiyɛli, ka Seaalitiyɛli bii daa nyɛ Zɛrubabɛli;.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa wum toondaana maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa ka di daa gyɛri u pam, ka u yɛli ban daa dɔli u maa, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, halli Izraɛli dima ni gba n na zi n‑nya sɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda hoo doo wa ŋɔn niŋi seem.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: Zɛrubabɛli bii daa nyɛ Abaayuda, ka Abaayuda mun bii daa nyɛ Ilayakyiim, ka Ilayakyiim bii daa nyɛ Eezɔr;.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ni, N yɛtti ya wa, niriba nin ti yi wuntampusiri n‑kyɛ na, ni wuntanluri gba, nti la'am ni Ibrahim, ni Yisahaaku, ni Yaakubu m‑paasi, n‑zinni ba ziziisi ni, Arizana naam kyuu diibu maa saha.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: Eezɔr bii daa nyɛ Zeedɔkku, ka Zeedɔkku mun bii daa nyɛ Eekyim, ka Eekyim bii daa nyɛ Ilayuuda;.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Halli ka seeba ban nyɛ naam zuliya dima maa, ba nin yiisi ba m‑basi, ni ba ti paai wa'aligu ya'aseelli ban ŋɔn bɛ zibsim ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: Ilayuuda bii daa nyɛ Ɛliyeeza, ka Ɛliyeeza mun bii daa nyɛ Matan, ka Matan bii daa nyɛ Yaakubu sɔ.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli toondaana maa, ni, Kulim yiri ni; nyin niŋi yɛdda seem maa, lala ka i nin nya! Ka u daabri maa daa nya tippu lala saha maa deede.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: Ŋɔn Yaakubu maa bii n daa nyɛ Gyoosɛfi; ka Gyoosɛfi mun daa nyɛ Mariama sira - ŋɔn Mariama mun daa dɔ'ai Yisa, sɔ ban boonni Kirisita, Masiiya la.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Tɔ: Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ Saimoon yiri, ka daa nya Saimoon deemma ka waari daa mari u, ka u dɔya n‑gyigra.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Ibrahim saha zaŋŋi nti paai NaaDauda saha ba kaanni la yaadima piiya ni anaasi; ka NaaDauda saha zaŋŋi nti paai ban daa gbaasi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Babɛlɔn sahaseaa la, ba kaanni la yaadima piiya ni anaasi yaasa; ka Babɛlɔn kyɛnni saha la zaŋŋi nti paai Yisa Masiiya mun gba saha la, ba kaanni la yaadima piiya ni anaasi yaasa m‑paasi.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuu n‑siisi pɔ'a maa n‑suŋŋi u ka u doaai n‑zaani. Ka u dooru daa basi u, ka u daa yi na n‑tum n‑ti ba.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɔ. Ban daa dɔ'ai Yisa Masiiya seem nŋɔa#:- Sahaseaa ban daa zaŋŋi u ma Mariama n‑ti Gyoosɛfi, ka na bu taasi u la, di saha ka ba daa yi nya ka Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko daa pun kyɛ ka pɔ'asariga maa mari puuwa.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Di daari zaanɔyuŋŋu, ba daa mari niriba pam ka arizimbeeri bɛ ba zugri ni, n‑kyɛ u saani na; ka u daa yɛli u yɛligu kɔkɔa, ka di katti arizimbeeri maa m‑basira, ka tibri baaridima zaasa.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: U sira Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu pam, ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, u ya sɔ'ai n‑za'asi u ka niriba bu ya baŋŋi di gbinni: ni, M bu boori ni ba ti diisi u vi.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: U daa niŋi dinŋɔ ni u kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya yɛligu maa ti niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:- U mammaŋŋa daa deaai ti yaa kɔŋŋsim, Ka daa zaŋŋi ti dooribeeri maa N‑ziili u maŋŋa.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Ŋɔn daa na kur teesiri lala yɛla maa, ka u daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, Nyini Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn nyɛ NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa wa, di zɔri dabeem ni i kyɛ ka ba taasi Mariama i yiri na, dama ŋɔn mari puseaa maa yi la Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko ni na.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Tɔ: ka sahanseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa nya daadam ban zoaai n‑gyiligi u maa, u daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba lɔŋŋma mɔ'ari maa ya'ari sa.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: U nin ti dɔ'ai bidibga, ka i purim u yuuri Yisa, dama u nin ti tiligi u niriba ba tumbeeri taalli zaa ni.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Ka zaaligu karimbasɔ daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, N daana, n nin dɔli i n‑kyɛnni luginseelli kam zaa.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa daa niŋni ni di kyɛ ka anabi maa yɛligu ti niŋi yɛlimaŋni - ti Dugma ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ka anabisɔ daa yɛli seem la, ni#:-.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɔbaasi la mari la vɔya n‑kpeera, ka niinsi mun mari teeri n‑tɔmna, amaa man Ninsaala Biiya ka luginseaa man nin zaŋŋi n zugu n‑dooli gba.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Pɔ'asarisɔ nin ti gbaai puuwa n‑dɔ'ai bidibga, Ka ba ti booli u yuuri Imanuɛl .

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: U nyaandɔlisɔ mun daa yɛliya, ni, Kur kyɛ ka n kuli ka m ba yi ti kpi ka n sɔ'ai u pɔi ka naayi kyɛ na n dɔli i.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Tɔ, ka sahaseaa Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn daa neaai goom ni la, u daa niŋi hoo ti Dugma malaaka maa ŋɔn sa yɛli u seem maa, ka kyɛ ka ba taasi u pɔ'a maa na n‑ti u.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Amaa ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Kyɛ ka kpiimma sɔ'ari ba kpiintaaba, nyin muna - dɔli ma.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Amaa ba daa bu la'am taaba n‑dooni halli ka Mariama ti dɔ'ai u pɔ'asaridɔ'am, bidibga; ka Gyoosɛfi daa puri u yuuri Yisa.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Tɔ; ka di saha maa ka u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni, ka u nyaandɔliba daa dɔli u n‑kpɛa.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Ka pɛsititoo daa doaai mɔ'ari ni n yi zeeri ni, halli ka koom ti kpeeri nyariŋŋu maa puuni. Yisa mun daa gbisiri ni.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Di saha ka ba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na n‑neaai u goom ni n‑yɛli u, ni, Tuligma ti, ti Dugma! - ka ti zaa kpiiri ni pɛsigu wa zugu.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa! Wula ka ya mari dabeem lala? Ka u daa doaai n‑tasi pɛsigu maa, ni mɔ'ari la, ka di lu n‑fɔ surimm!.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba ka boosiri, ni, Bɔ nira nŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tasiri pɛsigu ni mɔ'ari gba ka a wumna.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Tɔ: Ka ba daa du mɔ'ari maa duuri m‑paai Gadara dima tingbaŋŋu maa ni. M‑paai bɛni ka dɔppa ayi daa yi gbala zii maa na nti tuusi u, ka arizimbeeri daa daamni ba halli nti yakki, ka dizugu niriba daa bu laa n‑toaai n‑dɔli lala soori maa.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Di saha ka ba daa tasi n‑yɛtta, I ya niŋi ti la wula, nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya? I kyɛ kpa na ni i ti namsi ti, halli ka di saha maa na bu paai, bee.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Tɔ, ka di saha maa kurikyuudima pam gba daa bɛ la wa'aligu n‑dira.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: ka arizimbeeri daa susi Yisa n‑yɛli, I yi ya kari ti m‑basi, nyin kyɛ ka ti kyaŋŋi nti la'amni ni kurikyuudima wa n‑kpɛ ba ni.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Tɔ, ya kyɛma!, ka arizimbeeri maa daa yi dɔppa maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ kurikyuudima maa, ka ba zaa daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni n‑kpi.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa daa zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem, ka laa n‑yɛli arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa mari ninvugseeba maa yɛla.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Ka timpuuni niriba zaa daa yi na nti tuusi Yisa soori ni. Ka ban daa paai na n‑nya u maa, ba daa yɛli u, ni, u kyɛma n‑gaari ka kyɛ ba.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Tɔ. Di saha ka Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑taasi yoo ni, ka Sintaani daa zamsi u.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Ka u daa bɛ bɛni n‑loori noori dapisinaasi, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa, ka di dabsa maa daa ti gaari ka kɔm wum u.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Ka Sintaani, yɔmsigu daana, daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, ni, I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku kyɛ ka kuga wa leebi bundira.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni#:- Pa bundirigu kɔkɔ zugu ka ninsaala bɛ ni, Amaa yɛttɔ'a zaa an yiiri Naawunni noori ni na - Anŋɔ zugu ka u bɛ ni. .

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Ka Sintaani daa laa n‑zaŋŋi Yisa n‑taasi Gyɛrusalɛm tinkasi la ni, n‑zaŋŋi u n‑du n‑tɔm Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri saazugu sa.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: N‑yɛli u, ni, I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku zaŋŋi i maŋŋa m‑basi tiŋŋa? Di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Naawunni nin yɛli i yɛla n‑ti u malaakadima maa, Ni, ba zaŋŋma ba nuusi n‑teebi i saazugu Ka i napɔŋŋu ku tuui kugri zaa. .

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Ka di laa n‑sɔbi m‑paasi, ni, nyin di zamsi i Dugma Naawunni.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sintaani daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑duusi n‑tɔm zɔwa'alli saazugu m‑bulisi u duniya puuni tinsi zaasa, ni a gyirima maa.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Ka yɛli u, ni, I yi ti gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem ma Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, n nin zaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa n‑ti i.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, Kyɛma sa, Sintaani! Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni#:- Naawunni yinni kɔkɔa, Ŋɔn nyɛ ya Dugma la, Ŋɔn sumsi gbaanigu ni gyeeŋŋu. .

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Ka Sintaani ŋɔn daa yi zamsi u n‑naai maa, u daa bakki ni ka kyɛ u, ka malaakadima daa kyɛ na n‑guri u.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, ba gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari, u daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: U daa bu kpalim Nazarɛti tiŋŋa, amaa u daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni, tinseaa kan zɛ mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu ni, Zɛbulɔn ni Naftaali tingbana ziiya.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: Dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Zɛbulɔn tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Naftaali tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Yan bɛ teeku la soori maa, Yan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari sa: Yan bɛ Galilii, saamma tiŋŋa la ni, gba, wumma!#:-.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: Ya nyɛ la ban daa zi zibsim ni, Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya bɛ la neesim ni, nyain! Ya daa zi la kum tiŋŋa libilibi, Amaa neesim doaaya, ka di neaari ya zugu.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Di saha maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka Yisa daa gbaai mooligu n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛla n‑yɛtti, ni, Ya takkma ya beebu ni ya teesibeeri! Dama Arizana naam la bɛ la ya saani.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli la Galilii mɔ'ari duuri la nti nya niriba ayi, Saimoon ni u tunzɔ Anduru, ka ba daa lɔbri ba laama mɔ'ari maa ni, dama ba daa tumni la zingbaariba tuma.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, Ya dɔli ma na! N nin kyɛ ka ya leebi ninsaaligbaariba.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Ka ba daa basi ba laama maa yoomyooma ka dɔli u.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Ŋɔn daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tooni, u daa nya niriba ayi yaasa m‑paasi, Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii biiya, ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni; ka ban ni ba ba Zɛbidii daa bɛ nyariŋŋu ni m‑maanni ba laama. Ka Yisa daa booli ba ni ba dɔli u na.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: Di saha ka ba daa bakki ka kyɛ nyariŋŋu maa, halli n‑kyɛ ba ba Zɛbidii gba, ka kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Ka u daa gyiligi Galilii tinsi zaa n‑kpeeri ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑wulisiri ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Arizana naam yɛla, ka tibri niriba ban daa beeri zaa bɛni ka dooru balimbalim m mari ba, bee, ban kɔŋŋni yaa ni alaafɛa.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Tɔ; ka lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u yɛla daa paai halli Siriiya tingbaŋŋu ni sa, ka niriba daa mari ba baaridima zaa n‑kyɛ u saani na, dooru ni beerim kam zaa ŋɔn mari seeba la, ni arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa kpɛ seeba, ni kpisinkpiiri ŋɔn daa gbaai seeba, ni ban nuusi bee ban gbaya n daa kpi la. Ka Yisa daa tibi ba zaa.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Ka Galilii dima daadam pam daa yi na n‑dɔli u, ni Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu dima, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, ni Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu dima, ni tingbanseaa zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari wa sa.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Di saha ka Farisii dimseeba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na, ban ni zaaligu karimbaseeba. Ka ba daa yɛtti.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka i nyaandɔliba maa sa'amni la ti yaadima kaari? Dama ba bu peaari ba nuusi ka diri bundirigu.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ka bɔ n niŋi ka yarima za'asiri Naawunni zaaligu, ya yaadima kaari dɔligu zugu?.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Naawunni daa pun bu yɛli ya lala? - ni, Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima; ni, ŋɔn tɔ'asi bumbeoo zaŋŋi n‑kpa u ba bee u ma, ya nin kyɛ ka u kpi.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Ya mun yɛtti, ni, sɔ yi ti yɛli u ba, bee, u ma, ni, Yan da naa n‑nya seelli n saani ka n pun zaŋŋi n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Di saha ka NaaHɛroodi Tɛtirakyi la daa ti wum Yisa damni.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Ni, lala zugu di bu laa n‑kpa ni di daama gyeemni u ba ni u ma yaasa. Di zugu ka ya niŋi ka ya ba'a laa n‑ka Naawunni zaaligu ni, ya yaadima kaari dɔligu zugu.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: ka tɔ'asi n‑ti u kpambaya, ni, Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa suuri niriba koom la nŋɔa, ŋɔna n doaai kum ni; lala zugu n kyɛ ka u mari yiiko n‑tumni alahazibu tuma wa.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Ni, Ya birikɔnsi daandima wa! Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya yɛla seem la, ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:-.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Ka di gbinni n‑daa nŋɔa: Pɔ'asɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Hɛroodiyasi. Ŋɔn Hɛroodiyasi daa nyɛ la NaaHɛroodi beeri, Filipi, pɔ'a, ka Naaba daa fa u n‑di u. Dizugu ka Gyɔni daa yɛli Naa maa, ni, Di ka soori ni i di pɔ'a maa! Ka u pɔ'a Hɛroodiyasi zugu daa kyɛ ka NaaHɛroodi daa tum n‑gbaai Gyɔni m‑ba u kutti ka kpeesi u dansarika.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun ka n saani, ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Dizugu ka Hɛroodi daa boori u ku Gyɔni, amaa ka zɔri zama maa dabeem dama ba zaa daa sakki, ni, anabi n nyɛ Gyɔni.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Ba gyeemni ma la yɔri: Ban wulisiri la zaaliseaa maa pa la bunseelli zaa Na'ala ninsaala yɛligu kɔkɔa. .

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi dɔ'am dabsiri daari kyuu daa ti paai, ka Hɛroodiyasi bipuŋŋa daa wa niriba tooni halli ka Naaba sufu daa ti maai.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Ka Yisa daa booli niriba maa u saani na n‑yɛli ba, Ya wumma! Ya baŋŋma!.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: Ka dizugu u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, ni, u nin ti u ŋɔn boori seelli kam zaa.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Di pa la din kpeeri ninsaala noori ni n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira noori ni na n kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Ka bii maa ma daa wulisi u ŋɔn ya n‑yɛli seem, ka u daa susi Naaba, ni, u boori la Gyɔni Kɔsuura zugu, ka ba fikki ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na n‑ti u.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Di saha maa ka Gyɔni daa bɛ ni, dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa suuri niriba Naawunni koom la; ka ŋɔn Gyɔni Kɔsuura daa doaai m‑bɛ yoo ni, Gyuudiiya tingbaŋŋu maa ni. U daa bɛ bɛni m‑moonna.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti boosi u, ni, Ammi, i mi, ni, nyin yɛli seem la bu niŋi Farisidima maa nya'asim.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Ka di daa niŋi Naaba maa sufusa'aŋŋu, amaa u daa pun pɔ noori n‑ti bii maa u saamma tooni, ka di zugu u daa yɛli, ni, ba mari na n‑ti u.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni, Ya kyɛli ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri zaa, dama Arizana naam bɛ la ya saani.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, M Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni wa yi bu sa tiseaa, ba nin vukki lala tii maa n‑lusi.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Ka u daa kyɛ ka ba kyaŋŋi dansarika ni n‑fikki Gyɔni zugu bɛni.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya di teesiri taaba yɛla, ka Naawunni ku ti teesi ya gba yɛlibeeri.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa nya niriba maa, u daa kyaŋŋi nti du zoori la ni. U daa zinni bɛni ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Dama ŋɔnŋɔ yɛla ka Anabi Aizaaya daa pun tɔ'asi, n‑yɛli, ni#:- Ya wumma! Sɔ m bɛ moo ni n‑tasira, Ni, Ya maalim ti Dugma palli n‑ti u, Ya sɛ'asim u sɔya zaa ka a tuusi kyappi! .

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Ya kyɛli ba! Dama ba nyɛ la zoomma, ka zooma yi ya n‑dari u zoontaraana, ba zaa ya n‑lu la bɔ'ari ni.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na nti ti bipuŋŋa maa, ka u daa mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti u ma la.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kpaam u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi tinkpanseesi an daa tabi bɛni maa n‑wulisiri m‑moonni yɛligu maa n‑tiri niriba.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Ban daa ti dɔ'ai Yisa Bɛtlihɛm timpuuni n‑naai, Meegyai daa yi wuntampusiri tinwa'aligu ziiya n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na - ban n daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban baŋŋi saŋmariwuusi yɛla, ni bamsim pam m‑paasi. Di saha maa Bɛtlihɛm daa bɛ la Gyuudiiya naam suulinsi ni, ka NaaHɛroodi mun daa di lala tingbaŋŋu naam maa.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Ka yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Ka u daa gbaai ba wulisigu n‑yɛli, ni#:-.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑labi n‑kuli u toori tiŋŋa.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Ŋɔn Gyɔni daa yeeri la arakoomi kɔbri bunyeerigu, ka daa mari gbaŋŋu n‑loori u seaa, ka u bundirigu daa nyɛ toori, ni siiri.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Di saha ka Piita daa yɛli u, Kur wulisim ti nyɛsiri wa gbinni.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ na nti zaŋŋi u ningbiŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai, ka naayi kyaŋŋi n‑yɛli Yisa.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Di saha ka Gyɔni daa bɛ dansarika ni - Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa su niriba Naawunni koom la. U daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni seem, ka daa tum u nyaandɔliba Yisa saani na.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ya mira ka ya di tumni ya wungyeeŋŋu tuma paalu ni, ni niriba ti nya yan ŋɔn tumni seem. Ya yi niŋni a lala paalu ni, ya ku nya bunseelli Naawunni saani - Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ya Ba m‑bɛ arizana ni.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Ka u daa ti booli u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi na ka daa ti ba yiiko ni ba katti arizimbeeri m‑basira ka tibri dooru balibu kam zaa ni baaridima zaa.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: Ka Meegyai maa daa kur boosiri, ni, Yɛni ka ba dɔ'ai Izraɛli dima naaba la? Ti daa nya u saŋmariwuu maa wuntampusiri lugri ni, dizugu ka ti kyɛ na ni ti gyeem u.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Di daari maa ka Yisa daa yi yiri maa ni na n‑kyaŋŋi nti zinni mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni ka zaantɔliga dee n‑sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban nyɛ faradima ba sufu ni, dama Arizana naam nyɛ la ba dinni.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Di saha ka ninvugseeba daa mari ba baarisɔ ŋɔn daa gbarigi n‑dɔ bundoaa ni, n‑taasi Yisa saani na. Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ka ba niŋi yɛdda pam maa, u daa yɛli baara maa, ni, M biiya, kur mari sufukpeemni! n kyɛ i taalli m‑pɛŋŋi i.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑yi n‑kyɛ u saani na; ba daa yi la Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni na, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa ni, halli ni tinseesi zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari duuri m‑paasi.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Tɔ, ka lala saha maa ka Yisa daa dɔli puri ni n‑gatta, Vuusim dabsiseaa ni. U nyaandɔliba n daa dɔli u, ka kɔm daa mari ba ka ba daa kabri kasana maa n‑ŋɔbra.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Ka u daa yɛli, ni, Ya gba yam na bu gaari ban dinni maa?.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum Gyɔni yɛla maa, u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔa. Ka niriba maa daa wum lala ka daa yi tinsi zaa ni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli mɔ'aduuri maa ka kari u nɔba.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: ni, Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee ti na gɔsim di daama soori.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: I yi ti suŋŋi ŋɔn ka seelli ka susira, n‑tiri u bunni, nyin di ŋmɛ luŋŋa m‑mooli di yɛla, hoo birikɔnsi dima ŋɔn niŋni seem maa; ba niŋni lala ba la'aŋŋu duri maa ni, ni daasi ni; ba boori niriba nya ka pɛ'ai ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, lala niriba pun nya ba laara zaa n‑naai.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: U tuumudima pii ni ayi wa yuya nŋɔa#:- ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni n daa nyɛ Saimoon, sɔ ban daa pa yupaa, ni Piita la, ni u tunzɔ Anduru; ka Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii bii la, n daa bɛ ni, ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni;.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa wum ba lala boosigu maa, di daa gyɛri u pampam - ŋɔn ni u niriba ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm zaa - ka ba sufu daa sa'am.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Daadam pam daa la'am u saani na, ka dizugu u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni nti zinni. Nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la koom puuni ka daadam maa daa zɛ mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Bee wula ka i nin toaai n‑yɛli i mabiiya, ni, u kyɛ ka i yiisi munseaa kan bɛ u nimbiri ni, ka zaantɔliga maa kpalim m‑bɛ i maŋtoori dinni ni?.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sufu n sa'amna, dama Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba sufu maai.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Ka karimbaseeba daa boosiri ba sufu ni teesa, ni, U zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑zaantiri Naawunni zaaziiya.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: Ka ba daa paai u na ka wulisiri ban tum tumbɛseesi maa, ka u daa su ba tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi kɔsuubu, Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la ni.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ka Farisidima daa nya lala, ka yɛli u, Nyama! I nyaandɔliba wa tumni la puri tuma. Ba galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Ya bu mi di gbinni? - ni, bunseela an kpeeri ninsaala noori, u puuri ka a kpeera, ka gatti n‑yi bangyira zii la ni.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi nyariŋŋu maa puuni na, u daa laa n‑nya daadam maa, ka u sufu daa zɔ ba namboo, ka u daa tibi ba baaridima maa.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli Gyɔni niriba wa, ni, Ya labma nti yɛli Gyɔni yan wumni ka nyari seela:.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Nyin muna, i yi ya suŋŋi ŋɔn susira, di kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi di yɛla, halli ni i nuzaa di baŋŋi i nudirigu ŋɔn tumni seem.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: ni Filipi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Tɔmaasi ni Matiyu lampoo karikyi la; ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Taddiyusi.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Di saha ka u la'asi Izraɛli dima kpamma ban nyɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima, ni zaaligu baŋŋniba la, m‑boosi ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti tiligi niriba n‑naayi di naam la; ka yɛli, ni, Tinkani ka di wulisi, ni, ba nin ti dɔ'ai Masiiya la.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Ka u daa tɔ'asi n‑tiri ba n‑nyɛsiri ba nyɛsa yɛla pam; ni, Dɔsɔ n daa kyaŋŋi u puu, ni u ti waali u bumbura;.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sigsi ba mansi, dama ba nin di faari n‑su duniya wa.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ban teesiri seem, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, Wula ka ya teesiri teesibeeri?.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Ŋɔn daa nya ka Farisii dima ni Saddusii dima pam gba daa kyɛnni na ni u ti su ba Naawunni koom; ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmani la hoo wa'abeeri! Ŋɔni n wulisi ya Naawunni ŋɔn ya namsi ya seem ka ya yɛnni, ni, ya nin zɔ n‑yi namsigu maa ni?.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ammi, ya na zi n‑karim NaaDauda yɛla maa? Ni, ŋɔn ni u taaba daa bɛ ni ka kɔm daa gbaai ba.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Amaa din yiiri ninsaala noori ni na, u sufu ni na ka di yiira, ka din mun nin toaai n‑sa'am ninvugkasi.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Zaanoori saha ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni: tim niriba maa soori ni ba wurigi n‑kpɛ tinkpansi maa ni, n‑da diibu n‑di.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: ni, zoomma nini neaara, ka gbarisi kyɛnna, ka ban mari kɔmni nyari alaafɛa, ka tubkpira daandima wumna, halli ka ban kpi gba neaari kum ni; ni, ka lahabasuŋŋu ka ba moonni n‑tiri faradima.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: I yi sɔ'ai n‑tumni tunsuma, i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara zaa nin yɔ i.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: ni Saimoon Kyɛna'an doowa, ni Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti .

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa kan bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la ka ba nin dɔ'ai u. Ni, dama anabidima daa sɔbi n‑zaali, ni#:-.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: ka ŋɔn daa waanni maa, a seesi daa lu sɔkpaŋŋa ka niinsi daa kyɛ na n‑di a zaa.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Ni, Ya di zaŋŋi bunni din nyɛ kasi n‑tiri baasi, ba nin ŋmaligi n‑dum ya. Di zaŋŋi buna an veela n‑dooli kurikyuudima tooni, ba nin nɔ a ni n‑sa'am.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban boori Naawunni yɛla hoo nira ŋɔn boori bundira ni bunnyuura seem, dama Naawunni nin diisi ba ban boori diseesi ka ba ti tigi.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Yɛla ayi wa sunsukkuni, kani m bu tɔa - i yi yɛli sɔa, ni, u nya u taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi, bee i yi yɛli sɔa, ni, u doaama n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: See ka ya tum tunseesi an wulisi, ni, ya pun takki ya halibeoo ni ya teesibeeri zaa, hoo tisuŋŋu ŋɔn wanni walisuma seem la.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: ka u daa kpɛ Naawunni gyeeŋŋu zii maa, n‑zaŋŋi boroboro n‑ŋɔbi. Pa boroboro anŋɔ taabu ka ba dooli Naawunni tooni ka a kyisiri ni? Ii! A kyisiri n‑tiri u taaba - halli n‑tiri u mammaŋŋa gba - see maalimaanniba kɔkɔ n ŋɔbri a.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Dama teesibeeri zaa yiiri ninsaala puuni, ni u sufu ni na; an kyɛ ka u tumni tumbeeri - n‑kuuri niriba, n‑tumni la zina, m‑boori seeba pɔ'aba, n‑zuura, m‑ma'ari ziri n‑lusiri niriba kootu ni; ka tuuri ninsaala ni Naawunni.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ba ku kyaŋŋi seela. Ya tim ba seelli ka ba di.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam bu birisiri n yɛla zugu mari zugsuŋŋu.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: I yi ya puusi Naawunni, di niŋi hoo birikɔnsi dima: ba boori ba zɛ saazugu m‑puusiri gyeeŋŋu duri ni, ni sɔkyira ni, ni niriba ti nya ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Tɔ, ka niriba pii ni ayi wa ka Yisa daa tum, ka kpaasi ba zugu ni n‑yɛli, ni, Ya di kyaŋŋi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa saani, ya mun di kpɛ Samɛrii dima tinseelli ni.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Nyin Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa wa, Nyin bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la, I bu poora Gyuudiiya nabiisi sunsukkuni; Nasɔ m bɛ ni ŋɔn ti ya n‑guri ya - Ya Izraɛli dima maa - Hoo ban guri bunkɔbri seem la: Ŋɔnwa ti ya n‑yi nyin Bɛtlihɛm saani na.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ka u bumburiseesi mun daa lu kuga ziiya ka bɛni tanni daa bu zoaai, ka a daa tɔ bula yoomyooma dama tanni maa daa pɔsigi a.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Ya susima ka nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya kpaasima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban zɔri niriba namboo dama Naawunni nin zɔ ba gba namboo.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Ni, Amaa m boori la ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba, duniya wa puuni. Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa, N yɛtti i ni, ni, i doaama ka kpikki i bundoaa wa n‑kuli.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Yan di nyuuri bara n‑yɛtti taaba, ni, Ibrahim ya'ansi n nyɛ ti!; ya bu baŋŋi Naawunni yɛla, bee? Ni, u nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi halli kuga wa gba m‑maali ya'ansi n‑ti Ibrahim?.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Bee, ya na zi n‑karim Musa zaaligu yɛla? Ni, maalimaanniba tumni la ba tuma Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni Vuusim Dabsiri. Ban n tumni la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ka bu deaari taalli di zugu.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa n kyɛ ka ninsaala leebi da'ari daana, amaa nira yi bu peaai u nuusi ka di bundirigu, lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, Ti ka diibu kpa, m‑paasi paanu bunkpila anu, ni zaasim ayi kɔkɔa.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Gyɔni nyaandɔliba maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi, ka Yisa mun daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑ti daadam maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɔni yɛla, ka boosiri ba, ni, Bɔ ka ya daa kyaŋŋi mooni ni ya ti nya? Kyimnigu ka pɛbsigu zeera, bee?.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Nyin muna, i yi ya puusi Naawunni, nyin kpeem i duu puuni n‑yɔ kyɛburu la ka sɔ'ai m‑puusi n‑ti i Ba. Ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara zaa nin yɔ i.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Amaa ya kyɛma Izraɛli dima ban bɔrigri hoo peesi la saani.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi daa booli Meegyai maa asiiri ni, m‑maali m‑boosi ba, ni, Bɔn saha deede ka saŋmariwuu maa daa pusi saazugu na? Ka ba daa wulisi u di saha ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Amaa wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti bi, a bula maa daa gyɛkki n‑kpi, dama a nya'a maa daa bu sigi wa'aligu.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama ŋɔnkam susira deaari ni; ŋɔnkam boora nyari ni; ka ŋɔnkam kpaasira, ba nin yoaai n‑ti u.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban mari sufu yinni, dama ba nin ti nya Naawunni.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Ka doo maa siri daa doaai ka vaai n‑kuli.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ka Naawunni nin namsi tumbeeri dima hoo nira ŋɔn maali u leefu siri ni u ŋma tiseaa kan bu wanni suŋŋasuŋŋa seem. U nin ŋma ka n‑lusi n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑lɔsi bugum ni.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: Man yɛtti ya wa, ni, bunseelli n bɛ kpa n‑gaari Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Yisa daa yi bɛni m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya ni Saidoon tinsi ni.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, ba mara a na n‑ti u.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa? Ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.;.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Tɔ: ya yi ya m‑puusiri Naawunni, di mari yɛttɔ'ayɔna pam hoo ban pa Izraɛli dima ŋɔn niŋni seem. Dama ba teesi, ni, ba yi ti tɔ'asi n‑yuui, ni, Naawunni nin wum n‑ti ba dizugu.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Ka ya yi kyɛnna, ya moolima n‑yɛtti, ni, Arizana naam bɛ la ba saani.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛm Bɛtlihɛm nti bɔ bii maa lahabaari. Ka ya yi ti paai n‑nya u, ya tumma yooma na n‑yɛli ma, ka man gba nin kyaŋŋi nti gyeem u.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Ka seesi gba daa lu goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa zoaai na m‑mugsi n‑gabi kabula maa.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Sɔ m bɛ ya puuni ka u biiya nin susi paanu ka u mɔŋŋi, ka zaŋŋi kugri n‑ti u? - Ay!.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban zɔri sindaani m‑maari zaba ni nangbankpeemni yɛla, dama Naawunni nin booli ba u biisi.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa nya lala, dabeem daa gbaai ba, ka ba daa pɛ'ai Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi yiiko lala taabu n‑ti ninsaalidima.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Koom ka man mari n‑suuri ya wa, ni ya takki ya halibeoo ni ya teesibeeri tuma: amaa sɔ m bɛ n nyaaŋŋa n‑kyɛnni na, ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi Sii Kasi ni bugum n‑suuri ya. Ŋɔna n kpeem n‑gaari ma halli ka mani bu sumsi ni m kpikkri u namna n‑ti u gba.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Sɔppu la yɛliya, ni, Man Naawunni bu boori ni ya maanni m‑maanni bunkɔbri wa, amaa m boori ya zɔri niriba namboo. Ya siri da mi dinŋɔ gbinni, ya naa n‑ku galim niriba ka ba bu niŋi taalli halli beela.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Ka Kyɛna'ampɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa yi lala tinsi maa na daa fabinni pampam, ni, N daana, nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ma namboo! Arizimbeoo n kur mugsiri m bipumbila ka di bu veeli gba.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Di saha ka Yisa yɛli niriba maa, ni, ba zinma moori la zugu. Ka u daa zaŋŋi paanu anu ni zaasim ayi maa n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni ka ŋmaai paanudima maa n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba deaai n‑ti zama maa.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: ka laa m‑boosi ba n‑yɛli, Ka bɔ zugu ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi? - ni ya ti nya anabisɔ, bee? Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, halli u gaari anabi gba.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Yarima muna, yan di ŋmanni bana. Dama ya Ba pun mi yan boori seelli pɔi, ka ya naayi susi.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Ya tibma baaridima, ka kyɛ ka kpiimma neaai, ka kɔnsi kpaŋŋi. Ya karima arizimbeeri gba m‑basira. Ya bu nya? yarima daa deaai di la yɔri, ka ya gba tisima di yɔri.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Ka ban daa wum Naaba ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la, ba daa yi n‑lu Bɛtlihɛm soori yaasa, ka saŋmariwuu maa daa gaari ba tooni ka ba daa dɔla - ban daa pun nya seaa wuntampusiri zii la - halli nti paai n‑zaani saazugu n‑wulisiri bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Amaa kaburiseesi daa lu tam ziiya n‑tɔ bula, n‑zoaai, ka daa dɔ'ai kyi, halli n‑niŋi beelim kɔbkɔbga, ni pisiyooyoobu, ni pisitata.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Bee, u yi ti susi ziŋŋa ka u mɔŋŋi ka zaŋŋi waafu n‑ti u? - Ay!.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ban namsiri seeba Naawunni soori maa zugu la, dama Arizana naam nyɛ la ba dinni.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: U daa yi bɛni n‑gatta, ka naayi nya karikyisɔ, ka u yuuri boonni Matiyu, ka u daa zi ba lampoo tuma ziiya n‑tumna. Ka Yisa yɛli u, Doaama n‑dɔli ma! Ka u daa doaai n‑dɔli u.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: U kyɛnni na; ka pun maali u neema siri ni u pii u kabuubu tampii maa ka ka niŋi kasikasi. Ka u nin yeeli u kyi maa n‑yiisi wuurigu la ka kyɛ kabeelim. Kasuma maa ka u nin la'asi n‑su kusɔkku maa ni, ka naayi zaŋŋi wuurigu maa zaa n‑vaai m‑basi bugunseebu bun ka kpiisigu puuni.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Dama man Ninsaala Bii n su Vuusim Dabsiri fukumsi gba.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Yisa mun daa bu labsi u seela. Ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti susi u, ni, I bu ya kari u? - Dama u kur katti ti nɔba n‑fabinni pampam.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi, ka ba daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ ban daa sɔbi sɔ yɛla la, ni, Naawunni yɛliya:- Nnya, n tuumu nŋɔa; N tumni u ni u ti daŋŋi i tooni: Ŋɔna nin maali i kyɛnni maa soori n‑ti i.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Dinŋɔ ka ya nin puusi, ni#:- Ti Ba, nyin bɛ la arizana ni, Kyɛ ka i yuuri du.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya di mari ligri n‑suuri gyɛfa ni, la'akara halli ni la'abisi gba.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Ba sufu daa maaya pam, saŋmariwuu maa zugu.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Tɔ! ya nyɛ la ninvugseeba bam mari teesibeeri ya beebu ni, ka halli yarima gba yi mi yan zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑tiri ya biisi; ka dinŋɔna ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ saazugu ku zaŋŋi u piinisuma n‑ti ban susiri u.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ya, sahanseaa niriba yi tuuri ya, ka katti ya m‑basira, ka ma'ari ziri n‑yɛtti yɛlibeeri pam n‑tiri ya, ka ba niŋi lala man zugu.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri maa ni, ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa paasi ba zugu. Di saha ka lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima pam gba daa dɔli ba n‑kpɛa. Ba daa la'am nti di bundirigu.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa yi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni na, ka daa paai Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ni nti kyɛ Gyɔni saani na ni u ti kyɛ ka Gyɔni su u koom maa.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Ka Yisa daa bakki bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duu ni.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ba daa bu tum ma na faasee ni n kyɛ Izraɛli dima ban bɔrigri hoo peesi la saani na.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa di lala diibu maa mun daa nyɛ la dɔppa tusa anu, m‑paasi pɔ'aba ni biisi.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Amaa yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura gaari bidibseeba pɔ'aba ŋɔn pun dɔ'ai la zaa; amaa ka n ya yɛli ya yaasa, ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn poora zaa ka bɛ Naawunni naam ni, di daama yi gaari ŋɔna.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Kyɛ ka i naam kyɛ na; Kyɛ ka ba tumni i yuubu yɛla N‑niŋi duniya wa ni, Hoo ban niŋni arizana ni seem.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Yan di zaŋŋi booriga m‑paasi ya kyɛnni maa ni, bee bunyeera ayiyi bee namna, halli zangbeaa gba. Dama tuntumna zaa sumni ni ba ti u u bundirigu.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Ba daa ku mari la lala halli n‑ti paai bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ luginseaa maa, ka daa kpɛ yiri maa ni - Mariama ni u bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela. Ka ba daa gbaani n‑gyeem bii maa, ka daa lɔrigi ba neema n‑yiisi piini n‑tisi u - salima n daa bɛ ni, ni turaali zum ban daa zaŋŋni bunseebu n‑nyoori n‑gyeemni Naawunni la, ni kpaseebu bun mari toom ka bu ligri zoaai pam.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti boosi u, ni, Bɔ n kyɛ ka i tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'anyɛsa wa n‑tiri ba.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Ni, Dinŋɔna yan boori ni niriba niŋi seem n‑ti ya zaa, see ka ya niŋi lala n‑ti ba gba. Zaaligu yɛla maa zaa nla, ni anabidima maa bulisigu m‑paasi.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya sufu maai m‑pɛligi pam dizugu, dama ya laara zoaaya pam arizana ni. Ya bu nya? - lala ka ba daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi anabidima la.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ka baseeba ban daa dɔli Farisidima soori la daa nya lala, ka ba daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Wula ka ya karimba ni ninvugbanŋɔ taabu la'amni n‑dira.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Amaa Gyɔni daa bu sakki, ka yɛli u, Mani n da naa n‑kyɛ ka nyini su ma koom, ka nyin laa n‑kyɛnni n saani na, bee.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn nuu kpi daa bɛ bɛni. Ka ba daa boosi Yisa n‑yɛli, ni, Ammi, soori bɛ ni ni ba tibi niriba Vuusim Dabsiri maa ni? - Dama ba daa boori daliiri ni ba galim u.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Pɔ'a maa mun daa kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaantiri n‑ti u n‑yɛtta, N daana, kur suŋŋma ma.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri, ka ŋɔn mammaŋŋa daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Sahanseaa ka Gyɔni daa mooli u mooligu la, halli nti paai zuna, arizana naam yɛla ka ba moori n‑gbaari ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Tim ti zuna ti beoo kam bundirigu;.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Ni, Ya yi ti kpɛ tiŋŋa bee tinkpaŋŋa ni, ya boosima n‑nya ninvugsuŋŋu ziiya n‑kpɛ u yiri m‑bɛ ni halli ni ya ti yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba zaasi zaasimni m‑bugsi ba, ni, ba di naa n‑labi NaaHɛroodi saani, ka dizugu ba daa lɔkki sɔseaa n‑labi n‑kuli ba tiŋŋa.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Dama Naawunni siri ti yarima soori ni ya baŋŋi u naam yɛlisɔ'ara ŋɔn nyɛ seem, amaa ban kpalim la ku nya lala soori.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Ni, See ka ya dɔli la za'anɔbila maa n‑kpɛa. Dama za'anɔri bɛ ni n‑yɛlima, ka sɔsuŋŋu gba bɛ ni, ka a mun wa la bugum ni sa'aŋŋu ziiya - halli niriba pam n dɔli anŋɔ maa!.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Ni, Ya ŋmanni la yaarim, ni duniya zaa ti nya'asa. Amaa yaarim nya'asim yi ti yi ka kyɛ di, di laa n‑ya n‑niŋi la wula ka di nya'asim maa labi na yaasa? Di saha maa di laa n‑ka anfaani, ka ba yi zaŋŋi di ni m‑basi samanni ni, ka niriba yi kyɛnni n‑noori di.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa wum ban tɔ'asiri seem maa ka yɛli, Ninvugseeba ban mari alaafɛ bu boori tiimaraana, na'ala ban beeri m boori u.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, Kur kyɛ ka di niŋi lala pampaŋŋɔa. Dama di sumsi ni ti tumni bunseelli kam zaa la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali n‑ti ti seem maa. Ka di saha maa ka Gyɔni daa sakki ka daa su u koom maa.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya ninvugsɔ peoo yi ti lu bɔ'azuliŋŋu ni, ka di nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri daari, u nin yiisi u na, bee, u nin kyɛ u?.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Amaa Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Kyɛ ka ti daŋŋi n‑diisi biisi la. Di bu tu ni ti zaŋŋi biisi bundirigu maa m‑basi babisi la.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu, ni u ti bɛ bɛni u kɔkɔ m‑puusi Naawunni. Yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti ya zibi, ka Yisa daa kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni, u kɔkɔa.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Anabi dima la sɔppu, ni Musa zaaligu la, daa wulisiri anabitiri yɛla m‑mari yaa, halli nti paai sahanseaa ka Gyɔni daa ti kyɛ na.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Ka kyɛri ti taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ti, Hoo tin kyɛ seeba ban niŋi ti taalli la.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Ya yi ti kpɛ yiriseelli, ya puusim ba puusisuŋŋu.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Ban daa ti gaari n‑kuli n‑naai, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni n‑nya ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyaŋŋi u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti. See ka ya kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni halli ka man ti laa n‑ti ya kukɔri; dama NaaHɛroodi ya boori la bii maa ziiya ni u gbaai u n‑ku.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Sɔkam zaa ŋɔn mari seelli nin nya m‑paasi halli ka di ti zoaai, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli maa ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa gba, ba nin ti fa a ka kyɛ u yɔri.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Sɔseaa n bɛ ni, ka ka za'anɔri poora, ka soori maa bu yɛlima; ka mun wa la nyɔvuri ziiya, amaa niriba ban nya ka n‑dɔla bu zoaai.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Yaasa, ya ŋmanni la neesim, ni duniya zaa ti neaai suŋŋa. Tinseaa yi mɛ n‑zɛ zoori zugu, ka ku toaai n‑laa n‑sɔ'ai.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Ya kyɛma nti baŋŋi dinŋɔ gbinni, ni, Sufubaalim ka n boora, pa bunkɔbri sara maaligu. Pa tunsuma dima ka n kyɛ na ni m booli, amaa tumbeeri dima ka m boonna.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu n‑naai ka daa ti yi koom maa ni na, di saha maa ka sangbana daa yoaai ka u daa nya ka Naawunni Sii daa sigri u zugu na; Sii maa mun daa ŋmani la hoo naŋmanni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Ka ninsaala bu gaari peoo? Dizugu soori bɛ ni ni ti tum tunsuma Vuusim Dabsiri daari.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛliya, ni, Yɛlimaŋni, n Daana, amaa babisi la gba ku kɔŋŋi ba piisi ban su ba la sa'akyɛba an luri tiŋŋa la.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Ka nyariŋŋu maa daa pun kyaŋŋi tooni wa'aligu, m‑bɛ la mɔ'ari sunsukkuni. Ka pɛsigu daa zeeri ba tooni n‑nyaŋŋi nyariŋŋu maa koom maa ni, ka di daa mugsi ba pam.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Halli ya yi ti sakki n‑deaai n yɛligu wa, n ya yɛli ya, ni, Gyɔni nyɛ la Ilaygya - sɔ ban daa yɛli u yɛla, ni, u nin labi na la.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Ka di kyɛ ka ti nya zamsigu din nin nyaŋŋi ti. Amaa yiisima ti Yɛlibeeri Daana nuusi ni.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Ka yiri maa yi nyɛ yirisuŋŋu, yan kyɛ ka ya puusigu sufudoaa kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni; amaa ka yi pa la yirisuŋŋu, ya nin kyɛ ka ya puusigu sufudoaa maali n‑labi ya mammaŋŋa saani.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Ka u daa doaai yuŋŋu n‑zaŋŋi u pɔ'a ni bii maa n‑yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Ni, Dinzugu n tɔ'asiri yɛla zaa yɛttɔ'anyɛsa ni n‑tiri ba, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba nin gɔsiri ka ku nya seela: ba nin wumna, amaa ka ku baŋŋi seela.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Ni, Ya baŋŋma, ka ziri anabidima m bɛ ni. Ba ŋmani la kunduna ban yeeri peesi gbana, ni ba ti la'am ni peesi maa n‑gbaai ba.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑sɔ'ai luginseaa, bee n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka; see ka u na'a yi zaŋŋi ka ni, n‑tɔm bunseelli zugu ni ban kam zaa bɛ duu maa ni nin nya kan neaai seem maa.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Gyɔni Kɔsuura nyaandɔliseeba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na, m‑boosi u, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka tirima ni Farisidima maa loori noori ka nyin nyaandɔliba mun bu loora.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu na, n‑yɛtti, ni, M bii nŋɔa, ka n wuri u ni pam. N nini n tigi u.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Di saha ka u yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, Teaam i nuu maa! Ka u daa teaai ka u nuu maa daa nya tippu n‑leebi hoo kanla.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑ti u, ni, Mboo! Pɔ'a, i mari la yɛdda pam gba! Di nin niŋi nyin boori seem n‑ti i. Ka u bipuŋŋa maa daa nya tippu lala saha maa.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Ka din daa pɔri bɛkyikyeoo, ka Yisa daa kyɛ ba saani maa na n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Dama ya yi kyɛ ya taaba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana la gba nin kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
