local: Ka sɔ yi bu ti deaai ya, bee, n‑za'asi ya yɛligu wumpu, ya yi ti ya yi yiri maa ni, bee, tiŋŋa maa ni, ya kpaasim ya napɔna tankpoo m‑basi.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: Ba daa kpalim la bɛni halli ka NaaHɛroodi ti kpi. Ka dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka di siri niŋi hoo ti Dugma ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ka u anabisɔ ti tɔ'asi anabitiri ni, ni#:- N daa booli m bii maa Ka yiisi u Igyipti tingbaŋŋu la ni na.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Dizugu anabi Aizaaya yɛlisim maa daa yɛli n‑ti la bana, ni#:- Ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- Ya nin wum, m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ya ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Ya nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ya ku nya di.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Ba wala nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba. Goaa bu wanni sisibi ka kpikpaliga mun ku toaai n‑wali kinkama.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ya neesim neaai halli ka sɔkam zaa nya; ka ba nin ti nya ya tunsuma ka ti pɛ'ai ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni di zugu.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Dɔsɔ yi ti di pɔ'apaalli ka ba mari la deema di zugu; ammi, pɔ'apaalli maa sira yi ti bɛ u saamma sunsukkuni, ba sufu nin sa'am di saha maa, bee? Ay! Amaa sahanseaa kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yiisi amriya doo maa ba saani - di dabsa maa puuni ba nin ti lɔa.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Galilii mɔ'ari kpaŋŋa, nti du zoori zugu n‑zinni bɛni.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Ka Farisidima daa doaai ya'abi, n‑yi duu maa ni nti ŋmeeri taaba saawara m‑boori sɔseaa ba nin nya ni ba ku Yisa.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Amaa u nyaandɔliba daa nya u ka u kyɛnni koom zugu, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba yɛli, ni, kpiinyisigi n nyɛ u, ka daa ŋmɛ kuŋŋa dabeem zugu.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Amaa ya yi bu kyɛ ya taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi, dinŋɔna ya Ba maa gba ku kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, toom seebu Sɔdoom ni Gɔmɔra tinsi ninvugbeeri ŋɔn nin ti nya Zaadaari maa ku ti ŋmanni lala tiŋŋa niriba toom maa ŋɔn nin nyɛ seem.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Meegyai maa yɔmsi u ka sɔ'ai n‑kuli, u sufu daa yisigiya pam. Ka u daa zaali zaaligu, ni, ba kyaŋŋi Bɛtlihɛm, ni tinkpanseesi an bɛ gyilagyila la ni, nti ku biisi maa - bidibseeba ban dɔ'ai ka ba nyɛ dahinyinni halli nti paai yuuma ayi biisi la zaa. U lala zaaligu maa daa dɔlisi la sahaseaa Meegyai maa ŋɔn daa wulisi u la.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ wanni la walisuma. Tiseaa kan ka alaafɛ muna - walibeeri ka ka nin wali.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Ni, Sɔ na zi n‑zaŋŋi kyinkyimpaalli tanni n‑ligi bunyeerikurigu. Dama tampaalli maa nin yiisi kan ligi seela maa ka kyeaai bunyeerigu la, ka ka maali n‑sa'am m‑paasi.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ka bumbɔ ka n ya zaŋŋi n‑ŋmansi zuna niriba wa? - Ba ŋmani la hoo biisi ban zi daa ni m‑boonni taaba n‑yɛtti.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Ya di teesi, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti sa'am Musa zaaligu ni anabidima la bulisigu yɛla. Ay! N bu kyɛ na ni n ti sa'am a ni, amaa ni n ti kyɛ ka a niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Dama n niriba wa muna - Ba sufu ni teesa bu zulima, Ba ligi la ba tuba, Ba mumni ba nini: Dama di yi pa lala Ba nini naa n‑nyara, Ba tuba naa n‑wumna, Ba sufu ni teesa naa n‑neaara, Ka ba ti labi n saani na Nti nya tippu n saani. .

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Ka niriba pam daa mari ba baaridima n‑kyɛnni u saani na, gbarisi ni wɔbsi n daa bɛ ni, ni zoomma ni tubkpira dima, ni banŋɔ taabu pam. Ba da zaŋŋi ba m‑basi Yisa nɔba ziiya, ka u daa tibi ba zaa.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, u daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi mɔ'ari ni, ka niriba pam daa dɔli u. U daa tibi ba baaridima zaa.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Ka Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑yɛli ba, Ya mari sufukpeemni ka di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya! Ka mani nla.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Ni, Ya yi ti gbaai noori loobu sahanseaa, yan di miisi ya nindaa hoo birikɔnsi dima wa ŋɔn niŋni seem la. Ba yi kyɛ ka ba nini n da'ai ni sɔkam ti nya ka baŋŋi, ni, ba loori la noori. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya bu nya? - n tumni ya, ni ya kyaŋŋi nti ŋmanni la peesi ban bɛ kunduna sunsukkuni seem la. Dizugu ya mari yam hoo wugi la ŋɔn mari seem, ka bɛ baalimm hoo naŋmana la ŋɔn bɛ seem maa.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Di saha di siri daa niŋi hoo Anabi Gyɛrimaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛliseelli, ni#:-.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali walibeeri, halli ka kan ka alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali bunsuma.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Bee, ya teesim daam yɛla: ba mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akura ni. Ba yi niŋi lala, ga'akura maa nin ŋma, ka daam maa yuuli m‑basi, ka ga'a maa gba sa'am. Ay! Ga'apaala ka ba zaŋŋni n leeri dapaalli n‑niŋni a ni, lala ka abayi maa zaa nin kpalim suŋŋa.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: ni, Ti peebi yiiya n‑ti ya ka ya za'asi waa, ka ti laa m‑maanni la kuuri n‑ti ya ka ya bu sakki n‑kaasira.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, sahanseaa arizana ni duniya wa yi na kpalim m‑bɛ ni, ba ku yiisi halli yɛlifiiŋ bee yɛttɔ'abila gba zaaligu maa ni m‑basi, halli ni a seelikam zaa naari.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya nini ŋɔn nyari wa zugu ka ya mari la zugsuŋŋu! - Ni ya tuba ŋɔn wumni maa zugu gba.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Ka di daa niŋi daadam maa alahazibu, dama ba daa nya ka mugsi tɔ'asira, ka wɔbsi nyari tippu, halli ka gbarisi ti doaari n‑kyɛnni ka zoomma gɔsiri n‑nyari gba. Ka ba daa pɛ'ai Izraɛli dima Naawunni di zugu.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: amaa ka daa kpamsi ba, ni, ba di yɛli daadam maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Dinsaha ka Piita daa yɛli, N dugma, di yi siri nyɛ la nyini, nyin kyɛ ka n kyaŋŋi koom wa zugu n‑kyɛ i saani na.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Nyin muna, i yi ya gbaai la noori loobu, see ka i peaai i nini ka saasi i zugu.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Amaa ya gbaam ya mansi ninsaalidima saani: ba nin ti gbaai ya n‑taasi ba kootudima ni, ka feebi ya ba la'aŋŋu duri ni.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Raama ka kukɔri maa yi na, Kunkuma ni fabligu pam n nyɛ di. Reekyɛl n kumni u biisi zugu Halli ka sɔ ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑goaai u; Dama u biisi maa zaa na ka ni.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti ba dinŋɔ maa, kpeensɔ daa kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni ka yɛli u, M bipumbila kpiya ni. ni, Amaa kyɛm na n‑zaŋŋi i nuusi m‑pa u zugu, ka u nin ti nya u nyɔvuri.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Dinŋɔna Gyɔni daa kyɛ na ka u ba'a ka diibu ni nyuubu yɛla ni, ka ya yɛli, ni, arizimbeoo n zi u zugu ni.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Dizugu ninvugsɔ ŋɔn za'asiri zaaligu wa bumbila fiiŋ m‑basi, ka laa m‑bulisi niriba lala, ba nin ti booli lala nira, ni, Arizana naam nyaaŋŋa daana. Sɔ mun ŋɔn sakkri zaaligu wa bumbila m‑bulisira, ŋɔn mun ka ba nin booli Arizana naam toondaana.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Dama n yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, sahaseaa din gaari ka anabidima ni ninvugsuma pam daa boori ba da nya yan nyari bunseesi wa, ka mun bu nya a; ka daa laa m‑boori ba wum yan wumni seelli wa, ka daa bu wum.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Yisa mun daa booli u nyaandɔliba maa u saani na nti yɛli, N sufu bu maai niriba wa yɛla zugu, dama ba bɛ n saani ka di paai dabsa ata ka ka seelli ni ba di. M bu boori n kyɛ ka ba kuli yɔri, baseeba nin ligi soori ni.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Dinŋɔ daa niŋi ni di kyɛ ka seelikam siri ti niŋi hoo anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la: ni#:-.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Tɔ, kyɛm na! Ka Piita daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑tɔm koom maa zugu ka gbaai kyɛnni ni u ti paai Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: ka lala ka ninsaalidima ku baŋŋi i noori loobu yɛla, see ka i Ba ŋɔn bɛ la zisɔ'arli maa ni nin baŋŋi. Di saha ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara nin yɔ i.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Ba nin zaŋŋi ya n‑taasi gɔmnadima ni nadima saani, man zugu; ka lala nin ti wulisi yɛlimaŋni yɛla n‑tiri ba, ni tinsi zaa niriba m‑paasi ba zugu.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Tɔ. Ka NaaHɛroodi kum nyaaŋŋa, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa laa n‑kyɛ u saani na.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Lala zugu ka n yɛliya, ni, ba wala - di nyɛ la ba tuma maa - nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Ka Yisa daa yisigi n‑dɔli u, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑lɔ zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi daandima. Amaa Naawunni yam tumni n‑wulisiri kamaŋŋa yɛla.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Dama man ya n‑yɛli ya wa, ni, di yi ti pa la ya dɔli Naawunni soori suŋŋa n‑gaari zaaligu karimbadima ban dɔli seem maa, ni Farisidima ŋɔn dɔli seem maa, ya ku nya sɔseaa yan nin zaŋŋi n‑kpɛ Arizana naam ziiya.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya wumma! - ka baŋŋi bumbura nyɛsiri maa gbinni.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa yɛli u, ni, Yani ka ti nin nya bundirigu n‑diisi niriba wa zaa moo wa ni.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- N bii nŋɔa, mani n yiisi u: N wuri u, ka n sufu laa m‑pɛligi u zugu. N nin zaŋŋi n Sii Kasi n‑niŋi u ni, U nin wulisi n sariya karibu yɛla N‑ti tinsi zaa niriba maa.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Amaa Piita ŋɔn daa ti nya pɛsigu ŋɔn zeeri seem la, dabeem daa gbaai u ka u daa piili n‑simni koom maa ni. U daa kaasi n‑yɛli, Faam ma, n dugma.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Ya di la'asi ya neema n‑tiri ya mansi n‑niŋi ya arizikki duniya wa ni, dama bɛni ka leelisi nin ŋɔbri a ni, bee, a nin nyaamna, ka naayi sa'am, halli ka na'ayigsi kabri n‑kpeeri na n‑zuuri gba.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Dizugu ban ti ya gbaai ya n‑taasi sariya karibu ziiya, yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu birisiri yan yɛ ti yɛli seem, bee, n‑tɔ'asi seem zugu: dama di saha maa yi ti paai ya nin ti baŋŋi yan yɛ n‑yɛli seem.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: nti yɛli u, ni, Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni, dama ninvugseeba ban daa boori ba ku bii maa la kpiya.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Pa seeba ban boonni ma, ni, N Dugma, n Dugma zaa n nin kpɛ arizana naam ziiya, amaa seeba ban tumni m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la yuubu maa nin ti kpɛa.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Ka pɔ'asɔ daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa soori halli yuuma pii ni ayi ka ziim bu goaara. Ka pɔ'a wa daa tabli na n‑kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑siisi Yisa bunyeerigu.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa gbaai tinsi maa galiŋŋu - tinseesi ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni n‑tumni u alahazibu tuma ba saani la; ka di zaa yɔri ba daa za'asi, ni, ba ku wum u yɛligu n‑kyɛ ba tumbeeri ni ba teesibeeri yɛla.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, Di ku ninsaala. ka sɔ ŋɔn ku nin ti bɛ sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ni.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn wum Naawunni naam lahabaari maa ka bu baŋŋi di gbinni maa ka Yɛlibeeri Daana maa kyɛ na n‑fa yɛligu maa u sufu ni, ŋɔna n ŋmanni la bumburiseesi an lu sɔkpaŋŋa.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Boroboro abala ka ya mara? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Abayɔpɔi ka ti mara, ni zaasim beela.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: U ku ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni, Bee, u tasiri pampam. U ku kyɛ ka ba wum u kukɔri, Bee, n‑tɔ'asi pampam timpuuni.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Ka Yisa daa teaai u nuu yooma n‑gbaai Piita, ka yɛli u, ni, Nyin yɛddakɔŋŋna wa, wula zugu ka i birisira.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Amaa ya la'asim neenseesi an nin zaali arizana ni n‑niŋi ya arizikki maa; bɛni ka leelisi ku ŋɔbi a, bee, a ku nyaamna, ka na'ayigsi mun gba ku toaai n‑kabri n‑zuuri a.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Dama di pa yarima n ya n‑tɔ'asi amaa ya Ba Naawunni Sii maa nin bɛ ya saani n‑tɔ'asira.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Ka dizugu u daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Dabsiri maa yi ti paai na, niriba pam nin ti yɛli ma, ni, Ti Dugma, ti Dugma! I yuuri ni ka ti daa yɛli anabitiri yɛla; i yuuri ni ka ti daa kari arizimbeeri m‑basi; i yuuri ni ka ti daa tum alahazibu tuma pam! Bee, ti ma'ari ni.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: dama u daa yɛtti u sufu ni, ni, N yi kur siisi u bunyeerigu maa n nin nya alaafɛa.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Ni, Zugubeoo ka ya nin nya, yarima Kɔreezini dima ni Bɛtsaida dima wa! Dama ba yi di daa tum tunkara Taaya ni Saidoon tinsi ni hoo man tumni aseesi ya tinsi maa ni la, ban tumbeeri daandima wa daa naa m‑pun yɛ tankyɛra n‑zinni m‑bugi tampɛ'alim n‑wulisiri ba sufusa'aŋŋu ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka takki ba halibeoo beebu la ka di daa yuui.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn suuri yisigi n‑niŋi u mabiiya, u bɛ la sariya karibu ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa!, ba nin boosi u boosa gyintooriba duu maa ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, Nyin gyɛrigu wa!, u nin di wahala bugum ni.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ninvugsɔ mun ŋɔn ŋmani la bumbura an lu kuga zugu la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn yi wum yɛligu maa sahanseaa la, u yi deaai ka la ni sufupeelli;.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka niriba maa zini tiŋŋa maa.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: U ku kabi kika'ari kan mari daalimni; Bee, n‑kpiisi firila gaariga kan bu sakki nyoaari suŋŋa. U nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala Halli ka yɛlimaŋni ti nyaŋŋi zaa.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ka ba daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni ka pɛsigu maa daa kyɛ zeebu.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Dama i sufu kur ya bɛ la i arizikki ŋɔn bɛ seela.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Dɔseeba nin gbaai ba mabiisi n‑tisi ka ba ku ba, bee ba nin gbaai ba biisi, ka biisi mun nin yisigi n‑zabi ba badima ni ba madima halli nti ku ba gba.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, NaaHɛroodi bii Arikyileesi n diri u ba Hɛroodi naam n‑su Gyuudiiya maa, dabeem daa gbaai u ni u kyaŋŋi bɛni. Ka u daa zaasi zaasimni ka di wulisi u, ni, u kyɛm Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni nti zinni bɛni.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Di saha maa ka n nin mooli n‑yɛli ba, ni, Man zi ya kɔtaa! Ya zɔma ka kyɛ ma, yan tumbeeri dima wa! .

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑nya u ka yɛli, M biiya, kur mari sufukpeemni! I niŋi la yɛdda ka dizugu i nya tippu. Ka di saha maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka pɔ'a maa daa nya alaafɛa.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, Zaadaari halli Taaya ni Saidoon ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Dizugu i yi ti mari i piini n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai maaligu ziiya, di yi pa seem ka bɛni ka i ti teaai, ni, i niŋi i mabisɔ taalli.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: amaa ka di bu kpɛ u sufu ni ni yɛlimaŋni, ka u nya'a kaalinsi zugu u bu yuura: dizugu yɛlibeoo bee vɔlisigu yi ti kyɛ na Naawunni yɛligu maa zugu, u yi labi la nyaaŋŋa yooma.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: u daa zaŋŋi boroboro ayɔpɔi ni zaasim maa n‑tisi Naawunni pɛ'abu ka ŋmaasi n‑tisi u nyaandɔliba maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑tɔri daadam maa.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Tinsi zaa niriba nin ti wum u yuuri, Ka zaŋŋi ba tama n‑danni u. .

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba ban daa bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni daa gyeemni Yisa n‑yɛtti, ni, Naawunni bii n siri nyɛ u.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Ni, I nini n niŋi hoo i ningbina firila: i nini yi neaai suŋŋa, i ningbina zaa mari la neesim, nyain!.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Ba zaa nin yaai ya man yuuri zugu, amaa sɔ zaa ŋɔn nin kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑bari n‑zaani halli nti paai yɛla wa tariga maa nin nya tiliginsim.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti zinni Nazarɛti tiŋŋa ni. Ka dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka di siri daa niŋi hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem n‑zaali la, ni#:- Ba ti ya m‑boonni u la Nazarɛti nira.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Ni, Dizugu ŋɔnkam zaa wum n yɛligu ka dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la yam daansɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tampii zugu.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti paai kpeema maa yiri na. Ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka ba daa ŋmeeri kuyiri bunŋmeera ka di kur nyɛ la basaa ni. Ka dizugu u daa yɛli.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Yarima muna, ya Kapirinyum dima wa, ya bu duusiri la ya mansi m‑paai arizana ni? Amaa ba nin sigsi ya kpiimma tiŋŋa ni sa. Dama alahazibu tunseesi an daa tum i saani maa yi daa tum la Sɔdoom tiŋŋa puuni, ka daa naa n‑kpalim m‑bɛ ni, halli ni zuna.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: Di yi bɛ lala, see ka i kyɛ i piini maa n‑dooli maaligu zii la ni ka kyaŋŋi m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa ka nyin ni i mabii maa nya nangbanyinni pɔi, ka naayi labi na n‑tisi.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋmanni bumbura an lu goosi ni la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn yi wum yɛligu maa pɔi, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi duniya wa yɛla m‑mugsi u maŋŋa, ni bundaantirigu yɛla gba; ka dizugu ka Naawunni yɛligu bu toaai m‑bunni u sufu ni nti zoaai na n‑wanni tunsuma wala.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ka sɔkam zaa daa di n‑tigi; dɔppa maa daa paai tusa anaasi, m‑paasi pɔ'aba ni biisi maa. Ba daa ŋɔbi ka kyɛa, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa vaai dikyɛba la m‑paali peeri ayɔpɔi.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Di saha ka ninvugseeba daa zi dɔsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ zooma ka bu toaai n‑tɔ'asiri n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na; doo maa daa bɛ lala dama arizimbeoo daa bɛ u ni. Ka Yisa daa tibi zɔligu wa ka kyɛ la u laa n‑tɔ'asiri ka nyari yaasa.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa ba daa ti paai Gyɛnɛsarɛti tiŋŋa.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Amaa i nini yi sa'amna, i ningbina zaa bɛ la zibsim ni. Tɔ - ka neesim din bɛ i ni maa yi nyɛ la zibsim, di nin zibgi halli nti laa n‑ya'ai.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Ba yi ti mugsi ya tinseaa ni, ya kur yima n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa yaasa. Dama yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya na ku kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu tinsi zaa n‑naai ka Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka di zaa yɔri, yiri maa daa bu lu, ka nyeeba ŋɔn tɔm tampii maa ni zugu.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: Ya sɔkam zaa yima! Bipuŋŋa wa bu kpi, amaa u gbisiri ni. U daa tɔ'asi lala ka di ku ba la laari.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋmanni la bumbura an lu tam zii la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn wum lahabaari maa ka baŋŋi di gbinni la, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin wanni tunsuma wala - halli kɔbkɔbga, bee pisiyooyoobu, bee pisitata.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: M‑paasi, nyin ni sɔ yi mari sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ka nyin ni ŋɔn ti bɛ soori ni n‑kyɛnni sariya karibu ziiya, kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka ya maali yɛttɔ'a maa yooma, lala saha maa; di yi pa lala u nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi sarikatta nuu ni, ka ŋɔn mun nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi pirinsi nuu ni, ka ba nin kpari i dansarika duu ni.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba ka ba kyaŋŋi maa, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi Magyada tiŋŋa ziiya.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, Zaadaari halli Sɔdoom ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Di daa gyɛri zama maa pam, Yisa ŋɔn tum seem maa. Ba daa yɛtti taaba, ni, Pa NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa maa nŋɔa? - Pa tin guri sɔ kyɛnni na la nla.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Ka bɛni niriba maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u, ba daa tum nti yɛli tinkpansi zaa an daa gyiligi bɛni maa, ka ba daa mari ba baaridima zaa n‑kyɛ u saani na.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Ni, Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi daabri n‑ti dugmadima ayi: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka laa n‑zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Ya bu nya? - karimbii bu gatti u karimba, ka daabri bu gatti u dugma.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa mun wum n yɛligu ka bu dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la gyɛrigu sɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tambisigu ni.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa kari daadam n‑yiisi kpaŋŋa maa, u daa kpɛ bipumbila maa ŋɔn daa dɔ seelli maa ka daa gbaai u nuu, ka bii maa di doaai.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa m‑paasi, ni, Arizana naam maa ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u daa buri bumburisuma u puu.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, i ku laa n‑yi bɛni na see ka i ti yɔ i samni maa zaa n‑naai, halli ka kɔbɔ yinni bu kpalim gba.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Di saha maa Yisa daa yɛliya, M Ba Naawunni, nyin ŋɔn su arizana ni duniya wa, m pɛ'ari i ni, dama nyin zaŋŋi yɛla wa zaa n‑sɔ'ai ka kyɛ yam dima ni baŋŋniba, ka laa n‑kasigi a n‑wulisi n‑ti bileesi.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Amaa Farisii dima maa daa wum lala ka daa yɛli, U zaŋŋi ArizinNaaba yiiko n‑katti la arizimbeeri maa. Ii! - Biyɛlizibuli n siri mari u.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Ni, Dizugu ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, yan di zaŋŋi yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin niŋi seem n‑nya bundirigu n‑di, bee n‑nya situra n‑yɛa - yan di fabli anŋɔ zaa yɛla; dama nyɔvuri gaari bundirigu, ka ningbina gaari bunyeerigu, bee m ma'ari ni?.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Karimbii yi ti paai u karimba tataabu, di nin sakki u ni. Ka daabri yi paai u dugma saani gba, di sakkiya. Ba yi zaŋŋi Arizimbeeri Naa yuuri m‑pa yiriseaa yiridaana, ni, Biyɛlizibuli n nyɛ u, ba bu ya booli u yiri maa dima lala? - halli ni yuseesi an bɛ n‑gaari lala.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka yiri maa daa lu zaa, ka ka luuwa daa bu veela gba.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Di saha ka di lahabaari maa daa mooli n‑gyili tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: Ka ba daa susi u, ni, u kur kyɛ ka ba siisi u kparigu nangbanni, ka ban daa siisi zaa daa nya alaafɛa.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Ka yunseaa, sɔkam zaa ŋɔn daa ti gbisi, ka u dindaana daa kyaŋŋi nti buri niimbɛŋni n‑niŋi puu maa bumbula maa sunsukkuni, ka bakki.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ban daa yɛli, Di tum zina.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Ii, m Ba, yɛlimaŋni lala veela i saani.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ka Yisa daa mi ba n daa teesiri seem, ka di zugu u daa yɛli ba, ni, Tinseelli kam zaa kan timbiisi yi purigi n‑zabri taaba la nin sa'am zaa. Bee, tinseaa, bee, yiriseaa, m bɛ ni kan niriba yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala tiŋŋa, bee, yiri, ku toaai n‑zaani.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Ya gɔsim niinsi ban bɛ saazugu la; ba bu butta, ba mun bu la'asiri bundirigu n‑niŋni napaari ni, amaa ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la diisiri ba ni: ka yan mun mari bukaata n‑gaari ba, bee m ma'ari ni?.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba yaasa, ni, Dizugu yan di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya ninsaalidima zugu. Bunseelli zaa kan pili ka bu nyara, ba nin piligi ka ka sɔkam ti nya; ka yɛttɔ'asɔ'ara zaasa muna - sɔkam nin baŋŋi a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli anŋɔ zaa n‑naai maa, di daa niŋi daadam maa alahazibu pam u yɛttɔ'a maa zugu.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛ bɛni maa, ka daa kyɛnni soori zugu, zoomma ayi daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa. Ba daa kur tasiri n‑yɛtti, Nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ti namboo.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Ka bumbula maa ŋɔn daa ti zoaai ka kyi maa daa ti ya niŋi n‑yirigi, di saha maa ka niimbɛŋni gba daa ti bunni na.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Bunseelli kam zaa ka m pun deaai m Ba nuu ni. Sɔ ka ni m‑mi Bii maa na'ala u Ba maa m mi u; ka sɔ mun gba ka ni m‑mi m Ba maa na'ala mani u Bii maa m mi u; m‑paasi sɔkam man u Bii maa ŋɔn nin sakki n‑kasigi m Ba maa yɛla n‑wulisi n‑ti u la.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ya sɔ nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri yɛla m‑mugsi u maŋŋa halli nti yakki u nyɔvuri tariga m‑paasi dabsiri yinni gba?.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Dizugu Sintaani muna, u yi ti katti u mammaŋŋa niriba n‑zabri ba, ŋɔn gba suulinsi ya niŋi la wula n‑zaani?.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Man sɔ'ari zibsim ni n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya yɛttɔ'aseesi zaasa, ya kyɛ ka ba wum a la wuntampeeliga; man kpɛ duu puuni ka waasiri n‑yɛtti ya yɛliseesi la, yan duum saazugu m‑mooli a ka sɔkam zaa wum.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn gɔsiri la pɔ'asɔ kɔrisi gɔsibu, di daama sufu teesa puuni ka u pun zaŋŋi pɔ'a maa n‑tum zina.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Dama u daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'amaŋni, ka u bu birisira halli beela; ni, di pa la kaman ba zaaligu baŋŋniba la ŋɔn yi wulisiri seem la.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ yiri ni la, zoomma wa daa kyɛ u saani na; ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Ya niŋi yɛdda, ni, n nin toaai n‑tibi ya? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ii.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Ka yiri maa daana tuntumniba daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Ti dugma, pa bumburisuma ka i daa buri i puu maa ni? Ka niimbɛŋni wa yi la yani.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Ni, Ya ninvugseeba ban zi zikara n‑gyɛ zaa, ya kyɛma n saani na, ka n nin kyɛ ka ya vuusi.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Ka wula zugu ka ya zaŋŋi bunyeera yɛla m‑mugsiri ya mansi? Ya baŋŋma pɔ'a-veelli-ku-siisi-koom la yɛla: gɔsim n‑nya kan zoaari seem moo ni, ka bu tumni n‑waanni gumni.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ni, n zaŋŋi la Biyɛlizibuli yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri maa, ka dinŋɔna ya karimbiseeba ban katti ba la - ba zaŋŋi ŋɔni dinni n‑katti ba? Dizugu ban nin ti di ya yɛttɔ'a wa sariya n‑lusi ya.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Ni, Yaasa, yan di zɔri ninvugseeba ban kuuri niriba ningbina, ka ku toaai n‑kuuri ba siisi m‑paasi. Amaa ya zɔm sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑sa'am ningbina ni siisi zaa ka zaŋŋi a n‑lɔsi bugum ni la.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Dizugu i nudirigu nimbiri yi kyɛ ka i tum tumbeoo, nyin lukkma ka m‑basi. I yi kɔŋŋni nimbiri yinni ka mari nyɔvuri, di yi sɔ ni ba ti zaŋŋi i ningbina zaa nti kpeesi wahala ni, bugum ziiya.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa siisi ba nini maa ka yɛli, Kyɛ ka di niŋi hoo yan niŋi yɛdda seem maa.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Kyɛ ka man ni yarim la'am n‑tumna: di ŋmani la hoo nigi ayi ŋɔn darigri na'adoo seem, ya nin ti bamsi tuma man saani, dama ninvugmaŋni n nyɛ ma, ka laa m‑mari sufubaalim. Ya nin vuusi ni sufudoaa.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya wa, ni, halli NaaSulɛmaana ni u naam gyirima zaasa gba daa na zi m‑bɔbi suturiveela kaman mɔpuuma wa yinni veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, N dindaansɔ n niŋi lala maa. Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Dinŋɔna ti kyɛma n‑vɔ niimbɛŋni maa m‑basi.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ka m mun zaŋŋi la Naawunni Siiya n‑katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni, dinŋɔna Naawunni naam daŋŋi m‑paai ya na.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Ba bu zaŋŋi la'abila yinni n‑daari niimbisi ayi? Amaa din zaa yɔri, niimbila yinni ku toaai n‑lu tiŋŋa ka di pa la ya Ba maa yuubu ni.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Ka i nudirigu yi kyɛ ka i tum tumbeoo, nyin zaŋŋma ka m‑basi. I yi kɔŋŋni nuu yinni ka mari nyɔvuri, di sɔ ni ba ti zaŋŋi i ningbina zaa nti kpeesi wahala ni, bugum ziiya.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Ka ba nini maa daa neaai di saha maa. Ka Yisa daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ baŋŋi.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Dama n na'adoo wa ku ŋmaari ya, ka n ziya maa fa'asi ni.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi maa, wula zugu ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? - Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ay! Dama yan la'asiri niimbɛŋni maa, pa seem ya nin ti dari n‑vɔ kyi la gba m‑paasi.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Ka ba ya niŋi la wula n‑kpɛ yaa daana yiri n‑zu u neema maa ka di pa la ba daŋŋi n‑lɔ lala yaa daana maa ka naayi nyaŋŋi n‑fa u yiri ni buna zaasa?.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Yarima muna, Naawunni mi halli ya zɔbri gba yɛla, ka baŋŋi a zaa yinni yinni kaaligu ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ba daa yɛli, ni, Dɔsɔ yi ya n‑kari u pɔ'a, see ka u sɔbi pɔ'akaribu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Amaa ba daa yi na ka daa moonni u yɛla n‑gyinni tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri, ni, Bɔ ka ti ya di?, bee, Bɔ ka ti ya nyu?, bee, Bɔ ka ti ya yɛa.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Ya kur kyɛ ka kyi maa ni niimbɛŋni maa zaa la'am n‑zoaai halli ni kaŋmaa saha. Tɔ, ka n nin yɛli ban ŋmaari a maa, ni, ba voaama niimbɛŋni maa pɔi n‑lɔ a gbilagbila n‑nyɔa, ka naayi ŋma kyi maa n‑la'asi n‑zaŋŋi n‑niŋi n napoo ni. .

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Dizugu yan di zɔri dabeem, dama yarim mari bukyi pam n‑gaari niinsi maa zaa.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔnkam kari u pɔ'a, di yi pa la pɔ'a maa ŋɔn tum zina zugu, di daama pun kyɛ ka pɔ'a maa tum zina, ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn di lala pɔ'a gba tum zina.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ku sakki ni u zɛ n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔna n za'asi ma n‑yi n nyaaŋŋa; bee sɔ yi ku suŋŋi ma ni ti la'asi bunwurima, u wurimni a m‑basira.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Ka dɔppa wa ŋɔn daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi maa, ninvugseeba daa mari dɔsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ mukka n‑kpɛ Yisa saani na. Arizimbeoo daa bɛ doo maa ni, ka dizugu u daa kɔŋŋi yɛttɔ'a.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Ninvugseeba ban bu mi Naawunni la bɛ la anŋɔ zaa nɔba karibu ni. Amaa yarima mari ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni maa, ka u mi halli, ni, ya boori la anŋɔ zaa.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa m‑paasi, ni, Arizana naam ŋmanni la hoo zinzambeelli nira ŋɔn zaŋŋi m‑buri u puu ni.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man dinni, man gba nin ti zaani m Ba tooni arizana ni, n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔn nyɛ la n dinni.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, M‑paasi yaasa, ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, Di pɔ noori ka yi di nyaaŋŋa; i yi ti pɔ, ni, i zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑ti Naawunni, see ka i siri zaŋŋi n‑tisi.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Dinzugu ka n yɛtti ya maa, niriba yi tum tumbeeri, bee ba yi sa'am Naawunni yuuri, Naawunni nin kyɛ lala ala'akyi taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ba, amaa ba yi ti sa'am Sii maa yuuri, kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi ka ni.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kari arizinni maa n‑yiisi mukka maa ni maa, doo maa daa piili n‑tɔ'asira. Ka di daa niŋi sɔkam alahazibu. Zama maa daa yɛtti, ni, Ti na zi n‑nya nŋɔ taabu Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu wa ni.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Ay, see ka ya daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi ya Ba naam yɛttɔ'a, ni u yɛla zaa, m‑mugsi ya mansi pɔi, ka ŋɔn nin naayi zaŋŋi bunseelli din kpalim zaasa m‑paasi ya.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Ka nyɛ la bunseelli bumbura kam zaa ŋɔn galisi n‑gaari ka; amaa kan daa ti zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaa, ka daa leebi hoo tiiya, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri ka zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ka ŋɔnkam mun yi za'asi ma niriba tooni, man gba nin za'asi u m Ba tooni arizana ni.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di pɔ noori halli beela. Di pɔ arizana, dama Naawunni naam zizii nla.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Halli ŋɔn tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑galim man Ninsaala Bii maa gba, u nin toaai n‑nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi; amaa ŋɔn mun yɛli seelli n‑galim Sii Kasi maa, di daama ŋɔn kur ku nya u taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi, zuna halli ni sahaseelli zaa.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa Farisidima maa daa yɛli, Arizimbeeri Naaba ti u soori ni u katti arizimbeeri maa.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Di fabinni beoo yɛla zuna, see ka i kyɛ ka beoo maa sa ya fabli ka maŋtoori yɛla. Zuna mun mari la ka toori yɛlimugsa ka a sakki.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa, ni, Arizana naam ŋmanni la dabinseesi pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑gyɛri zum ni, ni u ti sɛ paanu. U daa zaŋŋi dabina beela n‑niŋi zum pam puuni - hoo zum kuriba ata - ka a dɔla, halli nti kyɛ ka zum maa zaa ti du n‑fuusi.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Ni, Ya di teesi, ni, m mari la nangbanyinni beesigu n‑kyɛ duniya wa na. Ay! M bu kyɛ na ni m mari nangbanyinni yɛla na, see zaba.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Mun di pɔ tingbaŋŋu, dama Naawunni napɔntɔmnigu nla. Di laa m‑pɔ Gyɛrusalɛm, dama Naaba Yiikodaana maa tiŋŋa nla.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya kyɛ ka tiseaa nyɛ tisuŋŋu, ka ka nin wanni walisuma. Di yi pa lala, tibeoo n nyɛ ka, ka ka nin wanni walibeeri. Ba gɔsiri la tii kam zaa wala ni ba ti baŋŋi tii maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Ka Yisa daa gyiligi tinsi maa zaa, ni tinkpansi maa gba, n‑wulisiri ba la'aŋŋu duri puuni, ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla, ka tibri ban daa beera ka dooru balimbalim m mari ba, bee, ban kɔŋŋni yaa ni alaafɛ zaa.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Tɔ. Anŋɔ zaa ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri niriba la, ka daa kur zaŋŋi ma'asa ni nyɛsa n‑tɔ'asira. U daa bu tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseelli ka ma'asa ka di ni.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Di pɔ halli i maŋtoori zugu gba, dama i ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka ka zɔbgu yinni ti pɛligi, bee, n‑sɔbgi.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Ya bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmanni la wa'abeeri! Ya ya niŋi la wula n‑yɛtti din veela, ka ya mammansi n nyɛ ninvugbeeri? Dama dinkam bɛ ninsaala sufu ni nti paali ka, dina ka u yiisiri u noori ni na.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: N kyɛ na ni n tugli la doowa ni u ba, ni bipuŋŋa ni u ma gba, ni bii pɔ'a ni u dinyaampɔ'a m‑paasi, ka ba zabi.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti nya daadam maa, u sufu daa zɔ ba namboo, dama ba daa ŋmani la peesi ban ka ŋɔn guri ba, ka di mugsi ba ka sa'am ba sufu.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Lala daa kyɛ ka anabi la yɛligu la ti niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:- N nin tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a n‑tiri ba - Yɛttɔ'anyɛsa ka n nin tɔ'asira: N nin kasigi yɛlisɔ'ara n‑ti ba Ba na zi m‑baŋŋi yɛliseesi, Halli duniya naaŋŋu saha zaŋŋi m‑paai zuna.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Kur tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti Ii, ii!, bee, Ay, ay!, dama din paasi lala zugu yi la Yɛlibeeri Daana la saani na.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Bunsuma yi bɛ ninvugsuŋŋu puuni m‑paali u, bundaana n nyɛ u, ka u nin yiisiri bunsuma maa na n‑tisira; ka ninvugbeoo ŋɔn mun yi paali ni bumbeeri yiisiri la bumbeeri n‑tisiri na.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Lala ka nira yi ti mari dindaana, u toori yiri puuni ka di daama nin bɛ ni.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Dizugu u daa yɛli n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ya kyi ŋmaabu tuma wa zoaaya pam, amaa ka ya tuntumniba maa mun bu zoaai;.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti kyeoosi zama maa n‑ti ba soori ni ba bakki, u daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri maa ni. U daa bɛ bɛni, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, Yɛlima ti niimbɛŋni an bɛ puu ni la nyɛsiri la gbinni.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli, ni, Sɔ yi ti sa'am i nimbiri, soori bɛ ni ni i gba sa'am u nimbiri; u yi ti ŋma i nyinni muna, soori bɛ ni ni i ti ŋmɛ u gba nyinni di zugu.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Man yɛtti ya wa, ni, sariya karibu Zaadaari ba nin boosi sɔkam boosa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'ayɔya kam zaa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Ŋɔn wuri u ba, bee, u ma, n‑gaari ma, di daama bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani; ka ŋɔn laa n‑wuri u biiya, doo bee pɔ'a, n‑gaari ma, ŋɔn gba bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: dizugu ya susim ti Dugma ŋɔn nyɛ puu daana wa, ka u tum niriba na m‑paasi ka ba ŋmaara.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; dɔsɔ ŋɔn buri bumburisuma la n nyɛ man Ninsaala Biiya.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di zabi ni ya ta'ai ya taalli daana tuma; faasee ka sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Dama i noori yɛligu zugu ka ba nin yiisi i na, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana; ka i noori yɛligu zugu yaasa ka ba nin lusi i m‑basi.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Halli ŋɔn mun ku sakki n‑zaŋŋi u kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔn gba bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Puu maa n nyɛ duniya wa, ka bumbulisuma maa n nyɛ ninvugseeba ban su naam maa beebu soori, niimbɛŋni bumbulibeeri mun n nyɛ ninvugseeba ban bɛ Yɛlibeeri Daana la suulinsi ni.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Di saha maa ka karimba dimseeba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u, ni Farisii dimseeba m‑paasi, n‑yɛli, ni, Ti Karimba, ti boori la i tum alahazibu tunseaa n‑wulisi ti.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Sɔ yi ti ya taasi i kootu ni n‑deaai i bunyeerigu, ka laa m‑boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Dindaansɔ ŋɔn daa buri bumbeeri la nyɛ la Sintaani; kaŋmaa saha n nyɛ duniya wa naari, ka ban ŋmaari maa n nyɛ malaakadima.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam zaa gyaari ya, mani ka u gyaari. Ka ŋɔnkam gyaari ma, Naawunni ka u gyaatira, dama ŋɔn Naawunni n tum ma na.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Zuna niriba wa nyɛ la ninvugbeeri ban boori zina. Ba kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni, ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku ti nya sɛseelli m‑paasi Anabi Gyoona alahazibu yɛla maa.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Ka sɔ yi ya mugsi i, ni, i zi u neema n‑kyaŋŋi maalli yinni, nyin ziima a n‑kyaŋŋi abayi n‑ti u.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Niimbɛŋni la ŋɔn la'asi n‑nyɔ seem la, lala ka di ti ya niŋi duniya naari Zaadaari maa.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa gyaari anabi ŋɔn nyɛ anabi maa zugu, ŋɔn gba nin deaai laara seaa kan nyɛ anabi dinni. Ka ŋɔnkam mun gyaari yɛlimaŋni daana ŋɔn nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana maa zugu, ŋɔn gba nin deaai laara seaa kan nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana dinni.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Gyoona ŋɔn daa bɛ zintitaari maa puu ni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata maa, lala ka man Ninsaala Bii maa gba nin bɛ tingbaŋŋu puuni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata bɛni.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti susi bunni i saani, zaŋŋma n‑ti u. Sɔ mun boori ni i pɛŋŋi u bunseelli, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti tum u malaakadima ka ba zaŋŋi bankam zaa daa kyɛ ka niriba tum tumbeeri, ni bankam zaa daa niŋi yɛlibeeri, n‑la'asi n‑yiisi u naam maa ni m‑basi.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Halli ŋɔnkam zaa mun yi ti zaŋŋi kɔmaasim n‑ti n nyaandɔlisɔ ni u ti nyu, ŋɔn nyɛ n nyaandɔla maa zugu, yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, di daama gba ku toaai n‑kɔŋŋi u laara.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Niniva dima gba daa wum Gyoona wa'azu yiisigu la ka daa kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑basi ka niŋi tuuba, ka dizugu ba nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari maa, dama ŋɔn gaari anabi Gyoona bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli, ni, i wuri i zɔ ka yaai i dindaana.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ka ba nin ti zaŋŋi ba n‑lɔsi bugum ni, ya'aseaa ban nin bɛ ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Pɔ'a-naaba sɔ ŋɔn daa su nudirigu luga tinseaa la daa yi wa'aligu n‑kyɛ na ni u ti wum NaaSulɛmaana yam yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu u nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari maa, dama ŋɔn gaari NaaSulɛmaana bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Di saha ka Naawunni niriba nin nya'ara, hoo wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn nya'ari seem maa, m‑bɛ ba Ba naam maa ni. Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya, ni, ya wura ya dindaandima, ka susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ban namsiri ya.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Tɔ; ka arizimbɛsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa kpɛ dɔsɔ puuni n‑su u. Ka ba daa yiisi u ŋɔn daa bɛ dɔsɔ puuni maa ni n‑kari m‑basi. Ka u daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gyinni yoo ni, m‑bɛ kukoo ni m‑boori vuusim ziiya, halli nti gyɛa.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Ni, Arizana naam ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- di daa niŋiya ni, ka dɔsɔ daa kyaŋŋi nti nya arizikki kan pi tiŋŋa, ka u gbi tanni n‑labsi m‑pi ka. Ka daa kuli nti koosi u marigu zaa n‑labi na nti da lala tingbanni maa.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Lala ka ya nin ti leebi bisuma n‑ti ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni. Ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka wuntaŋŋa pusiri n‑neaari ninvugbeeri m‑paasi ninvugsuma zugu; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka saa miiri n‑niŋni ban deaari u yɛla ni ban za'asiri u yɛla m‑paasi.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: Di saha maa ka u daa yɛli, ni, See ka n labi n doo maa saani - yiri seaa man daa yi ka kyɛ la ni! Ka u daa ti labi na n‑nya ka sɔsɔ ka bɛni ka ba pun pii yiri maa n‑sɛ'asi ka kasikasi ka ka veela;.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Ni, Arizana naam laa n‑ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- walima daansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa goori m‑boori kugsuma, bumpeela nyain, an ligri daa zoaai pam.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Ya yi wuri la ban wuri ya, ya nin nya laara di zugu? Ay! Halli lampodeaariba gba niŋni la lala - bee, m ma'ari ni?.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: ka dizugu u daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u arizintaaba ayɔpɔi seeba ban bɛ n‑gaari u mammaŋŋa, ni ba ti paasi u zugu, ka ba daa kpɛ nti bɛ bɛni. Di saha maa ka di daama beesigu daa bɛ pam n‑gaari din daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ seem. Ka di kur bɛ la lala zuna niriba saani.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti nya seaa ka ka veeli n‑gaari a zaa, u daa labi yiri ni nti koosi u marigu zaa n‑kyaŋŋi nti da ka.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Ka ya yi puusiri la ya mabiisi kɔkɔa, tunsuŋŋu bɔ ka ya tumna? Halli ban pa Izraɛli dima gba niŋni lala, bee, m ma'ari ni?.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa na tɔ'asiri n‑tiri zama maa, u ma ni u tunzɔppa daa paai na n‑zaani samanni, ni, ba boori n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti u.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Ni, Arizana naam laa n‑ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- zingbaariba daa lɔbi ba laama mɔ'ari ni n‑gba zaasim balibu kam zaa.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Ay! See ka ya kyɛ ka ya tuma n nyɛ an veela zaa, kaman ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni tuma ŋɔn veela zaa seem la.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ daa yɛli u, ni, I ma ni i tunzɔppa bɛ samanni m‑boori n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti i.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Laaŋŋu maa daa paali la ni zaasim. Tɔ, ka ba darigi ka n‑du mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu nti yiisi a n‑gamsi. Ba daa zaŋŋi bunsuma la n‑niŋi ba peeri ni; ka daa zaŋŋi bunseesi an bu veeli la n‑lɔbi m‑basi.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, M ma n nyɛ ŋɔni? ni, n tunzɔppa n nyɛ bandima.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Lala ti yɛ nyɛ di duniya naari daari; malaakadima la nin gamsi ninvugbeeri la ninvugsuma la puuni.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: ka daa teaai u nuu m‑bugsi u nyaandɔliba maa ka daa yɛli, Nnya ba! M ma ni n tunzɔppa maa nŋɔa!.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: n‑zaŋŋi ba n‑lɔsi bugum ni, ya'aseelli ban nin kumni ka ŋɔbri ba nyina la.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa tumni m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la yuubu nyɛ la n tunzɔa, bee n tunzɔpɔ'a, bee m ma.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Ammi, ya baŋŋi anŋɔ zaa gbinni, bee? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ii.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Di saha ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Dinŋɔ wulisiri, ni, zaaligu karimbasɔ ŋɔn labgi Arizana naam yɛla nyaandɔla ŋmanni la yiridaana ŋɔn kpeeri u neema duu ni n‑yiisiri bumpaala na, ka yiisi bunkura na m‑paasi.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba nyɛsa wa n‑naai, u daa bakki bɛni.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: n‑labi u maŋtoori tiŋŋa. U daa bɛ la ba la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti wulisi ba. Ka alahazibu daa gbaai ba, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, Yani ka doo wa lee n bamsi u yɛla wa zaa, ka laa n‑nya yiiko n‑tumni tunkara wa?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Pa kampinta bii maa nŋɔa? Pa u ma n nyɛ Mariama? - ka u tunzɔppa n nyɛ Gyeemsi, ni Gyoosɛfi, ni Saimoon, ni Gyuudasi la?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: U tunzɔpɔ'aba zaa gba bu bɛ la ti saani? Ka yani ka u lee n‑nya anŋɔ zaa.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Lala zugu ba daa bu boori u yɛla halli beela. Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Anabi kam nyari gyirima luginseelli kam zaa ni, na'ala u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa niriba ni u yiri dima saani.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Ka u daa bu tum tunkara pam bɛni, ban daa bu niŋi Naawunni yɛdda maa zugu.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Dabsa ayoobu ŋɔn daa ti gaari, Yisa daa zaŋŋi Piita ni Gyeemsi ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni n‑zi ba n‑gaari tooni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zɔtitaari zugu ka ba daa ti bɛ ba kɔkɔa.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Ban daa gɔsiri maa, ba daa ti nya ka Yisa takki ni, ka u nindaa daa ti nya'asiri hoo wuntaŋŋa la, ka u neema daa pɛligi nyain!.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa naai yɛttɔ'a wa, u daa yi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ya'ari, halli nti lɔŋŋi Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Dinsaha u nyaandɔliba ata maa daa nya ka Musa ni anabi Ilaygya daa bɛ ni ka ban ni ŋɔn daa sɔsira.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Ka zama pam daa dɔli u, ka u daa tibri ba bɛni.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Ti Dugma, ti kpa wa beesigu veela! I yi boori lala, n nin ti suga ata, i dinni yinni, ni Musa dinni, ni Ilaygya mun dinni.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Ka Farisii dimseeba daa kyɛ u saani na ni ba da u gbinni. Ba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, Yɛlim ti: ammi, doo mari soori ka u nin toaai n‑yiisi u pɔ'a daliiri kam zaa zugu.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Ka Piita noori ŋɔn na bu lu tiŋŋa, sangbampeelli daa ligi ba na, ka kukɔri daa tɔ'asiri sangbaŋŋu maa ni n‑yɛtti, ni, M bii maa nŋɔa, ka n wuri u: n nini tigi u pam: ya wumma u yɛligu.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, Ya bu karim Litaafi la? Ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa piili duniya naaŋŋu la, u daa naam ba doowa ni pɔ'a.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, dabeem daa gbaai ba. Ba daa sunni tiŋŋa dabeem maa zugu.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: ka daa yɛli ba, ni, dinŋɔ zugu doo nin kyɛ u ba ni u ma ka ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a la'am n‑tabli taaba. Ni, babayi maa nin la'am m‑bɛ ni kaman bunyinni.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyɛ na nti siisi ba ka yɛli ba, Ya yisigma! Yan di zɔri dabeem.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Dizugu ba laa m‑pa niriba ayi amaa bunyinni. Dinŋɔna Naawunni ŋɔn la'am bunseesi ni a leebi bunyinni maa, ninsaala di wɔligi a.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Ba daa wukki ba zugri saazugu, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; kulla Yisa kɔkɔ n daa zɛya.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Ka Farisii dima la daa boosiya, ni, Ka di niŋi la wula ka Musa daa zaali zaaligu la, ni, sɔ nin toaai n‑za'asi u pɔ'a? - ni, u zaŋŋma pɔ'aza'asigu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka u bakki.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ka ban daa ti yi zoori maa zugu n‑sigri na, Yisa daa kpaamni ba n‑yɛli, ni, Ya di yɛli sɔ yan nya seem wa, halli ka man Ninsaala Biiya ti doaai kum ni.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya tuppuwumni la zugu ka Musa daa ti soori, ni, ba niŋma lala. Amaa di daa pa lala piiligu saha la.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa boosi Yisa, ni, Wula zugu ka ti karimbadima yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya nin daŋŋi n‑kyɛ na pɔi, ka Masiiya naayi paai na.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, doowa yi kari u pɔ'a ka pɔ'a maa bu tum la zina, ka doo maa ti laa n‑di pɔ'asɔ, u niŋi zina.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ilaygya n siri ya n‑kyɛ na n‑sɛ'asi buna kam zaa.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba daa yɛli u, Di yi nyɛ lala ŋɔna, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi doo ni u pɔ'a yɛla, dinŋɔna di sum ni nira di laa n‑di pɔ'a.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Man mun yɛtti ya wa, ni, Ilaygya pun kyɛ na ka ba daa bu baŋŋi u, ka daa niŋi u ban daa boori seem. Lala ka man Ninsaala Bii gba ya n‑di wahala ba nuu ni.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Wulisigu wa muna - pa sɔkam zaa nin toaai n‑dɔli dinŋɔ maa, na'ala seeba Naawunni ŋɔn pun zaŋŋi n‑ti ba la.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Di saha maa ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura maa yɛla ka u tɔ'asiri ba maa.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Dizugu yɛla pam m bɛ ni n‑kyɛ ka doo ku toaai n‑di pɔ'a#:- baseeba m bɛ ni ka ba madima daa dɔ'ai ba lala, ka baseeba ninsaala daa niŋi ba lala, seeba mun niŋi la lala ba mammansi, Arizana naam maa zugu. Kyɛ ka sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑deaai wulisigu wa deaai di.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba ata wa daa sigi na nti paai daadam saani na. Ka zama maa puuni dɔsɔ daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti sɔmni u tooni.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Di saha maa ka ba daa mari bibisi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ni u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa ba zugri n‑susi Naawunni n‑ti ba. Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa nyikki ba di zugu.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: n‑yɛli, ni, N daana, zɔm n bidibga wa namboo! Kpisinkpiiri ŋɔn mari u n‑namsiri u pam. Di kur lusiri u bugum ni, bee, koom ni sahakam.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Yisa mun daa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛli bibisi maa, yan di ta'ari ba ni ba di kyɛ n saani na! Dama banŋɔ taabu n su Naawunni naam la.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Ka n mari u n‑kyɛ i nyaandɔliba wa saani na, ka ba bu nyaŋŋi n‑tibi u.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa biisi maa zugri, ka daa doaai m‑bakki bɛni.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, Ah! Ya zuna niriba wa n kur nyɛ bungɔlima ka ku sakki yɛdda niŋbu! N ya yuui la wula ya saani? Bee man nin di sugru ya saani ka di yuui wula? Tɔ, yan mari bii maa n‑kyɛ kpa na.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, ni, N Karimba, tunsuŋŋu bɔ ka n nin tum ka deaai nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Ka Yisa daa tasi arizimbeoo maa, ka u zɔ ka kyɛ bii maa, ka bii maa di kpaŋŋi zaa.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Ka u daa yɛli u, Wula ka i boosi ma bunsuma yɛla? Naawunni kɔkɔ n nyɛ bunsuŋŋu. I yi boori i kpɛ nyɔvuri, see ka i dɔli zaaligudima la.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti sɔ'ai n‑yɛli u, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka tirima guui arizimbeoo maa karibu.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Ka doo maa daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, Zaaligu bɔ n nyɛ a? Ka Yisa daa yɛli, Ni, Di ku ninsaala. Di tum zina. Di zu. Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Di nyɛ la ya yɛdda poorim zugu. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi da mari yɛdda ka di sa'ai zinzam beelli, ya naa n‑toaai n‑yɛli zoori wa, ni, ka doaai m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni sa, ka ka niŋi lala. Halli sɛseelli ku kɔŋŋi niŋbu ya saani.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima. ni, Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la. .

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Ban daa goori n‑gyinni Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni sahanseaa, Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑ti ninvugseeba nuusi ni.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Ka na'akyimbila maa yɛli, Anŋɔ zaa ka n daa pun dɔla. Bumbɔ m pɔsigi ma yaasa.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
