local: ka ba nin ku ma. Ka Ninsaala Bii maa kum maa nyaaŋŋa di nin paai dabsa ata ka u nin laa n‑doaai. Ka ba sufu daa sa'am pam di zugu.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, I yi boori n‑niŋi seelikam suŋŋa deede, kyɛma nti koosi i marigu zaa n‑zaŋŋi n‑tɔri faradima, ka i arizikki maali n‑zoaai Naawunni saani. Ka kyɛ na n‑dɔli ma.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Ban daa paai Kapirinyum tiŋŋa sahanseaa, ninvugseeba daa kyɛ ba saani na ni ba ti toosi Gyɛrusalɛm Gyeeŋŋu Yiri lampoo ligri maa. Ba daa yɛli Piita, ni, Ammi, ya karimba bu yoori ti lampoo la.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Tɔ, ka na'akyimbila maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala u daa bakki ni sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ay! U yoori, walla! Ka ban daa ti paai yiri maa ni, Yisa daa yɛli Piita, ni, N zɔ Saimoon, i teesi la wula? - Duniya wa nadima maa, bandima saani ka ba deaari lampoo ni piini? Ba timbiisi saani, bee, tinzundima saani.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Arizana naam ni.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, tinzundima maa saani, Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, dizugu timbiisi ka samni.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ka n yɛtti ya yaasa, ni, halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ seeriga voori ni m‑puligi n‑yi ka bundaana na ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Ni, Amaa ti bu boori ni ti daamni ba sufu, ka dizugu kur kyɛma mɔ'ari ni n‑lɔbi zingoaa; i yi daŋŋi n‑gbaai zinseaa, zaŋŋma u n‑yaam u noori ka i nin nya lampoo buyi la'afu: kana ka i nin zaŋŋi n‑yɔ man ni nyin samni.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba ŋɔn wum lala di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba boosi, ni, Ka ŋɔni n lee n‑nin tiligi.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Yisa daa gɔsi ba ka yɛli, Dinŋɔ tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani, amaa Naawunni ku kɔŋŋi bunseelikam niŋbu.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Dinsaha ka Piita daa yɛli u, Nyama, tirima n nyɛ seeba ban kyɛ yɛlli kam m‑basi ka dɔli i! Tɔ - bɔ ka ti nin mari di zugu.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Yisa daa yɛli, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, yaseeba ban pun dɔli man nyaaŋŋa la, ni, Ninsaala Bii maa yi ti zi u naam zizii maa ni, ka di bɛ la duniya paalli din kyɛ na la puuni - lala saha maa ka ya niriba pii ni ayi wa nin ti zini naam gbana zugu n‑katti Izraɛli za'anɔya pii ni ayi niriba sariya.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Ka sɔ yi bɛ ni ka u kyɛ u yiri, bee u babiisi ni u mabiisi, u ba ni u ma, bee u biisi bee u puri, man yuuri zugu, di daama nin deaai a taabu pampampam, ka u nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari m‑paasi.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Amaa ban daŋŋi tooni nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa, ka ban kpalimni nyaaŋŋa mun nin ti daŋŋi tooni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Yiridaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa daŋŋi asuba n‑doaai n‑yi na, ni u ti bɔ tuntumniba ni ba tum u puu tuma n‑ti u.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: U daa nya seeba, ka daa sakki ni u nin yɔ ba sɔkam dabsimunni tuma ligri, ka daa kyɛ ka ba kyaŋŋi u puu ma ni.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yi na kurigu awai saha nti kpɛ daa ni n‑nya ninvugseeba ka ba kpalim n‑zɛya n‑ka tuma.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Tɔ, ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ba gba kyɛma nti tum u tuma maa, ka u ti yɔ ba din tuusi.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Ka ba daa sakki ka kyaŋŋi. Ka u daa laa n‑yi na agbaa saha, ni kurigu ata saha yaasa, n‑niŋi lala.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Di daa ti paai kurigu anu saha, ka u daa laa n‑yi na n‑kpɛ daa ni ka seeba kpalim n‑zɛya ka ka tuma. Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka ya kpalim n‑zɛ kpa yɔriyɔri, halli ni zaanoori saha wa.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Sɔ na bu zaŋŋi ti ni ti tum. Ka u yɛli ba, Tɔ! Ya gba kyɛma m puu ni nti tum ka n nin yɔ ya.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Ka zaanɔyuŋŋu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, puu maa daana daa yɛli u tuma zuguraana, ni, u kyɛma nti booli u tuntumniba na ka u ti yɔ ba ba ligri. Ni, u daŋŋma m‑piili ban kpalim nyaaŋŋa la halli nti paai asuba dima maa.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Ka dizugu baseeba ban daa kyaŋŋi kurigu anu saha la daa kyɛ na nti deaai dabsimunni tuma ligri.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Tɔ; ka di daa ti paai asuba dima maa, ka ba daa teesi, ni, ba nin deaai ligri n‑gaari banla dina, amaa ba gba daa deaai dabsimunni tuma yoori.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Ka ban daa deaai lala maa, ba daa nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋi puu maa daana.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni, Ban kpalim nyaaŋŋa wa bu tum hoo tirima ŋɔn tum seem la. Saha beela kɔkɔ ka ba tum, ka tirima mun tum asuba halli ni yuŋŋu saha wa ka di wum ti pam; ka ti zaa deaai yoori yummu.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba ninvugsɔ, ni, N zɔa, wumma! M bu yɔmsiri i ni. I da bu sakki, ni, i ya deaai dabsimunni tuma ligri?.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Zaŋŋma i ligri n‑kyaŋŋi! M boori n zaŋŋi n‑tisi ŋɔn kpalim nyaaŋŋa wa hoo man zaŋŋi n‑ti i.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: Mani n su ligri maa. I bu sakki ni n zaŋŋi m mammantoori bunni n‑niŋi man boori seem? Bee, i zabri ma nyuuri n nirilim tuma zugu? .

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, Dizugu ban daŋŋi tooni nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa, ka ban kpalimni nyaaŋŋa mun nin ti daŋŋi tooni.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wa Gyɛrusalɛm, u daa zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑yi kpaŋŋa n‑yɛli ba.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: ni, Ya wumma: ti wa la Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, bɛni ka ba nin ti zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑niŋi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima nuusi ni. Ban nin ti kari n sariya n‑zaŋŋi ma taalli, ni, n sumni kum.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: ka naayi zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni, ni ba maali ansarisi, ka feebi ma ka ku ma. Amaa daba ata daari n nin yisigi kum ni.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Ka Zɛbidii pɔ'a daa mari u biisi, Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni, n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na nti gbaani n‑ti u ka susiri bunseelli u saani.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Ka u daa boosi u, ni, Bumbɔ n nyɛ di? Ka pɔ'a maa yɛli u, Ni, i kyɛ ka m biisi ayi wa ti zinni n‑tabli i tooni, i nudirigu ni i nuzaa ziiya, i naam zɔŋŋu puuni.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ya bu mi yan susiri seelli maa. Ya nin toaai n‑nyu wahala kɔbɛseaa ka man ya n‑nyu wa? Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, ii, ba nin nyaŋŋi.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Ya siri nin ti nyu n wahala kɔbeaa la, amaa mani bu su noori ni n wulisi ban nin zinni n nudirigu bee n nuzaa zii maa. Di nyɛ la : m Ba Naawunni n ya n‑yiisi niriba maa, ka u pun maali ba ziziisi siri n‑zaali ba.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Tɔ, ka u nyaandɔliba ban kpalim piiya la ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, ba suuri daa yisigi n‑niŋi mabiisi ayi wa, Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Dizugu Yisa daa booli ba ni ba la'amma taaba na, ka yɛli ba, Ya mi ninvugseeba ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la yɛla, ni, ba saani ka ban su ba fukumsi mari la yiiko ka niŋni putoo n‑tiri ba, ka ba kpamma su ba yɛla ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Amaa di pa lala ya saani. Ya sɔ yi boori tibsim, see ka u tumni n‑tiri ya daadam wa zaa.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Ya sɔ yi boori ni u leebi toondaana, see ka u leebi ya zaa daabri.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Kaman man Ninsaala Bii bu kyɛ na ni ba tumni n‑suŋŋni ma, amaa n kyɛ na ni n ti tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba, ka zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri m‑pa talima ka niriba pam nya tiliginsim.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa ti ya yi Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa maa, daadam pam daa dɔli ba.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Di saha ka zoomma ayi daa zi soori maa gbiriŋŋu, ka daa ti wum, ni, Yisa n gatta, ka ba daa tasi n‑yɛli, ni, Nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ti namboo.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ka daadam maa daa nyikki ba n‑yɛli, ni, ba sina ba vuuri. Amaa ba daa laa n‑tasi pampam m‑paasi, ni, Nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ti namboo.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ka Yisa daa zaani, ka booli ba na, nti boosi ba, ni, Bɔ ka ya boori ni n niŋi n‑ti ya.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, Ti Bapira, ti boori la i neaai ti nini wa ka ti nyara.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Ka Yisa daa siisi ba nini maa, ka di saha ka a daa neaai ka ba nyara, ka ba daa dɔli Yisa di daari maa.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na ka boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni n nyɛ ninvugtitaari n‑gaari zaa Arizana naam maa ni.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Ka u daa booli bii yinni n‑zaali ba tooni.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: ka yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, sɔ yi bu takki n‑leebi hoo bibila ŋɔn bɛ seem, u ku toaai n‑kpɛ Arizana naam maa ni.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Sɔ ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa ka ŋmanni bibila wa - ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn galisi n‑gaari sɔkam zaa Arizana naam ni.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Sɔ yi gyaari bibila wa taabu man yuuri zugu, mani ka di daama n gyaari.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Ni, Amaa sɔ yi yɔmsi bibisi wa yinni ka kyɛ ka u bu laa n‑niŋi ma yɛdda ka za'asi ma, lala nira nin ti nya ka ba yi da zaŋŋi nɛtitaari n‑lɔ u nangoori ni ka zaŋŋi u n‑lɔsi mɔ'azuliŋŋu ni, di naa n‑sɔ u beesigu.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Sufusa'aŋŋu m bɛ duniya wa ni, yɔmsigu zugu, ni yɛdda kɔŋŋsim zugu. Soori ka ni ka yɔmsigu ku kyɛ na, amaa sufusa'aŋŋu m bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yɔmsira ka kyɛ ka niriba kyɛ ba yɛdda niŋbu!.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Dizugu i nuu, bee, i gbari, yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin ŋmaam ka m‑basi. I yi mari nuu yinni, bee, gbari yinni, ka mari nyɔvuri, di sɔ i mari nuusi ayi, bee, gbaya ayi, ka kpɛ bugum bun bu kpiiri halli sahakam maa ni.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: I nimbiri yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin lukkma ka n‑lɔbi. I yi mari nimbiri yinni n‑kpɛ nyɔvuri ni, di sɔ i mari nini ayi ka kpɛ bugum wahala ni.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Nyama! Yan di zeem bibisi wa yinni yɛla. N yɛtti ya, ni, ba malaakadima m bɛ arizana ni n‑gyeemni m Ba Naawunni.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Ni, Ya teesi la wula? - Dɔsɔ yi mari u peesi kɔbga ka yinni ti bɔrigi, u bu ya bɔ u ziiya? Ay! - u nin kyɛ u peesi kɔbga‑yinni‑ka la ka ba kpalim m‑bɛ zoori zugu, ka kyaŋŋi nti bɔ yinni maa ziiya.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Ka u yi ti nya u, n yɛtti ya wa, ni, u sufu nin maai peoo yinni maa zugu n‑gaari peesi kɔbga‑yinni‑ka ban bu bɔrigi la.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Lala n nyɛ di ya Ba saani arizana ni: u gba bu boori u bibisi wa yinni basi yɔri.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Ka u daa yɛli, I mabii yi tum i taalli, kur kyɛma nti nya u, nyin ni ŋɔn kɔkɔa, n‑wulisi u u taalli ŋɔn nyɛ seem. U yi ti wum n‑ti i, i labsi u n‑lɔ mabiitiri nangbanyinni yaasa.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Amaa u yi ku sakki n‑wum n‑ti i, zaŋŋma ninvugyinni, bee, babayi m‑paasi i maŋŋa zugu ka ya kyaŋŋi nti tɔ'asi n‑ti u. Lala ka ya nin kyɛ ka sɛhara dima ayi-ata noori ni ka yɛttɔ'a yɛlimaŋni nin yi paalu.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Ka u yi ku sakki n‑wum n‑ti ya yaasa, ya kyɛ ka ya mabiisi zaa la'am n‑wumna. U yi ku sakki n‑wum la'aŋŋu maa noori, ya gɔsima u hoo ŋɔn pa la Naawunni nira, bee, ŋɔn nyɛ lampodeaara.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Ni, N yɛtti ya ni, ni, yan lɔ bunseelli duniya wa ni, dina n lɔ Arizana ni gba; yan mun lɔrigi bunseelli duniya wa ni, dina n lɔrigi Arizana ni gba.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Ka n yɛtti ya yaasa, ni, niriba ayi yi gbaai seelli duniya wa ni ka susi Naawunni di zugu, dina ka m Ba sɔ ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni nin ti niŋi n‑ti ba.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Niriba ayi-ata yi ti la'am taaba man yuuri maa zugu, n nin bɛ ba saani.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Ka Piita daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti boosi u, ni, N dugma, m mabii yi ti tum ma taalli, napɔmbula ka n nin kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u? Napɔmbuyɔpɔi, bee.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Pa la napɔmbuyɔpɔi, amaa napɔŋŋu pisiyɔpɔi zii buyɔpɔi.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, ni, Dama arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Nasɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa yɛ gɔsi u kpamma ŋɔn mari u ligri n‑tumni seem.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: U daa piili lala laasaabu, ka ba daa zi sɔ n‑kpɛ na, ka u daa di Naa maa samni salima ligri tusatusa.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Ka doo maa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑yɔ lala samni maa, ka Naaba daa yɛli, ni, ba koosima u ni u pɔ'a ni u biisi ni u marigu zaa n‑yɔa.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ka doo maa daa gbaanni Naa maa tooni ka bɛlimni u, ni, u dim sugru ka u nin yɔ samni maa zaa.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Ka Naaba maa sufu daa sa'am pam ka u daa zɔ u namboo ka kyɛ u samni zaa m‑pɛŋŋi u.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Tɔ; ka Naaba kpeema wa daa yi n‑kyɛnna, nti paai u kpeemtaraansɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ u sandaana, amaa u samni daa bu zoaai. Ka u daa gbaai di daama n‑diisi u nangoori n‑yɛli u, ni, u yooma u samni maa zaa.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ka doo maa daa gbaanni u tooni ka bɛlimni u, ni, u dim sugru ka u nin yɔ samni maa zaa.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Amaa u daa za'asi ka zaŋŋi u n‑taasi dansarika ni, halli ka u ti yɔ samni maa.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Ka ba taaba maa ŋɔn daa nya ka u niŋi u seem maa, di daa dam ba sufu ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti pooti n‑ti Naaba ŋɔn tum seem maa.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Ka Naa maa daa booli u na n‑yɛli u, Nyini ninvugyɔri wa! N da kyɛ i samni zaa m‑pɛŋŋi i, nyin da susi ma zugu.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Ka dizugu di bu sumsi ni i zɔ i taraana namboo? - kaman man zɔ i namboo seem maa.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Ka Naaba maa sufu daa yisigi m‑basi doo maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑kpari dansarika duu ni halli ni u ti yɔ u samni maa.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Tɔ: ka lala ka m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni gba nin niŋi ya, ya sɔkam yi ku siri zaŋŋi u sufu n‑kyɛ u mabii taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi u.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑tɔ'asi daadam maa, nyɛsiri puuni, ni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Nasɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa sɛ'asi pɔ'adiri kyuu n‑ti u biiya.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: U daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi nti booli ŋɔn pun zuusi seeba maa, ni, ba kyɛm na. Amaa ba daa bu sakki n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Dizugu u daa laa n‑tum seeba yaasa ni ba ti yɛli ba, ni, Kyuu maa saha paaya. N maali kyuu maa yɛla zaa siri n‑naai. M pun kɔri nigi ni bunkɔbkara la, ka di zaa niŋi la siri. Ya kyɛm pɔ'adiri kyuu maa na.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Amaa ba ba'aba daa ka u ni, ka ba zaa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi ba kyɛnni. Sɔ daa kyaŋŋi la u puu, sɔ mun daa kyaŋŋi daa:.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: ka seeba mun daa gbaai u daaba maa n‑diisi ba vi, halli nti ku ba gba.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka Naa maa suuri yisigi pam, ka u daa tum u soogyadima ni ba ti ku ninvugkuuriba wa ka nyoaai ba tiŋŋa maa bugum.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Tɔ; di nyaaŋŋa ka u yi daa booli u niriba maa na nti yɛli ba, ni, Kyuu maa saha paaya, amaa man daa zuusi seeba la bu sumsi ni ba di kyuu maa.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Dinzugu ya kyɛma n‑dɔli tiŋŋa wa pala ni sɔkara nti zuusi yan nya seeba zaa, ni, ba kyɛm pɔ'adiri kyuu maa na.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Tɔ: ka daaba wa daa yi n‑dɔli sɔya maa n‑la'asi ban daa nya seeba zaa na - ninvugsuma halli ni ninvugbeeri zaa gba - halli ka duu maa ti paali.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Amaa Naa maa ŋɔn daa kpɛ, ni u ti nya ban daa la'am na la, u daa nya dɔsɔ ka u bu yɛ pɔ'adiri kyuu neema.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Di saha ka u daa boosi u, ni, N zɔa, i niŋi la wula n‑kpɛ na ka bu yɛ kyuu maa neema? Ka doo maa kur fɔ surimm ka bu yɛli u seelli.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Tɔ; ka Naaba daa yɛli u niriba maa, ni, Ya gbaama u n‑lɔ u nuusi ni u gbaya ka zaŋŋi u n‑yi m‑basi zibsim ni sa. Bɛni ka ba kur kumna, ka ŋɔbri ba nyina. .

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Niriba pam ka ba booli, amaa ban gamsi ninvugseeba la bu zoaai.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Farisiseeba daa la'am taaba n‑gbaai gbaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba nin niŋi seem n‑gbaai Yisa u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Ka ba daa tum ba nyaandɔliseeba, ni NaaHɛroodi niriba, ni ba kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Ti Karimba, ti mi halli, ni, i nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu, ka i kur wulisiri Naawunni soori ni yɛlimaŋni: halli i ba'a ka niriba yɛla ni ka bu zɔri ninsaala nini.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna yɛlim ti: i yɛli la wula - di tuya ni ba yoori lampoo n‑tiri Room NaaTitaari, bee, di bu tuusi.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba teesibeeri ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka yɛli ba, ni, Yan birikɔnsi daandima wa! Wula zugu ka ya gbiiri m bɔ'a?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Ya tim ma lampoo la'afu na ka n nya. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi la'afu n‑ti u na.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: ka u yi boosi ba, ni, Ŋɔni makkri m bɛ la'afu maa ni wa? - ni ŋɔni yuuri n sɔbi m‑bɛ ka ni.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ka ba yɛli, NaaTitaari dinni. Di saha ka Yisa laa n‑labsi n‑yɛli ba, Dizugu ya zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naaba dinni n‑ti Naaba maa, amaa ka zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naawunni dinni n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Ka ban daa wum lala maa, di daa gyɛri ba pam u zugu. Ka u daa bakki ka kyɛ ba.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Tɔ; ka lala saha maa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Ba daa yɛli u, ni, Ti Karimba, Musa daa yɛli, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka u ka biiya, ŋɔn dim u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Tɔ; ka mabiisi bayɔpɔi n daa bɛ ti tiŋŋa wa. Ka ba kpeema n daa di pɔ'a ka kpi ka bu dɔ'ai biiya, ka kyɛ u pɔ'a maa n‑ti u nyeeri la.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Ka ŋɔn pa buyi daana la daa di pɔ'a maa ka kpi ka bu dɔ'ai biiya, ni buta daana maa gba daa niŋi lala, halli nti paai buyɔpɔi daana la gba.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka pɔ'a maa gba kpi.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna Zaadaari, niriba yi ti yisigi kum ni, babayɔpɔi wa puuni ŋɔni n nin nyɛ pɔ'a maa sira, dama ba zaa daa di u ni.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya burigi soori ya ziilinsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Sɔppu maa zugu, ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn mari yiiko seem la gba.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Zaadaari kum yisigri saha ba ku ti laa n‑diri pɔ'aba bee n‑kunni dɔppa, ba ti ya n‑ŋmanni la hoo arizana malaakadima la.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban kpi kum yisigri la yɛla, ya bu karim Naawunni ŋɔn daa yɛli seem? - ni.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba. - ya bu wum lala? U siri pa la ninvugkpiimma Naawunni amaa ninvugvuya Naawunni.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa ti wum dinŋɔa, di daa gyɛri ba, u wulisigu wa zugu.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farisii dima maa ŋɔn daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka Saddusidima sini ba noori seem la, ba daa la'am n‑kyɛ u saani na.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Ka ba ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Naawunni zaaligu baŋŋna daa ti boori ni u zamsi Yisa yam, ka doaai n‑zaani m‑boosi u, ni.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: N Karimba, Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali zaaligu maa zaa puuni, kani n lee m‑mugsi ka kpa n‑gaari zaa.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ni, Zaŋŋma i sufu n‑wuri i Dugma Naawunni, ni i siiya, ni i teesa zaa.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Kan mugsi n‑kpa n‑gaari zaaligu kam zaa nla.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Kan paasi buyi daana ŋmanni la kana: ka nyɛ la, ni, Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Musa zaaligu la zaa, ni anabidima wulisigu zaa gba, bɛ la abayi wa puuni.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farisii dima maa ŋɔn daa la'asi taaba, Yisa daa boosi ba.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: ni, Bɔ ka ya ya n‑yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Masiiya maa yɛla? - Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti di naam n‑fa u niriba m‑basi. Ni, naam za'anɔri kani ka u ti ya n‑yi na? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, NaaDauda dinni.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, Ka dinŋɔna, wula zugu ka NaaDauda daa tɔ'asi Masiiya maa yɛla, n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni Siiya yiiko ni, m‑boonni u, ni, u Dugma. Dama u daa yɛli#:-.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, Ni, Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni, Halli ka n zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni. .

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Ka di yi nyɛ la dina, ka NaaDauda booli ŋɔn Masiiya maa, ni, u Dugma, ka wula ka Masiiya maa nin toaai m‑bɛ NaaDauda za'anɔri ni.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Tɔ: ka di saha sɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑labsi Yisa seela, halli sɔ daa bu laa m‑boosi u boosa yaasa, lala dabsiri maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri daadam maa, ni u nyaandɔliba gba.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: ka yɛli ba, ni, Musa zizii maa ni ka Farisii dima zintira, ban ni karimbadima maa.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Dizugu ban yɛtti ya maa zaa, ya tumma a ni, ka ya dɔli ba wulisigu la suŋŋa; amaa ba tuma muna, yan di dɔli anŋɔ, halli beela! Dama ba tɔ'asira, ka bu tumna.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba loori la zitibsa n‑ziinni niriba, ka ba sɔ mun pun ku wukki ziiri maa halli beela n‑suŋŋi ba.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Ba tumni la ba tuma zaa ni ba ti kyɛ ka niriba gɔsiri a; ba mari la sabkara m‑pitta, ni saariveela ka ba paari ka puusira.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Deema ziiya ka ba boori la ba bɛ tooni, halli la'aŋŋu duri puuni gba ba zi la sɔkam zaa tooni.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Ba boori ni sɔkam zaa puusiri ba daasi puuni, ka gyeemni ba m‑boonni ba, ni Rabbi! , ni, N Karimba.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Yarim muna - yan di kyɛ ka ba boonni ya, ni, Rabbi . Karimba yinni m bɛ ni n‑ti ya zaa, ka ya mun zaa bɛ ni hoo mabiisi n‑ti taaba.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Di laa m‑boonni sɔ ya Ba, duniya wa puuni: ya Ba yinni m bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka ŋɔn mun bɛ la saazugu.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Yan di laa n‑kyɛ ka ba boonni ya ba toondaandima: ya toondaana bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka ŋɔn mun n nyɛ Masiiya maa.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Yarim saani, ya kyɛ ka ya ninvugtibsiri leebi sɔkam daabri.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusi u maŋŋa, ba nin sigsi u; amaa ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa muna, ba nin duusi lala nira maa pam.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya kpari la bamsim bamsigu za'anɔri, ka niriba ku toaai n‑kpɛa. Yarima mammansi ku sakki n‑kpɛa, ka laa n‑ta'ari ban kpeeri la soori.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya kyɛnni n‑gyinni teeku ni tingbaŋŋu ziisi zaa ni ya ti nya ya nyaandɔla yinni; yan mun yi ti nya sɔa, ya yi kyɛ ka u leebi Sintaani dinni n‑gaari ya mammansi ŋɔn nyɛ seem napɔmbuyi.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ la yarima zugu, yan wulisiri niriba soori ka ya mun nyɛ zoomma! Dama ya yɛtti, ni, ninvugsɔ yi ti pɔ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri, di pa seelli; amaa ŋɔn mun yi pɔ Yiri maa salima maa - ɔhoo! di nin dɔli u!.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Yan gyɛra wa! Ni yan zoomma wa! Abayi wa puuni, kani n tibsa n‑gaari? - Salima la n gaari? - bee, Gyeeŋŋu Yiri kan kyɛ ka salima maa leebi Naawunni dinni kasi?.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ni, ninvugsɔ yi ti pɔ sara maaligu bimbinni la, di pa seela, amaa ŋɔn mun yi pɔ piiniseaa kan pa bimbinni maa zugu - ɔhoo! di nin dɔli u!.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Yan zoomma wa! Abayi wa puuni, kani n tibsa n‑gaari? - Piini la gaari? - bee, maaligu bimbinni kan kyɛ ka piini maa leebi Naawunni dinni kasi?.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Dinŋɔna ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pɔ maaligu bimbinni maa, bimbinni maa ka u pɔa, ni bunseesi zaa an pa ka zugu gba.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pɔ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa, Yiri maa ka u pɔa, ni Ŋɔn su Yiri maa m‑bɛ ni gba.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Ka ŋɔn mun pɔ saazugu, Naawunni naam ziziiya ka u pɔa, ni Ŋɔn zi bɛni gba.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya yiisiri la naanzuuwa ni kpɛligu ni zinzam zaa zakka, amaa ka lee n‑kyɛ an mugsi n‑kpa n‑gaari lala maa m‑basira - di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni tumpu, ni nambɔzoobu, ni yɛdda niŋbu. Ya yi ti teaari anŋɔ ka bu laa m‑pɔsigi anla, di naa n‑sɔa.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ la yarima zugu, yan wulisiri niriba soori ka ya mun n nyɛ zoomma! Ya teeri n‑yiisiri saliga ya bunnyuura ni, ka mun nyuuri bun mari arakoomi n‑vɔnna.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Kɔbeesi ni dugri nyaansi ka ya peaara, amaa ka a puuni na paali ni da'ari ni ta'ayigsi tuma.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: N zɔ Farisidoowa, nyin zooma wa! See ka i daŋŋi m‑peaai kɔbeaa maa puuni pɔi, ka ka nyaaŋŋa gba nin naayi peaai kasi.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya ŋmanni la hoo gbala ka ba zaŋŋi tampeelli n‑ta a ka a veela, amaa a puuni na paali ninvugkpiimma kɔba ni da'aseesi an kyisiri zaa.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Lala ka yarima siri ŋmanni la yɛlimaŋni dima, niriba nini ni, amaa ya sufu puuni ka ya na paali birikɔnsi ni tuppuwumni.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, ka kur kyɛ ka ninvugsuma dabɔya veela.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni, Tirim yi daa bɛ ni ti badima saha, ti daa bu naa n‑zɛ ba nyaaŋŋa n‑ku anabidima wa.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Lala zugu ka ya wulisiri ya mansi halibeoo, ni, ya badima daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban daa ku anabidima maa.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Halli, ya badima maa ŋɔn daa pɔsigi tumbɛseesi la, yarim mun tumni a n‑tiri ba.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Yan bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmanni la hoo wa'abeeri! Ya ya niŋi la wula n‑zɔ n‑yi Bugum ni namsigu la ni?.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu n nin tum anabidima ya saani na, ni baŋŋniba, ni karimbadima; ka ya nin ku baseeba, ka kpaasi baseeba n‑tabli dɔpula zugu ni ba ti kpi, baseeba muna ka ya nin feebi ya la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka kari ba nti yi tinseaa n‑gaari tinseaa.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Dizugu ya nin ti zi taalli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban daa ku yɛlimaŋni dima zaa zugu, duniya wa piiligu ni la sa, m‑piili Eebli kuubu, ŋɔn daa nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana, halli nti paai Zakariiya, Barakaaya bii la, ban daa ku sɔ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, sara maaligu zii maa ni Dutitaari la sunsukkuni.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba kum taalli bɛ ya zuna niriba wa zugu ni.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Ka u daa gbaai kunkuma Gyɛrusalɛm zugu, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm, Gyɛrusalɛm! Waay, waay, waay! Nyin ŋɔn kuuri anabidima ka zaŋŋni tuumuseeba ban daa tum i saani maa n‑lɔbri ba kuga wa! N da kur boori la n la'asi i niriba luginyinni kaman nɔnyaaŋŋa ŋɔn la'asi u biisi ka yirigri u kpunkpama n‑ligri ba seem la; amaa ya bu sakki n‑ti ma.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Ya gɔsima! Ya Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa yi kpalim la daboo.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Dinzugu ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, halli ka ya ti yɛli Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na! .

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Dirindaari maa ka Arizana naam yɛla nin ti ŋmanni nŋɔa#:- Pɔ'asara piiya n daa bɛ amriya kyuu tuma ni; ba zaa daa zaŋŋi ba firiladima n‑guusiri amriya doo maa yiri, ni ba ti yi na n‑tuusi doo maa soori, u yi ti taasi pɔ'apaalli maa n‑kunni na.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Tɔ; ka babanu maa daa mari yam, babanu ban kpalim la mun daa gyɛrigiya ni.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Ban gyɛrigi la daa mari ba firiladima maa, amaa ba daa bu niŋi kpaam ga'a ni m‑mari m‑paasi.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Yam daandima maa mun daa mari kpaam ba ga'a ni m‑paasi.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Tɔ, ka amriya doo maa daa yuui paari na, dizugu daa kyɛ ka goom mari pɔ'asara wa zaa ka ba ti gbisi.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Ka yuntasuusi ka ba daa wum niriba damni, ka ba kaasiri n‑yɛtti, ni, Doo maa paai na: ya doaai n‑yi na nti tuusi u.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Di saha ka pɔ'asara la daa doaai m‑maali m‑maali ba firiladima maa.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Ka ban daa kɔŋŋi yam la daa ti yɛli ba taaba, ni, Ya ku ti ti kpaam ka ti niŋi ti firiladima maa ni? - Dama ti dina n kpiira.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Ka yam dima maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ay! Mma! - ti ku tisi. Ti gba kpaam ku paai ti. Ya mammansi kyɛma ban koosiri bu saani nti da na.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Dinzugu ka gyɛra maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi ni ba ti da kpaam na; amaa ban daa kyɛnni maa, amriya doo dima maa daa paai na. Di saha ka pɔ'asariseeba ban daa maali siri la daa dɔli n‑kpɛ pɔ'adiri kyuu duu maa ni. Ka ba daa yɔ dunoori maa n‑kpari.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Tɔ: ka di nyaaŋŋa ka pɔ'asaribanla daa paai na, nti yɛli, Ti Bapira, ti Bapira! I ku kyɛ ka ti kpɛ na.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Ka ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Mma! Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, ni, man bu mi ya. .

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Tɔ! Ka dizugu yan di kyɛ ka goom mari ya! Dama ya bu mi dabsiri maa paari na saha deede.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Di ŋmanni la nŋɔ : dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u boori ni u kyaŋŋi soori, ka u daa booli u toori daaba u saani na n‑zaŋŋi u marigu n‑niŋi ba nuusi ni.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: U daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ba, sɔkam hoo ŋɔn nin toaai m‑mari yam seem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi a gɔsibu. U daa zaŋŋi la'aboorisi anu n‑ti sɔa, ka sɔ mun daa deaai abayi, sɔ yaasa daa deaai la'abooriga yinni. Ka doo maa naayi gɔ u goorim. Di saha.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: ka ŋɔn daa deaai la'aboorisi anu la daa zaŋŋi a n‑walim, ka daa nya abanu m‑paasi a zugu.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: La'aboorisi ayi daana la gba daa tum lala nti nya la'aboorisi ayi m‑paasi.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa deaai la'abooriga yinni la daa mari u ligri maa n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gbi bɔ'ari m‑pi u daana maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi seelli n‑ti u maa.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka daaba wa dugma la daa kuli na, ka yɛli, ni, ba niŋma ligri maa laasaabu ka u nya.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa deaai la'aboorisi anu la daa kyɛ na m‑mari a na, ni abanu yaasa m‑paasi a zugu, ka yɛli, ni, M Ba, I daa bu zaŋŋi la la'aboorisi anu n‑ti ma? Tɔ - nnya abanu m‑paasi a zugu: man nya nyoori seelli m‑paasi nla.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ka u dugma daa yɛli u, ni, Mboo! Daabsuŋŋu ni yɛdda daana n nyɛ i! Nyin ŋɔn su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi n‑zɛ yimmu maa, n nin zaŋŋi bunkara fukumsi n‑ti i ka i mara. Dizugu kyɛm na ti di n nya'asim maa.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Ka la'aboorisi ayi daana la gba daa kyɛ na nti yɛli, M Ba, i daa bu zaŋŋi la la'aboorisi ayi n‑ti ma? Tɔ - nnya abayi m‑paasi a zugu: man nya nyoori seelli m‑paasi nla.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ka u dugma daa yɛli u, ni, Mboo! Daabsuŋŋu ni yɛdda daana n nyɛ i gba! Nyin ŋɔn su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi n‑zɛ yimmu maa, n nin zaŋŋi bunkara fukumsi n‑ti i ka i mara. Dizugu kyɛm na ti di n nya'asim maa.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa deaai la'abooriga yinni la naayi kyɛ na nti yɛli, M Ba, n daa mi halli, ni, putoo daana n kur nyɛ i, ni, i boori ni i nya bunseelli kam zaa yɔri, ka kyeeri nyin daa bu buri seelli.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Ka dabeem daa mari ma dizugu, ka n daa kur zaŋŋi i ligri maa n‑sɔ'ai; dinzugu deaam i ligri.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Ka u dugma daa yɛli u, Nyin bibeoo wa! Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa! Ni, i daa mi, ni, mani n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn boori buna yɔri, ka kyeeri man bu buri seelli, fa?.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: Tɔ! Ka bɔ n daa lee n‑niŋi i ka i daa bu zaŋŋi n ligri maa n‑dooli bankyi dima maa nuu ni, ka man da kuli na wa, n naa n‑kur nya nyoori m‑paasi n la'adaya maa zugu.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: - Ka daa yɛli u niriba maa, ni, Ya deaam ligri maa u saani n‑zaŋŋi m‑paasi sɔ ŋɔn mari la'aboorisi piiya maa.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Sɔkam zaa ŋɔn mari seelli, ba nin zaŋŋi n‑ti u ka u nya m‑paasi halli nti zoaai, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli maa ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa ka ba nin ti fa ka kyɛ u yɔri.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ni, Ka bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa muna - ya gbaama u ka zaŋŋi u n‑yi m‑basi zibsim ni sa. Bɛni ka ba kur kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina. .

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Ni, Dama Ninsaala Bii ŋɔn ya zaŋŋi u gyirima neesim n‑kyɛ na sahanseaa la, ka malaakadima zaa ŋɔn nin la'am n‑dɔli u, di saha u nin zini u naam ziziveelli zugu.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Ka ba nin la'asi duniya wa niriba zaa u saani na. Ŋɔn mun ti ya wɔligi ba taaba, hoo bunkɔbgura ŋɔn wɔligri u bunkɔbri seem, nti kyɛ ka peesi m bɛ kpa, buusi mun bɛ la kpa.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Ka u nin kyɛ ka peesi gaari nti zɛ u nudirigu ziiya, buusi mun zɛ la u nuzaa ziiya.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Di saha maa ka Naa maa nin ti tɔ'asi n‑ti baseeba ban zɛ u nudirigu zii la n‑yɛli, ni, Ya kyɛm na, yan zugsuŋŋu daandima wa, m Ba saani! Ya ti deaai naam maa, dama ba daa pun maali ka siri ni ka ti nyɛ ya dinni, halli duniya wa piiligu saha.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Dama kɔm ŋɔn daa mari ma sahanseaa la, ya daa zaŋŋi diibu n‑diisi ma; kɔnyuuri gba ŋɔn daa mari ma, ya daa zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ma ni n nyu: n daa yi tinseaa ni n‑goora, ka ya daa deaai ma hoo ya saana n‑kpeesi ya yiya ni.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Halli n daa ka kparigu ka kyɛnni yɔri, ka ya daa yeeli ma kyinkyina; ban mun daa gbaai ma n‑kpari dansarika duu, ka ya daa kaai ma bɛni.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Ka ninvugsuma wa nin labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ti Dugma, bɔn daari ka ti daa nya i ka kɔm mari i ka ti suŋŋi i? Bee, kɔnyuuri m mari i ka ti ti i koom ni i nyu?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Bɔn daari ka ti daa nya i ka i goora ka daa deaai i, bee, i daa kyɛnni yɔri ka ti yeeli i kparigu?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Bee, bɔn daari ka ti daa nya i ka i ka alaafɛa, bee, n‑kpari la duu, ka ti daa kyɛ i saani na n‑suŋŋi i di zugu.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Di saha ka Naaba nin labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan daa niŋi lala n‑ti m mabiisi wa yinni, halli ŋɔn poora zaa, mani ka ya daa suŋŋni maa.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Tɔ: ka di saha maa u nin ti tɔ'asi n‑ti baseeba ban zɛ u nuzaa zii maa, ni, Ya kyɛma sa! Zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu, ya kyɛm Bugum bun bu kpiira ziiya, dama ba maali bu siri n‑ti Sintaani ni u tuntumniba zaa.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Dama kɔm ŋɔn daa mari ma sahanseaa la, ya daa bu zaŋŋi diibu n‑diisi ma; kɔnyuuri gba ŋɔn daa mari ma, ya daa bu zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ma ni n nyu:.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: n daa yi tinseaa ni n‑goora, ka ya daa bu deaai ma hoo ya saana n‑kpeesi ya yiya ni; n daa kyɛnni yɔri, ka ya daa bu ti ma kparigu; n daa ka alaafɛa, ka ba daa kpari ma dansarika, ka ya daa bu suŋŋi ma.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ka niriba wa gba nin labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ti Dugma, bɔn daari ka ti daa nya i ka kɔm bee kɔnyuuri m mari i, bee, i daa ka beebu ziiya, bee kparigu, bee n‑kɔŋŋi alaafɛa, bee n‑kpari duu, ka ti daa bu suŋŋi i.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Di saha ka u nin labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan daa bu niŋi lala n‑ti banŋɔ yinni, halli ŋɔn poora zaa, mani ka ya daa mɔŋŋi suŋŋsim maa.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Ka lala niriba nin ti kyaŋŋi namsigu din ka naari ziiya, ka banŋɔ muna, ninvugsuma la, ba nin paai nyɔvuri kan ka naari ziiya.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti kaai, gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni tinkpamma maa zaa daa la'asi n‑gbaai gbaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nin ku Yisa seem.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ka ba daa ba Yisa bansi n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑niŋi Gɔmna Pailiti nuu ni.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Gyuudasi muna, sɔ ŋɔn daa yi Yisa nyaaŋŋa la, ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Yisa galinsi maa sum la ni kuubu, u sufu daa sa'amiya; ka u daa ya lugi anzulifa pisita la ni u ti ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni kpambaya la.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Nti yɛli ba, ni, Man galimiya! Dɔsɔ ŋɔn bu tum taalli ka n zaŋŋi n‑figsi n‑ti ya, ka ba ti yɛ n‑ku u. Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ka ti ba'a bɔ m bɛ ni? Ay - nyin yɛttɔ'a maa n kur nyɛ di.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Di saha ka Gyuudasi daa zaŋŋi anzulifa ligri maa n‑lɔbi m‑basi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, ka daa bakki. Ka di nyaaŋŋa u daa kyaŋŋi nti yuli u maŋŋa n‑kpi.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa zaŋŋi ligri maa ka yɛli, Ligri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi sɔ kum yɛla nŋɔa: ti mun kaya nin ta'ai, ni, di bu sumsi ni ziim ligri wa taabu ti paasi ti Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa ligri ni.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ka ba zaa daa la'asi n‑gbaai gbaari, ni, ba zaŋŋma ligri maa n‑da paaluseaa dugmeeriba la ŋɔn daa su la, ni ba ti mari n‑sɔ'ari tinzundima bɛni.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Lala zugu daa kyɛ ka ba boonni bɛni, ni, Ziim Paalu, halli ni zuna.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Di saha di siri daa niŋi hoo Anabi Gyɛrimaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛliseelli la, ni#:- Ba nin zaŋŋi anzulifa pisita la, Izraɛli dima maa ŋɔn daa sakki ni ba yɔ seesi la, Ni, ba baŋŋi, ni, u ligri ŋɔn nyɛ seem nla.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: Ni#:- Ba zaŋŋi a n‑da dugmeeriba paalu. Ti Dugma ŋɔn daa yɛli ma seem la nla.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa daa zaani Gɔmna maa tooni, ka u daa boosi u, ni, Nyini n nyɛ Izraɛli dima Naaba la?, ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem maa n nyɛ di.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpambaya maa ŋɔn daa galimni u la, u daa bu yɛli seelli.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Di saha ka Pailiti daa yɛli u, ni, I bu wumni ban galimni i seem wa.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Ka Yisa daa bu gyɛrigi, halli yɛlli yinni gba, ka di daa gyɛri Gɔmna maa pam.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Tɔ, ka Kyuu saha kaari soori n daa nyɛ di, ni, niriba daa yi wulisi dansarika niriba ninvugsɔ ban boora, ka Gɔmna maa ti yiisi di daama n‑kyɛ u ka u kyaŋŋi.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Tɔ: lala saha maa ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ sarika maa ni, ka u yuuri boonni Barabasi, ka sɔkam zaa daa mi u yɛla.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Ka daadam ŋɔn daa ti la'am na, Pailiti daa boosi ba, ni, Ya boori ni n zaŋŋi Barabasi m‑basi ya, bee, Yisa ban boonni sɔ Masiiya, Naaba, la.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: - dama u daa mi halli, ni, nyuuri zugu ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti u la.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Tɔ: ka Pailiti daa na kur zi la sariya karibu zɔŋŋu maa ni, ka u pɔ'a daa tum u saani na nti ti u lahabaari, ni, Di niŋi yɛlimaŋni daana wa seelli! Dama n zaasa puuni, yuŋŋu la, ka n sa namsiya pam u yɛttɔ'a zugu.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni kpamma maa daa kur tɔ'asiri n‑tiri zama maa n‑kpaasiri ba zugri, ka naayi kyɛ ka ba sakki ni ba ti susi Gɔmna maa, ni, u yiisima Barabasi n‑ti ba na, ka kyɛ ka Yisa baasi yɔri.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Dizugu Gɔmna maa ŋɔn daa boosi ba, ni, Niriba ayi wa puuni, ya yiisima yan boori sɔa!, ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Barabasi ka ti boora.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Ka Pailiti daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ka Yisa, ban boonni sɔ Masiiya Naaba la, muna? - Ya boori ni n niŋi u la wula? Ka ba zaa yɛli, Kyɛ ka ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ŋɔn mun daa boosi ba, ni, Ka taalli bɔ ka u daa tum? Ka ba daa maali n‑tasi pam n‑yɛtti, ni, Ba kpaasim u.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Ka Pailiti ŋɔn daa ti nya, ni, u yɛttɔ'a sɛseelli ku toaai m‑maali yɛlli maa, halli u tuma maa zaa yɔri ka niriba maa maali n‑vuuri nti yakki, u daa zaŋŋi koom m‑peaai u nuusi daadam maa tooni. Ni, Man nuu ka doo wa kuubu ni! Ya tuma nla.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Di saha ka zama maa zaa daa la'am n‑yɛli, Tɔ! Kyɛ ka u ziim taalli kpalim n‑nyɛ tirima dinni, ni ti biisi la dinni gba.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Di saha ka u daa yiisi Barabasi m‑basi n‑ti ba, ka daa feebi Yisa muna, ka zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ni ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Gɔmna soogyadima maa daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑kpɛ Baarikyi maa ni, ka booli ba taaba ni ba la'am na.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Ba daa yeaai u neema maa ka daa zaŋŋi naam kparizeoo n‑yeeli u.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ka daa zaŋŋi goosi m‑paam m‑pili u zugu hoo naam zupiliga, ka zaŋŋi kika'ari n‑gbibli u nudirigu hoo naam damni; ka daa gbaantiri u tooni m‑maanni u ansarisi, ni, Ndana, m Ba Izraɛli Naaba.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ba daa laa n‑tusi nantoori n‑niŋi u, ka zaŋŋi u kika'ari la n‑kpaasiri u zugu ni.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Ban daa deem u lala n‑naai ba daa yeaai ba kparigu maa ka labsi u maŋtoori neema n‑yeeli u. Ka daa mari u n‑yi ni ba kyaŋŋi nti kpaasi u.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ka ban daa ti kyɛnni maa, ba daa nya Sairiini tingbaŋŋu dɔsɔ, ka u yuuri boonni Saimoon. Ŋɔna ka ba daa gbaai n‑kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi Yisa dɔpulli daari maa n‑ziiri n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Dinŋɔ n nyɛ lahabasuŋŋu maa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa Kirisita Masiiya, Naawunni Bii maa. Di piili la kpa.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti paai luginseaa kan yuuri boonni Gɔligɔtta , ka di gbinni nyɛ la Zuguŋmaaŋŋu Zii la.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Dinŋɔna Naawunni daa pun kyɛ ka Anabi Aizaaya sɔbi, ni: Nnya n tuumu maa nŋɔ, N tumni u ni u ti daŋŋi i tooni: Ŋɔna nin maali soori maa n‑ti i.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: ba daa zaŋŋi daam bun gyɛri ni titoori, ni, u nyuuma! Amaa ŋɔn daa lam bu, u daa bu sakki n‑nyu bu.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: ni, Ya wumma! Sɔ m bɛ moo ni n‑tasira, Ni, Ya maalim ti Dugma palli n‑ti u, Ya sɛ'asim u sɔya zaa ka a tuusi kyippi! .

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa. Di saha ka ba daa puri u sutura n‑tɔri taaba, n‑toori tɛtɛ ni ba ti nya a puribu soori.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Tɔ; ka dizugu dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri Gyɔni - ŋɔn daa suuri niriba Naawunni koom la n‑daa la. U daa bɛ la moo ni m‑moonna n‑yɛtti, ni, Ya kyɛli ya tumbeeri m‑basi, ka lala zugu ya kyɛm na ka n su ya Naawunni koom, ka ŋɔn Naawunni nin kyɛ ya taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ya.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa zinni bɛni ka gɔsiri u ni.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Ka daadam daa la'am n‑yi u saani na pam; ba daa yi la Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni na, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa m‑paasi. Ka ba daa paai u na ka wulisiri ban tum tumbɛseesi maa, ka u daa su ba tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi kɔsuubu, Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la ni.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Ka ba daa sɔbi bunni n‑galimni u, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑tabli dɔpulli maa saazugu, ni, Yisa, Yɛhudiyadima Naaba la, nŋɔa. .

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Ŋɔn Gyɔni mun daa yɛ la arakoomi kɔbri kparigu, ka daa mari gbaŋŋu n‑loori u seaa, ka u diibu daa kur nyɛ la toori, ni siiri.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Di saha maa ka ba daa kpaasi na'ayigsi ayi gba: ka daa sa ba yinni u nudirigu ya'ari, ni ŋɔnla u nuzaa ya'ari.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: Tɔ; ka Gyɔni daa moonni, ni, Sɔ nin kyɛ n nyaaŋŋa na n‑kpeem n‑gaari ma, halli ka mani bu sumsi ni n kuntiri m‑pirigi u namna n‑tiri u.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Niriba ban daa gatti daa lɔbri ba zugu ka tuuri Yisa.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ya nyama! - koom ka mani zaŋŋni n‑suuri ya wa, amaa ŋɔn mun nin zaŋŋi Sii Kasi Vuusim n‑suuri ya.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: ni, Ɔhɔ! Nyini n daa yɛnni, ni, i nin daai Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la n‑lusi, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi daba ata m‑mɛ ka la yaasa! Tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ, i yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, nyin sigma dɔpulli maa zugu na n‑fa i maŋŋa m‑basi.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Di daa niŋiya ka Yisa daa yi Nazarɛti, tinseaa kan bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni la, ka daa kyɛ na nti kpɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari maa ni n‑su koom Gyɔni nuu ni.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Lala ka gyeeŋŋu kpamma, ni karimbadima ni tinkpamma maa gba, daa laari Yisa ka yɛtti taaba.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti yi koom maa ni na, u daa nya ka sangbana n bɔkki ka Sii maa daa sigri u zugu na. U daa gɔsi ka Sii maa gɔsibu daa ŋmani hoo naŋmanni ŋɔn bɛ seem la.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: ni, U daa faari la niriba m‑basira, amaa ka bu toaai n‑fa u maŋŋa m‑basi! Ni, Izraɛli Naaba n nyɛ u, fa! - tɔ, kyɛ ka u yi dɔpulli maa zugu n‑sigi na ka ti niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu ni na, ni, M bii n nyɛ i, ka n wuri i ni pam. N nini n tigi i.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: Ka ba daa laa n‑yɛtti, ni, U niŋi la Naawunni yɛdda, ka yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la Naawunni biiya: tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ŋɔn Naawunni yi siri boori u, ŋɔn faam u m‑basi ka ti nya.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Di saha ka Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi daa zaŋŋi u n‑taasi yoo ni.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Ka na'ayigsi ayi seeba ban daa kpaasi n‑sa Yisa ya'a ayi maa gba daa tuuri u.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: Ka u daa bɛ bɛni dapisinaasi, ka Sintaani daa zamsiri u. U daa kpalim la bɛni, ka mooni bunkɔbri kɔkɔ n daa bɛ u saani, ka malaakadima daa guri u.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Ka agbaa saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, tingbaŋŋu maa zaa daa sɔbgi zimm, ka zibsim daa ligi ya'ari kam zaa n‑yuui m‑paai zaanoori hoo kurigu ata saha.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Ka Naaba ŋɔn daa gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari sahanseaa la, di saha ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Di saha maa Yisa daa kaasiya pam, ni, iIlayi, Ilayi, lima sabakyitani? - di gbinni n nyɛ N Naawunni, n Naawunni, wula ka i kyɛ ma m‑basi.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: Ni, Saha maa paaya; Naawunni naam la bɛ la ya saani. Ya takkma ya beebu, ni ya teesibeeri, ka niŋni lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛdda.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Niriba ban daa zɛ bɛni maa seeba daa ti wum u yɛttɔ'a maa ka yɛli, ni, Ya wumma! U boonni la Ilaygya.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Galilii mɔ'ari duuri la nti nya Saimoon ni u sunzɔ Anduru, ka ba daa lɔbri laaŋŋu mɔ'ari maa ni, dama ba daa tumni la zingbaariba tuma.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Di saha ka ba ninvugyinni daa kyaŋŋi nti bɔ sɔ'asu n‑zaŋŋi n‑niŋi damiisiri ni, ka mari na n‑zaŋŋi n‑kusi kika'ari zugu n‑duusi m‑paai Yisa ni u ti nyu.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya dɔli ma na! N nin kyɛ ka ya leebi ninsaaligbaariba.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Ka banla daa yɛli, ni, Kyɛla! Kyɛ ka ti nya Ilaygya nin kyɛ na n‑fa u m‑basi, bee.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Yoomyooma ka ba daa basi ba laama maa ka dɔli u.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑tasi pampam ka naayi ti ka ni.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: Ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi tooni beela maa, u daa nya Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii biiya, ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni, ka ba daa bɛ nyariŋŋu ni m‑maanni ba laama.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Ka pateesa kan daa ya'ai Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Dutitaari puuni maa daa toaai buyi, ka saazugu zaŋŋi n‑sigri tiŋŋa ni; ka tingbaŋŋu maa daa damni ka tampiisi bɔkki.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Ka u daa booli ba, ni, ba dɔli u na, ka ba daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa, ka bakki ka kyɛ ba ba Zɛbidii, ni u tuntumniba, ka ba daa kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Ka gbala daa yaamna, ka Naawunni niriba kpiimma pam daa doaai na.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Kapirinyum. M‑paaya, ka Vuusim dabsa maa ni ka Yisa daa la'amni ni Kapirinyum dima n‑kpeeri wumpuusigu la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti wulisiri ba.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yisigi kum ni n‑naai, lala kpiimma pam daa yi gbala la ni na n‑kpɛ Naawunni tiŋŋa la timpuuni, ka niriba pam daa nya ba.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Di daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'amaŋni zugu, ka u bu birisira halli beela; ni, di pa la kaman zaaligu baŋŋniba la ŋɔn yi wulisiri seem la.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Soogyadima toondaana maa ni u niriba daa guri Yisa; ka ban daa nya tingbaŋŋu ŋɔn daa damni seem maa, dabeem daa gbaai ba pam, ka ba daa yɛli, ni, Doo wa siri daa nyɛ la Wumbiiya.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Di saha maa ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni, ka arizimbeoo daa kur bɛ u ni; di daama daa kaasi.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Ka pɔ'aseeba daa bɛ kpaŋŋa ka gɔsiri na - pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli u n‑yi Galilii na ni ba ti suŋŋni u la n daa nyɛ ba.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: ka yɛli, Yisa nyin Nazarɛti doo wa! Wula ka i ya mukki ti? I kyɛnni na ni i ti sa'am ti, bee? M mi nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa: ni, nyin nyɛ la ŋɔn nyɛ bunkasikasi ka yi Naawunni saani na.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Mariama ŋɔn daa yi Magdala na n‑daa la, ni Mariama sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Gyeemsi bila ni Gyoosɛfi ma, ni Zɛbidii pɔ'a gba.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Amaa Yisa daa nyikki u, ka tasi u n‑yɛli, ni, Sina i vuuri ka yi na ka kyɛ u.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Tɔ. Bundaansɔ n daa bɛ ni ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni la Gyoosɛfi: Arimatiiya tiŋŋa nira n daa nyɛ u, ka u daa dɔli la Yisa. Di saha, zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti maai, ŋɔn Gyoosɛfi daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai Gɔmna Pailiti na nti susi u soori ni u kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu nti sɔ'ai. Ka Pailiti daa yɛli, ni, tɔ, ba zaŋŋma n‑ti u.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Ka arizimbeoo maa daa dam doo maa n‑lusi kyirigiligi, ka kaasi pam m‑bakki ka kyɛ doo maa.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Ka Gyoosɛfi daa deaai ningbiŋŋu maa ka zaŋŋi kasinveelli n‑vili.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Ka di daa gyɛri ba zaasa, ka ba yi yɛtti taaba, Bɔ nŋɔa? Wulisigu paalli nŋɔa! U tɔ'asiri la ni yiiko, halli u yɛtti arizimbeeri gba ka ba sakkri u noori.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: Ka zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi gbalipaali ban kyiibi seaa tampii ni, ka daa bilim kugtitaari n‑ligi ka noori ka naayi bakki.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Ka lahabaari wa daa gyiligi tiŋŋa maa yoomyooma, ka u yɛttɔ'a n daa bɛ Galilii tinsi zaa ni.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, ni u tɔ'ama, daa zintiri n‑tuusiri tampifeoo gballi la dunoori ni.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Ŋɔn daa yi la'aŋŋu duu maa, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑ti kpɛ Saimoon ni Anduru yiri ni, ŋɔna ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: N‑neaai beoo - Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa n‑daa la - ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima daa la'am Gɔmna Pailiti saani na, ban ni Farisidima maa m‑paasi.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Di saha ka waari n daa mari Saimoon deempɔ'a ka u ningbina daa bii, ka u dɔya. Ka ba daa yɛli Yisa u yɛla.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Nti yɛli u, ni, Ti wumiya, ziridaana wa ŋɔn daa na bɛ ni maa, ni, u yɛtti, ni, u nin niŋi dabsa ata pɔi ka naayi doaai.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Ka u gaari tooni n‑gbaai pɔ'a maa nuu n‑suŋŋi u ka u doaai n‑zaani, ka u dooru daa basi u, ka u daa yi na n‑tum n‑ti ba.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna kyɛ ka soogyadima kyaŋŋi nti guri u tampifeoo gballi maa n‑niŋi dabsa ata la. Dama di pa seem u nyaandɔliba nin zu u n‑yiisi na, ka yɛli niriba, ni, u doaai la kum ni. Di yi bɛ lala, di naari nin niŋi yɔmsigu n‑gaari daŋŋni dinni ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Di daari zaanɔyuŋŋu, wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti luri maa, ba daa mari seeba ban beera, ni arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa bɛ seeba zugri la, n‑kyɛ u saani na;.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Ka Pailiti daa yɛli ba, Ya mari la ya maŋtoori tɔbbiisi; tɔ - kur kyɛma n‑niŋi yan nin nyaŋŋi seem n‑guri ka suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: ka tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa daa la'asi na nti mugi yiri maa za'anɔri.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti guri gballi maa, ka daa zaŋŋi bunni n‑tabli kugbiliŋŋu maa, ka laa n‑zaali tɔbbiisi ni ba gura.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Ka u daa tibi ba dooribeeri maa zaa ka kari arizimbeeri pam n‑yiisi niriba saani. U daa bu sakki ni arizimbeeri maa tɔ'asira, dama ba daa mi ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
