local: Ka din daa kaai beoo asuba pinipini, u daa yisigi n‑yi. U daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ moo ni nti bɛ u kɔkɔ m‑puusiri Naawunni.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Amaa Saimoon ni u taaba maa daa bɔ m‑bɔ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: nti nya u, n‑yɛli u, ni, Ba zaasa boori la i ziiya.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Ka ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya kyɛ ka ti gaari n‑kyaŋŋi tingbaŋŋu wa tinkpanseesi ni, bɛni gba ka n ya mooli lahabaari maa: dama dinŋɔ zugu ka n yi na.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Lala ka u daa yi n‑gyiligi Galilii tinsi zaa n‑yiisiri wa'azu ba la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka katti arizimbeeri daadam saani.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tɔ: ka kɔnsɔ daa ti kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni n‑susiri u; u daa yɛli, ni, I yi da sakki, i naa n‑kyɛ ka n kɔmni wa naai ka n kpɛ n‑taabi ni.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Ka Yisa daa zɔ u namboo ka daa teaai u nuu n‑siisi u, ka yɛli, N sakkiya, di naama.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Ka di saha maa ka u kɔmni maa daa basi u ka u daa niŋi kasi.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Ka Yisa daa kpaamni u ni nimmoaa n‑kyeoosi u:.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: ka yɛli, ni, Di yɛli seelli n‑ti sɔ zaa. Kur kyɛm nti zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑wulisi maalimaanna ka maali sariseesi Musa ŋɔn daa sɔbi n‑zaali zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kɔmni tippu zii la; dina n nin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, i siri nya tippu.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Amaa doo maa daa yi n‑kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa wum lahabaari maa, halli ka di ti niŋi ka Yisa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa kam puuni ka ba nya u, amaa u daa kpalim m‑bɛ la moo ni. Amaa di zaa yɔri, niriba daa yiiri la lugri kam n‑kyɛnni u saani na.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni m‑pɔtti Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa maa, u daa paai tinkpanseaa ban boonni Bɛtfeegyi la, kan zɛ Ɔlivii zoori kpaŋŋa. Di saha ka Yisa daa daŋŋi n‑tum u nyaandɔliba ayi ni ba gaari tooni.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: ni, Ya kyɛm ti tooni nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa kan zɛ ya tooni sa. Bɛni ya nin ti nya bunnyaaŋŋa n‑lɔ n‑zɛya, ni u bumbila u saani; ya lɔrigma ba m‑mari na.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Ka sɔ yi ya boosi ya seelli, yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori ba ni, ka mun nin labsi ba na yooma.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Dinŋɔ daa niŋiya, ni anabi maa yɛlisim niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Ya tɔ'asima n‑ti Zayɔn tiŋŋa wa, Hoo yan nin tɔ'asi n‑ti pɔ'asariga, Ni, Nyama, i Naaba n kyɛnni i saani na wa! U sigsi u maŋŋa baalimm Ka baari buŋŋa, Halli bumbila ka u baara. .

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Tɔ; ka nyaandɔliba ayi maa daa kyaŋŋi nti tum Yisa ŋɔn daa wulisi ba seem la.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Ban daa mari buŋŋa ni u bii maa na, ba daa zaŋŋi ba kyinkyina m‑pa bunsi maa nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa m‑ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Daadam ban daa dɔli u maa seeba daa zaŋŋi ba kyinkyina n‑yɛligri soori maa ni, ka seeba mun daa kabi n‑kabi tiisi wula, n‑daa dooli n‑dooli soori maa ni.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ tooni, ni seeba gba ban daa bɛ nyaaŋŋa n‑dɔli maa daa piili n‑kpalimna n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Hoosana! Tiliginsim daana! NaaDauda Biiya! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na! Tiliginsim daana gyirima bɛ la zugsaa.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni la, tiŋŋa maa zaa daa damni n‑vuura, ka ba boosiri taaba, ni, Ŋɔni n‑lee n‑nyɛ u.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ka ban daa dɔli Yisa la daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Anabi Yisa nla: u yi la Nazarɛti na, Galilii tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ka Yisa daa paai Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa nti kpɛ bɛni n‑katti ban daa koosiri ka daari neema Yiri maa puuni la n‑yiisiri ba. Baseeba daa takkri ligri bɛni, ka u daa daai ba teebulidima m‑basi, ni ŋmankoosiriba kugsi m‑paasi.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Ka yɛli ba, ni, Di sɔbi n‑zaali, ni, Ba nin booli n Yiri wa, ni, Wunsusigu Yiri. amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑labgi la ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela. .

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Zoomma ni kɔnsi daa kyɛ u saani na, Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, ka u daa tibi ba.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima, ni karimbadima maa sufu daa yisigi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi alahazibu tunseesi Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni la; ni biisi ban daa bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑tasiri n‑yɛtta, Hoosana! Tiliginsim daana! NaaDauda Biiya.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, I bu wum ban yɛtti seem wa? Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ii, n wumiya. Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu maa yɛla? - ni, ba pɛ'ari Naawunni, ni, Nyin, Naawunni, bamsi la biisi - halli bileesi gba - ni ba tiri pɛ'abu. .

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa kyɛ ba m‑bakki n‑yi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa n‑gbɛa.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai asusuba, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑labri timpuuni na, kɔm daa ti mari u.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Ka u daa gɔsi n‑nya ka kinkaŋŋa n zɛ sɔkpaŋŋa sa; ka u daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai ka ni, amaa ŋɔn daa ti paai tii maa na, u daa bu nya sɛseelli na'ala vaari kɔkɔa. Ka Yisa daa yɛli kinkaŋŋa maa, ni, I ku laa n‑wali walli yaasa! Ka tii maa daa gbirigi n‑kuui di saha maa.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa ti nya lala, di daa gyɛri ba pam. Ka ba boosiri n‑yɛtti, Bɔ n niŋi ka tii maa gbirigi n‑kuui lala yooma.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi mari yɛdda, ka bu birisira, ya nin toaai n‑niŋi man niŋi kinkaŋŋa wa seem. Ka pa dinŋɔ kɔkɔa, amaa halli ya yi ti yɛli zoori wa, ni, ka doaama n‑lɔbi ka maŋŋa m‑basi teeku puuni, di nin niŋi.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Ya yi susi Naawunni bunseelli kam zaa, ka niŋi yɛdda, ya nin nya.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa, ni u ti wulisiri niriba la. Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima daa kyɛ u saani na, ban ni tiŋŋa maa kpamma la, m‑boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum i lala tuma maa, bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, N gba ya boosi ya la boosigu yinni. Ya yi yɛli ma pɔi, ka man gba nin naayi labsi ya ya boosigu wa.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom la, bee, u yiiko yi la yani? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani, bee, ninsaalidima saani? Ka ba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑tiri taaba, ni, Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Ka ti yi sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ninsaalidima saani.? - Ay! ti zɔri la niriba, dama sɔkam sakkiya, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura daa nyɛ la anabi.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, Ti bu mi. Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Dinŋɔna n gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Tɔ; ka ya teesiri la wula? - Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni m‑mari u bidibsi ayi. Ka u daa kyaŋŋi u bii yinni saani nti yɛli u, ni, Kyɛma nti tum tiisi puu maa tuma zuna.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Ka bii maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ay! - N ku kyaŋŋi. amaa di nyaaŋŋa u daa laa n‑takki u teesa ka kyaŋŋi.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Di saha ka u daa paai u bii ŋɔnla, ka yɛli ŋɔn gba, ni, u kyɛma nti tum, ka ŋɔn mun sakki n‑yɛli, ni, Tɔ, n wumiya. amaa u daa bu kyaŋŋi.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Tɔ, ka babayi wa saani, ŋɔni n daa tum u ba ŋɔn boori seem? Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, Ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni la. Ka u daa yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ban deaari lampoo ni ban goori gyɛ'amiru la nin gaari yarima tooni n‑kpɛ Arizana naam ni.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Dama Gyɔni daa kyɛ na ni u ti wulisiri ya yɛlimaŋni soori, ka yarima daa bu niŋi u yɛdda, amaa ka lampodeaariba ni gyɛ'amirudima daa niŋi u yɛdda. Ka yarima muna, halli ni yan daa nya lala zaa, ya daa bu takki ya teesa n‑naayi niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa yaasa, ni, Yiri daansɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑daa sa u tiwanna puu. U daa mɛ la goomni n‑gyili puu maa, n‑gbi bɔkku ba n soorigri tiwala maa seela, ka laa n‑ti sugri puu maa puuni. U daa maali di zaa ka zaŋŋi puu maa n‑niŋi ninvugseeba nuu ni ni ba tumma puu maa tuma n‑tiri u: ka u daa naayi kyaŋŋi n‑gɔa.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Tɔ, ka tiisi maa wala saha ŋɔn daa ti pɔri, u daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani nti deaai u puu maa diibu na n‑ti u.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Amaa tuntumniba maa daa gbaai ba yinni m‑bu u, ka sɔ mun ka ba daa ku, ka daabsɔ yaasa ka ba daa lɔbi kuga.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Ka puu maa daana daa laa n‑tum seeba, daaba pam gba n‑gaari banla, ka tuntumniba maa daa niŋi ba zaa lala.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Ka u daa naayi tum u toori biiya ba saani, dama u daa teesiya, ni, Ba nin ti m maŋtoori bii maa gyirima.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya puu maa daana bii la, ba daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u, ka mari u faari maa.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Ka lala zugu ba daa gbaai u n‑zaŋŋi u m‑basi puu maa kpaŋŋa n‑ku u.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Tɔ: ka puu maa daana yi ti paai na, u nin niŋi tuntumniba wa wula.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, U nin ku tuntumniba wa zaa ka ba kum nin ti bɛ halli. Ka u nin zaŋŋi puu maa n‑ti tuntunseeba ban nin sakki n‑zaŋŋi u wala maa n‑ti u, a walibu saha yi ti paai.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu la? - ni#:- Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu; Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti. - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Ni, Dinŋɔna man yɛtti ya ni, ni, ba nin fa Naawunni naam yɛla ya saani nti zaŋŋi n‑ti tingbanseaa niriba, ban tuma sumsi ni ba deaai. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Ni, Ŋɔnkam zaa lu kugri maa zugu nin kabi m‑basi, amaa kugri maa ŋɔn lu sɔ zugu ka nin ŋmɛrigi u zaa.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Di saha ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni Farisii dima maa daa wum lala ka daa baŋŋi, ni, nyɛsiri maa galimni la bana.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ka ba daa boori soori ni ba gbaai u; amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam la dabeem, dama zama maa daa yɛnni, ni, anabi n nyɛ u.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa yi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑kyɛnni la, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, ni, u gɔsim Gyeeŋŋu Yiri duri ni gooma meebu ŋɔn veeli seem.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: U mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya bu nya anŋɔ zaa? Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, a seaa ku kpalim n‑tɔm ka kugkpeaa zugu, amaa a zaa nin ti ŋmɛ n‑wurim n‑lu tiŋŋa na.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa u daa zintiri Ɔlivii Zoori zugu, ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti sɔ'ai m‑boosi u, ni, Yɛlim ti wula saha ka dinŋɔ nin niŋi. Ka bumbɔ n ti ya niŋi n‑wulisi, ni, i ya n‑labi ni na, ka duniya wa ti ya paai ka saha tariga.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya baŋŋma suŋŋa ka di kyɛ ka sɔ yɔmsi ya.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Niriba pam nin zaŋŋi n yuuri n‑kyɛ na, ni, Mani n nyɛ Masiiya maa! ka ba nin yɔmsi niriba pam m‑basi.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya nin wumni tɔppu damni, bee di lahabaya; ya gɔsima! - yan di kyɛ ka yam-ŋmaa gbaai ya, dama lala nin niŋi pɔi, amaa pa naari maa nla.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Tinsi dima nin yisigi n‑tuusi taaba tɔppu, ni nadima gba nin tuusi taaba. Kɔm nin ti lu, ka tingbaŋŋu maa nin damni kpa, ka nin damni kpa;.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: anŋɔ zaa ti ya m‑bɛ ni hoo pɔ'a pudaana wɔlisigu piiligu beerim ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Di saha ba nin ti gbaasi ya ka zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi seeba nuusi ni, ka ba namsi ya ka ku ya. Ban pa Yɛhudiya dima maa zaa nin yaai ya man yuuri zugu.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Di saha maa ka niriba pam nin ti birisi ka labi nyaaŋŋa, ka seeba mun figsiri taaba ka yaai taaba.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Ka ziri anabidima pam gba nin ti yi na ka yɔmsiri niriba pam.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Ban za'asiri soori la nin ti maali n‑zoaai, ka di zugu niriba pam ku laa n‑wuri taaba suŋŋa.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Amaa sɔ zaa ŋɔn nin kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑bari n‑zaani halli nti paai yɛla wa tariga maa nin nya tiliginsim.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Ka ba nin ti mooli Arizana naam lahabasuŋŋu wa ka tinkam zaa niriba baŋŋi di yɛla suŋŋa, halli nti paai duniya wa lugri kam. Ba nin niŋi lala pɔi, ka bunseelikam naayi ti naai.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Ya nin ti nya bumbɛseelli kan kyisiri la - anabi Daniyɛli ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seaa yɛla nla - ka ba zaŋŋi n‑zaali luginseaa kan nyɛ Naawunni bunkasi la ni, n‑sa'amna kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn karimni anŋɔ baŋŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Tɔ: ya yi ti nya lala, di saha maa yan kyɛ ka ban bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu la ni maa zɔ n‑du zɔya ni.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ban bɛ pii zugu ti sigi tiŋŋa na n‑zaŋŋi yiri puuni sɛseela;.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: ka seeba ban bɛ puri ni di labi nyaaŋŋa nti zaŋŋi ba kpari.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Di saha maa di nin ti kpeem pam n‑tisi pɔ'apuusi dima ni pɔ'aseeba ban mari biisi ka ba na mɔ'ara.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Dizugu ya susim Naawunni ka yan zɔri sahanseaa wa bu kyɛ na waari saha, bee, Vuusim Dabsiri maa ni.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Dama di saha maa ka mugsi nin ti bɛ ni pam, ka a taabu n na zi n‑niŋi duniya piiligu saha halli ni zuna, ka ti ku laa n‑nya lala taabu yaasa.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Ka ba yi daa bu ŋmaai dabsa maa n‑gyiŋŋi, saalibii n daa ka ni n‑da naa n‑nya tiliginsim, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi seeba la zugu ka ba siri daa ŋmaai dabsa maa.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Ka di saha maa, sɔ yi ti yɛli ya, ni, Nyama, Masiiya maa bɛ la kpa. bee Kpa sa ka u bɛ ni. yan di niŋi ba yɛdda.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Seeba nin ti doaai na m‑ma'ari ziri, ni, ban n nyɛ Masiiya, bee, ban n nyɛ anabidima; ba nin tum alahazibu tunkara ni ba ti yɔmsi niriba m‑basi, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn yiisi seeba maa gba, amaa Naawunni ku sakki lala.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Ya bu nya? - man pun daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya seelli kam zaa.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Niriba yi ti yɛtti ya, ni, Ya gɔsima, u bɛ la yoo ni!, yan di kyaŋŋi; bee, ni, Ya gɔsima, u bɛ la duu puuni!, yan di niŋi lala yɛdda.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Dama Ninsaala Bii kyɛnni na dabsiri maa yi ti paai, u nin ŋmani hoo saa ŋɔn yi nya'asira, n‑neaai sangbana gbinni n‑yi wuntampusiri ya'ari wa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi wuntanluri ya'ari wa sa.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Tɔ: bunkpiiŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ seela, bɛni ka zuusi nin ti la'am taaba.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Amaa wahala maa nyaaŋŋa, di saha maa deede, ni#:- Wuntaŋŋa nin ti zibgi zimm! Ka namboaa la ku laa m‑pɛligi: Saŋmariwuusi nin ti yi saazugu n‑lu na, Ka arizana yiiko daandima zaa nin damna.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Di saha ka makkri nin ti niŋi sangbana ni n‑wulisi Ninsaala Bii maa yɛla, ka ninsaalidima ban bɛ duniya wa ni maa nin ti kumna - lugri kam, ni butiri kam, dima - ba yi ti nya sɔ ka u ŋmani ninsaala, bee, Ninsaala Biiya ka u pɔtti na, ka sangbana gyiligi u ka u ti mari yiiko ni gyirima veelim pam.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Ka u nin ti kyɛ ka ba peebi kikaa pampam pɔi, ka u naayi tum u malaakadima maa ka ba yi na m‑paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa n‑nya ŋɔn yiisi seeba la, ka la'asi ba taaba na.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Ni, Ya kyɛ ka kinkaŋŋa yɛla wulisi ya di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ya yi ti nya ka lala tii maa wula na bu kpaŋŋi ka a pusiri la vapaala, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, siga saha m pɔtta.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Ka di bɛ la lala: ya yi ti nya yɛla wa zaa, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, saha maa pɔriya.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli ya wa, ni, zuna niriba zaa ku gaari, see ka dinŋɔ zaa naayi niŋi.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu nin gaari, amaa man yɛttɔ'a wa ku gaari.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ni, Amaa di daari maa, bee di saha, ŋɔn ya m‑paai sahanseaa la ka sɔsɔ bu mi deede, halli malaakadima ban bɛ arizana ni la bu mi, Naawunni Bii maa gba bu mi, na'ala ti Ba maa kɔkɔ m mi.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Hoo din daa kur niŋni seem Nuhu saha la, lala ka di ti ya n‑niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba kyɛnni na saha maa.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Koom ŋɔn na bu kyɛ na n‑di ba sahanseaa la ka niriba daa diri ni ka nyuura, ka diri pɔ'aba ka zaŋŋni ba bipunsi n‑tiri dɔppa, halli nti paai Nuhu ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ nyarintitaari maa ni dahinseaa maa.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Amaa ni dinŋɔ zaasa, ba daa bu mi din ya n‑niŋi, halli ka koom maa daa ti di ba zaa n‑kyaŋŋi. Tɔ: di nin ti nyɛ la lala Ninsaala Bii maa ŋɔn yɛ n‑ti kyɛ na sahanseaa la.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Di saha dappa ayi nin ti bɛ ba puu ni n‑tumna, ka Naawunni kpikki yinni ka kyɛ yinni.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Ka pɔ'aba ayi nin ti la'am n‑neemni ba kyi neeri zugu, ka Naawunni kpikki yinni ka kyɛ yinni.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Yan di gbisi yaa! dama ya bu mi sahaseaa ka ya Dugma maa ti ya n‑kyɛ na.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Ya baŋŋma dinŋɔa: ni, yiddaansɔ yi da mi sahanseaa na'ayiga ŋɔn ya kyɛ u yiri na, u naa n‑ku gbisiri goom ka kyɛ ka u kabi n‑kpɛ u yiri maa ni na.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla maa, lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa ti ya kyɛ na.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli Ninvugŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn mari yam ni yɛdda? - ka u dugma zaŋŋi u yiri biisi maa fukumsi n‑niŋi di daama nuu ni, ni u tiri ba diibu, mɔnni basibu saha maa.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Pa ŋɔna n nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn nin nya sufupeelli, dama u dugma kuli na m‑paai u, u lala tuma maa ni?.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n siri yɛtti ya, ni, u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi n‑niŋi lala daabri nuu ni.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Amaa daabsɔ yi bɛ ni n‑nyɛ ninvugbeoo, ka ti yɛli u sufu ni, ni, u dugma maa yuuya pam ni u kulibu na.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: ka dizugu ka u piili m‑buuri u daabtaaba, ka dira, ka nyuuri daam danyuuriba saani m‑bugra;.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: u dugma maa yi ti paai dahinseaa daabri maa ŋɔn bu teesiri u yɛla, bee sahanseaa ŋɔn bu mi u yɛla maa.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: ka u dugma maa nin ti darigi u tubri ka ŋmaasi u bisibisi ka nin ti zaŋŋi u m‑paasi birikɔnsi dima maa zugu: bɛni ka ba kur kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wulisi yɛla wa zaa n‑naai, u daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: ni, Ya mi halli, ni, di kpalim la dabsa ayi ni Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu. Di saha ka ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii maa n‑tisi ni ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Alahadaari, bɛkyikyeoo, ka Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, ni u tɔ'ama la, daa kyaŋŋi ni ba ti gɔsi tampifeoo gballi maa.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Dinsaha ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni Izraɛli kpamma maa daa la'am Kayafasi yiri - sɔ ŋɔn daa di gyeeŋŋu suulinsi la.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Di daa bu yuui ka tingbaŋŋu daa vibgi pam, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi arizana ni n‑sigi na n‑vuui gballi dunoori kugbiliŋŋu maa m‑biligi ka n‑yi kpaŋŋa n‑zinni ka zugu.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: Ba daa ŋmeeri taaba saawara m‑boori sɔseaa ban nin zaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai n‑gbaai Yisa n‑ku u.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Ka u nindaa nya'ari ni n‑ŋmanni hoo saa ŋɔn yi nya'asi seem la, ka u neema pɛligi hoo gumni.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Ni, Ti di niŋi lala kyuu maa zama sunsukkuni, mira ka niriba maa nin zabi di zugu.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Dabeem daa gbaai gbaliguriba maa, halli ka ba ningbina kpi m‑maai hoo kum la.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yisa mun daa bɛ la Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa; u daa kpɛ la Saimoon yiri, ŋɔn daa nyɛ kunkɔŋŋa la.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli pɔ'aba maa, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya. M mi halli, ni, ya boori la Yisa ziiya, Nazarɛti dɔsɔ ban daa kpaasi la.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Ka ban daa ti dira, pɔ'asɔ daa mari turaali kɔlɔba n‑kpɛ na, turaali kpaam din mari nyunya'asiri ka di ligri zoaai pam n‑daa la, m‑paai na nti kpaai kpaam maa n‑saari Yisa zugu.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: U laa n‑ka kpa. U yigsiya hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem. Ya kyɛm na n‑nya, ŋɔn daa dɔ seela nŋɔa!.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa sufu daa yisigi, ka ba tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba, ni, Bɔzugu ka u sa'am buna lala?.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Amaa ya kyɛm yooma n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, u neaai kum ni ka u nin daŋŋi ya tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii. Bɛni ka ya nin ti nya u. Ya nyama, m pun yɛli ya ni.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Ba yi da zaŋŋi bu n‑koosi, ligri pam naa n‑yi na ka lala ligri naa n‑toaai m‑puri n‑tɔri n‑ti faradima.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Di daa gyɛri pɔ'aba maa pam; dabeem daa mari ba, ni sufumaasim gba. Ka dizugu ba daa zɔ yooma n‑kyaŋŋi nti yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛl u! Wula ka ya daamni pɔ'a maa? U tum la din veela n‑ti ma.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa tuusi ba soori ni m‑puusi ba, ni, alaafɛa? Ba daa pɔri u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni m‑bɔbli u nɔba ka gyeemni u.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Ya kur nin mari faradima ya saani sahakam. Amaa ya ku mari mani sahakam.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya di zɔri dabeem. Ya kyɛma nti yɛli m mabiisi la, ni, ba kyɛm Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni - bɛni ka ba nin nya ma.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Ŋɔn niŋi seem maa nyɛ la#:- ni, u kpaai bunnyuuma maa n‑saari n ningbiŋŋu, ka di gbinni nyɛ la n kum sɔ'abu siri maaligu.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Tɔ: ka pɔ'aba maa ŋɔn daa ti yi soori, tɔbbiisi la seeba ban daa guri gballi maa daa labi timpuuni nti yɛli Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima la seelli zaa din niŋi.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, ni, ba yi ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu wa, duniya maa ya'aseelli zaa, ba nin teaai u yɛla n‑tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ŋɔn niŋi seem wa.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Ka Toondaandima la daa la'asi kpamma zaa na nti zinni n‑ŋmɛ taaba saawara ka gbaai gbaari. Ba daa zaŋŋi la ligri pam n‑da tɔbbiisi maa noori.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Ka Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti, ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa yinni la, daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa saani.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: ka yɛli, ni, sɔ yi ti boosi ba Yisa yɛla, ban yɛlima, ni, ba daa gbisiri ni yuŋŋu ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la kyɛ na n‑zu Yisa ningbiŋŋu la u tampifeoo gballi maa ni.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: ka yɛli ba, ni, N yi zaŋŋi Yisa n‑figsi n‑ti ya, bɔ ka ya yɛ ti ma? Ka ba daa kaali anzulifa ligri pisita n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Ni, Gɔmna maa yi ti wum lala, ti nin tɔ'asi n‑ti u, ka kyɛ ka taaliseaa zaa ku gbaai ya.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Dizugu Gyuudasi daa piili m‑boori sɔsunseaa ŋɔn nin niŋi n‑zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti ba.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Ka tɔbbiisi maa daa deaai ba ligri ka daa tum ban daa bulisi ba seem la. Di zugu lahabaari kanŋɔ na bɛ Yɛhudiya dima maa saani halli ni zuna.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kyuu maa dahinyinni, Yisa nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na m‑boosi u, ni, Yɛni ka i boori ni ti kyaŋŋi n‑sɛ'asi i kyuu diibu siri n‑ti i.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliba piiya ni yinni maa mun daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Galilii. Ba daa du zɔseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa pun wulisi ba ka yɛla maa zugu.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ba kpeema timpuuni m‑paai za'ala saani nti yɛli u, ni, Ti Karimba maa yɛli yaa! - N saha maa pɔtti na; man ni n nyaandɔliba maa ya di Kyuu maa i yiri. .

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: ka bɛni ka ba daa nya u. Di saha ba daa gyeemni u ni, amaa baseeba daa birisiri ni.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Ka ba daa niŋi hoo ŋɔn pun yɛli ba seem maa, ka daa sɛ'asi kyuu maa siri.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Yisa daa paai ba na ka yɛli ba, ni, Ba ti ma la yiiko zaa duniya wa ni, ni arizana ni gba.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Zaanoori ŋɔn daa paai, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa daa la'am n‑zini.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛma n‑yi m‑paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa n‑wulisiri tinsi zaa niriba. Ya deaama ba n‑su Naawunni koom ya Ba Naawunni yuuri zugu, ni u Biiya, ni u Siiya Vuusim Kasi yuuri zugu gba.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Ka ban daa ti dira, u daa yɛliya, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ya yinni nin figsi ma n‑ti n dindaandima maa.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: Ya wulisima ba, ni, ba dɔla man zaali ya noori seem maa zaa. Ka ya baŋŋma, ni, n nin bɛ ya saani, halli ni duniya wa saha maa naari.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Ba sufu daa sa'am pam, ka ba daa yɛtti yinni yinni, N dugma, ka mani bee.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Di nyɛ la man ni sɔ ŋɔn la'amni n‑sugri diibu laa yinni ni - ŋɔna n nyɛ n figsira.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Ninsaala Bii maa nin bakki hoo din pun sɔbi n‑zaali u zugu seem la. Amaa ŋɔn mun figsi Ninsaala Bii maa n‑kyɛ ka ba gbaai u - di ku veeli u zugu. Ba yi daa kyɛ di daama dɔ'am, di naa n‑sɔa.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Di saha ka Gyuudasi, sɔ ŋɔn ya figsi u la, daa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli u, ni, N Karimba, ka mani, bee? Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Nyin yɛli.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti diri maa, Yisa daa zaŋŋi diseebu m‑puusi Naawunni pɛ'abu ka ŋmaai n‑zaŋŋi n‑tisi u nyaandɔliba, ka yɛli, Ya deaama n‑ŋɔbi; n ningbina nla.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Ka laa n‑zaŋŋi kɔbeaa m‑puusi Naawunni ka zaŋŋi n‑ti ba, ni, Ya zaa nyuum ka puuni.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: N ziim nla. N ziim wa nin ti yi na, ka di yiibu na nin suŋŋi niriba pam ka kyɛ ka nangbanyinni zaalipaalli ti zaani n‑tiri ba, tumbeeri taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi zugu.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Dama n yɛtti ya, n ku laa n‑nyu tiwala daam wa taabu yaasa, halli ni dindahinseaa man ni yan ŋɔn ti ya nyu di bumpaalli, m Ba naam beesigu ni.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ka ba daa yuum yuuma m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Ban daa yuum n‑naai ba daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Ɔlivii Zoori lugri ni.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Zuna yuŋŋu wa ya zaa nin wurim ka kyɛ ma, vi zugu. Di bu sɔbi n‑dooli lala? - ni, N nin ŋmɛ pɛgura maa ka u peesi maa wurim.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Man mun yi ti doaai kum ni, n nin daŋŋi ya tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ba zaa yi ti wurim ka kyɛ i, vi zugu, man ku kyɛ i saha kam.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, zuna yuŋŋu wa ka nyin mammaŋŋa nin kyiisi, ni, i zi ma, m‑maali n‑yɛli lala napɔŋŋu buta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Ka Piita labsi n‑yɛli ni nimmoaa, ni, Halli di yi zaan kum ni, n ku kyiisi i. Ka u nyaandɔlitaaba zaa gba daa yɛli lala.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa, nti paai ya'aseelli ban boonni Gyɛtisamanni. Ka Yisa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, Ya zinma kpa ka man kyaŋŋi sa n‑susi wunsusigu ka kyɛ na.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: U daa zaŋŋi la Piita ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni ka ba dɔli u, ka sufusa'aŋŋu ni beerim daa gbaai u ka di daa wum u pam.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, Beerim din bɛ n sufu ni wa zoaaya pam, n‑ŋmanni din ya ku ma. Ya kpalimma kpa ka man ni yarim ti guusira.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: U daa kyaŋŋi tooni beela m‑paasi nti zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑gbaani tiŋŋa ka susi, ni, M Ba, di yi ti nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi, n susiri i, ni, nyin kyɛ ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa gaari ka kyɛ ma: di kyɛ ka n nyu di! Amaa di pa la man ŋɔn boori seem la amaa nyin ŋɔn boori seem, di niŋma.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: U daa labi nyaandɔliba la saani na ka nya, ka babata maa zaa daa gbisiri ni; ka u booli Piita, ni, Saimoon, ni wula? I naa n‑ku toaai n‑gali goom halli hawa yinni n‑ti ma.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba zaa, ni, Ya guusima ka susi Naawunni ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba di zamsi ya halli ka ya ti lu. Ya sufu boori sakkri amaa ya ningbina ka yaa ka za'asiri ni.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Ka u daa laa m‑bakki yaasa n‑kyaŋŋi ka laa n‑susiri, ni, M Ba, soori yi ka ni ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa nin gaari ka kyɛ ma see ka n nyu di, tɔ! di pa la man ŋɔn boori seem la amaa nyin ŋɔn boori seem, di niŋma.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑labi m‑paai na ka ba gbisiri ni, dama ba nini daa mumsiri ni.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: Di daa niŋi dabsa ka Yisa daa labi n‑kuli Kapirinyum na n‑kpɛ yiri ni, ka u lahabaari daa paai sɔkam zaa, ni, u kuli na.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ ba, din daa paasiri buta. U daa laa n‑susiri n‑yɛtti lala.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Ka niriba pam daa la'am halli ka paalu beela daa ka duu maa ni; yiri maa samanni gba daa ka paalu halli beela. Ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a maa n‑tiri ba.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Ka u daa labi u nyaandɔliba maa saani na n‑yɛli ba, Ya na kpalim n‑gbisiri ni ka vuusira. Ni, Saha maa paaya. Ya nyama, ba pun yi n nyaaŋŋa ka figsi man Ninsaala Biiya n‑niŋi ala'akyi dima nuu ni.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Di saha ka niriba anaasi daa zi gbariga m‑paai na.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ya doaama ka ti kyaŋŋi! Minaafikka maa n kyɛnni na wa.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Ka daadam ŋɔn daa zoaai seem maa zugu ba daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑mari ba baara maa n‑kpɛ Yisa saani na. Lala maa zugu ba daa du pii maa zugu nti kyiibi voori n‑niŋi sunsukku, Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela maa deede. Ban daa kyiibi n‑naai la, ba daa kyɛ ka gbariga ni u sɔŋŋu maa zaa sigi na nti doonni Yisa tooni.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri maa, nnya Gyuudasi ŋɔn paari na, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la ni yinni. Niriba pam daa dɔli u: ba daa yi la gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni Izraɛli kpamma saani na, ka ba daa mari ba takoobidima ni dɔya na.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Tɔ, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ban niŋi yɛdda seem maa, u daa yɛli baara maa, ni, M biiya, n kyɛ i taalli m‑pɛŋŋi i.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Minaafikka maa daa pun wulisi u nyaaŋŋa ŋɔn ya n‑niŋi seem n‑kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi Yisa, ni, N yi ti puusi sɔ suŋŋasuŋŋa, ŋɔna nŋɔa. Yan gbaam u.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Ka karimbaseeba daa zi bɛni m‑boosiri ba sufu ni teesa, ni.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Ka dizugu Gyuudasi daa kyaŋŋi yooma m‑paai Yisa saani, ka yɛli u, N Karimba, aniŋwula! ka daa puusi u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Bɔ ka doo wa tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti wa? - lala ka u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑zaantiri Naawunni zaaziiya! Ŋɔni n‑lee n‑toaai n‑kyɛ niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba? Ka pa ni Naawunni kɔkɔa.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, N zɔa, niŋma nyin ya niŋi seem la! Tɔ, ka ba daa paai na n‑gbaai Yisa ba nuusi m‑bibgi u kirikiri.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yooma ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba sufu ni teesa maa zaa, ka boosi ba, ni, Bɔ zugu ka ya birisiri ya sufu ni teesa puuni lala?.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Ka ban daa dɔli Yisa maa sɔ daa foaai u takoobi n‑kyɛ kpambaya maa bisɔ n‑fikki u tubri m‑basi.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Yɛla ayi wa sunsukkuni kani m bu tɔ - i yi yɛli gbariga, ni, ba kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, bee i yi yɛli u, ni, u doaama ka zaŋŋi u sɔŋŋu la n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u zaŋŋma u takoobi maa n‑labsi n‑su ka teoo la ni, ni, Bankam zaa zaŋŋi takoobi n‑zabra, ban ka ba nin zaŋŋi takoobi n‑zabi n‑ku ba.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Amaa m boori la ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba duniya wa ni. Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, n da naa n‑toaai m‑booli n‑susi m Ba ka u suŋŋi ma, ka u daa naa n‑ti ma malaakadima suŋŋsim yoomyooma? - halli malaaka tɔbbiisi tusa ayoobu napɔŋŋu pii ni ayi gba, u naa n‑ti ma na.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: ni, N ya yɛli i ni, ni, i doaama ka kpikki i sɔŋŋu wa n‑kuli.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Amaa di yi niŋi lala, Sɔppu la ya niŋi la wula n‑niŋi yɛlimaŋni - ban daa sɔbi, ni, lala n sumsi ni di niŋi la.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Ka doo maa daa siri doaai n‑zaŋŋi u sɔŋŋu maa n‑yi, ba zaa tooni. Ka di daa niŋi ba zaa alahazibu ka ba daa pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta, Ti na zi n‑nya lala taabu, halli fiiŋ gba.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Ammi, n nyɛ la ban zabra ka faari neema ninvugsɔ, bee wula? - ka ya mari takoobidima ni dɔya n‑yi na ni ya gbaai ma dizugu? Beoo kam ka n daa yi zi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa n‑wulisiri niriba, ka ya daa bu gbaai ma.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑dɔli mɔ'aduuri maa ni. Ka niriba zaa daa kyɛ u saani na ka u daa piili n‑wulisiri ba.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Amaa anŋɔ zaa niŋiya, ni di siri niŋi hoo Naawunni anabidima maa sɔppu Litaafi ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa. Ka u nyaandɔliba zaa daa zɔ ka kyɛ u.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: U daa kyɛnni n‑gatta, ka naayi nya karikyisɔ, Alifiisi bii Liivai, ka u daa zi ba lampoo tuma ziiya n‑tumna. Ka Yisa yɛli u, Doaama n‑dɔli ma! Ka u daa doaai ka dɔli u.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu la ni, ka dɔsɔ nukpiiŋŋu daana n daa bɛ bɛni.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Tɔ. Ba daa gbaai Yisa m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi Kayafasi, Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la, yiri; bɛni ka karimbadima maa ni kpamma maa zaa daa pun la'am na.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ ŋɔn Liivai yiri maa, ban ni Yisa nyaandɔliba. Ka lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima pam daa dɔli ba n‑kpɛa, ka ba zaa daa la'am nti di bundirigu, dama lala niriba daa zoaaya, ka daa dɔli Yisa.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ka ninvugseeba n daa bɛ bɛni m‑paasi, ka ba daa boori daliiri ni ba galim Yisa. Ka dizugu ba daa gɔsiri u ni, ni ba ti nya u nin tibi sɔ Vuusim Dabsiri daari, bee?.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Ka Piita daa sɔ'ai n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa sa, halli n‑kpɛ Toondaana maa dundɔŋŋu ni. U daa zi bɛni, m‑bɛ soogyadima maa sunsukkuni, ni u ti nya di naari ŋɔn ya nyɛ seem.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Ka karimbadima maa, baseeba ban daa dɔli Farisii dima maa soori la, daa nya ka Yisa la'amni ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima n‑dira, ka ba daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Wula ka ŋɔn ni ninvugbanŋɔ taabu n la'amni n‑dira.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli doo maa, Doaam n‑zaani sunsukkuni.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Kpamma maa, ni la'aŋŋu dima zaa, daa moori la ni ba nya ziri sɛhara n‑galim Yisa ka ku u.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Ka Yisa daa wum ban tɔ'asi seem maa ka labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ninvugseeba ban mari alaafɛ bu boori tiimaraana, na'ala ban beeri m boori u. Pa tunsuma dima ka n kyɛ na ni m boonni ba, amaa tumbeeri dima ka m boonna.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: ka laa n‑yɛli ba zaa, Vuusim Dabsiri muna, soori bɛ ni, ni, nira tum tunsuma di dabsiri maa, bee tumbeeri ka u nin tum? M‑paasi yaasa, u tiligma sɔ nyɔvuri, bee u sa'am di ni? Ka niriba maa daa kur kpalim surimm.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Amaa ba daa bu nya lala sɛhara, halli ni niriba pam daa yi na ni ba ma'ai ziri n‑galim u. Ka niriba ayi daa naayi doaai n‑zaani na.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Gyɔni Kɔsuura nyaandɔliba ni Farisii dima daa ti loori noori, ka ninvugseeba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na m‑boosi u, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba maa loori noori, ban ni Farisii dima maa, ka nyin mun nyaandɔliba bu loora.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Ka u daa gɔsi n‑gyiligi ba zaa ka u suuri daa yisigi ka u sufu sa'am ba zugkpeeŋŋu ŋɔn zoaai maa zugu, ka naayi yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, Teaam i nuu maa! Ka u daa teaai, ka u nuu maa daa nya tippu.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: ni, Doo wa yɛliya, ni, u nin sa'am Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu ka zaŋŋi daba ata m‑mɛ seaa.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Ka Yisa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, Dɔsɔ yi ti di pɔ'apaalli ka ba mari la deema di zugu, ammi, pɔ'apaalli maa sira yi ti bɛ u saamma sunsukkuni, ba nin lɔ noori di saha, bee? Ay! Pɔ'apaalli daana yi ti kpalim m‑bɛ ba saani ba ku toaai n‑lɔ noori.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Ka Farisii dima daa doaai ya'abi, ban ni NaaHɛroodi nyaaŋŋa dima, n‑yi duu maa ni nti ŋmeeri taaba saawara m‑boori sɔseaa ba nin dɔli n‑ku Yisa.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa doaai m‑boosi u, ni, I ka labsigu? Bumbɔ nŋɔ ka ba yɛtti yɛttɔ'aseesi m‑paasiri i wa.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Amaa sahanseaa kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yiisi amriya doo maa ba saani - di dabsa maa puuni ba nin ti lɔa.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Tɔ. Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi mɔ'ari ni, ka Galilii dima pam daa yi na n‑dɔli ba. M‑paasi lala, niriba pam gba daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyɛ u saani na, ban daa wum ŋɔn pun tumni tunseesi maa zugu, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa niriba, ni Idumiya tingbaŋŋu dima, ni tingbanseaa zaa kan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari wa sa; halli seeba daa yi Taaya ni Saidoon tinsi ni gba n‑kyɛ na.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa sina, Toondaana maa daa laa m‑boosi u n‑yɛli, Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana zugu n nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi nangbampɔri ni - yɛlima ti#:- nyin nyɛ Masiiya ŋɔn ya fa ti m‑basi ka leebi naaba? Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Ka u daa laa m‑paasi ba nyɛsiri n‑yɛli, Sɔ na zi n‑zaŋŋi kyinkyimpaalli tanni n‑sɛ n‑ligi bunyeerikurigu. U yi niŋi lala, tampaalli maa yi yiisi kan ligi seela maa ka kyeaai bunkurigu la, ka ka maali n‑sa'am m‑paasi.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Lala zama maa zugu ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba boom nyariŋŋu m‑maali siri ni ba deaai u, ni, niriba maa ŋɔn kur gyiligri m‑mugri u pam.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑ti u, ni, I yɛliya; amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna#:- Ya nin nya Ninsaala Bii maa Ka u zi Naawunni Yaadaana maa nudirigu ya'ari Ka kyɛnni na ka saazugu sangbana gyiligi u. .

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Bee, sɔ mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akura ni. U yi niŋi lala, daam maa nin ŋma ga'akura maa ka sa'am m‑basi, ka ga'a maa gba sa'am. Ay! ga'apaala n zaŋŋni dapaalli.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Dama u daa tibi niriba pam, ka dizugu baaridima zaa kur daari ni n‑kyɛnni tooni ni ba ti siisi u.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Ka Toondaana maa suuri daa yisigi, halli ka u daa toaai u maŋtoori sutura dizugu ka yɛli, Ah! U tuuri la Naawunni! Ti bu laa m‑boori sɛharadima yaasa, bee bɔa? Ya pun wum ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an kyisira.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Di daa niŋi ka Vuusim dindahinseaa ka Yisa daa dɔli puri ni n‑gatta. U nyaandɔliba n daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli u, ka ba daa kabri kasana maa.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Arizimbeeri daandima maa muna ŋɔn daa yi nya u, ba daa yi gbaani tiŋŋa u tooni ka tasi n‑yɛli, Naawunni Bii n nyɛ i.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ya gbaai la wula? Ka ba zaa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, U niŋi taalli - taalli kan sumni la kuubu.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Amaa u daa kpamsi ba pam, ni, ba di yɛli daadam maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ka Farisii dima daa yɛli u, Nyama! Wula ka ba tumni puri tuma wa? Ba galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa du zoori zugu, ka booli ŋɔn boori seeba ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti paai u saani na.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Baseeba daa piili n‑tusiri nantoori n‑niŋi Yisa nini, ka daa lɔbri u tampɛ'asi ka daa buuri u.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ammi, ya na zi n‑karim NaaDauda yɛla maa? Ni, Abiyaata ŋɔn daa di maalimaanniba zuguraana saha maa, ka bukaata daa dooni ŋɔn NaaDauda zugu ka u daa bɛ ni, ŋɔn ni u taaba; ka kɔm daa gbaai ba.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: U daa yiisi niriba piiya ni ayi, ni ba ti kpalim n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa, ka u tum ba ni ba ti yi u saani n‑kyaŋŋi nti moonni lahabaari maa.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: ka boosiri u, ni, Nyin Masiiya, nyin anabi wa, bugma m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ŋmɛ i wa.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: ka u daa kpɛ la Naawunni gyeeŋŋu zii maa n‑zaŋŋi boroboro n‑ŋɔbi. Pa boroboro anŋɔ taabu ka ba dooli Naawunni tooni ka a kyisiri ni? - na'ala maalimaanniba kɔkɔ n ŋɔbri a ni. Halli ka u daa laa n‑tisi u taaba maa ka ba daa ŋɔbi.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: ka mari yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri m‑basira.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Tɔ; ka Piita daa na bɛ la tiŋni dundɔŋŋu maa puuni, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana yiri bipunsi sɔ daa kpɛ na nti yɛli u, ni, Nyin gba yi bɛ la Yisa, Galilii nira wa, saani.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Ka Yisa daa naa n‑yɛli ba, Vuusim dabsiri ka ba daa zaali ninsaala zugu, pa la Vuusim Dabsiri zugu ka ba daa maali ninsaala.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: Piiya ni babayi ka u daa yiisi maa, ka ba yuya nŋɔa#:- u daa pa Saimoon yupaa, ni Piita .

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa kyiisi ba zaa tooni, ni, M bu baŋŋi nyin tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi wa.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Dizugu man Ninsaala Bii n su Vuusim Dabsiri fukumsi gba.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: ni Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii bii la, n daa bɛ ni, ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni - ban ka u daa pa ba yupaa, ni Bɔ'anɛrigyeesi .

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Ka daa yi n‑kpɛ bunzɔŋŋu ni. Ka pɔ'asarisɔ daa nya u n‑yɛtti dappa ban bɛ bɛni maa, ni, Ŋɔnŋɔ n daa dɔli Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑wulisiri niriba, Galilii mɔ'ari kpaŋŋa. Niriba ban daa la'am u saani maa daa zoaaya pam, dizugu u daa kpɛ la nyariŋŋu puuni nti zinni. Nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la koom puuni ka daadam maa daa kpalim mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu n‑wumna.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: ni Anduru ni Filipi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Matiyu ni Tɔmaasi ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Taddiyusi ni Saimoon Kyɛna'an doowa.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Ka Piita daa kyiisi yaasa, ni, M pɔ la noori, ni, m bu mi doo wa.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Ka u daa nyɛsi ba nyɛsa ka a wulisi ba yɛla pam;.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: ni Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti .

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Din daa paasi beela, ban daa zɛ bɛni maa daa laa n‑yɛli Piita, ni, I ku toaai n‑kyiisi, ni, i pa ba nyaandɔla, dama i gba tɔ'asiri la Galiliitiri ka ti baŋŋi i yɛla di zugu.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: ka daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya wumma! Dɔsɔ n daa kyaŋŋi puu, ni u ti waali u bumbura;.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri ni, ka daadam daa laa n‑la'amni na halli ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑di bundirigu gba.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: ka ŋɔn daa waanni maa, a seesi daa lu la sɔkpaŋŋa, ka niinsi daa kyɛ na n‑di a zaa.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Ka Piita daa maali m‑poori n‑yɛtti yɛlikyɔ'ama, ni, M pɔya, ni, n yɛli la yɛlimaŋni. M bu mi doo maa! Di saha maa ka nɔroo daa kum.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: U niriba ŋɔn daa ti wum dinŋɔa, ba daa yi na ni ba kyaŋŋi n‑goaai u, ni, U zugu n labgi.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Tɔ, di saha maa ka u bumburiseesi daa lu kuga ziiya ka tanni ka ni see beela fiiŋ, ka daa tɔ bula yooma dama tanni maa daa bu zoaai;.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: ka Piita teaai Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli u seem, ni, I nin kyiisi ma napɔŋŋu buta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum. Ka u daa zɔ n‑yi samanni ka kumni kunkuma.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Di saha maa ka karimbaseeba daa pun yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑sigi na, ka ba daa bɛ bɛni n‑yɛtti, ni, Biyɛlizibuli m mari u. Ii! - ArizinNaaba yiiko ka u zaŋŋni n‑katti arizimbeeri maa.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: amaa wuntaŋŋa daa bii ka ka bula maa daa gyɛkki n‑kpi, dama a nya'a daa bu sigi wa'aligu.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Ka Yisa daa booli ba na ka zaŋŋi nyɛsa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, ni, Sintaani ya n‑niŋi la wula ni u yiisi Sintaani m‑basi?.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Ka seesi gba daa lu la goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa zoaai na n‑gabi kabula maa ka a daa kɔŋŋi dɔ'abu.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Tinseaa yi bɛ ni ka ka timbiisi yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala tiŋŋa ku toaai n‑zaani.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Amaa kaburiseesi daa lu la tam ziiya n‑tɔ bula, n‑zoaai, ka daa dɔ'ai n‑niŋi kyi, seesi daa niŋi m‑paai kabeelim pisitata, seesi pisiyooyoobu, seesi mun kɔbkɔbga.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Bee yiriseaa yi bɛ ni ka ka yiri dima yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala yiri ku toaai n‑zaani.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti ya n‑naai u yɛttɔ'a maa u daa yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Dizugu Sintaani muna, u yi ti za'asi u mammaŋŋa n‑zabra, ŋɔn gba ku toaai n‑zaani, u yɛla zaa naari nla.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti ziya u kɔkɔa, ninvugseeba ban daa wum u lala yɛttɔ'a maa daa dɔli u tuumudima piiya ni ayi la n‑kyɛ u saani na nti susi u, ni, u wulisim ba nyɛsiri wa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, sɔ ku toaai n‑kpɛ yaa daana yiri n‑zu u neema maa ka di pa la u daŋŋi n‑lɔ lala yaa daana maa ka naayi nyaŋŋi n‑fa u yiri ni buna zaasa.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Yarim muna - Naawunni siri ti ya soori ni ya baŋŋi u naam yɛlisɔ'ara ŋɔn nyɛ seem zaa. Amaa ban kpalim la nin wum yɛla zaa ka a bɛ nyɛsa ni.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya maa, Naawunni nin kyɛ ninsaalidima ala'akyi taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ba; halli nira yi tum tumbeeri, bee u yi sa'am Naawunni yuuri gba:.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: Ni#:- Ba nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ba ku nya seela: Ba mun nin wum m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ba ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Di yi pa lala, ba da naa n‑labi Naawunni saani na, Ka u da kyɛ ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba. .

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: amaa ŋɔn sa'am Sii Kasi yuuri maa, u ku nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi halli saha kan ka naari, ka u nin di la saha din ka tariga taalli.

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Di saha Yisa daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, Ya bu baŋŋi nyɛsiri wa gbinni? Ka ya ya niŋi la wula m‑baŋŋi nyɛsa zaa gbina?.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: - Ban daa yɛtti, ni, u mari la arizimbeoo, dizugu ka u daa yɛtti anŋɔ maa.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Tɔ, ya wumma: ŋɔn butti kyi la n nyɛ hoo ŋɔn moonni Naawunni yɛligu.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Ka Yisa ma, ni u sunzɔppa, daa paai na n‑zaani samanni ka tum sɔ ni u kpɛ m‑booli u na.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Ninvugseeba ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu soori ni la n nyɛ seeba ban yi wum yɛligu maa pɔi, ka di bu yuui ka Sintaani naayi kyɛ na n‑fa yɛligu maa ka kyɛ ba.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Daadam mun n daa zi n‑gyiligi u, ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, I ma ni i sunzɔppa bɛ samanni m‑boori i ziiya.;.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Ninvugseeba muna ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu kuga zugu la n nyɛ seeba ban yi wum yɛligu maa sahanseaa ba yi deaai ka ni sufupeelli;.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, M ma ni n sunzɔppa n nyɛ bandima.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: amaa di bu kpɛ la ba sufu ni ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ba nya'a kaalinsi zugu ba bu yuura: dizugu yɛlibeoo bee vɔlisigu yi ti kyɛ na Naawunni yɛligu maa zugu, ba yi labi la nyaaŋŋa yooma.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: ka daa gɔsi ban zi n‑gyiligi u maa ka daa yɛtti, N‑nya ba! M ma ni n sunzɔppa maa nŋɔa!.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Ninvugseeba muna ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu goosi ni la n nyɛ baseeba ban yi wum yɛligu maa pɔi.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Ŋɔnkam tumni Naawunni yuubu tuma nyɛ la n sunzɔ, bee n sunzɔpɔ'a, bee m ma.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: ka naa n‑zaŋŋi duniya wa yɛla m‑mugsi ba mansi, ni bundaantirigu ni sufuyuubu yɛla n yi ti ta'ai ka ba ku toaai n‑wumni yɛligu maa, dizugu ka Naawunni yɛligu bu toaai m‑bunni ba sufu ni nti zoaai na n‑wanni tunsuma wala.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Amaa ninvugseeba ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu tam ziiya la n nyɛ baseeba ban wumni Naawunni yɛligu maa ka deaari ka, ka laa n‑wanna: baseeba pisitata, seeba pisiyooyoobu, seeba mun kɔbkɔbga.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka, bee u zaŋŋi ka n‑sɔ'ai gadu lɔŋni. Ay! U yi zaŋŋni ka n‑tɔmni la saazugu.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Bunsɔ'ariseaa ka ni, ka ka ku ti yi paalu na; bee asiiri seaa ka ni ka ka ku yi na ka ba baŋŋi ka.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: U daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya kpasim suŋŋa ya wumpu ziiya! Yan ma'asiri seem n‑tiri niriba, lala ka ba nin ma'asi n‑ti ya gba, halli ba nin paasi ya lala.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari seelli ya n‑nya m‑paasi, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli maa ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa ka ba nin ti fa ka kyɛ u yɔri.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Ni, Naawunni naam ŋmani la nŋɔa#:- Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑daa yaam bumbura u paalu ni n‑naai;.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: di nyaaŋŋa u daa doontiri yuŋŋu n‑neaari beoo, n‑doontira, n‑neaara, ka lala saha zaa ka u bumbura maa bunni n‑zoaari tanni puuni, amaa ŋɔn daa bu baŋŋi din niŋi seem.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Ay! - Tanni maa daa tumni ka maŋtoori tuma n‑kyɛ ka kyi maa ti niŋi: a daa daŋŋi m‑buli m‑pusi n‑dɔ'ai, halli nti piri.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Ka kyi maa yi ti kuui, doo maa nin zaŋŋi u suuwa n‑yi na n‑ŋma a ni, dama kyi ŋmaabu saha paaya.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Ti nin yɛli, ni, Naawunni naam ŋmani la bɔa? Ma'asiri bɔ ka ti nin zaŋŋi n‑wulisi di gbinni? Tɔ: di ŋmani la nŋɔa#:-.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑zaŋŋi zinzambeelli, m‑buri ka tiŋŋa ni. Tɔ, bumbilifiiŋ nla, ka bumbura kam zaa n galisi n‑gaari ka.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Ka ka daa buli, n‑zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaasa, ka daa tɔ wula, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri a zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Tɔ. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri u yɛligu wa n‑tiri niriba la, u daa kur zaŋŋi ma'asa ni nyɛsa anŋɔ taabu n‑tɔ'asira. Lala ka di daa paai ba zaa yinniyinni bamsim ŋɔn sa'ai seem.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: U daa bu tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseelli ka ma'asa ka di ni, amaa ŋɔn daa ti bɛ ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔa, sahanseaa la u daa yi wulisiri ba la yɛttɔ'anyɛsa maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Di daari zaanoori maa ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba la, ni, Ya kyɛ ka ti lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari wa ya'ari sa.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi u ŋɔn daa pun bɛ nyariŋŋu seaa la ni, ka bakki n‑kyɛ zama maa mɔ'aduuri ni. Ka nyarinseesi gba daa dɔli u m‑paasi.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Di daa bu yuui ka pɛbsititaari daa ti yisigi ka daa mari nyariŋŋu maa n‑laantiri ni, halli ka koom daa ti kpɛ ka ni.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Yisa daa bɛ la nyariŋŋu maa nyaaŋŋa ziiya n‑kukki dafeeru n‑gbisira. Ka ba daa neaai u ka yɛli u, Ti Karimba, di bu mugsi i tin ya kpi wa zugu.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Ka u daa neaai ka tasi pɛbsigu ni koom maa, ni, Ya sina! Kur kyɛla!, ka a daa siri kyɛya, ka di lu n‑fɔ surimm!.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Din saha ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Bɔzugu ka dabeem gbaai ya lala? Ammi, ya na bu niŋi yɛdda, bee.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Ka dabeem pam daa maali n‑gbaai ba, ka ba daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba, ni, Bɔ nira nŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asiri pɛbsigu ni mɔ'ari gba ka a wumna.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Ka ba daa du mɔ'ari maa duuri m‑paai Gyɛrasa dima tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni maa, arizimbeoo daansɔ daa yi gbala zii la ni n‑kyɛ u saani na.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: Gbala zii wa ni ka u daa kur bɛ ni; ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑gbaai u m‑ba, halli kyɔrimi gba.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Halli ba daa pun zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba, amaa u daa kur kyisiri a ni - sɔsɔ daa ka yaa n nin toaai n‑gbaai u suŋŋa.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Lala zugu u daa kpalim m‑bɛ gbala maa ni, ni zɔya zugu, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa. U daa kaasira, ka mari kuga n‑ŋmaari u maŋŋa.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Tɔ; doo wa daa pun bɛ wa'aligu n‑nya Yisa.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: ka daa zɔ na n‑tuusi u soori nti gbaanni u tooni ka tasi n‑yɛli kpeeŋŋa, ni, I ya niŋi ma la wula, Yisa nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya? Poom noori n‑ti ma, ni, i ku namsi ma.
