eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Yisa daa pun yɛli u, ni, arizimbeoo maa yim na ka kyɛ u, ka lala zugu daa kyɛ ka u yɛli lala.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Yisa daa boosi u, ni, u yuuri? ka u yɛli, ni, u yuuri boonni la Zama, ni, Ti bɔbgu n nyɛ ti.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ka u yi susiri u, ni, u di kari ba n‑yiisi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Tɔ, ka di saha maa kurikyuudima pam gba daa bɛ la kunkunni lugri ni n‑dira.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: Ka arizimbeeri maa daa susi Yisa n‑yɛli, Kyɛ ka ti kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ kurikyuudima wa ni.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ka u daa ti ba soori, ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni - kurikyuudima maa mun daa paai tusa ayi - ka ba daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom daa di ba.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa daa zɔ la yoomyooma n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni niriba, ni ban tumni puri ni la, nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem maa.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Ka niriba daa yi na ni ba ti nya yɛttɔ'aseaa kan niŋi maa. Ban daa paai na maa, ba daa nya arizindima ŋɔn yi ka kyɛ dɔsɔ la, ka u zi la suŋŋa ka yɛ u sutura ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka u.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Di daa gbaai ba dabeem pam. Ka baseeba ban nini nya maa daa yɛli daadam maa Yisa ŋɔn kari arizimbeeri n‑tibi doo maa seem, ni kurikyuudima yɛla gba.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Ka ba daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑susi u, ni, u kyɛma ka kyɛ ba tiŋŋa maa.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni m‑bakki, arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa mari dɔsɔ maa daa susi u soori ni u dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Yisa mun daa bu sakki lala, amaa ka yɛli u, ni, Kulima i toori niriba saani n‑yɛli ba ti Dugma ŋɔn zɔ i namboo ka tum seem n‑ti i.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Ka doo maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi nti piili n‑gyinni Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu maa ni n‑yɛtti Yisa ŋɔn niŋi seem n‑ti u maa, ka alahazibu daa gbaai ba zaa.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa n‑du mɔ'ari maa duuri sa maa, daadam pam n daa la'am n‑kyɛ u saani na. Ka ŋɔn daa na bɛ mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu la ni.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: ba la'aŋŋu duu kpeensɔ daa kyɛ na ka nya u - doo maa yuuri daa boonni Gyayiruusi.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: Ŋɔna n daa zɔ na n‑gbaani u tooni n‑susi u n‑yɛli, M bipumbila n ya n‑kpi, ni, Kur kyɛm na n‑zaŋŋi i nuusi m‑pa u zugu, ka u nin nya nyɔvuri ni alaafɛa.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Tɔ; ka lala zugu ka Yisa daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi, ka niriba pam gba daa dɔla m‑mugri u soori ni.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka ziim bu goaara.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: U daa pun zɔ n‑gyili tiima dima saani halli nti gyɛa, ka di u marigu zaa ka bu nya tippu. Di zaa puuni u dooru maa daa bu baligra, amaa ka lee n daa paasiri ni.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Pɔ'a wa daa wum niriba ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri Yisa yɛla seem maa, ka lala zugu u daa la'am ni zama maa n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa nti tabli n‑siisi u bunyeerigu.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: dama u daa yɛtti, ni, N yi siisi u bunyeerigu n nin nya alaafɛa.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Ka di saha maa u soobu maa siri daa ŋmaai, ka u mammaŋŋa daa baŋŋi, ni, u dooru basiya ka u nya alaafɛa.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Di saha ka Yisa gba daa baŋŋi, ni, yiiko yi la u saani n‑kpɛ sɔa, ka u ŋmaligi daadam maa ni m‑boosi, ni, Ŋɔni n siisi n neema.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa yɛli u, Nyam daadam ŋɔn daari taaba seem, ka i laa m‑boosi, ni, Ŋɔni n siisi ma? fa.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa na gɔsiri n‑gyinna, ni u ti nya ŋɔn siisi u.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Di saha ka pɔ'a maa daa gyigri ni dabeem ka mun mi din niŋi u seem, ka u daa kyɛ na nti gbaani Yisa tooni ka yɛli u din niŋi u maa zaa kasi.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, M biiya, kyɛma ni alaafɛa. I yɛdda zugu ka i nya tippu.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Ka u daa na bɛ la yɛttɔ'a ni, ka ba daa yi kpeema maa yiri ni na nti yɛli kpeema maa, ni, I bii maa kpiya; wula zugu ka i laa m‑mukkri ti karimba maa yaasa.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Yisa daa wum ba lahabaari maa, ka yɛli kpeema la, ni, Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, kur niŋma yɛdda.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: U daa bu sakki ni sɔsɔ dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi na'ala Piita, ni Gyeemsi ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Ba daa paai kpeema maa yiri ka di kur nyɛ la basaa ni, ni kunkuma ni fabla.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Ka Yisa kpɛ nti yɛli, Wula ka di nyɛ basaa ni kunkuma? Bii wa bu kpi, amaa u gbisiri ni.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: U daa tɔ'asiya lala ka di ku ba la laari; amaa u daa kari niriba maa zaa n‑yiisi, ka zaŋŋi la bii maa ba ni u ma m‑paasi u nyaandɔliba ata la zugu ka ba kpɛ bii maa ŋɔn dɔ seela la.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Di saha ka u daa gbaai bii maa nuu ka yɛli u, Talitta kuumi! , din gbinni nyɛ Biiya, n yɛtti i wa, ni, i doaama.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: U daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ wum din niŋi maa, ka laa n‑yɛli ba ni ba zaŋŋma diibu n‑ti bii maa ka u di.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Ka Yisa daa doaai bɛni n‑labi n‑kuli u maŋtoori tiŋŋa, ka u nyaandɔliba daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari daa ti paai, ka u kyaŋŋi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti gbaai wulisigu. Niriba pam daa bɛ bɛni: ban daa wum u yɛla maa, di daa kyɛ ka alahazibu gbaai ba ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, Yani ka doo wa lee m‑bamsi u yɛla wa zaa? Yam bɔ ka ba zaŋŋi n‑ti u? - halli n‑ti u yiiko ka u tumni tunkara wa?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Pa kampinta maa nŋɔa? - Mariama bii la? - Ka u sunzɔppa n nyɛ Gyeemsi, ni Gyoosɛfi, ni Gyuudasi, ni Saimoon la - halli u sunzɔpɔ'aba gba bɛ la ti saani! Lala zugu ba daa bu boori u yɛla halli beela.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Anabi kam nyari gyirima luginseelli kam zaa ni, na'ala u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa niriba, ni u dɔ'ariba, ni u yiri dima maa saani ka u bu nyara.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Ka u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tum tunkara bɛni, di daa pa la u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa baaridima beela zugri ni, ka ba daa nya alaafɛa.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Ban daa bu niŋi Naawunni yɛdda seem la n daa gyɛri u pam. Ka Yisa daa gyili tinkpansi maa zaa n‑daa wulisiri niriba.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Ka u daa ti booli u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi na nti tum ba babayiyi. U daa ti ba yiiko ni ba katti arizimbeeri m‑basira.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Ka yɛli ba, ni, ba di zaŋŋi seelli m‑mari m‑paasi ba kyɛnni maa ni, na'ala zangbeaa: ni, ba di zaŋŋi bundirigu, bee booriga, bee n‑suuri ligri gyɛfa ni.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Ba pirim pa'atipa'ati amaa ba di zi bunyeerigu m‑paasi ban yɛ seaa la zugu.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya yi ti paai yiriseaa, ya kpeema ka bɛ bɛni halli ka ya ti yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ya yi ti paai tinseaa ka ba ku sakki n‑deaai ya, ka za'asi ya yɛttɔ'a wumpu, ya yim tiŋŋa maa ni ka kpaasi ya napɔna tankpoo m‑basi n‑niŋi tiŋŋa wa ni, ka lala nin leebi din wulisiri ba halibeoo ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa ti yi n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛligu, ni, niriba takkma ba beebu ka zɔ n‑yi ba tumbeeri ni.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Ka ba daa katti arizimbeeri pam m‑basira, ka daa zaŋŋi kpaam n‑soosiri ban daa ka alaafɛ maa ka tibri ba.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Di saha ka NaaHɛroodi daa wum lala, dama Yisa yuuri daa mooliya, ka seeba daa yɛtti, ni, Gyɔni ŋɔn daa suuri niriba koom la n nyɛ u, n‑doaai kum ni n‑labi na; lala zugu n kyɛ ka u mari yiiko n‑tumni alahazibu tuma wa.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Seeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, Ay, Ilaygya n nyɛ u. ka seeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, U nyɛ la anabi, hoo kurimbuni sa anabidima ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Amaa NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa ti wum di, u daa yɛliya, Gyɔni Kɔsuura n nyɛ u, mani n daa fikki u zugu, amaa u laa n‑doaai kum ni na.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Ka di gbinni n‑daa nŋɔa: NaaHɛroodi daa pun tum n‑gbaai Gyɔni m‑ba u kutti ka kpeesi u dansarika. Hɛroodiyasi zugu ka u daa pun niŋi lala: ŋɔn mun Hɛroodiyasi daa nyɛ la u beeri Filipi pɔ'a, ka Hɛroodi daa fa u n‑di.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Dizugu Gyɔni daa yɛliya, Di ka soori ni i di pɔ'a maa: i beeri pɔ'a n nyɛ u.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Di daa nyɛ la lala zaa zugu ka Hɛroodiyasi daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi Gyɔni, ka daa boori ni u ku u; amaa ka u daa bu nyaŋŋi, NaaHɛroodi zugu.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Dama Naaba maa daa zɔri Gyɔni pam ka guri u suŋŋa, ŋɔn daa mi u yɛla zugu, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu ka zɔri Naawunni maa. Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi la daa kyɛ ka NaaHɛroodi birisi pam u sufu ni, amaa di zaa yɔri u daa wumni u yɛla maa ni sufupeelli.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Tɔ; ka dahinseaa daa ti paai ka NaaHɛroodi daa diri u dɔ'am dabsiri kyuu n‑tiri u kpamma ni u tɔppu toondaandima, ni Galilii tingbaŋŋu ninvugtibsa zaa. Lala ka Hɛroodiyasi maa daa nya soori ni u tum u yuubu.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: ka u daa kyɛ ka u bipuŋŋa kpɛ na n‑wa waa n‑ti ba, ka di daa niŋi Naaba ni u saamma maa nya'asim pam. Ka NaaHɛroodi daa boosi bipuŋŋa maa, ni, Bɔ ka i boora? Nyin boori seelli zaa n nin zaŋŋi n‑ti i.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Ka u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, N nin ti i nyin boori seelli kam zaa halli di yi nyɛ n naam tingbaŋŋu maa purigri.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Ka bipuŋŋa maa daa yi m‑boosi u ma, ni, N susim bumbɔa?, ka u ma labsi n‑yɛli u, Susim Gyɔni Kɔsuura zugu.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Ka bipuŋŋa maa daa labi yooma n‑kpɛ Naaba saani nti susi n‑yɛli, Zaŋŋma Gyɔni zugu n‑niŋi laa ni n‑ti ma na ya'abi.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Ka di daa niŋi Naa maa sufusa'aŋŋu, amaa u daa bu boori ni u mɔŋŋi bii la, ŋɔn daa pun pɔ noori n‑ti u u saamma tooni zugu.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Ka dizugu u daa tum u gbandasɔ ni u kyɛm m‑mari Gyɔni zugu maa na. Ka gbandaari la daa kyaŋŋi ya'abi n‑kpɛ dansarika maa ni nti fikki Gyɔni zugu.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na. Ka Naaba daa deaai ka n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti bii maa, ka bii maa daa mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti u ma la.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, ba daa kyɛ na nti zaŋŋi kum maa m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa labi u saani na maa, ba daa yɛli u ban tum tunseesi zaa seem la, ni ba wulisigu yɛla. Di saha ka niriba pam daa kyɛnni Yisa saani na ka labra;.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: lala maa zugu Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu nya saha halli n‑di bundirigu. Dinŋɔna u daa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛ ka ti bakki n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi luginseelli sɔsɔ ŋɔn ka ni, m‑bɛ ti kɔkɔa, ka ya vuusi.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Amaa ban daa kyɛnni maa, niriba pam daa nya ba m‑baŋŋi ban nyɛ ninvugseeba. Dizugu ka ba daa yi tinsi zaa n‑kyaŋŋi ba nɔba n‑zɔ n‑daŋŋi Yisa ni u nyaaŋŋa m‑paai ba paari ziiya.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi nyariŋŋu maa puuni na, u daa laa n‑nya daadam pam, ka u sufu daa zɔ ba namboo, dama ba daa ŋmani la peesi ban ka ŋɔn guri ba, ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑wulisiri ba yɛla pam.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Ka saha ŋɔn daa pun yuui ka yuŋŋu zibri maa, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: I bu ya ti niriba maa soori ka ba kyaŋŋi? Ba nin wurim n‑kpɛ tinkpanseesi an gyiligi kpa maa ni, ni tiŋŋa maa zaa ni, n‑da bundirigu n‑di.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di. Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, Ammi, i boori la ti kyaŋŋi nti da ligri pam paanu na n‑diisi ba, bee? Ay! - ti ligri ku paai lala.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ka u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, Paanukpila ala ka ya mara? Ya kyɛm nti nya! Ban daa gɔsi n‑nya maa, ba daa labi u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Paanukpila anu ka ti mara; ni zaasim ayi.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Di saha ka Yisa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba kyɛ ka niriba maa puri ba mansi n‑zinni mɔmaasa la zugu.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Ka ba daa zinni kpa sa ni kpa sa, kɔbkɔbga bee pisinunu n‑tɔŋŋi taaba.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi paanu anu ni zaasim ayi maa n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni, ka purigi paanudima maa, ni zaasim maa m‑paasi, ka yi zaŋŋi di n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba tɔri n‑ti ba.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: ka nyaandɔliba maa daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la, ni zaasikyɛra la, m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa ŋɔbi lala paanu maa mun daa nyɛ la dɔppa tusa anu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa, sahanseaa u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa, Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni la n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri sa n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti ya n‑zibi la, nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la mɔ'ari maa sunsukkuni, ka Yisa daa kpalim m‑bɛ mɔ'aduuri ni u kɔkɔa.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Di saha ka pɛbsigu daa zeeri ba tooni n‑nyaŋŋi nyariŋŋu maa koom maa ni, n‑ta'ai ba kyɛnni, ka di daa mugsi ba pam; ka Yisa daa nya lala. Di daa paari bɛkyikyeoo saha, ka u daa kyɛ ba saani na n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu, ka daa ya yakki ba n‑gaari.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Amaa ba daa nya u ka u kyɛnni koom zugu, ka ba zaa daa yɛli, ni, kpiinyisigi n nyɛ u, ka daa ŋmɛ kuŋŋa.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Ban daa nya u ka di gyɛri ba maa, Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑yɛli ba, Ya mari sufukpeemni ka di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya! Ka mani nla.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Ka u kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni ka pɛbsigu maa daa kyɛ zeebu. Ka di laa n‑gyɛri ba, halli nti yakki.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Ba daa na bu baŋŋi ŋɔn diisi niriba tusa anu maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ka di zugu ba sufu maa daa bu zuligi na.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa, ba daa ti paai Gyɛnɛsarɛti tiŋŋa na, ka daa nya nyarima loobu ziiya bɛni, n‑lɔ ka yi na.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Ban daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni la, bɛni niriba maa daa baŋŋi ya'abi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: ka ba daa zɔ n‑gyili tingbaŋŋu maa zaa ka gbaai baaridima ziibu na. Ba daa doonni ba bundoosi zugu n‑ziiri n‑kyɛnni ban daa wum, ni, u bɛ seela zaa.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa, bee tinseaa puuni, halli m‑bɛ puri ni gba, ba daa zaŋŋi ban daa ka alaafɛ la n‑dooli daasi ni nti susi u, ni, u kur kyɛ ka ba siisi u kparigu nangbanni, ka ban daa siisi zaa daa nya alaafɛa.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ninvugseeba bɛ kpa, n‑ku kpi halli ka ba ti nya Naawunni naam kyɛnni na ni yiiko.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Dabsa ayoobu ŋɔn daa ti gaari, Yisa daa zaŋŋi Piita ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni n‑zi ba n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zɔtitaari zugu ka ba daa ti bɛ ba kɔkɔa. Ban daa ti gɔsiri maa, ba daa ti nya ka Yisa takki ni.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: ka u neema daa pɛligi m‑pɛligi nyain! - n‑gaari ninsaala ŋɔn nin toaai m‑peaai neema ka a pɛligi seem.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Dinsaha ka u nyaandɔliba ata maa daa nya anabi Ilaygya, ni Musa, ka ba daa bɛ ni n‑sɔsiri ni Yisa.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Ti Karimba, ti kpa wa beesigu veela! Kyɛ ka ti ti suga ata - i dinni yinni, ni Musa dinni, ni Ilaygya mun dinni.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Dabeem daa gbaai Piita ni u taaba halli, ka u daa bu mi ŋɔn yɛtti seem.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Di saha ka sangbaŋŋu daa sigi na nti ligi ba, ka kukɔri daa tɔ'asiri sangbaŋŋu maa ni, ni, M bii maa nŋɔa, n wuri u ni: ya wumma u yɛligu.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Ka lala saha deede ka ba daa gɔsi n‑gyili ban daa zɛ seelli maa zaa, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; kulla Yisa kɔkɔ n daa zɛ ba saani.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ka ban daa ti yi zoori zugu n‑sigri na, u daa kpaamni ba, ni, Ya di yɛli sɔ yan nya seelli wa, see ka man Ninsaala Biiya ti yisigi kum ni.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Dizugu ba daa deaai u noori wa ka daa bu yɛli sɔa, amaa ka kur boosiri taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Ka ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, Wula zugu ka ti karimbadima yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya ya n‑daŋŋi n‑kyɛ na pɔi, ka Masiiya naayi paai na.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ilaygya siri ya daŋŋi n‑kyɛ na n‑sɛ'asi buna kam zaa. Amaa wula zugu ka Litaafi maa yɛli, ni, Ninsaala Bii nin di wahala pam, ka ba laa n‑zeem u m‑paasi?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya ni, ni, Ilaygya pun kyɛ na ka ba daa niŋi u ban daa boori seem, hoo Litaafi maa ŋɔn daa pun sɔbi u yɛla seem la.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ka ban daa ti paai Yisa nyaandɔliseeba la ŋɔn daa bɛ seela, ba daa nya ka daadam pam gyiligi ba, ka karimbaseeba daa ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑tiri nyaandɔliba maa.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa ti nya Yisa, di daa niŋi ba alahazibu ka ba daa zɔ n‑kyɛnni u saani na nti puusi u.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Nangbankpeemni bɔ ka ya ni ban ŋmeeri maa.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Ka daadam maa puuni dɔsɔ daa labsi n‑yɛli, N Karimba, m bii wa mari la arizimbeoo ŋɔn kyɛ ka u bu tɔ'asira;.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: dizugu ka m mari u n‑kyɛ i saani na. Halli arizimbeoo maa yi ti yisigi u ka yi zaŋŋni u m‑basi la tiŋŋa, ka nantɔfufugri yiiri u noori ni na, ka u dumni u nyina, ka u niŋŋa zaa kpaŋŋi. N da yɛli i nyaandɔliba wa, ni, ba karim arizinni maa m‑basi amaa ba da bu nyaŋŋi n‑kari u.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ah! Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa! N ya n‑yuui la wula ya saani? Bee man nin di sugru ya saani ka di yuui wula? Yan mari bii maa n‑kyɛ n saani na.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ka ba daa mari u n‑kyɛ u saani na. Arizimbeoo maa ŋɔn daa nya Yisa, u daa lusi bii maa hoo kpisinkpiiri, ka bidibga maa daa lu tiŋŋa m‑bilimna, ka nantɔfufugri yiiri u noori ni.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi bii maa ba, ni, Dooru wa piili u ka di yuui, bee? Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Di daa piili ka u na nyɛ la bibila.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Napɔŋŋu paai seem ka arizinni maa zaŋŋni u m‑basiri bugum ni, bee koom ni, ni u ya ku u. Zɔm ti namboo ka suŋŋi ti, di yi nin toaai n‑niŋi.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Ni, di yi ti toaai, walla!, ni, Bunseelli kam nin toaai n‑niŋi yɛdda daana saani.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Ka bii maa ba tasi yooma n‑yɛli, N niŋi yɛdda, amaa n yɛdda maa bu zoaai ni. Kur suŋŋma ma ka n yɛdda paasira.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi, ni, daadam maa ya n‑lim ba na, ka dizugu u daa yɛli arizimbeoo maa, ni, Nyin arizinni tubkpira ni zɔligu daana wa, yim na ka di laa n‑kpɛ bidibga wa yaasa.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Arizinni maa daa yisigiya ka kyɛ ka bii maa daa kaasi pampam ka lu kpisinkpiiri; ka arizinni maa daa yi ka kyɛ u, halli ka bii maa daa kpalim n‑dɔya hoo kum, ka niriba maa pam daa yɛli, ni, u kpi ni.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Amaa Yisa daa gbaai bii maa nuu n‑suŋŋi u ka u daa doaai n‑zaani.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa Yisa daa ti bɛ duu ni, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kpɛ na nti sɔ'ai m‑boosi u, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka tirima guui arizimbeoo maa karibu.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Seelli ka ni n nin toaai n‑kari arizinni wa taabu na'ala wunsusigu kɔkɔa.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa ni u nyaaŋŋa daa yi bɛni nti goori n‑dɔla Galilii tingbaŋŋu sɔya. Yisa daa bu boori ni sɔ baŋŋi ŋɔn bɛ seela.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: dama u daa wulisiri u nyaandɔliba la; ka yɛtti ba, ni, Ninsaala Bii n nyɛ ma, ka ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑niŋi ninvugseeba nuu ni ni ba ti deaai ma n‑ku ma. N kum maa nyaaŋŋa di nin paai dabsa ata ka n nin laa n‑doaai.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi u yɛttɔ'a wa gbinni, ka dabeem mun daa mari ba ni ba boosi u di gbinni yaasa.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Ban daa ti paai Kapirinyum la ba daa bɛ la duu ni ka Yisa daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Nangbankpeemni bɔ ka ya da ŋmeeri soori ni la.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Amaa ba sɔsɔ daa bu labsi u seelli, dama soori maa ni ka ba yi ga'ari ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba taaba kpamni.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Yisa daa zini tiŋŋa ka booli u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la na nti yɛli ba, Sɔ yi boori ni u leebi toondaana, see ka u sigsi u maŋŋa n‑leebi sɔkam daabri.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Ka u daa yiisi bii yinni n‑zaali ba tooni.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: U daa zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑gɔbi bii maa ka yɛli ba, Sɔ yi gyaari bibila wa taabu man yuuri zugu, mani ka di daama n gyaari. Sɔ mun yi gyaari ma, pa man kɔkɔ ka u gyaari, amaa u gyaari la Ŋɔn tum ma na la gba.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa yɛli u, Ti Karimba, ti da nya la dɔsɔ ka u zaŋŋi i yuuri n‑katti arizimbeeri, ka ti da yɛli u, ni, u di laa m‑paasi lala yaasa, dama u bu dɔli ti.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya di goaari u, dama sɔ yi ti tum tunkarigu man yuuri zugu, di daama ku laa n‑ŋmaligi ya'abi ka tu ma.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Sɔ yi bu yi ti nyaaŋŋa, di daama nyɛ la ti nira.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, sɔ yi zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ya, yan nyɛ man Masiiya la niriba zugu, di daama ku kɔŋŋi u tuma laara.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Amaa sɔ yi yɔmsi bibisi wa yinni ka u kyɛ u yɛdda niŋbu ka za'asi ma di zugu di bu veeli gba. Lala nira nin ti nya ka ba yi da zaŋŋi nɛtitaari n‑yeeli u nangoori ka daai u m‑basi mɔ'ari ni, ni, di nin ti sɔ u beesigu.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Dizugu i nuu yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin ŋmaam ka m‑basi. I yi mari nuu yinni ka mari nyɔvuri, lala veela n‑gaari ni i mari nuusi ayi ka kpɛ wahala ni, bugum bun bu kpiiri ziiya.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: I nɔbri yi kyɛ ka i tum tumbeoo, nyin ŋmaam ka m‑basi. I yi mari napɔŋŋu yinni ka mari nyɔvuri, lala veela n‑gaari ni i mari napɔna ayi ka kpɛ wahala ni.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: I nimbiri yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin lukkma ka m‑basi. I yi mari nimbiri yinni ka kpɛ Naawunni naam ni, lala veela n‑gaari ni i mari nini ayi ka kpɛ wahalaseaa kan ka naari.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: seelli ka bugum bu kpiira, ka zunzuya kur kpalim n‑ŋɔbra.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Bugum nin maali sɔkam zaa tuma hoo yaarim ŋɔn maanni zeeri seem la.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yaarim nya'asa, amaa ka biim maa yi ti yi ka kyɛ ka, bɔ n nin laa n‑toaai n‑kyɛ ka ka mari biim yaasa? Ya sɔkam mari yaarim nya'asim maa taabu ya beebu ni, lala ka ya nin la'am m‑bɛ ni ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka ni.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ka Yisa ni u nyaaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni m‑pɔtti Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa maa, ba daa paai tinkpanseesi an boonni Bɛtfeegyi ni Bɛtani la, seesi an zɛ Ɔlivii zoori kpaŋŋa. Di saha ka Yisa daa daŋŋi n‑tum u nyaandɔliba ayi ni ba gaari tooni.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: ni, Ya kyɛm nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa kan zɛ ya tooni wa. Ya yi ti paai bɛni, ya nin nya ka buŋŋa lɔ n‑zɛya, bumbilisɔ ban na zi m‑ba u; yan lɔrigma u m‑mari na.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Sɔ mun yi boosi ya, ni, ni wula ka ya lɔrigri u? yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori u, ka mun nin labsi u na yooma.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti nya bumbila maa ka u lɔ n‑zɛ samanni ni n‑tabi yiri la za'anɔri kpaŋŋa. Ban daa yi ya lɔrigi u maa.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: ninvugseeba ban zɛ n‑tabi bɛni daa nya ba ka boosi ba, ni, Bɔ ka ya niŋni maa, bumbila maa ka ya lɔrigri wa.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli ba Yisa ŋɔn pun yɛli ba seem maa, ka dɔppa maa daa kyɛ ka ba lɔrigi u n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Di saha ka Farisiisi maa seeba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm na, ban ni zaaligu karimbaseeba.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Ban daa zaŋŋi buŋŋa maa n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na maa, ba daa zaŋŋi ba kyinkyina m‑pa buŋŋa maa nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Niriba wa ŋɔn daa la'am Yisa saani na la, ba daa nya ka u nyaandɔliba maa seeba daa diri diibu ni nuda'ari - di gbinni nyɛ la ba daa bu peaari ba nuusi hoo Farisii dima kaari ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem la.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Niriba pam gba daa yeaai ba sutura n‑yɛligi n‑dooli soori maa zugu, ka seeba mun daa ŋma tiisi wula puri ni n‑zaŋŋi a gba n‑yɛligi n‑dooli soori maa ni.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Ban mun yi bu dɔli ba soori m‑peaai ba nuusi ni nimmoaa, ba ku sakki n‑di bundirigu;.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ tooni, ni seeba gba ban daa bɛ nyaaŋŋa n‑dɔli maa, daa piili n‑kpalimna n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Hoosana! Tiliginsim Daana! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn mari ti Dugma yuuri na!.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: ka ba yi yi daa na see ka ba peaai ba mansi lala pɔi ka naayi di: ka ba yaadima kaari laa m‑paasi pam, hoo ban peaari kɔbeesi ni dugri ni tasilaasi seem.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ti yaaba NaaDauda naam labri n‑kyɛnni na: tabarikala! Tiliginsim Daana gyirima bɛ la saazugu! Hoosana! .

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Tɔ, ka lala niriba daa boosi Yisa, ni, Wula ka i nyaandɔliba maa bu dɔli ti yaadima kaari la, amaa ka diri ni nuda'ari.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Tɔ; lala ka Yisa daa kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑paai Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri. U daa gɔsi n‑gyiligi zii kam, amaa yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa pun ya zibi maa zugu u daa yi n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa maa ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya birikɔ'anu dima yɛla seem la; ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun yi n saani Ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai maa, ba daa yi Bɛtani n‑labri timpuuni na. Di saha ka kɔm daa mari Yisa.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Ban wulisiri la zaaligu wa, Ba gyeemni ma la yɔri; Di pa la bunseelli zaa Na'ala ninsaala yɛligu kɔkɔa. - Dama ya kyɛ Naawunni zaaligu ka dɔli saalidima kaari.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: ka u daa gɔsi u tooni sa n‑nya kinkaŋŋa, ni ka vaari, ka ka zibgi. Ka u daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai ka na, ni u ti nya ka waliya? Amaa ŋɔn daa ti paai tii maa ni, u daa nya vaari kɔkɔa, dama pa kinkama walibu saha n‑daa la.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli kinkaŋŋa maa, ni, Sɔ ku laa n‑di i wala yaasa, halli walli yinni gba! Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa wum ŋɔn yɛli seem wa.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Yarima muna - ya za'asiri Naawunni soori, ni ya ti dɔli ya mammaŋŋa kaari maa: yam nla, bee?.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ban daa ti paai Gyɛrusalɛm la, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, n‑daa ti piili n‑katti ban daa daari ka koosiri bɛni maa. U daa labgi n‑labgi ninvugseeba ban daa takkri ligri teebulidima, ni ŋmankoosiriba kugsi.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Ay! - Musa daa pun bu yɛli ya lala? - ni, Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima, ni, ŋɔn tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeoo zaŋŋi n‑kpa u ba bee u ma, yan kuum u.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: ka u daa bu sakki ni ba ziiri ziya ni neema n‑dɔli Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa n‑gatta.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Amaa yarima wulisiri, ni, nira ŋɔn laa n‑zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑suŋŋi u ba ni u ma, u yi ti yɛli ba, ni, Bunni maa nyɛ la Kɔribaani , bee Naawunni dinni.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Di saha ka u daa wulisi niriba maa, ni, Di pun sɔbi n‑zaali Naawunni Litaafi maa ni, ni, N Yiri wa ka ba nin booli, ni, Wunsusigu Yiri n‑ti duniya dima zaa. Amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑leebi ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela. .

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: ka mɔŋŋi u ba ni u ma dizugu, ni, u ka taalli.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni tiŋŋa maa karimbadima daa wum lala lahabaari, ka di saha ka ba daa piili m‑boori sɔseaa ban nin nya n‑ku Yisa. Dabeem daa gbaai ba, dama di daa gyɛri daadam maa zaa Yisa wulisigu maa zugu.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa bakki bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ya'ari, halli nti yakki Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la m‑bɛ ni. Ka zama daa la'am n‑kyɛ u saani na yaasa, ka u daa wulisi ba hoo ŋɔn daa yi pun wulisiri ba seem la.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Tɔ, ka zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti paai, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑booli niriba maa u saani na nti yɛli ba, ni, Ya wumma! Ya zaa baŋŋma!.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Ka Farisiseeba daa kyɛ u saani na ni ba ti da u gbinni. Ba daa boosi u, ni, Yɛlim ti: ammi, doo mari soori ni u nin toaai n‑kari u pɔ'a n‑yiisi, bee.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaari asusuba maasiri ka ba daa doaai n‑yi na. Ka ba daa kyɛnni soori zugu nti nya kinkaŋŋa maa ka ka pun gbirigi n‑kuui n‑kpi zaŋŋi m‑paai ka nya'a ni.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Ka Yisa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, Wula ka Musa daa zaali n‑ti ya.

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa bakki ka kyɛ daadam maa n‑kpɛ yiri maa ni maa, u nyaandɔliba daa susi u, ni, u wulisim ba yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Ka Piita daa teaai Yisa ŋɔn sa yɛli seem la ka yɛli u, N Karimba, gɔsima! Tiseaa nyin sa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli ka yɛlibeoo la na pun kpi ni.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Musa daa ti ti soori ni doo nin toaai n‑sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti u pɔ'a ka di wulisi, ni, u yiisi u.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya gba yam bu gaari niriba wa dinni maa zugu, fa? Ya bu baŋŋi di gbinni? Ni, bunseelli din kpɛ ninsaala puuni ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka ninvugkasi leebi la da'ari daana.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Yisa ŋɔn labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya niŋma yɛdda n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya tuppuwumni la zugu ka Musa daa sɔbi zaaligu wa n‑ti ya maa.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Dama lala buna bu kpeeri u sufu ni; ay! - u puuri ni ka a kpeera, ka gatti n‑yi u ningbina maa, bangyira ziiya. Dinŋɔ ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisi, ni, bundirigu zaa sumni diibu ka bu kyisira.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya sɔ zaa yi yɛli zoori wa, ni, ka doaam saazugu n‑lɔbi ka maŋŋa m‑basi teeku ni, ka lala nira ka zilisigu u sufu ni, ka niŋi yɛdda, ni, ŋɔn yɛli seem wa nin niŋi, di siri nin niŋi n‑ti u.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Amaa duniya piiligu ni, buna zaa naaŋŋu la saha, Naawunni daa naam ba la doowa ni pɔ'a, hoo Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli seem la.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, Din yiiri ninsaala puuni na muna, dina n sa'amni ninvugkasi maa.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu n ya yɛli ya ni, ni, ya yi puusi Naawunni ka susi seelli, ka niŋi yɛdda, ni, ya nin nya ka, ya nin nya yan susi bunseelli maa.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Ni, dinŋɔ zugu ka doo nin kyɛ u ba ni u ma, ka di pɔ'a, ka ba la'am m‑bɛ ni.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Dama teesibeeri zaa yiiri ninsaala puuni, ni u sufu ni, na; ana n kyɛ ka u tumni tumbeeri zaa - u tumni zina, n‑faara, n‑zuura, n‑kuuri niriba, m‑boori seeba pɔ'aba;.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Ya mun yi zaani m‑puusiri Naawunni, see ka ya teaai ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tum tumbeoo zaa n‑ti ya la yɛla, ka kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u; ka ti Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni gba nin kyɛ ya mammaŋŋa tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ya.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: kaman bunni yinni. Dinzugu ba laa m‑pa niriba ayi amaa hoo bunni yinni.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: m‑mari beem, ni zinsuŋŋu, ni putoo; m‑ma'ari ziri nti yɔmsi niriba, ka kur dɔli u maŋtoori sufu yuubu; n‑tuuri ninsaala ni Naawunni, m‑mari karimbaani ni gyɛrilim.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa laa m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni na. Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑gyinni bɛni, ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni tiŋŋa maa karimbadima ni kpamma maa daa kyɛ u saani na.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Dinŋɔna Naawunni ŋɔn la'am bunseesi ni a leebi bunyinni maa, ninsaala nira di wɔligi a.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Di daa bu yuui ka daadam pam daa laa n‑la'am Yisa saani na yaasa, ka daa ka seelli ni ba di. Ka Yisa daa booli u nyaandɔliba maa u saani na n‑yɛli ba.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: m‑boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum i lala tuma maa? Bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Ka ban daa ti labi n‑kpɛ yiri maa ni, u nyaandɔliba maa daa boosi u yɛlli wa gbinni.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: ni, N sufu bu maai niriba wa zugu, dama ba bɛ n saani ka di paai dabsa ata ka ka seelli din di.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Yisa daa yisigi n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya tiŋŋa la. U daa kpɛ yiri ni ka bu boori ni sɔsɔ baŋŋi, ni, u bɛ bɛni. Amaa u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, N nin daŋŋi m‑boosi ya gba boosigu yinni kɔkɔ pɔi; ya yi ti labsi ma ka n nin naayi labsi n‑ti ya yan boosi seem la.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Doo yi yiisi u pɔ'a ka laa n‑di pɔ'asɔ, u tum zina n‑nya taalli u pɔ'akurigu saani.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: N yi kyɛ ka ba kuli yɔri, baseeba nin ligi soori ni, dama baseeba yi la wa'aligu na.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Pɔ'asɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka mari bipuŋŋa ka arizimbeoo daa bɛ u ni. Ba daa dɔ'ai pɔ'a wa Fɔnisii tingbaŋŋu maa ni, ya'aseaa kan bɛ Siriiya naam fukumsi ni, u pa Izraɛli pɔ'a. Lala pɔ'a maa daa wum Yisa lahabaari ka daa yi yooma n‑kyɛ u saani na nti gbaani m‑puusi u, ka daa susi u, ni, u karim arizimbeoo maa n‑yiisi ka kyɛ u bii maa.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Tɔ: ka ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom la? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani bee ninsaalidima saani? - Ya labsim n‑yɛli ma.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Lala yaasa, pɔ'a gba yi za'asi u sira ka yi n‑kuli dɔsɔa, pɔ'a maa gba tum taalli.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, yani ka sɔ nin nya bundirigu moo wa ni n‑diisi niriba wa zaa?.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Amaa Yisa daa yɛli u, Kyɛ ka ti daŋŋi n‑diisi biisi la. Di bu tu ni ti zaŋŋi biisi bundirigu maa m‑basi babisi la.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Ka ba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑tiri taaba, ni, Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?;.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Sahanseaa yaasa ka ba daa mari bibisi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ni u zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑siisi ba, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa nyikki ba di zugu.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, Ya mari la boroboro abala? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Abayɔpɔi ka ti mara.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni, n Daana, amaa babisi ban dɔya ku kɔŋŋi ba piisi biisi sa'akyɛba an luri tiŋŋa la.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: ka ti yi sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ninsaalidima saani ..

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yisa mun ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi lala maa, u sufu daa bu maai; ka u daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ya kyɛ ka bibisi maa kyɛ n saani na, yan di ta'ari ba! Dama Naawunni naam nyɛ la banŋɔ taabu dinni.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Ka u daa kyɛ ka niriba maa zini tiŋŋa ka daa zaŋŋi boroboro ayɔpɔi maa ka daa pɛ'ai Naawunni ka ŋmaasi a n‑tisi u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba zaŋŋi n‑tɔri daadam maa. Ka ba daa niŋi lala.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Mboo! Labsigu seaa nyin labsi wa zugu, kur labma n‑kuli; i nin paai n‑nya ka arizinni maa yiya, ka kyɛ i bipuŋŋa maa.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑ti Yisa, ni, Ti bu mi. Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ka dinŋɔna man gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, sɔ yi bu sakki Naawunni naam yɛligu maa hoo bibila ŋɔn sakkri seem la, u ku toaai n‑kpɛ naam maasim maa ni.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Ba daa mari zaasim beela m‑paasi, ka u daa puusi anŋɔ gba zugu ka daa yɛli, ni, ba deaama n‑tɔri.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Ka dizugu u daa kuli nti nya u bii maa ka u dɔ la gadu zugu; ka arizimbeoo maa siri pun yi ni ka kyɛ u.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Dinsaha ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑gɔbi biisi maa ka daa susi Naawunni n‑ti ba.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ka sɔkam zaa daa di n‑tigi; niriba maa mun daa paai tusa anaasi. Ba daa ŋɔbi ka kyɛ, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa vaai bunkyɛba la m‑paali peeri ayɔpɔi. Ka Yisa daa kyeoosi niriba maa ka ba kyaŋŋi.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yi Taaya n‑dɔli Saidoon tiŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi, ka daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu maa puuni soori ni u ti paai Galilii mɔ'ari zii maa na.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ya n‑kyaŋŋi u kyɛnni maa yaasa, dɔsɔ n daa zɔ n‑kyɛ u saani na nti gbaani u tooni ka boosi u, ni, N Karimbasuŋŋu! N ya niŋi la wula n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: Ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi Dalimanuuta tiŋŋa ziiya.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u tuba daa bu wumna, ka u bu laa n‑nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri suŋŋa: ba daa mari lala doo maa n‑zi u m‑paai na nti susi Yisa, ni, u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Wula ka i booli ma, ni, ninvugsuŋŋu? Sɔ n ka ni n‑nyɛ bunsuŋŋu, na'ala Naawunni kɔkɔa.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Di saha ka Farisiseeba daa ti yi n‑kyɛ na nti ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni ni Yisa. Ba daa boori la ba bɛri u, lala zugu ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, u niŋma sisiri ka di wulisiri, ni, Naawunni mi u yɛla.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa u kɔkɔ ka kyɛ zama maa, ka zaŋŋi u nubisi n‑tum doo maa tuba ni; ka tusi nantoori n‑zaŋŋi n‑siisi doo maa zulimni.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Tɔ; ka i mi zaaligu maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ni, Di ku ninsaala.; ni, Di tum zina.; ni, Di zu.; ni, Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.; ni, di yɔmsi niriba n‑di ba neema; ni, Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima. .

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Yisa daa nyuusiya pam sufusa'aŋŋu zugu, ka yɛli, Wula ka zuna niriba wa kur boori alahazibu sisiri? Ay! Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya ku nya lala sisiri.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Ka daa gɔsi saazugu n‑nyurim u lɔŋŋu ni, ka yɛli doo maa, ni, Efatta'a! - dinŋɔ gbinni nyɛ la Yoaama.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ka doo maa yɛli, N Karimba, anŋɔ zaa ka n daa pun dɔli ka na nyɛ na'akyimbila.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Ka u daa basi ba ka labi n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ni u ti lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa ya'ari sa.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Ka doo maa daa piili n‑wumna; ka din daa ta'ari u yɛttɔ'a maa gba daa lakki, ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a kasikasi.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi doo maa ni sufumaasim, ka yɛli u, Yɛlli yinni kɔkɔ n kpalim ka i kɔŋŋi: kyɛma nti koosi i marigu zaa n‑zaŋŋi n‑tɔri faradima, ka i arizikki maali n‑zoaai Naawunni saani. Ka kyɛ na n‑dɔli man nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa tam ka bu mari boroboro n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, kulla boroboro yinni kɔkɔ n daa kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa puuni.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, ba di yɛli lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din niŋi maa n‑ti sɔa; amaa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti ba nimmoaa seem maa zaa yɔri, ba daa yɛtti niriba m‑paasira.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Tɔ, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yɛli lala maa, doo maa daa miisi u nindaa, ka daa bakki ni sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Ka Yisa daa kpaamni ba n‑yɛli, Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni NaaHɛroodi dinni gba.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Ka ban daa wum lala lahabaari maa muna, di daa niŋi ba zaa alahazibu ka ba daa yɛtti, ni, U tumni seelli kam zaa la suŋŋa. ni, U toaai n‑kyɛ ka tubkpiradima wumna, ka mugsi ni zɔlisi gba tɔ'asira.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi u nyaandɔliba maa ka yɛli ba, Di siri nin tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri ni taaba, ni, u yɛtti la lala ban ka boroboro la zugu.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ka yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ daa kpeesi u nyaandɔliba maa alahazibu, amaa Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, M biisi, di tɔ pam ni nira ti kpɛ Naawunni naam maasim ni!.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Amaa Yisa daa mi ban tɔ'asiri seelli wa, ka dizugu u daa boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya tɔ'asiri, ni, yan ka boroboro la zugu? Ammi, ya na bu mi, bee, m‑baŋŋi di gbinni? Ya yam maa na bu neaai, bee wula?.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ seeriga vɔbila ni m‑puli n‑yi ka bundaana na ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Ya nyɛ la seeba ban mari nini ka bu nyara; m‑mari tuba ka bu wumna! Ammi, ya bu teaai.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Di daa gyɛri u nyaandɔliba maa pam yɛlli wa zugu, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, Ka ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑tuligi.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: man ŋɔn daa ŋmaasi boroboro anu n‑tɔri niriba tusa anu la? Ka an daa kpalim la muna - peeri ala ka ya daa vaai? Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, piiya ni ayi.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑yiiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni na la, u nyaandɔliba yinni daa yɛli u, N Karimba, gɔsim kugnyalima wa ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni ban daa zaŋŋi a m‑mɛ duri ni gooma seem.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Yisa daa gɔsi ba ka yɛli, Dinŋɔ tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani, amaa Naawunni saani di bu tɔa. Dama Naawunni ku kɔŋŋi bunseelikam niŋbu.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Ka u laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Ka man daa ŋmaasi boroboro ayɔpɔi n‑tisi niriba tusa anaasi la, peeri ala ka ya daa vaai, an daa kpalim la?, ni, abayɔpɔi.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba nyɛsa ni, ni, Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑daa sa u tiwanna puu. U daa mɛ la goomni n‑gyiligi puu maa, n‑gbi bɔkku tiŋni, ban ŋmakkri tiwala maa seela n‑nya a koom, ka laa n‑ti sugri puu maa puuni. U daa maali di zaa ka zaŋŋi puu maa n‑ti ninvugseeba, ni ba tumma puu maa tuma n‑ti u; ka u daa naayi kyaŋŋi n‑gɔa.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, I bu nya mɛkara wa? Tɔ: dahinseaa kyɛnni na ka a kugseaa ku kpalim n‑tɔm ka kugkpeaa zugu, amaa a zaa nin ti ŋmɛ n‑wurim n‑lu tiŋŋa na.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Dinsaha ka Piita daa yɛli u, Nyama, tirima nyɛ seeba ban kyɛ bunseelikam m‑basi ka dɔli i.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Ni, Ka ya na bu baŋŋi, fa.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Tɔ, ka tiisi maa wala saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani, nti deaai puu maa diibu na.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa u daa zini Ɔlivii Zoori zugu, Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ya'ari sa, ka Piita ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni ni Anduru daa kyɛ u saani na nti sɔ'ai m‑boosi u, ni.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, sɔ yi bɛ ni n‑kyɛ u yiri, bee u babiisi ni u mabiisi, bee u ba ni u ma, bee u biisi, bee u puri, man zugu, ni Naawunni yɛligu wa zugu.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Ba daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Bɛtsaida, bɛni ka niriba daa mari zoonsɔ n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ka bɛlim u, ni, u siisi u.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Amaa ba daa gbaai daabri maa m‑bu u ka kari u n‑labsi yɔri na.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Yɛlim ti wula saha ka dinŋɔ nin niŋi. Ka bumbɔ nin ti niŋi n‑wulisi, ni, anŋɔ zaa ya paai a tariga.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: di daama nin ti nya bunseesi an gaari anŋɔ zaa pampaŋŋɔ saha - u nin nya yiya kɔbkɔbga, ni mabiisi ni babiisi, ni madima ni badima ni biisi, ni puri kɔbkɔbga m‑paasi - amaa ka u nya wahala ni vɔlisa gba: ka saha din kyɛnni na u nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Ka Yisa daa gbaai zooma maa nuu m‑mari u n‑yi tinkpaŋŋa maa ni na ka daa tusi nantoori n‑niŋi doo maa nini pɔi, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu ka naayi boosi u, ni, I nya seela.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Ka puu maa daana daa laa n‑tum sɔ, ka ba gbaai ŋɔn gba m‑bu n‑ŋmɛ u zugu ka zeem u.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya baŋŋma suŋŋa ka di kyɛ ka sɔ yɔmsi ya.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Amaa ban daŋŋi tooni muna - ba pam nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa, ka ban kpalimni nyaaŋŋa mun nin ti daŋŋi tooni.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Ka doo maa daa wukki n‑gɔsi ka yɛli, Ii! N nyari niriba, amaa ba ŋmanni la tiisi n‑kyɛnni n‑gyiligra.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑tum sɔ, amaa ba daa ku ŋɔna. U daa tum niriba pam ka ba daa bu baseeba, ka ku seeba.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Niriba pam nin zaŋŋi n yuuri n‑kyɛ na, ni, Mani nla! ka ba nin yɔmsi niriba pam m‑basi.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa bɛ la soori ni n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm. Yisa daa gaari tooni, u nyaandɔliba mun daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa ka di daa gyɛri ba pam; halli dabeem daa gbaai ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ba zaa. Yisa daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n yi kpaŋŋa ba kɔkɔ nti yɛli ba yɛliseesi an ti ya paai u.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Tɔ, ka Yisa daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u nuu m‑pa doo maa nini ni yaasa. Dinŋɔna doo maa daa gbaai la gɔsibu ni nimmoaa, ka u nini maa daa neaai ka u daa nyari suŋŋa.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Ninvugyinni n daa kpalim ni u tum, u toori biiya ŋɔn daa wuri sɔ pam la. Ŋɔna ka u daa naayi tum, dama u daa teesi, ni, Ba nin ti m maŋtoori bii maa gyirima.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya yi ti wumni tɔppu damni, bee di lahabaya, yan di kyɛ ka yam-ŋmaa gbaai ya, dama lala nin niŋi pɔi, amaa pa naari maa nla.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: U daa yɛli ba, Ya wumma: ti wa la Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, bɛni ka ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑ti gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima nuusi ni. Ban nin ti kari n sariya n‑zaŋŋi ma taalli, ni, n sumni kum, ka naayi zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u kulima yiri ni, ka yɛli u, Di labi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Amaa tuntumniba maa daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u ka faari maa nin kpalim ti dinni.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Tinsi dima nin yisigi n‑tuusi taaba tɔppu, ni nadima gba nin tuusi taaba. Tingbaŋŋu maa nin damni kpa, ni kpa, ni kpa sa; ka kɔm nin ti lu; amaa anŋɔ zaa ti ya bɛ ni hoo pɔ'apudaana wɔlisigu piiligu beerim ŋɔn bɛ seem la.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: ni ba maali ma ansarisi, n‑tusi nantoori n‑niŋi ma, n‑feebi ma ka ku ma. Amaa daba ata daari n nin yisigi kum ni.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi ni ba ti paai tinkpanseesi an pɔri Filipi Sizarii tiŋŋa la ni. Ka ban kpalim m‑bɛ soori ni la, u daa boosi ba, ni, Niriba maa yɛtti, ni, n nyɛ la ŋɔni.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Ka lala zugu ba daa gbaai u n‑ku n‑zaŋŋi u n‑lɔbi puu maa kpaŋŋa.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Amaa ya gbaam ya mansi, dama ba nin ti mari ya n‑niŋi kootu dima nuu ni, ba nin ti bu ya la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka ba nin kari ya sariya gɔmnadima ni nadima tooni. Di zaa nin niŋi man zugu, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi suŋŋa.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Ka Zɛbidii biisi, Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni la, daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, Ti Karimba, ti kyɛ la i saani na, ni, bunseelli m bɛ ni ka ti boora, ammi i nin niŋi n‑ti ti, bee.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Baseeba yɛtti, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura n nyɛ i; seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya; ka seeba mun yɛtti, ni, kurimbuni sa anabisɔ n nyɛ i.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Tɔ; ka wula ka puu maa daana nin niŋi? U bu ya n‑kyɛ na nti ku tuntumniba maa zaa? Ka u nin zaŋŋi puu maa n‑ti tuntunseeba.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: See ka lahabasuŋŋu maa daŋŋi m‑paai tinsi kam zaasa.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ka u daa boosi ba, ni, Bumbɔ n nyɛ di? Ni, bumbɔ ka n niŋma n‑ti ya.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Ka u daa maali m‑boosi ba, ni, Ka yarima muna? Ya yɛtti la wula? Ka Piita daa labsiya, I nyɛ la Masiiya Kirisita, Naawunni ŋɔn tumni sɔ na nti di naam ka tiligi ti.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu la? - ni#:- Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu;.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Ba yi ti mari ya n‑yi sariya karibu ziiya, yan di daŋŋi n‑fabinni yan nin tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi zugu: ya tɔ'asim yɛttɔ'aseesi an bɛ ya sufu ni di saha maa, dama di pa yarima n ya n‑tɔ'asi amaa Naawunni Sii Kasi maa nin tɔ'asi.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, I naam gyirima saha yi ti paai na, ni, ti boori ni ti zini n‑tabli i, yinni i nudirigu, ni yinni i nuzaa ziiya.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba nimbirimoaa, ni, ba di yɛli u yɛla lala n‑ti sɔsɔa.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti. - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Dɔseeba nin gbaai ba mabiisi n‑tisi ka ba ku ba, bee ba nin gbaai ba biisi, ka biisi mun nin yisigi n‑zabi ba badima ni ba madima halli nti ku ba.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ka u daa yɛli, Ya bu mi yan susiri seelli maa. Ammi, ya nin toaai n‑nyu wahala kɔbɛseaa man ya n‑nyu wa? Bee man ya n‑su tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi koom ka ya gba nin su lala maa.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Yisa daa piili n‑wulisiri ba, ni, Ninsaala Bii nin namsi pam, ka kpamma nin ti za'asi u, ban ni zaaligu karimbadima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima zaa. Ni, ba nin ku u, amaa dabsa ata daari u nin yisigi kum ni.

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Di saha ka kpamma maa daa boori la soori ni ba gbaai u, ban daa mi, ni, nyɛsiri maa galimni la ban zugu; amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam maa dabeem, dizugu ka ba daa kyɛ u ka bakki.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Ba zaa nin yaai ya man yuuri zugu, amaa sɔ kam zaa ŋɔn nin kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑bari n‑zaani halli nti paai yɛla wa tariga maa nin nya tiliginsim.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ni, Ii! Ti nin nyaŋŋi. Ka Yisa mun yɛli ba, Ya siri nin ti nyu n wahala kɔbeaa la, ka su n lala koom maa.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Ka daa tɔ'asi lala kasikasi ka di ka sɔ'abu. Lala zugu ka Piita daa mari u n‑labi kpaŋŋa m‑piili n‑kpaamni u, ni, u di tɔ'asiri lala.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa tum Farisiseeba ni NaaHɛroodi niriba ni ba ti kyɛ Yisa saani na n‑gbaai u u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Ya nin ti nya ka ba zaŋŋi bumbɛseelli kan kyisiri la n‑zaali luginseaa kan bu sum ni ka zɛya, n‑sa'amni yɛla. Kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn karimni anŋɔ baŋŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa. Tɔ: ya yi ti nya lala, di saha maa ya kyɛ ka ban bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni la zɔ n‑du zɔya ni.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: amaa man bu su soori ni n wulisi ban nin zinni n nudirigu bee n nuzaa zii maa. Naawunni pun maali ziziisi wa siri n‑zaali n‑ti ŋɔn gaŋŋi ninvugseeba la.
