eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Yisa mun daa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi u nyaandɔliba la ka tasi Piita n‑yɛli, Bakkma ka kyɛ ma, nyin Sintaani wa!, ni, I teesa wa bu yi Naawunni saani na, amaa a kur nyɛ la ninsaala teesa.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Ban daa ti paai na maa, ba daa yɛli u, Ti Karimba, ti mi halli, ni, i nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu; i ba'a ka niriba yɛla ni, ka bu zɔri ninsaala nini, amaa ka kur wulisiri Naawunni soori ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ban bɛ pii zugu ti sigi tiŋŋa na nti kpɛ duu puuni n‑zaŋŋi sɛseelli.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Tɔ, ka u nyaandɔliba piiya ban kpalim la ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, ba suuri daa yisigi n‑niŋi Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa booli daadam, ni, ba la'am na, ban ni u nyaandɔliba maa zaa, ka yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam boori u kyɛ n saani na, see ka u za'asi u mammaŋŋa yɛla, ka kpikki u kum dɔpulli m‑mari n‑dɔli ma.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna yɛlim ti: di tuya ni ba yoori lampoo n‑tiri Room NaaTitaari, bee di bu tuusi? Ti yooma, bee ti di yɔa? Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba peemsi-ka-nyii ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ka yɛli ba, ni, Wula zugu ka ya gbiiri m bɔ'a? Ya tim ma la'afu na ka n nya.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: ka seeba ban bɛ puri ni di labi nyaaŋŋa nti zaŋŋi ba kpari la.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Dizugu Yisa daa booli ba, ni ba la'amma taaba na, ka yɛli ba, Ya mi ninvugseeba ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la yɛla, ni, ba yɛtti, ni, ninvugseeba ban su ba fukumsi mari la yiiko ka tumni ba putoo, ka ba kpamma su ba yɛla ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ni lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi la'afu n‑ti u na, ka u yi boosi ba, ni, Ŋɔni fɔtoo m bɛ la'afu maa ni wa, ni ŋɔni yuuri n sɔbi m‑bɛ ka ni? Ka ba yɛli, NaaTitaari dinni.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Di saha maa di nin ti kpeem pam n‑tisi pɔ'apuusi dima ni pɔ'aseeba ban mari biisi ka ba na mɔ'ara.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Amaa di pa lala ya saani. Ya sɔ yi boori kpeema tibsim, see ka u tumni n‑tiri ya zaa.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti deaai duniya wa zaasa ka kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, nyoori bɔ ka u nin nya?.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Ka Yisa laa n‑labsi n‑yɛli ba, Dizugu din nyɛ Naaba dinni yan zaŋŋma n‑ti Naaba maa, ka zaŋŋi din nyɛ Naawunni dinni n‑ti Naawunni. Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam ŋɔn yɛli seem maa.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Dizugu ya susim Naawunni ka dinŋɔ di kyɛ na waari saha.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Ka sɔ yi boori ni u leebi toondaana, see ka u leebi ya zaa daabri.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Seelli mun ka ni ka ninsaala ya zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri n‑takki ka.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa. Ba daa yɛli u.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: dama di dabsa maa ka wahala nin bɛ ni pam, wahalaseaa ban na zi n‑nya Naawunni ŋɔn daa naam duniya wa sahanseaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi zuna, ka ku laa m‑maali n‑nya lala taabu yaasa.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Dama man Ninsaala Bii bu kyɛ na ni ba tumni n‑suŋŋni ma; ay! - n kyɛ na ni n tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba, ka zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri m‑pa talima ka niriba pam nya tiliginsim.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti kpalim m‑bɛ zuna niriba wa sunsukkuni n‑zɔri vi n yɛla zugu, ni n wulisigu zugu gba, ba tumbeeri ni ba yɛdda kaalinsi zugu, di daama muna - Ninsaala Bii yi ti kyɛ na sahanseaa la, ŋɔn gba nin ti zɔ vi u yɛla zugu; dama ŋɔn Ninsaala Bii siri nin zaŋŋi u Ba Naawunni gyirima ka kyɛ ka Naawunni malaakadima n dɔli u ka kyɛ na.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: ni, Ti Karimba, Musa daa sɔbi n‑ti ti n‑wulisi, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka kyɛ u pɔ'a, ka u na bu dɔ'ai biiya n‑ti u, see ka u di u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ka ti Dugma maa yi daa bu ŋmaai dabsa maa gyiŋŋa, saalibii n daa ka ni n‑da naa n‑nya tiliginsim, amaa ŋɔn daa yiisi seeba la zugu ka u siri daa ŋmaai dabsa maa.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa n‑kpɛ bɛni. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa naayi yi tiŋŋa maa ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba ni zama pam, ka zoonsɔ daa zi soori kpaŋŋa m‑maanni bara, doo mun yuuri daa boonni Baatimeeyusi, ni, Timeeyusi biiya.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Tɔ; ka mabiisi ayɔpɔi n daa bɛ ni. Ba kpeema maa daa di pɔ'a ka kpi ka bu dɔ'ai biiya.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ŋɔna daa ti wum, ni, di nyɛ la Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, n‑gatti maa, ka u daa tasi, n‑yɛli, Yisa, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Ka di saha maa, sɔ yi ti yɛli ya, ni, Nyama, Masiiya maa bɛ la kpa. bee Kpa sa ka u bɛ ni. yan di niŋi ba yɛdda.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Ka u nyeeri la di pukoori maa, n‑gba kpi ka bu dɔ'ai. Ka di daa nyɛ la lala n‑ti buta daana gba.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ka niriba pam daa nyikki u n‑yɛli, ni, u sina u vuuri. Amaa u daa laa n‑tasi pampam, ni, Yisa, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Seeba nin ti doaai na m‑ma'ari ziri, ni, ban n nyɛ Masiiya, bee, ni, ban n nyɛ anabidima; ba nin tum alahazibu tuma ni ba ti yɔmsi niriba m‑basi, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn yiisi seeba la gba, amaa Naawunni ku sakki lala.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Yɛlimaŋni babayɔpɔi maa zaa daa kpiya ka bu dɔ'ai biiya ni pɔ'a maa. Di nyaaŋŋa ka pɔ'a maa gba kpi.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Ka Yisa daa zaani, ka yɛli, Ya boolim u na!, ka ba daa booli zooma maa na, ka yɛli u, Mari sufupeelli, doaam na! Ka u boonni i maa.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Ya gbaam ya mansi, dama m pun daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya seelli kam zaa.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Tɔ: dinŋɔna Zaadaari, dɔppa wa zaa yi ti yisigi kum ni, ba ŋɔni n nin nyɛ pɔ'a maa sira, dama babayɔpɔi zaa daa di u.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Amaa di saha, wahala maa nyaaŋŋa, ni#:- Wuntaŋŋa nin ti zibgi zimm! Ka namboaa ku laa m‑pɛligi:.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Tɔ, ka zooma maa daa yisigi n‑zaani, ka yeaai u waari kparigu m‑basi, ka vaai n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa saani.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Lala n nyɛ di! Ya burigi soori ya ziilinsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Sɔppu maa zugu, ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn mari yiiko seem la gba.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi u, ni, Bɔ ka i boori ni n niŋi n‑ti i? Ni, N Karimba, m boori ni n nyari yaasa.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Saŋmariwuusi nin ti yiiri saazugu n‑luri na, Ka arizana yiikodaandima zaa nin damna.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ba yi ti yisigi kum ni sahanseaa, ba ku laa n‑diri pɔ'aba bee n‑kunni dɔppa; ba nin ti ŋmani la hoo arizana malaakadima la.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Kyɛma! - i yɛdda maa taasi la sookyi na n‑ti i. Ka di saha maa u nini maa daa neaai, ka u daa doaai n‑dɔli Yisa ka ba kyɛnna.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Di saha ka ba nin ti nya Ninsaala Bii maa ka u pɔtti na, ka sangbana nin gyiligi u, ka u ti mari yiiko pam ni gyirima veelim.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri la yɛla, ya bu karim Musa bukku la? Ŋɔn daa sɔbi bugum ni tutuuri yɛla Suuriseaa ni ka u daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni daa yɛli, Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Di saha maa ka u nin ti tum malaakadima maa ka ba yi na m‑paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa n‑zaŋŋi ŋɔn yiisi seeba la ka la'asi ba taaba.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: U siri pa la ninvugkpiimma Naawunni amaa ninvugvuya Naawunni. Ya burigi pam di zugu.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Ni, Ya kyɛ ka kinkaŋŋa yɛla wulisi ya di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ya yi ti nya ka lala tii maa yiisiri wuliŋmeena ka pusiri vapaala, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, siga saha pɔtta.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Ka karimbasɔ daa kyɛ na nti wum ba nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu maa. Ŋɔn daa baŋŋi, ni, Yisa daa labsi ba suŋŋa maa, u daa boosi u, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali zaaligudima maa zaa puuni, kani n lee n‑daŋŋi m‑mugsi ka kpa n‑gaari zaa.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Ka di bɛ la lala: ya yi ti nya ka yɛla wa zaa niŋna, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, saha maa pɔriya.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi u, ni, Kan mugsi ka kpa pam nyɛ la, ni, Ya Izraɛli dima, ya wumma! - Ti Dugma Naawunni nyɛ la Naawunni yinni;.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli ya wa, ni, zuna niriba zaa ku gaari see ka dinŋɔ zaa niŋi.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: dizugu zaŋŋma i sufu zaa n‑wuri i Dugma Naawunni, ni i siiya zaa, ni i teesa zaa, ni i yaa zaa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu nin gaari, amaa man yɛttɔ'a wa ku gaari.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Kan paasi buyi daana mun nyɛ la, ni, Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la. Zaaligu seaa laa n‑ka ni n‑gaari anŋɔa.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Ni, Amaa di daari maa, bee di saha, ŋɔn ya paai sahanseaa la ka sɔsɔ bu mi deede, halli malaakadima ban bɛ arizana ni la bu mi, Naawunni Bii maa gba bu mi, na'ala ti Ba maa mi.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Mboo! N Karimba, di veela. Di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni nyin yɛli, ni, kan kpa nyɛ la, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la bunyinni.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Dizugu ya gura, yan di gbisira, dama ya bu mi di saha maa.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: ka wunsɔ laa n‑ka ni m‑paasi yaasa na'ala u kɔkɔa. Ni, i yi zaŋŋi i sufu zaa n‑wuri u, ni i teesa, ni i yaa zaa, ka laa n‑wuri i taraana hoo i mammaŋŋa - lala mari anfaani n‑gaari sara maaligu zaa, bee, i kuuri bunkɔbri n‑tiri Naawunni.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Di ti ya n‑nyɛ la hoo dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa wa goorim ka puri u yiri tuma n‑tisi u tuntumniba seem, sɔkam zaa ni u tuma. Ka yɛli ŋɔn guri u yiri za'anɔri maa, ni, u di gbisi.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ka doo maa labsigu maa nyɛ labsisuŋŋu, u daa yɛli u, ni, I bu wɔ'a ni Naawunni naam maa ni. Di nyaaŋŋa vi daa gbaai ba, ka sɔ zaa daa bu laa m‑boosi u boosiseaa yaasa.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Dizugu ya gura suŋŋa, dama ya bu mi yiri maa daana lappu saha na - ammi, zaanoori, bee yuntasuusi, bee bɛkasigri, bee asuba?.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisira, u daa yɛliya, ni, Masiiya maa muna - Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti di naam n‑fa u niriba m‑basi - wula zugu ka karimbadima la yɛtti, ni, lala Masiiya maa nyɛ la NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa?.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: Ya sɛ'asima, ka di kyɛ ka u paai na n‑libgi ya ka ya na gbisira.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Amaa NaaDauda mammaŋŋa daa tɔ'asi Masiiya maa yɛla, n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko ni, ni#:- Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, ni, Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni, Halli ka n zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni. .

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Ka man yɛtti yarima seem wa, n yɛtti la sɔkam zaa, ni, ya sɛ'asima n‑gura.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Tɔ, ka NaaDauda mammaŋŋa boonni ŋɔn Masiiya maa, ni, u Dugma: ka wula ka ŋɔna nin toaai n‑nyɛ u ya'aŋŋa? Daadam maa daa wumni u yɛttɔ'a maa ka di daa niŋi ba nya'asim pam.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Ŋɔn daa wulisiri ba maa, Yisa daa yɛliya, Ya baŋŋma ninvugseeba ban wulisiri zaaligu la yɛla! Ba boori ba yeeri kparikara n‑gyinna, ka sɔkam zaa puusiri ba daasi ni.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: La'aŋŋu duri puu ni ba boori la ba zi sɔkam zaa tooni, halli deema gba ziiya ba bɛ la tooni.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba faari la pukɔya yiya pɔi, ka naayi wulisiri ba maŋŋa Naawunni puusigu maa ni, dama ba yuui puusigu ni. Lala niriba maa nin ti nya galiŋŋu pam.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yisa daa zinni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, ban daa toosiri piini duseaa la tooni. U daa gɔsiri daadam maa zaa ŋɔn zaŋŋi piini seesi n‑niŋi na. Bundaandima pam daa zaŋŋi ligri pam n‑niŋi.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Amaa pukɔsɔ daa kpɛ na, ŋɔn daa nyɛ faradaana, ka daa zaŋŋi ligri fiiŋ, kulla la'abisibisi ayi, n‑niŋi.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Ka Yisa daa booli u nyaandɔliba la na n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, pukoori faradaana wa tisi n‑gaari sɔkam zaa;.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: dama ban zaa yiisi la ba marigu pam puuni seelli n‑toosi, amaa pukoori wa ŋɔna, u marigu zaa seelli ŋɔn da ya m‑mari n‑diri la ka u zaŋŋi n‑toosi.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti kaai, gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa daa la'am, ban ni kpamma ni karimbadima maa - halli ba sariya karibu dima zaa. Ba daa la'asi n‑gbaai ba gbaari. Ba daa ba Yisa bansi n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑niŋi Gɔmna Pailiti nuu ni.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ka Pailiti daa boosi u, ni, Nyini n nyɛ Izraɛli dima Naaba la?, ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, Nyin yɛli seem maa n nyɛ di.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Ka kpamma maa daa galim u zaŋŋi n‑kpa yɛla pam zugu.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: ka Pailiti daa boosi u yaasa, ni, I bu ya labsi? Wumma ba galinsi maa zaa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Ka Yisa daa bu laa n‑gyɛrigi u seelli yaasa, ka di daa niŋi Pailiti alahazibu.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Tɔ, ka di daa nyɛ la kaari soori, ni, Kyuu maa saha niriba daa yi wulisi dansarika niriba ninvugsɔ ban boora, ka Gɔmna maa ti yiisi di daama sarika ni na n‑kyɛ m‑basi ka u kyaŋŋi.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Di saha maa ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Barabasi daa bɛ sarika ni: ŋɔna ni u taaba daa pun zabi Gɔmnanti dima maa n‑ku niriba di zugu, ka ba daa gbaai ba n‑niŋi sarika ni.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Ka daadam maa daa la'am na nti susiri Pailiti, ni, u dɔli lala Kyuu soori la n‑yiisi sɔ n‑ti ba na;.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: ka u daa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, Ya boori ni m basi Izraɛli dima Naaba wa n‑ti ya na.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: - dama u daa baŋŋi, ni, nyuuri zugu ka kpamma maa daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti u.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa daa figsi niriba maa n‑kyɛ ka ba yɛli Pailiti ni u basim Barabasi n‑ti ba, ka kyɛ Yisa.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Ka Pailiti laa n‑yɛli zama maa, ni, Ka ninvugsɔ ban boonni Izraɛli Naaba la muna? - Ya boori ni n niŋi u la wula.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ka ba tasi n‑labsi, ni, Kpaasim u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Pailiti daa boosi ba, ni, Taalli bɔ ka u lee n‑tum? Ka ba daa maali n‑tasi pam, ni, Kpaasim u.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Tɔ, ka Pailiti daa boori ni u maai daadam maa sufu, ka dizugu u daa yiisi Barabasi m‑basi n‑ti ba, ka daa kyɛ ka ba feebi Yisa ka zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ni ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Di saha ka soogyadima maa daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑kpɛ zɔŋŋu maa ni - ban boonni Baarikyi seela maa - ka booli ba taaba ni ba ti la'am na.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi naam kparigu n‑yeeli Yisa, ka paam goosi m‑pili u zugu hoo naam zupiliga.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: ka yi piili m‑puusiri u, ni, Ndana, m Ba Izraɛli Naaba.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi kika'ari n‑feebi u zugu, n‑tuusi nantoori n‑niŋi u, n‑gbaantiri u.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ka ban daa maali u ansarisi lala n‑naai maa, ba daa yeaai ba kparigu maa ka labsi u maŋtoori neema n‑yeeli u. Ka daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi ni ba ti kpaasi u.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban daa yi soori ba daa paai dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Saimoon. Ŋɔn daa kpeeri timpuuni ka ba daa mugsi u ka u zi Yisa dɔpulli daari maa.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑kyaŋŋi Gɔligɔtta ziiya .

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Bɛni ka ba daa mari daam ka niŋi tiim n‑niŋi, n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u ni u nyu; amaa u daa bu sakki.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa. Ka ba daa puri u sutura ni ba tɔri taaba, n‑toori tɛtɛ ni ba nya ŋɔn ya deaai.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Di daa nyɛ la hoo asuba kurigu awai, nɔnyeesiri saha, ban daa kpaasi u maa.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Ban daa sɔbi seelli n‑galimni u ni maa daa nyɛ la, ni, Yɛhudiyadima Naaba la nŋɔa. .

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Bɛni maa ka ba daa kpaasi na'ayigsi ayi gba: ka daa sa ba yinni u nudirigu ya'ari, ni yinni u nuzaa ya'ari.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Niriba ban daa gatti daa lɔbri ba zugu ka tuuri Yisa, ni, Ɔhɔ! Nyini n daa yɛtti yɛttɔ'ayɔya wa - ni, i nin wurim Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la n‑lusi, ka zaŋŋi daba ata m‑mɛ ka la yaasa!.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ yim dɔpulli maa zugu n‑sigi na n‑fa i maŋŋa m‑basi.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Lala ka gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa daa laari Yisa, ban ni karimbadima maa gba, ka yɛtti taaba, ni, U daa faari la niriba m‑basira, amaa ka bu nyaŋŋi n‑fa u maŋŋa m‑basi!.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Kyɛ ka ti nya Masiiya maa, Izraɛli Naa maa, ka u yi dɔpulli maa zugu n‑sigi na ka ti niŋi u yɛdda! Ka niriba ayi seeba ban daa kpaasi n‑sa Yisa ya'a ayi maa gba daa tuuri u.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Agbaa saha ŋɔn daa paaya, tingbaŋŋu maa zaa daa niŋi zimm, ka zibsim daa ligi zaa n‑yuui m‑paai zaanoori hoo kurigu ata, wuntankabri saha.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa kaasi pampam, ni, Ɛlooyi, Ɛlooyi, lama sabakyitani? - dinŋɔ gbinni nyɛ la, N Naawunni, n Naawunni, wula ka i kyɛ ma m‑basi.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Niriba ban daa zɛ n‑tabi bɛni maa seeba daa wum u yɛttɔ'a maa ka yɛli, ni, Ya wumma! U boonni la Ilaygya.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ka ba ninvugyinni daa zɔ na ni sɔ'asu n‑sukki damiisiri ni n‑zaŋŋi n‑kusi kika'ari ni n‑zaŋŋi n‑duusi m‑paai ni Yisa ti nyu, ni, Kyɛla! - ka ti nya Ilaygya nin kyɛ na n‑sigsi u na, bee.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Ka Yisa daa tasi pampam ka kpi.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Ka pateesa kan daa ya'ai Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Dutitaari puuni maa daa toaai buyi, n‑yi ka saazugu zaŋŋi n‑sigri tiŋni.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Soogyadima kpeensɔ n daa zɛ dɔpulli maa tooni: ka ŋɔn daa nya Yisa ŋɔn kpi seem maa, u daa yɛli, ni, Doo wa siri daa nyɛ la Wumbiiya.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Ka pɔ'aseeba daa bɛ kpaŋŋa sa ka gɔsiri na. Mariama ŋɔn daa yi Magdala na n daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ni Mariama ŋɔn daa nyɛ Gyeemsi bila ni Gyɔseesi ma, ni Saloomi.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Ban n daa pun dɔli Yisa ŋɔn daa na bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu saha la, ka daa suŋŋni u. Pɔ'aba pam ban daa dɔli u Gyɛrusalɛm na gba daa bɛ bɛni.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Tɔ. Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ŋɔn daa nyɛ gyirima daana kpamma maa sunsukkuni, ka daa guusiri Naawunni naam kyɛnni na m‑paasi. U yuuri daa boonni la Gyoosɛfi, ka u daa yi Arimatiiya tiŋŋa ni na. Di saha ka zaanoori daa ti maai Sɛ'asigu daari maa - Vuusim Dabsiri siri maaligu dabsiri nla - ka ŋɔn Gyoosɛfi daa mari u sufukpeemni n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Gɔmna Pailiti na nti susi u soori ni u kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu nti sɔ'ai.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Ka Pailiti ŋɔn daa wum, ni, Yisa pun kpi la, di daa gyɛri u pam.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: ka u daa booli soogyadima kpeema maa na nti boosi u, ni, Yisa pun kpi ka di yuui, bee? Ka Pailiti ŋɔn daa wum kpeema maa lahabaari maa n‑naai u daa zaŋŋi ningbiŋŋu maa yɛla n‑niŋi Gyoosɛfi nuu ni.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Ka Gyoosɛfi daa da kasiŋŋa ka sigsi kum maa na n‑zaŋŋi kasiŋŋa maa n‑vili u n‑zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi gbaliseaa ban pun kyiibi ka tampifeoo ni. Ka ba daa bilim kugtitaari n‑ligi ka noori.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Mariama Magdalapɔ'a la, ni Gyɔseesi ma Mariama, daa gɔsiri ni, ka nya ban daa zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu n‑sɔ'ai seela.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Yuunseaa n daa paai, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yuuma piiya ni anu NaaTaibiriyusi ŋɔn daa zinni Room NaaTitaari naam gbana zugu la. Di saha maa ka Pɔntiyusi Pailiti daa nyɛ Gyuudiiya tingbaŋŋu Gɔmna, NaaHɛroodi mun daa di Galilii naam, ka u beeri Filipi mun daa di Ituriiya ni Tirakɔnaati m‑paasi, ka Laasanyasi mun daa di Abiliini naam.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: Saha kanŋɔ yaasa ka Annasi ni Kayafasi daa di Izraɛli dima gyeeŋŋu suulinsi. Di daa nyɛ la saha kanŋɔ ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Gyɔni, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Zakariiya bii la, yoo ni sa.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Dizugu ka Gyɔni daa kyaŋŋi n‑gyinni Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ziiya gyilagyila ni, m‑moonni n‑yɛtti, ni, Ya kyɛli ya teesibeeri, ni ya tumbeeri tuma, m‑basi. Ka lala zugu ya kyɛm na ka man nin su ya Naawunni koom, ka Naawunni nin kyɛ ya taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ya.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Di zaa daa nyɛ hoo Anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn pun sɔbi u bukku maa puuni n‑yɛli seem la. U daa yɛnni, ni#:- Ya wumma! Sɔ m bɛ yoo ni n‑tasira, Ni, Ya maalim ti Dugma palli n‑ti u, Ya sɛ'asim u sɔya zaa ka a tuusi kyappi;.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Baa kam zaa nin ti nyɛri, Ka zɔya ni kunkuna zaa nin birigi n‑sigi; An gɔlim zaa nin teaai, Ka sɔgbiragbira la mun leebi bɔlibɔli;.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Ka ninsaala zaa nin ti nya M‑baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn nin tuligi u niriba seem la. .

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Tɔ; ka daadam daa la'am Gyɔni saanni na pam, ni u su ba Naawunni koom. Ka u daa yɛli zama maa, ni Ya bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmani la hoo wa'abeeri! Ŋɔni n wulisi ya Naawunni ŋɔn ya namsi ya seem ka ya yɛnni, ni, ya nin zɔ n‑yi namsigu maa ni?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: See ka ya tum tunseesi an wulisi, ni, ya pun takki ya halibeoo ni ya teesibeeri zaa, kaman tisuŋŋu ŋɔn wanni walisuma seem la. Yan di yɛtti taaba, ni Ibrahim ya'ansi n nyɛ ti!; ya bu baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi halli kuga wa gba m‑maali ya'ansi n‑ti Ibrahim?.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ka Naawunni nin namsi tumbeeri dima hoo nira ŋɔn maali u leefu siri ni u ŋma tiseaa kan bu wanni suŋŋa seem. U nin ŋma ka n‑lusi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑lɔsi bugum ni.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi lala maa, ka zama maa niriba daa boosi u, ni Ka dizugu ti ya niŋi la wula.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ŋɔn mari kpari ayi ŋɔn yiisim yinni n‑tisi ŋɔn pun ka halli yinni. Bee ŋɔn mari bundirigu gba ŋɔn purim di lala.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Tɔ; ka lampodeaariba gba daa kyɛ Gyɔni saanni na, ni u su ba Naawunni koom maa. Ba daa boosi u, ni, Ti Karimba, ka wula ka tirima ya niŋi.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya di deaari n‑yakkri ban wulisi ya seem maa.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Soogyadima gba daa boosi u m‑paasi, ni, Ka tirima ŋɔna? Ka u daa yɛli ba, Ya di mugsiri sɔ n‑deaari ligri bee n‑galimni sɔkam yɔriyɔri; ka ya kyɛ ka ya nini tigi ya yoori ŋɔn n‑nyɛ seem maa.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Tɔ; ka bana, ni Izraɛli dima maa zaa, daa kur guusiri Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum sɔ na n‑ti ba, ni u ti su ba naam fukumsi la. Ba daa puri lala naaba yuuri, ni, Masiiya, bee Kirisita. Dinŋɔna Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi u yɛttɔ'a seem maa, ba daa birisiri ni m‑boosiri taaba, ni, Ammi, Masiiya na'a nŋɔ, bee.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Amaa Gyɔni daa tasi n‑yɛli ba zaa, ni, Koom ka mani zaŋŋi n‑suuri ya wa, amaa sɔ kyɛnni na ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi Sii Kasi ni bugum n‑suuri ya. Ŋɔna n kpeem n‑gaari ma halli ka mani bu sum ni m pirigi u namna n‑ti u.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: U kyɛnni na, ka pun maali u neema siri ni u pii u kabuubu tampii maa n‑niŋi ka kasikasi. U nin yeeli u kyi maa n‑yiisi wuurigu la ka kyɛ kabeelim. Kasuma maa ka u nin la'asi n‑su u kusɔkku maa ni, ka naayi zaŋŋi wuurigu maa zaa n‑vaai m‑basi bugunseebu bun ka kpiisigu puuni.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Tɔ; ka Gyɔni daa kpamsiri niriba lala, ka paasiri ba yɛligu pam zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa ni.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Tɔ; ka NaaHɛroodi daa pun fa u maŋtoori beeri pɔ'a la - pɔ'a maa mun yuuri daa boonni Hɛroodiyasi - ka anŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ka Gyɔni daa galim Naaba maa dizugu, ni, Naaba galimiya pam, m‑paasi ŋɔn daa pun tum tumbɛseesi la zugu.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: Dinzugu NaaHɛroodi daa laa n‑tum tumbɛseaa kan gaari ŋɔn pun tum tunseesi la zaa ka kyɛ ka ba gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari dansarika ni.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Tɔ; amaa ban daa na bu gbaai Gyɔni sahanseaa la, ka ŋɔn daa na suuri daadam zaa Naawunni koom maa, ka Yisa gba daa su. Ban daa su Yisa koom n‑naai ka u daa puusiri Naawunni la, di saha ka saazugu sangbana daa yoaai.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa yi na n‑sigi u saanni na: Sii maa mun daa ŋmani la hoo naŋmanni. Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu na ka yɛnni, ni, M bii n nyɛ i, ka n wuri i pam; n nini n tigi i.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Yisa mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti ya piili u tuma maa, u yuuma n daa nyɛ kaman yuuma pisita. Niriba daa teesi, ni, u nyɛ la Gyoosɛfi biiya. Gyoosɛfi mun daa nyɛ la Heeli biiya.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Heeli Mattaati biiya, Mattaati Liivai biiya, Liivai Mɛlikyii biiya, Mɛlikyii Gyannai biiya, Gyannai Gyoosɛfi sɔ biiya.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: Gyoosɛfi Matatayasi biiya, Matatayasi Eemɔsi biiya, Eemɔsi Neehum biiya, Neehum Ɛsili biiya, Ɛsili Nagyeaai biiya.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: Nagyeaai Maati biiya, Maati Matatayasi sɔ biiya, Matatayasi Sɛmeeyini biiya, Sɛmeeyini Gyoosɛkyi biiya, Gyoosɛkyi Gyooda biiya.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: Gyooda Gyɔ'anan biiya, Gyɔ'anan Reesa biiya, Reesa Zɛrubabɛli biiya, Zɛrubabɛli Seaalitiyɛli biiya, Seaalitiyɛli Neeri biiya.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: Neeri Mɛlikyii biiya, Mɛlikyii Addi biiya, Addi Koosaam biiya, Koosaam Ɛlimadam biiya, Ɛlimadam Yeeri biiya.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: Yeeri Gyɔsuuwa sɔ biiya, Gyɔsuuwa Ɛliyeeziri biiya, Ɛliyeeziri Gyoorim biiya, Gyoorim Mattaati biiya, Mattaati Liivai biiya.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: Liivai Simiyɔn biiya, Simiyɔn Gyuuda biiya, Gyuuda Yisifu biiya, Yisifu Gyoonan biiya, Gyoonan Ilayakyiim biiya.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: Ilayakyiim Miliiya biiya, Miliiya Mɛnna biiya, Mɛnna Matata biiya, Matata Neetan biiya, Neetan NaaDauda biiya.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: NaaDauda Gyɛsi biiya, Gyɛsi Oobɛdi biiya, Oobɛdi Bɔ'azi biiya, Bɔ'azi Seaala biiya, Seaala Neeseoon biiya.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: Neeseoon Aminadaba biiya, Aminadaba Arimin biiya, Arimin Aani biiya, Aani Hɛzrɔn biiya, Hɛzrɔn Pɛrɛzi biiya, Pɛrɛzi Gyuuda biiya.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: Gyuuda Yaakubu biiya, Yaakubu Yisahaaku biiya, Yisahaaku Ibrahim biiya, Ibrahim Teera biiya, Teera Neehoori biiya.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: Neehoori Sɛrugu biiya, Sɛrugu Reoo biiya, Reoo Pɛligi biiya, Pɛligi Hibri biiya, Hibri Seela biiya.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: Seela Kyeenan biiya, Kyeenan Aafaksaddi biiya, Aafaksaddi Seemi biiya, Seemi Nuhu biiya, Nuhu Lamɛkyi biiya.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: Lamɛkyi Mɛtusala biiya, Mɛtusala Inɔkyi biiya, Inɔkyi Gyɛreedi biiya, Gyɛreedi Mahalaleelli biiya, Mahalaleelli Kyeenan biiya.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: Kyeenan Inɔsi biiya, Inɔsi Sɛtti biiya, Sɛtti Adam biiya; ŋɔn Adam muna, Naawunni bii n daa nyɛ u.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Yisa daa ti ya yi Gyɔdin mɔ'ari n‑labi na la, ka Naawunni Sii Kasi pun paali u ni.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: Ka Sii maa daa zaŋŋi u n‑taasi yoo ni sa ni u ti niŋi dapisinaasi; ka Sintaani daa zamsi u. Di saha maa u daa bu di bunseela halli ka kɔm ti naayi wum u yoo maa ni.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Ka Sintaani daa yɛli u, ni, I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku kyɛ ka kugri wa leebi bundirigu.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni, pa bundirigu kɔkɔ zugu ka ninsaala bɛ ni.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sintaani daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑duusi saazugu n‑wulisi u duniya puuni tinsi zaasa yooma.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Ka Sintaani yɛli u, ni, I bu baŋŋi, ni, mani n su anŋɔ zaa fukumsi? Ka man boori sɔ nuu ni ka n ya n‑zaŋŋi a n‑niŋi.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Dizugu i yi sakki n‑gbaani n‑gyeem ma Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, n nin zaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa ni a bunveela zaa fukumsi n‑ti i, ka a ti nyɛ i dina.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni#:- Naawunni yinni kɔkɔ N sumsi gbaanigu ni gyeeŋŋu. Ka Ŋɔna n nyɛ ya Dugma. .

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Ka Sintaani daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni ka zaŋŋi u n‑du n‑tɔm Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri saazugu sa, ka Sintaani yɛli, I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku zaŋŋi i maŋŋa m‑basi tiŋŋa?.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: Di pun bu sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ka u malaakadima kyɛ na n‑guri i?.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Ka laa n‑sɔbi, ni, I yi ya lu, ba nin teebi i saazugu ba nuusi ni ka i napɔŋŋu ku tuui kugri. .

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, ba yɛliya, ni, I di zamsiri i Dugma Naawunni.

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Ka Sintaani ŋɔn daa yi zamsi u n‑naai maa, u daa bakki ni ka kyɛ u saha beela.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa ka Sii Kasi kpeeŋŋu daa paali Yisa maa ni, ka u labi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni na. Ka bɛni tinkpansi maa, ni an zɛ gyilagyila zaa, daa wum u lahabaari maa.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: U daa kpeeri ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑wulisiri ba, ka ba zaa daa tiri u gyirima u yɛttɔ'a zugu.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Ka Yisa daa paai Nazarɛti - ban daa wubsi u tinseaa la nla - ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari daa ti paai, ka u daa kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duu ni hoo u soori ŋɔn daa kur nyɛ seem.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: N‑kpɛ bɛni, ka u daa yisigi n‑zaani ka ba zaŋŋi Anabi Aizaaya bukku la n‑ti u na ni u karim. Ka u daa lakki bukku maa ni u karim n‑ti ba. Ŋɔn daa lakki ka maa, u daa bɔ ya'aseaa kan daa wulisi, ni#:-.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Ti Dugma Sii maa bɛ man saani, Dama Ŋɔna n yiisi ma ni m mooli N‑wulisi u lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti faradima. Ka laa n‑tum ma ban gbaasi seeba saani Ni n ti yɛli ba, ni, ban nin yiisi ba m‑basi. Ni n kyɛm zoomma gba saani N‑yɛli ba, ni, ba nini nin neaai. Ni, ban mugsiri seeba yaasa nin nya soori Ni ba ti su ba mansi.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: Lala maa n ya wulisi, Ni, ti Dugma sahasuŋŋu maa paaya.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Di daa kpalim la dabsa ayi ni Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu, kyuseaa ban diri bundiripaala maa - seesi an ka dabinni la. Ka gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni zaaligu karimbadima maa daa boori sɔseaa ban nin zaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai n‑gbaai Yisa n‑ku.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Ŋɔn karim lala n‑naai maa, u daa labsi n‑kpabi bukku maa ka zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi ka laa n‑zini. Ka ban daa zi duu maa ni maa zaa kur bɛ la u gɔsibu ni.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Ni, Ti di niŋi lala Kyuu maa zama sunsukkuni; tin mira ka niriba maa nin zabi.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Ka u daa piili n‑yɛli ba, ni, Zuna, yan da wumni yɛla wa sahanseaa la, yɛla maa da kur niŋni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yisa mun daa bɛ la Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa; u daa kpɛ la Saimoon yiri, ŋɔn daa nyɛ kunkɔŋŋa la. Ka ban daa ti dira, pɔ'asɔ daa mari turaali kɔlɔba n‑kpɛ na, turaali kpaam n‑daa la, ka bu mari la nyunya'asiri ka bu ligri zoaai pam. U daa ŋma kɔlɔba maa n‑kpaai kpaam maa n‑saari Yisa zugu.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ka sɔkam zaa daa pɛ'ari u, ka ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛm ni nya'asim n‑tɔ'asiri u yɛttɔ'a maa seem n daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam. Baseeba boosiri taaba, ni, Pa Gyoosɛfi bii n‑kur ŋɔa.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Ka ban daa bɛ bɛni maa seeba sufu daa yisigi, ka ba tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba, ni, Bɔzugu ka u sa'am turaali wa?.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Ka dizugu ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Man kur mi halli, ni, ya nin teaai ma nyɛsiseaa yɛla - kan yɛtti, ni, Nyin tiimaraana wa, kur tibma i maŋŋa!, ni, Tin daa wum ka i tum seem Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni la, see ka i tum lala i mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa wa puuni! .

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Ba yi da zaŋŋi bu n‑koosi, ligri pam naa n‑yi na ka lala ligri naa n‑toaai m‑puri n‑ti faradima! Ka ba daa nyikki u lala.

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, anabi zaa maŋtoori timbiisi n nyɛ ban bu sakki n‑deaari u.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛl u! Wula ka ya daamni u? U tum la din veela n‑ti ma.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Amaa yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, Izraɛli pukɔya daa zoaai pam Anabi Ilaygya saha la, wari ŋɔn daa zaani yuuma ata ni gɔya ayoobu ka kɔm lu.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Ya kur nin mari faradima ya saani sahakam. Ya yi boori ya suŋŋi ba, ya nin nya soori. Amaa ya ku mari mani sahakam.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Amaa Naawunni daa bu tum Ilaygya ni u kyaŋŋi pukɔya wa sɔsɔ saani, amaa pukɔsɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ Zarifaati tiŋŋa saani ka u daa tum u - tiŋŋa wa n kur bɛ Saidoon tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Pɔ'a maa mun niŋi la hoo ŋɔn nyaŋŋi seem; u zaŋŋi turaali kpaam wa hoo ŋɔn daŋŋi n‑saari n niŋŋa n‑sɛ'asiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n kum sɔ'abu saha seem la.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Yaasa m‑paasi: Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ka kɔnsi daa zoaai pam Anabi Ilayseaa saha la, amaa ba sɔsɔ daa bu nya tippu, amaa Na'aman sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Siriiya nira la ka ba daa tibi.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli ya, ni, ba yi ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa duniya wa lugri kam zaa, ba nin teaai pɔ'a wa yɛla n‑tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ŋɔn niŋi seem maa.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa wum u yɛttɔ'a wa la'aŋŋu duu maa ni, ba zaa sufu daa yisigi ni.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Ka Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti, ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa yinni la, daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa saani ni u ti figsi Yisa n‑ti ba.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑yiisi ba tiŋŋa maa ni, m‑mari u halli ni zɔpiisi zugu ni ba daai u n‑lusi n‑ku u.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Ba sufu daa maaya ŋɔn daa yɛli ba lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu, ka ba daa ti noori, ni, ba nin ti u ligri. Dizugu Gyuudasi daa piili m‑boori sɔsunseaa ŋɔn nin niŋi n‑zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti ba.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: amaa u daa dɔli ba sunsukkuni n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ ba.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Kyuu maa dahinyinni, peesi kɔribu daari maa, ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa boosi u, ni, Yɛni ka i boori ni ti kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ m‑maali Kyuu diibu siri n‑ti i.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi n‑sigi m‑paai Kapirinyum - Galilii tingbaŋŋu tinseaa n nyɛ ka. U daa paai m‑bɛ ni, ka daa la'amni ni Kapirinyum dima n‑wulisiri ba, Vuusim Dabsiri kam zaa.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Ka Yisa daa tum babayi, n‑yɛli ba, Ya kpeem timpuuni. Ya yi ti kpeeri timpuuni maa, dɔsɔ nin paai ya na n‑ziiri koom. Yan dɔla u nyaaŋŋa.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Di daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'amaŋa la zugu, ka u bu birisiri halli beela.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: n‑kyaŋŋi yiri seaa ŋɔn nin kpɛ la ni, ka kpɛ n‑yɛli yiri maa daana, ni, ya Karimba maa nin ti boosi u, ni, dukani ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba ya kpɛ n‑di ba Kyuu bundirigu?.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni ka arizimbeoo daa kur bɛ u ni. Ka u daa kaasi pampam, ni.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Ka u nin wulisi ya dutitaari gyirambisa zugu ka ba pun maali ka siri n‑zaali. Ya sɛ'asima bɛni ka guri ti.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Aah! Yisa, nyin Nazarɛti doo wa! Wula ka i ya m‑mukki ti? I kyɛnni na ni i ti sa'am ti, bee? M mi nyin nyɛ sɔa: ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔn nyɛ bunkasikasi ka yi Naawunni saani na.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ka nyaandɔliba ayi maa daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni, ka di daa niŋi ba hoo ŋɔn pun yɛli ba seem maa, ka ba daa sɛ'asi Kyuu maa siri.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Amaa Yisa daa nyikki u ka yɛli, Sina i vuuri ka yi na ka kyɛ u! Ka arizimbeoo maa daa tɔ doo maa n‑lusi u tiŋŋa, ba zaa sunsukkuni, ka yi na ka kyɛ u, ka sɛseelli bu niŋi doo maa.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti paai, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa daa paai na.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Ka di daa yi gyɛri ba zaasa ka ba yi yɛtti taaba, ni, Ka yɛligu bɔ nŋɔa? U kur zaŋŋi yaa n‑tɔ'asiri u yɛttɔ'amaŋni n‑yɛtti arizimbeeri, ni, ba yim na, ka ba siri yiiri na, fimm.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ka ban daa ti dira, Yisa daa yɛliya, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ya yinni nin figsi ma n‑ti n dindaandima - ti ban la'am n‑diri wa yinni.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa daa mooli n‑gyili bɛni tinsi maa ni, ni a tinkpansi zaa.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Ba sufu daa sa'amiya, ka ba daa boosiri u yinniyinni, Mani, bee.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Ka u daa yi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑kpɛ Saimoon yiri. Di saha maa Saimoon deemma ka waari daa mara, ka u dɔya n‑gyigra, ka ba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Yisa m‑boosi u zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi pɔ'a maa yɛla.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Ka u daa yɛli, Di nyɛ la ya pii ni ayi wa yinni, man ni ŋɔn la'amni n‑sukkri laa yinni ni.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Ka u daa kpɛ n‑zaani u saani ka tasi dooru maa ka di yi ka kyɛ pɔ'a maa ka u daa doaai yooma n‑yi na n‑tum n‑ti ba.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Ninsaala Bii maa nin bakki n‑gaari hoo din pun sɔbi n‑zaali u zugu seem la. Amaa ŋɔn mun figsi Ninsaala Bii maa n‑kyɛ ka ba gbaai u - di tɔ n‑ti u! Ba yi daa kyɛ di daama dɔ'am, di naa n‑sɔa.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Tɔ; ka wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti lura, ka niriba pam daa zi ba baaridima n‑kyɛ u saani na, ka u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa sɔkam zaa zugu, ka tibi ba.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti diri maa, u daa zaŋŋi diseebu m‑puusi Naawunni pɛ'abu ka ŋmaai n‑zaŋŋi n‑tisi ba ka yɛli, Ya deaama; n ningbina nla.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Ka arizimbeeri daa yi na ka kyɛ niriba pam, ka kur tasiri n‑yɛtti, ni, Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la!, amaa u daa nyikki ba ni, ka bu sakki ni ba tɔ'asiri lala, dama ba daa baŋŋiya, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya, Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ la.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Ka laa n‑zaŋŋi kɔbeaa m‑puusi Naawunni ka zaŋŋi n‑ti ba, ka ba zaa daa nyu ka puuni.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa yi timpuuni n‑kpɛ moo ni. Ka ba zaa daa bɔ m‑bɔ nti nya u, ka susiri u n‑yɛnni, ni, u di laa n‑kyaŋŋi seela ka kyɛ ba;.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: U daa yɛli ba, N ziim nla. N ziim wa nin ti yi na, ka di yiibu na nin kyɛ ka nangbanyinni zaalipaalli ti zaani, n‑tiri niriba pam.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: amaa u daa yɛli ba, Di nyɛ la talasi ni m mooli Naawunni naam lahabasuŋŋu n‑gyili, ka tinseesi an kpalim la zaa gba wum: dizugu ka Naawunni tum ma na.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, n ku laa n‑nyu tiwala daam wa taabu yaasa, halli ni dindahinseaa man nin nyu di bumpaalli, Naawunni naam beesigu ni.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni nti kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑yiisiri wa'azu.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ka ba daa yuum yuuma m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Ban daa yuum n‑naai maa, ba daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Ɔlivii Zoori kpaŋŋa.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya zaa nin wurim ka kyɛ ma, vi zugu. Pa lala ka di daa sɔbi n‑dooli? - ni, N nin ŋmɛ pɛgura maa ka peesi maa wurim.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Amaa man yi ti doaai kum ni n nin daŋŋi ya tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Ka Piita yɛli u, Ba zaa yi ti wurim ka kyɛ i, vi zugu, man ku niŋi lala.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, zuna yuŋŋu wa i mammaŋŋa nin kyiisi, ni, i zi ma; i nin ti yɛli lala napɔŋŋu buta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum buyi.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Ka Piita labsi n‑yɛli ni nimmoaa, ni, Halli di yi zaan kum ni, n ku kyiisi i. Ka u taaba maa zaa gba daa yɛli lala.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai ya'aseelli ban boonni Gyɛtisamanni. Ka Yisa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, Ya zinma kpa ka n kyaŋŋi n‑susi Naawunni ka kyɛ na.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: U daa zaŋŋi la Piita ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni ka ba dɔli u, ka di daa mugsi u pam ka beerim daa gbaai u ka di daa wum u pam. Ka u daa yɛli ba, Beerim din bɛ n sufu ni wa zoaaya pam, n‑ŋmanni din ya ku ma. Ya kpalimma kpa ka gura.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Sufusa'aŋŋu ni beerim daa gbaai u ka di daa wum u pam, ka u daa yɛli ba, Beerim din bɛ n sufu ni wa zoaaya pam, n‑ŋmanni din ya ku ma. Ya kpalimma m‑bɛ kpa ka gura.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: U daa kyaŋŋi tooni beela m‑paasi nti zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑gbaani tiŋŋa ka susiri n‑yɛtti, ni, di yi nin toaai, ni, namsigu wa ŋɔn gaari ka kyɛ u.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Ka susi, ni, Abba , m Ba, seelikam bu gaari i. Kyɛ ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa gaari ka kyɛ ma: di kyɛ ka n nyu di! Amaa di pa la man ŋɔn boori seem la, see nyin ŋɔn boori seem maa niŋma.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: U daa labi na n‑nya, ka babata maa zaa daa gbisiri ni; ka u booli Piita, ni, Saimoon, i gbisiri ni? I na'a ku toaai n‑gali goom halli saha beela.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba zaa, ni, Ya gura, ka susiri Naawunni ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba di zamsi ya halli ka ya ti lu. Ya sufu boori sakkri amaa ya ningbina ka yaa ka za'asiri ni.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Ka u daa laa m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi n‑laa n‑susiri lala yaasa.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa laa n‑labi m‑paai na, ba daa gbisiri ni, dama ba nini daa mumni ni, dizugu ba daa bu mi ban ya labsi n‑yɛli u seem.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi ka labi na napɔmbuta la, u daa yɛli ba, Ya na kpalim n‑gbisiri ka vuusira. Ni, Di sakkiya! Saha maa paaya. Ya nyama, ba pun yi n nyaaŋŋa ka figsi man Ninsaala Biiya n‑ti ala'akyi dima.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ya doaama ka ti kyaŋŋi! Minaafikka maa n kyɛnni na wa.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri maa, Gyuudasi daa paai na, ŋɔn daa nyɛ u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la yinni yinni. Niriba pam daa dɔli u: ba daa yi la gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni karimbadima ni kpamma saani na, ka ba daa mari ba takoobidima ni dɔya na.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Minaafikka maa daa pun wulisi u nyaaŋŋa maa ŋɔn ya n‑niŋi seem n‑kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi Yisa, ni, Man nin puusi sɔ suŋŋasuŋŋa la, ŋɔna nla. Ya gbaam u m‑mari suŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Ka dizugu Gyuudasi daa paai na nti kyaŋŋi yooma m‑paai Yisa saani, ka yɛli u, N Karimba! ka daa puusi u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ka ba daa gbaai u kirikiri.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Ka ban daa zɛ n‑tabi bɛni maa sɔ daa foaai u takoobi n‑kyɛ kpambaya bisɔ n‑ŋmaai u tubri m‑basi.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, N nyɛ la ban zabra ka faari neema la sɔ, bee wula? - ka ya mari takoobidima ni dɔya wa n‑yi na ni ya gbaai ma dizugu?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Beoo kam n daa yi bɛ la ya saani, n‑daa zintiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisiri niriba, ka ya daa bu gbaai ma. Amaa see ka di siri niŋi hoo Naawunni Litaafi ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Ka ba zaa daa zɔ ka kyɛ u.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Ka na'akyimbilisɔ n daa bɛ bɛni, u mun daa gɔbi kyinkyinni kɔkɔ n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: Ka ba daa gbaai bii wa, amaa u daa zɔ m‑bakki yɔri ka u kyinkyinni maa kpalim ba nuu ni.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Ba daa mari Yisa n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana saani, bɛni ka gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni kpamma ni karimbadima maa zaa daa la'am na.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Ka Piita daa sɔ'ai n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa sa, halli nti kpɛ Toondaana maa dundɔŋŋu ni. U daa la'am la soogyadima maa saani n‑zi bɛni n‑wuusiri bugum.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Kpamma maa, ni la'aŋŋu dima zaa, daa moori la ni ba nya sɛhara n‑galim Yisa ka ku u, amaa ba daa bu nya lala sɛhara.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Sɛharadima pam daa yi na m‑ma'ai ziri, amaa a daa bu dɔli taaba.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Ka baseeba daa doaai n‑naai noori ni ba ti ma'ai ziri anŋɔ taabu, ni ba ti kpeesi u yɛla ni.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: ni, Ti daa wum ka u yɛli, ni, u nin sa'am Gyeeŋŋu Duseaa ninsaalidima ŋɔn mɛ maa, ka zaŋŋi daba ata m‑mɛ seaa niriba nuusi ŋɔn ku toaai m‑mɛa.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Ni di zaa ba sɛhara daa bu dɔli taaba deede.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa doaai ba zaa tooni m‑boosi Yisa, ni, I ka labsigu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban tiri yɛttɔ'aseesi m‑paari i wa.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa sina ka daa bu labsi n‑yɛli u seelli maa, Toondaana maa daa laa m‑boosi u n‑yɛli, Nyini n nyɛ Masiiya ŋɔn ya fa ti m‑basi ka di naam la? Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi, ni, Mani nla; ka#:- Ya nin nya Ninsaala Bii maa Ka u zi Naawunni Yaadaana maa nudirigu ya'ari Ka kyɛnni na ka saazugu sangbana gyiligi u. .

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Ka Toondaana maa suuri daa yisigi, halli ka u daa toaai u mammaŋŋa sutura dizugu ka yɛli, Ti bu laa m‑boori sɛharadima yaasa, bee bɔa?.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Ya bu wum ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an kyisiri wa? Ya teesi la wula? Ka ba zaa daa yi Yisa nyaaŋŋa, ni, u niŋi taalli - taalli kan sumni la kuubu.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Ba ninvugseeba daa piili n‑tusiri nantoori n‑saatiri Yisa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi bunni n‑vili u nini ka daa lɔbri u tampɛkka ka boosiri u, ni, Nyin anabi wa, bugma m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ŋmɛ i wa! Ka soogyadima maa daa deaai u ka gba ŋmɛ u.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Tɔ; di saha ka Piita daa na kpalim m‑bɛ tiŋni dundɔŋŋu maa puuni. Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana yiri maa pɔ'asarisɔ daa kpɛ na.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: n‑nya u ka u wuusiri bugum maa. Ka pɔ'asariga maa daa yɛli Nyin gba yi bɛ la Yisa, Nazarɛti nira wa, saani.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Amaa u mun daa kyiisiya; ka daa yɛli, ni, M bu mi! - M bu baŋŋi nyin tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi wa gbinni! Ka daa yi n‑kpɛ bunzɔŋŋu ni. Di saha maa ka nɔroo daa kum.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Ka pɔ'asariga maa daa nya u bɛni ka daa laa m‑piili yaasa n‑yɛtti n‑tiri ban zɛ bɛni maa, ni, Ŋɔnwa nyɛ la niriba wa yinni.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ka Piita daa kyiisi yaasa. Din daa paasi beela, ban daa zɛ bɛni maa daa laa n‑yɛli Piita, ni, I ku toaai n‑kyiisi, ni, i pa ba nyaandɔla, dama i gba yi la Galilii na.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Ka Piita daa piili m‑poori noori n‑yɛtti yɛlikyɔ'ama, ni, M pɔya, ni, n yɛli la yɛlimaŋni. M bu mi dɔsɔ yan tɔ'asiri u yɛla maa.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Di saha maa ka nɔroo daa kum m‑paasi buyi, ka Piita teaai Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli u seem la, ni, I nin kyiisi ma napɔŋŋu buta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum buyi. Ka u daa zɔ n‑yi ka kumna.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Vuusim Dabsiri maa ŋɔn daa ti gaari ka Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, ni Mariama Gyeemsi ma la, ni Saloomi, daa da kpanyuuma ni ba kyaŋŋi nti soosi Yisa ningbiŋŋu.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Alahari daari bɛkyikyeoo ka ba daa kyɛnni Yisa gballi maa ziiya.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Ba daa bɛ soori ni ka daa boosiri taaba, ni, Ŋɔni n ya m‑biligi kugri la gballi maa noori ni n‑ti ti.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Amaa ba daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka kugri maa pun biligi ni. Kugtibsiri n daa nyɛ ka.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Ba daa kpɛ gballi maa puuni, ka daa nya na'akyimbilisɔ, ka u yɛ neempeela faratiti n‑zi gballi maa ni, ba nudirigu ya'ari, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya. ni, Ya boori la Yisa ziiya, Nazarɛti dɔsɔ ban daa kpaasi la. U yigsiya. U laa n‑ka kpa. Ya nyam ban daa dooli u seela nŋɔa!.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Amaa ya kyɛma n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni Piita, ni, u daŋŋi ba tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii. Bɛni ka ba nin nya u, hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli ba seem la.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Di daa gyɛri pɔ'aba maa pam n‑ŋma ba yam; ka dizugu ba daa zɔ n‑yi gballi maa ni na. Dabeem ŋɔn daa mari ba pam la zugu, ba daa bu yɛli sɔ seelli. .

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: U daa yisigi Alahari daari asuba pinipini, ka di dabsiri maa u daa daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka Mariama Magdala pɔ'a la n nya u, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa kari arizimbeeri ayɔpɔi u zugu ni la.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Mariama daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa nyaandɔliseeba la saani, ka ba daa zi la ni sufusa'aŋŋu n‑kumna; ka u daa yɛli ba di lahabaari maa.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Ban daa ti wum, ni, Yisa bɛ u nyɔvuri ni ka pɔ'a maa pun nya u, ba daa bu niŋi lala yɛdda.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba niriba ayi daa kyɛnni ba nɔba m‑pɔtti moo ni, ka Yisa beesigu daa takki ka u yi ba saani.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Ba daa labi nti yɛli ban kpalim la, amaa ba daa bu niŋi ba gba yɛligu maa yɛdda.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Tɔ. Ka u daa naayi zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi u nyaandɔliba pii ni yinni la, ban daa ti diri diibu sahanseaa la, ka daa tɛrisi ba pam ba yɛdda kaalinsi maa zugu, ni ba tuppuwumni zugu, dama ba taaba la daa nya u u kum yisigri maa nyaaŋŋa ka ban mun daa bu niŋi ba yɛligu maa yɛdda.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya kyam nti gyili duniya lugri kam zaa m‑mooli lahabasuŋŋu wa yɛla n‑ti Naawunni ŋɔn naam sɔ zaa.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Ka ŋɔnkam niŋi yɛdda n‑su Naawunni koom nin nya tiliginsim, amaa ŋɔnkam za'asi ka bu niŋi yɛdda nin baasi yɔri.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Ka tunkara anŋɔ nin bɛ yɛdda dima saani: n yuuri maa ni ka ba nin kari arizimbeeri n‑yiisi niriba ni, ba nin tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: ba yi ti gbaai wugi ni ba nuusi, bee, n‑nyu tibeoo, di bu ya niŋi ba sɛseelli; ba nin zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa baaridima zugri ka ba nya alaafɛa.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Tɔ. Ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi dinŋɔ zaa n‑ti ba n‑naai, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u n‑duusi arizana ni ka u zini ŋɔn Naawunni nudirigu ziiya.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: U nyaandɔliba mun daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi ya'ari kam zaa nti mooli lahabaari maa. Ka ti Dugma daa bɛ ba saani n‑tumna, ka u daa wulisi, ni, ba yɛlisim nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, u tunkara seesi an daa dɔli ba maa zugu. Ammi .

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: M Ba Tiyɔfiluusi#:- Niriba pam daa kpaŋŋi ba mansi n‑sɔbi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din daa niŋi ti saani.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Ba daa sɔbi yɛttɔ'a maa hoo niriba ŋɔn daa yɛli ti seem maa, ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ di piiligu ni ka nya din daa niŋi la, nti leebi ban moonni lahabaari maa.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Lala zugu man gba daa teesi, ni, di sumni ni n sɔbi yɛla maa deede n‑ti i. Man pun dɔli bunseelli kam zaasa balabala halli nti kyɛ ka i baŋŋi din siri nyɛ yɛlimaŋni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛliseesi ban daa wulisi i la zaa.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Tɔ. Sahanseaa ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa nyɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu naaba la, dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Zakariiya. Ban maanni maaligu n‑tiri Naawunni la yinni n daa nyɛ u, ka daa yi la maalimaanniba zuliyaseaa ban daa boonni Abaigyadima la ni na. Ka u pɔ'a gba daa nyɛ la Aruuna za'anɔri ni pɔ'a, ka u yuuri boonni Ilizabɛti.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Ka babayi maa zaa daa nyɛ la ninvugsuma Naawunni saani, ka daa dɔli ti Dugma Naawunni zaaligu soori ni u kaya zaa, ka ka galinsi.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Amaa ba daa ka biiya, dama Ilizabɛti daa nyɛ la kundɔ'ara. M‑paasi yaasa, babayi maa zaa daa laa n‑kurigi ni pam.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Dahinseaa ka di daa ti paai ŋɔn Zakariiya ni u ya'ari dima ni ba tumni ba tuma n‑tiri Naawunni ba maalimaanniba soori ni, ka ba daa yiisi u ni u kpɛ ti Dugma Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni, ni u ti nyoaai turaali zum.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Ka daadam maa zaa daa zɛ dundɔŋŋu maa ni n‑susiri Naawunni lala turaali zum nyoaari saha maa.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi Naawunni saani na, nti zɛ turaali zum maa nyoaari zii nudirigu ya'ari.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Ka Zakariiya ŋɔn daa nya malaaka maa, u daa yirigi ni, ka dabeem gbaai u.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli u, Zakariiya, di zɔri dabeem, dama Naawunni daa pun wum i susigu, ka i pɔ'a Ilizabɛti nin ti dɔ'ai bidibga n‑ti i, ka i nin ti puri u yuuri Gyɔni.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: I nin ti mari sufupeelli ni sufumaasim pam i bii wa zugu, ka niriba pam gba nin ti mari sufupeelli u dɔ'am maa zugu.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: Dama u nin ti niŋi ninvugtitaari ti Dugma Naawunni soori ni. Ka u ku ti nyu dazeoo bee dapeelli, ka Naawunni Vuusim Sii Kasi nin ti kyɛnni u zugu ni na halli u dɔ'am saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: U nin ti kyɛ ka ya Izraɛli dima pam nin ti ŋmaligi n‑labi ya Dugma Naawunni saani. Ka u nin mari siiya ni yiiko hoo anabi Ilaygya ŋɔn daa mari seem la.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: nti zaŋŋi anabi Ilaygya sii maa ni u yiiko gba n‑daŋŋi Naawunni tooni na. U nin kyɛ ka yaseeba ban nyɛ yaadima la sufu ti labi m‑pɛligi ba ya'ansi zugu, ka kyɛ ka tuppuwumni dima ti takki n‑nya yɛm hoo ninvugseeba ban wumni Naawunni noori ŋɔn mari seem la, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka niriba maali siri n‑guri ti Dugma.
