eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Ka Zakariiya daa yɛli malaaka maa, N kurigiya ni, ka m pɔ'a gba kurigi. Ka wula ka n nin toaai n‑niŋi dinŋɔ yɛdda.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Mani n nyɛ Gyeebriyɛli, sɔ ŋɔn bɛ Naawunni tooni la. Ka ŋɔn Naawunni n tum ma na, ni n ti tɔ'asi i ka zaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu wa n‑ti i. N yɛttɔ'a maa saha yi ti paai, di nin niŋi lala. Amaa i bu niŋi di yɛdda.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: ka dizugu i ya leebi la zɔligu zuna wa, ka ku laa n‑tɔ'asi halli ka di saha maa ti paai.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Ka niriba maa daa guusiri Zakariiya, ka di ti gyɛri ba ŋɔn yuui Naawunni Duu maa ni zugu.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti yi na, u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba. Ka ba baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔn bɛ Duu maa ni maa, u nya la zii. Ka u yi mari la u nuusi n‑tɔ'asiri m‑bugsiri ba, ka yi kpalim n‑kɔŋŋi yɛttɔ'a.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Ka di daa niŋi dabsiseesi ka u maaligu tuma dabsa daa ti naai, ka u daa kuli u yiri.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka u pɔ'a Ilizabɛti daa gbaai puuwa, n‑sɔ'ari u maŋŋa niriba saani halli nti paai u gɔya anu daari.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni, Pampaŋŋɔ ti Dugma yi teaai n yɛla, ka niŋi ma nŋɔa, ni u yiisi ma vi ni niriba saani.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Tɔ; ka Ilizabɛti puu maa gɔya ayoobu saha, Naawunni daa tum u malaaka Gyeebriyɛli maa ka u kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni, ka ka yuuri boonni Nazarɛti la.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: Ka u kyaŋŋi pɔ'asarisɔ saani, ka pɔ'a maa mun yuuri boonni Mariama. Pɔ'asariga maa daa na zi n‑dooni ni doowa, amaa ba daa pun basi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin kuli dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi, ka u mun nyɛ NaaDauda za'anɔri biiya.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Tɔ. Di daa niŋi lala, ka saha kanŋɔna ka Room NaaTitaari Agasitisi daa tisi noori ni ba ti sɔbri u suulinsi ni niriba zaa yuya.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa ti paai Mariama saani na, nti yɛli u, ni, M puusiri i ni, nyin pɔ'asɔ Naawunni ŋɔn suŋŋi pam wa! Ti Dugma Naawunni bɛ i saani.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Ka sɔkam zaa daa kunni u toori tiŋŋa ni ba ti sɔbi u yuuri.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Ŋɔn daa puusi u seem maa daa gyɛri u pam, ka u birisiri di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seela.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Ka Gyoosɛfi gba daa yi Nazarɛti, Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni, nti sigi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtlihɛm, Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni. Ban daa dɔ'ai NaaDauda tinseaa nla; Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ la NaaDauda za'anɔri nira, dizugu ka u daa kyaŋŋi bɛni ni u ti sɔbi u yuuri.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli u, Mariama, di zɔri dabeem, ka i nya zugsuŋŋu Naawunni saani.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: Ŋɔn ni Mariama n daa la'am n‑kyaŋŋi, dama Mariama daa pun basi nangbambasiri, ni, u kuli u. Di saha maa ka Mariama daa mari pukarigu.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Wumma! I nin gbaai puuwa n‑dɔ'ai bidibga, ka i nin ti puri u yuuri Yisa.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Ban daa paai bɛni m‑bɛ ni maa, u dɔ'am saha daa ti paai.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: U nin ti mari tibsim, ka ba nin ti boonni u la Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya. Ka ti Dugma Naawunni nin ti zaŋŋi naam n‑diisi u, u yaaba NaaDauda ŋɔn daa di naseebu la.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Ka u daa dɔ'ai u pɔ'asaridɔ'am bidibga, ka zaŋŋi tana n‑vili u, n‑dooli bunkɔbri diibu bunni ni, dama ba daa bu nya doaa ziiya saamma yiri maa ni.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Ka u nin ti di naam n‑su ya yaaba Yaakubu butiri, halli ni saha din ka tɔriga, ka u naam maa ku ti mari naari.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Tɔ; ka pɛguriba daa bɛ tiŋŋa maa moo ni yuŋŋu, n‑guri ba peesi.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Ka Mariama daa yɛli malaaka maa, N na zi n‑dooni ni doowa. Ka wula ka dinŋɔ nin toaai n‑niŋi.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi ba saani na, ka ti Dugma gyirima neesim daa neaai n‑gyiligi ba, ka dabeem daa kpɛ ba pam.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Naawunni Sii Kasi Vuusim nin sigi i zugu na, m‑bɛ i ni, ka Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa wa yiiko nin ti bɛ i saani. Dizugu nyin ti ya dɔ'ai bikasi sɔ maa, ba nin ti boonni u la, ni, Naawunni Biiya.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli ba, Ya di zɔri dabeem! Ka n zi la lahabasuŋŋu na ni n ti ya, ka ka mari sufupeelli pam, ka nyɛ sɔkam zaa dinni.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Baŋŋma i dɔ'ara Ilizabɛti yɛla: ammi, ba daa bu boonni u, ni, kundɔ'ara? Tɔ: ŋɔn mun mari bidibga puuwa, ni ŋɔn kurigi maa zaa yɔri, ka ka gɔya ayoobu nŋɔa.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Dama ba da dɔ'ai ya Tuligra zuna yuŋŋu wa. NaaDauda tiŋŋa ka ba da dɔ'ai u. Ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya, Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiri, ni, u zaŋŋi sɔ n‑tum na, ka u nyɛ ti Dugma la.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Dama sɛseelli bu tɔ n‑nyaŋŋi Naawunni yiiko.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Amaa dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi, ni, n yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: ya nin ti nya bileaa maa ka ba vili u tana n‑dooli bunkɔbri diibu bunni puuni.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Ka Mariama daa yɛli u, Ti Dugma n su ma. Kyɛ ka di niŋi ma hoo nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem maa. Ka malaaka maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ u.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Di saha maa ka malaaka bɔbgu daa yi arizana n‑kyɛ na, m‑paasi malaaka yinni maa zugu, ka ba yuumni m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta#:-.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Mariama daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi yoomayooma n‑kyaŋŋi zɔya ni, nti paai tinseaa kan bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni la.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ saazugu mari gyirima pam, Ka daadam ban bɛ duniya wa ni nya sufudoaa ni nangbanyinni, Naawunni ŋɔn wuri ba zugu.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: M‑paai bɛni nti kpɛ Zakariiya yiri m‑puusi Ilizabɛti.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Malaakadima maa ŋɔn daa basi ba ka labi arizana ni maa, pɛguriba maa daa yɛtti la taaba, ni, Dinŋɔna, ti kyɛm Bɛtlihɛm nti nya bunseelli din niŋi maa, ti Dugma ŋɔn wulisi ti seelli la.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ilizabɛti ŋɔn daa wum Mariama puusigu maa, bii maa daa damni u puuri ni, ka Ilizabɛti paali ni Naawunni Sii Kasi.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi yooma, m‑paai m‑bɔ n‑nya Mariama ni Gyoosɛfi, ni bileaa maa, ka u dɔ bunkɔbri diibu bunni maa puuni.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: Ka u daa tasi n‑yɛli pampam, ni, Nyini m mari zugsuŋŋu n‑gaari pɔ'aba zaa, ka bisɔ ŋɔn bɛ i puuri ni maa gba mari la zugsuŋŋu!.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Ban daa nya lala maa, ba daa yɛli malaaka ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi bii maa yɛla seem la n‑ti ba.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: M mun nyɛ la ŋɔni, halli ka n Dugma maa ma kyɛ n saani na wa?.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa daa wum pɛguriba maa lahabaari maa, di daa yi niŋi ba la alahazibu.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Man ŋɔn kur wum i puusigu maa, bisɔ ŋɔn bɛ m puuri ni wa damni ni sufupeelli zugu.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Mariama mun daa zaŋŋi yɛla maa zaa n‑niŋi u sufu ni, n‑kur teesiri a yɛla.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: I niŋi yɛdda, ni, ti Dugma ŋɔn tɔ'asi seelli n‑ti i la siri nin niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ka di zugu nyini n nyɛ sufumaasim daana.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Ka pɛguriba maa daa labi n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima ni pɛ'abu ban daa wum ka nya seelli maa zugu. Ka di siri daa nyɛ la hoo malaaka maa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Ka Mariama yɛli#:- N tibgri ti Dugma n sufu ni.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Tɔ; ka bii maa dabsa anii daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ba daa gunni u ka puri u yuuri Yisa, dama ban daa na bu gbaai u puu saha seaa la, malaaka maa daa pun yɛli, ni, ba boolim u yuuri lala.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn tuligi ma la zugu ka n sufu pɛligi pam.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Tɔ. Di nyaaŋŋa ka saha daa ti paai ni Gyoosɛfi ni Mariama kyaŋŋi nti tum kaman Musa zaaligu ŋɔn yɛli seem, ni, ba maalim Mariama dɔ'am kasitiri maaligu. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi bii maa n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm, ni ba ti zaŋŋi u n‑ti ti Dugma.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: Dama u teaai u daabri tarintiri yɛla. Ka zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, Zamaana zaa nin ti boonni ma sufumaasim daana;.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Ba daa niŋi kaman din sɔbi n‑yɛli ti Dugma zaaligu ni seem, ni, ba zaŋŋma ba bikpamma, bidibsi la, n‑tisi ti Dugma.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Dama Yiiko Zaa Daana tum tunkara n‑ti ma, Ka u yuuri n nyɛ kasi.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Ka daa maali maaligu maa kaman ti Dugma zaaligu ŋɔn yɛli seem la, ni, ba kɔrim ŋmanzeesi ayi bee ŋmambisi ayi m‑maali maaligu.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Ka ban zɔri u la muna - Ban ka ŋɔn mun zɔri ba namboo saha kam.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Tɔ; dɔsɔ n daa bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm, ka u yuuri boonni Simiyɔn, ka doo wa daa zɔri Naawunni ka dɔli u zaaligu suŋŋa: ka guusiri Izraɛli dima tuligra soori; ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi daa bɛ u ni.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: U zaŋŋi u nuu ni yiiko n‑ŋmɛ karimbaani dima maa - Ba teesibeeri nin wurim m‑basi.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Sii Kasi daa pun kasigi n‑yɛli u, ni, faasee ka u ti nya ti Dugma Masiiya pɔi ka naayi kpi.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: U yiisi la nadima ba naama ni, Ka tibgri tarimma.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Ka Naawunni Sii maa daa kyɛ ka u kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Di saha maa ka Gyoosɛfi ni Mariama daa mari Yisa n‑kpeeri na ni ba ti niŋi u hoo zaaligu kaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: U zaŋŋi bunsuma n‑diisi kɔm ŋɔn mari seeba maa, Ka kari bundaandima ka ba bakki ni nuzaala.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Ka Simiyɔn daa deaai bii maa n‑zali, ka pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta#:-.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: U suŋŋi u daaba Izraɛli dima, Ka teaai ti yɛla n‑zɔ ti namboo;.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: Dizugu, n Dugma, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, I nin kyɛ ka m bakki duniya wa ka kyaŋŋi ni sufudoaa; Hoo nyin ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiri n‑ti ma seem la.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: Hoo ŋɔn daa yɛli ti yaadima seem la. U zɔ Ibrahim ni u nyaaŋŋa dima namboo Halli ni saha din ka naari.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Dama n nini nya i Tuligra maa.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Ka Mariama daa kpalim m‑bɛ Ilizabɛti saani n‑niŋi kaman gɔya ata, ka laa n‑labi n‑kuli u yiri.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: nyin daa zaŋŋi sɔ n‑ti butiri kam zaa wa.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Ka Ilizabɛti dɔ'am saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa dɔ'ai bidibga.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ neesim din ya neaai tinzundima M‑bugsi ba i soori; Ka i niriba muna, Izraɛli dima - I nin taasi gyirima na n‑ti ba.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Ka u ziziitaaba ni u dɔ'ariba daa wum, ni, ti Dugma zɔ u namboo pam, ka ŋɔn ni ban zaa daa mari sufupeelli.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Tɔ; ka Simiyɔn ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi bii maa yɛla maa, di daa niŋi bii maa ba ni u ma alahazibu.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Bii maa dabsa anii daari ka ba daa la'am na ni ba gunni u ka puri u yuuri. Ba daa ya zaŋŋi la u ba Zakariiya yuuri m‑puri u.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Ka Simiyɔn daa susi Naawunni susisuma n‑niŋi ba zugu, ka yɛli u ma Mariama, ni, Dinŋɔ ka Naawunni zaali, ni bii wa nin kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima pam ti sigsi ba maŋŋa ka laa n‑du yaasa. U nin ti laa n‑leebi ŋɔn ya wulisi niriba tuliginsim soori, ka baseeba nin za'asi u yɛla.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: ka u ma Ilizabɛti mun daa za'asi ka yɛli, ni, U yuuri nin boonni Gyɔni.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Ka i gba sufu nin sa'am di zugu, kaman takoobi ŋɔn kusi nira seem la. Ka yɛlisɔ'ara nin ti yi niriba pam sufu ni na.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay! - I dɔ'arisɔsɔ ka ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyɔni.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Ka pɔ'asɔ gba daa bɛ ni, n‑nyɛ anabi, ka u yuuri boonni Anna. Ka nyɛ Fanuwɛli biiya, Aseeri za'anɔri ni nira. U daa kurigi pam.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Ka daa mari ba nuusi m‑bugsiri m‑boosiri u ba, ni, u boori bii maa yuuri boonni la wula?.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: dama u daa zinni doo yiri la yuuma ayɔpɔi, ka daa zinni pukɔtiri muna halli ni yuuma pisinii ni anaasi. U daa kur bɛ la Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, yuŋŋu ni wuntaŋŋa saha zaa, n‑gyeemni Naawunni ni wunsusigu ni noori loobu.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Ka u daa susi bunni n‑sɔbi, ni, Bii maa yuuri nyɛ la Gyɔni.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Ka pɔ'a wa daa kyɛ na di saha maa, m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni, ka tɔ'asiri bii maa yɛla n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa guusiri Gyɛrusalɛm tuliginsim dabsiri la zaa.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: Ka di daa gyɛri ba zaa. Di saha maa ka Zakariiya zilimni daa di lakki ka u yi tɔ'asiri m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa tum ti Dugma zaaligu ŋɔn nyɛ seem zaa n‑naai, ba daa labi n‑kuli Galilii, m‑paai ba toori tiŋŋa Nazarɛti.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Ka dabeem daa gbaai ba ziziitaaba maa zaa, ka niriba zaa daa kur diri taaba azama n‑tɔ'asiri yɛla wa Gyuudii zɔya tinsi zaasa ni.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Ka bii maa daa zoaai na, m‑mari yɛm ni yaa, ka Naawunni wurilim bɛ u ni pam.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa daa wum yɛla maa daa zaŋŋi a n‑niŋi u sufu ni ka teesiri, ni, Bii wa lee n ti ya nyɛ la ninvugbɔa? Dama Naawunni yaa n daa bɛ u ni.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Yuumni kam Yisa ba ni u ma daa yi kyɛnni la Gyɛrusalɛm nti diri Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Ka u ba Zakariiya daa paali ni Vuusim Kasi maa, ka tɔ'asi anabitiri ni, n‑yɛtta#:-.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Ka bii maa ŋɔn daa ti paai yuuma pii ni ayi maa, ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑di kyuu maa hoo ban diri ka yuumni kam seem. Ka kyuu maa dabsa ŋɔn daa ti naai ka ba labri n‑kunni la.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Ti pɛ'ari ti Dugma, Izraɛli Naawunni, Dama u suŋŋi ti n‑yiisi ti wahala ni.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Yisa daa kpalim la Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka u ba ni u ma bu mi, dama ba daa teesi, ni, u bɛ la daadam maa ni.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Ka u daa bɔ tuliginsim daana n‑ti ti na, Dauda za'anɔri biiya - Naawunni ŋɔn daa su NaaDauda maa.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Ka dizugu daa kyɛ ka ba kyaŋŋi la dabsimunni kyɛnni; di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa naayi boori u ziiya ba dɔ'ariba ni ba zɔdima saani.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Naawunni daa pun wulisi ti u yɛla, U daa kyɛ ka u anabidima daa yɛli ti, Yuuma an gaari la ni - Ninvugsuma n daa nyɛ ba!.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: N‑daa bɔ u n‑kɔŋŋi bɛni, ka labi Gyɛrusalɛm nti boori u ziiya.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: Ni, u nin tuligi ti ti dindima ni, Ka yiisi ti ban yaai ti zaa nuu ni.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Di dabsa ata daari ka ba daa nya u Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, ka u zi karimbadima saani, n‑wumsiri ba yɛttɔ'a, ka boosiri ba boosa.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Naawunni n daa pɔ noori n‑ti ti yaaba Ibrahim, Ni, u nin yiisi ti ti dindima nuusi ni, Ka kyɛ ka ti gyeemni u ka dabeem ka ti: Ka ti niŋi ninvugsuma m‑bɛ u tooni kasikasi, Halli ni ti nyɔvuri tɔriga. Dizugu ka u daa zɔ ti yaadima namboo, Ka daa bu tam u yɛlisuŋŋu ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa daa wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, di daa niŋi ba la alahazibu u yɛm gyɛrigri zugu.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: M biiya, ba nin ti boonni i la Anabi - Anabi n‑ti Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa. Dama i nin ti daŋŋi ti Dugma Naawunni tooni M‑maali u sɔya n‑ti u.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Ban daa nya u maa di daa gyɛri ba ni, ka u ma boosi u, ni, M biiya, wula ka i niŋi ti seem wa? Man ni i ba yi boori la i ziiya ni sufusa'aŋŋu.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: I nin wulisi u niriba yɛm, Ni ba nin baŋŋi tuliginsim ŋɔn nyɛ seem: Ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Bɔ n niŋi ka ya boori n ziiya? Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, di kpa la talasi ni m bɛ m Ba tuma maa ni.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Dama Naawunni zɔ ti namboo pam. Ka u kyɛ ka neesim yi saazugu n‑kyɛ ti saani na.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Ka ba daa bu mi ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa gbinni.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: N‑neaai tirim ban bɛ zibsim ni, ni kum puuni, Ka zaŋŋi nangbanyinni ni sufudoaa soori N‑wulisi ti nɔba.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Ka u daa dɔli ba n‑labi n‑kuli Nazarɛti, ka deaari ba noori. Ka u ma daa zaŋŋi yɛla maa zaa n‑niŋi u sufu ni.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Tɔ. Ka bii maa daa zoaai na, m‑mari sufukpeemni, m‑bɛ moo ni halli ni dahinseaa ŋɔn daa ti zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti Izraɛli dima la.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Ka Yisa bamsim ni u yɛm daa paasira, ka u mari zugu pam Naawunni ni ninsaalidima saani.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Tɔ; ka dahinseaa ka Yisa daa ti zɛ Gyɛnɛsarɛti mɔ'ari la noori ni, ka niriba pam kpa'ari n‑kyɛnni u saani na ni ba ti wum Naawunni yɛligu maa.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Ka u daa nya nyarima ayi seesi an daa kpalim n‑dɔya mɔ'aduuri la ni; ka zingbaariseeba ban daa su a maa mun daa kyɛ a n‑dooli ka kyaŋŋi ni ba ti peaai ba laama.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Ka Yisa daa kpɛ nyarinseaa ni, nyariŋŋu maa mun daa nyɛ la Saimoon dinni, ka dinŋɔna u daa bɛlim Saimoon, ni, u tugsim ka n‑kpɛ koom maa ni n‑zaali tooni beela. Ka Yisa daa zini nyariŋŋu maa puuni n‑wulisiri niriba maa.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti wulisi ba n‑naai, u daa yɛli Saimoon, Kyɛma nti paai mɔ'ari wa kɔzuliŋŋu ni sa, n‑lɔbi ya laama maa n‑gbaai zaasim.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Ka Saimoon labsi n‑yɛli u, N Daana, ti sa pun lɔbi a yuŋŋu la n‑neaai beoo, ka mun bu gbaai halli yinni gba; amaa nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem wa zugu, n nin lɔbi.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ka ban daa siri niŋi lala ba daa gbaai zaasim pam, halli ka ba laama maa ti boori a kyeesi.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ka ba daa booli ba zingbaaritaaba ni ba zaŋŋma nyarinseaa la n‑kyɛ na n‑suŋŋi ba. Ka ba daa kyɛ na nti suŋŋi ba ka yiisi zaasim maa m‑paali nyarima ayi maa zaa, halli ka a ti ya sim koom maa ni.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Saimoon Piita ŋɔn daa nya lala maa u daa gbaani tiŋŋa Yisa tooni ka yɛli u, N Dugma, kyɛma ka kyɛ man tumbeeri daana wa m‑basi. M bu sumsi m‑pɔri i saani na.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa gbaasi zaasim seem maa zugu di daa gyɛri Saimoon ni bankam daa bɛ u saani maa pam.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Ka di daa niŋi Zɛbidii biisi Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni ban daa nyɛ Saimoon tuntuntaaba la gba alahazibu. Ka Yisa daa yɛli Saimoon Di zɔri dabeem! I yi gbaari la zaasim halli ni zuna; amaa zuna zaŋŋi kyaŋŋi i ya leebi la ŋɔn gbaari ninsaalidima n‑tiri Naawunni.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Ban daa ti zaŋŋi ba nyarima maa na nti dooli duuri ni, ba daa kyɛ ba bunseelli kam zaa m‑basi ka yisigi n‑dɔli Yisa.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti bɛ tinseaa ni maa, dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka kɔmni daa ti wali u ningbina zaa. Doo maa daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti kuntiri u tooni m‑bɛlimni u n‑yɛtta, N daana, i yi da sakki lala, i naa kyɛ ka n kɔmni wa naai ka n kpɛ n‑taabi ni.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Ka Yisa daa teaai u nuu n‑siisi u ka yɛli, N sakkiya: di naama! Ka u kɔmni maa daa siri basi u lala saha maa.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ka Yisa daa kpaam u n‑yɛli, Di yɛli sɔa, amaa kur kyɛm nti zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑wulisi maalimaanna ka maali sariseesi Musa ŋɔn daa sɔbi n‑zaali zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kɔmni tippu zii la; dina nin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, i siri nya tippu.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Amaa Yisa yuuri daa maali n‑du pam n‑kyaŋŋi halli, ka daadam pam daa la'am na ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka u tibri ba dooru balibu kam la.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Ka Yisa mun daa yisigri n‑kyɛnni moo ni, ni u ti bɛ u kɔkɔ m‑puusiri ka susiri Naawunni.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Dahinseaa ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ duu ni n‑wulisiri niriba, ka Farisii dima ni Naawunni zaaligu karimbadima seeba daa kpɛ duu maa na n‑zintiri n‑wumni u yɛttɔ'a maa; ba daa pun yi Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa ni, ni Galilii ni Gyuudii tingbana ni m‑paasi n‑kyɛ na. Di saha maa ka Naawunni yiiko daa bɛ u ni n‑kyɛ ka u tibri niriba.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Ka ninvugseeba daa zaŋŋi ba baarisɔ ka u gbaya ayi zaa daa kpi, n‑niŋi u sɔŋŋu ni n‑zi u na; n‑kyɛ na nti boori soori ni ba zaŋŋi u n‑kpɛ n‑dooli Yisa tooni.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: ka kɔŋŋi, daadam maa ŋɔn daa zoaai m‑mugsiri u maa zugu. Dinŋɔ zugu ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑du la yiri maa pii saazugu nti kyiibi voori n‑yoaai n‑sigsi u, ŋɔn ni u sɔŋŋu maa zaa, voori maa ni, halli nti paai tiŋŋa; ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑dooli Yisa tooni lala.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ban niŋi u yɛdda seem maa zugu, u daa yɛli baara maa dizugu, ni, N zɔsuŋŋu, n kyɛ i taalli m‑pɛŋŋi i.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Ka karimbadima ni Farisii dima maa daa boosiri ba sufu ni, n‑teesiri n‑yɛtta, Bɔ n niŋi ka doo wa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑zaantiri Naawunni zaaziiya seem wa? Ŋɔni n lee n‑toaai n‑kyɛ niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba? Ka pa ni Naawunni kɔkɔa.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba sufu ni teesa maa zaa, ka boosi ba, ni, Bɔ zugu ka ya birisira?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Yɛla ayi wa sunsukkuni, kani m bu tɔa - i yi yɛli sɔ, ni, ba kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, bee i yi yɛli sɔ, ni, u doaama n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, Amaa m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba, duniya wa puuni. Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa, N ya n‑yɛli i, ni, doaama n‑zaŋŋi i bundoaa wa n‑kuli.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Ka doo maa daa siri doaai n‑zaŋŋi u bundoaa maa ba zaa nini ni m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni ka va n‑kuli.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Ka di daa gyɛri ba zaa pam; ka ba pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta, Ti siri nya alahazibu tuma zuna.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Tɔ. Ka dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa yi nti nya lampodeaarisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Liivai. Ŋɔnŋɔ mun daa zi ba lampoo tuma ziiya n‑tumna, ka Yisa yɛli u, Doaama n‑dɔli ma.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyɛ u bunseelli kam zaa ka dɔli u.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Ka ŋɔn Liivai daa dugi diibu u yiri maa ni, ka daa booli Yisa n‑zuusi u ni u kyɛm na n‑di. Ka lampodeaariba ni daadam pam daa paasi u zugu n‑la'am na nti dira.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Lala zugu ka Farisii dima ni karimbadima ban daa bɛ bɛni maa daa galim Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ka boosiri ba, ni, Wula ka yarima ni lampodeaariba ni tumbeeri dima la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ban mari alaafɛ bu boori tiimaraana, na'ala ban beeri m‑boori u.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Pa tunsuma dima zugu ka n kyɛ na, ni m boonni, amaa tumbeeri dima ka m boonni ni ba kyɛm na n‑takki ba beebu, ni ba sufu ni teesibeeri zaa.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Ba mun daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Seeba ban dɔli Gyɔni maa loori la noori sahakam ka puusiri aduuwa, ka Farisii dima nyaandɔliba gba n niŋni lala, amaa nyini mun nyaandɔliba wa n kur diri ni ka nyuura.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Ka u daa boosi ba, Dɔsɔ yi ti di pɔ'apaalli ka ba deemni dizugu, ammi, pɔ'apaalli sira maa yi ti bɛ u saamma sunsukkuni ba nin lɔ noori di saha maa, bee? Ay! Ba bu ya lɔa.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Amaa sahanseaa kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yiisi amriya doo maa ba saani - di dabsa maa puuni ka ba nin lɔ noori.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Ka u daa laa m‑paasi ba nyɛsiri n‑yɛli, Sɔ na zi n‑toaai bunyeeripaalli tanni n‑ligi bunyeerikurigu. U yi niŋi lala u nin sa'am bumpaalli maa, ka tampaalli maa ku ŋmani bunkurigu la.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Sɔ mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akurigu ni. U yi niŋi lala, daam maa nin ŋma ga'akurigu maa ka yuuli m‑basi, ka ga'ari maa gba sa'am.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Ay! Ba zaŋŋma ga'apaala n‑leeri dapaalli n‑niŋi a puuni.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyu daam m‑miili mun bu boori dapaalli; amaa u yɛli, ni, dakurigu kɔkɔ n nya'asa u saani.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Ka Vuusim dindahinseaa ka Yisa daa dɔli puri ni n‑gatta, ka u nyaandɔliba daa piili n‑kabri kasana n‑ŋmeemni ba nuusi ni n‑ŋɔbri kabeelim maa.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ka Farisiseeba n daa boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya tumni puri tuma wa? Ya galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya na zi n‑karim NaaDauda yɛla wa? Ni, ŋɔn ni u taaba n daa bɛ ni ka kɔm daa gbaai ba.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: ka u daa kpɛ Naawunni gyeeŋŋu zii maa n‑zaŋŋi boroboro n‑ŋɔbi ka laa n‑tisi u taaba maa, ka ba gba daa ŋɔbi. Pa boroboro anŋɔ taabu ka ba zaŋŋi n‑doontiri la Naawunni tooni? - ka a kyisiri ni, di yi pa la maalimaanniba kɔkɔ ŋɔbri a.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Man Ninsaala Bii n su Vuusim Dabsiri fukumsi gba.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Ka Vuusim dahinseaa yaasa ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu ni n‑wulisiri ba; ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka u nudirigu daa pun kpi ni.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Ka Farisii dima ni karimbadima maa daa boori sɔseaa ni ba galim u; dizugu ba daa gɔsiri u ni, ni ba nya u nin tibi sɔ Vuusim Dabsiri, bee?.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Amaa Yisa daa mi ba sufu ni teesa ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka u daa yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, Doaama n‑zaani sunsukkuni!, ka u doaai n‑zaani.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, M boosiri ya ni: Vuusim Dabsiri muna, soori bɛ ni ni nira tum tunsuma di dabsiri maa, bee tumbeeri? M‑paasi yaasa, u tuligma sɔ nyɔvuri, bee u sa'am ka.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Ka u daa gɔsi n‑gyiligi ba zaa na, ka yɛli doo maa, Teaam i nuu maa!, ka ŋɔn daa niŋi lala, u nuu maa daa nya tippu.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Amaa ba sufu daa yisigi pam ka ba tɔ'asiri ni taaba ba nin niŋi Yisa seem.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Tɔ. Ka di saha maa ka Yisa daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti susi Naawunni, ka daa kur kpalim m‑bɛ ni yuŋŋu maa zaa n‑susiri Naawunni.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa booli u nyaandɔliba zaa ni ba la'am na, ka u daa gaŋŋi ba puuni ninvugpiiya ni bayi, ka daa laa m‑booli ba, ni, Tumudima la.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Ba yuya nŋɔa#:- Saimoon , ni u tinzɔ Anduru; Gyeemsi, Gyɔni, Filipi, Baatɔlɔmiyu.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: Matiyu, Tɔmaasi, Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Saimoon sɔ .

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: ni Gyeemsi Gyuudasi, ni Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti .

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Ka ŋɔn ni u tuumudima wa n daa yi zoori maa zugu n‑sigi na, ka u nyaandɔliseeba pam daa la'am na, ka daadam daa zoaai m‑paasi ba zugu; baseeba ban daa yi Gyuudiiya na, ni Gyɛrusalɛm, ni tinseesi an bɛ teeku duuri ziiya hoo Taaya ni Saidoon. Ba zaa daa kyɛ na ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a maa ka tibi ba dooru balibu kam la.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ka arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa mugsi seeba maa zaa daa nya tippu.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: ka niriba maa zaa daa kpaŋŋi ba mansi ni ba paai Yisa saani n‑siisi u, dama yiiko n daa yiiri u ni na n‑tibri ba zaa.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Ka u daa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi u nyaandɔliba maa ka yɛli#:- Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban nyɛ faradima, dama Naawunni naam nyɛ la ya dinni.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba kɔm ŋɔn mari pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti di n‑tigi. Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban kumni pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti laara.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ya, sahanseaa niriba yi yaai ya, ka kari ya m‑basi, ka tuuri ya ka za'asiri ya yuya, ni, a nyɛ la yubeeri, ka ba niŋi lala man Ninsaala Bii zugu.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Di nin wulisi, ni, ya laara zoaaya pam arizana ni; ka dizugu ya kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi dindaari maa, ka ya waam sufumaasim waa. Ya bu nya? - lala ka ba badima n daa niŋi anabidima la.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli, ni, Amaa#:- Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban nyɛ bundaandima, dama ya pun deaai ya nya'asim la.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban diri ya yuubu pampaŋŋɔa, dama kɔm nin ti gbaai ya. Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban laari pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti kaasiri ka kumna.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti ya, sahanseaa niriba zaasa yi tiri ya yusuma; ba badima daa pun niŋi lala n‑ti ziri anabidima la - Bee, m ma'ai ni.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Ni, Amaa n yɛtti ya ban wumni n yɛligu maa, ni: Ya wura ya dindaandima; ya tumma din veela n‑tiri ban namsiri ya.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Ya susim bunsuma n‑niŋi ban yɛtti ya zulima, ka laa n‑susi Naawunni n‑tiri ban tuuri ya gba.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u. Sɔ mun yi fa i bunyeerigu ka boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Ŋɔnkam susi i bunseelli, nyin zaŋŋma n‑ti u, ka sɔ mun yi kpikki i neema, nyin di laa m‑boosi u a yɛla.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Dinŋɔna ya yi boori ni niriba niŋi seelli n‑ti ya, ya niŋma lala n‑ti ba gba.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya yi wuri seeba ban wuri ya, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? Ay! Halli ala'akyi dima gba niŋni lala.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ka ya yi niŋni din veela n‑tiri ban niŋi ya suŋŋa maa, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? Ay! Halli ala'akyi dima gba niŋni lala.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Ya yi mari ya buna m‑pɛŋŋni n‑tiri ninvugseeba yan yɛnni, ni, ba siri nin yɔ n‑labsi n‑ti ya, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? Ay! Halli ala'akyi dima gba pɛŋŋni n‑tiri ba ala'akyi daantaaba ni ba nin ti labsi n‑ti ba bummunni yaasa.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Amaa see ka ya wuri ya dindaandima ka tumni tunsuma ka pɛŋŋni ya buna n‑tiri niriba kam, ka bu teesiri, ni, ya ti laa n‑deaai a yaasa. Din saha maa ka ya laara nin zoaai pam, ka ya nin leebi Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa la biisi. Pa ŋɔn Naawunni n kur niŋni nirilim n‑ti halli ban bu puusiri u, ni tumbeeri dima maa, gba?.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Dinzugu ya zɔm taaba namboo, hoo ya Ba Naawunni ŋɔn zɔri namboo seem la.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ya di teesiri taaba yɛla, ka Naawunni ku ti teesi ya gba yɛlibeeri; yan di zaŋŋni taaba taalli, ka Naawunni ku ti zaŋŋi ya gba taalli. Ya kyɛri ya taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, Ka Naawunni nin ti kyɛ ya taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ya gba.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Ya zaŋŋma piini n‑ti niriba, ka Naawunni ti ti ya gba, ka yan ti ya deaai seelli maa nin ti paali ya nuusi halli nti yaamna; dama yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem la, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑nyɛsi ba nyɛsiri wa, ni: Zooma nin toaai n‑dari u zoontaraana, bee? Ba bayi maa zaa ku lu bɔ'ari ni?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Karimbiiya bu gatti u karimba, amaa karimbii ŋɔn nya wulisigu suŋŋa, u bamsim ni u karimba bamsim nin ti sa'ai taaba.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni, ka zaantɔliga deen sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Wula ka i nin toaai n‑yɛli i mabiiya, ni, u kyɛ ka i yiisi munseaa kan bɛ u nimbiri ni, ka deen bu nyara zaantɔliseaa kan bɛ i maŋtoori dinni ni? Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka yi nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Yisa daa naa n‑yɛli ba, Tisuŋŋu bu wanni walibeeri, ka tibeoo mun bu wanni walisuma.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Ba gɔsiri tikam zaa maŋtoori wala maa nti baŋŋi bumbɔ tii n nyɛ ka. Dama nira zi n‑tɔsi kinkama goaa zugu, ka sɔ mun zi n‑tɔsi sisibi purinkpaŋŋu zugu.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Bunsuma yi bɛ ninvugsuŋŋu sufu ni m‑paali ka, u nin yiisiri bunsuma u sufu maa ni na; ka ninvugbeoo ŋɔn mun sufu paali ni bumbeeri yiisiri la bumbeeri na, dama dinkam bɛ ninsaala sufu ni m‑paali ka, dina ka u yiisiri u noori ni na.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya boonni ma, ni, N Daana, N Daana ka lee m‑bu tumni tunseesi man yɛtti ya, ni, ya tumma maa?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa kyɛ n saani na nti wum n yɛligu, ka dɔli di, n nin wulisi ya lala nira ŋɔn ŋmani sɔa:.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: u ŋmani la hoo tammeerisɔ ŋɔn daa gbi tiŋŋa n‑nyeebi u yiri n‑tɔm tampii zugu; ka koom daa ti kpɛ n‑gyiligi yiri maa ka bu nyaŋŋi n‑dam ka, dama ka daa mɛ suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Amaa ŋɔnkam mun wumni n yɛligu maa ka bu dɔli di, di nira ŋmani la hoo tammeerisɔ ŋɔn daa bu gbi tiŋŋa n‑nyeebi, amaa u kur mɛ n‑tɔm la tambisigu zugu, ka koom daa ti kpɛ n‑gyiligi yiri maa, ka ka daa di lu zaa, ka ka luubu maa daa bɛ pam.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi niriba maa yɛttɔ'a wa zaa nti naai, u daa kyaŋŋi la Kapirinyum.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Ka soogyadima toondaansɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka u daabwuttigu daa beera, halli ka dooru maa ti wum u halli ka u ti pɔri kum.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Di saha maa ka toondaana wa daa wum Yisa yɛla ka daa tum Izraɛli dima kpamseeba ni ba ti susi u ka u kyɛ na nti tibi u baara maa n‑ti u.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Ka ba daa paai u saani na nti bɛlim u n‑yɛtta, Soogyadima toondaana maa sum ni i suŋŋi u.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: dama u wuri ti zuliya wa halli, ka u mɛ la'aŋŋu duu n‑ti ti ni ti kpeeri m‑puusiri Naawunni gba.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Ban daa ti ya labi n‑kunni na ka Yisa daa dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi. Ŋɔn daa ti kyaŋŋi m‑pɔri toondaana maa yiri, ŋɔnwa daa laa n‑tum u zɔdima Yisa saani na, ni, u di mugsi u maŋŋa; ni, ŋɔn toondaana maa poora ni Yisa kyɛ u yiri ni na;.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: ni, ka mun bu laa n‑sum ni u gba kyɛ Yisa saani na. Amaa u kur yɛlim yɛttɔ'aseesi ŋɔn ya yɛli, ka u baara maa kpaŋŋi.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Ni, dama ŋɔn gba nyɛ la ban su ninvugsɔa, ka u mun gba su soogyaseeba fukumsi ka nin toaai n‑yɛli ba yinni, ni, u kyɛm luginseaa ka u kyaŋŋi, bee n‑yɛli sɔ, ni, u kyɛm na ka u kyɛ na, ka nin toaai n‑yɛli u daabri, ni, u tumma nŋɔ ka u tum di.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa wum soogyadima toondaana maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa, di daa gyɛri u pam: ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli daadam ban daa dɔli u maa, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, halli Izraɛli dima ni gba n na zi n‑nya sɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda hoo doo wa ŋɔn niŋi seem.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Ka toondaana maa ŋɔn daa tum ninvugseeba maa daa labi yiri maa ni na nti nya ka daabri maa pun kpaŋŋi zaa.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Neeni la, ka u nyaandɔliba ni zama pam daa dɔli u.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti pɔri tiŋŋa maa kpeebu, tiŋŋa maa ni na'akyimbilisɔ kpi ka ba ziiri u n‑yiiri na, ka tiŋŋa maa niriba kabri n‑dɔla. Ŋɔn daa kpi maa ma daa nyɛ la pukoori, ka daa ka bisɔ m‑paasi ŋɔn kpi maa.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Ka ti Dugma daa nya pukoori maa ka di niŋi u namboo, ka u yɛli u, Di kumna.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti siisi ban daa mari bunseaa n‑ziiri kum maa, ka ban daa ziiri u maa kpalim n‑zaani, ka u tasi m‑booli kum maa, ni, Na'akyimbila, n ya yɛtti i ni, ni, i doaama.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Ka kum maa daa siri neaai u kum ni n‑doaai n‑zini m‑piili yɛttɔ'a, ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑ti u ma maa.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Ka dabeem daa gbaai sɔkam ka ba pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtti, ni, Anabititaari kyɛ duniya wa ni na, ni u ti bɛ ti saani!, ka laa n‑yɛli, ni Naawunni gɔsi u niriba yɛla maa.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa zugu, lahabaari maa daa mooli n‑gyili Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni tinseesi an gyiligi bɛni maa zaa.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Tɔ. Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋni seelli kam zaa maa yɛla.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Ka u daa yiisi ba niriba ayi n‑tum ba ti Dugma Yisa saani ni ba ti boosi u, ni, Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee ti na gɔsim di daama soori.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Ka tuumudima babayi maa daa ti paai Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, Gyɔni ŋɔn suuri niriba Naawunni koom la n tum ti i saani na ni ti boosi i, ni, nyini n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee, ti na gɔsim di daama soori.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa tibri baaridima pam, ni ninvugseeba dooribeeri ŋɔn daa mari zaa, ka katti arizimbeeri niriba zugu m‑basira, ka neaari zoomma pam nini n‑tiri ba.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli Gyɔni niriba, ni, Ya labma nti yɛli Gyɔni yan nyari ka wumni seela: ni, zoomma nini neaara, ka wɔbsi kyɛnna, ka ban mari kɔmni nyari alaafɛa, ka tubkpiradima wumna, halli ka ban kpi gba neaari kum ni; ni, ka lahabasuŋŋu maa moonni n‑tiri fara dima.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, Ŋɔnkam bu birisiri n yɛla zugu mari la zugsuŋŋu.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Gyɔni tuumudima daa doaai n‑kuli, ka Yisa mun daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri daadam maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɔni yɛla, ka boosiri ba, ni, Bɔ ka ya daa kyaŋŋi mooni ni ya ti nya? Kyimnigu pɛbsigu ŋɔn zeera, bee?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi neensuma? Ay! - ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela ka diri bunnya'asa n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.;.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: ka laa m‑boosi ba n‑yɛli, Ka bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ya ya n‑nya la anabisɔ, bee? Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, halli u gaari anabi gba.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ ban daa sɔbi sɔ yɛla, ni, Naawunni yɛliya: Ya nyama! - N tuumu nŋɔa; N tumni u ni u ti daŋŋi i tooni: Ŋɔna nin maali i kyɛnni maa soori n‑ti i.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Amaa n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, Gyɔni gaari pɔ'aba ŋɔn pun dɔ'ai bidibseeba zaa; amaa ka n ya yɛli ya yaasa, ni, halli ŋɔn poora zaa m‑bɛ Naawunni naam ni gba yi gaari ŋɔna.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Tɔ; ka zama kam ban daa bɛ bɛni n‑wumni Yisa ŋɔn yɛli seem maa - halli lampodeaariba gba - ba zaa daa pɛ'ari Naawunni, ni, u soori n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni soori, dama ba zaa daa pun deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu Gyɔni saani.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Amaa Farisii dima ni karimbadima mun daa bu deaai kɔsuubu maa, ka dizugu ba daa za'asi Naawunni ŋɔn zaali ba seem.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ka bumbɔ ka n ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ŋmansi zuna niriba wa? Ba lee n‑ŋmanni la bɔa?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ba ŋmani la hoo biisi ban zi daa ni m‑boonni taaba n‑yɛtti, ni, Ti peebi yiiya n‑ti ya ka ya za'asi waa, ka ti laa m‑maanni kuuri n‑ti ya ka ya bu sakki n‑kumna.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Dama Gyɔni Kɔsuura la daa kyɛ na ka u ba'a ka bundira diibu, bee bunnyuura nyuubu, yɛla ni, ka ya yɛli, ni, arizimbeoo n zi u zugu ni.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑dɔli zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Amaa Naawunni yɛm wulisi kamaŋŋa la ban deaai ka zaa saani.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Tɔ. Ka Farisii dima dɔsɔ daa booli Yisa ni u kyɛ u yiri na ka ba la'am n‑zini bundirigu zii ni ba di.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ na n‑zini sahaseaa la, tiŋŋa maa ni pɔ'a tumbeeri daansɔ daa ti wum, ni, Yisa bɛ la Farisii maa yiri n‑diri bundirigu. Pɔ'a maa daa zaŋŋi kɔlɔbaveelli kan paali turaali kpaam.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ n‑zɛ Yisa nyaaŋŋa ka gbaai kunkuma, ka u nintam tɔ'ari n‑luri Yisa nɔba ni m‑peaari a, ka u mari u zɔbwɔ'ala n‑nyeesiri a, ka bɔbli a, ka zaŋŋi turaali maa n‑soosi Yisa nɔba.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Ka Farisisɔ ŋɔn daa booli Yisa na maa daa nya lala; ka yɛli u sufu ni, ni, doo wa yi da siri nyɛ la anabi, u naa m‑baŋŋi pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn siisiri u wa ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka mi, ni, tumbeeri daana n nyɛ u.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u Saimoon! M mari yɛttɔ'aseaa ni n yɛli i.; ka Saimoon labsi n‑yɛli u, Ndana, yɛlim ma.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Dɔppa ayi n daa bɛ ni, n‑daa pɛŋŋi dɔsɔ ligri. Ka ba yinni maa samni daa gaari u taraana samni maa kaman napɔŋŋu piiya.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Ka babayi maa zaa daa ka ligri n‑nin yɔ n‑labsi sandaana wa, ka u daa kyɛ u sama maa n‑ti niriba ayi maa zaa. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna niriba ayi wa sunsukkuni, ba ŋɔni nin lee n‑wuri ligri maa daana n‑gaari u kpeaa.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Ka Saimoon yɛli, ni, N teesiya, ni, ŋɔn samni daa galisi pam la. Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, I siri baŋŋiya.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: ka ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi pɔ'a maa ka yɛli Saimoon I nya pɔ'a wa? Man da kpɛ i yiri wa ni na, i da bu ti ma koom ka m peaai n nɔba: pɔ'a wa mun zaŋŋi u nintam m‑peaari n nɔba ka zaŋŋi u zɔbri n‑nyeesi a.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Ka nyin da bu puusi ma ka mɔ'asi n kparima. - ba tiŋŋa puusigu kaari nla - Amaa man kpɛ i yiri wa na halli wayoo ka pɔ'a wa na bu kyɛ n nɔba bɔbligu.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Din paasi yaasa, i bu niŋi kpaam n‑soosi n zugu, amaa pɔ'a wa ŋɔn zaŋŋi turaali kpaam n‑soosi n nɔba.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Dizugu n ya yɛli i, ni, ba kyɛ pɔ'a wa taalli din galisi pam m‑pɛŋŋi u, u wurilim ŋɔn galisi pam zugu; amaa ninvugsɔ taalli din bu galisi ka ba kyɛ di m‑pɛŋŋi u, di daama wurilim gba bu ya galisi.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Ka ŋmaligi n‑yɛli pɔ'a maa, N kyɛ i taalli maa m‑pɛŋŋi i.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Ka ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni ni Yisa n‑diri maa daa nyurimni ba kparima ni n‑yɛtti taaba, ni, Ŋɔni mun n lee nŋɔa? - halli nti kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Di saha la ka Yisa laa n‑yɛli pɔ'a maa Kyɛma ni sufudoaa; i yɛdda niŋbu n tuligi i.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Di saha maa Yisa daa gyiligi tinsi maa zaa ni a tinkpansi nti yiisi wa'azu ka yɛli ba Naawunni naam lahabasuŋŋu maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la daa dɔli u m‑bɛ u saani. Pɔ'aseeba gba daa dɔla, baseeba Yisa ŋɔn daa pun tibi ba dooribeeri ka kari arizimbeeri ni ba yi ka kyɛ ba. Pɔ'aba maa yinni n daa nyɛ Mariama ŋɔn daa yi Magdala tiŋŋa ni na, ka ŋɔna ka arizimbeeri ayɔpɔi daa yi u ni n‑kyɛ u la:.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: Gyeoowana gba daa bɛ ni, ŋɔn sira n daa nyɛ NaaHɛroodi yiri kpeema, ka u yuuri daa boonni Kyuuza; ni Suzanna, ni pɔ'aba pam m‑paasi ba zugu. Ba zaa daa dɔli Yisa ni u nyaaŋŋa n‑suŋŋni ba, ka mari ba maŋtoori neema n‑diisiri ba.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Tɔ, daadam n daa yi tinsi ni n‑la'asi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ka u daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri n‑ti ba, ni.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Pukpaarisɔ n daa kyaŋŋi puu, ni u ti waali u bumbura. Ka ŋɔn daa waanni maa, a seesi daa lu la sɔkpaŋŋa, ka niriba nɔ n‑nɔ a, ka niinsi gba daa nya a n‑di a zaa.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Tɔ, di saha maa bumburiseesi daa lu la kuga ziiya; ka an daa ti buli sahanseaa la, kuga maa daa kyɛ ka a bu nyaŋŋi n‑zoaai ka a gba daa gyɛkki n‑kpi, an daa kɔŋŋi tammaasiri maa zugu.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Tɔ, ka a seesi gba daa lu goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa doaai n‑gabi a ka a kpi.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Amaa bumbura maa seesi daa lu la tansuŋŋu ziiya. Dizugu a daa nɔbi suŋŋa ka laa n‑dɔ'ai pam ka u ŋma n‑nya kpalankɔbsi. Tɔ; ka ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑naai, ka u daa yeaai u noori n‑yɛli pampam, ni, Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Tɔ; di nyaaŋŋa u nyaandɔliba la daa boosi u la nyɛsiri maa gbinni;.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: ka u yɛli ba, Yarima ka ŋɔn Naawunni pun ti ya soori ni ya baŋŋi u naam yɛlisɔ'ara an nyɛ seem; amaa seeba saani di tɔ pam ni ba baŋŋi nyɛsiri gbinni, halli ba gɔsiri n‑gɔsiri gba ka ku nya, ka ba wumni gba ka bu ya m‑baŋŋi di gbinni.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Tɔ; nyɛsiri maa gbinni nŋɔa#:- Bumbura maa nyɛ la Naawunni yɛligu.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Bumburiseesi an daa lu sɔkpaŋŋa ni la wulisiri la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa ka Sintaani laa n‑yiisi ka ba sufu ni ka kyɛ ba: ka ba kɔŋŋi tuliginsim, yɛdda kaalinsi zugu.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Tɔ; ka kuga zugu dina la mun nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa, ka deaai ka ni sufumaasim; amaa ban ka nya'a zugu di bu yuura, dama sahasuŋŋu ka ba niŋi Naawunni yɛdda; amaa di yi tɔ sahanseaa, ba nin labi la nyaaŋŋa m‑basi.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Goosi ni dina la mun nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa ka ba teesa na kur bɛ duniya wa yɛla ni la, ni ka neema ni ka bunnya'asa ni la m‑paasi. Lala zugu ka yɛligu maa bu mari nyoori ba sufu ni.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Amaa tansuŋŋu dina la nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa ka deaai ka ni yɛlimaŋni n‑niŋi ba sufu ni; ka ba mari nyoori pam di zugu - ba sufu puu antaam nla.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Sɔ bu nyoaari firila ka laa m‑mari laa m‑pinni ka, bee n‑zaŋŋi n‑zaali gadu lɔŋni; ay! - ba tɔmni ka la saazugu ka ban kpeeri na maa nyari kasi.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Bunsɔ'ariseaa ka ni ka ba ku baŋŋi kan bɛ seem zaa.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Dizugu ya kpasim suŋŋa ya wumpu ziiya, dama ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari seelli ya n‑nya m‑paasi, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli maa ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn teesiri, ni, u mari seelli fiiŋ maa ka ba nin ti fa ka kyɛ u yɔri.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ma, ni u tinzɔppa, daa kyɛ u saani na ka bu toaai n‑nya u, daadam ŋɔn daa zoaai la zugu.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli Yisa, ni, I ma ni i tinzɔppa bɛ samanni m‑boori ni ba nya i.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Amaa u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ninvugseeba ban wum Naawunni yɛligu maa ka sakki u noori la n nyɛ ban nyɛ m ma ni n tinzɔppa.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Dahinseaa ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa paai mɔ'ari ni nti kpɛ nyariŋŋu ni. Ka u yɛli ba, Ya kyɛ ka ti kyaŋŋi nti du mɔ'ari wa duuri sa.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa piili n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu halli ka Yisa ti gbisi. Di saha ka pɛsititaari daa doaai mɔ'ari maa ni n‑zeera. Ka daa zɛ ni halli ka nyariŋŋu maa ti ya paali ni koom.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Ka dabeem yi gbaai u nyaandɔliba maa ka ba daa neaai Yisa u goom ni ka yɛli, Eeh, Ti daana! Ti zaa kpiiri ni, pɛsigu wa zugu! Ka ŋɔn daa neaai, u daa tasi pɛsigu maa ni kɔmiisi la. Ka di lu n‑fɔ surimm.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Di saha ka u yi boosi ba, ni, Ya yɛdda la lee?, ka dabeem maali n‑gbaai ba. Di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba ka boosiri, ni, Bɔ nira n nyɛ ŋɔnŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tasiri pɛsigu ni kooma gba ka a wumna.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa laa n‑kyaŋŋi halli nti paai Gyɛrasa tingbaŋŋu maa ni .

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Tiŋŋa maa ni ka arizimbeeri daa daamni dɔsɔa, ka lala doo maa daa bu yeeri kparigu ka laa m‑bu soori kuriga, ka u daa pun bɛ lala n‑yuui. U daa bu gbeeri yiri ni, amaa u daa bɛ la gbala zii ni n‑gyinna. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni sahaseaa la, arizinni daana wa daa yi u zugu na.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: Tɔ; ŋɔn daa ti nya Yisa, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa ka tasi pampam, ni, I ya niŋi ma la wula? - Yisa nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa wa biiya! N susiri i ni, ni, nyin di namsi ma.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa daa pun tasi arizimbeoo maa, ni, u yim ka kyɛ doo maa, ka dizugu ka arizinni maa daa tɔ'asi lala. Arizimbeoo maa daa yi ti lusi doo maa la sahaseaa, ba daa yi zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba n‑dooli u ka u kur kyisiri a ka arizinni maa kyɛ ka u zɔ n‑kpɛ yoo ni; ka di daa pun yuui lala.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa yi boosi u yuuri ka u yɛli, ni, u yuuri boonni la Zama, dama arizimbeeri pam n daa la'asi m‑bɛ u ni.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Arizimbeeri maa yi susiri u ni, ni, u di katti ba n‑yiisiri n‑taasiri ba bɔ'azuliŋŋu ni.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Tɔ; ka di saha maa kurikyuu bɔbgu gba daa bɛ la kunkunni lugri ni n‑dira, ka arizimbeeri maa daa susi soori, ni, Yisa sakkma ka ba yi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ka u daa ti ba lala soori. Ka arizindima maa daa yi doo maa ni n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni, ka ba vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom di ba.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa ŋɔn daa nya lala, ba daa zɔ yoomyooma n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni niriba, ni seeba ban daa tumni puri ni gba.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem maa. Ka niriba pam daa siri yi na ni ba nya yɛttɔ'aseaa kan niŋi maa. Ban daa paai na, ba daa nya doo maa, ka arizimbeeri yi ka kyɛ u, ka u zi la Yisa tooni ka yɛ u sutura ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka u. Di daa gbaai ba dabeem pam.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Ka ban nini daa nya maa daa yɛli daadam maa Yisa ŋɔn tibi arizimbeeri daana maa seem.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Niriba ban daa yi Gyɛrasa tinkpansi zaa maa daa yɛli u, ni, u kyɛma ka kyɛ ba, dama di kpeesi ba dabeem pam. Ka Yisa daa siri laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ni u labi.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa ŋɔn tibi dɔsɔ maa daa susi soori ni u dɔli u n‑kpalim m‑bɛ u saani; amaa Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u kulima u tiŋŋa.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: nti wulisi Naawunni ŋɔn tum seem n‑ti u. Ka u daa siri kyaŋŋi n‑gyili tiŋŋa maa zaasa nti mooli Yisa ŋɔn tibi u seem.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa labi la, niriba pam pun guri u, ni ba gyaari u.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u yuuri daa boonni Gyayiruusi; ba tiŋŋa wunsusigu la'aŋŋu duu kpeema n daa nyɛ u. Tɔ, ka ban daa kur gyaari u n‑naara, Gyayiruusi gba yi na n‑gbaani u tooni.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: ka yɛli u, ni, u bipungaŋŋa ka dooru mukki u yiri ni, bii maa mun yuuma daa paai hoo yuuma piiya ni ayi. Ni, u susiri Yisa, ni, u kyɛm yiri maa ni na dama bii maa pɔri kum. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa sakki lala ka bɛ soori ni n‑kyɛnni la, niriba kur bɛ la kpa'abu ni m‑mugsiri u, ka daari taaba ni ba nya u.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka tiimadima sɔsɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tibi u - u ziim daa bu ŋmaara:.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: ka pɔ'a wa daa tabli na n‑kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑siisi Yisa bunyeerigu, ka ziim maa daa di ŋmaai kyiribbi!.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Tɔ; din daa niŋi maa zugu Yisa daa boosi, ni, Ŋɔni n da siisi ma la? Ka niriba maa zaa kyiisi n‑naai, ka Piita gba yɛli, ni, N Daana, daadam maa ŋɔn zoaai m‑mugri i seem wa, ka sɔ siisi i.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Amaa Yisa daa bu sakki, ka yɛli Ay, amaa sɔ n siisi ma, dama m baŋŋiya, ni, yiiko yi n saani n‑kpɛ sɔ ni.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa nya ka di ka sɔ'abu, ka dizugu u daa kyɛ Yisa saani na, halli ka dabeem daa gbaai u gba, ka gbaani u tooni ka yɛli ba daliiriseaa kan kyɛ ka u siisi u, ni u siisigu maa ŋɔn tibi u dooru yoomyooma seem; ka ba zaa daa wumna.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa yi yɛli u, M biiya, kyɛma ni alaafɛa. I niŋi la yɛdda, di zugu ka i nya tippu.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Ka u daa kur bɛ la yɛttɔ'a ni, ka dɔsɔ yi kpeema maa yiri ni na nti yɛli u, ni, u bii maa kpiya, ni, dizugu u di laa m‑mukkri karimba maa u bii maa zugu.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa labsi n‑yɛli doo maa, ni, Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, kur niŋma yɛdda ka u nya alaafɛa.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Tɔ; ŋɔn daa mari nti paai yiri maa ni, u daa bu sakki ni sɔsɔ kpɛ duu maa ni, na'ala Piita ni Gyɔni ni Gyeemsi, m‑paasi bii maa ba ni u ma zugu.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Di saha maa ka yiri maa ni niriba zaa daa kur bɛ la kunkuma ni, ka kaasiri bii maa zugu; amaa Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya di kumna; u bu kpi, amaa u gbisiri ni.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: U daa tɔ'asi lala, ka di ku ba la laari, dama ba daa pun koaai bii maa tama zaa.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Tɔ; di saha ka u gyi bii maa nuu ka yɛli, Biiya, doaama.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
