local: U yɛligu daa bu lu tiŋŋa ka bii maa siiya pun labi na ka u daa doaai n‑zaani ya'abi; ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, ba zaŋŋma diibu na n‑ti bii maa ka u di. Ka dinŋɔ daa niŋi doo maa ni u pɔ'a alahazibu pam.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: amaa u daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di yɛli n‑ti sɔsɔ din niŋi seem.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka ti Dugma Yisa daa yiisi ninvugpisiyɔpɔi ni ayi m‑paasi ka puri ba niriba ayiyi n‑tum ba ni ba daŋŋi u tooni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseesi zaa u mammaŋŋa gba ŋɔn ya kyaŋŋi.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Ka yɛli ba, ni, Ya kyi ŋmaabu tuma wa zoaaya pam, amaa ka ya tuntumniba maa mun bu zoaai; dizugu ya susim ti Dugma ŋɔn nyɛ puu wa daana, ka u tum niriba na m‑paasi ba ŋma.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: N tumni ya ni ya yim soori n‑kyaŋŋi, amaa ya baŋŋma, ni, ya nyɛ la kaman pɛbiseeba ban bɛ kunduna sunsukkuni.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Yan di mari baagyi bee booriga bee namna n‑kyaŋŋi; ya mun di puusiri sɔ soori ni.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Ka ya yi ti paai n‑kpɛ yiri seaa zaa ya yɛlima, ni, Naawunni nin taasi u sufudoaa n‑niŋi yiri wa ni na:.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: ka sufudoaa daana yi siri bɛ bɛni, dinŋɔna ya gba sufudoaa nin bɛ u saani. Amaa di yi pa lala, di nin labi ya saani na.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Ya kpalimma yiri maa ni n‑diri ka nyuuri bunseelli kam zaa ba yi ti nyaŋŋi n‑ti ya, dama di sumsi ni tuntumna deaari u yoori. Yan di laa n‑yiiri yiri seaa n‑lɔŋŋni yiri seaa.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Ya mun yi paai tinseelli kam ka ba sakki n‑deaai ya, ya dim bundiriseelli zaa ban nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ya.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: ka tibi baariseeba ban bɛ tiŋŋa maa ni ka ba kpaŋŋi, ka yɛli daadam maa, ni, Naawunni naam paari ba na.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Amaa ya yi ti kpɛ tinseaa ka ba bu sakki ni ba deaai ya, ya dɔli lala tiŋŋa maa sɔya n‑yɛtta.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Halli ya tiŋŋa wa tankpɔseesi an pirigi ti nɔba wa gba ti nin pisigi m‑basi ya zugu, amaa see ka ya laa m‑baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni naam paari ya na.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Ka n yɛli ya, ni, Zaadaari yi ti paai, di nin tɔ n‑tiri lala tiŋŋa dima n‑gaari din ti ya n‑tɔ n‑tiri ninvugbɛseeba ban daa bɛ Sɔdoom tiŋŋa ni seem la gba.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Zugubeoo ka ya nin nya, yarima Kɔreezini dima ni Bɛtsaida dima wa! Dama ba di yi daa tum tunkara Taaya ni Saidoon tinsi ni hoo man tumni seesi ya tinsi maa ni la, ban tumbeeri dima wa daa naa n‑yɛ tankyɛra n‑zinni m‑bugi tampɛ'alim n‑wulisiri ba sufusa'aŋŋu ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka takki ba halibeoo beebu la: m‑pun niŋi lala ka di daa naa n‑yuui.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Amaa Zaadaari halli Taaya ni Saidoon ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Yarima muna, ya Kapirinyum dima wa, ya bu duusiri la ya mansi m‑paai arizana ni? Amaa ba nin sigsi ya kpiimma tiŋŋa ni sa.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: M biisi, ŋɔnkam sakki n‑wum ya noori wumni la mani, ka ŋɔnkam za'asi ya noori wumpu za'asiri la mani; ka ŋɔnkam mun za'asi ma za'asiri la Ŋɔn tum ma na la.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa tum ninvugpisiyɔpɔi ni ayi seeba la n daa ti kyaŋŋi ka labi u saani na ni sufupeelli, nti yɛli u, ni, Ti Dugma, halli arizimbeeri gba ka ti nyaŋŋi i yuuri zugu.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, N gɔsi n‑nya ka Sintaani mammaŋŋa yi saazugu n‑lu na kaman saa ŋɔn yi nyakki seem la!, ni.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Ya nyama, n ti ya soori ni ya nin toaai n‑nɔ wugi ni nansi yɔriyɔri, ka nyaŋŋi ti Dindaana maa kpeeŋŋu zaa, ka bunseelli kam ku toaai n‑niŋi ya seela.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Amaa dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi ni yan nyaŋŋi arizimbeeri maa zugu, amaa ya kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi ban pun sɔbi ya yuya arizana ni maa zugu.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Di saha maa ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa kyɛ ka Yisa sufu pɛligi pam, ka u yɛli, M Ba Naawunni, nyin ŋɔn su arizana ni duniya wa, m pɛ'ari i ni, dama nyin zaŋŋi yɛla wa zaa n‑sɔ'ai ka kyɛ yɛm dima ni baŋŋniba ka laa n‑kasigi a n‑wulisi bileesi. Ii, m Ba, yɛlimaŋni lala veela i saani.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Bunseelli kam zaa ka m pun deaai m Ba nuu ni. Sɔ ka ni m‑mi Bii maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ, na'ala u Ba maa m mi u; ka sɔ mun gba ka ni m‑mi m Ba maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ, na'ala mani u Bii maa m mi u; m‑paasi sɔkam man, u Bii maa, n nin sakki ni n kasigi m Ba maa yɛla n‑wulisi u.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa u daa ŋmaligi kpaŋŋa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔ n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ninvugseeba ban nya yan nyari bunseesi wa mari la zugsuŋŋu!.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Dama n yɛtti ya, ni, sahaseaa din gaari, anabidima ni nadima pam daa boori la ba da nya yan nyari bunseesi wa ka mun bu nya a; ka daa laa m‑boori la ba wum yan wumni seelli wa ka daa bu wum.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Tɔ; ka Naawunni zaaligu baŋŋnisɔ daa ti boori ni u zamsi Yisa yɛm, ka doaai n‑zaani m‑boosi u, ni, N Karimba, wula ka n ya n‑niŋi n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Wula ka di sɔbi Naawunni zaaligu ni? I Litaafi kariŋŋu wulisiri i la wula.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, Ni, I zaŋŋma i sufu zaa n‑wuri i Dugma Naawunni, ni i siiya zaa, ni i kpeeŋŋu ni i teesa zaa. ni, Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la. .

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Mboo! I labsi suŋŋa pam: dizugu niŋma lala ka i nin nya nyɔvuri maa.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Ka u mun daa bu boori ba galim u u boosigu maa zugu, ka dizugu u daa laa m‑boosi, ni, Ka ŋɔni n nyɛ n lala taraana.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kyɛnni ni u ti paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa. Ka u na kyɛnni n‑sigri zoori, ka sɔguriba daa luri u soori maa zugu m‑pirigi u sutura zaa, ka bu u halli ka u ti gyɛa, ka kyɛ u n‑dooli sɔkpaŋŋa ka u daa pɔtti la kum.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ka di daa bu yuui ka maalimaannisɔ ti dɔli soori maa na nti nya u, ka lɔkki n‑gaari ka kyɛ u.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Lala gba ka Liivai za'anɔri dɔsɔ gba daa ti dɔli soori maa na nti nya u, ka ŋɔn gba daa lɔkki n‑gaari ka kyɛ u.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Amaa ka Samɛrii dɔsɔ gba daa ti dɔli soori maa na nti paai bɛni n‑nya doo maa, ka di daa niŋi u namboo pam.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai doo maa saani n‑zaŋŋi daam m‑peaai u dansi maa, ka boaai kpaam n‑niŋi, ka zaŋŋi tana n‑lɔ u yuuma maa. U daa zaŋŋi u n‑ziili u mammaŋŋa bumbaarigu zugu n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ saamma kpeebu yiri m‑mari u suŋŋa.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Tɔ, ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka Samɛrii doo wa yi ya kyaŋŋi u kyɛnni la, u daa yiisi ligri n‑ti u yikpɛdaana maa ka yɛli u, ni, u gɔsim u baara wa suŋŋa halli ka u ti labi na. Ni, ligri maa yi ti pɔsigi ka u labi na, u nin yɔ ka samni maa an kpalim n‑ti u dirindaari maa.

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Tɔ: ba niriba ata maa sunsukkuni, ba ŋɔni n zaŋŋi doo maa n‑leebi u taraana? Ka u labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa zɔ u namboo la.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Gba kyɛma nti niŋi lala.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa ti paai tinkpanseaa, ka pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Maata zaŋŋi u nti kpeesi u yiri.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Ka Maata tinzɔ, ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Mariama la, daa kyaŋŋi nti zinni Yisa tooni n‑wumni u yɛttɔ'a.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: amaa Maata ŋɔn daa kur bɛ la bundirigu dugbu ni. Ka Maata daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti boosi u, ni, N Daana, i ba'a na'a ka ni n tinzɔ wa yɛla ni? - dama u kyɛ tuma maa zaa n‑gyeeli ma. Yɛlim u, ni, u suŋŋma ma.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Ɛɛh, Maata! I zaŋŋi la yɛla pam m‑mugsi i mammaŋŋa nimmoosi.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: amaa bunyinni kɔkɔ n kpa talasi, dizugu Mariama n yiisi bunsuŋŋu, sɔ mun ku toaai n‑deaai di u saani.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Ka niriba tusa tusa ŋɔn daa kur la'amni Yisa saani na halli nti mugi taaba - halli nti noori taaba gba - Yisa daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba ni ba gbaam ba maŋŋa suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina yɛla - di nyɛ la ba birikɔnsi.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba yaasa, ni, Bunseelli zaa kan pili ka bu nyara, ba nin piligi ka ka sɔkam nya; ka yɛttɔ'asɔ'ara zaasa muna - sɔkam nin baŋŋi a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Ya yi ti sɔ'ai zibsim ni n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi zaasa, ba ya n‑wum a la wuntampeeliga; yan kpɛ duri puuni ka waasi n‑yɛli yɛliseesi la, ba nin ti du saazugu m‑mooli a ka sɔkam zaa wum.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: N ya yɛli ya ni, yarim ban nyɛ n zɔdima la, ni, yan di zɔri ninvugseeba ban kuuri niriba ningbina, ka ku laa n‑toaai n‑niŋi ba seelli m‑paasi.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Amaa n nin wulisi ya din sumsi ni dabeem yi mari sɔa: ya zɔm sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi ya, ya kum nyaaŋŋa, n‑lɔsi bugum ni. Ya niŋma ma yɛdda, ni, ŋɔna ka di kpa talasi ni ya zɔri u.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Ammi, ya bu nya ka ba la'asiri niimbisi anu n‑koosiri la'abisi ayi? Amaa Naawunni na zi n‑tam halli niimbila yinni yɛla.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Halli ya zɔbri muna, Naawunni mi a gba yɛla, ka baŋŋi a zaa yinni yinni kaaligu ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Yan di zɔri dabeem, dama ya mari bukyi pam n‑gaari niinsi maa zaa.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la n dinni, man Ninsaala Bii gba nin ti zaani Naawunni malaakadima tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man gba dinni.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Ka ŋɔnkam mun yi za'asi ma niriba tooni, man gba nin za'asi u Naawunni malaakadima tooni.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑galim man Ninsaala Biiya, u nin toaai n‑nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi; amaa ŋɔnkam tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑galim Naawunni Sii Kasi maa, di daama ŋɔn kur ku nya u taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Ba yi ti zaŋŋi ya n‑taasi la'aŋŋu duri ni, bee nadima saani, yan di boosiri ya sufu ni yan ya labsi seem bee yan ya gyɛrigi seem.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: dama ba boosa maa saha ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi nin ti wulisi ya yan ya labsi seem.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Tɔ; ka dɔsɔ daa zɛ zama maa sunsukkuni n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, N Karimba, yɛlim m beeri wa ka u purigi ti ba faari n‑ti ma, ti ba ŋɔn daa kpi ka kyɛ u arizikki la tɔrli kan nyɛ n dinni.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli doo maa, N zɔa, ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ya sarikatta bee ya faariputta.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Di saha maa ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ba gbaam ba mansi ka di boori ligri nti yakki, dama ninsaala nyɔvuri bu bɛ la u marigu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ni kɔkɔa.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Ka u daa nyɛsi ba nyɛsiri, ni, Bundaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑kɔ n‑nya bundirigu pam;.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: ka daa yɛli u sufu ni, ni, Wula ka n ya n‑niŋi, dama n ka luginseelli man ya n‑zaŋŋi m bundirigu wa n‑kpeesi.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Ka yɛli, Man nin niŋi seem nŋɔa: n nin wurim n napaari wa ni m buya zaa ka takki m‑mɛ bunkara ka a gaari a, n‑zaŋŋi m bundira wa zaa n‑niŋi.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Lala zugu n nin yɛli n sufu ni, ni, m mari bundira ni neema pam din nin mari ma yuumbɔbgu, dinzugu din yi kpalim n‑ti ma yi nyɛ la n‑ziya n‑vuusira ka dira ka nyuura ka bɔbri n yuubu ka mari sufupeelli.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Amaa ka Naawunni yɛli u, Nyin gyɛrigu wa! Yuŋŋu wa zaazaa ka n nin deaai i nyɔvuri, ka bunseelli zaa nyin mari n‑kuunni maa nin leebi ŋɔni dinni.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Lala n nyɛ di n‑tiri ninvugsɔ zaa ŋɔn kur gɔsiri u ligri, ka ka seelli Naawunni saani.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, Dinzugu n ya yɛli ya, ni, yan di zaŋŋi yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin niŋi seem n‑nya bundirigu n‑di, bee n‑nya sutura n‑yɛa, - yan di fabli anŋɔ zaa yɛla;.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: dama nyɔvuri gaari bundirigu, ka ningbina gaari bunyeerigu.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Ya gɔsim niinsi ni luya; ba bu butta, ba mun ka napaari ni buya, amaa ti Dugma Naawunni diisiri ba ni: ka yarima mari la bukaata pam n‑gaari niinsi la.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ya sɔ nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri yɛla m‑mugsi u maŋŋa nti yakki u nyɔvuri tɔriga m‑paasi halli dabsiri yinni gba?.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Tɔ; ka ya yi ku toaai n‑niŋi yɛttɔ'abila dinŋɔ taabu, wula zugu ka ya zaŋŋni yɛliseesi an kpalim maa m‑mugsiri ya mansi?.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ya teesima pɔ'a-veelli-ku-siisi-koom yɛla: ya gɔsim n‑nya kan zoaari seem, ka bu tumna, ka mun bu waanni gumni, bee, n‑wugri tana n‑seera, amaa n yɛtti ya ni, ni, halli NaaSulɛmaana ni u naam gyirima zaasa gba daa na zi m‑bɔbi suturiveela kaman mɔpuuma wa yinni veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ moo ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi, wula ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? Yarim yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri yan ya di seelli yɛla, bee yan ya n‑nyu seelli.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Duniya wa ninvugseeba ban bu mi Naawunni la bɛ la anŋɔ zaa nɔba karibu ni - bee, m ma'ai ni? Amaa yarima mari ya Ba Naawunni, ka u mi halli, ni, ya boori anŋɔ zaa.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Ay, see ka ya zaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi, ka u nin zaŋŋi bunseelli din kpalim zaa m‑paasi ya.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Ni, Yarima n bibisi wa, yan di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, dama ti Dugma Naawunni yuubu nyɛ la ni u zaŋŋi u naam maa beebu n‑ti ya.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Ya koosim ya neema zaa ka zaŋŋi lala ligri maa n‑ti faradima. Ya maalim la'abooriseesi an bu kpiiri n‑ti ya mansi, ka zaŋŋi a n‑su ya arizana ni arizikki n‑dooli: bɛni ka a ku fiilim, dama na'ayigsi ku toaai m‑paai a, ka leelisi gba ku toaai m‑paai a n‑ŋɔbi.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Dama ya sufu kur ya bɛ la ya arizikki ŋɔn bɛ seela.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Ni, Dinŋɔna ya barim n‑zaani: ya bɔbma ka nyoaai ya firiladima m‑maali siri.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: hoo daaba ŋɔn maanni siri seem, ba yi ti guri ba dugma ŋɔn ya yi amriya kyuu ni n‑labi na. Ka u yi ti paai na m‑booli za'anɔri maa ni, ba nin guuri yoomyooma n‑yoaai n‑ti u.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Daabseeba ban bu ti gbisira, ka ziya n‑guri ba dugma halli ka u ti paai na - ay! ba nin ti mari sufupeelli pam. Ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya wa, ni, u nin yeaai u kparigu maa ka kyɛ ka ba zinni ka u gyeemni ba.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: U yi ti yuui halli ni yuntasuusi bee bɛkyikyeoo saha ka kuli na ka ba niŋi siri n‑guri u lala, ba siri nin nya sufupeelli pam.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Ya baŋŋma, ni, yiddaana yi da mi sahanseaa na'ayiga ŋɔn ya kyɛ u yiri ma na, u naa n‑guri ka ku kyɛ ka u kabi n‑kpɛ u yiri maa ni na.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Dizugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti kyɛ na sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Di saha ka Piita daa boosi Yisa, ni, N Dugma, tirim ka i nyɛsi nyɛsiri wa n‑tiri ti, bee sɔkam zaa.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli Ninvugŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn mari yɛm ni yɛdda? - ka u dugma zaŋŋi u yiri maa fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuu ni, ni u basiri mɔnni n‑tiri ba, mɔnni basibu saha.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn nin nya sufupeelli, dama u dugma kuli na m‑paai u u lala tuma maa ni: bee, pa ŋɔna?.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, ni, u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi n‑niŋi lala daabri nuu ni.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Amaa daabsɔ nin ti teesi u sufu ni, ni, u dugma maa yuuya pam ni kulibu na, ka dizugu u piili m‑buuri u taaba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, ka dira, ka nyuuri daam m‑bugra.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: Di daama dugma maa nin ti paai na dahinseaa daabri maa ŋɔn bu teesiri u yɛla, bee sahanseaa ŋɔn bu mi u yɛla. Di saha ka u dugma maa nin ti darigi u tubri ka ŋmaasi u bisibisi, ka nin ti zaŋŋi u m‑paasi ninvugseeba ban ka yɛdda maa zugu.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Ni, Amaa daabsɔ ŋɔn mi u dugma yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ka ku sakki ni u maali siri n‑niŋi di, ŋɔn nin ti nya buuri pam:.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: ka daabsɔ ŋɔn bu mi u dugma yuubu ka tum din sumsi la buuri, ŋɔn mun buuri maa nin baala. Ban ti tisi ninvugsɔ pam, ŋɔn saani ka ba nin ti laa m‑boosi pam yɛla. Ka ninvugsɔ ban zaŋŋi tuma pam fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuu ni, ŋɔn gba saani ka ba nin ti boori ni ba wum u tuma pam tuma lahabaari.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Dahinseaa ka Yisa daa ti bɛ luginseelli n‑susiri Naawunni; ka ŋɔn daa ti susi n‑naai, u nyaandɔlisɔ daa yɛli u, Ti Daana, wulisim ti Naawunni susigu soori hoo Gyɔni ŋɔn daa wulisi u nyaandɔliba seem la.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Bugum zugu ka n kyɛ na, ni n zaŋŋi di n‑tɔ n‑niŋi duniya wa puuni, ka n sufu boori di da pun nyoaai.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya yi ti susi Naawunni, ya susim nŋɔa#:- Ti Ba, kyɛ ka i yuuri du, Ka i naam kyɛ na.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: N yɛla laa n‑ŋmani hoo kɔsuubu, ka a na zɛya n‑guri ma, ka di nyɛ la din kyɛ ka n sufu bu dɔya see ka a ti niŋi n‑naai.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Tim ti dabsiri zaa ti beoo kam bundirigu;.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ka boosi ba, ni, Ammi, ya teesiya, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti zaŋŋi nangbanyinni n‑ti duniya wa dima? Ay! - n yɛtti ya, ni, nangbankpeemni ka n taasiri na.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Ka kyɛri ti tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ti, Dama ti gba kyɛri ban tum ti taalli tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ba: Ka di kyɛ ka ti nya zamsigu nti lu. .

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, yiri puuni niriba anu nɔya nin ti wɔligi taaba: ba niriba ata nin naai noori n‑za'asi babayi la, ka babayi gba nin ti za'asi babata maa.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya bu nya, nyin yi mari i zɔa, ka u kyɛ i saani na yuntasuusi nti yɛli i, N zɔa, zaŋŋma paanukpila ata n‑ti ma.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Doo ni u bii nɔya nin ti wɔligi taaba, ka laa n‑za'asi taaba; ka pɔ'a ni u bipuŋŋa nin ti za'asi taaba, halli pɔ'a ni u bii pɔ'a nin ti za'asi taaba gba.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: N saansɔ n yi wa'aligu m‑paai ma na yuŋŋu wa, m mun ka bundirigu n nin ti u.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Ka Yisa daa ti yɛli zama maa, ni, Ya yi ti nya ka sangbana doaari n‑yiiri gbinni ya'ari na, ya yɛtti, ni, saa nin mi, ka ka siri nin mi.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Ka nyini mun kpalim m‑bɛ duu puuni n‑gyɛrigi u, ni, Di daamni ma! Man ni m biisi pun kpɛ la doaa ziiya, n ku toaai n‑doaai n‑ti i bunseelli zaa.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Ka ya yi ti nya ka pɛbsigu zeeri n‑yiiri nuzaa ya'ari na, ya nin yɛli, ni, wɔligu nin niŋi, ka di siri nin niŋi.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N yɛli ya, ni, halli ni i ku doaai n‑ti u ŋɔn nyɛ i zɔ maa zugu, amaa doo maa ŋɔn kpalim n‑susiri i la zugu i nin doaai n‑ti u ŋɔn boori seem maa.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Yarima birikɔ'anu dima wa! Di kyaŋŋi la wula ka ya baŋŋi duniya wa ni sangbana an wulisiri seem, ka ku laa n‑toaai m‑baŋŋi zuna saha wa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Dinzugu ka n yɛtti ya, ni, ya susima ka ya nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka ya nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya boolima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Ni, Bɔ n lee n‑niŋi ka ya mammaŋŋa bu baŋŋni din tuusi? Sɔ yi ya zaŋŋi i n‑taasiri sarikatta saani, ka nyin ni ŋɔn na bɛ soori zugu n‑kyɛnna, see ka i kyɛ ka ya naai noori m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa; ka di yi pa lala u nin zaŋŋi i n‑kyaŋŋi sarikatta maa saani, ka sarikatta maa ti zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi dansarika gura nuu ni, ka gura maa mun naayi zaŋŋi i n‑kpeesi dansarika duu.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama ŋɔnkam susira deaari ni, ŋɔnkam boora nyari ni, ka ŋɔnkam boonna ba nin yoaai n‑ti u.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N ya yɛli i ni, ni, i ku toaai n‑yi bɛni na see ka i yɔ i samni maa n‑naai, halli ka la'afu yinni gba ku kpalim.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Yaseeba ban mari ya biisi wa, n ya boosi ya, ni, i bii yi ti susi ziŋŋa i saani, i nin zaŋŋi waafu n‑ti u, bee?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Bee u yi ti susi gyɛlli, i nin ti zaŋŋi naŋŋa n‑ti u? - Ay, i ku niŋi lala!.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Tɔ! ya nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban mari teesibeeri ya beebu ni, ka ni di zaa yɔri halli yarima gba mi yan zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑tiri ya biisi: dinŋɔna ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni bu ya zaŋŋi u Vuusim Kasi maa n‑ti ban susiri u.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa ti katti arizimbeoo sɔ saani ka arizinni wa daa kyɛ ka doo maa kpalim n‑nyɛ zɔligu. Ka arizinni maa ŋɔn daa yi na ka kyɛ u maa, zɔligu maa daa tɔ'asiri ni, ka di daa niŋi zama maa alahazibu.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Amaa niriba maa seeba daa yɛliya, Biyɛlizibuli m mari u: u zaŋŋi ArizinNaaba yiiko n‑katti la arizimbeeri maa.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Baseeba mun daa zamsi u n‑susiri u, ni, u kyɛ ka alahazibu tunseelli yi saazugu na ka ba nya.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yisa daa mi ba sufu ni teesa ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka dizugu u daa yɛli ba, Tinseelli kam zaa kan timbiisi ŋɔn yi purigi n‑zabri ni taaba nin sa'am zaa, bee yiri dima yi zabri ni taaba, lala yiri maa nin ti lu.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Dizugu ŋɔn Biyɛlizibuli muna, u yi ti za'asi u mammaŋŋa, ŋɔn gba ku toaai n‑zaani. Dama ya yɛliya, ni, Biyɛlizibuli yiiko ka n zaŋŋi n‑katti arizimbeeri.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Ka dinŋɔna n yi zaŋŋi la Biyɛlizibuli yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri maa, ka ya karimbiseeba ban katti ba la zaŋŋi ŋɔni dinni n‑katta? Dizugu ban nin ti kari ya yɛttɔ'a wa sariya n‑lusi ya.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ka m mun zaŋŋi la Naawunni yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni, dinŋɔna Naawunni naam pun daŋŋi m‑paai ya na.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Yaa daana yi ti mari u tɔppu neema n‑guri u yiri, sɔsɔ ku niŋi u marigu seela.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari yaa n‑gaari u yi ti tuusi u n‑nyaŋŋi u, u faari la u tɔppu neenseesi ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda maa, ka fa u yiri puuni buna zaa m‑paasi, n‑zaŋŋi a n‑tisi u nyaaŋŋa dima.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ku sakki ni u zɛ n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔna n za'asiri ma n‑yi n nyaaŋŋa; bee sɔ yi ku suŋŋi ma ni ti la'asi bunwurima, u wurimni a ni m‑basira.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Tɔ; ka arizimbɛsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa kpɛ dɔsɔ puuni n‑su u. Ka ba daa yiisi u ŋɔn daa bɛ dɔsɔ puuni maa n‑kari u m‑basi. Ka u daa yi n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinni yoo ni, tinkuuma maa ni, m‑boori vuusim ziiya, halli nti gyɛa. Ka u daa yɛli, ni, See ka n labi n doo maa saani - yiri seaa man daa yi ka kyɛ la ni.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Ka u daa ti labi na n‑nya ka ba pun pii yiri maa kasikasi n‑sɛ'asi ka suŋŋa.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Dizugu u daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u arizintaaba ayɔpɔi seeba ban bɛ n‑gaari u mammaŋŋa, ka ba daa kpɛ nti bɛ bɛni. Di saha maa ka di daama beesigu daa bɛ pam n‑gaari din daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ seem maa.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti anŋɔ maa, ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ zama maa puuni n‑tasi m‑booli, ni, I ma sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai i, ka daa mɔ'asi i maa - ni u zugsuŋŋu.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli u, Di pa lala. Zugsuŋŋu dima n nyɛ baseeba ban wum Naawunni yɛligu ka sakkri n‑dɔla.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Sahanseaa ka zama pam daa la'am na m‑maali n‑zoaari pam, ka Yisa daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba, ni, Zuna niriba wa n nyɛ ninvugbeeri. Ba ba'a kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni, ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku nya sɛseelli m‑paasi la Gyoona alahazibu yɛla zugu.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Ya nyama: Niniva dima ŋɔn daa nya Anabi Gyoona yɛla ka di daa niŋi ba alahazibu seem la, lala ka zuna niriba nin nya man Ninsaala Bii yɛla ka di nin niŋi ba alahazibu.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Pɔ'a-naaba sɔ ŋɔn daa su nudirigu lugri tinseaa la daa yi wa'aligu n‑kyɛ na ni u ti wum NaaSulɛmaana yɛm yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu u nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari, dama ŋɔn gaari NaaSulɛmaana bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Niniva dima gba daa wum Gyoona wa'azu yiisigu la ka daa kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑basi ka niŋi tuuba, ka dizugu ba nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari, dama ŋɔn gaari anabi Gyoona bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka, amaa u zaŋŋi ka n‑tɔm la bunseelli zugu ni ban kam kpeeri na zaa nin nya neesim maa.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Nira nini n nyɛ hoo u ningbina firila: i nini yi neaai suŋŋa, i ningbina zaa mari la neesim, nyain! Amaa a yi ti sa'amna, i ningbina zaa bɛ la zibsim ni.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Dizugu di kyɛ ka neesim din bɛ i ni maa ti leebi zibsim.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Ka i ningbina zaa yi neaai suŋŋa, halli ka zibsim ka di ni halli beelabeela, dinŋɔna di nin neaari ni zaa, kaman firila ŋɔn neaari seem la.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Tɔ; Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ yɛttɔ'a maa ni ka Farisisɔ daa zuusi u, ni u kyɛ u yiri maa na ka ba ti la'am n‑di bundirigu: ka u daa kyaŋŋi ka ba daa ti zinni ni ba di.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Ka di gyɛri Farisii maa ŋɔn daa nya ka Yisa bu daŋŋi m‑peaai u nuusi ka zinni ni u di maa zugu.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Ka ti Dugma daa yɛli u, Yarima Farisii dima peaari la kɔbeesi ni laasi nyaaŋŋa, amaa ka ya puuni na paali ninvugfaabu ni yɛlibeeri.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Yarim gyɛra wa! Pa Naawunni ŋɔn daa maali nyaaŋŋa la n daa maali puuni maa gba?.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Dizugu ya zaŋŋma din bɛ laasi maa puuni n‑tiri faradima, ka bunseelli kam zaa nin leebi kasi n‑ti ya.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima Farisii dima zugu! Dama ya yiisiri la naanzuuwa ni kpaligu ni zɛvaari zaa zakka, amaa ka lee n‑kyɛri yɛlimaŋni tumpu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba, ni wurilim zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni, m‑basira. Ya yi ti teaari anŋɔ ka bu laa n‑tam seesi la, di yi sɔa.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima Farisii dima zugu! Dama ya boori la tooni ziziisi ya yi ti la'am Naawunni la'aŋŋu duri maa ni, ka na boori ni sɔkam zaa puusiri ya daasi puuni.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya ŋmani la hoo gbala seesi niriba ŋɔn noori n‑gatta ka bu mi, ni, gballi nla.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Tɔ, ka di saha maa ka Naawunni zaaligu baŋŋnisɔ daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, N Karimba! i yi tɔ'asiri nŋɔa, i tuuri la tirim gba m‑paasira.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi u, ni, Yarima gba ban nyɛ zaaligu baŋŋniba, zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya zaŋŋi la zitibsa n‑ziinni niriba, ziseesi ban ku toaai n‑zi, ka ya sɔ mun pun ku wukki ziiri maa halli beela n‑suŋŋi ba.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, amaa ya yaadima n daa nyɛ ban daa ku ba.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Lala zugu ya wulisiri, ni, ya nini tigi ya yaadima ŋɔn daa niŋi seem la - ni, ban daa ku ba, ka yarima mun n maanni ba gbala.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Naawunni yɛm yɛla puuni ba daa pun yɛli, ni, Naawunni nin tum anabidima ni tuumudima na, ka ya nin ku baseeba ka kari baseeba nti namsi ba pam.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Dizugu zuna niriba nin ti zi taalli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban daa ku anabidima zaa yɛla maa, duniya piiligu ni la sa.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: n‑yi Eebli kuubu ni, halli nti paai Zakarii ban daa ku sɔ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, Dutitaari ni saramaaligu zii maa sunsukkuni la. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, Naawunni siri nin zaŋŋi ba taalli n‑ziili zuna niriba wa.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zaaligu baŋŋniba wa zugu! Dama ya zaŋŋi la bamsim bamsigu za'anɔri saafibii m‑basi. Yarima mammansi ku sakki n‑kpɛa, ka ya ta'ari ban kpeeri soori.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi bɛni, Farisii dima ni zaaligu karimbadima daa piili m‑boosiri u boosa ni ba lugsi u noori ni.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: Ba daa guusiri ni ba gbaai u, u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Tɔ. Di daa ti lu la Vuusim Dabsiri daari, ka Yisa kyaŋŋi Farisii dima kpeensɔ yiri, ni ba ti la'am n‑di bundirigu. Ka Farisidoo maa daa kur gɔsiri u.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Ka ba nya ka baarisɔ daa bɛ Yisa tooni, ka u ningbina zaa daa kur fuusi u dooru maa zugu.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi zaaligu baŋŋniba ni Farisii dima, ni, Di tuusiya ni nira kyɛ ka baara kpaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri daari, bee di bu tuusi.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ka ba zaa daa sini u. Ka Yisa daa tibi doo maa, ka ti u soori ni u labi n‑kuli.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Ya ŋɔni n lee nin mari u biiya - bee, halli u naafu gba - ka u ti lu buliga ni Vuusim Dabsiri daari, ka u ku kyaŋŋi nti yiisi u yoomayooma.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ka ba daa kɔŋŋi u labsigu.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi n‑nya ban daa booli ninvugseeba ni ba la'am na nti di maa, ka ba kur boori gyirima ziziisi n‑zintira. Ka daa yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri, ni.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Ba yi ti booli i ni i kyɛm amriya kyuu diibu ni na, di daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zinni gyirima zizii ni, dama di pa seem ŋɔn mari gyirima pam n‑gaari i nin ti kpɛ na.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn booli ya bayi maa na nin ti yɛli nyini, ni, i doaama ka kyɛ zizii maa n‑ti u. Di saha maa vi nin ti gbaai i, ka i doaai nti zinni tarimma ziziiya.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Ay! amaa ba yi booli i, see ka i daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nti zinni tarimma zizii ni, ka ŋɔn booli i nin kyɛ na nti yɛli, N zɔsuŋŋu, doaam na n‑du n‑zinni toonni zizii wa ni! Di saha maa ka i nin nya gyirima i taaba maa saani.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusi u maŋŋa, ba nin sigsi u; amaa ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa muna, ba nin duusi lala nira pam.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: U daa laa n‑yɛli dɔsɔ ŋɔn booli ba maa, ni, I yi ti boonni niriba ni ba kyɛ i yiri na n‑di kyuu bundirigu, di booli i zɔdima, bee i dɔ'ariba, bee i zizii kpɛseeba ban nyɛ bundaandima. Lala niriba nin ti booli i gba ba maŋtoori kyuusi diibu ni, ka lala ka ba nin yɔ i samni maa.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Amaa i yi ya di kyuu, boolim faradima, ni gbarisi, ni wɔbsi, ni zoomma.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: I yi niŋi lala i nin nya laara. Lala niriba ku nyaŋŋi n‑labsi n‑yɔ i. I nin nya zugsuŋŋu, dama Naawunni nin ti labsi n‑yɔ i, ninvugsuma yi ti neaai kum ni sahanseaa maa.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Tɔ, ka ninvugsɔ n daa bɛ ban la'amni n‑zintiri maa sunsukkuni, ka daa wum lala, ka u daa yɛli u, Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn la'am n‑diri kyuu maa diibu, Naawunni naam kyɛnni saha na.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Amaa ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Wumma! Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑ya di u kyutitaari, ka tum m‑booli niriba pam.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Tɔ, ka kyuu maa diibu daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi m‑booli ŋɔn pun zuusi ninvugseeba la, ni, ba kyɛm na, ni, di zaa siri biya.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Amaa ba zaa yinni yinni daa susiri u soori, ni, ba ku nyaŋŋi n‑kyɛ na. Ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑booli sɔ maa daa yɛli u, ni, u da la puu ka di kpa la talasi ni u kyaŋŋi nti kaai ka; ni, N susiri i ni: n ku toaai n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ka ba yinni gba laa n‑yɛli, ni, u da la na'akoora piiya ka u ya n‑kyaŋŋi nti nya ban tumni seem maa; ni, Dizugu n susiri i ni: n ku toaai n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Ka ba sɔ gba laa n‑yɛli, ni, u di la pɔ'a, ka dizugu ŋɔn gba ku toaai n‑kyɛ na.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Dizugu ka daabri maa daa labi na nti yɛli u dugma ba zaa ŋɔn yɛli seem maa. Ka yiri wa daana sufu daa yisigi pam, ka u daa yɛli u daabri maa, ni, Zɔm yooma n‑yi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa wa puuni m‑paai sɔkyira, ni daasi ni, nti booli faradima ni gbarisi ni zoomma ni wɔbsi, n‑zuusi ba na.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ka daabri maa daa kyaŋŋi ka labi na n‑yɛli u, N dugma, n niŋi nyin yɛli seem maa, amaa paalu na kur kpalim ni.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Ka u dugma maa daa yɛli u, Yim yaasa n‑dɔli sɔbisi ni tiisi gbina nti mugsi niriba ni ba kyɛ na ka n yiri wa paali zaa.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: N ya n‑yɛli ya ni, ni, ninvugseeba ban daa daŋŋi n‑zuusi maa zaa - ba sɔsɔ ku lam n kyuu bundirigu maa, halli beela! .

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Tɔ; ka zama pam daa la'asi n‑dɔli u, ka u daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ba.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Sɔ yi ti kyɛ n saani na, ka bu yaai u toori ba, ni u ma, u pɔ'a ni u biisi, u bɛya ni u tinzɔppa ni u tinzɔpɔ'aba, halli ni u mammantoori nyɔvuri gba, di daama ku toaai n‑niŋi n nyaandɔla.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Ni, Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ku sakki n‑kpikki u toori kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa ku toaai n‑niŋi n nyaandɔla.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Sɔ yi ti bɛ ya saani n‑teesiri, ni, u nin mɛ gyirambisa yiri, u nin daŋŋi n‑zinni n‑zamsi lala tuma ligri ŋɔn ya lu seem, n‑nya u yi mari ligri an nin toaai m‑mɛ ka n‑naai: bee, m ma'ai ni?.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Di yi pa lala, u nin ti piili ka nyeebri, ka ku toaai n‑naai ka zaa.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: ka ninvugseeba zaa ban gɔsi n‑nya ka piiligu la nin ti maali u ansarisi n‑yɛtta. Doo wa piili u yiri meebu ka kɔŋŋi ka naari.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Bee, nasɔ yi ti bɛ ni m‑boori ni u tuusi u nataraansɔ tɔppu, u nin daŋŋi n‑zinni m‑mari u sufu ni laasaabu n‑nya u langyima maa nyɛ la babala: bee, di pa lala? Ba yi ti paai tusipiiya ka u dindaana mari la tusa pisi, u nin boosi u maŋŋa, ni, u niriba maa nin toaai n‑nyaŋŋi ban zoaai n‑gaari ba buyi lala, bee?.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: U yi ku nyaŋŋi, u nin daŋŋi n‑tum biiya ka u kyaŋŋi naa ŋɔnla saani n‑susi u, ni, ba maalim ba yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Dizugu ninvugsɔ zaa ŋɔn bu za'asi u seelikam zaasa yɛla ku toaai n‑niŋi man nyaandɔla.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Ni, Yaarim nya'asa, amaa yaarim nya'asim yi ti yi ka kyɛ di, di laa n‑ya n‑niŋi la wula ka di nya'asim maa labi na yaasa? Di laa n‑ka bukyi: ba bu ya zaŋŋi di n‑niŋi puri ni bumbura.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: halli n‑niŋi binduuri gba, see ka ba kur ya zaŋŋi di m‑basi di yɔri. Ni, Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Tɔ. Sahanseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa ti booli u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la n‑la'am taaba, u daa ti ba soori ni ba mari yiiko ni fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi arizimbeeri yɛla, ka tibri dooru balibu kam.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: U daa tum ba ni ba yi nti mooli Naawunni naam lahabaari, ka tibi baaridima.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya di zaŋŋi bunseelli ya sɔkyɛnni maa ni: yan di zaŋŋi zangbeaa bee booriga, bee bundirigu, bee ligri, bee bunyeera ayiyi.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Ya yi kur ti paai yiri seaa, ya kpeema ka bɛ bɛni halli nti yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Niriba yi bu deaai ya, yan yiiri tiŋŋa maa sahanseaa la, ya kpaasim ya napɔntam m‑basi, ka di leebi din wulisiri ban bɛ seem n‑gyanni ba.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Ka dizugu ba daa yi soori n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gyili tinkpansi ni, m‑moonni lahabasuŋŋu maa ka tibri dooru balibu kam, lugri kam zaa.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Ka dizugu niriba daa yɛtti, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura n neaai kum ni, ka seeba yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya n labi na, halli seeba gba daa yɛtti, ni, kurimbuni sa anabisɔ n labi u nyɔvuri ni na. Ka NaaHɛroodi Tɛtirakyi la ŋɔn daa baŋŋi lala, ka wum dinkam zaa niŋni maa, di daa gyɛri u pam ka u daa yɛli, Man daa fikki Gyɔni zugu, ka ŋɔni yɛla ka n laa n‑wumni wa?, ka u daa kur kpaŋŋni u maŋŋa ni u ti nya u.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Tuumudima maa ŋɔn daa ti labi na, ba daa yɛli Yisa ban daa niŋi seem zaa. Ka u daa zaŋŋi ba ka ba foaai ba mammansi n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Amaa zama maa daa baŋŋi ŋɔn bɛ luginseaa ka dɔlisi u nɔba. Ka Yisa daa deaai ba ka tɔ'asi n‑ti ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam maa yɛla. U daa laa n‑tibi bankam zaa daa boori tippu.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Tɔ; ka wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti kabgi u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi maa daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, Tim niriba maa soori ka kyɛ ka ba wurim n‑kpɛ tinkpanseesi an gyiligi kpa maa ni, ni tiŋŋa maa zaa ni, nti bɔ bundirigu n‑di, ka nya goom ziiya, dama moo ni ka ti bɛ ni maa.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di.; ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, Ti ka diibu m‑paasi paanukpila anu, ni zaasim ayi kɔkɔa. See ka ti kyaŋŋi nti da bundirigu na n‑ti niriba pam wa zaa. Zama maa ni dɔppa mun daa paai niriba tusa anu.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Kyɛ ka niriba maa zinni ninvugu pisinunu.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Ka ba daa niŋi lala, ka sɔkam zaa zinni tiŋŋa.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Ka u zaŋŋi paanu anu ni zaasim ayi maa n‑gɔsi saazugu ka puusi Naawunni ka purigi bundirigu maa. Ka yi zaŋŋni di n‑tiri u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba tɔtti niriba maa.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Di saha maa ba zaa daa diya n‑tigi, ka nyaandɔliba maa daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Tɔ. Dindahinseaa Yisa daa ti bɛ ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa, ka u daa susiri la Naawunni u kɔkɔa; ka u daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ba yɛtti, ni, man nyɛ la ŋɔni.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ninvugseeba yɛliya, ni, i nyɛ la Gyɔni Kɔsuura; ka baseeba mun yɛliya, ni, i nyɛ la Ilaygya, halli ka seeba mun yɛli, ni, i nyɛ la kurimbuni sa anabisɔ ŋɔn labi u nyɔvuri ni na.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Ka Yisa labsi m‑boosi ba yaasa, ni, Ka yarima mun teesiya, ni, n nyɛ la ŋɔni?; ka Piita gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, I nyɛ la Masiiya - sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tum na la.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Ka Yisa daa kpamsi ba pam ni ba di yɛli sɔ, ni, u nyɛ la Masiiya maa.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: U daa laa n‑yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, See ka man Ninsaala Biiya di wahala pam. Tiŋŋa wa kpamma maa nin za'asi ma, ban ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima gba. Ba nin ku ma, ka daba ata daari n nin neaai kum ni n‑labi n nyɔvuri ni.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, Ŋɔnkam boori ni u dɔli ma, see ka u za'asi u maŋŋa ka zaŋŋi u kum dɔpulli n‑ziiri beoo kam n‑dɔli ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Dama ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Nira yi di duniya zaa ka kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, bee sa'amni ka, di suŋŋi u la wula?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi zɔri vi man ni n yɛligu maa zugu, ŋɔna zugu Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti zɔ vi, sahanseaa u yi mari u gyirima n‑kyɛ na, ni u Ba gyirima, ni malaakadima zaa m‑paasi u zugu.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛliya, ni, ninvugseeba bɛ yarima ban zɛya wa ni ka ku kpi pɔi, ka naayi nya Naawunni naam.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi lala nyaaŋŋa, di daa niŋi kaman daba anii ka u daa zaŋŋi Piita, ni Gyɔni, ni Gyeemsi m‑paasi u zugu, ka ba daa du zoori maa zugu ni ba ti susi Naawunni.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti susiri maa, u nindaa daa takkiya, ka u sutura neaai, nyain, kaman saa nya'asigu.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Ka ba daa nya ka dɔppa ayi daa yi na n‑la'am n‑tɔ'asiri ni Yisa - Musa ni Ilaygya n daa nyɛ ba.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Ba daa neaari ni veelim ni gyirima pam, ka ba yɛttɔ'a daa nyɛ la zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn nin paai Gyɛrusalɛm nti ka ni seem yɛla.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Di saha maa ka goom daa gbaai Piita ni u taaba maa: amaa ban daa ti neaai, ba daa nya Yisa gyirima veelim; ka nya dɔppa ayi maa, ka ba kpalim n‑zɛ u luga ni.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Ka niriba ayi maa ŋɔn daa ti ya n‑ŋmaligi n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ Yisa sahaseaa maa, Piita daa yɛli u, N Daana, ti kpa wa beesigu veela! Kyɛ ka ti ti suga ata - i dinni yinni, ni Musa dinni, ni Ilaygya mun dinni yinni. .

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa kur kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri lala maa sangbana di sigi na n‑lim ba, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba pam ban daa kpeeri sangbana maa ni zugu.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Ka kukɔri daa yi sangbana puuni na n‑yɛtti, ni, M bii nŋɔ, ŋɔna ka n gaŋŋi: ya wumma u noori.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Ka kukɔri maa ŋɔn daa ti tɔ'asi n‑naai, ba nya ka Yisa kɔkɔ n kpalim n‑zɛya. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi yɛlli maa n‑sina, ka daa bu yɛli ban daa nya seelli maa yɛla n‑ti sɔa.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: N‑neaai beoo ka ba daa sigi zoori maa zugu na, ka daa paai zama pam ziiya.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Ka niriba maa ni doo yinni daa kaasi m‑booli, ni, N Karimba! N susiri i ni, ni, i gɔsi m bii wa n‑ti ma, dama n ka bisɔ m‑paasi u zugu.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: I bu nya arizimbeoo ŋɔn zi u zugu n‑yisigri u seem? U yi ti bɛ m‑bɛ n‑kaasi pam, ka arizinni maa darigi u halli ka nantoori fufukku ti yiiri u noori ni na, n‑kur mugsiri u ka ku sakki ni u basi u.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Ka m pun susi i nyaandɔliba wa, ni, ba karima arizimbeoo maa m‑basi, ka ba bu nyaŋŋi.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, Ah! Ya zuna niriba wa n kur nyɛ bungɔlima, ka ku sakki yɛdda niŋbu! Daba ala ka mani ni yarima nin la'am m‑bɛ ni, bee daba ala ka n ya toaai n‑di ya sugru? Mari i bii maa n‑kyɛ kpa na.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa mari bii maa n‑kyɛnni Yisa saani na, arizimbeoo maa daa lusi u tiŋŋa ka darigi u. Ka Yisa daa tasi arizinni maa zugu ka kyɛ ka bii maa kpaŋŋi, ka u zaŋŋi u n‑labsi n‑ti u ba maa.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ka di daa niŋi niriba maa alahazibu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn galisi seem.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Tɔ; ka zama maa ŋɔn daa kur teesiri Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni alahazibu tunseelikam zaa yɛla maa, u daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ya kyɛ ka yɛttɔ'aseaa man ya tɔ'asi wa kpɛ ya tuba ni suŋŋa: ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Biiya n‑niŋi ninsaalidima nuu ni.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa gbinni, ka di daa sɔ'ai ni ba saani, ka ba daa kɔŋŋi di baŋŋni zaa. Ka ba mun daa bu boosi u m‑baŋŋi, dabeem ŋɔn daa mari ba maa zugu.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa ti ŋmeeri la taaba nangbankpeemni ni ba baŋŋi ŋɔn gaari u kpeaa ba puuni.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba sufu ni teesa, ka u booli bibila na n‑zaŋŋi u n‑zaali n‑tabli u mammaŋŋa, ka yɛli ba, ni.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: Ŋɔnkam deaari bibila wa tataabu man yuuri zugu deaari la mani, ka ŋɔnkam mun deaari ma deaari la Ŋɔn tum ma na maa. Dinŋɔna ŋɔn poora ya zaasa ni, di daama gaari ya zaa.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa yɛli Yisa, N Daana, ti da nya sɔ ka u mari i yuuri n‑katti arizimbeeri, ka ti bu sakki ni u tum lala, ŋɔn bu tabli ti la'aŋŋu n‑dɔla zugu.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Amaa ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, Di za'asiri u, dama ŋɔnkam bu zabri ya n nyɛ ŋɔn zɛ ya nyaaŋŋa.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ. Yisa saazugu lappu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai na, u daa gbaai la Gyɛrusalɛm kyɛnni ni nimbiri moaa.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Ka u daa tum tuumudima ni ba daŋŋma u tooni. Ka dizugu u tuumudima maa daa paai Samɛrii tinkpanseaa puuni nti maali siri zaa n‑guri u.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Ka tinkpaŋŋa maa dima daa ti za'asi, ni, ba ku deaai u, ŋɔn bɛ soori n‑kyɛnni Gyɛrusalɛm maa zugu.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba wa, ban yuya boonni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni la, ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, Ti Dugma, i boori ni ti kyɛ ka bugum yi saazugu na nti di ba, bee.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑tasi ba zugu;.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: ka ba daa doaai n‑gaari m‑paai tinkpanseaa nti gbɛ bɛni.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Yaasa, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa ti bɛ soori zugu n‑kyɛnna, dɔsɔ kyɛ na nti yɛli u, N nin dɔli i n‑kyɛnni luginseelli kam zaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɔbaasi mari la vɔya n‑kpeera, ka niinsi mun mari la teeri n‑tɔmna, amaa man Ninsaala Biiya ka luginseaa man nin zaŋŋi n zugu n‑dooli gba.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli dɔsɔ, ni, Dɔli ma!, ka di daama yɛli u, ni, Kur kyɛ ka n kuli ka m ba yi ti kpi ka n sɔ'ai u pɔi ka naayi kyɛ na n dɔli i.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Amaa ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Kyɛ ka kpiimma sɔ'ari ba kpiintaaba: nyin muna - kyɛma nti moonni Naawunni naam mooligu.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ka sɔ mun daa ti laa n‑kyɛ na nti yɛli Yisa, ni, N Daana, n nin dɔli i, amaa kyɛ ka n kuli n‑kyeoosi n yiri dima pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ na n‑dɔli i.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi u nuu n‑gbaai kuuri ka laa n‑gɔsiri u nyaaŋŋa bu sumni Naawunni naam beebu ni.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Sahanseaa ka lampodeaariba pam daa kyɛnni Yisa saani na, ni ala'akyi dima gba, ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Ka Farisii dima ni karimbadima daa nyurimni yɛttɔ'adɔ'asa, ni, Doo wa deaari la ala'akyi dima ka ŋɔn ni ban la'amni n‑dira.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Tɔ, ka Yisa daa yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri wa, ni.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Ya ninvugsɔ yi mari la u peesi kɔbga ka ba yinni ti bɔrigi, u bu ya m‑bɔ u ziiya? Ay! - u nin kyɛ u peesi kɔbga‑yinni‑ka la ka ba kpalim m‑bɛ moo maa ni ka kyaŋŋi nti gyili m‑bɔ pɛsɔ ŋɔn bɔrigi ziiya halli nti nya u.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Ka u yi ti nya u, u nin zaŋŋi u m‑bugi u bɔ'ari zugu ni sufupeelli m‑mari u n‑kuli yiri ni.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: U yi paai yiri ni na, u nin booli u zɔdima ni u kpeerintaaba ni ba la'amma na, ka yɛli ba. Ya paasim n zugu m‑mari sufupeelli, dama n nya m pɛsɔ ŋɔn daa bɔrigi la. .

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ni, N yɛtti ya ni, ni, lala gba ka ba mari sufupeelli arizana ni pam, ala'akyi daana yinni zugu, u yi ti takki u teesibeeri tuma n‑niŋi tuuba sahanseaa la: ka di gaari ban mari sufupeelli seem maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugsuma pisiwai ni awai seeba taalli ŋɔn bu dɔli ni ba niŋma tuuba.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Ka u laa n‑yɛli ba, Bee, yaasa: pɔ'a yi bɛ ni m‑mari u anzulifa ligri piiya ka a la'afu yinni lɔrigi n‑lu: u bu ya n‑nyoaai firila ka gbaai duu maa zaa piiri ni nimmoaa, ni u ti bɔ ka ziiya; halli nti nya?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Ka u yi ti nya ka n‑naai, u nin booli u zɔdima ni u kpeerintaaba ni ba la'am na, ka yɛli ba, Ya paasim n zugu m‑mari sufupeelli, dama n nya n la'aseaa kan da bɔrigi la.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Ka n yɛtti ya, ni, lala gba n nyɛ di Naawunni malaakadima saani: ba mari la sufupeelli, ala'akyi daana yinni zugu, sɔ ŋɔn takki u teesibeeri tuma n‑niŋi tuuba maa.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni m‑mari u bidibsi ayi.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Dindahinseaa ka doo wa bisaani maa daa ti yɛli u, M ba, tim ma n faari tɔrli! Ka u ba daa puri ŋɔn ni u beeri faari n‑ti ba.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Di daa bu yuui ka bisaani maa daa la'asi u marigu zaa n‑yi n‑kpa n‑kyaŋŋi tinwa'aligu. Bɛni ka u daa ti bɛ yɔriyɔri n‑sa'am u marigu zaa.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti di ŋɔn mari seelli kam zaa n‑naai sahanseaa la, kɔm daa lu tingbaŋŋu maa ni, ka u yi leebi ŋɔn kɔŋŋi bundira.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Dizugu u daa kyaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa ni dɔsɔ saani ni u tim u tunseaa; ka u daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u bɛ moo ni n‑gɔsiri u kurikyuudima n‑ti u.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Ka u daa yi ti boori la u di kurikyuu diibu maa, ka sɔ bu tiri u bunseelli.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Di saha maa ka u yɛm daa ti zuligi na, ka u daa teesi u sufu ni n‑yɛli, M ba yiri tuntumniba bɛ ni m‑mari diibu halli n‑diri ka kyeeri gba, ka man mun bɛ kpa wa ka kɔm kuuri ma wula?.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: N nin yi n‑labi m ba saani n‑yɛli u, M ba, n tum Naawunni taalli ni nyin gba taalli, m bu laa n‑sumni ni n nyɛ i biiya:.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: kur zaŋŋma ma n‑leebi i yiri tuntumna. Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi u ba saani.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa na bɛ wa'aligu n‑kyɛnni na maa, u ba daa nya u ka namboo daa gbaai u, ka u zɔ n‑tuusi u bii maa soori, ka zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑gɔbi u ka puusi u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Tɔ, ka bii maa yi yɛli u, M ba, n tum Naawunni taalli ni nyin taalli gba, m bu laa n‑sumni ni n nyɛ i biiya . amaa ka u ba daa yɛli u tuntumniba, ni.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Ya kyɛm yooma! Ya kyɛma nti zaŋŋi kparisuŋŋu na n‑yeeli u, ka zaŋŋi nuntuuwa m‑piri u nubila, ka zaŋŋi namna na m‑piri u nɔba ni!.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Ka kyaŋŋi n‑gbaai na'asuŋŋu la n‑kɔrigi m‑maali ka ya ti la'am n‑di sufupeelli kyuu.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Dama m bii wa daa kpi ni, ka laa n‑vu yaasa; u daa bɔrigi ni, ka n laa n‑nya u. Ka ba zaa sufu daa pɛligi pam di zugu.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Ka ban daa diri kyuu maa sahanseaa maa, doo maa bikpeema daa bɛ la puu ni. Ŋɔn daa ti doaai n‑kunni na, m‑pɔtti yiri maa ni na, u daa wum bunŋmeera ni waasi damni.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: ka daa booli yiri maa bisɔ nti boosi u, ni, Bɔ n niŋi.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, I tinzɔ la n kuli na, ka i ba gbaai na'asuŋŋu la n‑kɔrigi, ŋɔn labi na ni alaafɛ la zugu.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Ka bikpeema maa daa gyɛ suuri ka za'asi, ni, u ku kpɛ dundɔŋŋu maa ni. Ka u ba maa daa yi na nti bɛlim u.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Amaa ka u labsi n‑yɛli u ba, ni, Gɔsima! Yuunseesi an gaari wa zaa mani n kur namsira, n‑tumni tunkpeema n‑tiri i, ka mun na zi n‑za'asi i noori halli yummu; amaa di zaa yɔri i na zi n‑gbaai halli bubila gba n‑ti ma ni n deaama ka mani ni n zɔdima la'am n‑di kyuu.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Amaa i bisɔ ŋɔn sa'am i marigu ni pɔ'agoora wa ŋɔn ti gɔ n‑labi na, i gbaai na'asuŋŋu la n‑kɔrigi n‑ti u! Lala ka soori.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Ka u ba maa yɛli u, M bisuŋŋu, nyin kur bɛ la n saani saha kam, ka man mari bunseelli kam zaa nyɛ la i dinni.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Amaa di kpa la talasi ni ti di kyuu wa ka mari sufupeelli: dama i tinzɔ wa daa kpi ni, ka laa n‑vu yaasa; u daa bɔrigi ni, ka ti nya u yaasa. .

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Tɔ. Din saha maa ka ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ bɛni daa tɔ'asiri Galilii dimseeba yɛla: ba daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Gɔmna Pailiti daa ku ba, ban daa maanni ba sara sahanseaa la, ka ba ziim ni bunkɔbri ziim maa daa gyɛtti taaba.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, Kala, ya teesiya, ni, Galilii dima wa n daa nyɛ ninvugbeeri ni ala'akyi dima n‑gaari ba Galilii taaba zugu ka ba daa namsi ba dinŋɔ maa?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: N yɛtti ya, ni, Ay! Amaa faasee ka ya zaa takki ya teesibeeri ka kyɛ ya tunkyɔ'ama zaa: di yi pa lala ya gba nin baasi yɔri hoo ban daa baasi seem maa.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Bee, ninvugpiiya ni anii ŋɔna, seeba ban daa bɛ Sailɔ'am fɔŋŋu ni ka gyirambisa la daa lu n‑ku ba la: ya teesiya, ni, ban daa mari taalli pam n‑gaari Gyɛrusalɛm dima zaa?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: N yɛtti ya, ni, Ay! Faasee ka ya takki ya teesibeeri ka kyɛ ya tunkyɔ'ama maa: di yi pa lala ya gba nin baasi yɔri hoo ban daa baasi seem maa.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Di saha maa ka u yi yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri, ni, Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, m‑mari u tiwanni u gaadi puuni. U daa ti kyaŋŋi ni u tɔsi tii maa wala ka bu nya halli tiwalli yinni.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tumni u gaadi maa tuma n‑tiri u la, ni, Yuuma ata nŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na m‑boori tii wa wala ka bu nyara. Ŋmaam ka n‑lusi tiŋŋa! Ni, Wula ka ka kur diri tingbaŋŋu wa tam yɔriyɔri.
