eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, M Bapira, kyɛli ka yuumni yinni m‑paasi yaasa, ka n nin gbi n‑gyiligi ka, n‑niŋi tam n‑niŋi ka, ka yuundaari ka ka ti wali wala, tɔ!.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Ka mun yi na bu ti wali, dinŋɔna nyin ŋmaama ka m‑basi. .

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Bundaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, m‑mari u tuntunsɔ ŋɔn daa su u arizikki tuma fukumsi zaa. Ka ba daa kyɛ bundaana wa saani na nti wulisi u, ni, u tuntumna maa diri la u ligri maa yɔriyɔri.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Tɔ. Ka Vuusim Dabsiri dahinseaa Yisa daa ti bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑wulisiri ba.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Dizugu ka bundaana maa daa booli u na n‑yɛli u, Ka bɔ yɛttɔ'a ka n wumni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i saani wa? Kyɛma nti sɔbi laasaabu gbaŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n yɛliseesi an bɛ i nuu ni la zaa, ka mari na n‑ti ma; ka i ku laa n‑su n tuma maa fukumsi yaasa.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ni m‑mari arizimbeoo ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka u kɔŋŋi yaa halli nti paai yuuma piiya ni anii. Pɔ'a maa daa kuntiri ni ka bu nyaŋŋi n‑doaari n‑zaantiri kyippi.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Ka doo maa daa yɛli u sufu ni, ni, N yi ya niŋi la wula, n daana ŋɔn ya yiisi ma u tuma maa ni wa? N ka yaa di nin toaai n‑kɔa; di mun laa m‑mari ma vi ni m maanni bara. ni.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa nya pɔ'a maa, u daa booli u na n‑yɛli u, M ma, i dooru laa n‑ka i ni.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: Tɔ! M baŋŋiya man nin niŋi seem din nin kyɛ ka niriba nin deaai ma ba yiya ni, ba yi ti kari ma n kpa wa tuma wa ni m‑basi sahanseaa la.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: ka daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu. Ka pɔ'a maa daa di teaai u maŋŋa n‑doaai n‑zaani suŋŋa, ka gbaai Naawunni pɛ'abu.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Ka dizugu u daa la'asi la ninvugseeba ban di u daana sama maa zaa, yinniyinni: ka daŋŋi m‑boosi tooni daana maa, ni, u daana samni ala m bɛ u saani?.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Tɔ, ka la'aŋŋu duu zuguraana la sufu daa yisigi pam, Yisa ŋɔn daa tibi pɔ'a maa Vuusim Dabsiri maa zugu, ka u yɛli niriba maa, ni, Dabsa ayoobu m bɛ ni, ni ba zaŋŋi n‑tum tuma: dizugu lala dabsa maa puuni ya kyɛm na nti tibi, ka pa ni Vuusim Dabsa.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: - ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, kpaŋmana kɔbga; ka u daa yɛli u, Tɔ, deaam i samni gbaŋŋu ya'abi n‑takki n‑sɔbi, ni, kpaŋmana pisinu.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Ka ti Dugma labsi u, ni, Yarima birikɔ'anu dima wa! Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa ni ka ya nira zaa bu lɔrigri ya naafu bee ya buŋŋa n‑kyɛnni n‑nyuusiri ba koom?.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Ka daa laa m‑boosi ŋɔn pa tooni daana maa zugu gba, ni, u daana samni ala m bɛ u saani? ka u labsi n‑yɛli, ni, kabootu kɔbga; ka u daa yɛli u, Deaam i samni gbaŋŋu ya'abi n‑takki n‑sɔbi, ni, bootu pisinii.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Amaa pɔ'a wa ŋɔna, Ibrahim bii n nyɛ u, ka Sintaani daa ba u bansi yuuma piiya ni anii. Di bu sumni ni ni u bansi wa kabi m‑basi ka kyɛ u Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa ni.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: Ka u daana ŋɔn daa baŋŋi tumbeeri daana wa ŋɔn niŋi seem wa, u daa yɛli, Wooy! U mari la yɛm pam! Dama duniya wa ninvugbeeri mari la yɛm zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba duniya wa yɛla n‑gaari ban dɔli neesim soori maa ŋɔn mari seem.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Ŋɔn daa yɛli dinŋɔ n‑naai, vi daa gbaai ban za'asiri u yɛla maa zaa, amaa zama maa daa mari la sufupeelli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn daa tum tunveela maa.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Ni, N ya yɛli ya ni, ni, duniya wa ligri nyɛ la bunyɔya: ya zaŋŋma a m‑bɔ niriba zoori, ka lala nin kyɛ ka a yi ti gaari ka kyɛ ya sahanseaa maa, ya zɔdima nin zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi ba yiri maasim ni, saha din ka tɔriga.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ka Naawunni naam maa yɛla muna - a ŋmani la bɔa?.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Dama ba yi kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi ka u zɛ yimmu, ba yi ti kyɛ ka u su yɛttɔ'akara gba u nin zɛ yummu. Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn su yɛttɔ'abisi ka ka yɛdda a puuni, ŋɔn nin laa n‑kɔŋŋi yɛdda yɛttɔ'akara gba puuni.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: - A ŋmani la hoo zinzambeelli nira ŋɔn zaŋŋi m‑buri u puu ni; ka ka buli na n‑leebi hoo tiiya, ka niinsi tɔmni ka wula maa zugu.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Dizugu ya yi bu zɛ yimmu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi duniya wa la'ayɔya yɛla, ŋɔni nin lee nin sakki n‑zaŋŋi arizikki maŋni n‑niŋi ya nuu ni.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Ka daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, Bɔ yaasa ka n nin zaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla maa m‑ma'asi?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Bee ba yi zaŋŋi sɔ bunni n‑niŋi ya nuu ni ka ya bu zɛ yimmu, ŋɔni n lee nin zaŋŋi ya mammaŋŋa dinni n‑ti ya?.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Di ŋmani la dabinseesi pɔ'a ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑gyɛri zum ni, ni u ti sɛ paanu. U zaŋŋi dabina beela n‑niŋi zum pam, hoo kuriba ata, puuni, amaa a dɔli halli nti doaai zum maa zaa n‑fuusi.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi dugmadima ayi daabri: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka laa n‑kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ: Yisa daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli tinsi ni tinkpansi n‑wulisira; ka gatti n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Tɔ; ka Farisii dima daa nyɛ ban wuri ligri pam: ba daa wum Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa ka ba daa maanni u ansarisi.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa boosi u, ni, M Bapira, niriba beela n nyɛ ban nin tuligi, bee? Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Yarima n nyɛ ban boori ni ninsaala nya ya yɛla n‑yɛli, ni yɛlimaŋni niriba n nyɛ ya; amaa Naawunni ŋɔn mi din bɛ ya sufu puuni. Ninsaalidima ŋɔn tibgri bunseelli pam la nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn kyisiri seelli.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑kpɛ za'anɔmugriga maa ni, dama niriba pam nin boori ni ba kpɛ, ka ku nyaŋŋi.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Ni, Musa zaaligudima ni anabidima sɔppu yɛla n daa pun bɛ ni, halli ka Gyɔni ti kyɛ na; amaa di saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ba na kur moonni Naawunni naam lahabasuŋŋu ka sɔkam zaa zaŋŋi kpeeŋŋu m‑moori ni u kpɛ di maasim ni.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Ni, Yiri maa daana yi sarigi n‑doaai n‑zaani ka kpari za'anɔri maa, ya nin zaani za'anɔri tooni sa m‑boonna ka kpaasira, ka nin susiri, ni, Ti daana, yoaam n‑ti ti!; amaa u nin labsi n‑yɛli ya, ni, M bu mi yan yi seela na.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: Ni, Musa zaaligu muna - arizana ni duniya zaa yi ti gaari m‑basi, di bu tɔa; amaa zaaligu bumbila fiiŋ gba basibu tɔ ni halli nti yakki.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Ka ya nin yɛli u, ni, Tirima ni nyini n daa la'am n‑dira, ka nyuura, halli ka i daa dɔli ti tiŋŋa sɔya n‑wulisira.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Ni, Ŋɔnkam kari u pɔ'a ka laa n‑di pɔ'asɔ tumni la zina, ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn di lala pɔ'a maa gba tumni la zina.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Ka u nin labsi n‑yɛli ya, M bu mi yan yi seela na. Ya kyɛma sa, yarim tumbeeri dima wa.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, Bundaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑yeeri kparikara ni bunyeerisuma zaa, ka diri u sufu yuubu bundira dabsiri kam zaa.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Ka ya nin paai ya'aseelli niriba ŋɔn bɛ ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina la. Ka ya nin ti nya Ibrahim ni Yisahaaku ni Yaakubu, m‑paasi anabidima maa zaa, ka ba ti bɛ Naawunni naam ziiya, ka ba zaŋŋi yarima n‑yiisi m‑basi sa.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Ka faradaansɔ mun gba daa bɛ ni, ka u ningbina zaa daa niŋi nɔra: doo wa yuuri daa boonni la Lazarusi. Ka beoo kam ka ŋɔn Lazarusi daa yi kyɛnni bundaana wa yiri za'anɔri ni na n‑dɔ bɛni.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Niriba nin ti yi wuntampusiri n‑kyɛ na, ni wuntanluri, ni nudirigu ni nuzaa luga zaa, nti zinni ba ziziisi ni, Naawunni naam kyuu diibu maa ni.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: U daa yi dɔya m‑boori la bundaana maa bundirikyɛba ti lu tiŋŋa ka u piisi n‑di, ka baasi kur kyɛnni na n‑leenni u nɔra maa.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Ni, Yɛlimaŋni, ninvugseeba m bɛ ni n‑kpalim nyaaŋŋa ka nin ti leebi ban daŋŋni tooni, ka ban mun daŋŋi tooni la nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Tɔ, di daa niŋi lala, ka faradaana maa daa ti kpi, ka malaakadima zaŋŋi u n‑taasi ti yaaba Ibrahim saani. Ka bundaana maa gba daa ti kpi, ka ba sɔ'ai u.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Farisiseeba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, I bu ya yi kpa na n‑gaari n‑kyaŋŋi luginseaa? Dama NaaHɛroodi boori ni u ku i.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Ka ŋɔn mun daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ kpiimma ŋɔn bɛ seela n‑nyari wahala la, ka daa wukki u zugu n‑gɔsi saazugu n‑nya ti yaaba Ibrahim wa'aligu sa, ka Lazarusi bɛ u saani.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑ti ba, ni, Yɔbaa n kur nyɛ u! Ya kyɛma nti yɛli u, ni, zuna ni beoo n na kur nin katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni, ka tibri baaridima, ka daba ata daari n nin paai n tuma zaa tɔriga.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Ka bundaana maa daa tasi n‑yɛli, Ti Yaaba Ibrahim, zɔm ma namboo, ka tum Lazarusi ka u zaŋŋi u nubila n‑tum koom ni n‑tɔkki n‑niŋi n zilimni wa ni na, ka ka maai; ka n diri wahala pam bugum wa puuni.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Amaa di kpa ma la talasi ni n siri yi soori zugu zuna ni beoo ni daari, dama di ku toaai n‑niŋi ni ba ku anabisɔ kam zaa tinseelli ni, ka di pa la Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Ka Ibrahim yɛli u, M biiya, teaam nyin daa bɛ i nyɔvuri sahanseaa la yɛla, ni, i daa nyari i sufu yuubu, sahanseaa ka Lazarusi mun daa nyari wahala. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ŋɔn sufu maaya, ka nyin mun gba yi bɛ wahala ni.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Ka u daa gbaai kunkuma Gyɛrusalɛm zugu, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm, Gyɛrusalɛm! Waay, waay, waay! Nyin ŋɔn kuuri anabidima ka zaŋŋni ban daa tum tuumudimseeba i saani maa n‑lɔbri ba kuga wa! N da kur boori la n la'asi i niriba lugri yinni ni, kaman nɔnyaaŋŋa ŋɔn la'asi u biisi ka yirigri u kpunkpama n‑ligri ba seem la; amaa ya bu sakki n‑ti ma.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Din paasi dinŋɔ zugu, bɔ'atitaari n dɔya tirim ni yarima sunsukkuni, ka sɔ ku toaai n‑yi ti saani n‑yakki ka m‑paai ya saani sa, ka sɔ mun gba ku toaai n‑yi bɛni n‑kyɛ ti saani na.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Ya gɔsima! Ya Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa yi kpalim la daboo. Dinzugu n yɛtti ya ni, ni, ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa halli ka ya ti yɛli Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na! .

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Di saha ka bundaana maa daa yɛli, M Ba! N susiri i ni, ni, i tum Lazarusi ni u kyaŋŋi m ba yiri ni.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: dama m mabiisi anu na bɛ ni, ka u kyaŋŋi nti sɛ'asi ba ni ba di kyɛ na n‑kpɛ man ŋɔn bɛ wahala seaa wa ni.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Ka Ibrahim daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Musa sɔppu, ni anabidima dinni, bɛ la ba saani, dizugu ba wumma ba yɛligu ka sɛ'asi ba mammansi.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Ka bundaana maa laa n‑yɛli, N Yaaba Ibrahim, pa lala. Amaa sɔ mun yi da neaai kum ni n‑kyaŋŋi ba saani nti yɛli ba, ba da naa n‑takki ba beesigu n‑niŋi tuuba.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ka u yɛli u, Ba yi ku niŋi Musa ni anabidima yɛligu yɛdda, halli sɔ yi neaai kum ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti yɛli ba, ba ku niŋi u gba yɛligu maa yɛdda. .

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Di ku toaai n‑niŋi ka yɛttɔ'abeeri bu paari ya na, yɛttɔ'aseesi an lusiri niriba n‑kpeesiri tumbeeri ni. Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn taasi a na muna - waay, waay, waay!.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Ba yi da zaŋŋi neeri n‑lɔ di daama nangoori ni ka zaŋŋi u n‑lɔsi mɔ'azuliŋŋu ni, di da sɔ u zugu n‑gaari u kyɛ ka bibisi wa yinni lu n‑kpɛ tumbeeri ni.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Ni, Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa! I mabiiya yi tum i taalli, nyin sɛ'asim u; ka u yi deaai u galinsi maa, nyin kyɛri u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Halli u yi ti tum i taalli napɔmbuyɔpɔi dahinyinni ka ŋmaligi buyɔpɔi n‑yɛli i, ni, N galimiya. nyin kyɛri u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Tɔ, ka tuumudima maa daa yɛli ba dugma, ni, Paasim ti yɛdda.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Ya yi mari yɛdda ka di sa'ai hoo zinzam beelli, ya nin toaai n‑yɛli kinkaŋŋa wa, ni, ka voaama ka zaazii wa ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti sa mɔ'ari puuni, ka di nin niŋi lala.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, Ya puuni ŋɔni n lee m‑mari u daabri ka u koora, bee, ka u guri la bunkɔbri, ka u ti yi moo na ka u di yɛli u, Kyɛm na nti zinni n‑di bundirigu.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Kala, i ku yɛli u, ni, u kyɛm na n‑dugi bundirigu na nti zaali ka i di ka nyu pɔi, ka u mun naayi kyaŋŋi nti maali u siri n‑zinni n‑di ka nyu?.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Ka daabri maa ŋɔn tum lala maa, i nin puusi u dizugu? Ay! ban zaali u tunseesi maa nla.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: Dizugu lala n nyɛ di#:- ya gba yi niŋi ban zaali ya ni ya niŋi seem maa zaa, ya yɛlima, ni, Ti bu sumni pɛ'abu, dama ti tum la ti tuma ŋɔn nyɛ seelli kɔkɔa. .

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi soori zugu n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm, u daa dɔli Samɛrii ni Galilii tingbana sunsukkuni n‑gatta.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: N‑kpeeri tinkpanseaa ni, ka kɔnsi piiya daa ti kyirigi u.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: Ba daa zɛ la wa'aligu sa ka tasi pampam n‑yɛli u, Ti Ba Yisa, zɔm ti namboo.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ba lala maa, u daa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛma nti zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑wulisi maalimaanniba. Ban daa ŋmaligi n‑kyɛnni maa, ba daa nya alaafɛ zaa.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Ka ba ninvugyinni ŋɔn daa ti nya ka u kpaŋŋi maa n‑naai.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: u daa ŋmaligi n‑labi Yisa saani na, ka kur kaasiri pampam m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: halli nti sɔmni Yisa tooni m‑puusiri u. Doo maa mun daa nyɛ la Samɛrii nira. Di saha ka Yisa boosi u, ni, Pa ya niriba piiya n da kpaŋŋi maa?.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Ka niriba awai la kpalim m‑bɛ la yani? Bee sɔsɔ bu labi na ni u ti pɛ'ai Naawunni na'ala tinzuŋŋu nira wa kɔkɔa.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Ka ŋmaligi n‑yɛli u, Doaama! Ka i yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka i kpaŋŋi.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Farisiseeba daa ti boosi Yisa, ni, Bɔ saha ka Naawunni naam maa ti ya m‑piili? Ka u gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, Naawunni naam piiligu pa la niriba ŋɔn gɔsiri n‑nyari bunseelli.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Bee, sɔ di yɛli, Nyama, di bɛ kpa wa!, bee, Di bɛ kpa sa! Dama Naawunni naam pun bɛ la ya saani.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Saha kyɛnni na ka ya nin ti boori ni ya nya man Ninsaala Bii saha wa dabsiri yinni gba, ka ku nya ka.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Niriba nin ti yɛli ya, ni, Nnya kpa sa!, bee, Nnya kpa wa! - yan di kyaŋŋi, ya mun di dɔli ba.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Dama Ninsaala Bii dabsiri maa yi ti paai, u nin ŋmani hoo saa ŋɔn yi nya'asiri la, n‑neaai sangbana gbinni n‑yi ya'ari wa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya'ari wa sa.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Amaa faasee ka u daŋŋi n‑di wahala pam pɔi, ka zuna niriba ti za'asi u yɛla ka zeem u.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Ka din daa kur niŋi seem Nuhu saha la, lala ka di ti ya niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba dabsiri maa daari.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Niriba daa diri ni ka nyuura di dabsiri maa, ka diri pɔ'aba ka zaŋŋni ba bipunsi n‑tiri dɔppa, halli nti paai Nuhu ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ nyarintitaari maa ni dahinseaa maa. Di nyaaŋŋa ka koom daa kyɛ na nti di ba zaa.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: Lɔti saha la gba daa nyɛ la lala. Ba daa kur diri ni ka nyuura, ka daara, ka koosira, ka butti bumbura, ka meeri yiya;.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: halli nti paai Lɔti ŋɔn daa yi Sɔdoom tiŋŋa na dahinseaa maa, ka bugum daa yi saazugu na, ni farinwuta bugum, n‑laŋŋi n‑di ba zaa.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Lala ka di nin ti niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba dabsiri maa yi ti yi paalu.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Dindaari maa ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ti bɛ piiya duu zugu ka u neema kpalim m‑bɛ duu puuni, u di yɛli, ni, u sigi na n‑kpɛ n‑zaŋŋi a. Ka ŋɔn mun bɛ puu ni, ŋɔn di labi u nyaaŋŋa n‑kuli.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Ya teaama din daa niŋi Lɔti pɔ'a seem la.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Ŋɔnkam boori ni u tuligi u nyɔvuri nin ti sa'am ka m‑basi, ka ŋɔnkam sakki ni u nyɔvuri sa'amna, ŋɔn mun nin ti nya nyɔvuri ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: N yɛtti ya wa, ni, di daari maa ka niriba ayi nin ti la'am n‑dɔ bundoaa yinni zugu yuŋŋu, ka Naawunni nin ti kpikki ninvugyinni ka kyɛ ŋɔnla.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Ka pɔ'aba ayi nin ti la'am n‑neemni ba kyi neeri zugu, ka Naawunni kpikki yinni ka kyɛ ŋɔnla.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa boosi u, Ti dugma, yani zii nla? Ka u yɛli ba Bunkpiiŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ luginseaa, bɛni ka zuusi la'amni taaba m‑bɛ ni.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri din wulisiri, ni, di sumni niriba susiri Naawunni saha kam, ka ba di gyɛ ka kyɛ susigu.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: U daa yɛli ba, ni, Sarikattisɔ daa bɛ tinseaa. U daa bu zɔri Naawunni ka mun bu laa n‑kyɛ ka u ba'a bɛ ninsaalisɔ kam yɛlli ni.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Tɔ, ka pukɔsɔ daa bɛ lala tiŋŋa maa ni, ka u kur zɛya n‑kyɛnni doo maa saani na, ni, u tɔ'asim u yɛttɔ'a maa n‑fa u u dindaana nuu ni.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ka sarikatta maa daa yuui beela ka bu sakki ni u fa u m‑basi. Ka di daa niŋi lala ka u daa yɛli u sufu ni, ni, Halli ni m bu zɔri Naawunni ka m ba'a ka ninsaala yɛla ni la zaa yɔri.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: amaa pukoori wa ŋɔn kur mugsiri ma maa zugu, n nin kari u sariya ka fa u m‑basi, ka di yi pa lala u sahakam kyɛnni na wa nin ti ŋmɛ n nyɔya. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Ka ti Dugma daa laa n‑yɛli, Ya wumma sarikatta tumbeeri daana ŋɔn yɛli seem la.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Kala, ya teesiya, ni, Naawunni ku ti wum ninvugseeba ban kumni m‑paari u wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu, ni, u suŋŋma ba maa ka zɔ ba namboo, ka fa ba m‑basi?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N ya yɛli ya, ni, u nin fa ba m‑basi yoomyooma. Ka di zaa yɔri, man Ninsaala Bii yi ti labi duniya wa ni na, n nin nya ban mari yɛdda bɛni, bee? - Ay, m bu ya nya.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Tɔ. Yisa daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri n‑ti ninvugseeba ban gɔsi ba mansi, ni, ninvugsuma, ka mun gɔsiri ba taaba n‑zeemni ba. Ka nyɛsiri daa bɛ ni, ni.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Niriba ayi n daa bɛ ni, n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni ba ti gyeem Naawunni; ba yinni mun daa nyɛ la Farisii, ka ŋɔnla mun daa nyɛ lampodeaara.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Ka Farisii maa ŋɔn daa zɛ saazugu m‑puusi u wumpuusigu n‑ti u maŋŋa, ni, Naawunni, m pɛ'ari i pam man pa la hoo niriba zaa m bɛ seem maa, dama ba zuura ka tumni taalli, ka faari taaba pɔ'aba - halli m pa la hoo lampodeaara wa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Dama n loori noori dabsa ayi bɛkwai kam, ka yiisiri man nyari bunseelli kam zaa zakka.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Amaa ka lampodeaara ŋɔn daa zɛ u kɔkɔ kpaŋŋa sa. Vi ŋɔn daa gbaai u zugu u daa zɔri dabeem ka bu wukki u zugu n‑gɔsi saazugu, ka daa kur mari u nuusi m‑buuri u maŋŋa sufusa'aŋŋu zugu, ka yɛtta, M Ba Naawunni, zɔm man tumbeeri daana wa namboo! .

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ba, N ya yɛli ya ni, ni, di daa nyɛ la lampodeaara maa ŋɔn daa yi n‑kuli ka yi taalli ni, pa ŋɔnla. Dinzugu ŋɔnkam tibgri u maŋŋa nin fiilim, ka ŋɔnkam mun sigsi u maŋŋa nin nya tibsim.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Ba daa mari bileesi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ni u zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑siisi ba, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa nya ban niŋni seem ka daa nyikki ba di zugu.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yisa mun daa booli bibisi na ka yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ya kyɛ ka bibisi maa kyɛ n saani na, yan di ta'ari ba! Dama banŋɔ taabu n su Naawunni naam la yɛla.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli ya wa, sɔ yi bu sakki Naawunni naam yɛligu maa hoo bibila ŋɔn sakkri seem la, u ku toaai n‑kpɛ Naawunni naam maasim maa ni.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Tɔ; ka toondaansɔ daa boosi u, ni, N Karimbasuŋŋu! N ya niŋi la wula n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ka Yisa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, Wula ka i booli ma, ni, bunsuŋŋu? Sɔ n ka ni n‑nyɛ bunsuŋŋu, na'ala Naawunni kɔkɔa.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Tɔ; i mi zaaligu yɛla maa an nyɛ seem: ni, Di tum zina.; ni, Di ku ninsaala.; ni, Di zu.; ni, Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.; ni, Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima. .

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ka doo maa daa yɛli, Anŋɔ zaa ka n daa pun dɔli ka na nyɛ na'akyimbila.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Ka Yisa wum lala ka yɛli u, Yɛlli yinni n kpalim m‑pɔsigi i; see ka i koosi i marigu zaa n‑zaŋŋi n‑tɔri faradima, ka i arizikki maali n‑zoaai Naawunni saani. Ka kyɛ na n‑dɔli ma.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Tɔ, ka doo maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala u sufu daa sa'am pam, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni pam.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi u ka yɛli, Yɛlimaŋni, di nin tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Naawunni naam ni!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ kparipiim voori ni m‑puli n‑yi, ka bundaana ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Ka ban daa wum ŋɔn yɛli seem maa daa boosi u, ni, Ka ŋɔni n nin tuligi.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Yɛttɔ'aseesi an tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani maa pa la an tɔ Naawunni saani.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Di saha ka Piita daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Nyama, tirima kyɛ ti bunseelli kam ka dɔli i maa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ŋɔnkam kyɛ u yiri, bee u pɔ'a, bee u babiisi ni u mabiisi, bee u ba ni u ma - halli u maŋtoori biisi gba - Naawunni naam maa zugu.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: di daama nin ti nya laara n‑gaari anŋɔ zaasa pampaŋŋɔ; ka saha din kyɛnni na u nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑yi kpaŋŋa nti yɛli ba, Ya wumma, ti wa la Gyɛrusalɛm; ka anabidima ŋɔn daa sɔbi seelikam zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii yɛla la nin ti paai a naari.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Ba nin zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni, ni ba maali ansarisi n‑zeem ma.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: ka tusi nantoori n‑niŋi ma, ka naayi feebi ma ka ku ma. Amaa daba ata daari n nin yisigi kum ni.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi ŋɔn daa yɛli ba yɛttɔ'aseesi maa zaasa gbinni; di daa nyɛ la yɛttɔ'asɔ'ara ba saani, ka ba daa bu baŋŋi di gbinni.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑ti paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa, zoonsɔ zi soori gbiriŋŋu m‑maanni bara;.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: ka ti wum daadam ŋɔn daa gatti la damni ka boosi ni u baŋŋi din niŋna.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ka ba yɛli u, ni, di nyɛ la Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, n‑gatti maa.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Amaa u daa n‑tasi pampam n‑yɛli, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ka ban daa bɛ tooni maa daa nyikki u n‑yɛli, ni, u fooma u vuuri. Amaa u daa laa n‑tasi pampam, ni, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Ka Yisa kpalim n‑zaani ka yɛli, ni, ba zaŋŋma u na. Ka ŋɔn daa ti pɔri ba saani na, Yisa boosi u, ni.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Bɔ ka i boora ni n niŋi n‑ti i? Ni, M Bapira, m boori n laa n‑nyari yaasa.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Neaama ka nyari yaasa! I yɛdda maa kyɛ ka i nini neaaya.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Ka u nini maa daa neaai ya'abi ka u gyeemni Naawunni ka dɔli Yisa ka ba kyɛnna. Ka zama maa zaa daa nya din niŋi maa ka daa pɛ'ari Naawunni.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Dindahinseaa yaasa ka Yisa daa ti bɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisira niriba ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu yɛla maa n‑tiri ba. Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni tiŋŋa maa karimbadima ni kpamma maa daa la'am taaba n‑kyɛ u saani na nti boosi u, ni.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum lala tuma maa? Yɛlim ti! Bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, N gba ya boosi ya la boosigu yinni. Ya yɛlim ma.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani bee ninsaalidima saani.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri taaba, ni, Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Amaa ka ti yi sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ninsaalidima saani, niriba maa nin ti lɔbi ti kuga, dama sɔkam sakkri ni Gyɔni daa nyɛ la anabimaŋni.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, ba bu mi.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Dinŋɔna n gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba nyɛsiri, ni, Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑daa sa u tiwanna puu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi ninvugseeba nuu ni, ni ba tumni puu maa tuma n‑ti u, ka u daa gɔ ni n‑yuui beela.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Tɔ, ka sahasuŋŋu maa u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani nti deaai puu maa diibu na n‑ti u. Amaa tuntumniba maa daa gbaai daabri maa m‑bu u ka kari u n‑labsi yɔri.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Ka puu maa daana daa laa n‑tum sɔ, ka ba zaŋŋi ŋɔn gba m‑maali u ansarisi m‑bu u ka kari u n‑labsi yɔri.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑tum sɔ m‑paasi ata, ka ba pa u daŋŋa ka kari u m‑basi.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Ka puu maa daana daa yɛli, N yi ya niŋi la wula? - See ka n tum m mammantoori biiya man wuri sɔ la. Di pa seem ka ba ti ŋɔn gyirima.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya ka u kyɛnni na, ba ŋmɛ taaba saawara n‑yɛli, ni, Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u ka faari maa nin leebi ti dinni.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Ka ba daa gbaai u, n‑zaŋŋi u n‑lɔbi m‑basi puu maa kpaŋŋa sa nti ku u.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna wula ka puu maa daana nin niŋi? U bu ya n‑kyɛ na nti ku tuntumniba wa zaa? Ka zaŋŋi ka n‑ti tuntunseeba? Ka daadam maa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ba daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni tuligma ti ni dinŋɔ taabu.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi ba ka boosi ba, ni, Ka dinŋɔna yɛttɔ'aseaa kan daa sɔbi n‑dooli la gbinni nyɛ la wula? - ni#:- Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: - Ŋɔnkam zaa lu kugri maa zugu nin kabi m‑basi, amaa kugri maa ŋɔn lu sɔ zugu ka nin ŋmɛrigi u zaa.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Di saha ka karimbadima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa baŋŋi, ni, Yisa nyɛsiri galimni la bana, ka dizugu ba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam maa dabeem.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Ka ba daa gɔsiri u yɛla, ni ba pigsi n‑gbaai u, u yɛttɔ'a puuni. Ba daa tum ninvugseeba na ni ba yɔmsiri u, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛdda dima.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Amaa ba daa boori ni ba zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi Gɔmna nuu ni. Ba daa boosi u, ni, Ti Karimba, ti mi, ni, i yɛttɔ'a ni i wulisigu kur dɔli la soori deede; i ba'a ka niriba yɛla ni, ka i bu zɔri ninsaala nini, amaa ka i kur wulisiri la Naawunni soori ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Tɔ, dinŋɔna yɛlim ti: di tuya ni ba yoori lampoo n‑tiri Room NaaTitaari, bee di bu tuusi.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba teesibeeri maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem ka labsi n‑yɛli ba.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ya tim ma la'afu na ka n nya. Tɔ, ka ŋɔni makkri m bɛ la'afu wa ni wa, bee ŋɔni yuuri n sɔbi m‑bɛ ka ni? Ka ba yɛli, NaaTitaari dinni.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ka Yisa laa n‑labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Dizugu ya zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naaba dinni n‑ti Naa maa, ka zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naawunni dinni n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Ka ba daa kɔŋŋi u yɛttɔ'a maa gbaari, ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem zama maa ni maa, ka u labsigu maa daa gyɛri ba pam ka ba daa bu laa n‑yɛli seelli.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Tɔ; ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Ba daa yɛli u, ni, Ti Karimba, anabi Musa daa sɔbi n‑ti ti n‑wulisi, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka kyɛ u pɔ'a ka u na bu dɔ'ai bii n‑ti u, see ka u di u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Tɔ; mabiisi ayɔpɔi n daa bɛ ni, ka ba kpeema daa di pɔ'a ka kpi ka ka biiya.

eng: The second.
local: Ka ŋɔn pa buyi, ni buta daana gba, daa di u.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: halli ka babayɔpɔi maa zaa daa di u ka kpi ka bu dɔ'ai biiya.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka pɔ'a maa gba kpi.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna, Zaadaari, dɔppa wa zaa yi ti yisigi kum ni, ba ŋɔni n nin nyɛ pɔ'a maa sira? - dama babayɔpɔi zaa daa di u.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Zuna niriba wa diri la pɔ'aba ka kunni dɔppa;.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: amaa ninvugseeba ban sumni ni ba ti neaai kum ni m‑paai nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga ziiya maa muna - ban ku laa n‑diri pɔ'aba bee n‑kunni dɔppa.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: dama ba bu ti ya n‑kpi yaasa: ay! - ba nin bɛ ni la hoo malaakadima ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ba ti ya n‑leebi la Naawunni biisi, ban nyɛ kum ni neaari biisi la zugu.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri yɛla, halli Musa daa sɔbi n‑yɛli - suura seaa kan tɔ'asiri tutuuri la yɛla la ni - ni, Naawunni daa yɛliya, Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: U pa la ban kpi Naawunni, amaa ban vu Naawunni, dama sɔkam zaa nyɛ la ninvugvuri u saani.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Ka karimbaseeba daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, Ti Karimba, i yɛli suŋŋa.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Ka dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa sɔ daa bu laa n‑nya sufukpeemni ni u boosi u boosa yaasa.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Masiiya maa muna - sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum na ni u ti di naam ka fa u niriba m‑basi - wula zugu ka ba yɛtti, ni, lala Masiiya maa nyɛ la NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: NaaDauda mun daa tɔ'asi Masiiya maa yɛla n‑sɔbi Yuuma bukku maa ni, ni#:- Ti Dugma daa yɛli n Dugma maa, ni, Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: Halli ka n nin zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni. .

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Tɔ; ka NaaDauda yi boonni ŋɔn Masiiya maa, ni, u Dugma, ka wula ka u nin toaai n‑nyɛ u ya'aŋŋa.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn na kur wumni u wulisigu maa, Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Ya baŋŋma seeba ban wulisiri zaaligu maa yɛla! Ba boori ba yeeri kparikara n‑gyinna, ka sɔkam zaa puusiri ba daasi ni. La'aŋŋu duri puuni ka ba boori la ba bɛ tooni.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba faari la pukɔya yiya pɔi, ka naayi wulisiri ba maŋŋa m‑puusiri Naawunni n‑yuuri puusigu maa ni. Lala niriba maa nin ti nya galiŋŋu pam.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yisa daa ti paai Gyɛrikoo n‑kpɛ timpuuni n‑gatta.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u yuuri boonni Zakyeoosi. Bundaana n daa nyɛ u, ka u daa su tiŋŋa maa lampodeaari tuma fukumsi.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: U daa boori la u baŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa, amaa niriba maa ŋɔn daa zoaai pam maa zugu u daa kɔŋŋi la u nyaabu, dama u daa nyɛ la ninvuggyiŋŋa.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Lala zugu u daa zɔ n‑gaari ba tooni nti du kinkaŋŋa ni u ti nya u, dama Yisa daa ya dɔli la bɛni soori maa n‑gaari.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti paai tii maa gbinni na, u kpalim n‑zaani ka gɔsi tii maa saazugu ka yɛli, Zakyeoosi, sigma na yoomyooma, dama di kpa ma talasi ni n gbɛ i yiri ni zuna.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ka Zakyeoosi daa sigi na yoomyooma n‑gyaari Yisa ni sufupeelli.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Tɔ, ka niriba maa zaa daa nya Yisa ŋɔn kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ Zakyeoosi yiri maa, ka nyurimni ba kparima ni, n‑yɛtta, U kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ tumbeeri daana wa yiri.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Amaa ka Zakyeoosi daa zɛ ti Dugma tooni n‑yɛli u, ni, Nyama, pampaŋŋɔ n nin zaŋŋi n neema purigri n‑ti faradima, ka n yi daa fa sɔ buna yɔri, n nin laa n‑labsi di daama buna zii bunaasi zugu n‑ti u.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Zuna dabsiri wa tuliginsim paai i yiri wa ni na, dama nyin gba nyɛ la Ibrahim biiya.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Dama man Ninsaala Bii daa kyɛ na ni m bɔ n‑nya ban bɔrigi m‑basi la ka tuligi ba.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa wum lala maa, Yisa daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri wa m‑paasi ba. Ba daa pɔtti la Gyɛrusalɛm, ka niriba maa daa teesiri, ni, u ya kyɛ ka Naawunni naam maa piili la yooma.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Ka dizugu u daa yɛli ba, ni, Nabisɔ n daa bɛ tinseaa puuni, ka u daa ya doaai n‑kyaŋŋi tinwa'aligu sa n‑di naam ka labi na.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Ka dizugu u daa booli u daaba piiya na n‑zaŋŋi ligri n‑tɔri n‑ti ba sɔkam u tɔrli, ka yɛli ba, Ya zaŋŋma ligri wa n‑walimni n‑ti ma, halli ni n ti yi n goorim maa n‑labi na.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Tɔ, ka u daa kyaŋŋi u kyɛnni; amaa u tiŋŋa maa niriba daa bu boori u, ka ba daa tum niriba nti yɛli, ni, ba di diisi nira wa naam maa. Di zaa yɔri, u daa di naam maa.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Ŋɔn daa di naam maa n‑naai la, u daa labi n‑kuli na, ka daa tum m‑booli ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi ligri n‑ti daabseeba maa na, nti boosi ba ni u baŋŋi ban daa zaŋŋi ligri maa n‑tum n‑nya nyoori seem.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni maa daa kpɛ na nti yɛli, N daana i ligri la nɔbi ni m‑paasi a taaba napɔmpiiya.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Ka u yɛli u, Mboo! Daabsuŋŋu n nyɛ i! Nyin su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi n‑zɛ yimmu maa, n nin zaŋŋi tinsi piiya fukumsi n‑niŋi i nuu ni.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn pa u zugu la kpɛ na nti yɛli u dugma maa, ni, N daana, i ligri la nɔbi ni m‑paasi napɔmbunu.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Ka u yɛli ŋɔn mun gba, ni, I nin ti su tinsi anu fukumsi.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Tɔ, ka sɔ kyɛ na yaasa, ni, N daana, nnya i ligri la nŋɔa. N daa zaŋŋi bɔbga m‑pɔbi a n‑dooli, dabeem ŋɔn daa mari ma la zugu.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: Dama halibeoo daana n nyɛ i; i boori ni i nya bunseelli kam zaa yɔri, ka kyeeri nyin daa bu buri seelli.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Ka u yɛli u, Nyin bibeoo wa! I mammaŋŋa noori ni yɛttɔ'a ka n zaŋŋi n‑lusi i! Ni, i daa mi, ni, n nyɛ la halibeoo daana, fa! - m‑boori buna yɔri, ka kyeeri man bu buri seelli!.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Tɔ! Ka bɔ n daa lee n‑niŋi i ka i daa bu zaŋŋi n ligri maa n‑dooli bankyi ni? - ka man da kuli na wa, n naa n‑kur nya nyoori m‑paasi n la'adaya maa zugu.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: - Ka daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ban zɛ bɛni maa, ni, Ya deaam ligri maa u saani n‑zaŋŋi m‑paasi sɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni na maa.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Ka ba yɛli u, Ti daana, u ligri napɔmpiiya pun bɛ la u saani.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, ni, N yɛtti ya ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari beela ya n‑nya m‑paasi, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa ka ba nin ti ya fa ka kyɛ u yɔri.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Ni, Amaa n dindaandimseeba ban daa bu boori ni n di naam n su ba la, ya zaŋŋma ba zaa na n‑ku ba ka n nini nya. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛsi ba nyɛsiri wa n‑naai, u daa doaai n‑gaari ba tooni n‑kpa Gyɛrusalɛm soori.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti pɔtti tinkpanseesi an boonni Bɛtfeegyi ni Bɛtani la, seesi an zɛ Ɔlivii Zoori la lugri na, u daa tum u nyaandɔliba ayi, ni.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: Ya kyɛm ti tooni nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa kan zɛ ya tooni sa la. Ya yi ti paai bɛni, ya nin nya buŋŋa ka u lɔ n‑zɛya, bumbilisɔ nira ŋɔn na zi m‑ba; ya lɔrigma u m‑mari na.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Sɔ mun yi ya yɛli ya, ni, wula zugu ka ya lɔrigri u? yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori u.

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa tum ninvugseeba maa daa kyaŋŋi nti nya ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ka ban daa lɔrigri buŋŋa maa sahanseaa ka ninvugseeba ban daa su u la daa boosi ba, ni, Wula zugu ka ya lɔrigri u ni.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ti Dugma boori u ni.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Ka ba daa mari u m‑paai Yisa saani na, nti yeaai ba kyinkyina m‑pa buŋŋa nyaaŋŋa maa, ka daa kyɛ ka Yisa ba u.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ba u n‑kyɛnna, niriba maa daa na zaŋŋi la ba kyinkyina n‑yɛligri soori maa zugu.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai luginseelli soori maa ŋɔn daa zuligri n‑sigri Ɔlivii Zoori la, u nyaandɔliba maa zaa daa piili n‑tasira pampam m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Sufupeelli pɛ'abu ka ba daa moonna, ban daa nya alahazibu tunkariseesi ŋɔn tum la zugu.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Ka yɛtta#:- Zugsuŋŋu m bɛ n‑ti nasɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na! Sufudoaa m bɛ arizana ni! Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ saazugu mari gyirima pam.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Tɔ; ka Farisiseeba ban daa bɛ zama maa ni daa yɛli Yisa, ni.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Karimba! Kpaasim i nyaandɔliba maa zugu! Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, N yɛtti ya ni, ba yi pun sini vuuri, halli kuga wa gba naa n‑tasi n‑yɛli.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti pɔtti Gyɛrusalɛm n‑gɔsi n‑nya tiŋŋa maa, u daa kum ka zugu, ni.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: Yarima Gyɛrusalɛm dima, zuna ya yi da kur baŋŋi din nin ti ya sufudoaa, di naa n‑sɔa amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya bɛ la zibsim ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi di zii ni.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Ni, Dama saha kyɛnni na ka ya dindaandima nin ti gbi tanni n‑dooli n‑gyiligi ya tiŋŋa maa zaa, ka ta'ai ka sɔya zaa, ka di mugsi ya pam.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Ba nin ti daai ya gooma n‑lusi yarima ni ya biisi zugu, halli ni kugri yinni gba ku laa n‑kpalim m‑pa ka kpeaa zugu, dama ya daa bu baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn kyɛ na nti fa ya m‑basi sahanseaa maa.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Ka Yisa daa paai Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni nti kpɛ bɛni m‑piili n‑katti ban daa koosiri neema Yiri maa puuni n‑yiisiri ba.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: Ka yɛli ba, ni, Di sɔbi n‑zaali, ni, N Yiri wa nyɛ la Wunsusigu Yiri. amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑labgi la ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela. .

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Beoo kam zaa ka Yisa daa kpeeri Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisiri niriba, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni karimbadima maa daa boori sɔseaa ba nin nya n‑ku u, ban ni tiŋŋa maa ni kpamma maa.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Amaa ba daa bu mi ban ya niŋi seem, dama sɔkam zaa ba'a daa kur bɛ la u wulisigu maa ni.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa ti gɔsi n‑nya bundaandima ŋɔn daa bɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri n‑zaŋŋni ligri pam n‑niŋni aduuwa seem.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Ka nya pukɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ faradaana gba ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u ligri fiiŋ, kulla la'abisibisi ayi, n‑niŋi aduuwa.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, pukoori faradaana wa tisi n‑gaari ba zaa.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: dama ba sɔkam yiisi la u marigu pam puuni n‑tisi, amaa pukoori wa ŋɔna - u zaŋŋi la ŋɔn mari seelli zaasa n‑tisi ka kpalim yɔri.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Ka ninvugseeba daa tɔ'asiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa meebu ŋɔn veela seem, ni, kugveela m bɛ ni, ni niriba ŋɔn tisi bunsuma zaa m‑paasi.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, Yan gɔsiri bunseesi wa zaa maa muna - dahinseaa kyɛnni na ka a zaa nin ti wurim m‑basi halli ka kugseaa ku kpalim n‑tɔm ka kpeaa zugu.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Ti Karimba, wula saha ka dinŋɔ nin niŋi? Bee bumbɔ nin ti niŋi n‑wulisi, ni, anŋɔ zaa ya m‑paai ti na.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, Ya baŋŋma suŋŋa, ka di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya. Niriba pam nin zaŋŋi n yuuri n‑kyɛ na, ni, Mani n nyɛ u! bee Saha maa paaya! Yan di dɔli ba nyaaŋŋa!.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Ya yi ti wumni tɔppu damni, bee zaba yɛla, yan di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, dama see ka anŋɔ taabu daŋŋi pɔi, amaa dinŋɔ na bu wulisi, ni, naari paaya.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Tinsi dima nin yisigi n‑tuusi taaba tɔppu, ka nadima gba nin tuusi taaba.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Tingbaŋŋu maa nin damni kpa, ka ka nin damni kpa; ka kɔm nin ti lu, ni dooru m‑paasi; ka ba nin nya bunnyaara saazugu sangbana ni, ka a nin ti kpeesiri dabeem pampam.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Amaa ba nin daŋŋi n‑kari ya nti gbaai ya n‑taasi ba la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka kpari ya duu. Ba nin zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi nadima nuusi ni, ni gɔmnadima nuusi ni: man yuuri zugu ka ba nin niŋi ya lala.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ka anŋɔ nin ti kyɛ ka ya wulisiri ba yan mi n yɛliseesi.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Ka yɛli ba, ni, Ya kpaŋŋma ya sufu n‑zɛ yimmu, ba ti boosi ya boosa sahanseaa la; yan di kyɛ ka di mugsi ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin ti labsi ba seem.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Dama mani nin ti ti ya yɛm, ni yɛttɔ'aseesi yan nin labsi ba; ka ba ku toaai n‑za'asi bee n‑tɔ'asi n‑lusi ya.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Ya badima ni ya madima nin ti zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi ya dindaandima nuu ni, ka ya mabiisi gba nin ti niŋi lala, ni ya dɔ'ariba ni ya zɔdima. Ba nin ti ku yaseeba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Ba zaa nin ti yaai ya, man yuuri zugu:.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: amaa halli ya zɔbgu yinni gba ku ti sa'am m‑basi.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Ka yan kpaŋŋi ya mansi m‑bari n‑zaani maa zugu ya nin nya nyɔvuri.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya yi ti nya ka tɔbbiisi ziintiri n gyiligri Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, yan baŋŋma, ni, tiŋŋa maa sa'aŋŋu saha paaya.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Di saha maa ya kyɛ ka ban bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu la ni maa ti zɔ n‑du zɔya ni; ka ban ti bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, ban zɔma n‑yi ka ni, halli tinkpansi dima mun di kpɛ ka puuni na.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Dama tubdarigri saha n nyɛ di, hoo ban daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, sahaseaa kan gaari la.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Di saha maa nin ti kpeem pam n‑tisi pɔ'apuusi dima ni pɔ'aseeba ban mari biisi ka ba na mɔ'ara. Dama namsigu pam nin niŋi duniya wa ni, ka Naawunni sufu nin ti yisigi m‑basi tiŋŋa wa dima zugu.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Ba nin ti zaŋŋi takoobi n‑ku ba, ka gbaasi ban kpalim ka ba leebi daaba zuliya kam dima nuusi ni. Ka ban pa Izraɛli dima nin ti deaai Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa n‑noori n‑gyinna halli ti paai ba yuuma tɔriga - yuunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn pun zaali ni ba su ka maa.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ba nin ti nya alahazibu bunnyaara wuntaŋŋa ziiya, ni gɔri puuni, ni saŋmariwuusi ni. Wahala nin ti bɛ tinsi zaa puuni ka sɔkam sufu nin ti tulim teeku zugu, ni ka kɔgbana la vuuri pam.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Ka dabeem nin ti kpɛ niriba pam halli ka ba ligi dizugu, dama arizana yiiko daandima zaa nin ti damna ka niriba zɔri bunseesi an paari duniya wa na.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Di saha ka ba nin ti nya Ninsaala Bii maa ka u pɔtti na, ka sangbana nin gyiligi u, ka u nin ti mari yiiko ni gyirima veelim pam.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Anŋɔ zaasa yi ti piili n‑niŋna, di saha ya wukkma ya zugri n‑gɔsi saazugu, dama ya tuliginsim saha yi paari ya na.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Ka Yisa daa nyɛsi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri n‑ti ba, ni, Ammi, ya bu baŋŋi kinkaŋŋa ni tiisi zaa yɛla?.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Yan yi nya ka a pusiri vamaasa, ya yi baŋŋi, ni, siga saha pɔtta.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Ka di bɛ la lala: ya yi ti nya ka yɛla wa zaa niŋna, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni naam saha maa bu ya n‑yuui.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli ya wa, ni, zuna niriba zaa ku gaari see ka anŋɔ zaa niŋi.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu nin gaari, amaa man yɛttɔ'a ku gaari.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli Amaa yan di kyɛ ka ya kɔribeeri ni ya danyuubu ni duniya wa ni yɛla ti diisi ya n‑dooli tiŋŋa, halli ka di daari maa ti gbaai ya.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: hoo kyɛ'ariga: dama di nin ti paai ŋɔnkam bɛ duniya wa ni zaasa.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Dizugu yan di gbisiri sahakam, ka ya susiri Naawunni; di nin kyɛ ka ya nya kpeeŋŋu n‑tuligi n‑yi din ti ya niŋi maa zaa ni, ka naayi zaani Ninsaala Bii maa tooni.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa kur bɛ la Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri n‑wulisiri niriba beoo kam, amaa yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti paai u daa du Ɔlivii Zoori n‑gbɛ bɛni.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ka niriba zaa daa daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Ka niriba maa zaa daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi Yisa n‑taasi Gɔmna Pailiti saani;.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: ka daa gbaai u figsigu ka samni u, ka yɛtta, Ti nya ka doo wa boori la u lusi ti butiri wa yɛla zaa ka wulisiri ti niriba din bu tuusi; u ku sakki ni ti yɔ lampoo n‑ti Room NaaTitaari, ka laa n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya - Naaba nla.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa boosi Yisa, ni, Nyini nyɛ Izraɛli dima Naaba la? Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Nyin yɛli seem maa n nyɛ di.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa yɛli gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni zama maa, ni, M bu nya doo wa taalli.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Amaa ka toondaandima nini kur moaai ka ba maali n‑yɛtta. U wulisiri niriba Izraɛli dima tinsi zaa, n‑daa piili la Galilii halli nti paai kpa na, ka daamni niriba sufu pam.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna Pailiti ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, u daa boosi, ni, asee Yisa nyɛ la Galilii nira, fa? NaaHɛroodi mun daa bɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm lala saha maa, ka dizugu Pailiti ŋɔn daa baŋŋi, ni, Yisa yi la Naaba suulinsi tiŋŋa na maa, u daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi u n‑taasi NaaHɛroodi saani.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Ka Naaba maa ŋɔn daa ti nya Yisa, di daa niŋi u nya'asim pam, dama u daa pun wum u yɛla ka di yuui, ka u daa boori ni u nya u. U daa laa n‑teesiri, ni, di pa seem u nin tum alahazibu tunseaa ka u nya.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Lala zugu ka u daa boosi Yisa boosa pam, amaa Yisa daa bu gyɛrigi u seela.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima daa kur zɛ bɛni n‑samni u ni nimmoaa.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi ni u kambɔnsi daa fiilim Yisa ka maanni u ansarisi; ka daa zaŋŋi kpariveelli n‑yeeli u ka naayi kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi u n‑labsi Gɔmna Pailiti saani.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Naaba ni Gɔmna Pailiti daa pun mari la nangbankpeemni ni taaba, amaa di daari maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ba daa la'ai taaba zoori.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Tɔ. Gɔmna Pailiti daa booli gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpamma, halli ni daadam zaa, ni, ba la'amma na, nti yɛli ba.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: Yarima n zaŋŋi doo wa n‑taasi n saani na, ni, u burigri niriba zaa. Tɔ: ka n viisi u yɛla ya zaa tooni, ka bu nya u taaliseesi yan samni u n‑yɛtti seem maa zaa.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Halli NaaHɛroodi gba da bu nya u taalli, dama u kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi u n‑labsi ti saani na. Ya nyama, u bu tum tumbɛseaa din sumsi ni kum.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Dinzugu n nin kyɛ ka ba feebi u, ka kyɛ u m‑basi.

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Ka ba zaa daa duusi ba kukɔya maa n‑yɛli pampam, ni, Zaŋŋma ŋɔnŋɔ n‑ku u! Yiisim Barabasi n‑ti ti na.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: - Ŋɔn Barabasi mun daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn taasi mansuulinsi zaba tiŋŋa maa ni ka laa n‑ku niriba, dizugu ka ba daa pun gbaai u n‑kpeesi dansarika duu.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Gɔmna Pailiti daa boori la ni u basi Yisa, ka u daa laa n‑labi n‑tɔ'asiri ba di yɛlli maa;.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: amaa ka ba daa kur moaai nini n‑vuuri n‑yɛtta, Kpaasim u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu! Kpaasim u.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Ka u daa maali m‑boosi ba m‑paasi buta, ni, Bɔzugu? Tumbɛbɔ ka u lee n‑tum? M bu nya ka u tum tumbɛseelli din sum ni kum. Dinzugu n nin feebi u ka basi u ka u kyaŋŋi.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Ba daa kur tasiri pampam ni nimmoaa, ni, u kpaasim u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu. Ka ba kukɔri daa nyaŋŋi Gɔmna Pailiti ka u daa wum n‑ti ba.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: U daa basi noori n‑ti niriba maa ni ba yuubu maa nin zaani.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Ka daa yiisi zama maa ŋɔn daa boori dɔsɔ m‑basi n‑ti ba - dɔsɔ ban daa kpeesi dansarika duu ni daabriiya ni ninvugkuubu zugu. Ka daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑niŋi u ban daa boori seem.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Tɔ. Ka ban daa ti zaŋŋi Yisa n‑kyɛnni maa, ba daa tuusi Sairiini tingbaŋŋu dɔsɔ soori ni, ka u yuuri boonni Saimoon. Ŋɔna n daa kpeeri tiŋŋa maa ni na, ka ba daa gbaai u n‑zaŋŋi Yisa dɔpulli daari maa n‑ziili u, ni u ziiri a n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Ka daadam pam daa dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa, ka pɔ'aseeba daa bɛ bɛni n‑kumni ka fabinni u zugu.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Amaa ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ba, Yan Gyɛrusalɛm pɔ'aba wa, yan di kumni man zugu, amaa ya kumma ya mansi ni ya biisi zugu.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Ya nyama! - dabsa yi kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yɛli, ni, pɔ'aseeba ban na zi n‑dɔ'ai biisi la mari la zugsuŋŋu, ban ni pɔ'a kundɔ'ariba ni pɔ'aseeba ban ka bileesi.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Di saha maa: Ba nin ti yɛtti n‑ti zɔya, Ni, a mulimma ba na! Ka mun yɛli kunkuna, Ni, a sɔ'am ba! .

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Ka daa nyɛsi ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri, ni, Yɛla anŋɔ taabu zaa yi ti niŋi daari an maasa saha la ni, a yi ti kuui, di ya niŋi la wula.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Ka ba daa mari niriba ayi ban nyɛ tumbɛtumniba ni ba ti ku ba, m‑paasi Yisa zugu.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti paai luginseaa kan yuuri boonni Zuguŋmaaŋŋu Ziiya la ka ba daa kpaasi Yisa n‑tabli dɔpulli maa zugu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa. Ka ba daa kpaasi tumbɛtumniba ayi maa gba n‑tabli dɔpula, n‑zaŋŋi yinni n‑sa Yisa nudirigu ya'ari, ka zaŋŋi ŋɔnla muna n‑sa u nuzaa ya'ari.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, M Ba Naawunni, ba bu mi ban niŋni seem maa gbinni, dizugu kyɛri ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba! Ka ba daa puri Yisa sutura n‑tɔri taaba, n‑toori tɛtɛ ni ba ti nya sɔkam ŋɔn ya n‑deaai a yinni yinni.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Ka daadam daa zɛ kpaŋŋa ka gɔsiri u. Amaa ka tiŋŋa maa ni kpamma daa maanni u ansarisi, n‑yɛtta, Ni, u faari la niriba m‑basira; tɔ, u faam u maŋŋa gba m‑basi - u yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn gamsi sɔ n‑diisi Masiiya maa.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Ka soogyadima maa gba daa maanni u ansarisi, ka zaŋŋi damiisiri n‑ti u na ni u nyu;.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: ka yɛtti, ni, I yi siri nyɛ la Izraɛli dima Naaba, nyin faam i maŋŋa m‑basi.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Dama ba daa pun sɔbi bunni n‑tabli u dɔpulli maa saazugu, ni, Yɛhudiyadima Naaba la nŋɔa .

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Ka ban daa kpaasi tumbɛtumniba ayi maa yinni daa tuuri Yisa n‑yɛtti, Pa Masiiya n nyɛ i? Faam i maŋŋa m‑basi, ka fa ti gba m‑paasi.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Amaa ka u taraana daa nyikki u, ka boosi u, ni, I bu zɔri Naawunni? Kala, i bu nya ka ba kuuri tin ni ŋɔna zaa?.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Man ni nyini mun sumsi lala, ti daa tum tumbeeri ka ba namsiri ti dizugu, amaa doo wa bu tum taaliseaa zaa.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Ka ŋmaligi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, I yi ti paai i naam ziiya, teaam n yɛla.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, zuna dabsiri wa i nin ti bɛ n saani zisuŋŋu ziiya.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Tɔ. Agbaa saha daa pun paai, ka tingbaŋŋu maa zaa daa niŋi zimm, ka zibsim daa ligi zaa n‑yuui m‑paai zaanoori hoo kurigu ata saha.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: ka wuntaŋŋa daa bu neaai. Ka pateesa kan daa ya'ai Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Dutitaari puuni maa daa toaai m‑purigi buyi.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Di saha maa Yisa daa kaasi pampam n‑yɛli, M Ba Naawunni, n zaŋŋi n siiya n‑niŋi i nuu ni! Ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa n‑naai, u daa kpiya.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Ka soogyadima toondaansɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ bɛni maa daa nya din daa niŋi seem maa, ka pɛ'ai Naawunni n‑yɛli, Yɛlimaŋni doo wa siri nyɛ la ŋɔn ka taalli.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑zɛ bɛni ni ba gɔsi din ya n‑niŋi seem: ba zaa daa nya din daa baasi seem maa, ka daa labi n‑kuli ni sufusa'aŋŋu pam.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
