local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa mi seeba daa zɛ kpaŋŋa sa, ban ni pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli u n‑yi Galilii na. Ba gba daa nya din daa niŋi maa zaa.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Tɔ: dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi. U daa yi la Izraɛli dima tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Arimatiiya ni na. U daa guri Naawunni naam kyɛnni na. Tinkpamma maa yinni n daa nyɛ u, amaa u daa nyɛ la dɔsuŋŋu, ni yɛlimaŋni daana, ka daa bu la'am ni u taaba ban daa lɔ ba nin niŋi Yisa seem sahanseaa la.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Ŋɔna n daa kyaŋŋi Gɔmna Pailiti saani nti susi u soori ni u kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu nti sɔ'ai.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Ka Gyoosɛfi daa sigsi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa na n‑vili kasiŋŋa ka zaŋŋi u n‑dooli gballi ni - gbaliseaa ban daa pun kyiibi ka tampii ni, ka ba na zi n‑sɔ'ai sɔ bɛni.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Di daari maa daa nyɛ la Kyuu maa siri maaligu daari, Vuusim Dabsiri siri maaligu daari, ka Vuusim Dabsiri maa ya kpɛa.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Ka pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa n‑yi Galilii na maa daa dɔli n‑kyaŋŋi nti nya m‑baŋŋi tampifeoo maa, ni ba n ya n‑sɔ'ai u seem la;.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: ka daa labi n‑kuli nti bɔ turaali ni kpanyuuma n‑zaali. Ba daa vuusi Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa kaman zaaligu ŋɔn daa wulisi seem.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Duniya piiligu ni ka ban boonni sɔ Yɛligu maa daa pun bɛ ni. Yɛligu maa daa bɛ la Naawunni saani, halli ka Naawunni n daa nyɛ u gba.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Tɔ. Bundiripaalli Kyuu saha daa pɔtta, ban boonni Yakki-n‑Gaari la.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Di piiligu saha ka ŋɔn Yɛligu maa daa bɛ la Naawunni saani.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni zaaligu karimbadima maa daa boori sɔseaa ban nin zaŋŋi n‑gbaai Yisa, amaa ba daa zɔri zama maa dabeem.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Ŋɔn saani ka Naawunni daa naam seelikam zaa; halli bunseelli ka ni ka Naawunni daa naam, ka ŋɔn Yɛligu maa daa ka bɛni.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Di saha ka Sintaani daa kpɛ Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ sufu ni. Nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi maa ni yinni n daa nyɛ u, ka u yuuri boonni Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Yɛligu maa ni ka nyɔvuri daa yi na, ka lala nyɔvuri daa taasi neesim na nti neaai n‑ti ninsaala.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa ni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi toondaandima saani nti tɔ'asi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑figsi Yisa n‑niŋi ba nuusi ni.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Neesim maa muna - di neaari la zibsim ni, ka zibsim maa mun na zi n‑nyaŋŋi di, bee, m‑baŋŋi di gbinni gba.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Ba sufu daa maaya ŋɔn daa yɛli ba lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu, ka ba daa sakki ni ba ya n‑ti u la ligri dizugu.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka Naawunni daa tum u na; ŋɔn yuuri n daa boonni Gyɔni la.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Ka Gyuudasi daa sakki lala, ka daa piili m‑boori sɔseaa ŋɔn nin dɔli n‑zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti ba, ka di bɛ luginseaa ka zama maa ŋɔn ka ni.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: U daa kyɛ na ni u ti moonni neesim maa yɛla n‑tiri niriba, ka ba zaa ti wum, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda u mooligu zugu.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Kyuu maa dabsiri daa paai na, peesi kɔribu dabsiri maa.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Pa neesim maa maŋŋa n daa nyɛ u, amaa ka u daa kyɛ na ni u ti mooli neesim maa yɛla.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: ka Yisa daa tum Piita ni Gyɔni n‑yɛli ba, Ya kyam nti sɛ'asi Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu diibu yɛla n‑ti ti ka ti di.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Neesimaŋni n‑daa la; din neaari n‑niŋni ninsaalidima zaa. Lala saha maa ka neesim maa daa kyɛnni duniya wa na.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, Yɛni ka i boori ni ti kpɛ n‑sɛ'asi.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: U daa bɛ la duniya wa puuni. Amaa halli ni u saani ka Naawunni daa naam duniya maa gba, di zaa yɔri duniya dima daa bu baŋŋi u.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Nyama ya yi kur ti kpeeri timpuuni, doo nin paai ya na - dɔsɔ ŋɔn ziiri koom la: ya dɔla u nyaaŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ ŋɔn kpeeri yiri seaa ni.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: U daa kyɛ u mammantoori tingbaŋŋu na, amaa u mammantoori niriba maa daa bu deaai u.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: ka yɛli yiri maa daana, ni, ya Karimba la ya m‑boosi u m‑baŋŋi dukani ka ŋɔn ni u nyaaŋŋa ya n‑kpɛ n‑di ba kyuu bundirigu maa?.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Amaa ba zaa daa bu za'asi u, ka baseeba ban daa sakki n‑deaai u, ka niŋi u yuuri yɛdda maa, ban ka u daa tisi soori ni ba ti leebi Naawunni biisi.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Ka u nin wulisi ya dutitaari gyirambisa zugu ban pun sɛ'asi seaa n‑zaali. Ya sɛ'asim n‑ti ti bɛni.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Di pa la kaman ninsaala ŋɔn dɔ'ari biisi seem, pa ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi zugu, bee, dɔsɔ yuubu zugu, amaa ba dɔ'am yi la Naawunni mammaŋŋa saani na.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Ka ban daa kyaŋŋi maa, di daa niŋi ba hoo ŋɔn pun yɛli ba seem maa, ka ba daa sɛ'asi kyuu maa siri.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Tɔ: ka Yɛligu maa daa ti labgi la ninsaala. U daa bɛ ti saani, ka veelim ni yɛlimaŋni daa bɛ u ni n‑gaari sɔkam. Tirima n daa nya u gyirima ŋɔn bɛ seem maa; u Ba Naawunni ka biiya m‑paasi u zugu, ka bigaŋŋa gyirima n nyɛ u dinni.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Tɔ; ka kyuu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai na, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa la'am n‑zinni.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Gyɔni daa tɔ'asi u yɛla. U daa tasi n‑yɛli, Ŋɔnŋɔ ka n daa tɔ'asi u yɛla, ni, U paari la n nyaaŋŋa amaa u pun gaari n tooni gba. dama u daa pun daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni ka ba naayi dɔ'ai ma.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Nimmoaa ka m pun boori ni mani ni yarima la'am n‑di Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu wa pɔi ka n wahala diibu saha naayi paai.

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Veelim ni yɛlimaŋni an bɛ u ni maa, ana ka ti deaara. U veelim zugu ka ti siri nya veelim suŋŋa.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: N yɛtti ya ni, ni, n ku laa n‑di ka yaasa, halli ni Naawunni naam saha, ka kyumaŋni maa ti di.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Musa saani ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u zaaligu maa daa paai ti na, amaa Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani ka ti nya veelim ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Ka u daa zaŋŋi bunnyuura kɔbeaa la m‑puusi Naawunni ka yɛli Ya deaam n‑nyu, ka ti taaba!.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Sɔsɔ na zi n‑nya Naawunni. Amaa Naawunsɔ ŋɔn bɛ u Ba Naawunni saani u kɔkɔa, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka ti zaa baŋŋi u.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Dama n yɛtti ya ni, ni, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna n ku laa n‑nyu tiwala daam wa taabu halli ka Naawunni naam ti kyɛ na.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Tɔ: Gyɔni mooligu lahabaari nŋɔa#:- Dindahinseaa ka Izraɛli dima kpamma daa tum maalimaanniseeba ni ba suŋŋniba, ka ba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm na, ni ba ti boosi Gyɔni boosa. Ba daa yɛli u, ni, Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔni.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Ka u daa zaŋŋi diseebu m‑puusi pɛ'abu ka ŋmaai n‑zaŋŋi n‑tisi ba, ka yɛli, N ningbina nla. N zaŋŋi a m‑pa talima yarima zugu; lala ka ya niŋma ka teaari n yɛla.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa bu kyiisi, ka daa labsi yɛlimaŋni yɛttɔ'a paalu ni n‑yɛli ba, ni, u pa la Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum sɔ ni u ti su ba naam fukumsi la, ni, Man pa la Masiiya maa.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Ka bundira maa diibu nyaaŋŋa ka u daa laa n‑zaŋŋi kɔbeaa n‑niŋi lala yaasa, ka yɛli, Nangbanyinni zaalipaalli maa nla; n ziim ŋɔn nin yi na, ka di nin maali lala zaaligu maa n‑ti ya.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Ka ba daa maali m‑boosi u, ni, Ka dinŋɔna, i nyɛ la ŋɔni? Ilaygya bee?; Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ay, pa mani nla.: ni, Anabi maa n nyɛ i, fa?, ka u labsi n‑yɛli Ay.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya nyama! Mani ni dɔsɔ ŋɔn ya n‑yi n nyaaŋŋa la n la'amni n‑diri kpa wa.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ka ba naayi boosi u, ni, Ka dinŋɔna yɛlima ti nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa. Ka ti kyaŋŋi nti yɛli ban tum ti na maa. I mammaŋŋa yɛtti, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Man Ninsaala Bii maa nin bakki n‑kyaŋŋi kaman din pun lɔ seem, amaa ŋɔn mun yi n nyaaŋŋa n‑figsi ma n‑ti n dindaandima maa - di daama yɛla tɔ ni.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba hoo anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la, ni, Mani n nyɛ#:- Sɔ m bɛ yoo ni n‑tasira, ni, Ya sɛ'asima ti Dugma sɔya zaa Ka a tuusi kyippi! .

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Ka ba daa piili m‑boosiri taaba, ni, Ŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ u.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Tɔ; ka Farisii dimseeba gba ka ba daa tum na.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Tɔ; di saha ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa ŋmeeri taaba nangbankpeemni ni ba nya ŋɔni n‑gaari u kpeaa ba puuni.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: ka ban n daa boosi Gyɔni, ni, I yi pa la Masiiya maa, i pa Ilaygya, i mun pa anabi maa, wula zugu ka i suuri niriba Naawunni koom wa.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la saani, ba nadima n su ba yɛla ni kpeeŋŋu. Ba ninvugseeba ban su fukumsi maa n niŋni ba putoo, ka ba boonni ba, ni, Suŋŋniba!.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Man n suuri niriba Naawunni koom wa, amaa ninvugsɔ n zɛ ya sunsukkuni, halli ya na bu baŋŋi u gba;.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Amaa yarim saani di pa lala, see ka ya kyɛ ka ŋɔn nyɛ ya zaa kpeema leebi ya biiya, ka ya zuguraana leebi ŋɔn tumni bii tuma n‑tiri ya zaa.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn pa man nyaaŋŋa n‑kyɛnni na, amaa man bu sumsi ni n tum u daabri tuma ka lɔrigi u namtiri n‑ti u.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Ka boosi ba, ni, Ammi, ŋɔn ziya ka ba kpukkri bundira na n‑tiri u, ni ŋɔn kpukkri bundira na n‑tiri ŋɔn ziya la, ba ŋɔni n lee n‑gaari u kpeaa? - Pa ŋɔn ziya maa n gaari? Amaa man bɛ la ya saani kaman ŋɔn kpukkri bundira n‑tiri ya.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Tɔ; anŋɔ zaa daa niŋi la Bɛtani tiŋŋa maa ziiya, tinseaa kan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari sa; bɛni ka Gyɔni daa bɛ ni lala saha maa, ka daa suuri niriba Naawunni koom.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Ka yarima mun nyɛ ban pun kpalim m‑bɛ n saani, n wahala diibu ni, halli ni zuna.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Gyɔni daa nya Yisa ka u kyɛnni u saani na, ka yɛli, Ya nyama! Naawunni Pɛbila maa nŋɔa! Ŋɔn zaŋŋni duniya tumbeeri taalli m‑basira.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Dizugu m Ba Naawunni ŋɔn zaali ma naam seem la, lala ka n gba zaali ya naam.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Ŋɔnŋɔ ka n daa tɔ'asi u yɛla, ni, U paari la n nyaaŋŋa, amaa u pun gaari n tooni, dama u daa pun daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni ka ba naayi dɔ'ai ma.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Mani ni yarima nin ti la'am n‑zinni n naam zii maa ni, n‑diri ka nyuura. Di saha ya nin ti zinni naam gbana zugu n‑katti Izraɛli za'anɔya pii ni ayi niriba sariya.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Halli man daa bu mi ŋɔn nyɛ ninvugsɔa, amaa ŋɔn zugu ka n kyɛ na ni n ti suuri niriba koom - ni n ti kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima ti baŋŋi u.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Ni, Nyin Saimoon, nyama! Sintaani boori ni u zamsi ya hoo ban yeeli kyi n‑nya kasuma seem la.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa yɛli ŋɔn daa pun nya bunseesi yɛla, ni N daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka Sii maa daa yi arizana ni n‑sigri na hoo naŋmanni nti tɔm u zugu n‑kpalim m‑bɛ u ni.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa m pun susi Naawunni n‑ti nyini, ni, i yɛdda di fiilim. Ka i yi ti ŋmaligi n‑labi n saani na yaasa, nyin kpamsim i mabiisi wa gba yɛdda.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Di saha maa man daa na bu baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna nla. Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum ma ni n ti kyɛ na n‑suuri niriba koom, ŋɔna n daa laa n‑yɛli ma, ni, I yi ti nya ka Sii maa sigi na n‑tɔm dɔsɔ zugu n‑kpalim, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin mari Sii Kasi maa n‑suuri niriba koom.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, N Dugma, m maali siri ni n dɔli i halli nti kpɛ sarika duu ni, halli nti kpi gba.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔna n nya u, ni, Mani n yɛtti ya wa, ni, u siri nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛli i wa, nɔroo bu ya n‑kum zuna yuŋŋu wa halli ka i ti kyiisi napɔmbuta, ni, i zi ma.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai yaasa, ka Gyɔni daa laa n‑zɛya ni - ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba ayi.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Di saha ka u daa boosi ba zaa, ni, Man ŋɔn daa tum ya ka ya bu zi baagyi bee la'abooriga bee namna sahanseaa la, seelli daa pɔsigi ya? Ka ba daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: N‑daa nya, ka Yisa kyɛnni la u kyɛnni n‑gatta; ka u yɛli, Ya nyama! Naawunni Pɛbila maa nŋɔa.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, Tɔ; pampaŋŋɔ ŋɔna, ŋɔn mari baagyi ŋɔn zaŋŋma ka, ka ŋɔn mari la'abooriga gba zaŋŋma ka; halli ŋɔn ka takoobi ŋɔn koosim u kparigu n‑da.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba ayi maa daa wum u yɛttɔ'a maa ka daa doaai n‑kari Yisa nɔba.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, Ba zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u la'am m‑paasi tumbeeri dima ziiya. ka lala m‑paari ma na. Yɛlimaŋni n yɛla zaa naari ni maa.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑nya ka ba katti la u nɔba, ka u daa boosi ba, ni, Bɔ ka ya boora?; ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Rabbi, , yani ka i kpeera.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa yɛli u, Ti Dugma, nyama, takoobidima ayi nŋɔa!; ka u yɛli ba, Tɔ. Di sakkiya.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ! Ya kyɛm na nti nya!; ka ba daa dɔli u nti baŋŋi u yirikpeetiri ŋɔn bɛ seela. Ka ba daa kpɛ n‑gbɛ u saani, dama zaanoori saha daa pun paai.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Tɔ; ka u daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi Ɔlivii Zoori ziiya hoo u yuŋŋu kam soori ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa dɔli u.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Tɔ; ka niriba ayi wa daa wum Yisa yɛla Gyɔni saani, ka laa n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa. Ba yinni n daa nyɛ Anduru: Saimoon Piita sunzɔ maa nla.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Ya susim Naawunni ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba ku zamsi ya halli ka ya ti lu.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Ka dizugu u daa daŋŋi m‑bɔ u beeri Saimoon ziiya nti yɛli u, ni, Ti nya la Masiiya maa! .

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Di saha maa ka u tugsi tooni beela - n‑sa'asi hoo kugri lɔppu wɔ'alim.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: ka daa mari u n‑taasi Yisa saani na. Ka Yisa daa gɔsi u ka yɛli, Saimoon, Gyɔni biiya, n nyɛ i: tɔ! ba nin booli i Siifasi .

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑susi Naawunni n‑yɛli, M Ba, di yi nyɛ la i yuubu ŋɔn bɛ seem, kyɛ ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa gaari ka kyɛ ma: di kyɛ ka n nyu di! - Amaa di pa man ŋɔn boori seem la niŋma, amaa nyin ŋɔn boori seem maa niŋma.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Beoo ŋɔn daa laa n‑neaai, Yisa daa yɛli, ni, u ya doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu maa ni. Di saha ka u daa nya Filipi, n‑yɛli u, ni, Kyɛm na n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Ka malaaka yi arizana ni n‑kyɛ u saani na n‑kpamsiri u.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Ka Filipi mun daa nya Nataniyɛli n‑yɛli u, Ti nya la Musa ŋɔn daa sɔbi sɔ lahabaari n‑dooli zaaligu gbaŋŋu ni la, ka anabidima gba daa sɔbi u yɛla. Yisa nla, Gyoosɛfi biiya, ka u yi Nazarɛti tiŋŋa ni na.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Ka u sufu ŋɔn daa sa'am pam zugu, u daa laa n‑gbaai u wunsusigu ni nimmoaa, halli ka u wɔligu ti pusiri n‑ŋmanni hoo ziim n‑tɔ'ari n‑luri tiŋŋa.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Ka Nataniyɛli daa yɛli u, ni, Nazarɛti fa! Bunsuŋŋu nin toaai n‑yi tinkyɔ'aŋŋu wa na, bee? Ka Filipi daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Kyɛm na n‑nya.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn daa doaai wunsusigu maa ni, u daa labi u nyaandɔliba maa saani. N‑daa paai, ka ba daa gbisiri la ba sufusa'aŋŋu zugu.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti nya Nataniyɛli ka u kyɛnni u saani na, ka u daa tɔ'asi u yɛla, ni, Ya nyama! Izraɛli nira ni yɛlimaŋni nŋɔa. Ziri halli beela ka u ni.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ka u boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya gbisira? Ya doaama n‑susi Naawunni ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba ku zamsi ya halli ka ya ti lu.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Dizugu ka Nataniyɛli daa boosi u, ni, I mi ma la yani?; ka Yisa labsi u, ni, N daa nya i ka i zi la kinkaŋŋa la gbinni pɔi, ka Filipi naayi booli i.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Tɔ; ka ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri sahanseaa maa, niriba pam daa kyɛ u saani na. Ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Gyuudasi, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa yinni, daa bɛ ba tooni. Ka u daa paai Yisa saani na ni u ti puusi u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Di saha ka Nataniyɛli daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, Rabbi, i siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya! N‑laa n‑nyɛ Izraɛli dima naaba.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: ka Yisa boosi u, ni, Gyuudasi, i nin kyɛ ka i puusigu la figsi ma n‑niŋi n dindaandima nuusi ni, bee wula.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, I kur niŋi ma la yɛdda man yɛli i lala zugu, bee? Ni, n daa nya i kinkaŋŋa maa gbinni? I siri nin nya yɛliseesi an gaari lala pam.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Ka u nyaaŋŋa dima daa baŋŋi din ya n‑niŋi, ka daa yɛli, Ti Dugma, ti zaŋŋma takoobi n‑kyɛ ba, bee?;.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli u, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ya nin nya ka arizana za'anɔya yi yoaai ka Naawunni malaakadima duuri ka sigri man zugu, man ŋɔn nyɛ Ninsaala Bii maa.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: ka ba yinni daa zaŋŋi u takoobi n‑kyɛ kpambaya bisɔ n‑fikki u nudirigu tubri m‑basi:.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Ka bɛkwai maa piiligu dabsiri, Alahari daari maa, pɔ'aba maa daa doaai asuba pinipini, ka zaŋŋi turaaliseebu ban pun da n‑zaali la m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi gballi maa ziiya.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ban daa paai bɛni maa, ba daa nya ka kugseaa ban daa zaŋŋi n‑ligi gballi maa noori la daa pun bilim n‑yi kpaŋŋa ka kyɛ ka.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: amaa ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ya di niŋni lala!, ka daa siisi doo maa tubri maa n‑tibi u.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ka ban daa kpɛ gballi duu maa puuni, ba daa bu nya ti Dugma Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa tɔ'asi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi zuguraandima, ni kpamma maa, bankam zaa yi na ni ba ti gbaai u, m‑boosi ba, ni, N nyɛ la ban zabri ka faari neema maa ninvugsɔ, bee wula? - ka ya mari takoobidima ni dɔya n‑yi na?.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam. Di saha ka dɔppa ayi di yi na n‑zaani n‑tabli ba, ka ba sutura nya'ari nyain.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Beoo kam n daa bɛ la ya saani Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka ya daa bu zaŋŋi ya nuusi n‑gbaai ma. Amaa saha wa nyɛ la ya dinni, ni zibsim yiiko gba.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Ka dabeem daa gbaai pɔ'aba maa pam, ka ba sunni ba zugri tiŋŋa. Ka dɔppa maa daa boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya boori nyɔvuri daana kpiimma ziiya?.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa gbaai u n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la yiri. Ka Piita daa sɔ'ai n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa sa.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: U laa n‑ka kpa, u yisigiya. Ya teaam ŋɔn daa yɛli ya seem, ŋɔn daa na bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu sahanseaa la.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ka ban daa maali bugum dundɔŋŋu sunsukkuni ka la'am n‑zi bɛni maa, Piita gba daa zi ba saani.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: U daa yɛli ya, ni, di kpa la talasi ni ba zaŋŋi Ninsaala Bii maa n‑niŋi ala'akyi dima nuusi ni, ka ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, amaa ka u nin ti neaai kum ni daba ata daari.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Ka pɔ'asarisɔ nya ŋɔn zinni bugum maa ni maa, ka gɔsi u ka yɛli ba, Doo wa gba daa dɔli la Yisa.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Di saha ka pɔ'aba maa daa siri teaai Yisa ŋɔn daa pun yɛli ba seem maa.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Amaa ka Piita kyiisi n‑yɛli, Nyin pɔ'asariga wa, n zi u.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: ka daa labi n‑kuli yiri ni, ni ba ti yɛli Yisa nyaandɔliba pii ni yinni maa, ni ba taaba zaa.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Ka di toaai beela ka sɔ gba laa n‑nya u ka yɛli u, I gba nyɛ la niriba wa yinni! Ka Piita yɛli u, N zɔa, m pa ba yinni.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, n‑daa la, ni Gyeoowana, ni Mariama Gyeemsi ma la, ni pɔ'aseeba m‑paasi ba zugu. Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti mooli yɛla wa zaa lahabaari n‑ti Yisa tuumudima maa.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Ka di daa yuui beela, hoo hawa yinni, ka dɔsɔ gba daa ti tɔ'asi nimmoosi n‑yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni doo wa gba daa dɔli u, dama u gba nyɛ la Galilii nira.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Amaa ba yɛttɔ'a maa daa ŋmanni la yɛttɔ'ayɔya n‑ti ba, ka ba daa bu niŋi ba yɛdda.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Amaa ka Piita yɛli N zɔa, m bu mi nyin tɔ'asi sɔ maa yɛla. Ka ŋɔn daa kur bɛ ni n‑tɔ'asiri maa, nɔroo kum.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Amaa Piita mun daa doaai n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai gballi maa ni, n‑sunni n‑gɔsi ka puuni n‑nya tampeela maa kɔkɔ ŋɔn dɔya. Ka u daa labi n‑kuli, ka di niŋi u alahazibu din niŋi maa zugu.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Ka ti Dugma Yisa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi u, ka Piita daa teaai Yisa ŋɔn daa pun yɛli u seem la, ni, I nin kyiisi ma napɔmbuta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ. Di daari maa ka ba niriba ayi daa kyaŋŋi tinkpanseaa. Tiŋŋa maa yuuri daa boonni la Imeeyusi: ka nyɛ la maala ayɔpɔi zaŋŋi m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Ka Piita zɔ n‑yi samanni ka kumni kunkuma.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ka ban daa bɛ soori maa, ba daa diri azama n‑tɔ'asiri taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi an daa niŋi maa yɛla.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Di saha maa ka ninvugseeba ban daa mari Yisa maa daa maanni u ansarisi ka buuri u;.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Ka ban daa kyɛnni n‑sɔsiri sahanseaa maa, Yisa paai ba saani na. U daa paasi ba zugu ka ba bata zaa la'am n‑kyɛnna.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: ka zaŋŋi bunni n‑vili u nini ka boosi u, ni, Nyin anabi wa, bugma m‑baŋŋi! Ŋɔni n ŋmɛ i wa.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: Amaa ba daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑baŋŋi u.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Ka ba daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a pam n‑gyɛnni u ka tuuri u.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Ka u boosi ba, ni, Azambɔ ka ya diri ni taaba ya soori wa ni? Ka ba kpalim n‑zaani, ka ba sufu na kur sa'am ni.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Izraɛli dima kpamma zaa daa la'amiya, halli ba gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima zaa gba, ka zaŋŋi Yisa n‑taasi ba la'aŋŋu maa na.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ka ba yinni, ŋɔn yuuri boonni Kliyɔpaasi la, daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, Saanseeba zaa ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm puuni maa m mi din niŋi daba ayi wa puuni, di yi pa la nyin kɔkɔ, fa.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Ka yɛli u I yi siri nyɛ la Masiiya ŋɔn ya fa ti m‑basi ka leebi naaba la, nyin yɛlim ti! Amaa ka u labsi n‑yɛli, Halli n yi ti yɛli ya, ya ku niŋi yɛdda.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Ka u boosi ba, ni, Bumbɔ yɛla? Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛ Nazarɛti nira la yɛla ka ti tɔ'asira. Anabi n daa nyɛ u, ka u daa tum tunkara ka tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi an daa wulisi u yaa ŋɔn bɛ seem maa, Naawunni saani ni niriba gba saani.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: M mun yi na boosi ya boosigu, ya ku gyɛrigi ma.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Ni, ti gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni ti kpamma maa zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ka ba kari u sariya ka kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku u.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna Ninsaala Bii maa ya n‑zinni Naawunni Ŋɔn Su Yiiko nudirigu ya'ari.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Amaa tirim daa mari la tama, ni, ŋɔna n ya fa Izraɛli dima m‑basi. Ka din paasi dinŋɔ zugu, zuna nyɛ la daba ata daari yɛla wa ŋɔn niŋi maa.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Ka ba daa daa boosi u, ni, Dinŋɔna, Naawunni Bii n nyɛ i?; ka u yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, ya yɛliya.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Yaasa, pɔ'aseeba ban bɛ ti saani n kur kyɛ ka di gyɛri ti, dama ba da daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni zuna asuba wa, ka bu nya u ningbiŋŋu maa;.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Ka ba yɛli, Ti bu laa m‑boori sɛharadima yaasa, bee bɔa? Ti mammansi pun wum u noori ni yɛttɔ'a.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: ka labi na n‑yɛli ti, ni, ba nya malaakadima ka ba yɛli ba, ni, u neaaya.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Ka ti ninvugseeba gba doaai n‑kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni, ni ba nya; ka di nyɛ la hoo pɔ'aba maa ŋɔn da kur yɛli seem. Amaa ba gba da bu nya Yisa.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Ka ŋɔna n daa yɛli ba, ni, Yarima yamkɔŋŋniba wa! Ya bu wumni anabidima ŋɔn daa pun tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi la, ka bu sakki n‑niŋi a yɛdda yooma!.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Ammi, di daa bu kpa talasi ni Masiiya maa nya lala namsigu maa pɔi ka naayi kpɛ u naam gyirima ziiya.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Di saha ka u daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a m‑piili n‑wulisiri ba u mammaŋŋa yɛla Naawunni Litaafi ni. U daa piili la Musa sɔppu ni, halli nti paai anabidima ban daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa ti pɔri tinkpanseaa ban daa kyɛnni maa, u niŋi hoo ŋɔn yɛ n‑gaari la tooni.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Amaa ka ba goaai u n‑yɛli, Kpalimma ti saani, dama wuntaŋŋa luya ka yuŋŋu zibra. Ka u daa kpɛ, ni u ti gbɛ ba saani.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Ka bundirigu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai maa, ba daa la'am taaba ni ba di, ka u daa zaŋŋi la diibu n‑susi Naawunni n‑niŋi ka, ka ŋmaasi n‑ti ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Di saha ka ba nini di neaai ka ba baŋŋi u, amaa ŋɔn mun lili ka kyɛ ba.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Ka ba yɛli taaba, Ti sufu da bu pɛligi ŋɔn da tɔ'asiri n‑ti ti soori ni la? - ŋɔn da wulisi Litaafi maa gbinni la.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Tɔ: ka ba yisigi di saha maa n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ba daa paaya, ka nyaandɔliba piiya ni yinni maa, ni ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ ba saani la, la'am n‑ziya.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Ka ba yɛli ba, ni, Ti Dugma siri neaaya ka Saimoon nya u.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Di saha maa ka Imeeyusi dima maa daa yɛli ba din niŋi ba soori ni. Ka laa n‑yɛli ba ŋɔn ŋmaasi diibu n‑ti ba ka ba baŋŋi u seem.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Ka ban daa kur tɔ'asiri maa, Yisa mammaŋŋa di zaani ba sunsukkuni ka puusi ba.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Amaa dabeem daa gbaai ba pam. Ba daa teesi, ni, ba nya la kpiinyisigi.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Ka u boosi ba, ni, Bɔ n niŋi ka di mugsi ya, ka ya birisiri ya teesa ni maa?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Ya nyam n nuusi, ni n nɔba wa; mani nla! Ya siisim ma n‑nya! Siiya ka numni ni kɔba hoo man mari seem wa.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai la, u daa wulisi ba u nuusi ni u nɔba.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: Amaa ba sufu ŋɔn daa pɛligi seem maa zugu ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi lala yɛdda, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam. Ka u boosi ba, ni, Ya mari diseebu kpa.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi zinseerigu beela n‑ti u.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: ka u deaai ka n‑ŋɔbi ba tooni.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Di saha maa ka u yɛli ba, Yɛttɔ'a wa ka n daa pun tɔ'asi ya, man ŋɔn daa na bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la. N daa yɛli ya, ni, yɛttɔ'a pam pun sɔbi n‑dooli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n yɛla Musa zaaligu maa ni, ni anabidima yɛla ni, halli Yuuma sɔppu maa ni gba. Di kpa la talasi ni anŋɔ zaasa ŋɔn wulisi seem la nin baasi n‑niŋi.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Ka u neaai ba yɛm ni ba ti baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu maa gbinni.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: ka yɛli ba, Di sɔbiya, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum Masisɔ na maa nin nya namsigu lala pɔi, ka naayi neaai kum ni daba ata daari.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Ni, ba nin zaŋŋi Masiiya yuuri m‑piili Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑moonni lahabaari maa n‑ti duniya wa zaa niriba, ni, ba ŋmaligma ka kyɛ ba tumbeeri tuma, ni ba ti nya ba taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Yarima n nyɛ ban nyɛ buna wa zaa sɛhara dima maa.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: Ya nyama, n nin tum sɔ na hoo m Ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun basi nangbambasiri n‑ti ya seem maa. Yarima muna - ya kpalimma Gyɛrusalɛm halli ka ya ti deaai yiiko din yi saazugu ni na.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: U daa zaŋŋi ba n‑yi halli nti paai Bɛtani; ka daa duusi u nuusi saazugu n‑susi Naawunni alibarika yɛlisuma n‑niŋi ba.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa na kur susiri n‑niŋni ba sahanseaa maa, u du n‑kyaŋŋi saazugu ka kyɛ ba.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Ka ba mun daa gyeem u ka ŋmaligi n‑labi Gyɛrusalɛm ni sufupeelli pam.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Ka kur bɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni m‑puusiri Naawunni.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Yɛhudiya dima toondaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Nikɔdiimusi; u daa dɔli la Farisidima maa soori la.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔnwa daa kyɛ Yisa saani na yuŋŋu, nti yɛli u, M Ba Rabbi, ti mi, ni, i nyɛ la karimba, ka Naawunni tum i na; dama sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑niŋi alahazibu tunseesi nyin niŋni maa, di yi pa la Naawunni m bɛ u saani.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑nya Naawunni naam maa, di yi pa la ba maali n‑dɔ'ai u yaasa.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Ka Nikɔdiimusi daa yɛli, Ba laa n‑ya n‑niŋi la wula n‑nyaŋŋi n‑dɔ'ai bunkurigu yaasa? U ku toaai n‑labi n‑kpɛ u ma puu ni ka ba maali n‑dɔ'ai u.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i ni, ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑kpɛ Naawunni naam ni, ba yi bu dɔli koom suubu soori n‑dɔ'ai u, ni Naawunni Sii Vuusim soori gba.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Ninsaala ningbiŋŋu ŋɔn dɔ'ai seelli nyɛ la ninsaala, ka Naawunni Siiya mun ŋɔn dɔ'ai seelli nyɛ la siiya.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Dizugu di kyɛ ka di niŋi i alahazibu, man ŋɔn yɛli i seem, ni, see ka ba maali n‑dɔ'ai sɔ yaasa.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: I bu nya? - Pɛsigu maa yi zeeri n‑zeera, i mun wumni ka vuuri maa, amaa i ku baŋŋi kan yiiri ya'aseaa na, bee, kan kyɛnni ya'aseaa. Tɔ, ka Naawunni Vuusim ŋɔn dɔ'ai seeba saani di bɛ la lala.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Ka Nikɔdiimusi daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, Wula ka anŋɔ zaa nin toaai n‑niŋi.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Nyini n nyɛ karimba n‑tiri Izraɛli dima, ka i na zi anŋɔa, fa?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, tirima n tɔ'asiri tin mi seelli, ka tisiri lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tin na mi n‑nya; ka di zaa yɔri yarima sɔsɔ ku sakki n‑deaai ti lahabaari maa.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: N yɛtti ya duniya wa yɛla ka ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda; ka dinŋɔna ya ya niŋi la wula n‑niŋi ma yɛdda, man yi ti yɛli arizana yɛla n‑ti ya.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Ni, Ka sɔ mun na zi n‑du m‑paai arizana ni, na'ala ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pun yi arizana ni n‑sigi na - Ninsaala Bii maa nla.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Kaman hoo Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi tasiri kutti m‑maali waafu maa n‑kpaasi n‑tabli doo zugu seem maa, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa yoo puuni la, lala ka di niŋi talasi ni ba zaŋŋi Ninsaala Bii maa gba n‑sa saazugu.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: ka sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda la, di daama nin mari la nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Naawunni wurilim daa bɛ la duniya wa dima maa pam, halli nti zaŋŋi u bigaŋŋa n‑tisi, ni sɔkam yi niŋi u yɛdda u ku baasi yɔri amaa u nin mari nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Dama Naawunni daa bu tum u Bii maa ni u kyɛ duniya wa ni na nti kari duniya wa dima sariya, amaa u tum u na ni duniya dima maa ti nya tiliginsim u zugu.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Sɔ ŋɔn niŋi Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda zaa, u ku mari sariya; ŋɔn mun bu niŋi yɛdda zaa ka ba pun kari u sariya n‑lusi u, ŋɔn bu niŋi Naawunni Bigaŋŋa maa yɛdda zugu.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Sariya karibu gbinni nŋɔa: neesim maa daa pun kyɛ duniya wa ni na, amaa ninsaalidima maa daa boori zibsim maa ka kyɛ neesim, ba tuma ŋɔn bɛ pam zugu.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Dama sɔ yi niŋni tumbeeri, u nin gyɛ neesim maa ka ku sakki ni u kyɛ neesim maa saani na, dama u tumbeeri nin yi paalu na.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Amaa sɔ ŋɔn niŋni yɛlimaŋni tuma, ŋɔna nin kyɛ neesim maa saani na, ni u tuma ti yi paalu na ka ba baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni zugu ka u daa niŋi a.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu la ni, ka bɛni ka ŋɔn ni ban daa yuui beela n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Di saha ka Gyɔni gba daa suuri niriba: u daa bɛ la Aynɔn ziiya, ka di bu wɔ'a ni Saalim tiŋŋa. Koom daa zoaai bɛni, ka niriba daa kyɛnni u saani na ka u tiri ba kɔsuubu.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni ni Yɛhudiya ninvugsɔ zaŋŋi n‑gbaai suubu ni peaari kaari yɛla.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Ka ba daa kyɛ Gyɔni saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Ti Ba Rabbi, ti ya boosi i zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ i saani Gyɔdin mɔ'ari zii la - nyin daa tɔ'asi sɔ lahabaari n‑ti ti la. Tɔ, ŋɔna n suuri niriba Naawunni koom, ka sɔkam zaa kyɛnni u saani.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi, ni, Sɔ ku toaai m‑mari seelli, di yi pa la Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi bunseelli maa n‑ti u.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Ya mammansi daa pun wum man daa yɛli seem la, ni, man pa Masiiya maa, amaa Naawunni n tum ma ni n ti daŋŋi Masiiya maa tooni na.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Ya bu nya? - Ba yi maali amriya kyuu sahanseaa la, dɔsɔ ŋɔn mari pɔ'apaalli maa nyɛ la amriya doo maa; u zɔ ŋɔn zɛ u saani n‑wumni muna, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn sufu yi maai amriya doo maa kukɔri wumpu zugu. Lala ka man gba sufu maai pam maa.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Di sumni, ni, ŋɔna m paasira, ka man mun fiilimna.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Ŋɔn yi saazugu ni na gaari sɔkam zaa. Ŋɔn mun yi duniya wa ni na nyɛ la duniya wa nira, ka tɔ'asiri la duniya wa yɛla; amaa ŋɔn yi arizana ni na gaari sɔkam zaa.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Ŋɔnwa wulisiri ŋɔn pun nya ka wum yɛla maa lahabaari; amaa di zaa yɔri, sɔ ku sakki n‑deaari u yɛligu maa.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Amaa ŋɔnkam yi sakki n‑deaai u yɛligu maa, di daama nin diisi n‑tabli, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn tum dɔsɔ maa tɔ'asiri la Naawunni yɛligu, dama Naawunni bu ma'asiri u Sii maa beelabeela n‑tiri u.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Naawunni Bii maa muna - u Ba siri wuri u, ka zaŋŋi bunseelikam n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Ŋɔnkam yi niŋni Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Ŋɔnkam mun bu sakki n‑niŋi u yɛdda ku nya nyɔvuri, halli Naawunni sufugyeaa kpalim m‑bɛ u zugu.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa Yɛhudiya dima kyuseaa n daa bɛ ni, ka Yisa daa ti kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Tɔ, ka Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, za'anɔtitaari m bɛ ni, ka ba boonni ka Peesi Za'anɔri la: ka za'anɔri wa kpaŋŋa ka beeŋŋu bɛ ni, ka Hibruu dima boonni ka Bɛtzaata, ka ka mari luffadima abanu.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: A puuni ka zoomma, ni gbarisi, ni nukpiima dima, ni gbarikpiima dima, ni dooru baligu kam dima zaa dɔya.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Tɔ, ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka dooru daa mari u halli di yuumpisita ni anii.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yisa daa nya u ka u dɔ bɛni ka daa baŋŋi, ni, dooru maa daa pun mari doo wa ka di yuui; ka u daa boosi u, ni, Ammi, i boori i nya tippu.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Ka dooru daana maa labsi n‑yɛli, M Ba, man ka sɔsɔ kpa wa ka u nin zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi beeŋŋu wa puuni. Koom maa yi ti damni sahanseaa la; n yi ti kpaŋŋni la n maŋŋa ni n sigi di ni, sɔ n yi pun ti daŋŋi ma n‑sigi di puuni.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Doaama n‑zaani saazugu, ka kpikki i sɔŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Lala saha maa ka doo maa da nya tippu; u daa kpikki u sɔŋŋu ka piili n‑kyɛnna. Dindahinseaa lala ŋɔn daa niŋi maa daa nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri daari.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: ka di zugu Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa yɛli doo ŋɔn nya tippu la, ni, Zuna nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri, nyin nya'ai i sɔŋŋu wa di galim la ti zaaligu.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: U daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Dɔsɔ ŋɔn tibi ma maa n yɛli ma, ni, n zaŋŋma n sɔŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Ka ba boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni n nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn yɛli i, ni, i niŋma nŋɔ maa.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Amaa dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nya tippu wa daa bu mi Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛ sɔa, dama daadam n daa bɛ bɛni maa pam, ka Yisa daa sɔ'ai baalimm m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi kpaŋŋa.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa ti nya doo maa Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni ka daa yɛli u, ni, Wumma, i yi kpaŋŋiya pampaŋŋɔa, dinzugu kyɛli tumbeeri tumpu: ka di yi pa lala, bunseela din bɛ n‑gaari zaa nin ti niŋi i yaasa.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Ka doo maa daa di bakki n‑kyaŋŋi nti yɛli Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa, ni, Yisa n nyɛ ŋɔn daa tibi u maa.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Ka ba daa piili m‑mugsiri Yisa, ŋɔn daa tibri niriba Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa zugu.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, M Ba kur tumni ni saha kam zaa, ka man gba tumna.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Yɛlli wa daa kyɛ ka Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa di maali n‑lɔ la niya ni ba ku u; di mun pa la ŋɔn daa burigi Vuusim Dabsiri daari zaaligu zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ŋɔn daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la u mammantoori ba zugu m‑paasi; ni, u zaŋŋni u maŋŋa m‑ma'asiri la Naawunni.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya: Bii maa ku toaai n‑niŋi bunseela u mammantoori; u niŋni la u yi nya u Ba ŋɔn niŋni seelli la kɔkɔa. U Ba maa ŋɔn tumni seem, lala ka Bii maa gba tumna.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Dama u Ba maa wuri la Bii maa ka wulisiri u ŋɔn niŋni seelikam zaasa. Halli gba u nin ti wulisi u tunkara n‑gaari nŋɔa, ka di nin ti niŋi ya zaasa alahazibu.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Kaman u Ba maa ŋɔn kur neaari kpiimma ka tiri ba nyɔvuya seem la, lala taabu ka Bii maa gba zaŋŋni nyɔvuri n‑tiri ŋɔn boori seeba.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: M‑paasi lala, u Ba maa mun bu katti sariya u mammaŋŋa. U tisi u Bii maa la soori ni yɛlimaŋni ni u kari sariya.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: din nin kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa tibgi Bii maa kaman ban tibgri u Ba maa seem. Ŋɔn kam zaa bu tibgi Bii maa bu tibgi u Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum u na maa gba.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa wum n yɛttɔ'a wa ka niŋi ŋɔn tum ma na maa yɛdda, di daama mari nyɔvuriseaa kan ka naari. Ba bu ti yɛ kari u sariya, amaa u pun yi kum ni n‑kpɛ nyɔvuri ni.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka man yɛtti ya, ni, saha kyɛnni na, saha maa pun paai na gba, kpiimma ŋɔn ti yɛ wumni Naawunni Bii maa kukɔri, ka ninvugseeba ban ti wum maa nin ti bɛ nyɔvuri ni.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni mammaŋŋa ŋɔn mari nyɔvuri u saani seem, lala ka u zaŋŋi n‑ti u toori Biiya ni u mari nyɔvuri u saani.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: Ka laa n‑ti u yiiko ni u kari sariya m‑paasi, ŋɔn nyɛ Ninsaala Bii maa zugu.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka dinŋɔ zugu gyɛri ya yam, dama saha kyɛnni na ka ninvugseeba ban bɛ ba dabɔya ni zaasa nin ti wum Ninsaala Bii maa kukɔri.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: Di saha ka ba nin yi na: ka ban daa tum tunsuma la nin ti doaai kum ni n‑nya nyɔvuri, ka ban mun daa tum tumbeeri la nin ti doaai nti nya tubdarigri.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Man ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli m mammaŋŋa yiiko ni, see ka man wum pɔi, ka naayi dɔli man wum seem n‑kari sariya maa. Ka man sariya karibu wa nyɛ la din tuusi, dama m bu boori la m mammantoori yuubu, amaa ŋɔn tum ma na yuubu maa ka n boora.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, N yi di sɛhara n‑ti m mammaŋŋa yɛttɔ'a, lala sɛhara maa ku niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Ay! - Sɔ m bɛ ni n‑diri sɛhara n‑tiri n yɛttɔ'a maa; ka man gba baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔn niŋi ma sɛharaseelli maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Ya daa tum Gyɔni saani ni ya ti baŋŋi, ka ŋɔn Gyɔni daa di sɛhara n‑ti yɛlimaŋni yɛla.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Halli m bu deaari ninsaala sɛhara gba, n yɛtti Gyɔni yɛla dama m boori la ya wum ka nya tiliginsim.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Gyɔni muna - u daa nyɛ la bugum firila n‑nya'asira, ka ya daa sakki ni ya paai u neesim maa na, ka di daa niŋi ya nya'asim ni sufupeelli ka daa yuui beela.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Amaa m mari sɛhara seaa din gaari Gyɔni - tunseesi ka m Ba ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti ma ni n ti tum n‑naai maa nla: lala tuma maa siri diri la sɛhara n‑ti ma, ni, m Ba maa daa tum ma na.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Ka Farisidima maa daa ti wum, ni, Yisa la'amni la nyaandɔliba pam ka suuri ba Naawunni koom, halli nti gaari Gyɔni ŋɔn daa la'am seeba la.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: M‑paasi lala, m Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum ma na maa n nyɛ n sɛhara daana. Yarima mun na zi n‑wum u kukɔri, bee, ya baŋŋi ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: Dizugu Yisa ŋɔn daa wum ban daa tɔ'asiri seem la, u daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: Ka u yɛligu maa mun bu laa n‑kpalim ya saani, dama ya bu niŋi ŋɔn tum sɔ na maa yɛdda.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Ka u soori maa ni u daa kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli n‑yi la Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ya karimni Naawunni Litaafi la n‑viisira, ni, ka puuni ka ya nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga; tɔ - ana n nyɛ an diri sɛhara n‑tiri man yɛla.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa bɛ Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu maa ni la, u daa kyaŋŋi nti paai tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Sikaari la. Bɛni maa daa bu wɔ'a ni paaluseaa ka Yaakubu daa pun zaŋŋi n‑ti u bii Yisifu sahanseaa sa la.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Di zaa yɔri, ya mun ku sakki ni ya kyɛ n saani na n‑deaai nyɔvuri.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Tɔ; ka Yaakubu buliga daa bɛ bɛni; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi u soori maa nti gyɛa, u daa kyaŋŋi nti zinni la buliga maa ziiya - agbaa saha n‑daa la.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Man bu deaai gyirima ninsaalidima saani.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: U nyaandɔliba maa daa gaari n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni ni ba ti da bundirigu na. Ka Samɛrii pɔ'asɔ daa sigi buliga maa ni na ni u ti gbeaai koom, ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u tim u koom ka u nyu.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Amaa m mi ya yɛla, ni, ya ka Naawunni wurilim ya sufu ni.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, I nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira ka man mun nyɛ Samɛrii pɔ'a, dizugu wula ka i nin toaai n‑susi koom man saani.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Tɔ: m Ba yuuri zugu ka n kyɛ kpa na, ka ya ku sakki n‑deaai ma; amaa sɔ yi ti kyɛ na u mammaŋŋa yuuri zugu, ŋɔna ka ya nin deaai.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, I yi da baŋŋi la Naawunni ŋɔn tisiri seela, ka mi ŋɔni n tɔ'asiri n‑tiri i m‑boori koom wa, i da naa n‑yɛli u ka u ti i kɔsuŋŋu ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ya nin niŋi wula n‑niŋi yɛdda, sahanseaa ka ya na boori gyirima ya taaba saani ka ku sakki ni ya boori gyirima Naawunni ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa saani.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, M Bapira, i ka guuga ni i zaŋŋi n‑gbeaai koom, ka buliga wa mun zulim pam. Ka, i ya niŋi la wula n‑nya kɔsuŋŋu wa.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Tɔ: yan di teesiri, ni, man nin zaŋŋi ya taalli n‑ti m Ba maa; sɔ bɛ ni n nin zaŋŋi ya taalli n‑ti u na - halli Musa, yan gɔsi sɔ n‑yɛtti, ni, ya tama kur bɛ u ni la.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Ni, Ti yaadoo Yaakubu daa ti ti buliga wa; ŋɔn ni u biisi ni ba bunkɔbri zaa daa nyuuri ka koom. Nyini n‑gaari Yaakubu, bee.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Amaa ya yi daa siri niŋi ŋɔn Musa yɛdda, ya da naa n‑niŋi man gba yɛdda, dama Musa daa sɔbi man yɛla n‑ti ya.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyu koom wa, kɔnyuuri nin laa n‑gbaai u.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Amaa yan ŋɔn bu niŋi Musa sɔppu yɛla la yɛdda, ya ya niŋi la wula n‑niŋi man yɛtti ya yɛliseesi wa yɛdda.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyu kɔseebu man yɛ n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u maa muna - kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai di daama yaasa. Lala koom maa yɛ n‑leebi la din bunni u puuni, halli nti kyɛ ka u nya nyɔvurisuŋŋu din ka tariga.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛli, Tɔ, m Bapira, kur zaŋŋma lala koom n‑ti ma na, ka kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai ma yaasa halli ka n ku laa n‑sigri kpa na n‑gbeaari koom.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna kyɛma nti booli i sira maa na ka labi na.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, N ka sira. ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, I yɛli la yɛlimaŋni, ni, i ka sira.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: I na mun kuli dɔppa anu, halli nyin ni dɔsɔ ŋɔn na bɛ ni maa gba pa la i sira: i yɛli ma la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛli, Mma, m Bapira! N baŋŋiya, ni, anabi n nyɛ i.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ: kyɛ ka n boosi i bunseelli - ti Samɛrii yaadima daa gyeemni Naawunni zoori wa zugu, amaa ya Yɛhudiya dima maa yɛnni, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa kɔkɔ n nyɛ Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziiya ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Pɔ'a, kur niŋni ma yɛdda; saha kyɛnni na ka ya yi ya gyeemni ti Ba Naawunni, di ku nyɛ la zoori wa zugu, bee, Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni yɛttɔ'a.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Yarima Samɛrii dima kur bu baŋŋi yan gyeemni sɔa, ti mun Yɛhudiya dima mi tin gyeemni sɔa, dama tiliginsim yi la Yɛhudiya dima maa saani na.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Amaa saha kyɛnni na, halli ka pun paai na gba, ka ban gyeemni ti Ba Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni la nin dɔli Sii Kasi soori n‑gyeemni u, ni yɛlimaŋni soori gba. Lala soori ka Naawunni boori ni ba dɔli n‑gyeemni u.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Naawunni nyɛ la siiya, ka dizugu see ka ban ya gyeemni u dɔli la siiya soori maa n‑gyeemna, ni yɛlimaŋni soori gba.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, M mi halli, ni, ba n boonni sɔ Masiiya, bee Kirisita, la nin kyɛ na, ka u yi ti kyɛ na u nin wulisi ti bunseelli kam zaa.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, Mani nla - man ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑tiri i wa.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la daa labi na, ka ban daa nya u ka ŋɔn ni pɔ'a maa diri azama maa, di daa gyɛri ba pam. Amaa ba sɔ daa bu boosi pɔ'a maa, ni, I boori la bɔa?, bee, m‑boosi Yisa, ni, Bɔ n kyɛ ka nyin ni pɔ'a wa diri azama.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa daa kyɛ u kɔyuuri maa ka labi n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni nti yɛli niriba maa.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: ni, Ya kyɛm na nti nya dɔsɔ ŋɔn yɛli ma man daa pun tum tunseesi zaa. Pa ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Ka niriba maa daa yi tiŋŋa maa ni na, ni ba kyɛ Yisa saani na.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa bɛlim u, ni, Ti Ba Rabbi, deaama diibu n‑di.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Ka ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, M mari la bundirigu ni n di, ka ya ka di lahabaari maa.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Ka ba daa ŋmaligi m‑boosi taaba, ni, Ka sɔ n zi bundirigu na n‑ti u, bee.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, Man bundirigu maa nyɛ la nŋɔa#:- n niŋni Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu yɛla, ka laa n‑tumni u tuma zaa nti naai.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Ya bu nya? - ya yɛtti, ni, gɔya anaasi na kpalim ni kyi kyeebu saha, bee, m ma'ari ni? Amaa n ya yɛli ya ni, ni, ya wukkma ya nini n‑gɔsi puri maa suŋŋa - kyi maa pun bi n‑sakki kyeebu. Ni, pampaŋŋɔ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: ka ŋɔn kyeeri kyi maa deaari la u tuma ligri, ka la'asiri la kyi maa n‑doonni n‑guri nyɔvuri kan ka tariga; lala ka ŋɔn daa buri a maa, ni ŋɔn kyeeri maa gba, nin la'am taaba n‑di nya'asim.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Lala ka yɛligu maa niŋni yɛlimaŋni, ni, Ninvugsɔ n butta, ka sɔ mun n kyeera.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Yarima muna - n daa tum ya ni ya kpɛ puseaa yan na bu tum ka tuma la ni, ni ya ti kyeeri kyi. Ninvugseeba pun tum ka tuma, ka ya mun deaari ba n‑nyari anfaani.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Tɔ, ka tiŋŋa wa Samɛrii niriba pampam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda pɔ'a maa lahabaari zugu, dama u daa yɛli ba, ni, U yɛli ma man daa pun tum tunseesi zaa.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Dizugu Samɛrii dima maa ŋɔn daa kyɛ u saani na maa, ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma m‑bɛ ba saani. Ka Yisa siri daa kpalim bɛni n‑niŋi daba ayi.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Ka ba daadam pam m‑paasi niriba wa zugu daa wum u toori yɛligu ka niŋi u yɛdda di zugu.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Di nyaaŋŋa, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi n‑yakki Galilii mɔ'ari la, seaa kan yuuri laa m‑boonni Taibiriyasi mɔ'ari la.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Ka daa yɛli pɔ'a maa, ni, Pampaŋŋɔ di pa la nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem maa zugu ka ti niŋni yɛdda, amaa ti mammaŋŋa n wum u noori nti baŋŋi, ni, u siri nyɛ la duniya dima Tiligra maa.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Ka daadam pam daa dɔli u, dama ba daa nya ka u tum alahazibu tuntumsa n‑tibri baaridima la.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi u daba ayi bɛni n‑naai, u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Yisa daa du zoori zugu nti zini bɛni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Yisa mammaŋŋa daa pun yɛli, ni, anabi ka gyirima u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Di daa bu yuui ka Yɛhudiya dima maa ya puusi ba Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai Galilii la, niriba maa daa deaai u ni, dama ba gba daa kyaŋŋi la Gyɛrusalɛm nti puusi kyuu maa ka nya ŋɔn tum seem zaa kyuu maa ni.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Ka Yisa daa ti gɔsi n‑nya ka daadam pam daa kyɛnni u saani na, ka u daa boosi Filipi, ni, Ya'akani ka ti yɛ nya bundirigu n‑da n‑diisi daadam pam wa zaasa.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Ka Yisa daa naa n‑labi Galilii Kanna tinkpaŋŋa ni, bɛni ka u daa pun kyɛ ka koom la labgi daam. M‑paai bɛni ka Naaba kpambari sɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u bii daa bɛ la Kapirinyum m‑beera.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Ka Filipi daa yɛli u, ni, Halli ti yi yɛ da bundirigu ka sɔkam zaa deaai beelabeela gba, di nin gaari anzulifa la'akɔba kɔbsiyi.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Ka doo maa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Yisa yi Gyuudiiya m‑paai Galilii na, u daa doaai n‑kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, u kyɛma Kapirinyum na nti tibi bii maa, ni, di kur kpalim la beela ni u kpi.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: U nyaandɔliba maa ni sɔa, Anduru, ŋɔn nyɛ Saimoon Piita sunzɔ la, daa yɛli u, ni.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Yisa mun daa yɛli u, ni, Ya yi bu nya alahazibu tunkara, ya sɔ ku sakki n‑niŋi yɛdda.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Bidibbila m bɛ kpa m‑mari boroboro kpila abanu ni zimi abayi, amaa a mun pun ku sakki niriba wa zaa.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Ka kpambari maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ay, n daana! Kur kyɛm na ka m bibila maa di kpi.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Ya kyɛ ka niriba maa zinni tiŋŋa. . Ka daadam maa zaa daa zinni tiŋŋa; ba daa nyɛ la kaman ninvugtusa anu laasaabu.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Kyɛma! I bii maa ku kpi. Ka doo maa daa niŋi Yisa yɛligu maa yɛdda ka daa labi n‑kuli.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Yisa daa zaŋŋi boroboro maa, ka puusi Naawunni, ka yi puri a n‑tɔri n‑tɔri daadam seeba ban daa zi tiŋŋa maa. Lala ka u daa niŋi zaasim maa gba, ka ba zaa daa di nti sakki ban boori seem.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Ŋɔn daa labri n‑kunni maa, u daa tuusi la u daabseeba soori ni, ka ba daa ti u lahabaari, ni, u bii maa mari alaafɛa.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Ba zaasa ŋɔn daa ti di n‑tigi, Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, ba la'asim dikyɛba an kpalim zaa, ni ba di kyɛ ka halli beela gba basi.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ka u daa boosi ba m‑baŋŋi, ni, saha kani ka bii maa nya sookyi? - ka ba daa labsi, ni, Soosa la wuntaŋŋa, kurigu yinni saha, ka u ningbimbiisim maa sa basi u.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Ka ba daa la'asi bunkyɛba seesi m‑paali peeri pii ni ayi - ban di boroboro kpila abanu maa ni ka kyɛ dikyɛba maa.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Ka doo maa daa teaai, ni, lala saha deede ka Yisa sa yɛli u, ni, I bii maa ku kpi. Di zugu ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Ka niriba ban daa bɛ bɛni maa daa nya Yisa ŋɔn tum makkri tuntumsa wa seem la, ka daa yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni anabisɔ ŋɔn daa yɛ kyɛ duniya ni na la nŋɔa.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Makkri tunkanŋɔ daa paasi la buyi ka Yisa daa tum, ŋɔn daa yi Gyuudiiya n‑kyɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu na maa.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yisa daa pun mi, ni, ba nin kyɛ na m‑mugsi u n‑diisi u naam; dinzugu u daa bakki ka laa n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori maa u kɔkɔa.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti paai, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑sigi mɔ'ari la ni.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu, n‑yakkri mɔ'ari maa n‑kpa Kapirinyum ya'ari sa. Ka yuŋŋu mari nti zibi ka Yisa na bu kyɛ ba saani na.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Lala saha maa, pɛsigu n daa zeera n‑damni koom maa pam.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: U nyaandɔliba maa daa duusi nyariŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi la kaman maala ata‑anaasi laasaabu, ka yi ti nya ka Yisa tɔm koom maa zugu n‑kyɛnni m‑pɔtti nyariŋŋu maa na, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, ka mani nla.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Dizugu ba daa sakki n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kpeesi nyariŋŋu maa puuni; ka nyariŋŋu maa ŋɔn kur ti siisi tingbaŋŋu, ba daa di paai la ban daa wa ya'aseelli maa ni.
