eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa neaai, daadam ban daa kpalim mɔ'aduuri la sa daa nya ka nyariŋŋu yinni kɔkɔ n sa bɛ ni. Ba daa mi suŋŋa, ni, Yisa sa bu la'am n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa u nyaandɔliba saani, kulla nyaandɔliba maa n sa kpɛ m‑bakki.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ nyarinseesi n daa yi Taibiriyasi tiŋŋa n‑kyɛ na, halli nti paai hoo ban sa bɛ ya'aseaa la ni, sahanseaa ka Yisa sa puusi Naawunni boroboro maa zugu n‑ti ba ka ba ŋɔbi la.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Ka zama maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa ka bɛni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba gba, ba daa kpɛ la lala nyarima maa n‑kyaŋŋi Kapirinyum m‑boori Yisa ziiya.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Ka ban daa ti paai u ka u bɛ mɔ'ari ya'ari sa maa, ba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, Ti Ba Rabbi, bɔn saha ka i paai kpa na.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di pa la yan sa nya makkri tunkarigu m‑baŋŋi ka gbinni maa zugu ka ya boori n zii maa, amaa yan sa ŋɔbi paanu maa n‑tigi la zugu ka ya boori ma.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Ka di daa niŋi daba ata ka ba daa ti ya puusi amriya kyuu Galilii tingbaŋŋu tinkpanseaa kan yuuri boonni Kanna la. Yisa ma gba daa bɛ la amriya maa ni.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Yan di tumni m‑boori bundiriseelli din bu yuura, amaa ya tumma m‑bɔ bundiriseelli din nin kpalim m‑paai nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Lala bundirigu maa ka Ninsaala Bii maa nin zaŋŋi n‑tisira. Dama ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn pun daalim n‑wulisi, ni, u nyɛ la u dinni.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa booli Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba gba ni ba bɛ amriya maa ni.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Ka ti ya niŋi la wula n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn boori ni ti tum seem.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Ka amriya yiri dima bunnyuuriseelli ban daa nyuuri maa daa ti pɔsigi. Ka Yisa ma daa yɛli u, ni, ba laa n‑ka daam.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Naawunni ŋɔn boori yan tum seem nŋɔa#:- ya niŋma yɛdda n‑niŋi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa tum na maa.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, M ma, n nuu bɛ la i yɛttɔ'a wa ni? Ay! - n saha na bu paai.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, Alahazibu bɔ ka i ya tum ka ti nya ka niŋi i yɛdda? Bɔ ka i ya tum?.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: U ma mun daa yɛli yiri maa biisi, ni, U yi ti yɛli ya, ni, ya niŋi seem, ya niŋma lala.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Ti yaadima ban daa bɛ yoo ni sahanseaa la, ba daa diri la manna - kaman Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli seem, ni, U daa kyɛ ka paanu yi arizana ni na ka ba di. .

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiya dima zaaligu daa wulisi, ni, ba mari koom n‑zaali ba wungyeeŋŋu kaari ningbimpeaari zugu; ka lala zugu kɔsinsi ayoobu daa zɛ bɛni, bunkara.

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di pa la Musa n daa ti ya arizana ni paanu maa, amaa di nyɛ la m Ba Naawunni n tiri ya arizana ni paanu ni yɛlimaŋni maa.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa ti yɛli yiri maa biisi, ni, ba gbeaama koom na m‑paali sinsi maa. Ka ba daa gbeaai na m‑paali a zaa.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn tisiri paanuseelli maa nyɛ la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yi arizana ni na n‑zaŋŋi nyɔvuri n‑tiri duniya dima maa.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Ka u yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, pampaŋŋɔ ya gbeaam m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti ŋɔn su kyuu yɛla maa. Ka ba daa niŋi lala.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ka ba daa susi u, ni, Ka dinŋɔna, ti Bapira, kur zaŋŋma paanu wa n‑ti ti sahakam.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Ka kyuu maa daana daa deaai n‑nyu beela, ka di daa pun labgi daam; doo maa daa bu baŋŋi daam maa ŋɔn yi seelli na, amaa biseeba ban daa gbeaai bu na la daa mi. Di saha ka kyudaana maa daa booli pɔ'apaalli sira maa na.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, Mani n nyɛ paanu wa kan tiri nyɔvuri la. Ŋɔnkam kyɛ n saani na - kɔm ku laa n‑gbaai u; ka ŋɔnkam mun niŋi ma yɛdda - kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai u yaasa.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: nti yɛli u, ni, I niŋi la wula? Dama niriba yi daŋŋi n‑yiisiri daseebu bun nya'asa n‑tisiri zama maa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi bumpɛtti n‑ti ba, ban pun nyu n‑tigi sahanseaa la. Nyin mun na bu yiisi bunsuŋŋu wa na halli ni pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Amaa m pun yɛli ya, ni, ya pun nya ma amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Dinŋɔ n nyɛ alahazibu tunseelli Yisa ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑tum n‑wulisi u yiiko ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem la. Galilii tingbaŋŋu tinkpanseelli kan boonni Kanna ka u daa tum alahazibu tumni maa. Ka u nyaandɔliba daa ti niŋi u yɛdda di zugu.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: M Ba ŋɔn zaŋŋi sɔ zaa n‑ti ma la nin kyɛ n saani na. Man mun ku kari sɔ zaa ŋɔn kyɛ n saani na;.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Kapirinyum, ŋɔn ni u ma, ni u sunzɔppa, ni u nyaandɔliba. Ka ba daa paai nti niŋi dabsa ayi bɛni.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: dama man bu yi arizana ni n‑sigi na ni n tum la m maŋtoori yuubu, amaa Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu ka n tumna.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Sahanseaa ka di daa ti pɔtti Yɛhudiyadima maa Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu puusigu la saha, ka Yisa daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Ka Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu maa nŋɔa#:- ni, m mari Ŋɔn ti ma bunseelikam; ni, n di kyɛ ka sɔ zaa baasi yɔri, amaa ka n kyɛ ka sɔkam yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: nti kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑nya ban daa diri daa bɛni. Baseeba daa koosiri nigi ni peesi ni ŋmana, maaligu zugu, ka seeba mun daa zintiri n‑takkri ligri, lampoo maa zugu.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Yaasa, Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu maa nŋɔa#:- ni, ŋɔnkam nya Naawunni Bii maa ka niŋi u yɛdda la nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Di saha ka u daa zaŋŋi miisi m‑mili a taaba ka mari n‑feebri ba zaa n‑katti n‑yiisiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka laa n‑kari ba bunkɔbri, ka daai la'atakkriba teebulidima ka kyɛ ka ba ligri maa zaa kpaai m‑basi.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Ka Yɛhudiya dima wa daa piili n‑nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋni u, u yɛligu zugu, ŋɔn yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la paanuseelli din yi arizana ni n‑sigi na maa.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Ka daa yɛli ŋmankoosiriba, ni, ba mari anŋɔ zaa n‑kyaŋŋi, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka m Ba Naawunni Yiri leebi daa.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Ka ba yɛtti taaba, ni, Pa Gyoosɛfi bii Yisa n nyɛ u? Ti mi u ba ni u ma - ka wula ka u yɛtti, ni, u yi la arizana ni na.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba daa teaaya din daa sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, Nimbirimoaa i Yiri maa zugu mari ma n‑daamna.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya kyɛli ya kparima nyuriŋŋu wa!.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Di saha maa ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli u, ni, Alahazibu tumni bɔ ka i nin tum n‑wulisiri ti, ni, soori bɛ ni, ni i niŋni anŋɔ zaa.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Sɔ ku toaai n‑kyɛ n saani na, di yi pa la m Ba ŋɔn tum ma na maa ti booli u n‑ti ma na, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, ya wurimma Yiri wa m‑basi, ka n nin laa n‑labi m‑mɛ ka n‑zaali, daba ata saha.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Anabidima maa sɔppu ni di daa sɔbiya, ni, Naawunni n yɛ wulisi sɔkam zaa. Ŋɔn kam zaa wum n‑tisi u Ba Naawunni ka baŋŋi u wulisigu wa gbinni nin kyɛ n saani na.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, Yuuma pisinaasi ni ayoobu ka ba daa zaŋŋi m‑mɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa, ka nyin ti yɛli, ni, i nin mɛ ka dabsa ata saha, fa.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Dinŋɔ bu wulisi, ni, sɔ na mi n‑nya Naawunni ti Ba maa; ŋɔn bɛ Naawunni saani la kɔkɔ n na mi n‑nya u Ba maa.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Amaa u ningbina yɛla ka u daa yɛtti maa, n‑ŋmansi hoo Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: N yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ŋɔn kam zaa niŋi yɛdda mari nyɔvuriseaa kan ka tariga.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti neaai kum ni la nyaaŋŋa sahanseelli la ka u nyaandɔliba daa naayi teaai, ni, u daa pun yɛli ba yɛlli wa n‑zaali. Ka dizugu ba daa ti niŋi yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni ŋɔn Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri boroboro.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu puusigu saha la, niriba pam daa niŋi u yɛdda, ban daa nya alahazibu tunseesi ŋɔn daa wulisiri la zugu.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Ya yaadima daa di la manna maa yoo la ni, amaa ka daa kpiya.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Yisa mun daa bu niŋi ba yɛdda, dama u daa baŋŋi ba zaa yɛla.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Amaa boroboroseaa kan yi arizana ni n‑sigi na maa nŋɔa; ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi ka ku kpi yaasa.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: U daa bu sakki m‑basi u maŋŋa n‑ti ba, dama u daa pun mi din bɛ ninsaala kam sufu ni, ka pa ni sɔ yɛli u.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri boroboro kan yi arizana ni na maa. Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi lala boroboro maa, u nin bɛ ni halli saha kan ka naari. Boroboroseaa man ŋɔn tisiri maa nyɛ la n ningbiŋŋu, ka n zaŋŋni n‑tisira, din nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima ti nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka Yɛhudiyadima maa piili la sugyeaa nangbankpeemni ba sunsukkuni. Ka ba boosiri taaba, ni, U ya niŋi la wula n‑zaŋŋi u ningbina n‑ti ti ka ti ŋɔbi.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, Man yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ya yi bu ŋɔbi Ninsaala Bii ningbiŋŋu numni ka nyu u ziim la, ya ku nya nyɔvuri ya mammansi ni.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu numni ka nyu n ziim maa mari nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Ɔlivii Zoori luga ni.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Dama n ningbina n nyɛ bundirimaŋni; ka n ziim maa nyɛ bunnyuurimaŋni.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Asuba bɛkyikyeoo ka Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka niriba maa zaa daa la'asi n‑gyiligi u na, ka u daa zinni tiŋŋa m‑piili n‑wulisiri ba.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu wa ka nyu n ziim wa, di daama bɛ la ma ni ka man gba bɛ u ni.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Di saha ka zaaligu karimbadima ni Farisii dima daa pun gbaai pɔ'asɔ ka u bɛ zina tumpu ni. Ka ba daa mari pɔ'a maa n‑kyɛ na, ka zaali u ba zaasa tooni.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: M Ba sɔ ŋɔn vu maa n tum ma na, u zugu ka n bɛ n nyɔvuri ni. Ka lala n nyɛ di n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu maa - ŋɔn gba nin bɛ u nyɔvuri ni man zugu.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Ti Karimba, nyama! Pɔ'a wa ka ba gbaai ka u bɛ zina tumpu ni.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Tɔ, kanŋɔ nyɛ la boroboroseaa kan yi arizana ni n‑sigi na; di pa la ya yaadima ŋɔn daa ŋɔbi boroboro ka daa kpi la taabu. Ŋɔn kam ŋɔbi boroboro wa nin bɛ ni halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Ti zaaligu la puuni, Musa yɛliya lala taabu pɔ'a yɛla, ni, ba zaŋŋma kuga n‑lɔbi u nti ku u. Ka nyin mun yɛli la wula.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wulisiri Kapirinyum la'aŋŋu duu la ni, ka u daa tɔ'asi dinŋɔa.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Ba daa yɛli lala ni ba zamsi Yisa n‑nya yɛttɔ'aseaa din nin kyɛ ka ba galim u. Amaa ka Yisa daa sunni u zugu ka mari u nubila n‑sɔbri n‑niŋni tiŋŋa.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: U nyaandɔliba maa pam daa wum dinŋɔ ka yɛli, ni, Wulisigu wa kpeema pam nti ya'ai. Ŋɔni n yɛ n‑toaai n‑wum n‑ti ka.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Ban daa zɛ bɛni ka boosiri u boosa maa, u daa wukki u zugu ka yɛli ba, ni, Ya ninvugsɔ ŋɔn na zi n‑tum ala'akyi zaa, di daama ŋɔn daŋŋma tooni n‑zaŋŋi kugri n‑lɔbi u.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Ba daa bu yɛli u, amaa Yisa daa pun mi, ni, ba nyurimni la u yɛttɔ'a wa zugu, ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Kala, dinŋɔ zugu ka ya gba boori ya labi nyaaŋŋa?.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Tɔ, ka u daa laa n‑sunni u zugu yaasa ka sɔbri n‑niŋni tiŋŋa.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Tɔ, ka ya yi da nya ka Ninsaala Bii labri ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ya'aseaa la, fa?.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Ban daa ti wum dinŋɔa, ba zaa daa bakkiya n‑kyaŋŋi kpaŋŋa, halli ka ba kpamma maa n daa daŋŋi bakkri. Di daa kpalim la Yisa kɔkɔa, ka pɔ'a maa na kur kpalim n‑zɛ bɛni.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Bunseelli din tisiri nyɔvuri nyɛ la Naawunni Siiya Kasi: ninsaala yiiko ka tunsɛseaa halli beela. Yɛttɔ'aseesi man tɔ'asi n‑ti ya maa nyɛ la Sii maa yɛla n‑tiri nyɔvuri na.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Ka u daa wukki u zugu ka boosi pɔ'a maa, ni, Ba lee? Sɔsɔ bu laa n‑kpalim ni u galim i.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Amaa ni di zaa yɔri ya seeba bu niŋi yɛdda.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa yɛli, ni, M Ba, sɔ bu kpalim. Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, Man gba bu ya n‑galim i. Kyɛma, amaa di laa n‑tumni tumbeeri yaasa.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli m‑paasi, ni, Nŋɔ zugu ka n yɛli ya, ni, sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑kyɛ n saani na ka di pa ni m Ba maa n tisi soori ka u niŋi lala.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba maa yaasa, ni, Mani n nyɛ duniya wa neesim. Ŋɔn kam zaa dɔli ma nin ti nya nyɔvuri neesim, ka ku laa m‑bɛ zibsim puuni n‑kyɛnna.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka u nyaandɔliba maa pam daa ŋmaligi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑labi ka bu laa n‑dɔli u yaasa.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Farisii dima maa daa yɛli u, ni, Ay! Nyin mammantoori sɛhara ka i dira; nyin ŋɔn tɔ'asi seem wa ka gbinni.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la, ni, Ka yarima muna, ya nin boori ya gba bakki.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, Ay! Halli man yi pun diri m mammantoori sɛhara maa zaa yɔri, man yɛtti seem maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, dama m mi man yi ya'aseelli na, ni man wa ya'aseela. Yarima mun bu mi man yi seelli na, bee, man wa seela.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Ka Saimoon Piita yɛli, ni, N dugma, ŋɔni saani ka ti ya kyaŋŋi? Nyini m mari yɛliseesi an tisiri nyɔvuri kan bu naara.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Ya katti la sariya n‑dɔli la ya ninsaala soori; man mun bu katti sɔ sariya.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Ka tirim mun yi niŋi yɛdda m‑baŋŋi, ni, nyini n nyɛ ninvugkasisɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Amaa n yi da ya n‑kari sariya, n sariya karibu maa da yɛ nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, dama pa la n kɔkɔ m‑bɛ kpa wa; m Ba daa tum ma na, ka ŋɔna n bɛ n saani.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, ni, Mani n gamsi yarima ninvugpiiya ni ayi wa, bee m ma'ai ni? Di zaa yɔri, ya yinni nyɛ la Sintaani nira.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Di mun pun sɔbi ya zaalikpana la ni, ni, niriba ayi yi di sɛhara ka ba yɛla dɔli la taaba deede, dinŋɔna ban yɛli seem la nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni maa, u daa ti nya dɔsɔ ban daa dɔ'ai ka u pun nyɛ zooma.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: U daa tɔ'asiri la Saimoon Isikariyɔti bii Gyuudasi yɛla. Dama, halli ni ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ni yinni maa zaa yɔri, ŋɔna n daa nyɛ ŋɔn ti ya figsi u maa.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Tɔ: ka mani mammaŋŋa n nyɛ n sɛhara daana, ka M Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum ma na maa gba nyɛ n sɛhara daana yinni.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: U nyaandɔliba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, Ti Karimba, ŋɔni tumbeeri n‑kyɛ ka ba dɔ'ai u zooma wa? Di daa nyɛ la u mammantoori tumbeeri, bee, u ba ni u ma tumbeeri zugu.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa Yisa daa gɔ n‑gyiligi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni. U daa bu boori u gɔ n‑gyiligi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni, dama Yɛhudiya dima maa daa boori la ba ku u.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ŋɔn n zoom seem maa pa la u tumbeeri zugu, ka laa m‑pa la u badima tumbeeri zugu. Ay! - Ŋɔn zoom maa muna - din nin kyɛ ka Naawunni yiiko ti yi paalu u zugu.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Ka ba boosi u, ni, I ba maa lee? Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya bu mi ma, ya mun bu mi m Ba maa. Ya yi da mi ma, ya da naa m‑baŋŋi m Ba maa gba.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Yɛhudiya dima Suga Kyuu saha daa ti pɔtta.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Din na nyɛ wuntaŋŋa saha wa, faasee ka ti tum n‑tisi Ŋɔn tum ma na maa; yuŋŋu yi ti zibi sɔsɔ ku ti laa n‑toaai n‑tum.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Yisa daa tɔ'asi dinŋɔ maa zaa Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, duseaa piini adakka la ŋɔn daa zɛya la ni. Sɔsɔ mun daa bu gbaai u, dama u saha maa daa na bu paai.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: ka Yisa sunzɔppa maa daa yɛli u, ni, Kur kyɛli nyin bɛ ya'aseaa wa ka kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni; lala ka i nyaandɔliba maa nin nya tunseesi nyin yi niŋni maa.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Man ŋɔn na bɛ duniya wa ni maa, mani n nyɛ duniya neesim.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba yaasa, ni, N nin ti bakki kpaŋŋa ka kyɛ ya; ya nin boori n ziiya, amaa ya nin ti kpi ya mammantoori tumbeeri tumpu puuni. Ya ku nyaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man wa ya'aseaa maa.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi boori ni niriba baŋŋi u yɛla, u bu sɔ'ari n‑tumna. I yi ya tum anŋɔ taabu, kur kyɛ ka duniya zaa nya.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti yɛli nŋɔ maa nyaaŋŋa, Yisa daa tusi nantoori m‑basi tiŋŋa ka gyɛri n‑gyɛri tanni ni nantoori maa. U daa zaŋŋi tanni maa n‑tari n‑tari doo maa nini.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli, ni, Ni, ti ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn wa ya'aseelli maa. Dinŋɔ wulisiya, ni, u yɛ n‑ku la u mammaŋŋa, bee.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: Halli u sunzɔppa maa sɔsɔ daa bu niŋi u yɛdda gba.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: ka yɛli u, Kyɛma nti peaai i nindaa Sailɔ'am beeŋŋu la ni. : ka doo maa daa kyaŋŋi nti peaai u nindaa maa ka labi na ka u nini nyara.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya nyɛ la tiŋni wa dima, amaa man yi la saazugu sa na. Yarima yi la duniya wa ni na, amaa man mun bu yi duniya wa ni na.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Dizugu ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, N saha maa na bu paai. Yarima muna - sahaseaa zaa niŋi la deede n‑ti ya.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Tɔ, ka u ziziitaaba daa ti nya u, ban ni niriba ban daa pun nya u ka u daa susira pɔi ka dinŋɔ daa ti niŋi maa. Ka ba daa boosi, ni, pa doo wa n yi zi tiŋŋa n‑susiri la?.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Lala zugu ka n pun yɛli ya, ni, ya ti ya n‑kpi la ya tumbeeri tumpu puuni. Ya yi bu ti niŋi yɛdda, ni, Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa, ya siri nin ti kpi ya tumbeeri puuni.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Duniya maa ku toaai n‑yaai ya; amaa man mun ka duniya maa yaai, dama man yi yɛtti ka, ni, ka sɔya bu veeli maa.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Seeba daa yɛliya, ni, Ŋɔna nla, amaa ka seeba mun daa yɛli, ni, Ay, pa ŋɔna nla; u kur ŋmani u ni. Ka doo maa mammaŋŋa daa ti yɛli, ni, Ay! - Mani nla.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔni? Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, Man n daa yɛli ya seem maa n‑yi piiligu la ni na.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya kyɛma Kyuu maa ni. Man bu wa Kyuu wa ni, dama saha maa bu paai ma na.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Ka di kyaŋŋi la wula ka i nini yi nɛ pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Ya zugu m mari yɛla pampam n ya n‑tɔ'asi, m mari la yɛla pam ni n galim ya. Ŋɔn tum ma na maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka n yɛtti la man wum seem u saani maa n‑tiri duniya wa dima.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: U daa yɛli nŋɔa, ka daa kpalim m‑bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Dɔsɔ ban boonni Yisa la n daa niŋi tanni n‑tari n‑tari n nimbiya ka yɛli ma, ni, n kyɛma nti peaai n nindaa Sailɔ'am beeŋŋu la ni. Ka n daa kyaŋŋi, ka man daa ti peaai n nindaa maa n‑naai, n di neaai ni.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ba daa bu baŋŋi, ni, Yisa yɛtti la u Ba yɛla.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Amaa u sunzɔppa maa ŋɔn daa ti gaari Kyuu maa ni, Yisa gba daa kyaŋŋiya. U daa bu yi paalu ni, amaa ka sɔ'ai ni.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ka ba boosi u, ni, U bɛ la yani? Ka u labsi n‑yɛli, ni, N bu mi.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Dinzugu u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya yi zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii maa n‑duusi saazugu, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa; di saha maa ka ya nin ti baŋŋi, ni, n bu niŋni bunsɛseela m mammantoori yiiko ni, amaa n yɛtti la yɛttɔ'aseesi m Ba maa ŋɔn wulisi ma ni n yɛlim seem maa.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Tɔ; ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa boori u ziiya Kyuu maa ni, ka daa boosiri taaba, ni, U lee.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa zi dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ zooma maa n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima saani.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Ŋɔn tum ma na la bɛ la n saani; u bu kyɛ ma m‑basi n kɔkɔa, dama n tumni n‑tigri la u nini saha kam zaa.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Ka daadam maa daa sɔ'ai n‑tɔ'asiri taaba n‑yɛtti u yɛla halli n‑ŋmeeri taaba nangbankpeemni, dama baseeba daa yɛtti, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu, ka seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Ay! Di pa lala - u yɔmsiri la niriba.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Dindahinseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi tanni n‑tibi doo la zoonsi maa daa nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri daari.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: Niriba pam ban daa wum Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem wa daa niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Amaa sɔsɔ daa bu yi paalu na n‑tɔ'asi u yɛla, dama ba daa zɔri la Yɛhudiya dima maa dabeem.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Tɔ, ka Farisii dima daa laa m‑boosi u ŋɔn niŋi seem ka u nini maa ti nyara maa yaasa. U yɛli ba, ni, U daa niŋi la tanni n‑niŋi n nimbiya ni; ka n peaai n nindaa maa, ka yi di nyara pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ban daa niŋi u yɛdda maa n‑yɛli, ni, Ya yi sakki n‑deaai n wulisigu maa, ya siri nyɛ la n nyaandɔliba.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Farisii dima seeba daa yɛliya, ni, Dɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi nŋɔ maa ku toaai n‑yi Naawunni saani na, dama u bu dɔli la Vuusim Dabsiri zaaligu. Ka seeba daa yɛli, ni, Bɔ zugu ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ala'akyi daana ti tum alahazibu nŋɔ taabu? Ka ba nɔya daa di wali.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Kyuu maa dabsa purigri daa na paai na pɔi, ka Yisa naayi kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni nti piili bulisigu.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Ya nin baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni maa, ka yɛlimaŋni maa nin ti kyɛ ka ya ti su ya mansi.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ka Farisii dima daa laa m‑boosi doo maa yaasa, ni, I yɛliya, ni, u tibi la i zoonsi maa: tɔ, ka nyin teesiya, ni, u nyɛ la ŋɔni? Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Anabi n nyɛ u.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Ka di daa niŋi Yɛhudiya dima maa alahazibu, ka ba yɛli, Di niŋi la wula ka doo wa mi bukku pam lala, ka sɔ mun na zi n‑wulisi u.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, Ti nyɛ la Ibrahim butiri dima; ti na zi n‑niŋi daaba n‑ti sɔsɔa. I wulisi la bɔa? - nyin ŋɔn yɛtti, ni, Ya nin ti su ya mammansi. .

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Halli gba Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa na bu boori ni ba niŋi yɛdda, ni, u daa nyɛ la zooma ka pampaŋŋɔ n yi nyara, halli ka daa ti booli u ba ni u ma na.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Yisa mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Man n bulisiri seela pa la m mammaŋŋa bulisigu, amaa di yi la Ŋɔn tum ma na la saani na.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, N yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ŋɔn kam zaa tumni tumbeeri nyɛ la daabri n‑ti tumbeeri maa. Ala'akyi n su u.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: nti boosi ba, ni, Ya bii nŋɔa? Ya yɛliya, ni, ya daa pun dɔ'ai u la zooma; tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ di kyaŋŋi la wula ka u nini yi nyara.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Ŋɔn boori ni u niŋi Naawunni ŋɔn boori seela maa, ŋɔna nin baŋŋi man bulisiri seelli maa yɛla, ni, di yi la ŋɔn Naawunni saani na, bee, n tɔ'asiri la m maŋtoori yiiko.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Daabri pa la ŋɔn ya n‑kpalim n‑kur bɛ yiri maa ni, amaa yiri biiya mun nyɛ la ŋɔn ya n‑kpalim n‑kur bɛ yiri maa ni halli sahakam.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: U ba ni u ma maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ti mi, ni, u nyɛ la ti biiya, ka ti laa m‑mi, ni, ti daa pun dɔ'ai u la zooma.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Ya bu nya? - ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri ni u toori yiiko nyɛ la ŋɔn boori pɛ'abu ni gyirima n‑tiri u maŋŋa. Amaa ŋɔn boori pɛ'abu ni gyirima n‑tiri ŋɔn tum u maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka galinsi ka u saani.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Ka Bii maa yi ti ti ya mansuulinsi, dinŋɔna ya siri kur nyɛ la ban su ba mansi ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Amaa ti bu baŋŋi pampaŋŋɔ ŋɔn yi niŋi seem nti nyaŋŋi n‑yi nyara maa, ti mun bu mi ŋɔn tibi u zoonsi maa. Boosim u; u pa bibila, u mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑tɔ'asi n‑labsi.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Ammi, Musa daa ti ya zaaligu maa, bee, u daa bu tisi? Amaa ya sɔsɔ bu sakki n‑dɔli zaaligu maa: wula zugu ka ya boori ni ya ku ma.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: U ba ni u ma maa daa yɛli lala dama ba daa zɔri la Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa dabeem maa, dama ban daa pun naai taaba noori n‑yɛli, ni, sɔ zaa yi ti sakki n‑yɛli, ni, u niŋi yɛdda, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Masiiya maa, ba nin ti yiisi di daama ba la'aŋŋu duu maa ni m‑basi.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: M mi halli, ni, ya nyɛ la Ibrahim butiri ni dima. Amaa di zaa yɔri ya boori la ya ku ma, dama ya bu boori ya sakki n‑deaai n yɛligu.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Ka zama maa daa labsi, ni, I mari la arizimbeoo i ni! Ŋɔni n boori ni u ku i.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Lala maa zugu ka u ba ni u ma maa daa yɛli, ni, u pa bibila; ni, ba boosim u maa.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: N tɔ'asiri la man daa nya m Ba saani yɛliseesi maa, ni ŋɔn wulisi ma seem; amaa ya mun tumni la yan ŋɔn daa wum seem ya toori ba la saani.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Vuusim dabsiri maa ni ka n daa tum makkri tumni yinni ka di daa niŋi ya alahazibu.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Ba daa laa m‑booli ban daa pun dɔ'ai dɔsɔ ka u nyɛ zooma maa ni u ti kyɛ na napɔmbuyi daana yaasa, n‑yɛli u, Yɛlim Naawunni zugu, ni, i nin tɔ'asi yɛlimaŋni! Ti pun mi, ni, dɔsɔ ŋɔn tibi i maa nyɛ la ala'akyi daana.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ti ba n nyɛ Ibrahim. Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya yi da siri nyɛ Ibrahim biisi, ya naa n‑niŋi ŋɔn daa niŋni seem maa.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Amaa Musa daa ti ya bidibsi gunibu kaari - halli di daa pa la Musa n daa piili di, amaa ya yaadima maa - ka di zugu ya zaŋŋni biisi n‑guntira, halli Vuusim Dabsiri daari gba.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Man bu mi, ni, u nyɛ la ala'akyi daana, bee, u pa ala'akyi daana. Yɛlli yinni ka man kur mi: n daa zoomna, ka pampaŋŋɔ n mun nini yi nyara.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Man yɛtti ya seem la zaa kur nyɛ la yɛlimanseaa man wum Naawunni saani la, amaa di zaa yɔri, ya boori la ya ku ma. Ibrahim daa bu niŋi nŋɔ taabu!.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Ba daa boosiya, ni, Bɔ ka u daa dee n‑niŋi i? U daa dee n‑tibi i zoonsi maa la wula.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Tɔ: ka ba yi guni biiya Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa, ni, ba di burigri Musa zaaligu yɛla maa, wula ka ya suuri yisigri n‑niŋni mani, man ŋɔn kyɛ ka nira nya alaafɛ zaa Vuusim Dabsiri daari ni?.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ay! - Ya niŋni la ya ba ŋɔn niŋni seem la. Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni mammaŋŋa kɔkɔ n nyɛ ti Ba, ka ti gba siri nyɛ u biisi - ti pa la ban ka daandima.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: U daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, N daa pun yɛli ya, ka ya daa za'asi wumpu. Wula zugu ka ya laa m‑boori ya wum ka yaasa? Bee, ya gba boori la ya niŋi u nyaandɔliba.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Ya kyɛli birikɔnsi sariya karibu din dɔli ya nini gɔsibu ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ka katti sariyamaŋni.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Naawunni yi da siri nyɛ ya Ba, ya naa n‑wuri ma, dama n yi la Naawunni saani na, ka yi bɛ kpa wa pampaŋŋɔ maa. N daa bu kyɛ na la m mammantoori yiiko puuni, amaa ŋɔna n daa tum ma na.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Ka ba daa di gbaai u tuuri ka yɛli u, Nyini n nyɛ lala doo maa nyaandɔla; amaa tirima ŋɔn mun nyɛ la Musa nyaandɔliba.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Ka Gyɛrusalɛm niriba seeba daa yɛli, ni, Pa doo wa ka kpambaya boori ni ba ku la?.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Bɔ n taasi ka ya bu baŋŋi man yɛtti seem maa gbinni? Di nyɛ la yan bu boori ni ya wum n lahabaari maa zugu.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Ti mun mi, ni, Naawunni daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Musa; amaa lala doo maa ŋɔna, halli ti bu mi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Nyama, u bɛ la zama ni n‑tɔ'asira ka ba bu yɛli sɛseela n‑galim u! Kala ba siri mi, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa?.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Ya nyɛ la ya ba Sintaani biisi, ka ya dɔli la ya ba maa sufu yuubu ŋɔn boori seem zaasa. Piiligu ni sa u daa pun nyɛ la ninvugkuura, ka daa na zi n‑labi yɛlimaŋni ya'ari na, dama yɛlimaŋni ka u ni. U naaŋŋu pun nyɛ u ma'ai ziri, dama ŋɔna n‑nyɛ ziridima zaa ba.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Mma! - alahazibu nŋɔa! Ya bu mi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na, amaa u mun tibi ma n zoonsi ni!.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Amaa Masiiya maa yi ti kyɛ na, sɔsɔ n bu ti yɛ baŋŋi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na. Tirima mun zaasa mi doo wa ŋɔn yi ya'aseelli ni na.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Amaa man tɔ'asiri la yɛlimaŋa, dinzugu ka ya bu niŋi ma yɛdda maa.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Ti mi, ni, Naawunni bu wumni n‑tiri ala'akyi dima, u wumni n‑tiri la ninvugseeba ban tiri u gyirima ka niŋni ŋɔn boori ba tum seem.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wulisiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni la, u daa tɔ'asi ni kukɔtitaari n‑yɛli, ni, Kala ya siri mi ma ka baŋŋi man yi ya'aseelli na? N bu kyɛ na m mammantoori yiiko puuni. Ŋɔn tum ma na maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana. Yarima bu mi u.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Ya ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑wulisi n ala'akyi galinsi ŋɔn nyɛ seem? Ka n yi tɔ'asi yɛlimaŋni, wula zugu ka ya bu niŋni ma yɛdda?.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Ti na zi n‑wum, ni, sɔsɔ neaai ban pun dɔ'ai sɔ zooma nini duniya piiligu ni sa.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: amaa man mi u, dama n yi la u saani na, ŋɔna mun daa tum ma na.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na la - di daama n wumni Naawunni yɛla. Amaa yarima mun bu yi u saani na, ka lala maa zugu kyɛ ka ya za'asi wumpu.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Di yi da pa ni doo maa yi la Naawunni saani na, u naa n‑ku nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi nŋɔ taataabu wa.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Ba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa sɔsɔ daa bu zaŋŋi u nuu n‑siisi u, dama u saha daa na bu paai.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Ka ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, Kala di daa bu dɔli soori tin daa yɛli, ni, i nyɛ la Samɛrii nira ka arizimbeoo bɛ i ni la.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ka ba labsi, ni, Ba daa dɔ'ai i ka i zoaai la ala'akyi ni, ka i mun ti laa m‑boori i wulisi tirima? Ka ba daa kari u n‑yiisi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Amaa daadam maa puuni niriba pampam daa niŋi u yɛdda ka daa yɛli, ni, Kala Masiiya maa yi ti kyɛ na, u nin ti toaai n‑tum alahazibu tuma n‑gaari doo wa ŋɔn tum seem wa.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Arizimbeoo ka ma ni. N tibgri la m Ba maa, amaa yarima n bu tibgri ma.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Farisii dima maa daa wum daadam maa ŋɔn daa waasiri n‑tɔ'asiri Yisa yɛla seem maa. Dinzugu Farisii dima maa ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa tum ba Pirinsi ni ba ti gbaai Yisa.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum bunseela din daa niŋi maa, u daa bɔ doo maa ziiya nti boosi u, ni, Kala i niŋi Ninsaala Bii maa yɛdda.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Man bu boori la m mammantoori tibsim. Amaa ninvugsɔ n bɛ ni m‑boori n tibsim, ka ŋɔna n nyɛ sarikatta maa.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, N yɛ bɛ ya saani la saha beela kɔkɔa, ka n nin ti laa m‑bakki n‑labi Ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa saani.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, M Bapira, yɛlim ma ŋɔn nyɛ ninvugsɔa, ka n nin niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa sakki n‑deaai n wulisigu maa ku kpi halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ya nin ti bɔ n ziiya, amaa ya ku ti nya ma, dama ya ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi man ti yɛ m‑bɛ seela maa.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, I daa pun nya doo maa, halli ka mani, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑tiri i wa pampaŋŋɔ, nla.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, Pampaŋŋɔ ti yi baŋŋiya, ni, i mari la arizimbeoo i ni! Ibrahim daa kpiya, ka anabidima gba daa kpi; ka nyin yɛtti, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa sakki n‑deaai i wulisigu maa kur ku kpi halli saha kan ka naari, fa!.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli taaba, ni, Ya'akani ka u wa ka ti bu yɛ nya u? U ti yɛ n‑kyaŋŋi la ti niriba ŋɔn bɛ Griika tinseesi la ni n‑wulisiri ban, bee?.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Ka doo maa daa di gbaani tiŋŋa Yisa tooni ka yɛli, ni, N niŋi yɛdda, n dugma.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Ti ba Ibrahim daa kpiya, ka anabidima gba daa kpi. Ni, nyini n gaari Ibrahim, bee? Nyin teesiya, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: U yɛliya, ni, ti nin ti bɔ u ziiya ka ku nya u; ni, ti mun ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn ti yɛ bɛ seela maa. Di wulisi la wula.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, N kyɛ duniya wa na ni n ti di la sariya, ka zoomma nini neaai ka ban nini nyari mun leebi zoomma.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, N yi da yɛ tibgi m maŋŋa, lala tibsim maa da yɛ nyɛ la yɔri. Ŋɔn kur tiri ma tibsim nyɛ la m Ba maa, ninvugsɔ yan yɛli, ni, u nyɛ ya Naawunni la.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farisii dima seeba daa bɛ bɛni ka daa wum ŋɔn yɛli seem maa, ka ba boosi u, ni, Yɛlimaŋni, i bu wulisi, ni, tirima gba nyɛ la zoomma, bee.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Kyuu maa naari ni - ka dabsitibsiri maa daari nla - Yisa daa doaai saazugu n‑tɔ'asi ni kukɔtitaari n‑yɛli, ni, Kɔnyuuri yi mari sɔ zaa, u kyɛm n saani na nti nyu.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: Ya na zi m‑baŋŋi u, amaa man ŋɔn mi u. N yi yɛli, ni, n zi u, dinŋɔna n leebi la ziri daana kaman ya taabu. Amaa m mi u, ka sakkri n‑deaari u yɛla.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya yi da nyɛ la zoomma dinŋɔna ya da bu tum taalli; amaa yan yɛtti, ni, ya nini nɛ wa ŋɔna, di wulisiya, ni, ya na bɛ taalli ni.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Kaman Litaafi ŋɔn pun yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda, ni, nyɔvuri kɔpɔbrim nin buli n‑yi u sufu ni na. .

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Ya ba Ibrahim sufu daa maaya pam, ni, ŋɔn ya nya n saha maa; u siri daa nya di ka daa mari sufupeelli pam.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑kpa Sii Kasi yɛla, ni, ban niŋi u yɛdda la nin ti yɛ deaai Sii maa. Lala saha maa Sii Kasi maa daa na bu kyɛ na, dama Yisa daa na bu paai naam gyirima neesim maa ni.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, Halli i na bu paai yuumpisinu gba, ka yɛli, ni, i daa nya Ibrahim.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Daadam maa ni ninvugseeba daa wum ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem maa ka daa yɛli, ni, Doo wa siri nyɛ la anabi maa.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, N siri yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, pɔi ka ba daa dɔ'ai Ibrahim, ni, Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa. .

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Ka seeba daa yɛli, ni, Ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya la! Amaa ka seeba mun yɛli, ni, Masiiya maa bu yɛ yi Galilii na.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa di piisi kuga ni ba lɔbi Yisa, amaa ka Yisa daa sɔ'ai u mammaŋŋa m‑bakki n‑yi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Litaafi yɛliya, ni, Masiiya maa yɛ yi la NaaDauda za'anɔri ni na, ka u ti yi Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa ni na, tinseaa NaaDauda ŋɔn daa bɛ ni la.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Dinzugu nangbanwala daa bɛ daadam maa puuni Yisa zugu.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Ba seeba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa sɔsɔ daa bu zaŋŋi u nuu n‑siisi u.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Pirinsi ŋɔn daa ti labi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa ni Farisii dima maa saani na, ba daa boosi ba, ni, Wula ka ya bu mari u na.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Ka Pirinsi maa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Sɔ na zi n‑tɔ'asi hoo doo maa yɛttɔ'a wa taabu.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Ka Farisii dima maa boosi ba, ni, U na'a yɔmsi ya gba ni?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Ya na mi n‑nya ka kpambaya, bee, Farisii dima ninvugyinni n niŋi u yɛdda?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Ay! - Amaa daadam wa zaa bu mi Musa zaalikpana la ŋɔn nyɛ seem, dinzugu ba bɛ la mbusu ni.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Ninvugtibsa wa yinni ŋɔn daa bɛ bɛni daa nyɛ la Nikɔdiimusi - dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pun kyaŋŋi nti nya Yisa la. U daa yɛli u taaba maa, ni.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Ti zaaligu soori puuni, ti ku toaai n‑tu sɔ pɔi ka naayi yi wum u yɛttɔ'a, bee, ka naayi boosi m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn niŋi seela maa.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, Eeh! - i gba yi la Galilii na, bee? Kur gɔsim Litaafi maa suŋŋa: i nin baŋŋi, ni, anabisɔ bu yɛ yi Galilii na. .

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Tɔ, ka ba sɔkam zaa daa kuli u yiri.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bu dɔli zampakka maa za'anɔri ni n‑kpɛa, amaa u ba ya'aseaa zaa n‑kpɛa, lala doo maa nyɛ la na'ayiga ni fafaara.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Dɔsɔ mun yi dɔli zampakka za'anɔri ni n‑kpeera, ŋɔna n nyɛ peesi maa pɛgura.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Za'anɔgura maa nin yoaai za'anɔri n‑ti u, ka bunkɔbri maa nin wum u kukɔri maa. U nin booli u maŋtoori peesi ba yuya ni, ka ba nin dɔli u ka yi na.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Ba yi dɔli u n‑yi na n‑naai, u nin bɛ ba tooni, ka bunkɔbri maa dɔli u, ba m mi u kukɔri la zugu.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Ba ku dɔli sɔ m‑paasi u zugu: ban na zi dɔsɔ yi ti kpɛ na, ba nin zɔ ka kyɛ u, dama ba bu mi u kukɔri.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yisa daa yɛli yɛttɔ'a wa n‑ti ba, amaa ba daa bu wum di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, man nyɛ la za'anɔta'ariga n‑ti peesi maa.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Sɔkam zaa ŋɔn pun daŋŋi ma na muna - na'ayigsi n daa nyɛ ba, ni fafaariba, amaa peesi maa daa bu wum n‑ti ba.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Mani n nyɛ za'anɔri maa. Sɔkam yi dɔli man saani na n‑kpɛ nin nya tiliginsim. U nin kpeeri ka laa n‑yiiri na n‑nya moori n‑ŋɔbra.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Na'ayiga maa muna - ŋɔn yi kpeeri ni na ni u ti zu peesi maa, ka kɔri ba ka sa'am. Man mun kyɛnni na ni ya ti nya nyɔvuri - nyɔvuri din gaari zaa.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Mani n nyɛ pɛgurisuŋŋu maa, ka nin zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri n‑takki m peesi maa dina.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Ba yi ti yɔ tuntumnisɔ ni u tum pɛgura tuma, amaa u pa la pɛgurisɔ ŋɔn su peesi maa la, di daama yi nya ka kunduŋŋu kyɛnni na, u nin kyɛ peesi maa ka zɔa. Ka kunduŋŋu maa nin gbaai bunkɔbsɔ, ka kyɛ ka ban kpalim maa zɔ n‑wurigi.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Doo wa tumni la tuma maa ligri zugu, ka u ba'a ka peesi maa yɛla ni, di zugu ka u nin zɔ ka kyɛ ba.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Mani n nyɛ pɛgurisuŋŋu maa. M mi man su seeba maa, ka ba gba mi ma; hoo m Ba Naawunni ŋɔn mi ma suŋŋa, ka man mun mi u gba. Ka man nin zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri n‑takki m peesi dina.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: Halli pɛseeba m bɛ ni n‑nyɛ n dina, amaa ka ba ka zampakka wa puuni. Faasee ka m mari ba na m‑paasi, ka ba nin ti wumni n kukɔri maa, ka ba zaa la'am m‑bɛ luginyinni ka ba pɛgura yinni kɔkɔ n su ba.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Lala zugu ka m Ba wuri ma, dama n sakki ni n zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri n‑dooli, ni n ti deaai di yaasa.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Sɔ bu faari n nyɔvuri n saani: man zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi, ka man nin laa n‑toaai n‑deaai ka yaasa. Nŋɔ ka m Ba pun zaali ma ni n niŋi.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Tɔ; ka nangbanwala daa laa m‑bɛ niriba maa sunsukkuni yaasa, Yisa yɛligu wa zugu.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Baseeba n daa yɛtti, ni, u mari la arizimbeoo ka yinyaari gbaai u, ni, Wula zugu ka ya laa n‑wumni u yɛligu.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Baseeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, Arizimbeoo daana ku toaai n‑tɔ'asiri nŋɔa! Arizimbeoo ya niŋi la wula n‑kyɛ ka zoomma nini laa n‑neaara.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Di daa nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Piiligu Kyuu, ka ba puusi ka waari saha maa.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa bɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la ni, n‑gyinni NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa la puuni.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Di saha maa ka niriba maa daa gyiligi u ka boosi u, ni, Bɔn saha ka i nin yiisi ti birisigu ni? Kur yɛlim ti paalu ni, nyin nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti di Masiiya naam ka fa ti m‑basi, bee.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, M pun yɛli ya, amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda. Halli tunseesi man zaŋŋni m Ba yuuri n‑tumni maa, ana n wulisiri n yɛla.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda, dama ya pa la n peesi maa.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: M peesi la n wumni n kukɔri maa; m mi ba, ka ba yi dɔli ma.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: N tiri ba nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka ba ku baasi yɔri halli saha kan ka tariga, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑fa ba man saani.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: M Ba maa pun zaŋŋi ba n‑ti ma, ka ŋɔna n galisi n‑gaari seelikam, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑fa ba m Ba saani.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Man ni m Ba maa - ti nyɛ la yimmu.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Lala saha ka niriba maa daa laa m‑piisi kuga ni ba lɔbi Yisa.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, N daa niŋi alahazibu tunsuma pam, ka ya nini nya, tunseesi ka m Ba Naawunni daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma ni n tumni a ni. Tunsuma wa muna - kani zugu ka ya boori ni ya lɔbi ma kuga.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Pa bunsunseaa zugu ka ti ya lɔbi i, amaa nyin ŋɔn tu Naawunni zugu. Ninsaala n kur nyɛ i, ka i boori la i zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑labgi Naawunni.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Ka Yisa labsi ba n‑yɛli, ni, Di sɔbi n‑dooli ya mammaŋŋa zaaligu sɔppu ni, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni, ya nyɛ la Naawuna; bee, m ma'ari ni?.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Tɔ; ti mi halli, ni, kariŋŋu maa yi yɛli seelli, di siri nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna: ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asi n‑ti baseeba ban daa deaai u yɛligu la, ni, Naawuna n nyɛ ba.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: Ka man ŋɔna - m Ba maa n yiisi ma ka tum ma ni n kyɛ duniya wa ni na: ka man ŋɔn yɛtti, ni, Naawunni Bii n nyɛ ma, wula ka ya nin toaai n‑yɛli, ni, n tuuri la Naawunni?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: N yi bu tumni tunseesi m Ba ŋɔn boori ni n tumna - tɔ, dinŋɔna yan di niŋi ma yɛdda.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Amaa n yi tumni a ni, halli ya bu niŋni ma yɛdda, ya nin toaai n‑niŋi tuma maa yɛdda, ka lala zugu ka ya nin mi ka baŋŋi di gbinni, ni, m Ba maa bɛ la n saani, ka man gba bɛ u saani.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Ka dizugu ba daa laa m‑boori ni ba gbaai u, amaa u daa liisi n‑yi ba nuusi ni yaasa.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑yakki Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la yaasa, nti paai ya'aseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa yi daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom la. Bɛni ka Yisa daa kpalim n‑gbɛa.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Ka niriba pam daa kyɛ u saani na ka yɛli, ni, Gyɔni daa bu tum alahazibu tunseelli, amaa ŋɔn daa pun yɛli yɛliseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi doo wa yɛla daa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Ka niriba pam daa niŋi u yɛdda bɛni.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Lazarusi; Bɛtani nira n daa nyɛ u. Ka lala saha maa u daa beeri ni. Bɛtani wa ka Mariama ni u beeri Maata gba daa bɛ ni.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Mariama mun daa nyɛ la Lazarusi sunzɔpɔ'a: halli, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa niŋi turaali n‑yɔ'asi ti dugma Yisa gbaya la, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u zɔbwɔ'ala n‑nyeesi - ŋɔn Mariama n‑daa la.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Tɔ: ka pɔ'aba ayi wa n daa tum Yisa saani na, nti yɛli u, ni, Ti dugma, i zɔ maa beeri ni.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, u daa yɛli, Ay! Dooru wa ku toaai n‑nyɛ u kum dooru; dinŋɔ niŋiya ni di kyɛ ka Naawunni yuuri ti du, dama Naawunni Bii maa nin nya gyirima di zugu.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Yisa daa wuri Maata ni u sunzɔ la, ni ba sunzɔdoo maa m‑paasi.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa wum, ni, Lazarusi ka alaafɛ maa, u daa kpalim ŋɔn daa bɛ luginseelli maa n‑niŋi la daba ayi yaasa.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Ka daa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, Ya kyɛ ka ti labi Gyuudiiya.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa labsi u, ni, Ti Karimba, saha beela din daa gaari la ka bɛni niriba maa daa ya lɔbi i la kuga. Kala! I na boori la i labi bɛni.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Yisa mun daa yɛli ba, ni, Wuntaŋŋa ni ka neesim bɛ ni n‑niŋi hawa pii ni ayi, bee wula? Dizugu ŋɔnkam kyɛnni wuntaŋŋa ni bu tuura, dama u nini n nya duniya wa neesim maa.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Ŋɔn mun kyɛnni yuŋŋu saha, u tuuri ni maa, dama neesim ka u saani.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ti zɔ gbisiri ni, see ka ti kyaŋŋi ni ti neaai u.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: U nyaandɔliba mun daa yɛli u, Ti dugma, u yi gbisira, u nin nya alaafɛa, bee bɔa.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Tɔ: Yisa daa wulisiya, ni, Lazarusi kpi ni; ba mun daa mi, ni, u tɔ'asiri la hoo sɔ n gbisiri gɔmaŋni la.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Dizugu Yisa daa kasigi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Lazarusi kpi ni;.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: ni, Amaa yarima zugu ka n sufu maai pam, tin daa ka u saani maa zugu, dama lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ya niŋi yɛdda. Tɔ - ti doaama n‑kyaŋŋi u saani.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Ka Tɔmaasi, ban daa boonni sɔ Gyeaa la, daa yɛli u nyaandɔlitaaba maa, ni, Tɔ! - ka dinŋɔna ti dɔla n‑kyaŋŋi, ka tin ni ŋɔna nin la'am n‑kpi.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yisa ŋɔn ti paai na maa, ba daa yɛli u, ni, ba pun sɔ'ai Lazarusi di daba anaasi.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ: Gyɛrusalɛm zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtani bu paai maala ayi.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: ka Yɛhudiya dima pam daa kyɛ na ni ba ti kaai Maata ni Mariama ka kpamsi ba sufu zaŋŋi n‑kpa ba sunzɔdoo maa kum zugu.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Maata ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Yisa wa ni na, u daa doaaya n‑yi n‑tuusi u soori, amaa Mariama ŋɔn daa kpalim m‑bɛ la yiri maa ni.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Maata daa yɛli Yisa, ni, N dugma, i yi daa da bɛ kpa, n sunzɔdoo maa naa n‑ku kpi!.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Amaa n pun mi, ni, halli pampaŋŋɔ gba Naawunni nin tum nyin susi u bunseelli kam zaasa n‑ti i.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, I sunzɔdoo maa nin yisigi kum ni n‑labi nyɔvuri ni yaasa.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Ka u yɛli, ni, M pun mi, ni, Zaadaari ka u nin ti yisigi u kum ni.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Mani n‑nyɛ ŋɔn neaari kum; nyɔvuri gba n nyɛ ma. Ŋɔn kam zaa niŋi ma yɛdda na nin bɛ ni, halli u yi pun kpi gba;.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: ka ŋɔn kam zaa bɛ ni ka niŋi ma yɛdda ku kpi halli saha kan ka naari. Ammi, i niŋi dinŋɔ yɛdda.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Ka u yɛli u, ni, Ii, n dugma! N niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n‑nyɛ Masiiya - Naawunni bisɔ ŋɔn daa yɛ kyɛ duniya wa ni na nla.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maata ŋɔn daa yɛli wa n‑naai, u daa labi nti booli u sunzɔpɔ'a Mariama kpaŋŋa n‑yɛli u, ni, Ti Karimba la kyɛnni na, ka boosiri i yɛla.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mariama ŋɔn daa ti wum di, u daa doaai saazugu n‑kyaŋŋi yoomayooma n‑yi ni u ti tuusi u soori.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Tɔ, ka niriba pam daa bɛ yiri maa ni n‑kpamsiri Mariama sufu maa, ka ba daa ti nya ka u doaai yooma n‑yi, ka ba gba daa doaai n‑dɔli u n‑yi na. Ba daa teesiya, ni, u wa la gballi maa ni, ni u ti kum.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Mariama daa paai Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ ya'aseaa maa, ka ŋɔn daa ti nya u, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa Yisa nɔba ni ka yɛli, ni, N dugma, i yi daa da bɛ kpa, n sunzɔdoo maa naa n‑ku kpi.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yisa daa nya Mariama ka u kumna, ka niriba ban paasi u zugu maa gba daa kumna; ka di daa niŋi Yisa yɔri ka u sufu sa'am.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Ka Yisa boosi ba, Ya'akani ka ya sɔ'ai u? Ka ba yɛli Yisa, ni, Ti Bapira, kyɛm na nti nya.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Yisa daa kumiya.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Ka niriba maa yɛli, ni, Nyama ŋɔn wuri Lazarusi seem.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Amaa ka ba seeba daa yɛli, ni, U daa neaai zoomma nini, bee, u daa bu niŋi lala? Kala u naa n‑ku toaai n‑tuligi Lazarusi kum maa ni.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: U sufusa'aŋŋu maa puuni, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni, ka ka daa nyɛ la tampifeoo ka ba zaŋŋi kugri n‑ligi ka noori.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, Ya yiisima kugri maa kpaŋŋa! Ka Maata, dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa kpi maa sunzɔpɔ'a la, daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, N dugma, poosigu nin bɛ ni, ban sɔ'ai u di daba anaasi nŋɔa.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, N da pun bu yɛli i, ni, i yi niŋi yɛdda i nin nya Naawunni naam gyirima ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Ba daa biligi kugri maa n‑tiili kpaŋŋa. Ka Yisa gɔsi saazugu ka yɛli, ni, M Ba, n puusiri i, nyin pun wum n‑ti ma zugu.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: M mi, ni, i wumni n‑tiri ma saha kam, amaa n tɔ'asi dinŋɔ la niriba ban zɛ kpa wa zugu, din yɛ kyɛ ka ba niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n siri tum ma na.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: Ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi wa n‑naai maa, u daa tasiya ni kukɔtitaari m‑booli, ni, Lazarusi, yim na.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Ka u daa yi na, ka kasiŋŋa vili n‑vili u nuusi ni u gbaya, ka tanseaa mun vili u nindaa ni. Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Lɔrigma u ka kyɛ ka u kyaŋŋi.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Niriba pam ban daa kyɛ na ni ba ti kaai Mariama maa daa nya Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa, ka ba daa niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Amaa ba seeba daa labi nti yɛli Farisii dima Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Dizugu Farisii dima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa zini n‑tɔ gyina ka yɛli, ni, Ti yɛ niŋi la wula? Nyama alahazibu tuntunseesi doo wa ŋɔn tumna!.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Ti yi kyɛ ka u na kpalim n‑tumni lala, sɔkam zaa nin ti niŋi u yɛdda, ka Room dima nin ti sa'am ti Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa, ni ti tiŋŋa wa zaa, m‑basi.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Ba puuni, dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri n daa boonni Kayafasi. Ŋɔna n daa nyɛ Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana lala yuumni maa ni. Ŋɔnŋɔ daa doaai n‑yɛli, ni, Yan gyɛra wa, wumma!.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Ya bu mi di n bɛ seem maa? - ni, ya yi kyɛ ka ninvugyinni kpi n‑ti niriba di sɔ ya kyɛ ka ti butiri zaa sa'am m‑basi.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, u daa bu tɔ'asi di la u mammantoori; amaa ŋɔn daa nyɛ Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana di yuumni maa zugu, u daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛla, ka di gbinni nyɛ la : ni, see ka Yisa kpi n‑ti Yɛhudiya niriba;.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: ka di pa la ba butiri maa kɔkɔ zugu, amaa ka di la'am Naawunni biisi ban wurigi m‑bɛ tinsi tinsi zaa ni la m‑paasi.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Dirindaari maa ka Yɛhudiya kpambaya daa di lɔ niya ni ba ku Yisa.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Dinzugu Yisa daa bu laa n‑goori paalu ni Gyuudiiya tingbaŋŋu ni, amaa ka daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑pɔtti ziseaa niriba ŋɔn daa ka ni la, m‑paai tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Ɛfirayim la ni. Bɛni ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba daa bɛ ni.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Kyuu la n daa ti pɔtta, ka niriba pampam daa yi tinkpansi maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm ni ba ti maali ba mansi siri kasikasi pɔi ka Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa naayi paai.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Ba daa boori la Yisa ziiya, ka ban daa ti la'asi m‑bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni la, ba daa boosiri la taaba, ni, Ya teesi la wula? Ni, u nin kyɛ kyuu wa ni na, bee, u ku kyɛ na.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa ni Farisii dima daa yɛli, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa mi Yisa ŋɔn bɛ ya'aseaa, ŋɔn tisim ba lahabaari, din nin kyɛ ka ba gbaai u.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Di daa kpalim dabsa ayoobu pɔi, ka ba ya di Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la. Di saha ka Yisa daa kyɛ Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa na, tinseaa Lazarusi yiri ŋɔn daa bɛ ni, Lazarusi sɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa pun neaai kum ni la.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Di zugu ba daa dugi bundira n‑ti Yisa, ka ŋɔn ni Lazarusi daa la'am n‑dira, ban ni daadam pam: ka Maata daa gyeemna.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Di saha ka Lazarusi sunzɔpɔ'a Mariama la daa zaŋŋi ga'ari ni turaali kpaam, bun mari nyunya'asiri ka bu ligri zoaai pam, m‑mari na nti soosi Yisa nɔba, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u zɔbwɔ'ala n‑nyeesiri a. Ka yiri maa ni sɔkam daa ti wumni turaali maa nyunya'asiri maa.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti muna, Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa ya figsi u la, daa yɛli.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: ni, Wula zugu ka turaali wa bu koosi? Ba yi da koosi bu, ligri pam da naa n‑yi na ka lala ligri naa n‑toaai m‑puri n‑tɔri n‑ti faradima maa.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: Ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa gbinni daa pa la u ba'a m bɛ faradima maa yɛla ni, amaa ligri yɛla ni, dama u daa guri la ba la'abooriga, ka mun daa zuuri ka puuni la'aseesi.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Amaa Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, Ay, kyɛl u! U zaŋŋi turaali kpaam wa n‑zaali, see ka n sɔ'abu daari ti paai.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Ya kur nin mari faradima ya saani sahakam. Man mun ku kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani sahakam.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Yɛhudiya dima daadam pam daa ti wum, ni, Yisa daa bɛ bɛni. Dizugu ba daa la'asi na ni ba ti nya u, ka di pa lala zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ni ba ti nya Lazarusi gba, Yisa ŋɔn daa yisigi sɔ kum ni la.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Lala zugu ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima daa ŋmɛ taaba saawara ni ba ti baŋŋi ba nin niŋi seem n‑ku Lazarusi gba.
