eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: Dama ŋɔn Lazarusi yɛla zugu daa kyɛ ka Yɛhudiyadima pam daa niŋni la Yisa yɛdda.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: N‑neaai beoo ka Yisa daa lu soori, ni u kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka tiŋŋa maa puuni zama pam daa ti wum lala.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: ka daa zaŋŋi tiisi wula n‑yi na ni ba ti tuusi u soori. Ka ba daa ŋmeeri kpaliŋŋa n‑tasiri n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Hoosana! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti u - N‑ti sɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri n‑kyɛnni na! Zugsuŋŋu n‑ti Izraɛli Naaba.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Yisa mun daa nya buŋŋa m‑ba, hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:-.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Di zɔri dabeem, nyin pɔ'asariga wa, Nyin Zayɔn tiŋŋa wa! Nyama, i Naaba n kyɛnni i saani na wa! U sigsi u maŋŋa baalimm, Ka baari bumbila n‑kyɛnni na.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Lala saha maa ka u nyaandɔliba daa bu baŋŋi anŋɔ gbinni; amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ u gyirima zii la n‑naai maa ba daa naayi teaai yɛla wa zaa m‑baŋŋi, ni, ba daa daŋŋi n‑sɔbi Yisa yɛla lala.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Di saha ka niriba pam daa dɔli Yisa n‑kyɛnni na. Ba daa dɔla m‑moonni Lazarusi yɛla, ni, ŋɔn Yisa n daa booli u, ni, u yim gballi maa ni na, ka daa neaai u kum ni.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Yɛttɔ'a wa zugu gba ka niriba pam daa yi na ni ba ti tuusi u, ŋɔn daa tum makkri tumni wa zugu.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ka dinŋɔna Farisii dima maa daa fabinni n‑tiri taaba, ni Ya bu nya? - Ti zabri u n‑gyɛa! Duniya wa dima maa zaa yi na ni ba ti dɔli u nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Ninvugseeba daa bɛ la timpuuni, ka ba daa yi Griika dima tinsi na ni ba ti di Kyuu maa.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Ka Griika dima wa seeba daa kyɛ Filipi saani na, nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa yi Bɛtsaida na - tinkpanseaa kan zɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni. Ba daa yɛli u, ni, Ti Bapira, ti boori ti nya Yisa.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Filipi mun daa paai Anduru saani na ka ba daa la'am m‑bɔ Yisa ziiya nti yɛli u.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Di saha ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ! Saha maa paai na: sahanseaa ba nin zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti Ninsaala Bii maa.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, kaburli yi bu buri tiŋŋa n‑kpi, ka kpalim la ka kɔkɔa; amaa ka yi ti kpi muna, ka nin buli n‑niŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Di wulisiya, ni, sɔ yi ti boori u nyɔvuri pam, u nin kɔŋŋi ka; sɔ mun yi bu boori u nyɔvuri lala, duniya wa puuni, di daama nin guri di m‑mari halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn boori u tumni n‑tiri ma, kyɛ ka u dɔli n nyaaŋŋa: ka man ŋɔn ya bɛ seela maa, bɛni ka n tuntumna wa nin ti bɛ ni. Ka ŋɔn tumni n‑tiri ma la, m Ba Naawunni nin ti di daama gyirima.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Pampaŋŋɔ ka n sufu sa'am: n ya n‑yɛli la wula? - N nin yɛli, ni, M Ba, fa ma m‑basi saha wa yɛlimugsa wa puuni. bee? Ay! dama yɛlimugsa wa zugu ka n daa kyɛ na nti paai saha wa na.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: N nin yɛli, ni, M Ba, kyɛ ka i yuuri nya pɛ'abu!, bee? Ka di saha ka kukɔri n daa tɔ'asi saazugu na, ni, N siri pun kyɛ ka n yuuri nya pɛ'abu, ka ka nin laa n‑nya pɛ'abu yaasa.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Zama maa ni ninvugseeba daa ti wum lala ka yɛtti taaba ni, Saa tasiya! Ka seeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, Ay! Amaa malaakasɔ tɔ'asi n‑ti u.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Pa man zugu ka kukɔri wa tɔ'asi n‑yi na, amaa yarima zugu.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ka ba katti duniya wa sariya. Ka ŋɔn su duniya wa fukumsi la muna - ba nin lusi u n‑kari m‑basi kpaŋŋa sa.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Man muna, ba yi ti zaŋŋi ma n‑sa n‑ya'ali saazugu maa n nin kyɛ ka sɔkam kyɛnni n saani na.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa, u daa wulisiri ŋɔn ya n‑kpi seem maa.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Ka daadam daa yɛli, ni, Ba daa pun yɛli ti ti zaaligu ni, ni, Naawunni yi ti kyɛ ka Masiiya maa kyɛ na n‑di naam n‑tuligi ti, u nin kpalim m‑bɛ ni sahakam; tɔ, ka nyin mun yɛtti, ni, Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti sa n‑ya'ali saazugu. Dinŋɔna, ŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ Ninsaala Bii wa.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Saha beela yaasa ka neesim maa nin kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Tɔ; yan mari neesim sahanseaa la, see ka ya kyɛnni suŋŋa ka zibsim di gbaai ya. Sɔ yi ti kyɛnni zibsim ni, di daama bu mi ŋɔn kyɛnni seela.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Yan mari neesim maa sahanseaa la, see ka ya niŋi neesim maa yɛdda, ka ya nin leebi neesim maa niriba. Yisa daa yɛli lala n‑naai, ka daa kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai ka kyɛ ba.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Halli ŋɔn daa pun tum alahazibu tuma ni makkri tuma ka ba nini nya maa zaa yɔri, ba daa bu sakki n‑niŋi u yɛdda.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Di daa niŋi lala ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem siri daa ti niŋi lala, ni#:- Ti Dugma, ŋɔni n daa niŋi tin moonni seem la yɛdda? Naawunni ŋɔn wulisiri u yiiko maa, ŋɔni mun n nyara? Ay! - sɔ ka ni.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Di wulisiya, ni, ba ku toaai n‑niŋi yɛdda. Aizaaya mun daa maali n‑yɛli, ni#:-.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: U kyɛ ka ba nini n zoomna, Ka ba sufu ku sakki n‑wumna. Di yi pa lala ba nini nin neaai n‑nyara, Ka ba sufu nin wumni n‑takkra, Nti nya tippu n saani.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Aizaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi lala, u na pun nya Naawunni gyirima di saha maa, ka daa tɔ'asi u yɛla maa.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Halli kpamma maa pam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, amaa ba daa bu kyɛ ka sɔ baŋŋi, dama ba daa zɔri Farisii dima maa dabeem, ni, di pa seem ba nin kari ba n‑yiisi ba la'aŋŋu duri ni.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Di wulisiya, ni, ba daa boori ni ninsaadima ti pɛ'ai ba, pa ni Naawunni pɛ'ai ba.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Yisa mun daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, Sɔkam n niŋni ma yɛdda, pa mani ka u niŋi yɛdda maa, amaa Sɔ ŋɔn daa tum ma na la ka u niŋna.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyari ma la gba nyari la Sɔ ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Man kyɛnni na hoo neesim, ni ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda la ku ti kpalim zibsim ni.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Ninvugsɔ mun ŋɔn wumni n yɛla wa n‑tumni lala maa, n ku kari u sariya n‑lusi u: dama n daa bu kyɛ duniya wa na ni n ti kari ka sariya, amaa n kyɛ na ni n ti tiligi duniya maa.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Ninvugsɔ mun yi zeemni ma ka bu sakkri n‑tiri n yɛligu maa, di daama sarikatta m bɛ ni: man ŋɔn yɛli yɛliseelli - kana n ya kari u sariya n‑lusi u, Zaadaari maa.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Pa m mammantoori yɛla ka n yɛtta. Ay! - m Ba daa tum ma na ka daa zaali ma man ya yɛli yɛliseesi, ni man ya tɔ'asi seem.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: M mi halli, ni, ŋɔn zaaligu maa nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Man ŋɔn yɛtti yɛliseesi muna, hoo m Ba ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ma seem la, lala ka man gba tɔ'asira.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Ban daa ti piili m‑puusi Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa sahanseaa la, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba n daa bɛ ni: u daa baŋŋiya, ni, saha maa paaya ni u bakki ka kyɛ duniya wa n‑kyaŋŋi u Ba saani. Ka ŋɔn daa pun wuri u niriba wa seem la - baseeba ban ya kpalim m‑bɛ duniya wa puuni maa - u daa na wuri ba halli ni u yaa tariga.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Di saha ka Sintaani daa kpɛ Gyuudasi sufu ni, ŋɔn Saimoon Isikariyɔti bii la, ni u ti kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi u sufu ni teesibeeri n‑gbaai Yisa figsigu la. U daa pun niŋi lala pɔi, ka ba naayi la'am ni ba ti di.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Yisa mun daa mi halli, ni, u Ba Naawunni pun zaŋŋi bunseelikam zaa n‑tisi u nuu ni; ka laa m‑mi, ni, u daa yi ŋɔn Naawunni saani na ka ya labi n‑kyaŋŋi u saani yaasa.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Ka dinŋɔna u daa doaai bundira diibu zii la ni n‑zaani nti yeaai u kparigu ka zaŋŋi bɔduuwa n‑lɔ u seaa ni.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Ka naayi zaŋŋi koom n‑niŋi tasilaa la ni n‑gbaai u nyaandɔliba nɔba peaari; ka u daa zaŋŋi bɔduuwa la n‑nyeesi n‑nyeesi a n‑ti ba.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: U daa niŋi lala nti paai Saimoon Piita saani na: ka ŋɔn Piita daa yɛli u, ni, Kala, n dugma, i ya peaai la man nɔba bee.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Man ŋɔn niŋni seem maa muna, i ku toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni pampaŋŋɔa; di nyaaŋŋa ka i nin ti baŋŋi.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Ka Piita yɛli, Ay, n ku sakki ni i peaai n nɔba la, halli sahakam! Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, N yi bu peaai i, i nuu laa n‑ka n yɛla ni.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Ka ŋɔn Saimoon Piita daa yɛli, ni, Tɔ, n dugma! Ka dinŋɔna pa n nɔba kɔkɔ ka i ti ya peaai - see ka i peaai n nuusi ni n zugu m‑paasi.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli, ni, Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pun su koom, di daama bu laa m‑boori suubu yaasa, amaa u peaai u nɔba kɔkɔa. Lala nin kyɛ ka u leebi bunveelli kasi zaa. Ni, Ii, ya siri nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban peaai kasikasi. Amaa pa ya zaa.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: Tɔ: ka ŋɔn daa peaai ba zaa n‑naai maa, u daa maali n‑yɛ u kparigu n‑labi bundira diibu ziiya. Di saha ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ammi, ya baŋŋi man ŋɔn niŋi ya seem maa?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Tɔ, di gbinni nŋɔa#:- Ya boonni ma la, ni, ya karimba ni ya dugma, bee m ma'ai ni? Ka yan yɛtti seem wa, di mari soori, dama man nyɛ la lala n‑ti ya.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Tɔ, ka mani, ya karimba ni ya dugma la, n peaai ya nɔba n‑ti ya - ka di gbinni nyɛ la: see ka yarim zaa gba ti peaari ya taaba nɔba n‑tiri ba.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: N tum tuma wa n‑ti ya ni n ti wulisi ya ya tunsuŋŋu soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ka ya gba ti tumni lala.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, daabri bu kpeem u dugma, bee, tuumu n‑kpeem ŋɔn tum u.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Anŋɔ zaa ka ya pun baŋŋi: tɔ, zugsuŋŋu nin bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka di nyɛ la yan wumni a n‑dɔli a maa.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: Pa ya zaa yɛla ka n yɛtti maa: man mi man gamsi ninvugseeba la. See ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli maa siri nin ti niŋi lala, ni: Man ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn la'amni n‑diri diibu ni taaba, di daama yi la n nyaaŋŋa nti sa'am n yɛla. .

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: Ni, Pampaŋŋɔ n daŋŋi n‑wulisiri anŋɔ zaa n‑tiri ya pɔi, ka a ti naayi niŋi. Ka dizugu a yi ti siri niŋi lala, ya nin baŋŋi n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn Bɛ ni maa.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ya n‑deaai n tuumusɔ suŋŋa, mani ka u deaara; halli ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn deaari ma muna - Sɔ ŋɔn tum ma gba ka u deaara.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli dinŋɔ n‑naai maa, sufusa'aŋŋu daa ti gbaai u, ka u daa yɛli u noori yɛlimaŋni zaa, ni, Ya sɔ n yɛ koosi ma n‑figsi ma.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa sufu daa gyɛtta, ka ba gɔsiri taaba m‑birisiri ni ba ti baŋŋi sɔ maa.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Ka Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ daa pɔri n‑tabli u saani; ŋɔn daa wuri pam sɔ n‑daa la.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Ka Saimoon Piita daa bugsi n‑yɛli doo wa, ni, u boosima Yisa m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn tɔ'asiri sɔ yɛla maa.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Di saha ka nyaandɔla maa daa vuui m‑paai Yisa n‑sɔ'ai m‑boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni nla, n dugma.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, I yi ti nya ka n ŋmaai diibu n‑sugi laa ni n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti sɔa - tɔ, ŋɔna nla. Ka u daa ŋmaai n‑sugi ka zaŋŋi n‑ti Gyuudasi, Saimoon Isikariyɔti bii la.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Ka Gyuudasi ŋɔn daa deaai diibu la n‑naai, di saha ka Sintaani daa kpɛ u sufu ni. Ka Yisa yɛli u, Nyin ŋɔn ya tum seelli maa, tumma yooma.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: U nyaandɔliba maa mun daa bu mi u yɛligu wa gbinni.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Halli Gyuudasi daa su ba la'abooriga yɛla, ka baseeba daa yɛtti, ni, Yisa tɔ'asiri ligri yɛttɔ'a n‑ti u, ni, u kyɛma nti da ba kyuu neema an kpalim na, bee, n‑yiisi zakka n‑ti faradima.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Tɔ, ka Gyuudasi daa deaai diibu la ka doaai yooma n‑yi. Di saha ka yuŋŋu daa zibi zimm.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Gyuudasi ŋɔn daa bakki n‑naai la, Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, Pampaŋŋɔ ka Ninsaala Bii maa gyirima yɛ yi paalu. Ka Naawunni gba gyirima yi paalu u saani na.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Ka din bɛ la lala zugu, dinŋɔna Naawunni nin kasigi ka u gyirima ti yi paalu Ninsaala Bii la saani, halli yoomyooma ka u nin niŋi lala.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ni, M biisi, n ku laa n‑yuui ya saani: di kpalim la beela ka ya nin ti bɔ m‑bɔ n ziiya n‑gyɛa. Man daa pun yɛli lala n‑ti Yɛhudiya dima maa, ni, man wa seelli maa, ya ku toaai m‑paai bɛni: tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ n yɛtti lala n‑tiri ya gba.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: N zaanni la zaalipaalli n‑tiri ya, ni, ya wuri taaba; hoo man daa wuri ya seem la, yan mun wuri taaba lala.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Ya yi siri yɛ wuri taaba lala, dinŋɔna sɔkam zaa nin baŋŋi, ni, ya nyɛ la n nyaandɔliba.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Ka Saimoon Piita daa boosi u n‑yɛli, Yani ka i wa, n dugma? Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi u, ni, Man n wa seelli maa, i ku toaai n‑dɔli ma m‑paai bɛni pampaŋŋɔa: amaa i nin ti naayi dɔli n nyaaŋŋa m‑paai sahanseaa.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli u, ni, N dugma, bɔ n lee n‑kyɛ ka n ku toaai n‑dɔli i pampaŋŋɔa? N sɛ'asiya ni n kpi n‑ti i.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ni, i sɛ'asiya ni i kpi n‑ti ma, bee? Ay! - yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, nɔroo sa ku ti kum ka di pa la ka nyin sa nin pun kyiisi napɔmbuta, ni, i bu mi ma.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am. Ya niŋma Naawunni yɛdda, tɔ - ka niŋi man gba yɛdda.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Duri pam m bɛ m Ba yiri maa ni, ka n wa ni n ti maali beebu ziiya siri n‑zaali ya. Di yi da pa lala, n naa n‑ku yɛtti ya, ni, lala n nyɛ di. M bu ma'ari ya la ziri.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Ka man kyaŋŋi m‑maali ya beebu zii n‑naai, n nin labi na nti zaŋŋi ya n‑kyaŋŋi, ka man ni yarima la'am m‑bɛ ni.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Ya mi man wa luginseaa maa soori.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Ka Tɔmaasi daa yɛli u, Ay, n dugma, ti bu mi nyin wa luginseaa maa; ti ya niŋi la wula m‑baŋŋi soori m‑paai bɛni sa.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Mani n nyɛ soori maa; mani n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni gba, ka mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri m‑paasi. Sɔ ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai ti Ba Naawunni saani faasee ka u dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Yan yi baŋŋi ma wa, ya nin baŋŋi m Ba gba; ka pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ya yi baŋŋi u, ka laa n‑nya u.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Ka Filipi daa yɛli u, N dugma, wulisim ti i Ba maa - lala yi sakki ti.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, N zɔ Filipi, man ni yan zaa kpɛya n‑yuui pam, ka i na bu mi ma, fa? Ŋɔnkam pun nya ma pun nya m Ba maa gba. Ka wula ka i yɛli lala? - ni, Wulisim ti i Ba maa.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: I bu niŋi yɛdda, ni, m bɛ la m Ba saani, ka m Ba mun bɛ la man saani? Yɛttɔ'aseesi man tɔ'asi n‑ti ya maa pa la seesi an yi m mammaŋŋa saani na. Ay - m Ba ŋɔn bɛ n saani maa - ŋɔna n tumni tuma maa.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Ya niŋma ma yɛdda, ni, m bɛ la m Ba maa saani ka m Ba gba bɛ man saani. Di yi pa lala, ya niŋma ma yɛdda n tuma maa zugu.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda nin tum kaman man tumni seem maa. U nin tum halli tunkpeema n‑gaari lala gba, dama man wa la m Ba maa saani.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Ka ya yi ti susi bunseelikam zaa n yuuri zugu, n nin tum lala, ka lala yi kyɛ ka m Ba gyirima ti yi paalu, man u Bii maa zugu.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Ya yi susi ma bunseelikam n yuuri zugu, n nin niŋi di.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya yi wuri ma, ya nin sakki n noori zaaligu maa.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Ka n nin susi m Ba Naawunni, ka u nin ti ya suŋŋnisɔ yaasa, ka ŋɔn Suŋŋna maa nin bɛ ya saani halli saha din ka tariga.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Naawunni Sii nla, yɛlimaŋni daana. Duniya wa dima ku toaai n‑deaai u, dama ba bu nya u, bee, m‑baŋŋi u. Yarima mun mi u, dama u kpalim la ya saani ka u nin ti bɛ ya ni.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: N yi ti kyaŋŋi, ya ku kpalim ya kɔkɔa, hoo kpibisi ŋɔn kpalim seem. Ay! - N nin labi ya saani na.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Di kpalim la beela ka duniya dima ku laa n‑nya ma, amaa yarima nin nya ma; ka man ŋɔn vu maa zugu, ya gba nin vu.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Dabsiri maa yi ti kyɛ na, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, m bɛ la m Ba maa saani, ka ya mun bɛ n saani, halli ka man gba bɛ ya saani.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Ŋɔnkam deaai la n noori zaaligu ka sakkri n‑tumni lala, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn wuri ma. Ka ŋɔnkam wuri ma muna - m Ba nin wuri di daama, ka man gba nin wuri u, ka nin kasigi m maŋŋa n‑wulisi u.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Di saha ka Gyuudasi bila daa yɛli , ni, N dugma, i ya niŋi la wula n‑kasigi i maŋŋa n‑wulisi ti, ka duniya dima maa ku nya i.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ŋɔnkam wuri ma nin deaai n yɛligu, ka m Ba nin wuri u, ka man ni m Ba nin kyɛ u saani na ka ti la'am m‑bɛ ni.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Ŋɔnkam mun bu wuri ma ku deaai n yɛligu maa, halli ka yɛligu maa pa la n dinni, yɛliseaa yan pun wum la, amaa m Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na yɛligu n nyɛ ka.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: Ni, Dinŋɔ ka m pun yɛli ya ka man kur bɛ la ya saani maa.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Suŋŋna maa muna, u yi ti paai na, ŋɔn nin wulisi ya bunseelikam, ka teaari ya man ŋɔn pun yɛli ya seem maa zaa. Ŋɔna n nyɛ Sii Kasi maa, ka m Ba Naawunni nin tum u na man yuuri zugu.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Sufudoaa ka n tiri ya; di nyɛ la m mammaŋŋa sufudoaa ka n zaŋŋi n‑tiri ya. Pa duniya dima maa ŋɔn mari sufudoaa seem maa tataabu ka n zaŋŋni n‑tiri ya. Yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am, ya mun di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Ya pun wum man yɛli ya seem, ni, n wa ni maa, ka n nin laa n‑labi ya saani na. Tɔ, ya yi siri wuri ma, ya sufu nin maai dizugu, dama n wa la m Ba saani maa, dama ŋɔna n gaari ma.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: N ya n‑yɛli ya ni pɔi, ka di zaa naayi niŋi; ka di yi ti niŋi, ya nin mari yɛdda di zugu.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: N ku laa n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya, bee, n‑diri azama, ka di ti yuui, dama ŋɔn su duniya wa fukumsi la kyɛnni na. U bu su la mani.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: amaa di niŋni lala nti kyɛ ka duniya dima nin baŋŋi, ni, n wuri la m Ba maa, ka lala zugu n tumni la bunseelikam zaa ŋɔn zaali ma seem. Tɔ: ya doaama ka ti kyaŋŋi.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Dinŋɔ ka n yɛli ya, ni, yan di kyɛ ka ya yɛdda maa nyɛŋŋi ya.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Ba nin ti kari ya n‑yiisi ba la'aŋŋu duri maa ni, ka saha nin ti kyɛ na ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ti ku ya nin ti teesi, ni, u gyeemni la Naawunni di zugu.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Niriba nin ti niŋni anŋɔ n‑tiri ya, dama ba bu mi m Ba maa, bee, mani.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Amaa n yɛli ya dinŋɔa ni di kyɛ ka saha maa yi ti paai na, ya nin teaai man daa yɛli ya seem nŋɔa. Ka u laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, N daa bu daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya dinŋɔ piiligu ni, man daa na bɛ la ya saani maa zugu.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni la Ŋɔn tum ma na la saani, ka din zaasa yɔri ya sɔ bu boosi ma man ya n‑kyaŋŋi seela.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Amaa man ŋɔn yɛli ya lala zugu ka sufusa'aŋŋu yi mari ya zaa.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, di kpa ni n nin bakki ka kyɛ ya, dama n yi bu kyaŋŋi, Suŋŋna maa ku kyɛ na. Man mun yi ti kyaŋŋi, n nin tum u ya saani na.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: Ka u yi ti kyɛ na, u nin kasigi n‑ti duniya wa niriba ba tumbeeri taalli yɛla, ni ba yɛlimaŋni kaalinsi yɛla, ni Naawunni sariya karibu yɛla.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: U nin wulisi ban tum taalli seem yɛla, dama ba bu niŋi ma yɛdda;.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: u nin wulisi ban ka yɛlimaŋni seem yɛla, dama n kyɛnni la m Ba saani ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa;.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: u nin laa n‑wulisi, ni, ba nin ti nya sariya karibu ni yɛlimaŋni, dama ba pun kari duniya wa Daana maa sariya n‑lusi u.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Tɔ. N na mari yɛla pam ni n yɛli ya, amaa di nin zoaai pam n‑gaari ya.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Amaa Sii Kasi maa nin ti kyɛ na n‑kasigi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa zaa n‑ti ya. U bu ti ya zaŋŋi u toori yiiko n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya, amaa u ya tɔ'asi la ŋɔn wum seelli, ni bunseesi an nin ti kyɛ na yɛla m‑paasi.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: U nin ti ti ma gyirima, dama u ti ya deaai la man ŋɔn yɛli u seem la n‑yɛli ya.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: M Ba ŋɔn mari seesi - a seelikam zaa nyɛ la n dinni, lala zugu ka n yɛli, ni, Sii Kasi maa nin ti zaŋŋi n seelikam zaa n‑yɛli ya.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Ni, Di nin ti niŋi saha beela ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma, ka saha beela yaasa ya nin nya ma.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: U nyaandɔliseeba daa yɛli taaba, Bumbɔ ka u yɛtti ti wa? - Ni, Saha beela ya ku nya ma, ka saha beela ka ya nin nya ma yaasa. ni, Di nyɛ la n kyɛnni m Ba saani. .

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Ka ba yɛli, Bɔ ka u wulisiri, ni, saha beela? - Ay! ti ku toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Ka Yisa daa mi, ni, ba boori ba boosi u, ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, Dinŋɔ ka ya boosiri taaba wa, bee bɔa? - ni n wulisiri, ni, saha beela ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma, ka saha beela ya nin nya ma yaasa?.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya nin ti kum ka fabli, amaa ka duniya wa dima nin ti di nya'asim; ya sufu nin sa'am, amaa ya sufusa'aŋŋu maa nin ti takki sufudoaa.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Pɔ'a pudaana yi ya dɔ'ai, u mari sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u beerim saha paai u lala; amaa u yi ti dɔ'ai n‑naai sahaseaa maa, u ku laa n‑teaari beerim maa yaasa: ay! - u mari sufudoaa, dama u baŋŋiya, ni, u dɔ'ai bii la ka u bɛ ni.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Ka sufusa'aŋŋu mari ya pampaŋŋɔa, amaa ya nin ti nya ma yaasa ka ya mari sufumaasim ni sufudoaa, ka sɔ ku laa n‑fa ya sufudoaa wa ya saani.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Dirindaari maa di pa la ya nin ti susi bunseelli man saani: yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi susi seelli zaa m Ba saani u nin ti ya, man yuuri zugu.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Halli ni pampaŋŋɔ ya na bu susi seela n yuuri zugu: susima ka ya nin nya, ka ya nin nya sufudoaa pam.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: N daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'anyɛsa n‑yɛtti ya dinŋɔa, amaa saha kyɛnni na ka n ku ti tɔ'asi ya nyɛsa ni, amaa n nin kasigi n‑yɛli ya m Ba yɛla.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Di daari ya nin ti susi n yuuri zugu, ka di pa la n yɛtti ya, ni, man nin susi m Ba n‑ti ya;.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: ay! - m Ba maa mammaŋŋa wuri ya, dama ya gba wuri ma ka niŋi yɛdda, ni, n yi la ŋɔn Naawunni saani na.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: N siri yi la m Ba saani n‑kyɛ duniya wa na; ka n nin laa n‑yi duniya wa ni n‑labi m Ba saani yaasa.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba daa yɛli u, ni, Tɔ! Pampaŋŋɔ i kasigi n‑yɛli ti, pa nyɛsa ni yaasa.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ti yi mi, ni, i mi seelli kam; ka i karimba sɔ ku laa m‑boosi i boosa n‑wulisiri i maa. Dinŋɔ zugu ti baŋŋiya, ni, i siri yi la Naawunni saani na.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, ni, Ya siri niŋi yɛdda, fa?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Ya bu nya? - saha kyɛnni na, halli m‑pun paai na gba, ka ya nin ti wurim; ya sɔkam nin zɔ n‑kuli u yiri ka kyɛ ma n kɔkɔa - amaa n siri pa n kɔkɔa, dama m Ba maa bɛ la n saani.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N yɛtti ya anŋɔa, ni di ti kyɛ ka ya mari sufudoaa n saani. Duniya wa ni ya mari yɛla, amaa ya mari sufukpeemni dama m pun nyaŋŋi duniya maa.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli anŋɔ n‑naai, u daa wukki u nini n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni n‑susi, ni, M Ba, saha maa paaya. Tima i Bii gyirima, ni i Bii maa gba ti zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti i.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Dama i pun zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti u, ni u ti su sɔkam yɛla ka zaŋŋi nyɔvuri kan ka tariga n‑ti baseeba nyini, m Ba, ŋɔn daa ti u la zaa.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Nyɔvuri kan ka tariga wa nŋɔa: ni, ba ti baŋŋi nyini, m Ba, ni, nyin kɔkɔ n nyɛ Naawunni yinni ni yɛlimaŋni; ka laa m‑baŋŋi Yisa Kirisita, nyin ŋɔn tum sɔ la na.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Tɔ: n tum tunseesi nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma ni n ti tum la, halli nti naai: ka lala ka n zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti i, duniya wa puuni.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Ka dinŋɔna pampaŋŋɔa, m Ba, tim ma gyirima i saani hoo man daa nya gyirima seem pɔi ka duniya wa naayi piili la.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: N yiisi i yɛla n‑wulisi seeba nyin ŋɔn daa yiisi duniya wa ni n‑ti ma la. I maŋtoori niriba n nyɛ ba, ka i daa zaŋŋi ba n‑ti ma ka ba deaai i noori.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ba baŋŋiya, ni, seelikam nyin ti ma la yi la i saani na.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Dama n daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin daa ti ma la n‑ti ba, ka ba deaai a. Ba daa mi suŋŋasuŋŋa, ni, n yi la i saani na, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n daa tum ma na.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Ban ka n susiri i zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba zugu. M bu susiri n‑tiri la duniya wa dima, amaa seeba nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma la zugu ka n susira, dama ban n nyɛ i niriba.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Halli man mari seela zaa nyɛ la i dinni, ka nyin ŋɔn mari seela n nyɛ n dinni - ka niriba wa zugu ka n nya gyirima.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Man ku laa m‑bɛ duniya wa ni, amaa ban na kpalim m‑bɛ la duniya ni, ka man mun kyɛnni i saani na. M Ba Kasi, guri ba suŋŋa i yuuri yiiko la zugu - yuseaa ka i daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la - ni ba ti bɛ yimmu hoo man ni nyin nyɛ yimmu seem la.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Man ŋɔn daa bɛ ba saani sahanseaa la, n daa zaŋŋi la yuseaa nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma la n‑guri ba. Ka ba bɛ ni ka ba sɔsɔ bu baasi yɔri, ka di yi pa la baasiyɔri daana la kɔkɔa, ni din daa sɔbi n‑dooli la siri ti niŋi lala.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: N kyɛnni la i saani na pampaŋŋɔa, amaa anŋɔ ka n yɛtta, man na bɛ duniya wa puuni maa, ni di kyɛ ka ba ti nya n sufupeelim zaa ka bu pɔsigi seela.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: N zaŋŋi la i yɛligu n‑ti ba, ka duniya dima kur yaai ba di zugu, dama ba pa la duniya wa niriba, hoo man gba ŋɔn pa duniya wa nira seem.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: N susigu pa la, ni, i yiisi ba duniya wa ni, amaa, ni, i ta'ai Sintaani Yɛlibeeri Daana ka kyɛ ba.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Pa duniya wa niriba n nyɛ ba, hoo man gba ŋɔn pa duniya wa nira seem.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: I yɛligu nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni; dinŋɔna kur kyɛ ka yɛlimaŋni wa mari ba, ni ba ti leebi i niriba kasikasi.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Hoo nyin daa tum ma duniya wa ni na seem la, lala ka n gba tumni ba duniya ni.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Ban zugu ka n zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑ti i; ni ba gba nin ti zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑ti i, yɛlimaŋni maa zugu.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: Pa niriba wa kɔkɔ zugu ka n susiri n wunsusigu wa: n susigu laa m‑bɛ ni n‑tiri ninvugseeba zaa ban nin niŋi ma yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑yi ba lahabaari mooligu maa.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Ii, m Ba, lala nin kyɛ ka ba zaa leebi yimmu kaman nyini m bɛ ma ni la, ka man gba bɛ i ni: lala ka ba gba nin bɛ ti ni, ka di nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima baŋŋi, ni, nyin daa tum ma na, ka ba niŋi lala yɛdda.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: Man mun deaai la gyirimaseaa nyin ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la ka laa n‑ti n niriba wa. Di nin kyɛ ka ba bɛ yimmu, kaman man ni nyini, m Ba, ŋɔn bɛ la yimmu seem la.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Ka n nin bɛ ba ni, ka nyin mun bɛ ma ni. Kyɛ ka ba bɛ ni ni nangbanyinni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima baŋŋi, ni, nyin daa tum ma na ka man mun wuri niriba wa kaman nyin ŋɔn wuri ma seem.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: M Ba, ninvugseeba nyin zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la - m boori ba bɛ n saani man ŋɔn bɛ seela zaa, ka ba nin nya n gyirima - nyin ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi gyirimaseaa n‑ti ma la. Ka di nin wulisiri, ni, i daa wuri ma pɔi ka daa naayi naam duniya wa.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: M Ba yɛlimaŋni daana, halli ni duniya wa dima na zi i yɛla, man mun mi i, ka n niriba wa gba mi, ni, i tum ma na.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: N gba pun kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi i, ka kur kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa. Di nin kyɛ ka wurilim bɛ ba saani, ka di nyɛ wurilim seaa nyin mari zaŋŋi n‑ti ma; ka man gba nin bɛ ba saani.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Di saha ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑tisi ka ba zaŋŋi daari n‑feebi u.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Ka soogyadima maa daa zaŋŋi goosi m‑paam, ka yɛtti, ni, naam zupiliga n nyɛ ka. Ba daa zaŋŋi ka m‑pili u zugu, ka zaŋŋi naam kparizeoo n‑yeeli u.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Ba daa kyɛnni u saani na nti maanni u la ansarisi, ni, Ndana, m Ba Izraɛli Naaba!, ka lɔbri u pɛ'asi.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Pailiti mun daa labi n‑yi n‑yɛli daadam maa, ni, Tɔ! m mari u na nti ti ya, ni ya baŋŋi, ni, man bu nya u taaliseaa.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Ka Yisa daa yi na, ka u na pinni la goosi zupiliga maa, ka yɛ kparizeoo la. Ka Pailiti daa yɛli ba, ni, Nnya doo maa.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa mun daa nya u, ban ni ba Pirinsi dima maa, ka daa gbaai tasibu, ni, Ya kpaasim u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu! Kpaasim u! Pailiti mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, ni, Tɔ: ya deaam u n‑kpaasi ya mammansi! Man mun bu nya u taaliseaa ni n galim u.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Yɛhudiyadima maa daa labsi n‑yɛli Ti mari la ti zaaligu ka di wulisi, ni, di sum ka u kpi, dama u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni, Naawunni bii n nyɛ u.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, dabeem daa kpa u m‑paasi.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Ka u daa mari Yisa n‑labi n‑kpɛ Baarikyi zɔŋŋu maa puuni m‑boosi u, ni, Yɛni ka i yi na?; ka Yisa daa bu labsi u seela.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli u, I ku tɔ'asi n‑ti ma? I bu mi, ni, m mari yiiko ni n kyɛ ka ba basi i, bee, ba kpaasi i n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku i.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Ka Yisa yɛli u, I ka yiiko n nin toaai, di yi pa la yiiko maa yi Naawunni saani na. Dinzugu ninvugsɔ ŋɔn figsi ma n‑ti i la nin deaai taalli n‑gaari i.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Di saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka Pailiti yi boori sɔseaa ŋɔn nin dɔli m‑basi Yisa. Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa tasiri n‑yɛtta, ni, I yi basi doo wa di wulisiya, ni, i pa la Room NaaTitaari maa zɔa! ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yɛnni, ni, u nyɛ la naaba - tɔ! di daama za'asiri NaaTitaari la naam maa.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Gɔmna Pailiti ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa mari Yisa n‑yi na, ka daa zini u sariya karibu bimbinni maa zugu. Bɛni ka ba daa boonni, ni, Litɔsitɔroota la, ka ka yuuri Hibruuli ni daa nyɛ la Gabbata - ka di gbinni Kuga Ziiya maa.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Di saha maa n daa nyɛ Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu siri maaligu daari, ka di daa paai agbaa saha ka Pailiti daa yɛli Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, Ya nyama, ya Naa maa nŋɔa.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Ban mun daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, Mari u m‑bakki! Kpaasim u n‑tabli! Ka Pailiti daa boosi ba, ni, Ammi, ya bu boori n ku ya Naaba maa? Di saha ka gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, Ay! - Ti ka Naaba, m‑paasi Room NaaTitaari maa zugu.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Di saha ka Pailiti daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑tisi ni ba ti ku u. Dinzugu ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: n‑kyɛ ka u bugi u maŋtoori kum dɔpulli daari n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai ya'aseaa ban boonni Zuguŋmaaŋŋu Ziiya, di n nyɛ, Hibruuli ni, Gɔligɔtta la.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Bɛni maa ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli maa zugu ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa, ka laa n‑kpaasi niriba ayi m‑paasi u zugu, n‑zaŋŋi ba yinni dɔpulli n‑sa Yisa ya'ari wa sa, ka zaŋŋi kanla muna n‑sa u ya'ari wa sa, ka Yisa daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Gɔmna Pailiti daa pun sɔbi bunni n‑tabli dɔpulli maa saazugu, ni, Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, nŋɔa: Yɛhudiyadima Naaba. .

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Yɛhudiya niriba pam daa karim lala sɔppu maa, dama ban daa kpaasi Yisa seela ni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni daa bu wɔ'a; halli ba daa sɔbi ka Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a, ni Room yɛttɔ'a ni Griiktiri gba.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Yɛhudiya gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa mun daa yɛli Pailiti ni, Ay! di sɔbi, ni, Yɛhudiyadima Naaba amaa, ni U yɛli u mammaŋŋa yɛla, ni, Yɛhudiyadima Naaba n nyɛ ma. .

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Ka Pailiti daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Man ŋɔn sɔbi seem la, di sɔbiya.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Soogyadima anaasi maa ŋɔn daa kpaasi Yisa n‑naai, ba daa deaai u situra m‑puri bunaasi n‑tɔri taaba. U waari kparigu mun n daa nyɛ bunsuŋŋu, ka ba wugi ka bummunni ka bu sɛ ka tanni:.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: ka ba daa yɛli taaba, ni, Ya di kyɛ ka ti toaai ka n‑tɔri see ka ti tɔ tɛtɛ m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ya deaai! Lala ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli siri daa niŋi, ni#:- Ba zaŋŋi n situra n‑tɔri n‑ti taaba Ka tɔ la tɛtɛ n kyinkyinni zugu. Tɔ: soogyadima maa ŋɔn daa tum seem nla.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Tɔ, ka pɔ'aba daa zɛ n‑tabi Yisa dɔpulli ziiya: u ma Mariama n daa bɛ ni, ni ŋɔn Mariama sunzɔ la; ni u tɔ'ama, Klɔpaasi pɔ'a; ni Mariama Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la gba.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Ka Yisa daa nya ka u ma n zɛya, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa wuri pam la gba. Ka u yɛli u ma maa, ni, Nyama, m ma, i bii nŋɔa.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: ka laa n‑yɛli doo maa, ni, Nyama, i ma nŋɔa. Ka di saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna u nyaandɔla wa daa deaai Yisa ma u toori yiri maa ni.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Tɔ: di daa niŋi lala, ka Yisa daa naayi ti baŋŋi, ni, u tum bunseelikam zaa n‑naai. Di saha ka u daa yɛli, Kɔnyuuri m mari ma! - ka yɛligu wa gba dɔli la din daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli Litaafi maa ni seem la.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Di saha ka damiisiri n daa bɛ dukku ni n‑zɛ bɛni. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi sɔ'asu n‑niŋi daam maa ni n‑kusi ka n‑tabli bunni zugu n‑duusi m‑paai u noori, ni, u nyuuma.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nyu n‑naai maa, u daa yɛli, ni, Di naaya!, ka daa sunni u zugu n‑leebi bunni din ka ni.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Dirindaari maa daa nyɛ Kyuu maa siri maaligu dabsiri, ka lala zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima maa daa bu boori la niriba n kpalim n‑tabli dɔpula maa ni ba ti kpi ka di bɛ la Kyuu la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ni, dama dabsitibsiri daari n‑daa la. Ka ba daa susi Gɔmna Pailiti, ni, u kyɛ ka ba kabsi niriba maa gbaya ka ba kpi yooma ka ba sigsi ba na.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Di zugu ka soogyadima maa daa paai ba saani n‑kabsi doo yinni gbaya, ni ŋɔnla gba gbaya - dɔseeba ban daa la'am n‑kpaasi Yisa saani la.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Amaa ban daa paai Yisa saani na nti nya ka u pun kpi ni maa, ba daa bu kabsi u gbaya.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Amaa ba ninvugsɔ daa zaŋŋi u kpanni n‑kusi Yisa pulugri ka ziim daa toosi n‑yi na, ni koom m‑paasi.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Tɔ, ya bu nya? - ŋɔn yɛtti lahabaari wa n daa bɛ bɛni ka u nini nya. U lahabaari yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: u mammaŋŋa mi halli, ni, yɛlimaŋni n nyɛ di, ni ya gba ti niŋi yɛdda di zugu.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Anŋɔ zaa daa niŋi lala ni di kyɛ ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli siri niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni, U kɔba muna - aseaa zaa ku kabsi.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi yaasa, ni, Ba nin gɔsi n‑nya ban daa kusi sɔ la.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Tɔ: dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi, ŋɔn daa yi Arimatii tiŋŋa la na. Ka ŋɔn Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔli la Yisa, amaa u daa sɔ'ai n‑dɔli ni, Yɛhudiyadima maa dabeem zugu. Ka Yisa kum nyaaŋŋa u daa kyɛ Pailiti saani na nti susi u, ni, u kyɛ ka u deaai Yisa ningbiŋŋu la n‑sɔ'ai. Ka Pailiti daa sakki n‑ti u. Ka Gyoosɛfi daa kyaŋŋi nti deaai ningbiŋŋu maa m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Nikɔdiimusi gba daa dɔli ŋɔn Gyoosɛfi n‑kyɛ na, ka u daa mari turaaliseesi ni kpanyuuma an mari nyunya'asa ka a ligri zoaai. U daa mari a pam gba. Nikɔdiimusi ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ Yisa saani na yuŋŋu la n‑daa la.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa n‑vili n‑vili tampeela n‑niŋni turaali dima wa, hoo ba Yɛhudiyadima kum sɔ'abu kaari ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Ka gaadi daa bɛ bɛni, ziseaa ban daa ku Yisa la ni, ka gaadi maa puuni ka ba daa pun kyiibi tampifeoo gballi duu, amaa ka na bu zaŋŋi sɔ n‑sɔ'ai ka puuni.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Bɛni ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑dooli, dama ka daa bu wɔ'a: ba daa na kur teesiri la Yɛhudiyadima Kyuu maa siri maaligu dabsiri yɛla.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban zaŋŋi tiseaa wala koom m‑maanni ba daam la: ni, Mani n nyɛ tiwanni maa ni yɛlimaŋni, ka m Ba mun nyɛ la tii maa daana.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Wuliseaa kan bɛ ma ni ka bu wanni wala, m Ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi; amaa wuliseaa kan wanni walisuma, u nin gɔsi ka suŋŋa n‑waasi, ka ka niŋi kasi, ni ka ti maali n‑wanni pam.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Tɔ; yarima muna - ba pun gɔsi ya suŋŋa ka ya yi niŋi kasi, ka di nyɛ la yɛttɔ'aseesi man tɔ'asi n‑ti ya wa zugu.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Ya kpalimma m‑bɛ man saani, ka n gba kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni. Tiwulli ku toaai n‑wali wala ka toori yiiko ni, faasee ka ka kpalim m‑bɛ tii maa yiiko ni: lala ka ya gba ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli, see ka ya kpalim man saani ka man ti ya yiiko.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Mani n nyɛ tii maa, ka ya mun n nyɛ ka wula. Ya sɔ ŋɔn kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni ka n gba kpalim m‑bɛ u ni, di daama nin toaai n‑wanni wala pam, amaa man yi ka ya saani ya ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa bu kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni, ba nin ŋmaai di daama m‑basi, hoo wuliseaa kan bu wanna, ka ba basi ka, ka ka kuui; ka ba la'asi anŋɔ taabu nti nyɔa, ka a di bugum.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ya yi kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni, ka n yɛligu maa kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni, ya yi ti susi bunseelli kam zaa, ya nin nya di.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: M Ba yuuri nin nya pɛ'abu dinŋɔ zugu, ni, ya wanni la wala pam, ka ya leebi n nyaandɔliba.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: M Ba ŋɔn wuri ma seem, lala ka n gba wuri ya; ya kpalimma m‑bɛ n wurilim maa ni.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ya yi deaai n zaaligu la ya nin kpalim m‑bɛ n wurilim maa ni; hoo man daa deaai m Ba zaaligu la ka kpalim m‑bɛ u wurilim maa ni la.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, N tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑ti ya, ni n sufupeelli la ti kpalim ya saani ka ya sufupeelli zoaai.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Man zaaligu nŋɔa#:- ni, ya wuri taaba hoo man wuri ya seem wa.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Ninsaala ku toaai m‑mari wurilim n‑gaari nŋɔa, ni, sɔ n zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri m‑pa talima u zɔdima zugu.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Yarima mun n nyɛ n zɔdima. Ya yi sakki n‑tumni man boori seem, n zɔdima n nyɛ ya.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: N ku laa m‑booli ya n daaba yaasa, dama daabri zaa bu mi u dugma ŋɔn tumni seela. Ay! M boonni ya la n zɔdima, dama n zaŋŋi man wum yɛliseesi m Ba saani zaa n‑wulisi ya.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Pa yarima n daa yiisi ma, amaa mani n daa yiisi ya, ka tum ya ni ya yima n‑wanni wala pam ka ya wala nin ti yuui. Ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ya yi boori seelli m Ba saani, man yuuri zugu, ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ya.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n yɛtti ya, ni, see ka ya wuri taaba.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Duniya dima yi yaai ya, yan baŋŋma ka ba pun yaai mani pɔi, ka naayi yaai ya.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ya yi da nyɛ la duniya wa maŋtoori niriba, duniya wa da naa m‑boori ya yɛla; amaa yan pa duniya wa niriba zugu, man ŋɔn gamsi ya n‑yiisi duniya wa yɛla ni zugu, lala ka duniya wa yaai ya.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Ya teaam man ŋɔn daa yɛli ya seem la, ni, daabri bu gaari u dugma. Ba yi da tum ma putoo, ya gba ka ba nin niŋi ya putoo. Ban mun yi deaai n noori, ba nin deaai ya gba noori.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Anŋɔ zaa ka ba nin niŋi ya man zugu, dama ba bu mi m Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: N yi daa bu kyɛ na n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, ba daa naa n‑ku deaai taalli; amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑kyiisi n‑yi ba taalli maa ni.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Ŋɔn yaai ma zaa, m Ba gba ka u yaai maa m‑paasi.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: N yi daa bu wulisi ba tunseesi an na zi n‑tum, ba daa naa n‑ku deaai taalli; amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba pun nya ma ka laa n‑yaai ma, n‑tabi m Ba gba.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Di bɛ la lala ni di kyɛ ka ba zaaligu yɛligu la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni, M bu niŋi ba seela ka ba yaai ma yɔri.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Saawara Daana maa mun ya n‑kyɛ na, man ŋɔn ya n‑tum sɔ maa ni u yi m Ba saani m‑paai ya na - Naawunni Sii nla, ka u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka yiiri m Ba saani na. U yi ti kyɛ na, u nin zaŋŋi n yɛla n‑wulisi ya, dama n sɛhara daana n nyɛ u.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Ka di sumsi ni ya gba di n sɛhara n‑wulisiri niriba, dama ya daa bɛ n saani, halli piiligu ni sa.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Yɛla wa zaa nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa maali n‑wulisi u maŋŋa n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa yaasa, ka ba daa bɛ Taibiriyasi mɔ'ari ziiya. Di daa niŋi nŋɔa#:-.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliseeba daa la'am m‑bɛ ni: Piita daa bɛ ni, ni Tɔmaasi, ban daa pa sɔ yupaa, ni, Gyeaa la maa, ni Nataniyɛli ŋɔn daa yi Kanna ni na, tinseaa kan zɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu maa ni; ka Zɛbidii biisi maa daa paasi ba zugu, ni niriba ayi yaasa.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Di saha ka Saimoon Piita daa yɛli, ni, N wa la zaasim gbaabu tuma ni. Ka ba zaa yɛli u, ni, Tɔ! Ti gba ya dɔli i n‑kyaŋŋi. Ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi mɔ'ari la ziiya n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du. Ka ba daa tum halli ka beoo neaai, n‑wɔlim n‑gyɛa. Ba daa bu gbaai halli ziŋŋa yinni.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: N‑neaai beoo asuba pinipini ka Yisa daa zɛ mɔ'agbiriŋŋu ni. Amaa u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna nla.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Ka u daa tasi n‑yɛli ba, ni, M biisi, yee! Ammi, ya gbaai la bundira?, ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Ni, Tɔ: see ka ya zaŋŋi laaŋŋu maa n‑lɔbi nyariŋŋu maa nudirigu lugri ziiya! Lala ka ya nin ti nya. Ka ba daa niŋi lala. Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, ba ya voaai laaŋŋu maa n‑niŋi nyariŋŋu puuni, ka vaai n‑kɔŋŋi, dama zimi pampam daa bɛ ka ni, ka ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑voaara.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Di saha ka nyaandɔlisɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa wuri pam la daa ti yɛli Piita, ni, Ti Dugma nla! Ka Saimoon Piita ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, u daa yɛ u kparigu - dama u daa pun yeaai ka, ni u tumna - ka yikki n‑lu mɔ'ari koom maa ni.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Ka u nyaandɔlitaaba daa duusi nyariŋŋu maa n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa: ba daa mari laaŋŋu maa, ni zimi maa, n‑darigri halli nti paai mɔ'aduuri maa na: di daa bu wɔ'a gba.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Ba daa paaya, nti nya bugunsaala, ka zaasim beela ni paanu daa tɔm ka zugu n‑seera.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, Ya zaŋŋi yan na pun gbaai zinseesi la na.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Di saha ka Saimoon Piita daa labi n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni nti gbaai laaŋŋu maa n‑darigi n‑niŋi tinkuumni maa ni. Zimi pam n daa bɛ ka puuni, bunkara zaa, ka a daa paai zinkɔbga ni pisinu ni ata: ka di zaa yɔri, laaŋŋu maa daa bu sa'am a tibsim zugu.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, N zuusi ya! Ya kyɛm na n‑su noori! Ka vi daa mari ba ka ba daa bu boosi u, ni, Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔni?, dama ba daa mi, ni, ti Dugma n‑daa la.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi paanu maa n‑ti ba na, ni zinseera gba.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Tɔ: Yisa ŋɔn daa wulisi u maŋŋa n‑ti u nyaandɔliba, ni, u yisigi kum ni na, zii napɔmbuta n‑daa la.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ka ban daa di n‑naai maa, Yisa daa yɛli Saimoon Piita, ni, Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, i wuri ma n‑gaari niriba wa, bee? Ka u daa yɛli, Ii, n Dugma, i mi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa. Ka u yɛli u, ni, Wubsima n pɛbisi wa.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Ka u daa maali n‑yɛli u yaasa, ni, Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, i wuri ma, bee? Ka u daa yɛli, Ii, n Dugma; i mi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa. Ka u yɛli u, Kur guri n peesi.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Ka u maali n‑yɛli u napɔmbuta daana, ni, Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, ammi, i siri nyɛ la n zɔa? Di saha ka Piita sufu daa bu maai, Yisa ŋɔn boosi u di napɔmbuta wa, ni, u zɔ n nyɛ u, bee? Di zugu u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, I mi n yɛla halli zaa, n Dugma; ka baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa. Ka Yisa yɛli u, Kur wubsiri n peesi maa.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, nyin ŋɔn daa nyɛ na'akyimbila, i daa bɔbi i maŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi i sufu yuubu kyɛnni, amaa nyin yi ti kurigi maa i nin teaai i nuusi ka ba nin yeeli i n‑taasi i ya'aseaa i sufu ŋɔn bu boora.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli u lala, u daa wulisiri la u kum soori yɛla n‑tiri u, ni, lala ka u nin ti ti Naawunni gyirima. Ka naayi yɛli u, ni, Kur dɔli n nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Di saha ka nyaandɔlisɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa wuri pam la daa bɛ bɛni, ka Piita daa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi n‑nya nyaandɔla wa ka u dɔli la ŋɔn ni Yisa nyaaŋŋa ka ba sɔsira: dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pɔri n‑tabli Yisa saani yuŋŋu Kyuu bundira diibu saha nla, sahanseaa ka u daa vuui m‑paai Yisa saani m‑boosi u, ni, Ni, ti ninvugsɔ ya figsi i - ka ŋɔni nla, n dugma.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Ka Piiya daa nya u ka daa boosi Yisa n‑yɛli, ni, N Dugma, ka ŋɔnŋɔ fa.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Halli n yi ti boori ni u kpalim m‑bɛ ni halli ni n ti labi na, pa i yɛlli nla: nyin mun ka n yɛtta - kur dɔli ma.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Lala maa zugu ka ninvugseeba daa yɛtti taaba, ni, nyaandɔla wa ku kpi: amaa Yisa daa bu yɛli, ni, u ku kpi; u daa yɛli Piita, ni, Halli n yi ti boori ni doo wa kpalim m‑bɛ ni halli ni n ti labi na, pa i yɛlli nla.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa nya anŋɔ zaa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ti la; ka tin mun mi, ni, ŋɔn wulisi seem wa zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa tum tuntumsa pam m‑paasi anŋɔ zugu. A zaa yi ti sɔbi n‑dooli yinniyinni, n bu teesi, ni, paalu naa m‑bɛ duniya wa zaa puuni ni ba ti mari lala litaafidima maa n‑su.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: M Ba Tiyɔfiluusi: N piiligu gbaŋŋu maa ni, n daa sɔbi bunseelli kam zaa Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi ka wulisi, sahanseaa ŋɔn daa piili u tuma maa la.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: N daa sɔbi di zaa yɛla, halli nti paai dabsiseaa ban daa zaŋŋi u n‑duusi arizana ni la. N daa wulisi i, ni, ba daa namsi u n‑ku u, amaa di nyaaŋŋa u daa wulisi u maŋŋa u tuumudima saani - ŋɔn daa pun yiisi seeba la. Ni, u daa niŋi lala dabsa pisinaasi puuni ka tum tuma pam n‑kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, u laa m‑bɛ u nyɔvuri ni; ka diri azama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla. Ni, u daa kpaam ba, Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi yiiko ni, ka Naawunni naayi zaŋŋi u n‑duusi arizana ni.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Tɔ; lala dabsa maa, sahaseaa ban ni ŋɔna ŋɔn daa la'am n‑diri maa, u daa yɛli ba u kpaaŋŋu maa puuni, ni, ba di yi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, di yi pa ni ba ti nya piiniseaa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba la, piiniseaa u Ba ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori, ni, u nin ti ba maa.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ni, Gyɔni mun daa zaŋŋi koom n‑su ya, amaa dabsa ayi saha ya nin deaai Vuusim Kasi suubu.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Sahanseaa yaasa, Yisa nyaandɔliba zaa daa la'am u saani na, ka ba daa boosi u, ni, Ti Dugma, pampaŋŋɔ ka i nin zaŋŋi naam suulinsi la nti labsi n‑niŋi Izraɛli dima nuu ni yaasa, bee.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Di zaa bɛ la m Ba nuu ni; ŋɔna nin zaali saha ni seelli kam zaa ti niŋi. Dizugu di bu kpa ni yarima baŋŋi dabsa bee saha maa yɛla ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Amaa Sii Kasi maa yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, yiiko nin kpɛ ya sufu ni n‑kyɛ ka ya niŋi sɛhara dima m‑mooli man yɛla Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ni Gyuudii ni Samɛrii tinsi zaa, halli nti paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑naai, ba daa nya ka u duuri la saazugu nti kpɛ sangbana ni, ka sangbaŋŋu maa daa ligi u ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya u yaasa.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Ba daa kur gɔsiri ni, halli ka u ti kpɛ sangbana ni. Di saha maa ka niriba ayi daa gbirigi n‑zaani ba saani, niriba maa mun neema daa pɛligiya ni, nyain!.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Ba daa yɛli nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Yan Galilii dima wa, wula zugu ka ya kpalim n‑zɛya n‑gɔsiri sangbana ni? Ya pun nya ka Yisa da du m‑paai arizana ni; tɔ, ka ŋɔn Yisa siri nin labi na, hoo yan nya u ka u du arizana seem la.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Tɔ. Ka di nyaaŋŋa Yisa nyaandɔliba daa doaai n‑yi Ɔlivii Zoori maa zugu n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm; lala tiŋŋa maa ni zoori maa daa bu wɔ'a.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ba daa kpɛ tiŋŋa maa ni m‑paai yiri la, n‑du n‑kpɛ gyirambisa duseaa ban daa gbɛ la. Ba daa nyɛ la Piita ni Gyɔni, ni Gyeemsi, ni Anduru, ni Filipi, ni Tɔmaasi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Matiyu, ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Saimoon sɔ ban daa boonni Zɛlɔti la, ni Gyeemsi Gyuudasi.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Banŋɔ zaa nini n daa moaai n‑la'amni taaba m‑puusiri Naawunni, ban ni pɔ'aba maa, ni Mariama, Yisa ma la, ni Yisa mabiisi.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Tɔ; ka di dabsa maa dahinseaa ka nyaandɔliba maa zaa daa la'am taaba, m‑paai niriba kɔbga ni pisi. Di saha maa ka Piita daa doaai n‑zaani n‑tɔ'asi, ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: M mabiisi, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi daa kyɛ ka NaaDauda daa pun yɛli Gyuudasi yɛla - Gyuudasi sɔ ŋɔn daa beeli ninvugseeba ban daa gbaai Yisa la - ka di sɔbi n‑dooli. Dinŋɔna, soori ka ni see ka u yɛligu maa siri niŋi hoo ban daa yɛli seem wa.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Ni, ŋɔn Gyuudasi nyɛ la ti la'aŋŋu wa nira, ka u nuu bɛ ti tuma wa zaa ni.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Baŋŋma, m Ba Tiyɔfiluusi, ni, ŋɔn Gyuudasi daa deaai ligri u tumbeoo zugu, ka daa zaŋŋi a n‑da puu, ka u daa lu tiŋŋa u nindaa zugu n‑kpi. U puuri daa tasi, ka u puuri ni nyoori daa yi na m‑basi.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Gyɛrusalɛm niriba zaa daa wum di yɛla, ka dizugu ba daa booli puu maa, ni, Akyɛlidama - di gbinni nyɛ la Ziim Ziiya.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Dama di sɔbiya Yuuma gbaŋŋu ni, ni#:- U yiri nin labgi daboo, Sɔ ku laa m‑bɛ ka ni. ka di laa n‑sɔbi, ni#:- Kyɛ ka sɔ deaai u ziiya n‑tumna.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Dizugu di sumni ni ninvugsɔ ti ya la'am ni ti, ni u ti niŋi sɛhara, ni, u daa siri nya ka baŋŋi, ni, ti Dugma Yisa daa yisigi kum ni. U nin nyɛ la dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ti saani sahanseaa ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ ni ka ti daa la'amni n‑tabinni u saani maa, n‑yi Gyɔni ŋɔn daa piili n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom la, halli nti paai dabsiseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa du arizana ni la.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Ka ba daa yiisi lala niriba ayi: Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Baasabasi la, ka ba daa pa u yupaa, ni, Gyasitisi la; ka ŋɔn paasi buyi mun yuuri n daa boonni Matayasi.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Dinŋɔna ba daa susi Naawunni, ni, Ti Dugma, nyini mi sɔkam zaa sufu ni teesa, ka dizugu wulisim ti niriba ayi wa puuni nyin, Naawunni, ŋɔn boori sɔ.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: ni u ti zaani Gyuudasi zaaziiya n‑tumni i tuma maa. Gyuudasi mun daa bakki nti kyaŋŋi u maŋtoori zii la ni.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Ka ba daa tɔ la tɛtɛ ni ba yiisi ba yinni, ka Matayasi daa di tɛtɛ maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u m‑paasi tuumudima piiya ni yinni la ni.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Tɔ. Ka Pɛntikoosi Kyuu maa ŋɔn daa ti paai na, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa zaa daa la'am m‑bɛ duu yinni puuni.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Ba daa zinni, ka di daa bu yuui ka ba daa wum ka bunseelli niŋi vuuri ka di ŋmanni pɛbsigu titaari n‑yiiri saazugu n‑sigri na, ka di daa kyɛ na m‑paali duseaa ban daa bɛ ni la.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Ka ba daa nya ka bunseelli gba daa kpɛ duu maa ni na, n‑ŋmanni bugum zilima. Neesim din daa ŋmanni bugum zilima maa daa purigi n‑zinni ba zaa yinni yinni zugu.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Ka Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi daa kpɛ ba zaa ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu. Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn daa ti ba yɛttɔ'a seem, lala ka sɔkam daa tɔ'asira.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Di saha maa Yɛhudiyadima pam n daa pun la'am m‑bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa puuni; ninvugsuma n daa nyɛ ba, ka ba daa yi duniya zaa tinsi maa ni na.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
