local: Lala niriba maa daa wum vuuri maa, ka dizugu daa kyɛ ka ba daa la'am yiri maa ni na. Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, ban daa ti wum ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba, ba yinni yinni yɛttɔ'a puuni.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Di daa niŋi ba zaa alahazibu pam, ka ba daa boosi taaba n‑yɛli, ni, Yɛlimaŋni, niriba ban tɔ'asiri wa pa la Galilii niriba?.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Dahinseaa ka Piita ni Gyɔni ti kyɛnni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, din nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima Naawunni susigu saha la.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Ka di niŋi la wula ka ti zaa yinni yinni wumni di gbinni u mammantoori yɛttɔ'a puuni?.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Ka ninvugseeba daa zi gbariga na - ninvugsɔ ban pun dɔ'ai u lala u biilim ni, nti dooli Gyeeŋŋu Yiri za'anɔseaa ban daa boonni Za'anɔveelli la ziiya. Ba daa yi pun ziiri u la lala na nti doontiri bɛni dabsa zaa, ka u maanni bara zama maa saani, ban daa yi kpeeri Yiri maa ni sahanseaa la.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Ti seeba bu yi Paatiya na, ni Midiya, ni Ɛlaam m‑paasi? Ti seeba mun bu yi Mɛsɔpɔteemiya na, ni Gyuudiiya, ni Kappadɔsiiya, ni Pɔntusi ni Ɛseaa?.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Tɔ; barimaanna wa n daa nya ka Piita ni Gyɔni ya n‑kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni na, ka u maanni ba bara.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Yaasa m‑paasi, ti seeba yi la Firigyiiya na, ni Pamfiliiya ni Igyipti, ni Sairiini tiŋŋa kan bɛ Libii tingbaŋŋu ya'ari sa - halli ti seeba nyɛ la Room tiŋŋa saamma ka kyɛ na.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Ka Piita mirigi u nini n‑kpa u, ni Gyɔni gba m‑paasi, ka yɛli u, Gɔsim ti.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Yɛhudiyadima n nyɛ ti, ni seeba ban dɔli Yɛhudiyadima kaari: Kyiriti dima n nyɛ ti, ni Arabii dima m‑paasi. Ka ti sɔkam zaasa wumni ka ba tɔ'asiri Naawunni ŋɔn tum tunkara seesi n‑ti ti, ti toori yɛttɔ'a puuni.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Ka u wukki u zugu n‑gɔsiri ba, ka teesiri, ni, ba ya ti u seelli.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Di daa gyɛri daadam maa pam, ka ba daa boosiri taaba n‑yɛtta, Bɔ ka dinŋɔ wulisira.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Ka Piita yɛli u, N ka la'afu ni n ti i, amaa n nin ti i man mari bunseelli: Yisa Masiiya yuuri zugu, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Nazarɛti tiŋŋa nira la, doaam saazugu n‑zaani ka leebi ŋɔn kyɛnna.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Baseeba mun daa tuuri ba n‑yɛtta, Ba nyuuri la daam m‑bugi.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: N‑daa yɛli lala, ka daa gbaai doo maa nudirigu n‑suŋŋi u ni u doaai na. Di saha ka u napɔna ni u napɔnkpaasi daa kpaŋŋi zaa.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Di saha maa ka Yisa tuumudima piiya ni ayi la zaa daa yisigi; ka Piita daa bɛ ba tooni, n‑tasi pampam m‑booli zama maa n‑yɛli, Yarima n Yɛhudiyataaba wa, ni ya zaa ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, ya baŋŋma dinŋɔ gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka wum n yɛligu wa!.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: ka u doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnna, ka dɔli ba halli nti kpɛ Yiri maa puuni. Ka kur gbaai kyɛnni ka yikkri m‑puusiri ka pɛ'ari Naawunni.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Niriba wa bu nyuuri la daam m‑bugi, hoo yan teesiri seem la; di na kur nyɛ la asuba paalli!.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Ka sɔkam daa nya u kyɛnni maa ka pɛ'ari Naawunni.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Ay! Dinŋɔ ka anabi Yɔweelli daa wulisi, ni#:-.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: ka baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna n yi nyɛ gbariga n‑zintiri Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Za'anɔveelli ziiya m‑maanni bara la. Ka din daa niŋi doo maa daa gyɛri ba ka daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Naawunni yɛliya#:- Di nin niŋi Dabsiri maa daari - N nin zaŋŋi n Vuusim Kasi n‑kpeesi nira kam zaa ni; N nin kyɛ ka ya biisi nin tɔ'asiri man Naawunni yɛligu - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa. Ya na'akyimbisi nin ti nyari din nin ti niŋi, ka ya ninvugkura nin ti zaasiri zaasimaŋa.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Tɔ; gbariga maa daa kur vili Piita ni Gyɔni m‑mari ba kirikiri, ka daadam zaa daa kabi n‑zɔri n‑dɔli ba, halli nti la'am Yiri maa maasim ziseaa ban daa boonni NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa la ni.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Yɛlimaŋni, n nin teaai halli n daaba gba yɛla - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa - ka n nin zaŋŋi n Sii maa n‑ti ba, Ka u nin kyɛ ka ba tɔ'asiri man Naawunni yɛligu.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Piita ŋɔn daa nya lala, u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, Yan Izraɛli dima wa, bɔ n niŋi ka yɛlli wa niŋi ya alahazibu? Bee wula ka ya ti gɔsiri ti hoo tirima mammaŋŋa yiiko zugu n kyɛ ka gbariga wa doaai n‑kyɛnni wa? - bee, ti mammaŋŋa ban nyɛ ninvugsuma zugu.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: N nin wulisi n kpeeŋŋu sangbana ni, ka n nin zaŋŋi di n‑wulisi tingbaŋŋu ni gba, kaman ziim ni bugum ni nyoosi.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ay! Naawunni tuma nla - Naawunni ŋɔn daa nya pɛ'abu ti yaadima saani: Ibrahim daa gyeem u, ni Yisahaaku, ni Yaakubu - ŋɔn Naawunni n daa pun duusi u yiribii Yisa yuuri: amaa yarima daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑taasi Gɔmna Pailiti saani nti kari u sariya, ka Gɔmna ŋɔn daa boori ni u basi u sahanseaa la ya daa za'asi u yɛla.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Wuntaŋŋa maa nin zibgi limm, Namboaa maa mun nin moaai hoo ziim. Ka dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ya Dugma Dabsiri maa nin ti paai na, dabsititaari n nyɛ ka, ni bunveelli.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Ii! - Ninvugsuŋŋu sɔ ŋɔn daa laa n‑nyɛ Naawunni nirimaŋni ka ya daa za'asi, ka daa laa n‑kyɛ ka Gɔmna zaŋŋi ninvugkuurisɔ m‑basi n‑ti ya.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Ka ŋɔnkam zaa mi ti Dugma yuuri nti susiri u, nin nya tuliginsim. .

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: ka ku Nyɔvuri Daana maa. Naawunni mun daa neaai u kum ni. Tirima n daa nya ka baŋŋi, ni, lala n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Ka dinŋɔna, ya Izraɛli niriba, ya wumma n yɛttɔ'a wa! N tɔ'asiri Yisa ŋɔn nyɛ Nazarɛti nira la yɛla, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi la. Ka ya gba baŋŋi u yɛla, Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka u daa tum alahazibu tuma ya sunsukkuni maa zugu.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu da kyɛ ka gbariga wa kpaŋŋi, u yuuri yɛdda niŋbu zugu. Ya nyari doo wa ka mi u; ŋɔn niŋi Yisa yuuri yɛdda maa kyɛ ka u nya alaafɛ zaa pampaŋŋɔa, ka ya mammansi nini nya.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Ka di zaa nyaaŋŋa di daa niŋi hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun teesi n‑zaali seem la; ka ba daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑niŋi ya nuu ni, ka ya mun daa deaai u n‑tisi ninvugbeeri ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku u.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, M mabiisi, m baŋŋiya halli, ni, ziilinsi zugu ka ya daa niŋi Yisa lala, yan ni ya kpamma zaa.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Amaa Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni, n‑tiligi u kum wahala puuni, dama soori daa ka ni ni kum kpalim m‑mari u.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Naawunni mun daa pun kyɛ ka u anabidima zaa tɔ'asi u Masiiya yɛla, ni, Naawunni ya n‑tum Masisɔ na ni u ti su ti naam fukumsi la: ka kyɛ ka ba yɛli m‑paasi, ni, ŋɔn Masiiya maa nin ti di wahala pam. Tɔ: yan daa niŋi Yisa seem maa zugu, di daa siri niŋi lala.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: NaaDauda daa bu tɔ'asi u yɛla? Ni#:- N daa daŋŋi n‑nya ka baŋŋi, ni, n Dugma nin bɛ n tooni saha kam; Ŋɔna n zɛ n nudirigu ziiya ka n ku laa n‑damna.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Dizugu ya niŋma tuuba ka ŋmaligi n‑kyɛ ya tumbeeri tuma zaa, ka Naawunni nin deaai ya taalli zaa sindaani.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Lala zugu ka n sufu daa maai, ka n zulimni tɔ'asiri nya'asim pam: Ka m mari la tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n ningbiŋŋu yɛla.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: Dama dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti Dugma nin taasi maasim saha na n‑ti ya, ka naayi tum ŋɔn pun yiisi Masisɔ nti diisi naam fukumsi maa, ŋɔn Yisa nla, ka kyɛ ka u labi na n‑di di zaa.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Nyin, Naawunni, ku basi n siiya n‑ti kpiimma tiŋŋa, Bee, i kyɛ ka i bisuŋŋu maa nya poaari.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn nin laa n‑kpalim m‑bɛ arizana ni, halli ka Naawunni ti kyɛ ka seelli kam zaa nin ti siri maali n‑niŋi hoo ŋɔn boori seem. Sahanseaa kan gaari la sa u daa tisi lala noori ka kyɛ ka u ninvugsuma ni anabidima daa wulisi ti lala.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Nyini n wulisiri ma i beesigu soori, Ka kyɛ ka n bɛ i tooni ka nya sufupeelim pam.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Musa daa yɛliya: Ti Dugma Naawunni nin ti yiisi sɔ ya mabiisi puuni n‑kyɛ ka u niŋi anabi, kaman ŋɔn daa yiisi ma seem la. ni, U yi ti yɛli ya seelli kam, ya dɔli ka lala.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: Tɔ; dizugu, m mabiisi, ya kyɛ ka n yɛli ya paalu ni, ni, ti yaaba NaaDauda daa ka ni, ka ba sɔ'ai u, ka u gballi na kpalim m‑bɛ kpa, halli nti paai zuna dabsiri wa.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Ka sɔ kam zaa ŋɔn nin ti za'asi lala anabi yɛligu maa wumpu, ŋɔna nin ti ya m‑baasi yɔri. .

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Amaa ŋɔn Dauda daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni pɔ noori n‑ti u, ni, ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi u za'anɔri bisɔ n‑diisi u u naam maa. Ka yɛli m‑paasi, ni, Naawunni ku basi u siiya n‑ti kpiimma tiŋŋa, bee, n‑kyɛ ka u ningbiŋŋu maa nin poaai la; ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa, u daa tɔ'asi la anabi yɛligu, ka daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri la Masiiya la kum yisigri la yɛla.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, Yɛlimaŋni, anabidima zaa - n‑yi Samuwɛli halli ni zuna - ba zaa daa tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti zuna saha wa yɛla.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Tirima muna zaa daa pun baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni daa yisigi ŋɔn Yisa kum ni, ka ti tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya, ni, u siri yisigiya.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Ya gba nyɛ la anabidima wa ya'ansi, ka ya su nangbambasiseelli Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi n‑ti ya yaadima maa. U daa yɛli ti yaaba Ibrahim, ni, u biisi zuliya zugu ka duniya wa niriba zaa nin nya zugsuŋŋu.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: Ka u naayi doaai n‑du saazugu n‑zi u zizii kan bɛ Naawunni nudirigu maa ni: n‑ziya, ka niŋi ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori ni u nin niŋi seem la, ka daa deaai Vuusim Kasi maa u Ba saani ka tum u ti saani na. Ka yan nyari ka wumni seelli wa zaa yi la ŋɔn Vuusim saani na.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Dizugu u daa yiisi u yiribii maa n‑tum u na, ni u ti kyɛ ka yarima daŋŋi n‑nya lala zugsuŋŋu maa. Ya beesigu takkri zugu ka ya zaa nin yi ya tumbeeri ni.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Ya bu nya? - di daa pa la Dauda mammaŋŋa n daa du arizana ni, amaa u daa yɛli, ni#:- Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, Ni, Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: Halli ka n nin zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni. .

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Lala zugu ya kyɛ ka ya Izraɛli niriba zaa sakki ka baŋŋi, ni, halli ni ya daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ku u maa zaa yɔri, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u ti niŋi ti Dugma, ni Masiiya ti Tuligra.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa wum yɛttɔ'a wa n‑naai la, ba sufu daa sa'amiya pam, ka ba daa boosi Piita ni u taaba maa, ni, Ti mabiisi, wula ka ti ya n‑niŋi.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, Tuuba! Ya kyɛli ya tumbeeri tuma m‑basi, ka ya sɔkam zaa yinni yinni deaai Naawunni koom suubu, Yisa Masiiya Tuligra yuuri zugu. Ka Naawunni nin kyɛ ya taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ya, ka ya nin deaai piini Naawunni saani, piini maa mun nyɛ Naawunni Sii Kasi kpeebu ya sufu ni.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Dama Naawunni n daa pun pɔ noori maa n‑ti yarima, ni ya biisi maa; n‑ti seeba ban bɛ wa'aligu ni seeba zaa ti Dugma ŋɔn ti ya m‑booli la.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Tɔ; ka Piita daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'asuma pam m‑paasi ba, ka laa n‑kpamsi ba nimmoaa, n‑yɛli, ni, Ya tuligma ya mansi n‑yi zuna niriba wa sunsukkuni na, dama bumpoosa n kur nyɛ ba.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Ka zama maa ni seeba ban daa niŋi yɛdda n‑niŋi u yɛligu maa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu. Di dabsiri maa ka ba ninvugtusa ata n daa paasi Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ni.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑wumni Yisa tuumudima wulisigu maa ni nimbiri moaa, ka la'amni ni ban n‑diri diibu ka puusiri Naawunni ni taaba.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Tɔ; ka dabeem daa sigi sɔkam zaa zugu na, ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u tuumudima tumni tunkara ni alahazibu yɛla.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Ka ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa la'am m‑bɛ ni, ka daa sakki n‑yɛtti, ni, ba zaa marigu nyɛ la yimmu.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Ba daa koosiri ban mari neenseesi la m‑putti ligri maa n‑tiri taaba, hoo sɔkam bukaata ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Ba daa la'am m‑bɛ ni dabsiri kam zaa Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka laa n‑la'amni taaba n‑diri diibu ba mammantoori yiya ni. Ba daa diri nya'asim pam ka ba sufu daa maai zaa.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: Ba daa pɛ'ari Naawunni, ka niriba zaa mun daa boori ba yɛla. Ka beoo kam zaa ti Dugma daa kyɛ ka niriba daa nya tuliginsim n‑la'amni na m‑paasiri ba zugu.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Tɔ. Piita ni Gyɔni daa na kur bɛ ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni n‑tiri zama maa, ka maalimaanniba daa kyɛ na, ni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi toondaana, ni Saddusidima maa m‑paasi.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Ban daa ti paai ba saani na la, ba sufu daa yisigiya pam dama Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa tɔ'asiri ka wulisiri zama maa, ni, niriba nin ti yisigi kum puuni hoo Yisa ŋɔn daa pun yisigi seem la.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Ka dizugu ba daa gbaai Piita ni Gyɔni n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni, ka guri ba ni beoo neaai, din daa pun zibgiri maa zugu.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, dama niriba pam ban daa wum Piita ni Gyɔni yɛttɔ'a la daa sakki ba teesa puuni n‑ti Yisa. Seeba ban zaa daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa nyɛ la dɔppa tusa anu.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ba daa mari ba Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑booli zuguraandima ni ba kyam na n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa - ban ni tinkpamma ni karimbadima maa zaa.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Annasi sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana gba daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni, ni Kayafasi ni Gyɔni ni Alɛkzanda ni ba dɔ'am kpamma zaa.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Ba daa zaali Piita ni Gyɔni kpamma maa tooni, ka boosi ba n‑yɛnni, ni, Ya zaŋŋi la kpeeŋŋu bɔa, bee ŋɔni yuuri ka ya mara, n‑niŋi dinŋɔa.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Bɛni ka Naawunni Sii daa suŋŋi Piita ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, Toondaandima wumma! Kpamma wumma!.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: Ya kur moaai la nini m‑boosiri ti zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsunseaa din sa niŋi ka gbariga wa nya tippu:.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: tɔ! - ya baŋŋma, halli Izraɛli tiŋŋa niriba zaasa baŋŋma, ni, di nyɛ la Nazarɛti doo la, Yisa Masiiya, yuuri zugu n kyɛ ka gbariga wa zaani ya tooni zuna ka mari alaafɛ wa. Ŋɔn Yisa ka ya daa kyɛ ka ba gbaai u n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, amaa Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u daa yisigi u kum puuni.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Di daa nyɛ la Yisa ka Naawunni Litaafi la daa yɛtti u yɛla, ni#:- Tammeeriba, ya wumma! Yan daa za'asi kugseaa la - Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Yisa kɔkɔ nin ti fa ya m‑basi, dama Naawunni daa bu kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ bɛ duniya wa ni ka nira yi ti booli u yuuri ka u fa u m‑basi, m‑paasi ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Ka di daa nyɛ la alahazibu n‑tiri ban daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni maa, Piita ni Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri ni sufukpeemni seem ka daa bu mari dabeem ba yɛttɔ'a maa puuni zugu. Ni, ba pa la karikyidima, amaa zugsabilli dima n kur nyɛ ba. Ka ba daa baŋŋi, ni, ba nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑yɛli seelli, dama ba nini daa nya ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ tippu la daa bɛ ba saani n‑zɛya.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Di saha ka ba yɛli ba, ni, ba yim duu maa ni n‑gura, ka daa naayi piili n‑tɔ'asi taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba yɛla.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: ni, Ti ya niŋi dɔppa wa la wula? Ninvugseeba zaa ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni mi halli, ni, ba tum tunkarigu. Ti ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, di pa lala.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Tɔ - ya kyɛ ka ti kpaamni ba, ni, ba di laa n‑zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti sɔsɔa. Lala nin kyɛ ka yɛttɔ'a wa ku laa m‑moonni n‑tiri niriba.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Ka ba daa laa m‑booli ba ni ba kpɛ na, ka yɛli ba, ni, sɔseaa laa n‑ka ni ka ba nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑tɔ'asi niriba, bee, n‑wulisi niriba.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Amaa Piita ni Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya gaŋŋma ya mammansi din tuusi Naawunni saani: ti sakkma n‑ti ya noori la, bee, ti sakkma n‑ti Naawunni noori?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Tirima muna - soori ka ni ka ti ku tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tin daa nya ka wum bunseesi la.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Ka kpamma daa maali n‑kpaamni ba, ka daa zaŋŋi ba m‑basi ni ba kyaŋŋi. Ka daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑namsi ba, dama daadam zaa daa pɛ'ari Naawunni, din daa niŋi seem maa zugu.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nya tippu n‑wulisi Naawunni gyirima ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa muna - ba daa dɔ'ai u ka di paai yuuma pisinaasi.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Tɔ. Ka ban daa zaŋŋi Piita ni Gyɔni m‑basi la, ba daa labi ba taaba saani nti yɛli ba, ka daa kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpamma maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa zaa.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Ka ba zaasa ban la'amni m‑bɛ ni maa daa pɛ'ai Naawunni, ni, Ti Daana Naawunni, nyini n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa naam saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu maa, ni teeku gba, ni seelikam zaa an bɛ a puuni.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: I daa kyɛ ka i Vuusim Kasi suŋŋi i yiribii la, ŋɔn nyɛ ti yaaba Dauda, ka u daa yɛli#:- Bɔzugu ka tinzundima nyuuri bara? Bɔzugu ka tinsi pam niriba teesiri teesiyɔya?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Duniya wa nadima la'am la taaba, - Zuguraandima la'asi m‑bɛ luginyinni, Ni ba ti tuusi ti Dugma ni u Masiiya maa tɔppu.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka ba daa siri la'am taaba tiŋŋa wa puuni, ni ba ti tuusi Yisa: ŋɔn mun nyɛ la i bisuŋŋu sɔ nyin daa diisi Masiiya maa, ka Pɔntiyusi Pailiti, ni NaaHɛroodi - halli tinzundima ni Izraɛli tiŋŋa niriba zaa - daa la'asi na n‑tuusi u.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Ba daa tum seela zaa nyin, Naawunni, ŋɔn daa pun teesi n‑zaali seem maa.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ, ti Dugma, wumma ban yɛli ti seem, ni, ba nin namsi ti. Tim ti sufukpeemni ni ti ti tɔ'asi i yɛla.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: ka kyɛ ka i nuu yaa bɛ ti saani, ni ti tibi baaridima ka tumni tunkara ni alahazibu tuma i maŋtoori bii Yisa yuuri ni.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ka ban daa puusi lala n‑naai sahanseaa maa, luginseaa ban daa la'am m‑bɛ ni la daa miimni pam. Naawunni Sii Kasi daa kpɛ ba n‑ti ba yaa, ni ba ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛligu nimmoosi, ka bu mari dabeem.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Di saha maa ka ninvugseeba zaa ban daa sakki n‑ti Yisa la daa mari nangbanyinni ni sufu yinni. Ba sɔsɔ daa bu yɛli, ni, u marigu kam zaa nyɛ la ŋɔn kɔkɔ dinni: ba daa la'amni n‑tɔtti seelikam zaa n‑tiri taaba.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Yisa tuumudima la daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛla ni yaa, n‑yɛtti niriba, ni, ti Dugma Yisa yisigi kum ni ka ba mammansi nini nya. Ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba zaa mari sufumaasim pam.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: ka fara daa laa n‑ka ba sɔa. Dama seeba zaa ban daa mari puri bee yiya la daa yi koosiri a ni m‑mari ligri maa na.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: nti ti tuumudima la, ka ba tɔri n‑ti sɔ zaa ŋɔn mari bukaata.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Di daa niŋi lala; ka dɔsɔ daa bɛni ka u yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi, amaa Yisa tuumudima maa daa pa u yupaa, ni, Baanabasi - di gbinni nyɛ la Saawaradaana. Liivai za'anɔri nira n daa nyɛ u, ka ba daa dɔ'ai u Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Doo wa daa koosi u tingbanni ka daa mari ligri maa na n‑ti tuumudima maa.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Din daari asuba pinipini - Alahadaari n‑daa la - halli beoo ŋɔn daa na bu neaai la, Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, daa kyaŋŋi dabɔri maa ni. Ka u daa ti nya ka kugri maa laa n‑ka dabɔri maa noori ni.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ka u daa zɔ n‑labi Saimoon Piita saani na, ŋɔn ni Yisa nyaandɔla sɔa, dɔsɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa wuri pam la. M‑paai ba na nti yɛli ba, ni, Ba zaŋŋi ti Dugma n‑yiisi u dabɔri maa ni na, ka ti bu mi ba n zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi seela.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ka Piita ni nyaandɔla wa daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi dabɔri maa ni.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Ba bayi maa daa zɔra, ka ŋɔnla daa zɔ n‑gaari Piita n‑daŋŋi u tooni m‑paai dabɔri maa na.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: U daa kunni n‑nya tampeela maa ŋɔn dɔya, amaa u daa bu kpɛ dabɔri duu maa ni.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Di saha ka Piita daa kari u nɔba ka naayi paai na nti kpɛ dabɔri duu maa ni. Ŋɔn gba daa gɔsi n‑nya tampeela ka a dɔya.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: ka daa laa n‑nya kyinkyinseaa ba n daa zaŋŋi m‑pili Yisa zugu la - kana mun daa kpabi ni n‑dɔ kpaŋŋa sa, ka daa ka tampeela maa ziiya.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Di saha maa ka nyaandɔla la gba daa kpɛa - sɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi tooni m‑paai bɛni la. Ŋɔn mun daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka daa niŋi yɛdda.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Ka ba bayi maa daa labi n‑kuli.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Mariama mun daa kpalim n‑zɛ la dabɔri maa zii n‑kumna. U daa kur kpalim n‑kumni maa, ka kunni n‑gɔsi dabɔri duu maa puuni.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: nti nya malaakadima ayi ka ba yɛ neenpeela n‑zi n‑tabi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa ŋɔn daa dɔ seela. Ba yinni n daa zi Yisa zugu ya'ari ni, ka ŋɔnla mun daa zi u gbaya ya'ari.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ka ba daa boosi Mariama n‑yɛli, Pɔ'a, bɔ n niŋi ka i kumna?; ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ba zaŋŋi la n Dugma, ka m bu mi ba n zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi seela.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: Di saha ka u daa ŋmaligi u nyaaŋŋa nti nya Yisa ka u zɛya, amaa u daa bu baŋŋi, ni, Yisa nla.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Ka Yisa daa boosi u, ni, M ma, bɔ n niŋi ka i kumna? I boori la sɔ ziiya, bee?: ŋɔn mun daa teesi, ni, gaadi maa daana n nyɛ u, ka dizugu u daa yɛli u, ni, M Bapira, nyin yi zaŋŋi u m‑bakki, nyin wulisi ma ka n kyaŋŋi nti zaŋŋi u.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, Mariama!, ka ŋɔn mun daa ŋmaligi u saani nti yɛli u, ni, Rabooni! .

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, Di siisiri ma, dama n na bu labi m Ba maa saani. See ka i kyaŋŋi nti yɛli m mabiisi maa, ni, n doaaya ka n ya n‑labi m Ba Naawunni saani, ka u nyɛ la ba gba Ba Naawunni.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Di saha ka ŋɔn Mariama Magdala la daa kyaŋŋi nti yɛli Yisa nyaandɔliba maa, ni, Mani n nya ti Dugma!, ni, u daa yɛli anŋɔ zaa n‑ti u.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Di dabsiri maa, Alahadaari maa, zaanoori saha, ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa la'am taaba n‑kpɛ duu ni nti kpari dunoori maa, dama ba daa zɔri la Yɛhudiya dima maa dabeem. Di saha ka Yisa daa kpɛ na nti zaani ba sunsukkuni m‑puusi ba.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa puusi ba n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi ni u pulugri n‑wulisi ba. Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba maa sufu daa maai pam, ni, ba nyaya, ni, ba Dugma nla.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑puusi ba yaasa, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, M Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na seem la, lala ka n ya n‑tum yarima gba.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka u daa vuusi vuusim n‑ti ba ka yɛli, Ya deaam Sii Kasi la.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Ya yi ti kyɛ sɔ tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, di daama siri nin nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi: ka yan mun yi kyɛ ka sɔ taalli ti kpalim n‑dɔli u, di daama nin kpalim m‑mari taalli.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Tɔ, ka Tɔmaasi daa ka bɛni, ban daa pa sɔ yupaa, ni, Gyeaa la: Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ ba saani na sahanseaa la u daa ka ba saani.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Sahanseaa ka u nyaandɔlitaaba daa ti nya u nti yɛli u, ni, Ti nya ti Dugma!, ŋɔn Tɔmaasi daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, M mammaŋŋa yi bu nya u nuusi ka kpaasi daalima m bɛ a ni, halli n‑laa n‑zaŋŋi n nubila wa n‑tum u nuusi dansi wa ni, ka zaŋŋi n nuu n‑kpeesi u pulugri la daŋŋa ni gba - ay! di yi pa lala n ku niŋi yɛttɔ'a maa yɛdda.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Ka di daa niŋi dabsa anii ka ba daa maali n‑la'am n‑kpɛ duu ni, ka Tɔmaasi mun gba daa bɛ ba saani. Ba daa laa n‑kpari dunoori, ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ na nti zaani ba sunsukkuni m‑puusi ba.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Di saha ka u daa yɛli Tɔmaasi, ni, Tɔ! zaŋŋi i nubila n‑tum na n‑nya n nuusi, ka zaŋŋi i nuu n‑kpeesi n pulugri wa daŋŋa wa ni! Ka di laa n‑za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu, amaa ka niŋi yɛdda.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Ka Tɔmaasi daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Nyin n nyɛ Naawunni n‑ti ma: n Dugma n nyɛ i.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Yisa mun daa yɛli u, I nini n nya maa, ka di zugu ka i niŋni yɛdda, bee? Zugsuŋŋu siri nin bɛ ni n‑tiri seeba ban bu nya amaa ka niŋni yɛdda.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Tɔ: di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni - Yisa siri daa tum tunkara ni makkri yɛla pampam m‑paasi anŋɔ zugu, ka daa wulisi u nyaandɔliba maa. Pa anŋɔ yɛla zaa n sɔbi n‑niŋi bukku wa puuni.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Aseesi mun sɔbi n‑dooli kpa, ni ya ti niŋi yɛdda ka baŋŋi, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Masiiya maa, Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ na ka diisi u naam la - halli Naawunni Bii n nyɛ u gba. Ka anŋɔ sɔbiya, ni ya ti niŋi lala yɛdda ka lala zugu ya nin ti nya nyɔvuri ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Tɔ. Ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Ananayasi, ka u pɔ'a mun yuuri Safayira. Ŋɔna n daa koosi u tingbanseaa.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: ka ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a daa niŋi nangbanyinni ka u sɔ'ai ligri maa seesi n‑ti u maŋŋa, ka daa zaŋŋi an kpalim m‑mari na n‑dooli tuumudima maa tooni.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli, Ananayasi! Wula ka Sintaani paali i sufu ni, halli ka i ma'ai ziri n‑ti Sii Kasi maa, ka zaŋŋi la'aseesi nyin daa deaai i tingbanni zugu maa n‑yiisi aseesi n‑sɔ'ai m‑mara?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Tingbanni maa daa pa la i dinni? Ka nyin daa koosi ka maa, i daa bu su ligri maa? Bɔ n kyɛ ka i niŋi lala? I bu ma'ai n‑ti la ninsaala, amaa Naawunni.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananayasi ŋɔn daa wum nŋɔ maa, u daa lu tiŋŋa n‑kpi. Ka dabeem pam daa gbaai ban daa wum din daa niŋi maa zaa.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Ka na'akyimbisi maa daa doaai m‑pili u ka zaŋŋi u n‑zi n‑yi nti sɔ'ai.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Tɔ, ka di daa yuui beela ka u pɔ'a daa kpɛ na, ka daa bu mi din pun niŋi.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Piita daa boosi u, ni, Yɛlima! la'aseesi ban daa yɔ n‑ti nyin ni Ananayasi tingbanni maa zugu la zaa nŋɔa? Ka u daa sakki, ni, Ii, ka ligri maa nla.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli u, Wula ka ya niŋi nangbanyinni ni ya zamsi ti Dugma Sii maa? Gɔsima! ninvugseeba ban da zaŋŋi i sira n‑sɔ'ai la nɔba na bɛ la za'anɔri ni wa, ka ba nin zi i gba n‑yi.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Di saha ka ŋɔn gba daa lu tiŋŋa Piita tooni ka kpi. Ka na'akyimbisi maa daa labi n‑kpɛ na, n‑nya u ka u kpi, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑zi n‑yi n‑sɔ'ai u sira maa lugri.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Dabeem pam daa gbaai Yisa nyaandɔliba ban daa la'amni taaba maa, ni ninvugseeba ban daa ti wum lahabaari maa zaa m‑paasi.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka tuumudima maa daa tumni alahazibu tuma pam niriba maa saani n‑wulisiri u gyirima. Ba zaa daa kur la'amni la NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa maa maasim ni.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Sɔsɔ daa ka sufukpeemni din nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka ba la'am n‑tabli ba saani, halli ni nyaandɔliba maa daa mari gyirima niriba maa saani pam.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Ni di zaa yɔri, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba pam daa niŋni ti Dugma yɛdda m‑paasira nyaandɔliba ban pun bɛ ni maa zugu.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Lala maa zugu ka niriba daa ziiri ba baaridima n‑kyɛnni sɔkpaŋŋa n‑doontiri gadu ni sɔna zugu, ni, Piita yi ti gatta, baseeba di yi ti kpɛ halli u sisii maasim ni gba.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Zama daa la'am tinkpansi maa ni, n‑kyɛnni Gyɛrusalɛm na n‑ziiri ba baaridima maa na, m‑paasi baseeba arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa daamni maa, ka ba zaa daa nya tippu.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa ni u taaba ban daa dɔli Saddusidima soori la daa doaai n‑zabri ba nyuuri pam.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Ba daa gbaai tuumudima maa n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Amaa yuŋŋu daa ti paai ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yoaai sarika maa dunɔya maa ka daa yiisi ba na.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: ni, Ya kyɛma nti zaani Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni n‑yɛli niriba maa lahabaari maa zaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi beesigu paalli wa ni.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Tɔ; beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ba daa kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka daa piili n‑wulisiri niriba hoo malaaka maa ŋɔn yɛli ba seem la. Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ni u nyaaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti paai kootu duu ni ka daa booli kpambaya maa ni Izraɛli kpamma zaa ni ba la'am na, ba daa tum sarika maa ni m‑boori tuumudima maa.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Amaa Pirinsi maa ŋɔn daa paai sarika maa ni, ba daa bu nya ba bɛni, ka daa labi na n‑tisi lahabaari.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: ni, Ti nya ka dunɔya maa kpari ni suŋŋa, ka ban guri maa zɛ za'anɔya maa ni, amaa tin yoaai ti bu nya sɔ duu maa puuni.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Ban daa wum lahabaari maa, di daa gyɛri Pirinsi toondaana ni gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa pam, ka ba daa birisiri, ni, anŋɔ ya n‑naai la wula?.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Di saha maa ka sɔ daa kpɛ na n‑yɛli, Ya gɔsima! Yan sa zaŋŋi dɔseeba n‑niŋi dansarika duu ni la - ba zɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisiri niriba maa.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi toondaana maa daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi u nyaaŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi nti zi tuumudima maa na; amaa ba daa bu zaŋŋi yaa n‑gbaai ba, dama ba daa zɔri la zama maa dabeem, ni, di pa seem ba nin lɔbi ba kuga.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Ban daa zi ba na sahanseaa la, ba daa kyɛ ka ba zaani la'aŋŋu maa puuni, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi ba, ni.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: Ti daa bu zaali ya zaalikpeemni, ni, yan di laa n‑wulisiri yuuri wa zugu?, ni, Amaa ya mun daa pun kyɛ ka yɛligu wa yɛligi m‑paali Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa zaa. Halli ya boori ni ya kyɛ ka doo wa kum taalli tabli ti gba.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Ka Piita ni u taaba daa labsi n‑yɛli, Ti nin sakki la Naawunni noori, ka pa ninsaala noori.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Naawunni, sɔ ti badima ŋɔn daa gyeem maa: ŋɔna n daa neaai Yisa kum ni. Yarima muna daa pun zaŋŋi u n‑kpaasi n‑yuli doo zugu ka ku u.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Ŋɔn ka Naawunni mun daa duusi n‑ziili u nudirigu ya'ari ziziiya, hoo nabiiya ni tuligra. U nin ti kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima takki ba halibeoo n‑kyɛ ala'akyi, ka Naawunni naayi kyɛ ba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Tirima n nyɛ ban nya ka wum anŋɔ zaa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem la, ka ti yɛtti niriba lala; ka yɛtti zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Vuusim Kasi yɛla, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti seeba ban deaai u yɛligu maa.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Ban daa wum dinŋɔ maa, suuri daa ku ba, ka ba daa boori ni ba ku tuumudima maa.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Amaa Farisisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Gameeliyɛli daa doaai ni u tɔ'asi la'aŋŋu maa ni. U daa nyɛ zaaligu la karimbasɔ daadam maa zaa ŋɔn daa tisiri gyirima n‑gaari karimbadima zaa. U daa yɛli, ni, ba yiisim niriba maa beela.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: ka u daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba n‑yɛli, ni, Ya Izraɛli dima wa, ya teesim suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi yan ya n‑zaŋŋi dɔppa wa n‑niŋi seem maa.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Ya bu nya? - sahanseaa la ka Tiyudaasi daa yi na n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugtibsisɔa, ka niriba kɔbsinaasi daa dɔli u; amaa ba daa ku u, ka ŋɔn ni ban yɛla zaa daa yi naai la yɔri.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Ŋɔnŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Gyuudasi, Galilii nira la, gba daa yi na, niriba kaaligu la saha, ka daa zaŋŋi ninvugseeba ka ba daa bu sakki Gɔmnanti yɛligu. Ba daa ku u gba ka u nyaandɔliba zaa daa wurigi.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Dizugu, yɛttɔ'aseaa kan zɛya wa ni, man ya n‑kpaamni ya, ni, ya kyɛli dɔppa wa m‑basi, ka ba kyaŋŋi. Dama ba yɛligu, ni ba tuma maa, yi nyɛ la ninsaala dinni, a nin lu.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Amaa a yi yi la Naawunni saani na, ya ku toaai n‑goaai niriba wa. Di ya n‑kyɛ ka ya zabri ni Naawunni! Ka u yɛttɔ'a wa daa kyɛ ka ba takki ba teesa.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Ka ba daa booli tuumudima maa na n‑feebi ba, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, ba di laa n‑tɔ'asi Yisa yuuri yɛla, ka daa kyɛ ka ba bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Ba mun daa yi la'aŋŋu maa ni ni sufumaasim, dama ba daa sumsi ni ba diisi ba vi Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Beoo kam ba daa bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni niriba yiya puuni, ka daa bu kyɛ wulisigu; ba daa na kpalim m‑moonni la lahabasuŋŋu, ni, Yisa n nyɛ Masiiya - Masisɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tum na ka laa n‑diisi u naam.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Sahanseaa Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa zoaari m‑paasiri la, nangbankpeemni n daa lu ba puuni; di daa nyɛ la seeba ban tɔ'asiri Griiktiri la ni ban tɔ'asiri Hibruuli la sunsukkuni. Griiktiri dima maa daa galimni la Hibruuli dima maa, ni, Ban yi tɔtti diibu n‑tisiri dabsa zaa la, ti pukɔya bu nyari ba tɔrli.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Lala zugu ka tuumudima piiya ni ayi la daa booli nyaandɔliba zaa ni ba la'amma taaba, ka yɛli ba, ni, Tirima kyɛli Naawunni yɛligu tuma ka gɔsiri diibu yɛla, bee? Ay! - lala ka soori!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Dizugu, ti mabiisi, ya yiisim ya niriba ayɔpɔi, seeba ban bɛ ninvugsuma m‑mari Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko, ni yɛm; ka ti ti kyɛ ka ba su diibu tuma maa.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Ka tirima mun zaŋŋi ti sufu zaa n‑niŋni Naawunni puusigu ni lahabasuŋŋu wulisigu ni.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Ka ba zaa daa sakki n‑ti yɛligu wa, ka daa yiisi Sitiivin ŋɔn daa mari yɛdda pam ni Vuusim Kasi yiiko la, ni Filipi ni Pirɔkyɔrusi, ni Nikanoori, ni Taimoon, ni Paamɛnaasi, ni Nikɔlaasi ŋɔn daa yi Antiyɔkku ni na la, ka daa pun yi Griika dima gyeeŋŋu soori la ni, ni u ti dɔli Yɛhudiya dima dinni.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Niriba wa ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisiri tuumudima maa, ka ba daa susi Naawunni n‑ti ba ka zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ba zugri n‑diisi ba tuma maa.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa ka Naawunni wulisigu wumpu maa daa kur yɛligri ni, ka ban daa dɔli Yisa soori maa kaaligu daa nɔbi Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, halli ka Yɛhudiyadima maalimaanniba pam daa sakki n‑deaai Yisa yɛdda.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Sitiivin muna - Naawunni daa zaŋŋi gyirima ni yiiko n‑ti u, ka u daa tum alahazibu tunkara ka Yɛhudiyadima niriba daa nya.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Amaa ninvugseeba daa doaai n‑ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni ni Sitiivin. Ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni ban daa boonni Sumaŋŋa La'aŋŋu Duu la ni maa n daa nyɛ ba, ni Sairiini dima ni Alɛkzandiriya dima, ni seeba ban daa yi Silisii na, ni Ɛseaa.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Amaa ba daa bu nyaŋŋi Sitiivin, dama Naawunni Siiya yɛm daa bɛ u yɛttɔ'a maa ni.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Dizugu ba daa bɔ dɔseeba ni ba yɛlima, ni, ba wumiya ka Sitiivin tɔ'asiri n‑galimni Musa yɛla, halli ni Naawunni yɛla gba.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ban daa tɔ'asi ba ziri yɛttɔ'a lala maa, ba daa kyɛ ka niriba zaa daa doaai n‑gbaai Sitiivin, ban ni ba kpamma ni ba karimbadima maa m‑paasi, m‑mari u n‑kyaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa kpambaya saani ni ba kari u sariya.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Ba daa kyɛ ka ninvugseeba kpɛ na n‑tɔ'asi m‑ma'ai ziri, ni, Doo wa kur tɔ'asiri ni n‑galimni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la, ni zaaligu maa.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Ti wumiya ka u yɛli Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛ Nazarɛti nira la yɛla, ni, ŋɔna nin sa'am Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa, ka takki ti kaya seesi Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ti la.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Ka seeba ban daa zi sariya karibu zii la ni zaa nini daa gɔsiri la Sitiivin la, ka ba daa ti nya u nindaa maa, ka ka ŋmanni la hoo malaaka dinni.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Ka Sɔla gba daa bɛ ni n‑sakkri ban daa ku Sitiivin yɛla maa. Ka Sitiivin ŋɔn daa kpi la, ninvugsunseeba daa kpikki u n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai, ka daa fabli pam u kum zugu. Tɔ: ka di saha maa ba daa piili n‑namsiri Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa la'amni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni maa. Dizugu ba daa zɔ n‑yirigri zaa, n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu; kulla Yisa tuumudima kɔkɔ n daa kpalim timpuuni.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Sɔla mun daa kpalim n‑dɔla Yisa niriba maa ni nimmoaa, ni u ti sa'am ba yɛla. U daa dɔli la yiri yiri n‑gbaasiri ba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa, baseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori la, m‑mari ba n‑kyɛnni n‑kpatti dansarika ni.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Tɔ; ka baseeba ban daa zɔ n‑yirigi lala maa daa dɔli la tinsi tinsi n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri niriba.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Filipi mun daa kyaŋŋi Samɛrii timpuuni. Ŋɔn daa ti paai la, u daa gbaai mooligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Masiiya maa yɛla.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Di saha ka daadam pam daa la'am na, ka daa wumni Filipi yɛttɔ'a la ni nimbiri moaa, ka nyari tunkariseesi ŋɔn daa tumna. Ba daa yoaai ba tuba n‑wumsi ŋɔn yɛli seem zaa.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Dama arizimbeeri daa kaasi ka yi ninvugseeba ban mari ba ni, ka wɔbsi ni gbarisi gba daa nya tippu;.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: ka di daa mari sufupeelim n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ Samɛrii timpuuni maa.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ tiŋŋa wa ni ka u yuuri boonni Saimoon. U daa niŋni la tiim tuma, ka di daa mari alahazibu n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban bɛ tiŋŋa maa ni la. U mun daa laa n‑duusi u maŋŋa, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugtitaari.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Nira kam, bibila halli ni ninvugkurigu gba, daa maai ba mansi n‑wum Saimoon yɛttɔ'a maa, ka yɛtti, ni, Naawunni kpeeŋŋu daansɔ ban boonni, ni, Buntitaari la n kur nyɛ u.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Din daa kyɛ ka niriba maa daa boori u yɛla maa daa nyɛ la: u daa pun tum u tiima tuma ba saani ka di daa yuui pam.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Amaa niriba ŋɔn daa ti sakki n‑ti Filipi yɛttɔ'a maa, ni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam soori la, ni Yisa Masiiya yuuri yɛla, ba zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Saimoon mammaŋŋa gba daa sakki lala, ka ba daa su u gba koom. U daa zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa n‑tabli Filipi ni nimbirimoaa, ka daa nya Filipi ŋɔn daa niŋni tunkara ni alahazibu tunseesi, ka di daa gyɛri u pam.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Tɔ; di nyaaŋŋa Gyɛrusalɛm ni tuumudima maa ban daa ti wum, ni, Samɛrii dima sakki n‑ti Naawunni yɛlisuma la, ba daa tum Piita ni Gyɔni ba saani na.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Piita ni Gyɔni ŋɔn daa paai na la, ba daa susi Naawunni n‑tisi seeba ban pun sakki n‑ti Yisa, ni, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi nin sigi ba saani na.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Dama ba daa na kur deaai la koom suubu ti Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu, ka Naawunni Sii Kasi la na bu bɛ ba saani.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Di saha ka Piita ni Gyɔni daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa niriba maa zugri ka Naawunni Sisuŋŋu daa kyɛ ba saani na.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Ka Saimoon daa nya Naawunni tuumudima maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa niriba zugri ka Sii maa kyɛ ba saani na seem maa, ka u daa zaŋŋi ligri ni u ti ti Piita ni Gyɔni.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: ka yɛli ba, ni, Ya tim man gba yaa kanŋɔ taabu, ka ninvugsɔ zaa man ti zaŋŋi n nuu m‑pa u ni, Naawunni Vuusim Suŋŋu maa nin kyɛ di daama saani na.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, I nin kpi n‑sa'am m‑basi, nyini ni i ligri maa zaa! - nyin teesi, ni, i nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi ligri n‑da Naawunni piini maa zugu.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: I ka yɛttɔ'aseaa dinŋɔ puuni; i nuu ka ti faari diibu yɛla ni, dama i sufu bu zɛ yimm suŋŋa Naawunni saani.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Dizugu kyɛli i tumbeoo ni i teesibeeri wa zaasa, ka susi ti Dugma; pa seem u nin di sugru n‑ti i, i sufu ni teesa nyin teesi seem la zugu.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Dama n nya ka i zɛ la tumbeeri toom ziiya, ka ba pun zaŋŋi ala'akyi kutti m‑ba i.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Di saha ka Saimoon daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ya bɛlimma ti Dugma Naawunni n‑ti ma, ni, nyin yɛli seem la di paai ma na.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Ban daa yɛli Saimoon dinŋɔ maa, ba daa yi n‑tɔ'asi Yisa yɛla n‑tisi niriba maa ka wulisiri ba, ka daa labi n‑kuli Gyɛrusalɛm. Ban daa bɛ soori zugu n‑kunni maa, ba daa tɔ'asi Yisa lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tisi Samɛrii dima tinkpansi pam puuni.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Tɔ: ka Filipi muna - Naawunni malaaka daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u ka yɛli u, ni, Maalim siri n‑dɔli nuzaa lugri soori, sɔseaa kan yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kpa Gaza tiŋŋa ni la. - Ŋɔn daa yɛli sɔseaa la, yoo ni soori n nyɛ ka.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Ka Filipi daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi. Di saha maa ka Itiɔpiiya dɔsɔ daa dɔli soori maa zugu n‑kunna. Itiɔpiiya tingbaŋŋu Naaba kpeema, namɔ'aliga, n daa nyɛ u - pɔ'aba naam n‑daa la, ka ba boonni di, ni, Kandaasi. Ŋɔn n daa su Pɔ'a-naaba maa tuma n‑gɔsiri u neema ni u ligri yɛla zaa n‑ti u. U daa pun kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm nti puusi Naawunni ka daa naai.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: ka kpɛ u tarɔkku ni n‑zinni, ka wuri datti u n‑kyɛnna. Ŋɔn daa bɛ soori n‑kunni maa, u daa karimni la anabi Aizaaya bukku la puuni.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Ka Naawunni Siiya daa yɛli Filipi, ni, Kyɛm nti paai tarɔkku wa.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Ka Filipi daa zɔ m‑paai bɛni ka wum, ka doo maa karimni la Aizaaya bukku la puuni; ka u boosi u, ni, Ammi, i baŋŋi nyin karimni seelli wa gbinni.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Sɔ yi bu wulisi ma, man mammaŋŋa ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni?, ka daa naayi yɛli Filipi, ni, u duum na n‑zinni u tarɔkku maa puuni.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Tɔ, ka Aizaaya gbaŋŋu maa puuni luginseaa ka u daa karimni maa n‑daa nŋɔa#:- I yi mari peoo n‑wa ni i ti ku u, U yi bɛ la baalimm, ka bu yigra; Bee pɛbila ŋɔn ku kaasi U kɔrigri ziiya - Lala ka di daama gba daa bu yɛli seelli.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Ba daa zeem u ka di u sariya ba yuubu: Sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑yɛli u nyaaŋŋa dima yɛla, Dama u laa n‑ka duniya wa ni.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Tɔ, ka Itiɔpii doo maa daa yi boosi Filipi, ni, Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna yɛlim ma: ŋɔni yɛla ka anabi la tɔ'asiri wa? U daa tɔ'asiri u mammaŋŋa, bee, sɔ mun sa.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Ka Filipi daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a maa ka piili luginseaa doo maa ŋɔn daa karimni ni la, n‑wulisi u lahabasuŋŋu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Ban daa bɛ soori zugu n‑kyɛnni la, ba daa ti paai la bɔ'aseaa ka koom daa bɛ ka ni. Ka Itiɔpii doo maa yɛli Filipi, ni, I bu nya koom wa? Seelli bɛ ni din nin ta'ari ma, ni, n ku toaai n‑deaai Naawunni koom suubu, bee.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Ka yɛli sɔ ŋɔn duusiri tarɔkku maa, ni, u zaanma, ka ba daa zaani. Ka ŋɔn ni Filipi daa sigi na n‑kyaŋŋi bɔkku maa puuni, ka Filipi daa naayi su u Naawunni koom maa.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Ka ban daa ti yi koom maa puuni na, ti Dugma Vuusim daa fa Filipi n‑kyaŋŋi, ka Itiɔpii doo maa daa bu laa n‑nya u yaasa, amaa u daa dɔli u soori n‑kyɛnni ni sufupeelim.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Ka Filipi mun daa ti bɛ Azootisi tiŋŋa, ka u daa dɔli la bɛni tinsi maa m‑moonni Yisa lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri ba, halli nti paai Sizariiya timpuuni.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Ka di saha maa ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi Sitiivin, ni, Di zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, bee.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Ka Sitiivin daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, M badima, ni m mabiisi, ya wumma! Ti yaaba Ibrahim ŋɔn daa bɛ Mɛsɔpɔteemiya tingbaŋŋu ni la, ka na bu yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Kyaraani tiŋŋa - di saha ka Naawunni, Gyirima Daana, daa kasigi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi u lala saha maa.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: nti yɛli u, ni, Kyɛl i tingbaŋŋu wa, ni i dɔ'am zaa, ka bakki n‑kyɛ tingbanseaa man ya wulisi i maa na.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Di saha u daa kyɛ u tiŋŋa maa, Kalidii dima tiŋŋa la, ka daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kyaraani tiŋŋa. U ba ŋɔn daa ti kpi, Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u yi bɛni ka gaari m‑paai la tingbanseaa yan na bɛ ni la.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Ka daa bu ti u tingbanseaa zaa ni u bɛ ni n‑su ka, halli beela; amaa ka daa yɛli u, ni, u nin zaŋŋi tingbaŋŋu maa n‑ti u ni u nyaaŋŋa zaa, ka ba nin ti di ka faari n‑su, halli ni di saha maa ti yaaba Ibrahim daa ka biiya.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Naawunni daa yɛli kanŋɔ yɛttɔ'a, ni, u ya'ansi nin ti bɛ tinseelli ni, ka tiŋŋa maa niriba nin zaŋŋi ba n‑kyɛ ka ba leebi ba daaba n‑niŋi ba wahala yuuma kɔbsinaasi.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli Ibrahim, ni, Amaa man nin ti kyɛ ninvugseeba ban su ba maa saani na ka darigi ba tuba pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka i niriba maa yi na m‑bɛ kpa n‑gyeemni ma.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Ka Naawunni daa zaali dɔppa gunibu kaari, ni di ti wulisi ŋɔn ni Ibrahim ŋɔn loori la taaba nangbanyinni seem. Tɔ - ka dizugu ban daa dɔ'ai Ibrahim bii Yisahaaku sahaseaa la, ba daa guni u daba anii daari maa; Yisahaaku bii mun daa nyɛ Yaakubu, ka Yaakubu mun daa dɔ'ai u bidibsi pii ni ayi la, tin boonni seeba Izraɛli za'anɔya kpamma maa.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Ka kpamma maa daa ti zabi nyuuri n‑niŋi ba tinzɔ Yisifu, n‑daa ti zaŋŋi u n‑koosi n‑ti Igyipti dima. Amaa Naawunni daa zaani u nyaaŋŋa Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: n‑yiisi u wahala zaa puuni; ka laa n‑ti u bamsim, ka kyɛ ka u nya gyirima Igyipti naaba NaaFirawuna saani. Ka Naaba daa kyɛ ka u su ŋɔn NaaFirawuna yiri zaŋŋi n‑tabli Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Di saha ka kɔm daa ti lu Igyipti ni Kyɛna'an tingbana zaa ni, ka daa ti niŋi ba wahala pam; ka ti yaadima maa daa boori bundirigu ka kɔŋŋi.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Tɔ, ka di saha maa ka ti yaaba Yaakubu daa ti wum, ni, kyi bɛ Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni; ka u daa daŋŋi n‑tum ti yaadima maa, ni ba kyaŋŋi Igyipti nti da a na.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa ti laa n‑doaai n‑kyaŋŋi buyi kyɛnni. Di saha maa ka Yisifu daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi u bɛya dima ka ba daa baŋŋi u, ka NaaFirawuna gba daa baŋŋi Yisifu bɛya maa ŋɔn nyɛ seeba.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu Yisifu daa tum, ni ba booli u ba Yaakubu ni u yiri dima zaa na - ba daa paai ninvugpisiyɔpɔi ni anu.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Ka Yaakubu daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi nti zinni Igyipti, halli ka ŋɔn ni ti yaakpamma zaa daa ti ka ni.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ka ba daa zi ba n‑labi Sɛkyeem tiŋŋa na, nti sɔ'ai ba ba gbala ziseaa ni, Sɛkyeem ziseaa ka ti yaaba Ibrahim ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi anzulifa n‑da Heemɔri biisi saani la.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Tɔ. Ka Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun yɛli Ibrahim, ni, u nin suŋŋi u seem la, ka di saha daa naayi pɔtta; di saha maa ka ba zuliya maa daa nɔbi n‑zoaai pam Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: halli ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa zi Yisifu yɛla daa ti di Igyipti naam.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: ka daa zaŋŋi u teesibeeri n‑namsiri ti yaadima ka mugsiri ba. U daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi ba bileesi m‑basira, ni ba ku laa n‑nya biisi ban vu yaasa.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Di saha ka ba daa dɔ'ai Musa, ka u daa veeli pam ka Naawunni nini tigi u yɛla; ka u ba ni u ma daa mari u duu ni halli nti paai gɔya ata u ba yiri.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka sahanseaa daa ti paai ni ba zaŋŋi bii maa m‑basi, ka NaaFirawuna bipuŋŋa daa piisi u nti wubsi n‑zaŋŋi u, ni u leebi u mammaŋŋa biiya.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Ka Musa daa bamsi Igyipti dima bamsim zaa nti leebi yaa daana u yɛttɔ'a zugu ni u tuma zugu m‑paasi.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Tɔ: Musa ŋɔn daa ti paai u yuuma pisinaasi, ka di daa kyɛ u sufu ni na, ni, u kyɛm nti kaai u dɔ'ariba, Izraɛli dima la.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi nti nya ka Igyipti dɔsɔ ŋɔn mugsiri ŋɔn Musa dɔ'ariba maa yinni ka di daa bu dɔli soori, ka u daa ŋmɛ Igyipti doo maa n‑ku u.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: U daa teesiya, ni, u mabiisi nin baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni n tum u na ni u ti fa ba m‑basi, amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi lala.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Ka dizugu beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka Musa laa n‑yi nti nya Izraɛli dima maa niriba ayi ŋɔn zabra. Ka u kyaŋŋi ni u ti goaai ba ka yɛli, M mabiisi, ya baŋŋma, ni, ya nyɛ la mabiitiri yinni dima; ka wula ka ya ŋmaligi n‑zabra.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa niŋni u kpeaa putoo maa daa gyɛ la suuri n‑niŋi Musa ka boosi u, ni, Ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi nyini n‑leebi ti toondaana, bee, ti sarikatta.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: ni, I boori la i ku ma, hoo nyin sa ku Igyipti doo la seem maa, bee.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Tɔ; ka Musa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ka u daa zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Midiyan tingbaŋŋu ni. Bɛni ka u daa kpalim m‑bɛ ni, halli nti dɔ'ai u bidibsi ayi.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Di yuuma pisinaasi nyaaŋŋa ka malaaka daa pusi n‑yi Musa zugu na, yoo kan yuuri boonni Sainayi la ni, n‑ŋmanni hoo bugum n‑diri tutuuri puuni.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Ka Musa ŋɔn daa nya lala ka di gyɛri u, ka u vuui m‑paai bɛni ni u ti gɔsi suŋŋa. Di saha ka u daa wum ti Dugma kukɔri, ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri u, ni.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba. Ka dizugu dabeem daa gbaai Musa ka u ningbiŋŋu daa gyigra; ka u daa bu laa n‑sakki n‑gɔsiri bɛni.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ka ti Dugma daa laa n‑yɛli u, ni, Pirigma i namna maa, dama nyin ŋɔn zɛ seela maa nyɛ la din bɛ kasi n‑ti ma.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Ni, M pun nya n niriba ŋɔn diri wahala seelli Igyipti dima nuu ni, ka laa n‑wum ba fabla zaa; lala zugu ka n sigi na ni n ti fa ba m‑basi. Ka yɛli, ni, Ka dizugu kyɛm na ka n taasi i Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Dinŋɔna - ya bu nya? - ŋɔn Musa sɔ Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa za'asi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi i n‑leebi toondaana bee, ti sarikatta?, ŋɔna ka Naawunni daa tum na ni u ti leebi ba toondaana ni ŋɔn nin fa ba m‑basi. U daa niŋi lala, malaaka sɔ ŋɔn daa pusi n‑yi u zugu tutuuri maa puuni la suŋŋsim zugu.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Ka u siri daa fa ba n‑yiisi ba na, ka daa tumni alahazibu tuma ni tunkara pam Igyipti puuni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi TeekuZeoo ziiya, halli yoo ni gba, ban daa goori bɛni sahaseaa yuuma pisinaasi la.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Tɔ; ŋɔn Musa daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa yɛli Izraɛli dima, ni, Naawunni nin yiisi sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ya dɔ'ara, n‑kyɛ ka u niŋi anabi hoo mani ŋɔn bɛ seem la.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Ŋɔna n daa nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa zaani ti Izraɛli badima ni malaaka sunsukkuni. Dama ba daa la'am yoo ni, ka malaaka maa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ŋɔna, Sainayi zoori zugu, ni u ti yɛli n‑ti ba. Bɛni maa ka u daa deaai Naawunni yɛligu din tiri nyɔvuri maa yɛla n‑wulisiri n‑tiri ti na.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Amaa ti yaadima maa daa za'asi u noori ka waligi ba mansi ka kyɛ u, ka boori Igyipti lappu:.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: ka daa yɛli Aruuna, ni, u zaŋŋma salima m‑maali wuna n‑ti ba, wunseesi ŋɔn nin kyaŋŋi ba tooni n‑guri ba yɛla, ni, Ŋɔn Musa muna - sɔ ŋɔn daa yiisi ti Igyipti ni na la - ti bu laa m‑mi din niŋi u.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Di saha ka ba daa maali bunni hoo na'abila, ka kɔri bunkɔbri m‑maali ka, ka ba sufu pɛligi pam ban daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑maali bunseelli maa zugu.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Ka dizugu Naawunni daa yiisi u nuu ka kyɛ ba, ka ba daa susiri la saŋmariwuusi: lala zugu ka anabisɔ daa sɔbi n‑dooli Bukku maa puuni, ni#:- Ya Izraɛli dima wa! Yan daa bɛ yoo ni maa - Halli nti paai yuuma pisinaasi la - Lala saha maa zaa, yan daa kɔrigri bunkɔbri maa Man Naawunni ka ya daa kɔrigri n‑ti ma, bee?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Ay! Ya daa ziiri ya wunseesi duri - Wunseaa yan boonni Mɔlɔkyi la, Ni ya saŋmariwuseaa yan boonni Rɛfan la. Ya daa maali buna ni ya puusiri a ni: Dizugu man nin zaŋŋi ya nti basi, N‑taasi ya Babɛlɔn tiŋŋa tooni sa.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Tɔ, ka ti yaadima ŋɔn daa bɛ yoo ni la, ba daa mari la Naawunni tanti duu; kana ka ba daa maali, ka ka daa ŋmanni la hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi Musa n‑wulisi u seem maa.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Tɔ; ka ti yaadimseeba ban daa pa nyaaŋŋa la daa deaai duu maa m‑mari ka n‑zi na - ban ni Gyɔsuuwa daa niŋi lala.
