Di daa nyɛ la ban daa katti niriba burabura la saha, ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi m‑basi ka kyɛ ka u niriba kpɛ na. Ka ba daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala, halli nti paai NaaDauda saha.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Ŋɔn Dauda ka Naawunni nini daa tigi u yɛla, ka dizugu u daa susi soori Naawunni saani ni u ti bɔ gyeeŋŋu ziiya n‑ti u yaaba Yaakubu yiri dima.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Amaa u bii NaaSulɛmaana maa n daa ti mɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Amaa dinŋɔ zaa nyɛ la yɔri, dama Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa bu kpeeri nuusi ŋɔn mɛ duseaa puuni m‑bɛ ni. Ka dizugu anabisɔ n daa yɛli, ni#:-.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Dinŋɔ ka ti Dugma yɛtta: Arizana nyɛ la n naam ziziiya, Tingbaŋŋu maa mun nyɛ n gbaya ŋɔn tɔmni seela: Ka yiri bɔ ka ya lee n‑ya m‑mɛ n‑ti ma? Bee, luginseelli m bɛ duniya wa ni Ka n nin zinni n‑vuusira?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Pa mani n daa zaŋŋi n nuusi m‑maali anŋɔ zaa? .

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Tɔ; ka Sitiivin daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, yan tuppuwumni dima wa! Ya nyɛ la seeba ban za'asiri yɛlimaŋni, hoo tinzundima ban bu guni la ŋɔn bɛ seem la! Saha kam ka ya zabri Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi maa. Yan niŋni seem la, ya yaadima soori ka ya dɔli maa!.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Anabisɔ n daa bɛ ni ka ya yaadima daa bu namsi u, halli yinni gba, bee? Ba daa bu ku Naawunni tuumudima maa? - Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun tum tuumuseeba maa ni ba daŋŋi m‑moonni Yɛlimaŋni Daana ŋɔn ya n‑kyɛ na la yɛla maa. Ka pampaŋŋɔ yarima mun gbaai la Yɛlimaŋni Daana mammaŋŋa n‑yi u nyaaŋŋa ka ku u.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Ya daa deaai Naawunni zaaligu malaakadima saani, amaa ka lee m‑bu dɔli ka halli beela.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Tɔ: ka Sitiivin yɛligu wa ŋɔn daa lu ba tuba ni, ba sufu daa doaai pam, halli ka ba nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋni u.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Amaa Naawunni Sisuŋŋu daa paali Sitiivin ni, ka u wukki u zugu n‑gɔsi saazugu n‑nya Naawunni gyirima veelim, ka nya ka Yisa zɛ Naawunni nudirigu ziiya.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Di saha ka Sitiivin daa yɛli, Ya nyama! N nya ka arizana lakkri ni, ka Ninsaala Bii maa zɛ la Naawunni nudirigu ziiya.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Ka ba zaa daa gbaai kaasigu n‑nyɛri ba tuba ka zɔ n‑la'am n‑gyiligi u na.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: Ka daa gbaai u n‑yiisi ba tiŋŋa maa tɔriga ni nti lɔbi u kuga. Ka ban daa di sɛhara n‑galim u ka laa n‑lɔbi u maa daa yeaai ba bunyeera nti dooli na'akyimbilisɔ tooni, ka u yuuri boonni la Sɔla.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Ban daa lɔbri Sitiivin kuga sahanseaa maa, u daa susi la ti Dugma n‑tɔ'asiri, ni, N Dugma Yisa, deaam n nyɔvuri.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑kaasi n‑yɛli, N Dugma, di zaŋŋi ba tumbeoo wa taalli n‑ziili ba! Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai, u daa ti leebi Naawunni dinni.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa puusi Naawunni n‑naai, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi nti yakki Kyiddirɔn kuliga baa ni. Bɛni ka tiisi puu daa bɛ ni, ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa paai nti kpɛ ka ni.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn ya figsi u maa, Gyuudasi la, daa mi bɛni, dama ba zaa daa kyɛnni tiisi puu maa ni sahakam ni ba ti diri taaba azama.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Dizugu ŋɔn Gyuudasi daa mari soogyadima, ni ninvugseeba ban daa nyɛ gyeeŋŋu toondima ni Farisii dima niriba, ka ba daa kyaŋŋi bɛni. Ba daa mari ba tɔppu neema ni bugum mɔkpala ni firiladima n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai puu maa ni.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yisa mun daa pun baŋŋi bunseelikam din ya niŋi u, ka u daa yi na nti boosi ba, ni, Ŋɔni ka ya boora.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Yisa ziiya ka ti boora, Nazarɛti doo la. Ni, Mani nla. Halli Gyuudasi gba, minaafikka la, daa bɛ la ba saani n‑zɛa.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli, ni, Mani nla. maa, ba zaa daa labi ba nyaaŋŋa n‑lu la tiŋŋa.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Ka Yisa daa maali m‑boosi ba yaasa, ni, Ŋɔni ka ya boora?; ka ba yɛli u, ni, Yisa zii ka ba boora, ni, Nazarɛti doo la.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, M pun yɛli ya, ni, mani nla; dinŋɔna di yi nyɛ la mani ka ya boora, ya kur kyɛ ka niriba wa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi. .

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Lala daa kyɛ ka ŋɔn pun yɛli seem maa siri ti niŋi, ni, M Ba, ninvugseeba ka i zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la, m bu kyɛ ka ba sɔsɔ ti baasi yɔri.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ka Saimoon Piita daa mari takoobi, ka u daa foaai ka ka kyɛ dɔsɔ nudirigu tubri n‑fikki m‑basi. Doo maa daa nyɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la daabsɔa, ka u yuuri boonni Malikyiisi.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Ka Yisa daa yɛli Piita, ni, u zaŋŋi u suu maa n‑labsi n‑su ka teoo maa ni, ni, M Ba zaali soori wa n‑ti ma, hoo kɔbeaa ka u zaŋŋi n‑ti ma bunnyuura ni n nyu - ka n ya niŋi la wula n‑za'asi ka nyuubu.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Di saha ka soogyadima maa, ni ba zuguraana, ni Yɛhudiya dima maa Pirinsi la m‑paasi, daa gbaai Yisa n‑lɔ u.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: N‑daa di zaŋŋi u n‑daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Annasi saani - Kayafasi deema n‑daa la, ka ŋɔn mun Kayafasi daa di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana lala yuumni maa.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Ŋɔn Kayafasi muna, u daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pun ŋmɛ u saawara n‑ti Yɛhudiya dima maa, ni, ba yi ti kyɛ ka ninvugyinni ti kpi n‑ti niriba maa zaa, di nin sɔa.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Tɔ; di saha ka Saimoon Piita daa kari Yisa nɔba n‑dɔla - ŋɔn ni Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ m‑paasi. Ba daa paai Anaasi yiri maa na, ka lala yiri dima maa daa mi nyaandɔlisɔ la yɛla suŋŋa, ka di zugu ba daa kyɛ ka u kpɛ dundɔŋŋu maa ni.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Piita mun daa kpalim n‑zɛ samanni maa ni, amaa u taraana la daa labi na n‑yɛli pɔ'asariga la, za'anɔgura maa, ka mari u n‑kpɛa.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Ka pɔ'asariga la daa yɛli Piita, ni, Ammi, nyin gba pa la doo wa nyaandɔla? Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay! Pa mani.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Waari saha n‑daa la, ka dundɔŋŋu ni dima maa - daaba ni Pirinsi la - daa pun la'asi saala n‑nyoaai bugum, ka ba daa zɛ ni n‑wuusira. Ka Piita gba daa la'am ba saani n‑wuusiri bugum maa.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi Yisa u nyaandɔliba yɛla ni u wulisigu.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, N kasigi n‑yɛli paalu ni n‑ti duniya dima maa zaa: saha kam ka n wulisiri la la'aŋŋu duri ni, bee Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, luginseesi ka Yɛhudiya dima maa zaa n la'amni taaba. N daa bu sɔ'ai n‑yɛli seela.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Bɔ zugu ka i boosiri la mani? See ka i boosi baseeba ban daa wum maa m‑baŋŋi man daa yɛli ba seem. Ban m mi man ŋɔn daa yɛli seem n‑ti ba.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai, sapaasinsɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ n‑tabi bɛni daa ŋmɛ Yisa ka yɛli u, ni, Lala ka i labsi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa, bee.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Man yi tɔ'asi n‑kyirim, see ka i di n kyiriŋŋu wa sɛhara. Amaa n yi yɛli suŋŋa, wula ka i ŋmɛ ma.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Di saha ka Annasi daa kyɛ ka ba lɔ u n‑zi n‑taasi Kayafasi saani, Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa n daa nyɛ u gba.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Lala saha maa ka Saimoon Piita daa kpalim n‑zɛya n‑wuusiri u maŋŋa. Ka ba yɛli u, ni, Pa nyin gba n nyɛ u nyaandɔliba yinni? Ka u daa kyiisiya ka yɛli, ni, Ay! pa mani.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana daabsɔ gba daa yɛli u, ni, N da bu nya i tiisi puu maa ni, ŋɔn Yisa saani? - daabri wa daa nyɛ la dɔ'ara n‑ti Piita ŋɔn fikki dɔsɔ tubri la.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Piita daa kyiisiya yaasa, ka di saha ka nɔroo daa kum.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Bɛkyikyeoo saha ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑yi Kayafasi yiri ni n‑kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri ni. Ba mammansi daa bu kpɛ Baarikyi puuni, ni ba di di kyisigu taalli ka kɔŋŋi Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa diibu di zugu.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Dinzugu Gɔmna Pailiti daa yi ba saani na, m‑paai ba nti yɛli, ni, Taalli bɔ ka ya zaŋŋi n‑galim doo wa.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Doo wa yi da pa la tumbeeri daana, ti naa n‑ku zaŋŋi u n‑tisi.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Pailiti daa yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, ya zaŋŋma u n‑kari u sariya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya toori kaari zaaligu yɛla. Ka Yɛhudiya dima wa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay! - soori ka ni ka ti nin kari sɔ sariya n‑lusi u n‑ku u.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Di saha ka Pailiti daa laa n‑kpɛ Baarikyi puuni ka booli Yisa na nti yɛli u, ni, Nyini n nyɛ Izraɛli dima Naaba la.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, I yɛtti la lala i maŋtoori noori ni, bee, seeba n yɛli lala n‑ti i.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa yɛli u, Yɛhudiyadoo n nyɛ ma? - Ay! i maŋtoori butiri niriba maa zaŋŋi i n‑ti ma na - halli ya Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana gba. Tumbɛbɔ ka i daa tum.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, N naam pa la duniya wa puuni naam. Di yi pa lala, n nyaandɔliba da naa n‑zabi n‑ta'ai ma ka sɔ ku zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi Yɛhudiya dima maa nuu ni. Man mun naam suulinsi bu yi la duniya wa ni na.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Ka Pailiti yɛli, Asee i nyɛ la naaba, fa? Ka Yisa daa labsi, ni, Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔn yɛli, ni, naaba n nyɛ ma. Dinŋɔ zugu ka ba daa dɔ'ai ma, ni n di sɛhara n‑tiri yɛlimaŋni yɛla. Ŋɔnkam nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana wumni n kukɔri maa.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Bumbɔ n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni wa? Ka Pailiti ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai, u daa laa n‑yi Yɛhudiya dima maa saani nti yɛli ba, ni, M bu nya taalli doo wa saani.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Amaa ya kaari m bɛ ni, ni, n zaŋŋi dansarika daana sɔ n‑yiisi n‑ti ya na, ya Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu wa zugu. Tɔ, ya boori n yiisi Yɛhudiya dima Naaba wa n‑ti ya na, bee.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Amaa ba daa laa n‑kaasi pampam n‑yɛtti, ni, Pa doo wa, see Barabasi.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Di saha maa ka Sɔla daa boori la nimmoaa ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑ku Yisa nyaandɔliba. U daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la saani.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: nti susi gbanseesi ka ba sɔbi n‑ti la'aŋŋu duri an bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa niriba la, ni, u yi ti nya ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori bɛni, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, ni, u mari la soori ni u gbaasi ba n‑lɔ m‑mari ba n‑labi Gyɛrusalɛm na.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Tɔ: ka ŋɔn daa ti wa u Damasikusi maa ka daa ti pɔtti bɛni, ka neesim nyakki n‑yi saazugu na nti gyiligi u.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: U daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri u, ni, Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔ zugu ka i namsiri ma.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Ka Sɔla daa boosi n‑yɛli, Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?, ka u labsi n‑yɛli. Mani Yisa: mani ka i namsiri maa.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Ni, Doaam saazugu yooma n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi. Ba nin ti yɛli i nyin ya n‑niŋi seem.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli Sɔla maa daa wum kukɔri maa, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; ka ba daa kɔŋŋi yɛligu di zugu.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Sɔla mun daa doaai saazugu n‑lakki u nini, amaa u daa zoomiya ka ban daa dɔli u maa daa dari u n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Damasikusi.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Sɔla daa zoomiya daba ata ka koom bee diibu daa bu gaari u noori ni.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Tɔ; ka nyaandɔlisɔ n daa bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa, ka u yuuri boonni Ananayasi. U daa zaasi zaasimni, ni, ti Dugma tɔ'asi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ananayasi!, ka u daa sakki, Naa! Mani nŋɔa, n Dugma.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Ka ti Dugma daa yɛli u, ni, Kyɛm sɔseaa kan boonni Sɔtuusiri la ni nti kpɛ Gyuudasi yiri m‑boori Taasusi dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Sɔla la ziiya. Pampaŋŋɔ u na puusiri ni.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: U pun nya zaasimni, ni, dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Ananayasi kpɛ u saani na nti siisi u, ni u nini maa ti neaai yaasa.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Ananayasi ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, u daa yɛliya, Mma, n Dugma! Niriba pam pun ti ma doo wa lahabaari, ni, ŋɔn daa niŋi i niriba putoo Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: halli ni u deaai soori gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa saani, ni, u mari la soori ni u kyɛ na n‑gbaasi ninvugseeba ban gyeemni i la.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Ka ti Dugma daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ni dinŋɔ zaa, kur kyɛma! Dama doo maa nyɛ la n tuntunsɔ ŋɔn ya mari n yuuri nti ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima, ni ba nadima, ni Yɛhudiyadima maa m‑paasi.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: N nin wulisi u ŋɔn nin di wahala seem n yuuri zugu.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Di saha ka Ananayasi daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi. U daa kpɛ yiri maa ni n‑zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa Sɔla zugu ka yɛli, M mabii Sɔla, ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti i soori maa zugu la, ŋɔna n tum ma i saani na, ni n kyɛ na nti neaai i nini la ka kyɛ ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi bɛ i saani ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Dinsaha maa ka bunseesi an daa ŋmanni hoo pɛ'ari n yi Sɔla nini ni n‑lu tiŋŋa, ka Sɔla daa laa n‑neaai. Di saha ka Ananayasi daa su Sɔla Naawunni koom.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: U daa di diibu ka u yaa daa labi u na. Tɔ: Sɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi timpuuni saani n‑yuui beela.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: U daa piili n‑yiisiri wa'azu tiŋŋa maa la'aŋŋu duri ni, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Naawunni biiya.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Di daa gyɛri ban wumni maa zaa, ka ba daa tɔ'asiri taaba, ni, Yɛlimaŋni, pa dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba gbaasiri lala yuuri nyaandɔliba maa la nŋɔa? Ka u daa niŋi lala Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ kpa na ni u ti gbaasi ba n‑lɔ n‑taasi bɛni n‑niŋi gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa nuu ni. Pa ŋɔna nŋɔa.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Sɔla mun daa kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri, ni, yɛlimaŋni Yisa siri nyɛ la Masisɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tum na la. Dizugu Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi daa kɔŋŋi yɛlli, ni ba lusi u u yɛttɔ'a maa ni.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Saha daa ti paai ka Yɛhudiyadima maa daa la'am taaba ni ba ti baŋŋi ban nin niŋi seem n‑ku Sɔla.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: Amaa lala lahabaari daa paai Sɔla na. Ba daa yɛli, ni, ba nin guri tiŋŋa maa za'anɔya wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu saha zaa, ni ba gbaai u n‑ku u.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Amaa dindahinseaa, yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti sɔbgi sahanseaa la, u nyaandɔliba daa du tiŋŋa maa goomni zugu ka zaŋŋi la kusɔkku n‑lɔ ka miisi n‑kyɛ ka u sigi ka puuni, ka dinŋɔna ba daa sigsi u tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Sɔla daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa boori ni u paasi la Yisa nyaandɔliba la saani; amaa dabeem daa mari ba. Ba daa bu basi yɛm, ni, u dɔli la Yisa ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Di saha ka Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi Sɔla n‑kyaŋŋi nti puusi Yisa tuumudima maa. U daa wulisi ba din daa niŋi Sɔla soori maa ni seem maa, ni, ti Dugma daa kyɛ ka Sɔla nya u ka daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u. Halli ni Sɔla ŋɔn daa bɛ Damasikusi timpuuni seem n‑tɔ'asiri paalu ni, ni sufukpeemni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla ka u daa wulisi ba gba.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sɔla daa paasi ba zugu ka ba gyinni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka u daa wulisi niriba zaa ni sufukpeemni, Yisa yuuri zugu.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Amaa ŋɔn ni Griika dima ŋɔn daa ti di azama halli nti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni la, ba daa boori ni ba gbaai u n‑ku u.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Ka u Yisa nyaandɔlitaaba ban daa ti baŋŋi lala maa, ba daa mari u n‑kyaŋŋi Sizarii tiŋŋa n‑zaali u soori, ni u kuli Taasusi tiŋŋa.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Tɔ; di saha ka Yisa niriba daa laa n‑ka daaŋŋu Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu zaa ni, halli Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu ni Galilii gba ba daa bu nya yɛttɔ'aseaa. Ba daa nɔbri ni, n‑tumni ti Dugma ŋɔn boori seem, ka bɛ ni Naawunni Sii Kasi sufukpeemni zugu.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Piita daa gyinni n‑kaari la tinsi halli nti paai Naawunni niriba ban bɛ Lidda tiŋŋa ni.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: U daa ti nya dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Iniyasi; gbariga n daa nyɛ u ka kur kpalim n‑dɔya, ka di paai yuuma anii.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Piita daa yɛli u, Iniyasi, Yisa Masiiya n tibi i, doaama n‑kpabi i bundoaa wa! Tɔ, ka Iniyasi di va n‑doaai.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Ka niriba ban daa bɛ Lidda tiŋŋa ni, ni Seaarɔn tingbaŋŋu dima maa zaa daa nya u n‑niŋi yɛdda n‑ŋmaligi n‑dɔli ti Dugma.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Tɔ; ka pɔ'asɔ n daa bɛ Gyɔppa tiŋŋa, Yisa nyaandɔliba sunsukkuni, ka u yuuri boonni Tabita, bee Dookasi . U pupeelim ni u tunsuma daa ka tɔriga.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Amaa saha daa ti kyɛ na ka u beeri halli nti kpi. Ba daa peaai u kɔbeoo, ka mari u n‑du n‑kpɛ gyirambisa duseaa n‑dooli.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Tɔ, ka ba tiŋŋa Gyɔppa ni Lidda tiŋŋa daa bu wɔ'a, ka dizugu nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa pun wum, ni, Piita bɛ Lidda, ba daa tum niriba ayi bɛni ni ba ti booli u nimbirimoaa na. Ni, ba susim u, ni, Kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ yooma na.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Piita ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa doaai n‑dɔli ba yooma. Ŋɔn daa ti paai na, ba daa mari u n‑du saazugu n‑kpɛ duu maa ni, ka pukɔya maa zaa daa gyiligi u na, n‑kumna; ka ba daa wulisiri u neenseesi Dookasi ŋɔn daa sɛ n‑ti ba, u nyɔvuri saha la.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Piita daa kyɛ ka ba zaa yi duu maa ni. U daa gbaani tiŋŋa ka susi Naawunni pɔi, ka naayi ŋmaligi n‑kpa kum maa ka yɛli, Tabita, doaam n‑zaani! Ka u daa neaai u nini n‑gɔsi Piita ka doaai n‑zinni.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Piita daa suŋŋi u ka u doaai n‑zaani saazugu, ka daa booli Yisa nyaandɔliba ni pukɔya maa ni ba kpeem na, nti wulisi ba, ni, pɔ'a maa nya nyɔvuri.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Gyɔppa dima maa zaa daa wum lahabaari maa, ka ba pam daa niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Piita mun daa kpalim Gyɔppa ka di yuui beela. U daa gbeeri la Saimoon, gbanzaba sɔ, yiri ni.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Tɔ. Yisa tuumudima ni ba nyaandɔlitaaba, seeba ban daa bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu maa ni la zaa, daa ti wum, ni, ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima daa deaai Naawunni yɛligu maa.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm sahanseaa la, baseeba ban daa dɔli Yɛhudiyadima kaari ni nimmoaa la daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni n‑galimni u.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: ka yɛtti, ni, I kpɛ la yɔkɔla dima yiya ka nyin ni ban n di.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Dizugu Piita daa piili yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba din daa niŋi seem zaa deede.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: ni, Man daa bɛ Gyɔppa timpuuni, ka daa puusiri Naawunni; ka di daa niŋi ma hoo goom ka n gbisiri n‑zaasira, ka n daa nya zii, ni, n daa nya ka bunseelli daa sigri na, ka ka ŋmanni hoo tanti gbantitaari ban lɔ ka tuba anaasi n‑sigsiri ka halli nti paai man saani na.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Ka n daa gɔsi suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi din bɛ ka ni, n‑nya ka bunkɔbri daa bɛ ka puuni - bunkyɛnna ni bunvuura, halli ni niinsi gba.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ka n daa wum kukɔri, ni, Piita, doaama n‑kɔrigi n‑ŋɔbi.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ay, n Dugma! N na zi n‑di bunseelikam kan kyisira, bee, m‑mari da'ari.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ka kukɔri maa maali n‑yɛli ma, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn tisi bunseelli, ni, bunsuŋŋu, nyin di kyisiri ka, ni, da'ari.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Tɔ, ka di daa niŋi lala napɔmbuta, ka bunni maa naayi labi n‑du saazugu.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Di saha maa ka niriba ata daa paai na, n‑zaani yiri seaa tin daa bɛ ni maa tooni, ni, ba tum ba la Sizariiya, ni ba kyɛ man saani na.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Naawunni Sii Kasi daa yɛli ma, ni, n di guusi ka dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi. M mabiisi bayoobu wa gba daa dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi, halli nti paai n‑kpɛ doo maa yiri.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: U daa yɛli ti ŋɔn daa bɛ u yiri maa puuni ka daa nya malaaka seem, ka u yi na nti yɛli u, ni, Tumma Gyɔppa m‑booli Saimoon na - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, Piita la.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: U nin tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑ti ya, an nin kyɛ ka nyin ni i yiri zaa nya tuliginsim.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Tɔ; man daa piili n‑tɔ'asi sahanseaa maa, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi daa kyɛ ba zugu na hoo ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ti zugu na piiligu ni seem la.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Di saha ka n daa teaai ti Dugma ŋɔn daa yɛli seem, ni, Gyɔni daa zaŋŋi koom n‑su ya, amaa yarim mun nin deaai Vuusim Kasi suubu.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Dizugu Naawunni daa pun ti ba lala piini hoo piiniseaa ŋɔn daa ti ti, sahanseaa tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda la. Ka mani n nyɛ ŋɔni ka nin ta'ai Naawunni ŋɔn tum seem.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Ban daa ti wum dinŋɔ maa, ba daa bu laa n‑ga'ai, ka daa gbaai Naawunni pɛ'abu n‑yɛtti, ni, Ban pa Yɛhudiyadima gba ka Naawunni ti soori ni ba takki ba teesibeeri n‑niŋi tuuba ni ba ti nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Tɔ. Sitiivin kuubu nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa nya namsigu pam Yɛhudiyadima tinsi la ni, ka ba pam daa wurim n‑kyaŋŋi tinseesi, halli ka baseeba daa paai Fɔnisii tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa. Ba daa bu tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu n‑ti sɔ na'ala Yɛhudiyadima kɔkɔa.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Amaa baseeba daa bɛ ni, ban daa yi la Saapirusi ni Sairiini tingbana la ni na, ka ba daa kyaŋŋi Antiyɔkku ka daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri Griikadima maa gba, n‑yɛtti ti Dugma Yisa lahabasuŋŋu yɛla maa.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Ka Naawunni nuu daa bɛ ba saani, ka niriba pam daa niŋi yɛdda ka takki ba beebu n‑deaai ti Dugma maa.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Dinŋɔ lahabaari maa daa ti paai Yisa niriba la'aŋŋu dima Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka ba daa tum Baanabasi ni u kyaŋŋi Antiyɔkku.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Ŋɔn daa paai bɛni nti nya Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun tisi piinisuma n‑tiri bɛni niriba maa, u sufu daa maaya: ka u daa kpamsi ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑zɛ ti Dugma maa nyaaŋŋa ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Dama dɔsuŋŋu n daa nyɛ u, ka Sii Kasi daa bɛ u ni, ka u yɛdda gba zoaai. Di saha ka niriba pam daa la'am m‑paasi ban daa dɔli Naawunni soori maa zugu.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Tɔ, ka dinzugu Baanabasi daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Taasusi tiŋŋa ni u ti bɔ Sɔla ziiya.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti nya u, u daa zi u n‑labi n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku na. Ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla daa la'am ni Naawunni niriba bɛni n‑niŋi yuummunni zaa, n‑wulisiri niriba pam. Antiyɔkku ka ba daa piili m‑boonni Yisa nyaandɔliba, ni, Kirisita niriba la.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Di saha maa ka anabiseeba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku na.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Ba yinni ŋɔn yuuri boonni Agabusi la daa doaai, ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa daa kyɛ ka u wulisi ba, ni, kɔm nin ti lu tinsi zaa ni.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Ka dizugu Yisa niriba maa daa ŋmɛ saawara n‑gbaai gbaari, ni, ba ya n‑suŋŋi la ba mabiseeba ban bɛ Gyuudiiya la, sɔkam ni u yaa tɔriga.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Ba daa niŋi lala, ka kyɛ ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla deaai ba piini maa n‑zi n‑kyaŋŋi kpamma maa saani.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Ka Pɔla daa gɔ n‑gɔ, halli nti paai Dɛribee ni Lisitira tinsi. Bɛni ka Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Timɔti. U ma gba daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, ka mun daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyapɔ'a, amaa u ba daa nyɛ la Griika doowa.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Di saha maa ka yɛddaniŋniba ban bɛ Lisitira ni Aikoonyum la daa yɛtti, ni, Timɔti nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔla daa boori ni Timɔti dɔli u ka ba kyaŋŋi. Ka lala zugu u daa guni u. Yɛhudiyadima ban daa bɛ bɛni zugu ka u daa niŋi lala, dama ba zaa baŋŋi, ni, Timɔti ba daa nyɛ la Griika nira.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa dɔli tinsi maa, ka daa yɛli Yisa niriba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑sakki zaaliseesi tuumudima ni kpamma ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm maa ŋɔn daa zaali n‑ti ba la, ka dɔli a suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Dizugu ban daa la'amni n‑dɔli Yisa maa daa mari yaa ni yɛdda, ka ba daa zoaari m‑paasiri sahakam.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Tɔ: ka ba daa gɔ n‑dɔli Firigyii ni Galɛseaa tingbana dama Naawunni Sii Suŋŋu daa bu ti ba soori ni ba tɔ'asiri lahabaari maa Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Lala zugu ba daa dɔli nti paai Misii tingbaŋŋu maa tɔriga. Ba daa yɛnni, ni, ba nin kpɛ Bitinii tingbaŋŋu maa ni, amaa Yisa Siiya daa bu sakki lala n‑ti ba.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Lala zugu ba daa lɔkki n‑gaari Misiiya nti paai Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Di daari yuŋŋu maa Pɔla daa zaasi zaasimni, n‑nya Masidoonya nira ŋɔn zɛya m‑bɛlimni u, ni, Kyɛm Masidoonya na nti suŋŋi ti.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa nya zaasimni kanŋɔa zugu, ti daa sɛ'asi ya'abi ni ti yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu, dama ti daa teesi, ni, Naawunni n daa booli ti ni ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tisi bɛni niriba maa.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Ka dizugu ti daa kpɛ la nyariŋŋu n‑yi Tirɔ'asi n‑du n‑lɔŋŋi m‑paai Samɔtreesi, ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti kaai ti daa gaari m‑paai Niyapɔlisi tiŋŋa.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: N‑daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Filipaai - kana n nyɛ Masidoonya ya'ari wa tinsi toondaana, ka nyɛ la Room dima suulinsi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka ti daa yuui beela.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa, ti daa yi tiŋŋa maa puuni n‑kyaŋŋi kuliga ya'ari; bɛni ka ti daa teesi, ni, Yɛhudiyadima maa daa la'amni n‑gyeemna. Ti daa zinni bɛni n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri pɔ'aseeba ban daa la'am bɛni maa.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u yuuri boonni Liidiya. U daa yi la Tayatayira tiŋŋa ni na, ka daa koosiri kyinkyinsuma an ligri daa zoaai. Pa Yɛhudiyapɔ'a n daa nyɛ u, amaa u daa dɔli Yɛhudiyadima soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni. Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u nini neaai, ka u daa wum Pɔla yɛttɔ'a maa ka sakki.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu, ka u daa susi ti, ni, ti kyɛm na nti bɛ u yiri. Ni, Ya yi siri baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la yɛdda daana n‑ti ti Dugma, yan kyɛm na. Ka ŋɔn daa bɛlim ti lala zugu daa kyɛ ka ti sakki lala n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Dindahinseaa ka ti daa ti wa puusigu ziiya, ka daa ti tuusi pɔ'asarisɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ daabri, ka daa mari arizinni ŋɔn daa nyɛ ba'akɔligu dinni ka daa kyɛ ka pɔ'a maa bugri m‑baŋŋni yɛla. Lala tuma zugu ka baseeba ban daa su pɔ'a maa daa nyari ligri pam u bugbu maa ni.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Ka pɔ'a wa daa dɔli Pɔla nyaaŋŋa n‑kaasiri n‑yɛtta, Niriba wa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ yiiko zaa daana la tuntumniba. Ba yɛtti ya la yan nin niŋi seem n‑nya tuliginsim.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: U daa kpalim n‑dɔla, ka daa kur tɔ'asiri lala dabsa pam. U daa ka'ali Pɔla pam, halli ka u daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli arizinni maa, ni, Yisa Masiiya yuuri zugu ka n yɛtti i - yim na ka kyɛ u! Ka arizinni maa daa siri yi na ya'abi.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Tɔ, ka ban daa su pɔ'asariga maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑nya u bugbu ligri la, ba daa gbaai Pɔla ni Sailasi n‑daai ba n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni nti niŋi tiŋŋa zuguraandima nuu ni.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Ba daa taasi ba sariya karibu ziiya la, ka yɛli, Niriba wa nyɛ la Yɛhudiyadima, ka mari nangbankpeemni n‑daamni ti tiŋŋa wa yɛla.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Ba wulisira sɔseesi an bu sumni ni ti wumna, bee, ti dɔla; dama Room niriba n nyɛ ti.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Di saha ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑galimni Pɔla ni Sailasi, ka tiŋŋa maa sariyakattiba daa zaŋŋi ba n‑toaai ba kpari n‑yeaai ka kyɛ ka ba feebi ba daari.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Ka dizugu ba daa bu ba pam, ka zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi sarika ni; ka yɛli sarikadaana maa, ni, u kparim ba suŋŋa.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Ka sarikadaana ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa deaai ba n‑zaŋŋi n‑niŋi sarika dutoonni la ni, ka ba ba nɔba ni datibsiri.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Tɔ; ka yuntasuusi ka Pɔla ni Sailasi daa puusiri Naawunni ka yuumni yuuma n‑tiri u, ka ba dansarikataaba daa wumsira.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Di saha ka tiŋŋa maa daa dam, ka dansarika yiri maa daa miimna, halli ni ka nyeeba maa gba. Ka ka duri maa za'anɔya maa daa yoosi, ka ban daa ga ba nɔba kyɔrimidima la zaa daa gaasi n‑lu.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Ka dansarikadaana maa ŋɔn daa neaai goom ni n‑nya ka duri maa za'anɔya la zaa daa yoosi, u daa teesi, ni, dansarika dima maa zaa zɔya, ka dizugu u daa foaai u takoobi, ni u ku u maŋŋa.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Amaa Pɔla daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, Di niŋi i maŋŋa seelli! Ti zaa na bɛ kpa.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Tɔ, ka dansarikadaana maa daa booli, ni, ba mari bugum na, ka daa kpɛ yooma na. U daa zɔri dabeem n‑gyigra, ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑ti Pɔla ni Sailasi.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Ka u daa mari ba n‑yi na; ka boosi ba, ni, M Badima, n ya n‑niŋi la wula n‑nya tuliginsim.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, Niŋma ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda, ka i nin nya tuliginsim - nyin ni i yiri dima zaa.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Di saha ka ba daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛla maa n‑tisi ŋɔn ni u yiri dima maa.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Ka dirindaari maa yuŋŋu ka dansarikadaana maa daa zaŋŋi ba m‑peaai ba nɔra la n‑ti ba, ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Ka daa mari Pɔla ni Sailasi n‑kpɛ u yiri maa na nti ti ba bundirigu ka ba di: ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa mari sufupeelim pam ba Naawunni yɛdda niŋbu zugu.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, tiŋŋa maa zuguraandima daa tum pirinsi na ka yɛli, ni, Ya yiisim niriba wa m‑basi.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Dizugu dansarikadaana maa daa yɛli lala n‑ti Pɔla, ni, Zugudaandima maa yɛliya, ni, ti yiisim nyin ni Sailasi m‑basi; dizugu ya kyɛma, ka Naawunni nin paasi ya sufumaasim.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Amaa Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Halli ba sa bu tɔ'asi ti yɛligu gba amaa ba sa bu ti zama maa tooni, tirima mun nyɛ la Room suulinsi niriba, ka sa zaŋŋi ti n‑kpeesi dansarika ni. Ka zuna ba yi boori la ba sɔ'ai m‑basi ti baalimm, fa! Ti ku sakki, walla! Faasee ka ba mammansi kyɛ na n‑yiisi ti m‑basi.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Tɔ; ka pirinsi maa daa labi nti yɛli lala n‑ti zuguraandima maa. Ka ban daa wum, ni, Pɔla ni Sailasi nyɛ la Room suulinsi niriba la, dabeem daa gbaai ba.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Lala zugu ba daa kyɛ na ni ba ti zɔ ba mammantoori sindaani Pɔla dima saani na, ka daa mari ba n‑yi dansarika maa ni na, n‑susi ba, ni, ba yim ba tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Ka ba daa yi dansarika maa na n‑kyaŋŋi Liidiya yiri n‑nya yɛddaniŋniba maa, ka tɔ'asi kpamsigu yɛttɔ'a n‑ti ba pɔi, ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi ŋɔn daa paai Aikoonyum tiŋŋa, di daa kur niŋi lala yaasa. Ba daa kpɛ Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑tɔ'asi lala, halli ka Yɛhudiyadima ni tiŋŋa maa niriba pam daa niŋi yɛdda.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa za'asi yɛdda niŋbu la daa tugli ban pa Yɛhudiya niriba la n‑kyɛ ka ba suuri yisigi, ka ba daa zabi ni yɛddaniŋniba maa.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa yuui bɛni ka daa bɛ ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri ti Dugma yɛla; ka ti Dugma Naawunni daa zɛ ba nyaaŋŋa, ka daa ti ba yiiko n‑kyɛ ka ba tumni alahazibu tuma, din wulisi, ni, ba yɛttɔ'a maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni, ŋɔn Naawunni nyɛ la Wurilim Daana.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Dizugu tiŋŋa maa niriba daa puri la buyi: baseeba daa zɛ la Yɛhudiyadima maa nyaaŋŋa, ka seeba mun daa zɛ Yisa tuumudima maa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa ni buriseesi niriba daa la'am ni tiŋŋa maa toondaandima, ni ba teesi ba nin niŋi tuumudima maa putoo seem ka lɔbi ba kuga.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa ti baŋŋi niriba maa teesa ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka daa zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Lisitira ni Dɛribee - tinseesi an bɛ Likawɔnii tingbaŋŋu ni. Ba daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala tinsi, ni a tinkpansi zaa ni.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: m‑moonni Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri ba.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Tɔ; Lisitira timpuuni ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ ni n‑nyɛ gbariga: ba daa dɔ'ai u la lala, ka u daa na zi n‑kyaŋŋi halli ni di dabsiri maa.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: U daa zi ni n‑wumsiri Pɔla yɛttɔ'a maa; ka Pɔla daa nya u, ka gɔsi u suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi, ni, yɛdda daa bɛ gbariga maa ni, ka u nin toaai n‑nya alaafɛa.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Ka dizugu u daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli u, Doaam saazugu n‑zaani i gbaya zugu, kyappi! Ka doo maa daa yisigi n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Tɔ, ka zama maa ŋɔn daa nya Pɔla ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa, ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri pampam ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a ni, ni, Ti tingbaŋŋu wa buga maa labgi la ninsaala n‑sigi ti saani na.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Ka dizugu ba daa booli Baanabasi la, ni, Zeoosi - ba buga zaa naaba yuuri n‑daa la; Pɔla mun yuuri ka ba daa boonni, ni, Hɛrimiisi - ba bugseaa ŋɔn daa nyɛ u bugtaaba wuddaana maa yuuri n‑daa la - Pɔla ŋɔn daa nyɛ yɛttɔ'a toondaana maa zugu.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Zeoosi gyeeŋŋu yiri daa zɛ la tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa; ka dizugu tindaansɔ ŋɔn daa su Zeoosi maaligu yɛla la daa zaŋŋi na'araari ni tiisi puum n‑kyɛ tiŋŋa maa za'anɔri zii na, ni ŋɔn ni niriba maa ya maali la sara hoo ba tiŋŋa kaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Amaa Pɔla ni Baanabasi ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi ban daa teesi, ni, ba ya n‑niŋi seem maa, ba daa toosi ba sutura sufusa'aŋŋu zugu, ka vaai n‑zɔ n‑kpɛ zama maa sunsukkuni, ka tasi.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: n‑yɛli, Zama! Bɔ zugu ka ya lee n‑niŋni lala? Tirima gba nyɛ la ninsaalidima hoo yan nyɛ seem wa! Halli dinŋɔ zugu n daa kyɛ ka ti kyɛ na m‑moonni Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tiri ya, ni ya ti kyɛ bunkyɔ'ama wa gyeeŋŋu ka labi na n‑tabli Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana saani. Ŋɔna n daa naam arizana ni duniya wa ni teeku zaa, m‑paasi bunseelli kam din bɛ a ni.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Saha din gaari sa halli ni zuna, u daa kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa niŋi la u sufu yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: amaa u daa bu kyɛ ka ba kɔŋŋi yɛliseesi an wulisiri ba u yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, dama u daa zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa tunsuma n‑ti ya. Seoo ni saha ka u tiri ya saa, ka tiri ya agazeeri saha m‑paasi ni ya ti nya ya puri antaam. U tiri ya bundirigu ka kyɛ ka ya mari sufumaasim.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Ni ba yɛttɔ'a wa zaa yɔri, di daa tɔ pam ni ba kyɛ ka daadam maa bu maali ba hoo ban maanni ba buga seem.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yɛhudiyadimseeba daa yi Antiyɔkku Pisidii tiŋŋa na, ka seeba daa yi Aikoonyum na. Ba daa tɔ'asi niriba maa n‑kyɛ ka ba deaai ba noori, ka zaŋŋi kuga n‑lɔbi Pɔla halli nti yɛli, ni, u kpi ni; ka vuui u n‑yiisi tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa ka kyɛ u.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Amaa ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa ŋɔn daa yi na n‑zɛ n‑gyiligi u, u daa doaai n‑labi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ŋɔna ni Baanabasi daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Dɛribee.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Tɔ; ka ba daa ti mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti tiŋŋa wa niriba, ka daa kyɛ ka niriba pam daa niŋi Yisa nyaandɔliba. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa labi n‑dɔli Lisitira, ni Aikoonyum, halli nti labi m‑paai Antiyɔkku.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Ba daa kaai Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban bɛ lala tinsi maa ni n‑kpamsiri ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi m‑bari n‑zaani ni sufukpeemni, dama din bɛ seem maa, faasee ka ti nya namsigu pam pɔi ka naayi paai Naawunni naam beebu.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Ya'aseaa zaa Yisa nyaandɔliba ŋɔn daa la'am m‑bɛ ni, Pɔla dima daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa seeba zugri ni ba diisi ba Yisa tuma kpamma. Ba daa lɔ la noori n‑susi Naawunni, ni, u gɔsi ban daa niŋi u yɛdda maa ka guri ba yɛla suŋŋa.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa dɔli Pisidii tingbaŋŋu nti paai Pamfiliiya.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Ka bɛni ba daa kpɛ Pɛriga timpuuni nti tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu pɔi, ka naayi kyaŋŋi Ataliiya, tinseaa kan zɛ teeku gbiriŋŋu la.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Bɛni ka ba daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi n‑labi n‑kuli Antiyɔkku, dama bɛni niriba daa daŋŋi n‑susi Naawunni wurilim tunsuma n‑ti ba, ni ba deaai tuntumsi maa, ka pampaŋŋɔ ba daa tum n‑naai.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Ka ban daa paai na, ba daa la'am ni Yisa nyaandɔliba la'aŋŋu dima zaa, ka daa wulisi ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum seem n‑ti ba maa, ni, Naawunni yoaai la soori, ni ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la gba ti dɔli ka niŋni Yisa yɛdda.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Ka ba daa bɛ bɛni nyaandɔliba maa saani n‑yuui beela.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Di saha maa ka NaaHɛroodi daa piili n‑gbaari ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni taaba n‑dɔli Yisa soori maa n‑niŋni ba putoo.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: U daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi takoobi n‑ku Gyɔni beeri Gyeemsi la.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti nya ka lala niŋi Yɛhudiyadima la nya'asim maa, u daa gbaai Piita gba - dinŋɔ daa niŋi la Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la saha -.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: n‑gbaai u n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni. Soogyadima piiya ni ayoobu n daa guri u: ba daa puri ba la banaanaasi, ni ba deaari taaba n‑guri u. Dama Naaba daa yɛnni, ni, Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu ŋɔn ti ya n‑gaari la, u nin yiisi u na n‑ti zama maa.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Dizugu Piita daa kpalim m‑bɛ sarika duu maa ni, amaa Yisa nyaandɔliba daa kur la'amni n‑susiri la Naawunni n‑tiri Piita ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Tɔ; ka saha maa ŋɔn daa ti paai, kaman beoo ka Hɛroodi ya yiisi Piita sarika maa ni na n‑ti niriba maa. Ka lala saha maa, yuŋŋu, Piita daa gbisiri ni; ka ba daa zaŋŋi kyɔrimi n‑ga u n‑tabli soogyadima ayiyi u ya'a ayi zaa, ka daa guri u lala. Ka soogyaseeba daa zɛ n‑guri sarika za'anɔroo maa m‑paasi.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Di saha ka ti Dugma malaaka gbirigi n‑yi bɛni na, ka neesim daa neaai sarika duu maa ni. U daa gbaai Piita bɔkku n‑neaai u, ka yɛli u, Doaam yooma! Ka kyɔrimidima maa daa yi Piita nuusi ni n‑lu tiŋŋa.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Malaaka maa daa yɛli u, ni, Loom i sɛlooriga yooma ka piri i namna!, ka u daa niŋi lala. Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli Zaŋŋma i kparigu n‑yɛ ka dɔli ma.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Tɔ, ka Piita daa dɔli u n‑yi duu maa ni, ka bu baŋŋi, ni, malaaka maa yɛla ŋɔn niŋi seem maa zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni; u daa teesi, ni, u zaasiri ni.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑yakki soogyadima zaaziisi buyi, nti paai kutti za'anɔseaa kan yiiri timpuuni maa. Ban daa ti paai sahanseaa la, kyɛburu maa daa yoaai ka toori, ka ba daa yi. Ba daa dɔli la sɔroo n‑kyɛnna, ka malaaka maa ti kyɛ Piita m‑basi ka kyaŋŋi.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa yi Ateensi n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Kɔrinti tiŋŋa.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Di saha ka Piita daa ti baŋŋi din daa niŋi, ka yɛli, M baŋŋiya! Di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Naawunni n siri tum u malaaka ni u kyɛ na n‑tiligi ma NaaHɛroodi nuu ni, ni Yɛhudiyadima ŋɔn yɛli, ni, ban nin niŋi ma seem la ni zaa.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: U daa nya Yɛhudiyadɔsɔ bɛni ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Akwila la, ka u nyɛ la Pɔntusi tingbaŋŋu nira, ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a Prisila. Ba daa pun yi la Italii tingbaŋŋu ni na, NaaKlɔdiyusi ŋɔn daa kari Yɛhudiyadima zaa ni ba yi ni Room tiŋŋa la zugu. Pɔla daa kyaŋŋi nti kaai ba.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Ka u teesi m‑baŋŋi, ka kyaŋŋi Mariama yiri, ŋɔn bii daa nyɛ Gyɔni, sɔ ban daa boonni Maaki la; bɛni ka niriba pam daa la'am n‑susiri Naawunni.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: ka naayi kpɛ ba yiri nti gbɛa; ka ŋɔn ni ban la'am n‑tumna, dama ba zaa daa seeri la gbana ni tantidima.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa kpaasi za'anɔri kyɛburu maa, ka boonna; ka pɔ'asarisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Rooda daa kyɛ na ni u ti baŋŋi din bɛ ni.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Vuusim Dabsiri zaa u daa kpeeri la Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu nti diri azama n‑tiri Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima zaa, m‑boori ni ba sakki n‑deaai yɛligu maa.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: U daa baŋŋi kukɔri maa, ni, Piita n nyɛ u, ka u sufu daa maai pam; halli ka u bu yoaai n‑ti u, amaa u daa zɔ n‑kpɛ nti yɛli ba, ni, Piita zɛ la samanni.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Tɔ; ka Sailasi ni Timɔti ŋɔn daa yi Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu na, Pɔla nini daa moaai la Naawunni yɛligu yɛla ni kɔkɔa, n‑wulisiri Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, Yisa n siri nyɛ Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ na ni u ti di Masiiya la.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli u, Gyɛrigu wa!, amaa u daa kur tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u U malaaka n nyɛ u.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn daa kyiisi u, ka tuuri u maa, u daa pisigi u sutura n‑wulisi ba, ni, u za'asiri ba yɛla, ka yɛli Tɔ, ka dizugu ya yi ti kpi, ya yɛla kpalim m‑bɛ ya mammaŋŋa zugu ni. Man yiisi n nuu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din sumsi ni n tum seem n‑ti yarima maa ni. Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna n ya n‑kyaŋŋi la ban pa Yɛhudiyadima saani.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Di saha maa ka Piita daa kpalim n‑kpaasiri ka boonna, ka ba daa naayi yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑nya u, ka di daa niŋi ba alahazibu.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Ka u daa gaari n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri seaa kan daa pɔri la'aŋŋu duu maa ziiya; yiri maa daana yuuri n daa boonni Titiyusi Gyasitisi, u mun daa nyɛ la ŋɔn dɔli Naawunni soori hoo Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn dɔli seem la.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuu m‑bulisi ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba vuuri, ka tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ba ti Dugma ŋɔn niŋi seem n‑yiisi u sarika ni na. Ka laa n‑yɛli, ni, ba yɛlim dinŋɔ n‑ti Gyeemsi ni ba mabiisi zaa; ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi seela.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Di saha maa Krispusi - la'aŋŋu duu maa kpeema n‑daa la - daa ti niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda, ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa. Ka Kɔrinti dima maa pam ban daa wum yɛligu maa daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda ka ba daa su ba Naawunni koom.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, di daa gyɛri soogyaseeba ban daa guri Piita maa pam, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, Piita bɛ la yani?.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Di saha maa ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka Pɔla zaasi zaasimni u yuŋŋu goom puuni, ka daa yɛli u, Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i. Kpalimma n‑tɔ'asira, di kyɛ yɛttɔ'a maa!.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Ka NaaHɛroodi daa yɛli, ni, ba yiisim u na, ka ba daa bɔ u zii n‑kɔŋŋi. Ka u daa boosi soogyadima maa boosa, ka tisi noori, ni, ba kuum ba. Di nyaaŋŋa NaaHɛroodi daa yi Gyuudiiya n‑kyaŋŋi Sizariiya nti niŋi dabsa ayi bɛni.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: Dama m bɛ i saani ka sɔ ku toaai n‑zabi n‑sa'am i yɛla. Nyama! - m mari n niriba pam tiŋŋa wa puuni.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Tɔ; ka NaaHɛroodi sufu daa yisigi n‑niŋi Taaya ni Saidoon niriba ka ba daa la'am taaba n‑kyɛ na ni ba ti nya u. Ba daa kur tɔ'asiri n‑tiri Bilasitusi - ŋɔn daa gɔsiri Naaba duu yɛla n‑tiri u la - halli ka u ti sakki ni u nin zɔ ba sindaani Naaba maa saani. Ba tinsi maa ŋɔn daa deaari ba bundira Naa maa tingbana ni la zugu n‑daa la.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ ni, n‑niŋi yuumni ni purigri, n‑wulisiri ba Naawunni yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Ba daa zaali yɛttɔ'a maa dabsiri: ka dabsiri maa ŋɔn daa ti paai, NaaHɛroodi daa bɔbi u naam kpariveela ka zinni bimbinni maa zugu, ka tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'anya'asa.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Tɔ: ka di daa niŋi lala ka Galeoo daa di Akaaya tingbaŋŋu Gɔmna. Di saha maa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa la'am n‑zabi Pɔla ka daa zi u n‑kyaŋŋi kootu ni.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Ka zama maa daa tasi n‑yɛli, Haaya! Di pa la ninsaala n tɔ'asiri wa, amaa Naawunni n tɔ'asiri maa.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Ka yɛtti, ni, Doo wa bɛlimni niriba n‑nyaŋŋna, ni, ba dɔli sɔseaa kan pa ti zaaligu soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Di saha ka Naawunni malaaka daa ŋmɛ Hɛroodi ya'abi n‑dooli, ŋɔn daa bu tiri Naawunni gyirima zugu. U daa poaai n‑yiiri zunzuya, halli nti kpi.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa ya n‑tɔ'asi sahanseaa la, Galeoo mun daa yɛli Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, Ya yi da galimni u la zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi taalli, bee, tumbeeri niŋbu zugu, di naa m‑mari soori ni n wum n‑ti ya.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Naawunni lahabaari maa daa yɛligiya ni ka mari yaa.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Amaa di nyɛ la boosa zaŋŋi n‑kpa noori yɛttɔ'a ni, ni yuya, ni ya zaaligu wa yɛla - ay, ya mammansi maalim a! N ku sakki n‑leebi sarikatta anŋɔ yɛla zugu.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Di saha ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla daa tum ba tuma Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni n‑naai, ka daa labi Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa na. Ka Gyɔni sɔa, ban daa pa yupaa, ni, Maaki la, daa dɔli ba n‑kyɛ na.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Ka daa kyɛ ka ba kari ba n‑yiisi kootu duu maa ni.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Ka ba zaa daa tuligi n‑zaŋŋi Sɔsitɛniisi - sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ la'aŋŋu duu la kpeema maa - m‑bu u kootu maa tooni. Amaa ka Galeoo ba'a daa ka lala maa ni.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ Kɔrinti n‑niŋi dabsa ayi, ka naayi kyeoosi u mabiisi maa ka daa kyaŋŋi Kyɛnkyiriiya - tinseaa kan zɛ teeku gbiriŋŋu ni nla - nti kpɛ nyariŋŋu ni, n‑du ni u ti kyaŋŋi Siriiya tingbaŋŋu; ka Prisila ni Akwila daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi. Kyɛnkyirii maa ni ka u daa puni u zugu, ŋɔn daa pun pɔ noori la zugu.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Tɔ; ka Apɔloosi ŋɔn daa bɛ Kɔrinti tiŋŋa la, Pɔla daa dɔli sɔseaa kan daa du tingbaŋŋu puuni la, nti paai Ɛfɛsusi na. Bɛni ka u daa paai Yisa nyaandɔliseeba.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gaari nti paai Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa, ka u daa kyɛ ba bɛni. U mammaŋŋa daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu maa, ni u ti di azama n‑ti ba.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: ka daa boosi ba, ni, Ammi, ya daa deaai Sii Kasi maa, yan daa niŋi yɛdda sahaseaa la? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay! - ti na zi n‑wum Sii Kasi yɛla.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma ba saani dabsa beela m‑paasi; amaa u daa bu sakki lala.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ka u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, Ka Naawunni kɔsuubu bɔ ka ya daa deaai? Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Gyɔni kɔsuubu.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: U daa susi soori n‑kyeoosi ba ka yɛli, Naawunni yi ti ti ma soori, n nin labi na. ka naayi bakki ka kyaŋŋi.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Pɔla daa yɛliya, ni, Gyɔni kɔsuubu gbinni n daa nyɛ la: ni, ninvugsɔ yi takki u beebu n‑kyɛ tumbeeri tuma zaa ka ŋmaligi n‑teesi teesisuma; amaa Gyɔni mammaŋŋa daa yɛli niriba maa, ni, ba niŋma yɛdda nti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin dɔli u nyaaŋŋa la, ka ŋɔna n nyɛ Yisa.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Ŋɔn daa yi Ɛfɛsusi ni maa, u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Sizariiya ka sigi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni ni u ti puusi Naawunni yɛddaniŋniba maa. Ka daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ka ban daa ti wum dinŋɔa, ba daa su Naawunni koom maa, ti Dugma Yisa yuuri ni.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: N‑niŋi dabsa ayi bɛni, ka laa n‑yi tiŋŋa wa ni n‑gaari n‑dɔli tinseesi an bɛ Galɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Firigyii tingbaŋŋu, n‑kpamsiri Yisa nyaandɔliba maa zaa.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Ka Pɔla daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa ba zugri, ka Naawunni Sii maa daa paali ba na, ka ba daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'azuna balibubalibu, ni anabitiri yɛla.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Tɔ: ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Apɔloosi. Yɛhudiyadoo n daa nyɛ u, ka u daa yi Alɛkzandiriya tiŋŋa ni na, ka daa mari yɛm pam, halli nti mi Naawunni Bukku yɛla zaa. Ŋɔna daa ti paai Ɛfɛsusi di saha maa.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Ba zaa daa nyɛ la kaman dɔppa piiya ni ayi.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Ba daa pun wulisi u Naawunni soori, ka u daa tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri Yisa yɛla ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa deede, amaa u daa mi la Gyɔni kɔsuubu kɔkɔa.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni halli gɔya ata saha, ka daa kpeeri la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni sufukpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam beebu, ni u ti kyɛ ka ba sakki u yɛla maa.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Ka u daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa la'aŋŋu duu maa puuni. Ka Akwila ni Prisila ŋɔn daa ti wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, ba daa booli u ni, ni, u kyam ba saani na, ka daa wulisi u, m‑paasi u Naawunni soori maa yɛla suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Amaa baseeba mun daa mari la zugkpeeŋŋu, ka daa za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu ka sa'amni Yisa soori maa yɛla paalu ni. Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kyɛ ba, ka daa zaŋŋi baseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa maa n‑wɔligi n‑yi ba saani. Ka beoo kam ba daa kpeeri Tiranusi kariŋŋu duu la ni, n‑tɔ'asiri ka diri azama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Apɔloosi daa boori ni u bakki n‑kyaŋŋi Akaaya tingbaŋŋu maa ni. Ka ti mabiisi daa kpamsi u, ka daa sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba mabiseeba ban bɛ bɛni maa, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑deaai u. Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa la'am ni ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn pun kyɛ ka ba niŋi u yɛdda maa, ka daa suŋŋi ba pam.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Ba daa niŋni nŋɔa, halli yuuma ayi, ka Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu dima zaa, Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima gba, daa ti wum ti Dugma yɛligu maa.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Dama Yɛhudiyadima ŋɔn daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni sahanseaa la, u daa zaŋŋi Naawunni Bukku yɛla n‑tɔ'asi n‑nyaŋŋi ba niriba tooni, n‑wulisiri, ni, Naawunni Masiiya maa nyɛ la Yisa.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Lala saha maa ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka Pɔla daa tumni alahazibu tunkara pam.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi kpakyeesa ni antikyidima an daa siisi u, n‑zi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti baaridima ka ba nya alaafɛa, ka arizimbeeri daa zɔ ka kyɛ ba.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa gyinni n‑katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni maa daa mɔya ni ba zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑katti ba. Ba daa yi yɛnni, ni, Yisa sɔ Pɔla ŋɔn moonni u yɛla la - ŋɔna yuuri zugu ka n yɛli, ni, i yim na.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Ba sunsukkuni seeba n daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyabiisi ayɔpɔi, seeba ban daa nyɛ Sikyiiva - gyeeŋŋu toondaansɔ - biisi.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Amaa arizimbeoo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, M mi Yisa, ka mi Pɔla gba, amaa bandima n nyɛ yarima.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Ka dɔsɔ arizinni ŋɔn daa bɛ u ni maa daa gbaai ba ni n‑nyaŋŋi ba zaa ka pa ba dansi, ka ba naayi zɔ n‑yi yiri maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi yɔri hoo ban daa dɔ'ai ba seem.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Ka Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima ban daa bɛ Ɛfɛsusi la zaa ŋɔn daa ti wum lahabaari kanŋɔa, dabeem daa gbaai ba, ka ba daa tisi ti Dugma Yisa yuuri gyirima pam.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Ka di saha ka niriba pam ban daa niŋi yɛdda daa kyɛ na n‑yɛli ba taalli ni ba tumbeeri yɛla paalu ni.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Halli baseeba ban daa tumni tibeeri tuma, ni ba'akɔligu dima gba, daa la'asi ba tiima gbana n‑nyoaai a bugum zama maa tooni. Ban daa kaali m‑baŋŋi lala gbana maa laasaabu ligri maa, ba nya ka a zaa la'am n‑zoaai pam, hoo ba tiŋŋa anzulifa la'aseesi tusa pisinu.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Lala daa kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛligu maa daa kyɛnni tooni m‑paari niriba pam, ka mari yiiko.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa gbaai kyɛnni, ni u ti dɔli Masidoonya ni Akaaya tingbana, ka gaari halli nti paai Gyɛrusalɛm. Ni, N yi kyaŋŋi n kyɛnni wa n‑naai, see ka n kyaŋŋi Room tiŋŋa gba.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: U daa kpalim Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu n‑yuui beela m‑paasi, ka daa tum u suŋŋniba ayi - Timɔti ni Ɛrasitusi - ni ba daŋŋi u tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Tɔ; ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lala saha maa ka daaŋŋu pam daa doaai niriba maa ni, Yisa soori maa zugu.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Anzulifa mankyeerisɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Dimitiriyusi la. U daa maanni buga gyeeŋŋu neema n‑tiri ba bugseaa kan yuuri boonni Atɛmisa la, ka daa ŋmanni la pɔ'a. Ka lala tuma zugu ka anzulifa tuma baŋŋniba daa ti nya ligri pam.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔna Dimitiriyusi daa booli baseeba zaa ban daa tumni lala tuma taabu maa, ni, ba la'amma na, ka yɛli ba, N zɔdima! ya mi halli, ni, ti nyari arizikki pam ti tuma wa zugu.
