eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Ka ya nyari ka wumni doo wa, Pɔla, yɛla: u tɔ'asiri n‑nyaŋŋi niriba pam Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa wa puuni, ni Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu zaa. U yɛtti, ni, ninsaala ŋɔn maali wunseesi pa la Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ba sakkri lala n‑ti u.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Dizugu di pa seem ni ti tuma wa nin ti kɔŋŋi ka yusuŋŋu; ka din paasi lala zugu, ti gyeeŋŋu duu maa, ni ti bugri tibsigu daana Atɛmisa sɔ ŋɔn bɛ ka puuni la gba nin ti kɔŋŋi gyirima. Pampaŋŋɔ ka Ɛseaa dima zaa - halli duniya wa dima zaa gba - gyeemni u ni. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba nin ti tam u yɛla, Pɔla yɛttɔ'a zugu.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Ban daa wum dinŋɔa, ba sufu daa yisigiya, ka ba daa piili n‑tasiri pampam, ni, Ɛfɛsusi dima bugri! Atɛmisa, yiiko daana.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Ka di daa bu yuui ka tiŋŋa maa zaa daa damni n‑vuura. Niriba maa daa gbaai Gayusi ni Arisitaakusi, baseeba ban daa dɔli Pɔla n‑yi Masidoonya ni na la, ka daa vaai n‑guui hoo ninvugyinni nti paai deema ziiya n‑kpɛ bɛni.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Pɔla daa boori la u yi daadam maa tooni na, amaa u karimbiisi daa bu ti u soori.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Halli bɛni tinkpamma maa seeba gba ban daa di Pɔla zoori daa tum u saani na n‑susiri u, ni, u di kpɛ deema zii maa ni.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: La'aŋŋu maa daa mari la nangbanwala. Seeba n daa tasiri la yɛlli yinni, ka seeba mun tasiri yɛliseaa. Ka niriba maa pam daa bu mi la'aŋŋu wa gbinni ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Yɛhudiyadima maa mun daa kyɛ ka Alɛkzanda kyaŋŋi tooni, ka zama maa puuni ninvugseeba daa tasiri n‑yɛtti u ŋɔn nin niŋi seem. U daa zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑wulisi ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba vuuri ka u tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ba ŋɔn ni u taaba ŋɔn ka taalli seem.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Amaa ban daa ti baŋŋi, ni, u nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira, ba zaa daa la'am n‑tasiri, ni, Ɛfɛsusi dima bugri! Atɛmisa, yiiko daana!; ka kpalim n‑tasiri lala halli nti yuui hoo hawa ayi saha.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka tiŋŋa maa Sakatiri daa naayi kyɛ ka ba sini ba vuuri, ka yɛli, ni, Ya Ɛfɛsusi dima, wumma! Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa wa puuni ka ti guri la Atɛmisa yiikodaana, ni u gyeeŋŋu duu maa, ni u ba'akugri kan daa yi arizana n‑sigi na la. Duniya wa zaa puuni, ninvugsɔ m bɛ ni ŋɔn bu baŋŋi lala? Ay! - sɔ ka ni.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔ zugu, yɛla anŋɔ zɛya ka sɔ ku toaai n‑kyiisi a wa; di bu sum ni ya fɔ surimm? - ka ku gyigi n‑niŋi seelli.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Ya zi niriba wa n‑kyɛ kpa na, amaa ba bu zuuri gyeeŋŋu duri neema, ba mun bu tuuri ka galimni ti bugri maa.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu Dimitiriyusi ni u tuntuntaaba yi nya la taaliseaa sɔ zaa saani, kootudima m bɛ ni, ni gɔmnadima gba bɛ ni: ba nin toaai n‑galim lala nira ba saani.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Di yi ti nyɛ la yɛttɔ'aseaa m‑paasi ka ya boori ya yiisi paalu, di sumsi ni ya mari ka na tiŋŋa maa la'aŋŋu dabsisuŋŋu maa daari.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Din nyɛ seem maa, di bu ya'ai ni ba zaŋŋi ti yɛttɔ'a n‑kyaŋŋi n‑galim ti, zuna daaŋŋu yɛla maa zugu. Di yi ti bɛ lala, ti ku toaai n‑wulisi din taasi zuna yɛttɔ'a maa na, dama daliiri suŋŋu din taasi ka na ka ni.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: Ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai, u daa kyɛ ka zama maa doaai n‑wurigi n‑kuli.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ Sizarii tiŋŋa ka u yuuri boonni Kɔniiliyusi. U daa nyɛ la toondaana sɔ ŋɔn daa su soogya kɔbga fukumsi, ka u soogyadima daa nyɛ ban boonni Italii Dima maa.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa dɔla Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni. U daa suŋŋi Yɛhudiya faradima maa ka susiri Naawunni saha kam.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Di daa niŋi dahinyinni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi hoo wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, ka doo wa daa nya zii. U daa kur nya Naawunni malaaka ka u kpɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, Kɔniiliyusi.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa gɔsi u suŋŋa ka dabeem daa gbaai u; ka u daa sakki, ni, Naa, n dugma! Bɔ n nyɛ di? Malaaka maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, I wunsusigu yɛla ni i tunsuma n‑tiri faradima zaa naayi paai la Naawunni teaari, ka u sufu maai i zugu.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Ka dizugu pampaŋŋɔ tumma niriba ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɔppa m‑bɔ Saimoon ziiya - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, Piita la -.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: ka ba mari u na. U gbeeri la Saimoon sɔ, gbanzaba, saani; u yiri maa zɛ la teeku gbiriŋŋu ziiya.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Tɔ, ka malaaka maa ŋɔn ti u lahabaari wa n‑naai, u daa bakki ka kyɛ u. Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa booli u yiri niriba ayi ni u soogyadima yinni ŋɔn gba daa dɔli Naawunni soori ni nimmoaa ka bɛ u yiri la ni.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: U daa yɛli ba seelli kam din niŋi maa, ka daa tum ba Gyɔppa.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Tɔ; beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka ban daa ti kyɛnni m‑pɔtti Gyɔppa na, Piita daa du yiri la pii maa zugu, agbaa saha, ni u ti puusi Naawunni.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Tɔ. Ninvugseeba daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa na. Lala niriba daa wulisiri ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa la, ni, see ka ba guni hoo Musa ŋɔn daa zaali seem la; ni, di yi pa lala ba ku toaai n‑nya tuliginsim.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Di saha ka kɔm daa ti gbaai u, ka u boori seelli ni u di; ka ban daa maanni diibu sahanseaa maa, u daa nya zii.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa ŋmɛ ba nangbankpeemni pam yɛttɔ'a wa zugu. Ka dizugu ba daa bɔ niriba m‑paasi Pɔla ni Baanabasi zugu n‑tum ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑nya Yisa tuumudima ni kpamma maa, lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: U daa nya ka sangbana yoaai ka bunseelli daa sigri na, ka ka ŋmanni hoo tanti gbantitaari ban lɔ ka tuba anaasi n‑sigsiri ka tiŋŋa na.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Tɔ; ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la'aŋŋu dima ŋɔn daa tum ba maa, ba daa dɔli Fɔnisii ni Samɛrii tingbana n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba nyaandɔlitaaba n‑wulisiri ba ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ŋɔn niŋi tuuba seem maa. Ka di daa niŋi ba nya'asim pam.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: N‑nya, ka bunkɔbri daa bɛ ka puuni - bunkyɛnna ni bunvuura, halli niinsi gba.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Ka ban daa ti kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni maa, ba daa paai yɛddaniŋniba ni tuumudima ni kpamma saani na. Ka ban daa gyaari ba n‑naai maa, ba daa piili ba yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba tum seem.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ka u wum kukɔri, ni, Piita, doaam n‑kɔrigi n‑ŋɔbi.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Amaa Yisa yɛddaniŋniseeba ban daa dɔli Farisii dima soori maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa yɛla. Ba daa yɛli, ni, di sumsi ni ba guni ba, ka kpamsi ba, ni, ba dɔli Musa zaaligu yɛla zaa.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Ay, n Dugma! N na zi n‑di bunseelli kam kan kyisiri, bee, kan mari da'ari.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Tɔ; ka din daa toaai beela, tuumudima ni kpamma maa daa la'am, ni ba ti tɔ'asi lala yɛttɔ'a maa n‑nya a soori ziiya kasi.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ka kukɔri maa maali n‑yɛli u, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn tisi bunseelli, ni, bunsuŋŋu, nyin di kyisiri ka, ni, da'ari.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa la'am n‑dɔli Yisa soori Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa maa ni la sunsukkuni ka karimbadima ni anabidima daa bɛ ni. Baanabasi daa bɛ ni, ni Simiyɔn, sɔ ban boonni Naagya , bee Dɔsabilli, la, ni Luusiyusi, sɔ ŋɔn yi Sairiini tingbaŋŋu ni na, ni Manɛ'an - ŋɔna ni NaaHɛroodi Tɛtirakyi la daa la'am m‑bɛ ni, halli ni Hɛroodi naam diibu saha - ni Sɔla gba.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Tɔ, ka di daa niŋi lala napɔmbuta, ka bunni maa naayi labi n‑du saazugu.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Ka ba daa ti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dinŋɔ zugu n‑yuui, ka Piita naayi doaai n‑yɛli ba, ni, M mabiisi, ya mi halli, ni, mani ka Naawunni daa daŋŋi n‑yiisi, ni n kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la wum lahabasuŋŋu wa man noori ni ka niŋi yɛdda.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Ban daa gyeemni ti Dugma ka loori noori sahanseaa la, Naawunni Sii Kasi maa daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya yiisim Baanabasi ni Sɔla n‑zaali n‑ti ma, ka ba tum man yiisi ba ni ba ti tum tunseesi la.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Piita ŋɔn daa kur teesiri yɛlli wa zugu ka di gyɛri u pam, lala saha ka dɔseeba Kɔniiliyusi ŋɔn sa tum la gba daa pun baŋŋi Saimoon yiri maa ziiya m‑paai na n‑zɛ samanni.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn mi din bɛ ninsaala sufu ni la daa wulisi, ni, ba niŋi yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni; dama u daa ti ba u Sii Kasi hoo ŋɔn daa pun ti ti gba seem la.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Dizugu ba daa puusi Naawunni ni noori loobu pɔi, ka naayi zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ba zugri ka zaali ba soori.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Ka ba daa booli m‑boosi n‑nya saana ŋɔn yuuri boonni Saimoon Piita la bɛ la yiri maa ni, bee?.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Naawunni bu wulisi, ni, ti bɛ ti kɔkɔ ka kyɛ ba, dama ba gba yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka u kpiisi la ba sufu ni taalli n‑ti ba.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Tɔ; ka Naawunni Sii Kasi ŋɔn daa tum Baanabasi ni Sɔla maa, ba daa kyaŋŋi la Sɛlusii tiŋŋa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Piita daa na kur teesiri ŋɔn nya seesi maa yɛla, ka Naawunni Sii maa ti yɛli u, Dɔppa ata m boori i ziiya;.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Ka pampaŋŋɔ, bɔ n niŋi ka ya ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni ni Naawunni? - ka zaŋŋi zitibsa n‑ziili ban ya n‑dɔli u la zugu, ziseesi tirima ŋɔn bu nyaŋŋi n‑zi - halli ti yaadima gba daa bu nyaŋŋi.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Ban daa ti paai bɛni n‑sigi Salamiisi tiŋŋa, ba daa bɛ bɛni n‑kpeeri Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duri ni, nti moonni Naawunni yɛligu. Ka Gyɔni gba daa dɔli, n‑suŋŋni ba.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: dizugu doaama n‑sigi tiŋŋa ni sa! See ka i dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi, ka di kyɛ ka i sufu gyɛri i di zugu. Man Naawunni n tum ba na.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ay! Amaa tirima niŋni yɛdda, ni, ti Dugma Yisa wurilim tuma zugu ka u kyɛ ka ti nin tuligi, ka lala n nyɛ di n‑tiri ban gba.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu muna - ka bɛ la teeku kooma sunsukkuni; ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gyili ka tinsi zaa, nti paai Pafoosi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka ba daa nya tiimaraansɔ, ka u yuuri boonni Baa Yisa. U daa nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira, ka zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa n‑leebi anabi.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Ka Piita daa sigi n‑yɛli niriba maa, ni, Mani ka ya boori maa. Dinŋɔna, bɔ zugu ka ya kyɛ kpa na.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Di saha maa ka daadam maa zaa daa sini vuuri ka wumni Pɔla ni Baanabasi yɛttɔ'a, ka ba daa wulisi ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba tum tunkara ni alahazibu yɛla seem maa, ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: ka daa bɛ Sɛgyeoosi Pɔla yiri ni, sɔ ŋɔn daa di Gɔmna n‑su Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu zaa fukumsi la. Ŋɔn Pɔla daa mari la yɛm pam: u daa booli Baanabasi ni Sɔla ka ba kyɛ na, ŋɔn daa boori ni u wum Naawunni yɛligu zugu.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Naawunni malaaka ŋɔn sa wulisi Kɔniiliyusi seem la zugu. Ŋɔn Kɔniiliyusi mun nyɛ la soogya toondaana, ka u dɔli la soori ka gyeemni Naawunni, ka Yɛhudiyadima zaa tiri u gyirima. Malaaka maa sa yɛli u, ni, u kyɛ ka nyin kyɛ u yiri maa na ka u ti wum yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin mari n‑yɛli u maa.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Ban daa ti tɔ'asi n‑naai maa, ka Gyeemsi daa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli, ni, M Mabiisi, ya wumma!.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Tɔ; ka niriba daaŋŋu maa ŋɔn daa naai la, Pɔla daa booli Yisa nyaandɔliba maa na, ni u ti kpamsi ba. Ka ŋɔn daa kpamsi ba n‑naai, u daa kyeoosi ba ka daa kpa n‑wa Masidoonya.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Amaa ŋɔn Ɛlimasi muna - lala ka ba daa takki tiimaraana maa yuuri - Ɛlimasi daa kyiisi la yɛligu maa, ka daa kpaŋŋni u maŋŋa n‑ta'ari Gɔmna maa ni u di niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti basi ba maa, ti daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi kyippi m‑paai Koosi tiŋŋa. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa kyaŋŋi la Roodisi tiŋŋa, n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Patara tiŋŋa.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Ka Piita daa zi ba n‑kpɛ ni ba ti gbɛ u saani. Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai u daa niŋi siri ka ŋɔn ni ban n kyaŋŋi. Ka Gyɔppa ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa gba daa dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simiyɔn na pun wulisi ti Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yiisi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ninvugseeba seem, ni ba ti leebi ŋɔn Naawunni niriba.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: U daa gɔ n‑dɔli bɛni tinsi maa zaa n‑tɔ'asiri pam ka kpamsiri niriba, halli nti paai Griika tingbaŋŋu.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Di saha ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi yiiko daa paali Sɔla - sɔ ban pa u yupaa, ni, Pɔla la - ka u daa gɔsi tiimaraana maa suŋŋa.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Bɛni ka ti daa nya nyarinseaa kan wa Fɔnisiiya, ka dinzugu ti daa kpɛ ka ni.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai yaasa, ba daa paai Sizarii tiŋŋa. Kɔniiliyusi daa pun booli u dɔ'am ni u zɔdima zaa, ni, ba la'amma na, ka daa guusiri ba soori maa zugu.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Ka ŋɔn yɛli seem ni anabidima yɛligu maa dɔli taaba, dama ba daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:-.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: n‑daa bɛ bɛni gɔya ata. U daa ti ya kyaŋŋi Siriiya, amaa lala saha maa ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai gbaari, ni, ba ti daam u, ka dizugu u daa ŋmaligi n‑labi n‑dɔli Masidoonya.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: ka yɛli u, ni, Nyin Sintaani bii wa! I nyɛ la yɛlisuma zaa dindaana. I mari la yɔmsibeeri, ka boori ni i zaŋŋi ti Dugma yɛlimaŋni n‑leebi ziri! Ay - nyin kyɛl lala!.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Ti daa nya Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu n‑kyɛ ka ti nuzaa ya'ari ka gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Siriiya nti sigi Taaya tiŋŋa maa ni. Bɛni ka ba daa ti ya yiisi nyariŋŋu maa neema.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Piita ŋɔn daa ya kpɛ yiri maa ni, Kɔniiliyusi daa paai u bɛni n‑gbaani u tooni.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Nyama! NaaDauda naam suulinsi pun fiilim; Kaman sugri kan wurim n‑lu. Amaa di nyaaŋŋa man Naawunni nin ti labi na Ni n ti laa m‑paasi ka yaasa, Ka maali ka suŋŋa.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi la daa yi la u tuma tingbana zaa ni na; ba mun yuya nŋɔa: Pirusi bii Soopata la, sɔ ŋɔn daa yi Bɛriiya ni na; Arisitaakusi ni Sɛkundusi mun yi Tɛsalɔnika ni na; Gayusi mun yi Dɛribee ni - m‑paasi Timɔti zugu; Tikyikusi ni Tirɔfimusi mun yi Ɛseaa ni na.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Ti Dugma nin zaŋŋi u nuu n‑ŋmɛ i pampaŋŋɔ. I nin ti leebi zooma, ka ku laa n‑nya wuntaŋŋa neesim ka di paai sahaseaa! Ka di saha ka Ɛlimasi daa ti nya ka zibsim kpɛ u nini ni, ka u daa tuutiri n‑gyinna, m‑boori sɔ ŋɔn nin datti u n‑kyɛnna.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Tirima mun daa bɔ Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ziiya, n‑nya ba. Di saha ka ti daa bɛ ba saani n‑niŋi dabsa ayɔpɔi; ka Naawunni Sii maa daa kyɛ ka ba yɛli Pɔla, ni, u di kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli u, Doaama! Man gba nyɛ la ninsaala.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Ba nin bɔ ba Dugma ziiya dizugu - Ban kam kpalim nyaaŋŋa maa; Ni tinsi zaasa niriba ban kpeeri n yuuri maasim ni maa. Man Naawunni yɛli lala, ka mun nin niŋi:.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Banŋɔ zaa daa daŋŋi tooni n‑guri ti, Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa maa ni.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Ka Gɔmna maa ŋɔn daa ti nya lala, ka wum ban daa tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛla seem, di daa niŋi u alahazibu, ka dizugu u daa niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Ka ti dabsa ŋɔn daa ti naai maa, ba daa yi na ni ba zaali ti soori. Nyaandɔliba maa zaa ni ba pɔ'aba ni biisi daa beeli ti, halli nti yi tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa, ka ti daa gbaani teeku duuri n‑susi wunsusigu.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Ka ba daa gbaai sɔsiga ni taaba ka kpeera, halli nti paai daadam zaa la'aŋŋu.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: Sahaseaa an gaari sa ka ba daa pun baŋŋi lala. .

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Tirima mun daa yuui beela, halli ka Boroboropaalli Kyuu daa ti gaari pɔi, ka ti naayi kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑yi Filipaai tiŋŋa; ka daba anu nyaaŋŋa ti daa ti paai ba la Tirɔ'asi na. Ti daa bɛ bɛni dabsa ayɔpɔi.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla dima daa laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑yi Pafoosi tiŋŋa n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Pɛriga, tinseaa kan bɛ Pamfilii tingbaŋŋu ni. Bɛni ka Gyɔni Maaki daa basi ba ka labi n‑kuli Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: ka daa kyeoosi taaba bɛni. Ka tirima naayi kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ka ba mun daa labi n‑kuli.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Ka Piita daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya mi halli, ni, soori ka ni n‑ti Yɛhudiya ninvugsɔ ni u ti la'am ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pa Yɛhudiya nira, bee, n‑kpɛ u yiri ni. Amaa Naawunni wulisi ma, ni, n di kyisi ninsaalisɔ yɛla, ni, da'ari daana n nyɛ u.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Ka Gyeemsi daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Man teesa nyɛ la nŋɔa: ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la yi ti niŋi tuuba n‑dɔli Yisa, di bu sumni ni ti daamni ba yɛlimugsa.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Ka Alahadaari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ti daa la'am taaba ni ti puri taaba paanu. Pɔla daa tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba maa n‑yuui, halli ni yuntasuusi, dama u daa boori ni, beoo yi ti neaai, u nin bakki.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Pɔla dima mun daa yi Pɛriga n‑gaari tooni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Antiyɔkku tinseaa, kan bɛ Pisidii tingbaŋŋu la ni. Ka Yɛhudiyadima Vuusim Dabsiri daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ba daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu ni n‑zinni.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Ti daa yi Taaya n‑du m‑paai Tɔlimeesi tiŋŋa, n‑sigi m‑puusi ti mabiisi la, ka daa bɛ ba saani dabsiri yinni.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Dizugu, yan daa booli ma sahaseaa maa, n daa doaai n‑kyɛ na ka bu guusi. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna, ya bu ya n‑kyɛ ka n baŋŋi bunseelli zugu yan booli ma na maa.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: See ka ti sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba, ni, ba di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, a seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ka ba di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Ti daa la'am la gyirambisa duseaa ni, ka firiladima pam n daa bɛ bɛni;.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Ka ban daa karim Musa sɔppu kariŋŋu maa n‑naai la, ni anabidima sɔppu kariŋŋu gba, la'aŋŋu kpamma maa daa tum ba saani na, ni, Ti mabiisi, ya yi mari lahabaseaa kan nin kpamsi niriba wa, ti boori ni ya tɔ'asi n‑ti ti.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa laa n‑du m‑paai Sizarii tiŋŋa nti kpɛ Filipi yiri, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ tuntumniba ayɔpɔi la ni yinni, ka daa yiisiri lahabasuŋŋu maa wa'azu.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa labsi n‑yɛli, Di dabsa anaasi nŋɔ ka n daa bɛ n yiri wa ni, saha hoo kanŋɔa, wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, n‑susiri Naawunni. Ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suturinya'ara daa pusi ya'abi n tooni na.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Dama ba daa kur tɔ'asiri Musa yɛla ti yaadima saha halli ni zuna; ba karimni a ni tinsi kam zaa puuni, ka moonni a Vuusim Dabsiri kam, la'aŋŋu duri puuni.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: ka dizugu goom daa mari na'akyimbilisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Yutikusi. U daa zinni n‑tabli la takoro ni. Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri n‑yuui maa, Yutikusi daa gbisi n‑zɔkki, n‑yi gyirambisa maa, kan tɔm buta zugu, n‑lu tiŋŋa na. Ba daa sigi na n‑kpikki u, ni, u kpiya.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Ka dizugu Pɔla daa yisigi n‑zaani, n‑gaasi u nuu, ka daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri, ni, M mabiisi, ya Izraɛli dima, ni ban dɔli Izraɛli dima soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni, ya wumma!.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Tɔ; ka Filipi daa mari u bipunsi, pɔ'asara anaasi ban bɛ u yiri maa ni, ka ban ka Naawunni daa tiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a ni ba tɔ'asira.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: ka yɛli, Kɔniiliyusi, i wunsusigu yɛla ni i tunsuma n‑tiri faradima zaa naayi paai Naawunni teaari ka u sufu maai i zugu.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Tɔ; ka ba zaa daa la'am taaba - tuumudima maa, ni kpamma, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa zaa - ka daa gbaai ba gbaari, ni, ba ya n‑yiisi la ba ninvugseeba n‑tum ba Antiyɔkku, ban ni Pɔla ni Baanabasi. Ka dizugu ba daa yiisi la Gyuudasi , ni Sailasi; ban mun daa bɛ la Yisa nyaandɔliba toondaandima sunsukkuni.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Amaa Pɔla mun daa sigi na n‑dooni bii maa zugu n‑gɔbi u, ka yɛli ba, Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya. U nɛ ni maa.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Naawunni n su Izraɛli dima yɛla: u daa yiisi ti yaadima ka kyɛ ka ba daa zoaai ka ba yuuri du, ban daa nyɛ saamma Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni sahanseaa la. Tɔ, ka di nyaaŋŋa u daa zaŋŋi u yiiko ni kpeeŋŋu n‑yiisi ba na.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Ti daa bɛ ba saani n‑yuui beela pɔi, ka anabisɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Agabusi daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Ka dizugu pampaŋŋɔ tumma niriba ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɔppa m‑bɔ Saimoon ziiya - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, Piita la - ka ba mari u na. U gbeeri la Saimoon sɔ, gbanzaba, saani: u yiri maa zɛ la teeku gbiriŋŋu ziiya.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Ban ka ba daa tɔlim gbaŋŋu maa, ni#:- Nnya, tirima, ya mabiseeba ban nyɛ Yisa tuumudima ni kpamma la, m puusiri ya: yaseeba ban nyɛ ti mabiisi, halli ka ya pa la ti Yɛhudiyataaba la, ka bɛ la Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Siriiya ni Silisii tingbana la zaa.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Ka ti daa laa n‑du saazugu, nti ŋmaai paanu m‑puri n‑ti taaba n‑di; Ka Pɔla daa laa n‑gbaai u yɛttɔ'a maa, halli ka beoo ti neaai, ka naayi bakki.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: ka daa gɔsiri ba yɛla yuuma pisinaasi yoo ni;.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Ka u daa kyɛ ti saani na n‑zaŋŋi Pɔla sɛlooriga n‑lɔ u mammaŋŋa gbaya ni u nuusi; ka yɛli, ni, Naawunni Sii Kasi yɛliya: Lala ka Yɛhudiyaseeba ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm maa nin ti lɔ sɛlooriga wa daana, n‑zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi u ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa nuusi ni. .

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Dizugu ka n daa tum yooma m‑booli i maa, ka i gba niŋi suŋŋa n‑kyɛ na wa. Pampaŋŋɔ ti zaa bɛ la Naawunni tooni ni ti ti wum Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti i ni i ti yɛli ti.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Ti wumiya, ni, seeba daa yi ti saani m‑paai ya na - tirima mun daa bu tum ba - ka daa daamni ya n‑sa'amni ya sufu ni yɛttɔ'aseesi ban yɛtti maa.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Na'akyimbila la muna - ba daa mari u n‑kuli ka u mari alaafɛa; ka di daa niŋi ba sufumaasim pampam.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: m‑paai Kyɛna'an tingbaŋŋu na. Bɛni ka u daa sa'am bura ayɔpɔi ka daa zaŋŋi ba tiŋŋa maa n‑ti u niriba.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Tin daa wum nŋɔa, tin ni ti niriba ban daa bɛ bɛni maa zaa daa bɛlim Pɔla, ni, u di kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Tɔ; ka Piita daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli, Pampaŋŋɔ n yi baŋŋiya, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni, Naawunni bu gamsira.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Dizugu ka ti la'am taaba n‑sakki n‑gbaai, ni, ti yiisi ninvugseeba n‑tum ya saani na, m‑paasi Pɔla ni Baanabasi zugu. Halli ni Pɔla ni Baanabasi gba nyɛ la ti zɔsuma.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Tɔ; Pɔla daa pun yɛli, ni, u ya kyaŋŋi la nɔba m‑paai Asɔsi tiŋŋa, ka dizugu tirima mun daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni ni ti ti tuusi u.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa daa paai yuuma kɔbsinaasi ni pisinu ziiya. Dinŋɔ ŋɔn daa gaari maa, Naawunni daa yiisi kpambaya n‑ti ba, halli ka anabi Samuwɛli saha daa ti paai na.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Amaa Pɔla daa yɛli ti, di saha maa, ni, Wula zugu ka ya niŋni lala? Ya kumni n‑sa'amni la n sufu. Man mun niŋi siri ni n kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm ka ba ti zaŋŋi ma n‑lɔ - halli ba yi ku ma gba - n Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: amaa u deaari butiri kam niriba ban gyeemni u ka tumni din tuusi seem la.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ka daa zaŋŋi ba nyɔvuya m‑pa talima ti Dugma Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai ti Asɔsi na maa, u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa na ka ti daa du n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Mitiliini tiŋŋa.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Ni, Ka lala saha maa ba daa susi ti Dugma, ni, ba boori la naaba, ka u daa zaŋŋi la Sɔla, Kyiisi bii la, n‑ti ba - sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Bɛngyamin za'anɔri nira la. U daa di la naam ka di paai yuuma pisinaasi.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: Ka tin daa kpamsiri u n‑gyɛ maa, ti daa sini ka yɛli, Naawunni yuubu niŋma.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Yɛttɔ'a maa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti Izraɛli dima, ni, ba nin nya sufudoaa Yisa Masiiya zugu; ni, ŋɔn Yisa siri nyɛ la sɔkam zaa Dugma maa.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Dizugu ti tumni la Gyuudasi ni Sailasi ni ba zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a m‑paasi tin sɔbi seelli wa, n‑wulisiri di gbinni.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa paai Kyiyɔsi tingbaŋŋu zii sa. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti laa n‑neaai ti daa lɔŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Samɔsi ya'ari: n‑neaai beoo yaasa ka ti daa paai Miliitusi tiŋŋa.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Ka di nyaaŋŋa Naawunni daa basi Sɔla ka yiisi Dauda, Gyɛsi bii la, ka u leebi ba naaba. Naawunni daa tɔ'asi Dauda yɛla nŋɔa: N nya ka Gyɛsi bii Dauda n nyɛ man boori sɔa: ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin tum man boori seem n nyɛ u.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ. Ka lala dabsa ŋɔn daa ti naai, ti daa sɛ'asi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Ya zaa mi halli din daa piili n‑niŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu maa ni, sahanseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa mooli Naawunni koom suubu yɛla, halli nti paai Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu zaa.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Tɔ: Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi ŋɔn gbaai n‑zaali seem nŋɔa - ka ti gba sakki lala: ni, ti di daamni ya m‑paasi yɛttɔ'akpana ayi-ata wa#:-.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa boori ni u ti gaari yooma m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm, ka Pɛntikoosi Kyuu dabsa ku ti gaari; dizugu u daa bu boori ni u ti yuui Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni, see ka u kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑yakki n‑gaari Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna di daa nyɛ ŋɔn NaaDauda za'anɔri dɔsɔa, Yisa, ka Naawunni daa yiisi, hoo ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori seem la, ni u ti leebi ŋɔn nin fa Izraɛli dima m‑basi.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Ka nyaandɔliseeba ban bɛ Sizariiya la daa dɔli ti n‑zi ti n‑kyaŋŋi Mineesɔn yiri, ni ti kpɛ u saani - u nyɛ la Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu nira ka daa pun yuui Yisa dɔligu ni.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Yisa, Nazarɛti doo maa yɛla nla: Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka u Vuusim Kasi ni u yiiko bɛ u ni seem la, ka lala ka Naawunni daa wulisi, ni, u diisi u naam. Ni, u daa gyili n‑tumni tunsuma ka tibri Sintaani ŋɔn daa daamni seeba zaa, Naawunni ŋɔn daa bɛ u saani zugu.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: ni, ya di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ya di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla ni. Ya yi gbaai ya mansi ka kyɛ anŋɔ maa, ya nin niŋi suŋŋa. Naawunni paasim ya goom.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Ka dizugu ŋɔn daa bɛ Miliitusi maa, u daa tum Ɛfɛsusi m‑booli Yisa nyaandɔliba kpamma maa na.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti ya piili u tuma maa, Gyɔni daa pun daŋŋi tooni m‑moonni n‑tiri Izraɛli dima, ni, ba kyɛli ba tumbeeri ka takki ba beebu, ka su Naawunni koom.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ti daa paai Gyɛrusalɛm na, ka ti mabiisi maa daa deaai ti ni sufumaasim.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tɔ. Ka dinŋɔna tirima n nyɛ u sɛhara dima; ti yɛtti, ni, ti siri daa bɛ ni n‑nya ŋɔn Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem Yɛhudiyadima tingbana zaa ni, halli Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni gba. Ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli doo zugu n‑ku u;.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Tɔ; ka dɔppa maa daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai Antiyɔkku na, ka daa la'asi yɛdda dima maa zaa nti ti ba gbaŋŋu maa.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Ban daa ti paai u na, u daa yɛli ba, ni, Ya mi halli m beesigu ŋɔn daa bɛ seem, man daa bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la; man daa piili m‑bɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu maa ni halli nti yuui.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Ka sahanseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa ya paai u tuma naari, u daa yɛli, Ya teesiya, ni, n nyɛ la ŋɔni? M pa la yan guri sɔ maa. Amaa ya wumma! U bɛ la n nyaaŋŋa n‑kyɛnni na, ka man bu sumni m‑pirigi u namna n‑ti u! .

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Pɔla daa zi ti n‑kpɛ Gyeemsi saani ka kpamma maa zaa gba daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: amaa Naawunni daa doaai u kum ni di daba ata daari, ka kyɛ ka niriba nya u.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Ban daa karim ka maa, di daa niŋi ba sufumaasim ka laa n‑kpamsi ba sufu pam.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: N daa gyeemni Naawunni ni mansigsigu, ni kunkuma; halli ni Yɛhudiyadima ban daa boori ni ba tum ma tumbeeri zaa.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: Ka Pɔla daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ, m mabiisi, yarima ban yi Ibrahim butiri ni na, ni seeba ban bɛ ya saani n‑gyeemni Naawunni la, tirima saani ka tuliginsim lahabasuŋŋu wa paai na.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Ka tin daa puusi ba n‑naai maa, Pɔla daa yɛli ba u tuma zaa yinni yinni - tunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u n‑tum n‑ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Pa sɔkam n daa nya u, amaa tirima - Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun yiisi seeba ni ti niŋi u sɛhara dima maa - tin daa nya u. Tin ni ŋɔna n daa la'am n‑di ka nyu, u kum yisigri nyaaŋŋa maa.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Gyuudasi ni Sailasi gba daa nyɛ la anabidima, ka ba daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anya'asa pam n‑kpamsiri ba mabiisi maa.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Ya mi, ni, n daa bu kɔŋŋi wa'azu yiisigu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseelli kam din nin suŋŋi ya, ka wulisiri a n‑tiri ya paalu ni, ka laa n‑gyinni yiri yiri n‑tɔ'asiri maa.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Gyɛrusalɛm niriba ni ba toondaandima daa bu baŋŋi anabidima sɔppu yɛla gbinni, halli ni ba karimni a ni Vuusim Dabsiri kam zaa maa zaa yɔri: ka dizugu ba daa kyɛ ka lala yɛla maa daa niŋi lala; ka daa kari ŋɔn Yisa sariya n‑yɛli, ni, u luya ni.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Ban daa wum lala, ba daa pɛ'ai Naawunni, ka laa n‑yɛli Pɔla, ni, Ti mabiiya, i bu nya? Niriba tusatusa bɛ la ti saani ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima ka niŋi Yisa yɛdda. Ka banŋɔ zaa nyɛ la ban boori ni ba na kpalim n‑dɔli ba zaaligu kaari maa.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: U daa kpamsi ti, ni, ti wulisim niriba ka laa n‑di sɛhara, ni, ŋɔna ka Naawunni yiisi ni u ti niŋi sɔ ŋɔn nin kari niriba kam zaa sariya - ban na nɛa, ni ban pun kpi zaa m‑paasi.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Ban daa niŋi saha beela bɛni, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa zaali ba soori, ka taaliseaa ka ni. Ba daa labi n‑kuli ban daa tum ba maa saani.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: N yɛli Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima zaa, ni, ba ŋmaligma n‑kyɛ Naawunni saani na, ka niŋi ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Halli ba daa bu nya daliiri seelli din wulisi, ni, di sumni ni ba ku u gba, ni di zaa yɔri ba daa susi Pailiti, ni, u kyɛ ka ba ku u.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Tɔ, ka ba wum i yɛla maa, ni, i wulisiri Yɛhudiyaseeba ban bɛ tinzuna tinsi zaa ni, ni, ba kyɛli Musa zaaligu yɛla; yaasa, ni, i yɛtti ba, ni, ba di laa n‑guni ba biisi n‑dɔli kaari maa.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Halli ŋɔn Yisa yɛla ka anabidima zaa daa tɔ'asiri n‑diri sɛhara, ni, ŋɔn yuuri zugu ka nira kam zaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda nin nya u tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Tɔ. Saha ŋɔn daa ti gaari beela, Pɔla daa yɛli Baanabasi, ni, Kyɛ ka ti labi n‑kaai tinseesi tin daa pun kyaŋŋi n‑wulisi Naawunni yɛttɔ'a maa, n‑nya ti mabiisi maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ ka Sii Kasi m mari ma n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm: m mun bu mi din ya n‑niŋi ma bɛni.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ba daa tum anŋɔ zaa hoo Sɔppu maa ŋɔn daa pun wulisi u yɛla seem la; ka tum lala n‑naai. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa sigsi u kum dɔpulli maa zugu na n‑zaŋŋi u n‑sɔ'ai gballi ni.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Dizugu ti ya niŋi la wula? Ba nin baasi n‑wum, ni, i kyɛ kpa na.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a wa, Naawunni Sii Kasi maa daa kpɛ ban wumni u yɛla maa zaa sufu ni.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Ka di saha ka Baanabasi daa boori ni u zaŋŋi Gyɔni, sɔ ŋɔn boonni Maaki la, ka ba kyaŋŋi.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Man mi seaa nyɛ la, ni, tiŋŋa kam zaa puuni ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa yɛtti, ni, m baŋŋma, ni, n nin nya sarika duu yɛla ni wahala.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Amaa Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Tɔ; wumma! Ti nin wulisi i, nyin nin niŋi seem. Ti mari la ti dɔppa anaasi ban pun dɔli ti kaari m‑pɔ noori.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Ka Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban dɔli Piita na daa nya lala: Yɛhudiya niriba n daa nyɛ ba, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, dama halli ban pa Yɛhudiyadima gba ka Naawunni tisi u piini, ni, u Sii Kasi maa kpaai m‑paali ba.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Amaa Pɔla daa bu sakki ni ba zaŋŋi u, ŋɔn daa bakki ka kyɛ ba Pamfilii tingbaŋŋu ni maa zugu, ka daa bu laa n‑dɔli ba n‑la'amni n‑tumna.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Amaa m ba'a ka m maŋtoori nyɔvuri maa ni, see ka n dɔli n beebu soori nti paai ka naari; ka ti tum tunseaa ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn zaali ma zaa, halli nti wulisi Naawunni piinisuma lahabaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem zaa.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Ka di daa niŋi dabsa pam ka u daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisiri n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa pun dɔli u n‑yi Galilii n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na. Ban mun niŋi u sɛharadima, m‑moonni ban nya ka wum yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑tiri Naawunni niriba maa, halli ni zuna.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Dinŋɔna zaŋŋma lala niriba maa ka sɛ'asi i maŋŋa kasikasi n‑dɔli ba n‑yɔ ba nangbampɔri sama zaa n‑ti ba, ka ba nin toaai m‑puni ba zugri ka naayi naai ba yɛla zaa. Ka dina nin kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa baŋŋi, ni, ban yɛtti yɛttɔ'aseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla maa, ni, lala yɛttɔ'a maa ka gbinni, amaa i mammaŋŋa gba tumni la kaari ni ta'ara ŋɔn zaali seem maa.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Dama ba daa wum ka ba tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'azuna balibubalibu m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Di saha ka Piita daa boosi n‑yɛli.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ka ba daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dizugu, halli ka daa wɔligi ka kyɛ taaba. Ka dinzugu Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi Maaki ka ba kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Saapirusi.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ m mi, ni, ya sɔ ku ti laa n‑nya ma yaasa; ya saani ka n daa bɛ ni n‑gyinni m‑moonni Naawunni naam yɛla n‑ti ya zaa.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Dizugu tirima gba bɛ la kpa wa, ni ti yɛli ya lahabasuŋŋu maa, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa tisi noori n‑ti ya yaadima la.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ka niŋni Yisa yɛdda muna - ti daa pun sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba n‑yɛli tin daa gbaai seem, ni, ba mari ba mansi suŋŋa ka di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, ni seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim: ka di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: ni, Sɔ nin toaai n‑ta'ai niriba wa ni ba di su Naawunni koom? Ba deaai la Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa hoo tin gba ŋɔn daa deaai u seem la.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Pɔla mun daa zaŋŋi la Sailasi m‑bakki; nyaandɔliba maa daa zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi Naawunni nuu ni ka kyeoosi ba.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Di zugu n yɛli ya zuna, ni, ninsaala kam kum taalli bu dɔli ma.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: lala ka u siri tum n‑ti ti, tin nyɛ ba ya'ansi la; ka u yisigi Yisa kum ni. Dina ka ba daa sɔbi Yuuma bukku maa ni, yuumni ayi daana puuni la, ni#:- M bii n nyɛ i; Zuna dabsiri wa ka n dɔ'ai i.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Pɔla siri daa zaŋŋi niriba maa ka daa sɛ'asi u maŋŋa; ka ŋɔn ni ban zaa daa kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni u yɛli n‑ti maalimaanniba n‑zaali ba mansɛ'asigu naari dabsiri maa, di nyɛ la dabsiseaa ban ya maali maaligu maa ba zaasa zugu n‑naai ba nangbampɔri yɛla.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Dizugu u daa ti noori ni ba suum ba Naawunni koom Yisa yuuri ni. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma m‑bɛ ba saani n‑niŋi dabsa ayi.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Tɔ: ka Pɔla dima daa kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Amfipɔlisi ni Apɔloonya tinsi n‑yakki n‑gaari nti paai Tɛsalɔnika tiŋŋa, tinseaa Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu ŋɔn daa bɛ ni la.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Ka ba daa dɔli la Siriiya ni Silisiiya n‑kpamsiri ban niŋi yɛdda n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni Yisa maa.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Halli m bu kɔŋŋi ni m mooli Naawunni ŋɔn teesi, ni, u nin niŋi seem zaa n‑ti ya.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: N‑laa m‑paasi, ni, ŋɔna nin yisigi u kum ni, ka ku laa n‑nya poosigu yaasa: u daa yɛli nŋɔa#:- N nin zaŋŋi bunsunseesi Dauda ŋɔn daa deaai la, Ka a nyɛ la bunveela Ka birisigu laa n‑ka a puuni la, M‑mari na n‑ti i.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Tɔ; ka dabsa ayɔpɔi maa daari ŋɔn daa ti paari na, Yɛhudiyaseeba ban yi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni na daa ti nya Pɔla, ka u bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka ba daa dam zama maa ka daa gbaai u.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Dizugu ka Pɔla daa dɔli sɔseaa ŋɔn daa yi dɔli sahakam la, ka daa ti kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu maa ni Vuusim Dabsiri daari. U daa niŋi lala ka di daa paai Vuusim Dabsa ata, ka ŋɔn ni niriba diri azama. Ka u daa teaari ba Naawunni Litaafi yɛla.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Ka di daa laa n‑wulisi luginseaa ni, n‑yɛli, ni#:- I ku kyɛ ka i maŋtoori ninvugsuŋŋu nin leebi bumpoosiri.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Ka tasiri n‑yɛtta, Yarim Izraɛli dima, ya ku suŋŋi ti? Doo wa n nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn wulisiri niriba ya'ari kam, ka tuuri ti butiri, ni ti zaaligu, ni ti Yiri wa! Halli u zi la Griika dima gba n‑kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa na, n‑kyɛ ka ka da'ai zaa.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Dizugu, m Badima, n susiri ya, ni, ya gɔsim ya mansi, ka laa n‑guri ninvugseeba Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑niŋi ya nuusi ni ni ya gɔsiri ba maa, hoo ban guri peesi seem mooni la. Naawunni niriba n nyɛ ba, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u maŋtoori ziim n‑yɔ seeba samni, ni ba ti nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum tuligra sɔ na n‑diisi u Masiiya naam la. Ka u daa wulisiri ba di gbinni, ni, lala Masiiya maa soori nyɛ la wahala diibu soori, ka di nyaaŋŋa kum neaari nin bɛ ni. Ni, dizugu Masiiya maa n nyɛ ŋɔnŋɔa, ni, Yisa sɔ man moonni u yɛla n‑tiri ya maa.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Ba daa pun nya Tirɔfimusi - Ɛfɛsusi nira la - Pɔla saani, ka ba bɛ la timpuuni; lala zugu ka ba daa ti teesi, ni, ŋɔna ka Pɔla zi n‑kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa na.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Tɔ, ka NaaDauda ŋɔn daa tum Naawunni yuubu u duniya wa ni beebu saha halli nti naai maa, u daa ti ka ni, m‑paai u yaadima saani. Ammi, di saha u ningbina daa bu poaai? Ay - a poaaya!.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Ka tiŋŋa maa zaa daa doaai, ka niriba maa daa zɔ n‑la'am n‑gbaai Pɔla n‑darigi u n‑yiisi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka daa yɔ Yiri maa za'anɔya yoomyooma.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiyadimseeba daa deaai u yɛligu maa ka daa paasi Pɔla ni Sailasi zugu; ka Griika dimseeba ban puusiri Naawunni la, ba bɔbgu, ni pɔ'akpamma gba daa bu kɔŋŋi paasigu.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: M mi halli, ni, n yi ti bakki, di nin ti bɛ kaman kunduna putoo dima nin ti kpɛ na, ka ba ku ti zɔ bunkɔbri maa namboo halli beela.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa yisigi sɔ u kum ni la - ŋɔn mun daa bu poaai.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Ka ban daa moori ni ba ku u sahanseaa la, lala lahabaari daa paai langyima kpeema maa saani, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm zaa bɛ la zaba ni.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa zabi la nyuuri, ka daa booli ninvugkyɔ'ama seeba ban daa guusi n‑guusiri daa puuni, ka ti la'asi zama pam n‑daamni tiŋŋa maa niriba. Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑zaani Gyeesɔni yiri tooni, Pɔla ni Sailasi ŋɔn daa kpɛ seelli la, ni, u yiisim ba n‑ti ba na.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Di pa seem, halli yarima puuni gba seeba nin ti doaai n‑ŋmaligi yɛlimaŋni maa ka kyɛ ka nyaandɔliba maa ti dɔli ba m‑burigi n‑yi sɔsuŋŋu maa ni.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Tɔ; ya baŋŋma, ni, di nyɛ la ŋɔna zugu ka ba moonni n‑tiri ya, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ya taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ya. Ka lala taalli maa muna - Musa ŋɔn daa wulisi kaari maa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑yiisi ya ka puuni.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Di saha u daa doaai ya'abi n‑zaŋŋi langyima ni ba toondaanseeba n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai bɛni. Ka zama maa ŋɔn daa ti nya kpeema maa ni langyimadima maa, ba daa kyɛ Pɔla buubu.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Amaa ban daa bu ti nya ba maa, ba daa gbaai Gyeesɔni ni ninvugseeba ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda la, n‑darigi ba n‑kyaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa tinkpamma saani. Ba daa kur tasiri n‑yɛtta, Ninvugseeba ban sa'amni duniya zaa yɛla maa paai ti na!.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Dizugu ya guusima sahakam! Ya teaama n yɛla, ni, yuuma ata an gaari wa n daa bu kyɛ ya kpamsigu ni nintam, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ya baŋŋma suŋŋa, ka di ti nya wahala hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa wulisi seem la, ni#:-.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Ka kpeema maa daa kyɛ ka ba gbaai u, n‑lɔ u kutti ayi. Ka daa boosi, ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa, ni ŋɔn niŋi tumbɛseelli.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: Ba kyɛ ti tiŋŋa wa na, halli ka Gyeesɔni deaai ba n‑kpeesi u yiri ni. Ba galimni la NaaTitaari zaaligu la, dama ba yɛliya, ni, Nasɔ m bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Yisa.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ n nin zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi Naawunni nuusi ni, ni u piinisuma yɛla an nin toaai m‑maali ya yɛla suŋŋa, ka kyɛ ka ya di ya faari tɔrli Naawunni mammantoori niriba zaa sunsukkuni.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Ya nyam dinŋɔa, yarima bunyaarisi wa! Di nin ti gyɛri ya pam: ya kyɛma sa! Ka baŋŋi, ni, n tumni n tuma ya dabsa wa ni - Alahazibu tunseaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ka yɛdda, Halli ba yi ti wulisi ya ka yɛla gba. .

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Ka zama maa seeba daa tasiri n‑yɛtti kookooba ka daa bu yɛtti yɛlli yinni, ka u daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑baŋŋi di gbinni ni yɛlimaŋni, ba vuuri maa zugu. Ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, ba zaŋŋma u m‑mari n‑kyɛ Baarikyi yiri maa na.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Ban daa yɛtti lala yɛttɔ'a maa, ba daa ka'ali la zama ni tinkpamma maa pam.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: N kɔrisi daa ka sɔsɔ ligri ni, bee, u sutura ni.

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Tɔ: Ka ban daa puusi n‑naai ka daa yiiri la'aŋŋu duu maa ni na, ba daa yɛli Pɔla ni Baanabasi, ni, ba labma na Vuusim Dabsiri kan kyɛnni na nti tugi yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai dɔliga maa ni, langyima maa daa kpikki u n‑zi, niriba ŋɔn kpa'ari n‑daamni seem la zugu.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Ka kpamma maa daa zaali Gyeesɔni ni u taaba samni ka yɛli ba, ni, ba di laa n‑zaali yɛttɔ'aseaa, ka naayi basi ba.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Ya zaa mi, ni, n daa tumni n nuusi tuma wa, ni n ti ku m bukaata zaa, ni n taaba dinni gba.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa wurigi n‑naai, Yɛhudiyadima maa pam, ni seeba ban daa dɔli ba Naawunni soori la, daa dɔli Pɔla ni Baanabasi; ka ba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑kpaamni ba, ni, ba mari Naawunni piiniseaa ban deaai la suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Ka zama maa daa kur dɔli ba n‑tasiri pampam, ni, Ya kyɛ ka u baŋŋi.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Ka baseeba ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa doaai di dabsiri maa yuŋŋu, n‑zaali Pɔla ni Sailasi soori, ni ba kyaŋŋi nti paai Bɛriiya. Ban daa ti paai lala tiŋŋa maa, ba daa kpɛ la Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu la ni.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Ka n daa niŋi lala, ni n ti wulisi ya, ni, di sumni ni ya gba tumni lala ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai n‑suŋŋni ban ka yaa la. Yan di tam ti Dugma Yisa yɛligu la, ni, piini tisibu mari anfaani n‑gaari piini deaari. .

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri maa ŋɔn daa paai na la, tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa daa la'amiya, ni ba ti wum Naawunni yɛligu maa.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti ya zaŋŋi Pɔla n‑kpeesi Baarikyi yiri maa ni, u daa boosi kpeema maa n‑yɛli, ni, Soori bɛ ni, ni, n yɛli seelli n‑ti i?; ka u daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, I wum la Griiktiri, fa?.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Bɛni niriba maa sufu daa neaai n‑gaari Tɛsalɔnika dima dinni. Ba daa deaai Naawunni yɛligu maa ni nimmoaa, ka daa gbaai Naawunni Litaafi kariŋŋu dabsa kam, ni ba ti baŋŋi di siri bɛ lala, bee?.

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Tɔ; ka ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa n‑susi Naawunni, ŋɔna ni ban zaa.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn daa ti nya daadam wa, nyuuri daa wum ba pam, ka ba daa gbaai kyiisigu, ni, Pɔla ŋɔn wulisiri yɛttɔ'aseesi la pa la yɛlimaŋni, ka tuuri u tupeela.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Ka dinŋɔna i pa la Igyipti nira la? - Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa dam dɔppa tusa anaasi ni ba za'asiri gɔmnanti noori n‑yi n‑kpɛ yoo ni n‑kuuri niriba la.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Ba pam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, ni Griika pɔ'akpamma m‑paasi, ka ba dɔppa gba daa bu kɔŋŋi yɛdda niŋbu.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Ka ba zaa daa kumna, ka gɔbri u m‑puusiri u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Amaa Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi ba sufukpeemni n‑labsi ba, n‑yɛli, Tɔ! Ti yi da bu daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka yarima wum Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa, di naa n‑ku mari soori. Amaa yan za'asiri maa ka yɛnni, ni, ya bu sumsi ni ya nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari maa; tɔ, ti nin labsi ti zikki la ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Ka Pɔla daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay! Yɛhudiya nira n nyɛ ma, ka n yi la Taasusi ni na, tinseaa kan zɛ Silisii tingbaŋŋu ni la. Tinseaa kan bu kɔŋŋi gyirima suulinsi nira n siri nyɛ ma, walla! Dizugu n susiri i, ni, i ku kyɛ ka n tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba wa.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadimseeba bam bɛ Tɛsalɔnika la ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Bɛriiya gba ka Pɔla moonni Naawunni yɛligu maa, ba daa kyɛ na n‑daamni daadam maa ka ka'anni ba.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Din daa niŋi ba sufusa'aŋŋu pam daa nyɛ la ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa, ni, ba ku laa n‑nya u nindaa yaasa. Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa beeli u m‑paai nyariŋŋu maa ni.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Dama lala ka Naawunni daa zaali ti noori, ni#:- N yiisi i, ni i ti leebi neesim, N‑neaai n‑ti tinzundima ban pa ya niriba; Ka laa n‑leebi tuliginsim soori N‑tiri duniya wa lugri kam zaa dima. .

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti u soori maa, Pɔla daa zaani dɔliga maa saazugu ka wukki u nuu n‑gaasi n‑wulisi zama maa; ka din daa fɔ surimm, u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a, Hibruuli, ni; n‑yɛtti, ni.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Dinzugu di daa bu yuui ka yɛddaniŋniba daa zaŋŋi Pɔla n‑taasi halli nti paai teeku gbiriŋŋu; Sailasi mun daa kpalim m‑bɛ Bɛrii timpuuni, ŋɔn ni Timɔti gba.

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Ka ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ban daa ti wum lala, ba mari la sufupeelli pam m‑pɛ'ari ti Dugma yɛligu maa. Ka ba seeba daa nyɛ seeba ban ya n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda di zugu.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa zaali Pɔla soori maa daa mari u n‑kyaŋŋi halli nti paai Ateensi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka Pɔla daa tɔlim ba Sailasi ni Timɔti saani, ni, ba yɛlim ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑kyɛ u saani na yooma. Ka ba naayi labi n‑kuli Bɛriiya.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Ka Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu daa gyili m‑paai bɛni tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Pɔla ŋɔn daa bɛ Ateensi n‑guri Sailasi ni Timɔti maa, u sufu daa bu dɔya, ŋɔn daa kur gɔsiri n‑nyari ba'a ni buga ŋɔn daa zoaai bɛni seem maa zugu.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima daa kpa'atiri la pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli ba Naawunni soori ka nyɛ tiŋŋa maa ninvugtibsa la, ni tinkpamma maa m‑paasi, halli ka di ti paai ni ba zabri Pɔla ni Baanabasi n‑niŋi ba putoo tuma, ka kari ba n‑yiisi ba tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Ka dizugu u daa kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni, nti tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa n‑tiri Yɛhudiyadima maa ni baseeba ban daa dɔli ba soori la, ka laa n‑kpeeri daa puuni dabsa kam nti tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ban daa gatti bɛni zaa.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Lala zugu ka Pɔla dima daa kpaasi ba napɔntam m‑basi, n‑wulisi, ni, ba za'asiri ba ni; ka doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Aikoonyum tiŋŋa.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Karimbadima n daa bɛ bɛni, baseeba ban daa dɔli Ɛpikuruusi soori, ni seeba mun daa dɔli Sitoowa dima soori. Lala karimbadima ni Pɔla n daa mari nangbankpeemni, ka ninvugseeba daa boosiri n‑yɛli, Yɛttɔ'ayɔya daana wa yɛtti la wula?; baseeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, Asee u tɔ'asiri wumpaala yɛla. Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri Yisa yɛla, ni kumyisigri yɛla maa zugu ka ba daa yɛtti lala maa.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Ka ninvugseeba ban daa deaai lahabasuŋŋu maa ka niŋi yɛdda la daa mari sufupeelli, ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa bɛ ba saani.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Ba daa la'amni la Ariyɔpagusi kunkunni zugu nti tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu ba daa taasi Pɔla n‑du bɛni ka daa yɛli u, I ku kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi wulisigu paaliseaa nyin wulisiri wa?.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Ti na zi n‑wum yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin tɔ'asiri wa taabu, ka ti boori ni ti baŋŋi a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Ba daa yɛli lala, dama Ateensi tiŋŋa niriba ni ba saamma ban bɛ bɛni daa ka sufupeelli tuma di yi pa la ba gyinni n‑tɔ'asiri ka wumni lahabaseaa kan nyɛ bumpaalli.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa doaai n‑zaani kunkunni zugu la'aŋŋu dima maa tooni ka yɛli, Ya Ateensi dima, wumma! M pun gɔsi m‑baŋŋi, ni, ya dɔli la ya ba'a ni buga yɛla zaa, halli ni nimmoosi.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Dama man daa goori n‑gɔsiri ya bungyeemna, ka nya wumpuusigu bimbinseaa ka ba daa boaai n‑sɔbi ka yɛla ka saazugu, ni, Kanŋɔ nyɛ la wunsɔ dinni, amaa ti na zi u. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ya puusiri u ni ka na zi u, man mun moonni la u yɛla n‑tiri ya.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Naawunni n nyɛ u: u daa naam duniya wa, ni seelli kam zaa an bɛ ka ni. Ŋɔna n su saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu zaa. Ammi, lala Naawunni nin kpeeri la ninsaala ŋɔn mɛ gyeeŋŋu duri seesi n‑gbɛa?.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Bee, u kɔŋŋni bunseelli ka ninsaala nin mari ka na n‑ti u? Ay! di pa lala. Dama ŋɔn mammaŋŋa mari nyɔvuri ni alaafɛ ni bunseelli kam zaa n‑tisira.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: U daa kyɛ ka ninsaalidima butiri kam zaa daa yi ninvugyinni ni na, ni ba ti yɛligri m‑bɛ duniya wa luga zaa puuni; ka daa zaali ba yinni yinni ba saha yɛla, ka laa n‑zaali ba tinsi tɔrisi ŋɔn bɛ seela.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: U daa niŋi lala ni ba ti boori u yɛla ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka kyɛnni tooni beelabeela ni ba ti baŋŋi u - halli ni anŋɔ zaa, u siri bu wɔ'a ti sɔkam saani.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Dama ŋɔn zugu ka ti bɛ ni, ka kyɛnna, ka mari nyɔvuri. Ya timbiseeba daa bu yuumni lala? Ba daa yɛliya, ni#:- Ti gba nyɛ la u biisi.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Tɔ, ka ti yi siri nyɛ u biisi maa, ti di teesiri, ni, wummaŋni bɛ la hoo salima, bee anzulifa, bee kugveela, bee bunseelli kam tuma baŋŋniba ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑tumni la.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Sahanseaa din gaari la Naawunni daa pun di sugru zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninsaalidima tuma la ni, dama ba daa na zi u yɛla. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ u kpaamni la nira kam zaa, ban bɛ seelikam zaa maa, ni ba takkma ba teesibeeri ka kyɛ ba ala'akyi tuma.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Dama u na pun zaali dabsiri, dabsiseaa ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi u yɛlimaŋni maa n‑kari duniya zaa niriba sariya. Ka di nin dɔli dɔsɔ yɛligu - dɔsɔ ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn pun yiisi di zugu la. U daa pun wulisi ti zaa, ni, di siri nin niŋi, ka daa zaŋŋi di daama n‑kyɛ ka u neaai u kum nyaaŋŋa.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti wum Pɔla ŋɔn tɔ'asiri kum neaari yɛliseesi la, baseeba daa maali u la ansarisi, amaa baseeba mun daa yɛliya, Ti boori la i laa n‑tɔ'asi lahabaari wa yɛla n‑ti ti yaasa.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa bakki n‑yi la'aŋŋu maa ni.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Ka ninvugseeba daa paasi u zugu ka niŋi yɛligu maa yɛdda. Diyɔnisusi n daa nyɛ ba yinni, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ ba tinkpamma maa ni yinni, ni pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Damɛrisa, ka ninvugseeba daa paasi ba zugu yaasa.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: M Badima, ya wumma! M mabiisi, ya wumma! N ya tɔ'asi n‑wulisi n yɛla yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Tɔ, ka ban daa ti wum ka u tɔ'asiri ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a n‑tiri ba maa, ba daa kyɛ ba vuuri ka fɔ surimm. Ka Pɔla daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba, ni.
