eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: N nyɛ la Yɛhudiyadoowa, ka ba daa dɔ'ai ma Taasusi, Silisii tingbaŋŋu ni; amaa Gyɛrusalɛm wa ka ba daa mari ma ka n zoaai na. Kpa wa ka n karimba Gameeliyɛli daa wulisi ma; n daa bamsi ti yaadima yɛla zaa ni nimbirimoaa, ka n daa zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni hoo yarim ban la'am la kpa zuna wa ŋɔn zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑tiri u seem la.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Man daa namsiri la ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori wa, halli nti ku baseeba. N daa gbaasi ba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, n‑zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi dansarika duu ni.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Ya yi ti boosi ya Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa, ni kpamma la'aŋŋu dima zaa, ba nin sakki, ni, n daa niŋi lala. Halli ka ba mammansi daa sɔbi gbana n‑ti ma, ni, m mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑tisi ti mabiseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa la, ni, n gbaam Yisa niriba maa n‑zi n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na, ka ba darigi ba tubri.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Ni, Man ŋɔn daa ti wa bɛni maa ka daa ti pɔtti tiŋŋa maa ni, wuntaŋŋa agbaa saha, neesim pam daa nyakki n‑yi saazugu na n‑gyiligi ma.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: N daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, ni, Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔ zugu ka i namsiri ma.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Ka n daa boosi n‑yɛli, Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?, ka u labsi n‑yɛli ma, ni, Mani Yisa, Nazarɛti nira la: mani ka i namsiri maa.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ma maa daa nya neesim maa, amaa ba daa bu wum kukɔri maa ŋɔn tɔ'asi ma seem.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Ka n daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, Ka dinŋɔna, n Dugma, n niŋma wula?; ka u yɛli, Doaam saazugu n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi. Ba nin ti yɛli i man pun gbaai, ni, i niŋi seem.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: N daa zoomiya, neesim ŋɔn daa neaai pampam la zugu, ka n nyaandɔliba maa daa dari ma n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Di saha ka Ananayasi daa kyɛ n saani na - bɛni dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa dɔli Naawunni zaaligu ni nimmoaa nla, ka tiŋŋa maa Yɛhudiyadima zaa daa tiri u gyirima.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: U daa kpɛ na n‑zaani n‑tabli ma, ka yɛli, M mabii Sɔla, neaam i nini n‑nyara! Di saha maa n daa neaai n nini n‑wukki n‑gɔsi u.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Di saha ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, Naawunni, sɔ ti yaadima maa ŋɔn daa gyeemni la - ŋɔn Naawunni n yiisi i, ni i ti baŋŋi ŋɔn boori seem, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u Yɛlimaŋni Daana n‑wulisi i, ka kyɛ ka i wum yɛttɔ'a u mammaŋŋa noori ni.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: U daa niŋi lala, ni i ti niŋi sɛhara daana n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri sɔkam zaa nyin nya ka wum bunseesi zaa yɛla.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Dinŋɔna bɔ zugu ka i laa n‑guusira? Doaam saazugu nti su Naawunni koom: boolim u yuuri ka u peaai i tumbeeri da'ari zaa m‑basi. .

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa n daa labi la Gyɛrusalɛm na, nti kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni n ti puusi Naawunni. Ka man daa puusiri sahanseaa maa, di daa niŋi ma hoo man zaasiri ni;.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: ka n daa nya, ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, ni, Doaam yooma n‑yi Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa wa ni, dama kpa wa niriba ku sakki n‑niŋi i yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n yɛla maa yɛdda.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli u, N Dugma, ba mi halli, ni, n daa kpeeri la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑gbaasiri ninvugseeba ban niŋi i yɛdda maa, ka kpatti ba duu ni ka buuri ba.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: M‑paasi, ba mi, ni, ban daa ku i sɛharadaana Sitiivin sahanseaa la, ni, n daa bɛ bɛni n‑kpamsiri ba ka guri ba neema.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Amaa u daa yɛli ma, ni, Kur kyɛma! Dama n nin tum i, halli ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa saani. .

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Tɔ; ka zama maa daa kur wumsiri la Pɔla noori, halli ka u ti yɛli lala maa; amaa di saha ka ba daa gbaai tasibu n‑yɛtti, ni, Ya mari u n‑kyaŋŋi! N‑ku u gba! Lala nira bu sumsi ni duniya wa beebu.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Ba daa damni la ba mansi, n‑gba'atiri ba neema, ka yeenni tanni.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Ka dizugu langyima kpeema maa daa yɛli u niriba, ni, ba mari Pɔla n‑kpɛ ba Baarikyi yiri nti boosi u boosa ka feebi u, ni ba ti baŋŋi daliiriseaa din kyɛ ka Yɛhudiyadima maa zeesiri ba mansi n‑niŋi u lala.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Amaa ban daa loori u feebgu zii maa ni sahanseaa la, u daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba toondaansɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ bɛni maa, ni, Ammi, ya nin zaŋŋi Room suulinsi nira n‑feebi? Halli ka bu kari u yɛttɔ'a maa sariya m‑baŋŋi u taalli - di mari soori.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Ka toondaana maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, u daa kyaŋŋi langyima kpeema maa saani nti boosi u, ni, Bɔ ka i ya n‑niŋi? Doo maa nyɛ la Room suulinsi nira.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Ka kpeema maa daa kyaŋŋi Pɔla saani nti boosi u n‑yɛli, Room suulinsi nira n siri nyɛ i?; ka u labsi n‑yɛli u ni, Ii.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Ka kpeema maa yɛli u, Man daa yɔ la ligri pam ka naayi deaai lala suulinsi beebu maa.; ka Pɔla labsi n‑yɛli u, ni Man muna - ba daa dɔ'ai ma la lala suulinsi ni.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Dinzugu langyinseeba ban ya boosi u boosa la daa labi ba nyaaŋŋa, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba kpeema maa, dama u daa pun lɔ doo maa, ka naayi baŋŋi, ni, Room suulinsi nira n nyɛ u.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Tɔ; ka langyima kpeema maa daa kpalim m‑boori ni u baŋŋi yɛttɔ'a maa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni wula zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima daa ya galimni u. Ka dizugu beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai u daa yiisi Pɔla na, ka zaali noori, ni, gyeeŋŋu toondaandima la'asima ni kpamma zaa, ka kyɛ ka Pɔla kpɛ na n‑zaani ba tooni.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Pɔla ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u nini n‑kpa kpamma la'aŋŋu dima maa, u daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli, M mabiisi, n nyɔvuri puuni halli ni zuna, m beesigu nyɛ la bunkasi Naawunni tooni.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana Ananayasi daa yɛli ban zɛya la, ni, ba ŋmeem u noori u yɛlisim maa zugu.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli Naawunni nin ti ŋmɛ i gba! I ŋmani la hoo goonkuriseaa ban zaŋŋi titaari n‑ta, ka ka gɔsibu veela, amaa ka puuni bu zaani suŋŋa. I zinni n‑katti ma sariya Naawunni zaaligu zugu, ka i mammaŋŋa sa'amni zaaligu maa ka yɛtti, ni, ba ŋmeem ma.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Ka ban daa zɛya la daa yɛli u, ni, Ammi, i tuuri la Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa lala.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, M mabiisi, ya gaafara! N da bu mi, ni, Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana n nyɛ u. Ba daa bu sɔbi lala, Naawunni yɛligu maa ni? Ni, I di yɛli yɛttɔ'abeoo n‑tiri ŋɔn su i butiri fukumsi. .

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa baŋŋiya, ni, kpamma maa purigri daa dɔli la Saddusii soori, ka seeba mun dɔli Farisii dinni. Ka dizugu u daa tasi pampam la'aŋŋu maa puuni n‑yɛli, M mabiisi, ya wumma! N nyɛ la Farisii doo, ka m badima gba daa dɔli Farisii soori maa. Yɛttɔ'aseaa tin la'amni na ni ti tɔ'asi nyɛ la yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man mari tama seelli kum yisigri ni.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa, Farisii dima ni Saddusidima maa nɔya daa wɔliya, ka la'aŋŋu maa purigi buyi.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Nangbankpeemni wa gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: Saddusidima daa yɛtti, ni, ninvugseeba ban kpi la ku laa n‑yisigi, bee, n‑neaai kum ni; ni siisi ni malaakadima yɛla pa la yɛlimaŋni: Farisii dima mun daa sakkri n‑tiri kum yisigri, ni siisi ni malaakadima yɛla zaa.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Ka dinzugu vuuri pam daa doaaya, ka seeba ban daa nyɛ zaaligu karimbadima ka bɛ la Farisii dima ya'ari la daa yi paalu na n‑yɛtti, ni, Ti bu nya doo wa taaliseaa; di bu ya'ai malaaka bee siiya n tɔ'asi n‑ti u maa.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Sugyeaa maa daa paasiri ni, ka dabeem daa gbaai langyima kpeema maa, ni, ba nin bu Pɔla n‑toosi u; ka dizugu u daa yɛli u langyimadima maa, ni, ba kpeem na n‑gbaai Pɔla n‑yiisi n‑yi daadam maa sunsukkuni, ka mari u n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri ni.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Tɔ; ka dirindaari maa, yuŋŋu, ka ti Dugma daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi Pɔla, ka yɛli, Mari i dablim maa! Nyin pun tɔ'asi n yɛla suŋŋa Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni seem la, lala ka i nin laa n‑tɔ'asi Room timpuuni gba.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Yɛhudiyaseeba daa la'am taaba m‑pɔ noori, ni, ba ku di diibu, bee n‑nyu seelli, na'ala ba ti ku Pɔla n‑naai.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Ban daa la'am m‑pɔ lala noori maa daa gaari niriba pisinaasi.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Tɔ; ka dizugu ba daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ni kpamma maa saani nti yɛli, Tirima la'am taaba m‑pɔ noori, ni, ti ku laa n‑lam diibu, na'ala ti ku la Pɔla.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna see ka yarima ni kpamma maa zaa ti susi langyima kpeema maa, ni, ya ya maali n‑viisi doo maa yɛttɔ'a maa suŋŋa, ka dizugu ba mari u n‑kyɛ ya saani na. Tirima muna nin maali siri ni ti ku u pɔi ka u ti paai kpa na.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Amaa Pɔla tinzɔpɔ'a bisɔ daa wum ban gbaari seem maa, ka daa kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri maa ni, nti kpɛ n‑yɛli Pɔla.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Ka di saha Pɔla daa booli langyima toondaansɔ na nti yɛli u, ni, u zaŋŋma bii wa n‑kpɛ kpeema maa saani, ni, u mari la lahabaseaa ni u ti u.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Ka u daa mari u n‑kpɛ kpeema maa saani n‑yɛli, Pɔla sɔ ban kpari dansarika duu ni la m booli ma n‑yɛli, ni, m mari na'akyimbila wa n‑kyɛ i saani na, ni, u mari la bunseelli ni u yɛli i.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Ka langyima kpeema maa daa gbaai bii maa nuu n‑kyɛ ka ba labi kpaŋŋa sa, ka boosi u, ni, Bumbɔ ka i mari ni i yɛli ma.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Ka u daa yɛli, Yɛhudiyadima maa gbaari la gbaari, ni, ba yɛlim i, ni, i sa nin zaŋŋi Pɔla beoo asuba m‑mari u n‑kyaŋŋi ba kpamma la'aŋŋu maa ni, ni, ba ya maali n‑viisi la u yɛttɔ'a maa suŋŋa.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Di wum n‑ti ba! Dama baseeba nin ti gu u soori zugu - ba gaari ninvugpisinaasi. Ba sa pɔ la noori, ni, ba ku ti di diibu, bee n‑nyu seelli, na'ala ba ku la Pɔla n‑naai. Ba pun sɛ'asi, ka kur guusiri la din ya yi i saani na.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Ka kpeema maa daa kpamsi u, ni, u di kyɛ ka sɔ baŋŋi, ni, u pun yɛli u lala lahabaari maa, ka basi u ka u bakki.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka langyima kpeema maa daa booli u toondaandima ayi na nti ti zaali ba noori, ni, Ya sɛ'asim langyima kɔbsiyi, ni wuribaariba pisiyɔpɔi, ni kpana dima kɔbsiyi m‑paasi, ni ba maali siri n‑kyaŋŋi Sizariiya zuna yuŋŋu wa, sahanseaa din yi ti zibi suŋŋa.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Ka bɔ bumbaarigu n‑ti Pɔla gba, ka u ba, ka ti taasi u Gɔmna Filiksi saani ni alaafɛa.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Ka u daa sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti Gɔmna maa, ni:.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Man Klɔdiyusi Lisiyasi n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa m‑puusiri m Ba Gɔmna Filiksi, sɔ yuuri ŋɔn du tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Doo wa ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai ka daa boori ni ba ku u; amaa n daa baŋŋiya, ni, Room suulinsi nira n nyɛ u, ka daa zaŋŋi n langyinseeba n‑fa u ba saani.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: N daa boori ni m baŋŋi ban gbaai u seelli zugu, ka n daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi ba kpamma la'aŋŋu ni;.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: ka n ti baŋŋi, ni, galimni maa nyɛ la seelli din yiiri ba zaaligu yɛla ni na, amaa ba daa bu wulisi u kum taalli ŋɔn nyɛ seem, halli dansarika taalli gba.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka n daa wum lahabaari, ni, ba ya sɔ'ai n‑niŋi la doo maa yɛttɔ'abeoo, ka dizugu n kyɛ ka ba taasi u i saani na. Ban galimni u maa muna - n yɛli ba, ni, ba mari ba yɛttɔ'a maa m‑paai i saani na.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Tɔ; ka langyimadima maa daa tum hoo ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa, ka mari Pɔla n‑kyaŋŋi yuŋŋu maa, halli nti paai Antipatirisi.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa kaai maa, ban daa kyɛnni nɔba la daa ŋmaligi n‑labi ba Baarikyi ni, ka kyɛ ka wuribaariba maa mari Pɔla, halli nti paai ba kyɛnni maa tɔriga.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Ba daa paai la Sizariiya n‑zaŋŋi gbaŋŋu maa n‑ti Gɔmna maa, ka daa zaŋŋi Pɔla gba n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Tɔ, ka Gɔmna maa daa karim gbaŋŋu maa, ka boosi Pɔla ŋɔn yi tingbanseaa na, ka baŋŋi, ni, Silisiiya;.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: ka u yɛli u, ni, Ban n galimni i maa yi ti paai na, n nin wum i yɛttɔ'a maa. Ka u daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi u n‑kpeesi NaaHɛroodi naam yiri kurigu ni duseaa n‑guri u.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Dabsa anu nyaaŋŋa ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana Ananayasi daa paai na, ni kpamseeba m‑paasi u zugu, n‑taasi Tɛritulusi ba yɛttɔ'atɔ'asira na; ka ba daa wulisi Gɔmna n‑yɛli ban galimni Pɔla seem.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Ka ba daa booli Pɔla na, ka Tɛritulusi daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a ni u ti galimni u, ni, Ti Ba gyirimadaana, Filiksi, wumma! Di nyɛ la i yiiko ni ka ti mari sufudoaa; ka di laa n‑nyɛ la i yuubu ni ka ti tingbaŋŋu wa maali, ka takki n‑nyɛ bunsuŋŋu.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Seelikam zaa, ni ya'ari kam zaa, ti deaari i lala suŋŋsim maa; ka dizugu ti puusiri i la wurilim puusigu.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Tɔ, ka ti bu boori ni ti sa'am i saha yaasa, amaa ti susiri i ni, i sufubaalim zugu, ni, i wum ti yɛlli beela.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Dama ti nya ninvugkyɔ'aŋŋu wa, ka u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn daamni Yɛhudiyadima maa duniya wa ya'ari kam zaa, ka ba zabra; ka u nyɛ la toondaana n‑ti Nazarɛti wɔligri soori dima wa.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Halli u daa ya sa'am Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa n‑kyɛ ka ka da'ai zaa, ka dizugu ti daa gbaai u. Ti daa yɛtti, ni, ti nin dɔli ti soori n‑kari u sariya;.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Di saha ka ŋɔn Lisiyasi daa yɛli, ni, baseeba ban ya galim u, see ka ba kyɛ i saani na. Tɔ, ka i yi ti viisi m‑boosi u lala boosa, i mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑wum u saani bunseesi zaa tin galimni u a zugu maa.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa zaa daa la'am n‑galimni u m‑paasi, n‑yɛtti, ni, anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna maa ŋɔn daa wulisi u, ni, u tɔ'asima maa, Pɔla daa yɛliya, M mi halli, ni, i pun katti sariya tingbaŋŋu wa puuni n‑yuui; ka dizugu n sufu maaya pam dinŋɔ zugu, ni, di nyɛ la nyini tooni ka n ya tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a wa.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: I nin toaai m‑boosi m‑baŋŋi i mammaŋŋa, ni, di bu gaari dapiiya ni ayi man daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na ni n ti gyeem Naawunni.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Ba daa bu nya ka man ni sɔ ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ba mun daa bu laa n‑nya ka n damni daadam maa, la'aŋŋu yiya ni bee timpuuni.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Ba mun ku toaai n‑wulisi i, ni, yɛlimaŋni bɛ ban yɛtti n‑galimni ma bunseesi wa ni, halli beela.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Kulla dinŋɔ ka n sakki n‑ti i, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: n gyeemni la ti yaadima Naawunni n‑dɔli ban boonni sɔseaa, ni, wɔligri soori la. Amaa lala soori dɔligu puuni ka n laa n‑niŋni yɛdda n‑niŋi din bɛ Naawunni zaaligu puuni zaa, ni anabidima ŋɔn daa sɔbi seem zaa m‑paasi;.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: ka laa m‑mari tama Naawunni zugu, ni, ninvugsuma ni ninvugbeeri zaa nin ti doaai kum ni, ka lala tama ka ninvugseeba ban galimni ma la gba mara.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Lala zugu ka n kur moori saha kam ni nimbirimoaa, ni n sufu ni teesa ku diisiri ma galinseelli zaa vi, zaŋŋi n‑kpa Naawunni, ni ninsaalidima ni.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa ka yuunseesi daa gaari, ka pampaŋŋɔ n daa kyɛ na ni n ti tisi piini n‑tiri faradima m butiri taaba sunsukkuni, ni n Naawunni piini m‑paasi.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Man daa niŋni lala maa zugu, n daa sɛ'asi m maŋŋa kasikasi n‑kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, ka ba daa nya ma bɛni. Di saha ka zama daa bu dɔli ma, man mun daa bu daamni sɔa.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyaseeba ban daa yi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni na n daa bɛ ni - halli ba yi daa nya taaliseaa n saani, di sumni ni ban da bɛ la kpa wa n‑galimni ma.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban zɛya wa, see ka ba yɛli ban daa nya n taaliseaa, man daa zɛ ba la'aŋŋu maa ni sahanseaa la.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Ay! Taalli ka ni, di yi pa la yɛttɔ'a yinni man daa zɛ ba tooni n‑kaasi n‑yɛli seaa la, ni, Yɛttɔ'aseaa tin tɔ'asiri kpa wa zuna, nyɛ la yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri yɛla. .

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna Filiksi daa nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn mi Yisa soori yɛla an bɛ seem maa deede; ka dizugu di saha maa u daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a maa n‑zaali dabsiri. Ni, Langyima kpeema Lisiyasi yi ti paai na sahanseaa, n nin kari ya sariya maa.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: U daa yɛli langyima toondaana maa, ni, ba zaŋŋma Pɔla n‑kpari, amaa ba kyɛ ka u nya paalu beela, ka ba di za'asi u zɔdima kpeebu na ni ba ti suŋŋi u.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Di daa niŋi dabsa beela ka Gɔmna Filiksi daa kyɛ na, ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a Diruusila, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyapɔ'a la; ka u daa tum m‑booli Pɔla na, ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa Masiiya yɛdda niŋbu.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Amaa ŋɔn daa paai u kpaamni yɛla zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugmaŋni tunsuma ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ni mansɛ'asigu yɛla, ni Naawunni ŋɔn ya kari niriba sariya la, di saha ka dabeem daa gbaai Filiksi ka u daa yɛli, Kyɛla ka kyaŋŋi pampaŋŋɔ! N yi ti nya dahama n nin booli i na yaasa.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: U daa laa n‑teesi m‑paasi, ni, Pɔla ya n‑ti u la ligri, ka dizugu u daa kur boonni u na sahakam, ni ba diri azama.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Amaa yuuma ayi ŋɔn daa gaari, Pɔrikyuusi Fɛsitusi daa deaai Filiksi; ka Filiksi mun daa boori ni u maai Yɛhudiyadima maa sufu, ka dizugu u daa kyɛ ka ba kpalim n‑kpatti Pɔla.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Tɔ; ka Fɛsitusi ŋɔn daa ti di Gɔmna fukumsi maa sahanseaa la, u daa niŋi dabsa ata Sizariiya timpuuni pɔi, ka naayi yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Ka Yɛhudiyadima gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni ba kpamma maa daa paai u na nti galimni Pɔla.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: ka susiri Gɔmna maa, ni, u kpaŋŋma u maŋŋa n‑suŋŋi ba yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ka kyɛ ka ba taasi Pɔla Gyɛrusalɛm na. Ba daa susi u lala dama ba daa boori ni ba sɔ'ai n‑guri soori ka yi ya'abi na n‑ku u.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Amaa Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Sizarii tiŋŋa ka ba daa pun guri Pɔla, halli ni u mammaŋŋa ya n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni ka u kyɛnni saha bu ya yuui.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Ni, Ka dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ya zuguraanseeba doaai n‑dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni, ka taaliseaa yi bɛ doo maa saani, ya nin toaai n‑galim u n‑wulisi lala.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna maa daa kpalim m‑bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm n‑niŋi daba anii-awai ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi Sizariiya. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa zi kootu ni ka kyɛ ka ba mari Pɔla na.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ na maa, Gyɛrusalɛm dima daa kyɛ na n‑zaani n‑gyiligi u n‑galimni u yɛttɔ'abeeri ni taalitibsa yɛla; amaa ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑wulisi anŋɔ zaa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Ka Pɔla mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, ni, M bu tum taaliseaa n‑galim Yɛhudiyadima zaaligu, bee ba Gyeeŋŋu Yiri, yɛttɔ'aseelli ni; halli n‑galim Room NaaTitaari yɛla gba.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Amaa Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa boori ni u suŋŋi Yɛhudiyadima maa m‑maai ba sufu; ka dizugu u daa yɛli Pɔla, ni, Ammi, i nin sakki, ni, i kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm, ka n nin tɔ'asi i yɛttɔ'a wa bɛni.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli u, N zɛ la NaaTitaari kootu ni, ka bɛni ka di sumni ni ba kari n sariya. I mi halli, ni, n na zi n‑tum taaliseaa n‑galim Yɛhudiyadima maa.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Halli n yi da tum tumbɛseaa kan sumsi ni kum, n ku za'asi, ni, ba ku ma dizugu. Amaa ba yi galimni ma yɔriyɔri n ku sakki, ni, sɔ zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ba nuu ni. Dizugu n susi, ni, i zaŋŋi n yɛttɔ'a wa n‑zaali n‑ti NaaTitaari saani.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Di saha ka Fɛsitusi ni u kpamma maa daa ŋmɛ saawara; ka naayi sakki, ni, I susiya, ni, ti zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑zaali n‑ti NaaTitaari. Tɔ; NaaTitaari saani ka i siri nin kyaŋŋi.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Di daa niŋi dahinseesi ka NaaAgrippa ni u pɔ'a Bɛriniisi daa ti paai Sizarii na, ni ba ti puusi Gɔmna Fɛsitusi.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Ba daa yuui bɛni beela, ka Fɛsitusi daa zaŋŋi Pɔla yɛla n‑wulisi n‑ti Naaba maa, ni, Gɔmna Filiksi daa kyɛ ka dɔsɔ kpalim la dansarika duu ni;.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: ka man daa kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm la, gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpamma maa daa kyɛ n saani na ni ba ti tɔ'asi lala doo maa yɛla, ni, n kpaŋŋma m maŋŋa n‑tɔ'asi u yɛttɔ'a n‑lusi u n‑ti ba.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Man mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, ni, di pa la ti Room dima kaari ni ti lusi sɔa, see ka ti daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka ŋɔn ni ban galimni u la ti kpɛ na n‑tɔ'asi taaba yɛttɔ'a maa yɛla, ka u mari kukɔri n‑kyiisiri ba galiŋŋu maa.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Tɔ, ka ba daa dɔli ma n‑kyɛ kpa na; man mun n daa bu zaali yɛttɔ'a maa dabsiri, amaa beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, n daa zini kootu ni, n‑kyɛ ka ba mari doo maa na.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Ka baseeba ban daa galimni u yɛla maa ŋɔn daa ti zaani ni ba ti tɔ'asi, ba daa bu wulisi tumbɛseesi man daa teesi, ni, ba naa m‑mari n‑zaali u.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Amaa di daa nyɛ la nangbankpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba maŋtoori wungyeeŋŋu yɛla, ni dɔsɔ ŋɔn boonni Yisa la, ni, u kpiya; Pɔla mun yɛli, ni, u na bɛ la u nyɔvuri ni.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Dizugu n daa bu baŋŋi man ya n‑gbaai yɛttɔ'a maa seem, ka daa boosi u, ni, u nin sakki, ni, ti kyɛm Gyɛrusalɛm n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa, bee?.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Amaa u mun daa susiya, ni, ti zaali yɛttɔ'a maa, halli ni u ti kyaŋŋi ti Ba NaaTitaari saani n‑kari sariya maa. Ka dizugu n daa kyɛ ka ba na guri u dansarika duu ni, see ka n nya soori ni ti zaŋŋi u n‑taasi ti Dugma Naaba saani.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Ka NaaAgrippa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, M boori ni n wum doo maa yɛttɔ'a m mammaŋŋa. Ka Fɛsitusi daa yɛli u, ni, Beoo i sa nin wum u yɛla.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, NaaAgrippa ni Bɛriniisi daa yi na ni ba naam gyirima pam, ka kyɛ na nti kpɛ kootu dutitaari maa ni, ban ni soogyadima kpamma maa ni tiŋŋa maa ninvugtibsa zaa. Ka Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi Pɔla m‑mari u n‑kpɛ na.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Di saha ka Gɔmna maa daa yɛli, M Ba NaaAgrippa, wumma! Zama, ya wumma! Ya gɔsim doo wa! Ŋɔnŋɔ yɛla zugu ka Yɛhudiya sɔkam zaa daa kyɛ n saani na Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka laa n‑kyɛ kpa na yaasa, ka tasiri pampam, ni, di bu laa n‑sumsi ni doo wa kpalim u nyɔvuri ni.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Man mun daa viisi u yɛla, ka daa bu nya tumbɛseaa u saani kan sumsi kum. Amaa u daa susi yɛttɔ'a zaaligu n‑ti ti Ba NaaTitaari, ka dizugu n daa gbaai u yɛla lala, ni, n nin tum u Naaba saani.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Amaa n na ka bunseelli ka n nin toaai n‑sɔbi u yɛttɔ'a maa yɛla yɛlimaŋni maa deede n‑tɔlim n‑ti Naaba. Ka dizugu, NaaAgrippa, n kyɛ ka u kyɛ i tooni na, ni yarima zaa tooni, ni ti ti viisi u yɛla; nti nya yɛttɔ'aseaa man nin ti sɔbi n‑tɔlim.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Dama di bu dɔli la soori ni n tum yɛttɔ'adaansɔ Naaba saani ka na ka yɛttɔ'aseaa, ni n wulisi Naaba din kyɛ ka ba galimni u.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Di saha ka NaaAgrippa daa yɛli Pɔla, ni, Tɔ, i mari kukɔri ni i ti wulisi ti i yɛla ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Pɔla mun daa zaŋŋi u nuu m‑bugsi n‑ti ba, ka gbaai u yɛttɔ'a.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Ni, M Ba NaaAgrippa, n yɛtti, ni, m mari zugsuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n zuna yɛttɔ'a wa yɛla. Dama di nyɛ la nyini tooni ka m mari la kukɔri ni n gyɛrigri n‑kyiisiri ban galimni ma yɛla zaa.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: M mi halli, ni, i baŋŋi ti Yɛhudiyadima kaya ni ti nangbankpeemni yɛla zaa. Dizugu n susiri i, ni, i kur di sugru n‑wum n yɛttɔ'a wa.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ; ka Yɛhudiyadima zaa mi m beebu yɛla ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem, ni, n na'akyimni saha halli ni zuna n daa bɛ la m maŋtoori timbiisi saani pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na nti bɛ bɛni.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Ba daa mi n yɛla ka di yuui: ba yi sakki n‑yɛli yɛlimaŋni, ba nin yɛli, ni, n daa dɔli la ti zaaligu nimmoosi soori maa, di nyɛ la Farisii dima dinni.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Ka pampaŋŋɔ ba zaŋŋi ma n‑zaali kpa wa la ti yaadima tama maa yɛla zugu, Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti noori, ni, u nin ti ba maa; ka dinŋɔ zugu ka ba galimni ma, walla!.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Lala yɛla maa ka ti za'anɔya pii ni ayi niriba zaa guri ni tama ni nimmoaa, ni ba ti nya lala yɛla maa. Ka dizugu ba kur gyeemni la Naawunni, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa. Naaba wumma - di nyɛ la lala Naawunni noori yɛligu maa zugu ka ba galimni ma, halli ni ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima gba!.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Bɔzugu ka ya ku nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni siri neaari la niriba kum ni?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Tɔ; ka dizugu man gba daa teesi, ni, di sumni ni n kpaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑zabi ban dɔli Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, yɛdda soori maa.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Ka n daa niŋi lala n‑namsiri ba Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni: n daa gbaasi Naawunni niriba pam n‑kpari duu ni, dama gyeeŋŋu toondaandima daa ti ma soori, ni, n tum lala. Halli ban daa ku ba sahanseaa la, n daa bu yiisi n nuu ba yɛla maa ni.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Yinyaari daa kpɛ ma lala, halli ka la'aŋŋu duri zaa puuni ka n daa darigri ba tuba ka mugsiri ba, dama n daa boori ni ba ti za'asi Yisa yɛla; halli n daa kyaŋŋi tinseesi gba m‑paasi, ni n ti namsiri ba.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Ka lala zugu n daa deaai gyeeŋŋu toondaandima yiiko ka yi soori ni n kyaŋŋi Damasikusi tiŋŋa.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Ka di daa paai wuntaŋŋa agbaa saha, ka neesim pam daa nyakki n‑yi saazugu ni na nti gyiligi man ni ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ma la zaa. Lala neesim maa daa gaari wuntaŋŋa neesim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Ti zaa daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka n wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni, Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔzugu ka i namsiri ma? I ŋmani la na'asɔ ŋɔn koori ka za'asira, ka ba feebri u.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Ka n daa boosi n‑yɛli, Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?, ka u labsi n‑yɛli ma, ni, Mani Yisa: mani ka i namsiri maa.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Amaa doaam n‑zaani saazugu! Man wulisiri la m mammaŋŋa n‑tiri i, ni n zaŋŋi i n‑leebi n tuntumna. I nin ti yɛli niriba nyin nya yɛttɔ'aseesi zuna wa, ka laa n‑yɛli ba lala bunseesi nyin nin nya yaasa.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: N nin fa i m‑basi Yɛhudiyadima nuu ni, ni ban pa Yɛhudiyadima nuu ni gba, dama ban saani ka n ya tum i.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: I nin kyɛ ka ba nini neaai; i nin kyɛ ka ba ŋmaligi ka kyɛ zibsim maa, n‑kyɛ neesim maa ni na; i nin kyɛ ka ba ŋmaligi n‑yi Sintaani fukumsi ni m‑paai Naawunni saani na, ka ba nin ti niŋi ma yɛdda halli nti nya ba taalli zaa kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi, ka la'am Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Tɔ, NaaAgrippa, kur wumma! Di zugu n daa bu za'asi Naawunni ŋɔn daa wulisi ma zii yɛla maa.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Ay! - n daa daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Damasikusi m‑mooli n‑ti ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba teesibeeri ni ba tumbeeri yɛla m‑basi ka ŋmaligi n‑kyɛ Naawunni saani na ka tumni lala tunsuma maa. Ka di nyaaŋŋa n daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na m‑moonni lala, ni Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu maa ni m‑paasi, ka naayi kyaŋŋi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa tinsi gba n‑wulisiri ba yɛttɔ'a wa yɛla.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Ka anŋɔ zaa zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai ma Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni n‑yɛli, ni, ba ku ma.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti suŋŋi ma zugu, n na bɛ ni halli ni zuna; ka n zɛya n‑wulisiri niriba wa zaasa, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa; m bu yɛtti sɛseela na'ala anabidima ŋɔn daa pun yɛli, ni, di nin niŋi seem, ni Musa gba daa yɛli lala.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: Di nyɛ la: ni, Naawunni ŋɔn diisi sɔ Masiiya la nin ti di wahala; ni din paasi lala, ni, u nin leebi ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni n‑yisigi kum ni, ka dizugu u nin ti kyɛ ka neesim neaai n‑tiri u maŋtoori niriba, ni ninvugseeba ban paasi ba zugu maa zaa.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Tɔ; ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi u yɛla lala n‑naai, Fɛsitusi daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, N zɔ Pɔla, i zugu sa'amiya! I kariŋŋu pam ni i bamsim zugu n kyɛ ka yinyaari kpɛ i.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Ay, n zugu bu sa'am. I bu nya, m Ba Fɛsitusi, ni, n tɔ'asiri hoo ninvugmaŋni ŋɔn tɔ'asiri seem, n‑wulisiri yɛlimaŋni yɛla?.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: N tɔ'asiri yɛla maa zaa ka bu sɔ'ari seelli, man tɔ'asiri Naa maa tooni zugu; ka di pa la u na zi n‑wum anŋɔ zaa lahabaari. U mi man tɔ'asiri yɛliseesi maa gbinni suŋŋa, dama ba daa bu sɔ'ai n‑tum anŋɔ zaa.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: M Ba NaaAgrippa, i niŋi anabidima yɛla maa yɛdda? - Ii! M mi halli, ni, i niŋiya.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Ka Naa maa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, Dinŋɔna, i teesiya, ni, di ku yuui ka i kyɛ ka n leebi Kirisitadoowa, bee.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, Di yi yuui, bee, di yi bu yuui, n siri boori ni nyini, ni seeba zaa ban wumni n yɛttɔ'a zuna wa m‑paasi i zugu, nin ti leebi hoo man ŋɔn bɛ seem la - amaa pa la ban lɔ ma kyɔrimi wa taabu.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Tɔ; ka di saha Naaba daa yisigi n‑zaani saazugu, ŋɔn ni Gɔmna ni Bɛriniisi ni ba taaba ban zintiri bimbinni zugu la zaa.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Ka ban daa doaai n‑kyɛnni maa, ba daa sɔsiri n‑yɛtti taaba, ni, Doo wa bu tum sɛseelli din sumni kum, halli din sum ni dansarika duu kparibu gba.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Ka NaaAgrippa yɛli Fɛsitusi, ni, U yi da bu zaali u yɛttɔ'a NaaTitaari saani, u naa n‑toaai n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi yɔri.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti gbaai ti Room kyɛnni yɛla maa, ni, ti kpeem nyariŋŋu n‑dɔli teeku koom n‑kyaŋŋi, ba daa zaŋŋi Pɔla ni ban daa bɛ dansarika duu ni m‑paasi u zugu la, n‑niŋi langyima toondaansɔ nuu ni. Toondaana maa yuuri daa boonni Gyuliyusi: u daa su la langyinseeba fukumsi ban daa boonni NaaAgasitisi Dima la.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Ka ti daa nya nyarintitaseaa kan yi Addiramitiyum tiŋŋa ni na, ka ka ya kyaŋŋi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ya'ari sa. Ti daa kpɛ ka ni, tirim ni Arisitaakusi, ŋɔn daa yi Tɛsalɔnika ni na, kan nyɛ Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu ni tinseaa.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa paai Saidoon tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka Gyuliyusi daa ti Pɔla soori ni u sigi n‑kyaŋŋi nti kaai u zɔdima, ni ba gɔsi u yɛla; dama Gyuliyusi daa nyɛ la nirilim daana.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Tin daa yi bɛni sahanseaa maa, ti daa du m‑burigi n‑gaari Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu nyaaŋŋa sa, dama pɛsiŋŋu daa bu zeeri suŋŋa n‑ti ti.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Ka ti daa kyaŋŋi tooni n‑yakki teeku ziseaa kan bɛ Silisii ni Pamfilii tingbana ya'ari maa, halli nti paai Maayira tiŋŋa kan zɛ Lisii tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Bɛni ka toondaana maa daa nya nyarinseaa kan yi Alɛkzandiriya tiŋŋa ni na, ni ka ti kyaŋŋi Italii tingbaŋŋu ni, ka daa kyɛ ka ti kpɛ kana.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Ti daa du n‑kyaŋŋi; ka di daa niŋi lala, ka ti kyɛnni daa mukki ti pam, ka ti kyɛnni beelabeela ka naayi paai Kunidoosi tiŋŋa. Di saha ka pɛsiŋŋu daa tuusi ti, ka dizugu ti daa ŋmaligi n‑yakki nti dɔli Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu ni. N‑kyɛnni n‑kyɛ ka tiŋŋa wa bɛ ti nudirigu ya'ari sa, n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gaari Salimooni tiŋŋa;.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: n‑dɔli beelabeela ka naayi paai ya'aseaa ban boonni Nyarima Ya'aveelli la - bɛni ni Lasaya tiŋŋa daa bu wɔ'a.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Ti daa yuui beela bɛni, ka saha daa bu laa n‑veeli ni ba kyɛnni teeku maa ni, dama Yɛhudiyadima nɔlɔri saha daa pun gaari. Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kpamsi ba.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: n‑yɛli, ni, N zɔdima, m mi halli, ni, ti yi ti kyaŋŋi tooni yaasa di nin kyɛ ka ti yɛla sa'am zaa - pa nyariŋŋu ni neema kɔkɔa, amaa ni ti nyɔvuya gba.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Di zaa yɔri, dama langyima toondaana maa daa niŋi nyariŋŋu daana maa ni u nyarinduusira yɛla yɛdda, n‑deaai ba yɛligu ka za'asi Pɔla dinni.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Ban daa bɛ seelli maa pa la zaazisuŋŋu, dama waari saha ka pɛsiŋŋu nin zeeri n‑kpeeri bɛni n‑daamni ba. Dizugu ba niriba pam daa ŋmɛ saawara n‑gbaai, ni, ba bakkma bɛni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi; ni, di bu ya'ai ni ba ti paai Kyiriti tinseaa ban boonni Fiiniksi la, dama bɛni ba nin toaai n‑kpɛ ya'aseaa kan veeli n‑ti nyarima, waari saha maa, halli ni kan bɛ seem maa ku kyɛ ka lala pɛsima ti kpɛ na.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Tɔ; ban daa ti nya ka pɛsisuŋŋu daa zeeri n‑yiiri ba nyaaŋŋa na, ba daa teesi, ni, ba nin toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ba kyɛnni ban daa gbaai seem la, ka daa foaai buntibsiri kan ta'ari kyɛnni la, n‑du n‑dɔli Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu gbiriŋŋu ziiya.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Amaa di daa bu yuui ka pɛsinkpeeŋŋu daa yisigi Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu ya'ari sa, n‑zɛ n‑daai ti, ni ti yi tingbaŋŋu maa ziiya n‑kyaŋŋi teeku sunsukkuni. Ba boonni lala pɛsiŋŋu, ni, Dirinyaaŋŋa la. Ti daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tuusi pɛsiŋŋu maa, ka kur kyɛ ka ka daari ti n‑kyɛnna.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Ti daa ti paai tingbambila kan zɛ teeku kooma sunsukkuni, ka ka yuuri boonni Kɔdaa la. Ka daa gaari ka nyaaŋŋa, ka moori n‑nyari wahala pam n‑darigri nyarimbila maa n‑kpeesiri buntitaari la puuni n‑lɔ ka suŋŋa; ka laa n‑nyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi mikara n‑lɔ n‑gyiligi nyarintitaari maa sunsukka, hoo sɛlooriga ŋɔn loori nira seaa seem. Dabeem daa mari ba, ni, di pa seelli ti nin paai zibɛseaa ban daa boonni Siritisi la. Dizugu ba daa sigsi nyariŋŋu tantiseaa kan daa kyɛ ka ka kyɛnni la, ka kyɛ ka pɛsiŋŋu zeeri ka daari ti yɔri.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Di daa niŋi dabsiri yinni yaasa ka ti daa diri wahala pɛsiŋŋu maa zugu, ka dizugu ba daa gbaai la neema maa basibu.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: M‑paai dabsa ata daari ka ba zaa daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi n‑yiisi nyariŋŋu maa neema zaa m‑basira.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Di daa yuui lala ka ti daa bu nya wuntaŋŋa neesim, bee saŋmariwuusi; ka pɛsibeoo daa kpalim n‑zeeri ti, ka ti daa laa n‑ka tama, ni, ti nin tuligi halli beela.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Tɔ; ka tin daa bu di diibu ka di yuui la, Pɔla daa zɛ saazugu ba zaa sunsukkuni n‑yɛli, N zɔdima, ya yi daa wum n noori la ka daa bu yisigi Kyiriti tin daa bɛ seela maa, di naa n‑sɔa. Lala yisigri zugu ka ti yɛla sa'am maa.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ n kpamsiri ya, ni, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu dɔya; ya sɔ ku basi yɔri, kulla nyariŋŋu maa kɔkɔa.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Dama n sa zaasi zaasimni ka malaaka yi Naawunni saani na - man gyeemni Naawunni sɔ ka u su n yɛla zaa. Ka malaaka maa sa zɛ n‑tabli n saani na.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: ka yɛli ma, ni, Pɔla, di zɔri dabeem! See ka i nin ti zɛ NaaTitaari tooni: ka dizugu Naawunni zaŋŋi la ninvugseeba ban la'am m‑bɛ i saani nyariŋŋu wa puuni zaa n‑ti i.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Dizugu, n zɔdima, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu dɔya. N niŋi Naawunni yɛdda, ni, u nin tum hoo ŋɔn sa yɛli ma seem la.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Ti nin baasi m‑paai tingbanseaa kan bɛ teeku kooma sunsukkuni maa ni.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Tɔ: di daa niŋi lala, halli nti paai dapiiya ni anaasi daari, yuŋŋu: ti daa kur kyɛnni ka labri na Addiriya teeku maa puuni. Di dabsiri yuntasuusi saha maa ka nyariŋŋu dima maa daa teesi, ni, ti ti pɔtti tinseaa zii na.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ba daa ma'asi m‑baŋŋi koom ŋɔn zulim seem, ka di na zulim pam. Ka kyaŋŋi tooni beela ka laa m‑ma'asi n‑nya, ni, di bu laa n‑zulim hoo ban daa daŋŋi m‑ma'asi seem la.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Di saha ka dabeem daa gbaai ba, ni, di pa seem ba nin ŋmɛ n‑firi luginseelli din bu veeli puuni, ka dizugu ba daa zaŋŋi buntibsa anaasi n‑lɔ miisi n‑lɔ nyariŋŋu nyaaŋŋa n‑sigsi koom maa ni, ni a ti ta'ai nyariŋŋu kyɛnni; ka naayi kpalim n‑susiri wunsusigu n‑guri bɛkyikyeoo saha.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Di saha ka nyariŋŋu dima daa yɛtti, ni, ba ya zɔ ni ka kyɛ ti; dizugu ba daa lɔrigi nyarimbila maa n‑sigsiri ka n‑niŋni teeku koom maa ni; ka ma'ari ziri, ni, ba ya sigsi buntibsa tooni sa m‑paasi an bɛ nyaaŋŋa zugu.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Amaa Pɔla daa yɛli toondaana maa ni u niriba maa, ni, Bana yi bu kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ya sɔ ku ti tuligi.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Ka langyimadima daa ŋmaai nyarimbila miisi maa ka kyɛ ka ka lu m‑basi.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti ya neaai, Pɔla daa tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba maa, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑di diibu beela, ni, Ya loori la ya nɔya ka di paai dabsa piiya ni anaasi, ka kur guusiri ka bu diri seela.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Ya bu ya di diibu pampaŋŋɔa? Lala nin suŋŋi ya, ka ya ti yi ya wahala wa puuni ni alaafɛa. Ya sɔsɔ ku basi yɔri - halli ni ya ninvugsɔ zɔbgu yinni gba.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi diibu m‑puusi Naawunni di zugu ba zaa tooni, ka ŋmaai n‑ŋɔbi.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Di saha ka ba zaa sufu daa kpaŋŋi, ka ba gba daa deaai diibu n‑di.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Ti daa nyɛ la ti niriba kɔbsiyi ni pisiyɔpɔi ni ayoobu nyariŋŋu maa puuni.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Ka ban daa di diibu n‑naai, ba daa zaŋŋi kyi an daa bɛ nyariŋŋu maa puuni n‑yiisi m‑basi koom maa ni, ni di kyɛ ka nyariŋŋu maa ku laa n‑tibsa pam.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa nya ka tingbaŋŋu bɛ ni, amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi ka. Amaa ba nya ya'aseaa ban teesi, ni, mɔ'abisigu m bɛ ni ka tampikara ka ni; ka daa boori ni ba ti kpɛ bɛni n‑kyɛ ka nyariŋŋu maa firi tanni maa ni n‑zaani.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Dizugu ba daa lɔrigi buntibsa maa m‑basi koom maa ni, ka lɔrigi nyarimpaari la ni ba ti laa m‑piili n‑duusiri; ka zaŋŋi tantibila n‑duusi n‑tabli nyariŋŋu tooni, ni ka ti gbaai pɛsiŋŋu n‑daai ba ka ba kyaŋŋi.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Amaa ba daa paai ya'aseaa ka kooma daa kyɛnni n‑tuusiri taaba ka labra, ka ba daa burigi n‑firi, halli ka nyariŋŋu maa toonni ku sakki n‑kyaŋŋi bee n‑labi, sahanseaa ka kooma maa daa ŋmeeri ka nyaaŋŋa n‑kabri ka.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Di saha ka langyimadima daa boori ni ba ku dansarika niriba maa, ni, di bu ya'ai ba sɔ nin ti dugi koom m‑paai tingbaŋŋu n‑zɔa.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Amaa ba toondaana daa bu sakki lala, ŋɔn daa boori ni u tuligi Pɔla zugu. U daa yɛli, ni, ban mi koom dugbu la, ni, ba kpeem koom puuni n‑dugi m‑paai tingbaŋŋu maa.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: Ni, ban kpalim muna - ba gbaam taabudaari, bee, nyariŋŋu dakyɛra maa, m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi: ka lala zugu ba zaa naayi paai teeku duuri maa ni alaafɛa.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti paai tiŋŋa maa n‑tuligi maa, ti daa ti baŋŋi, ni, tingbaŋŋu maa yuuri boonni la Malita.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Tiŋŋa maa niriba daa suŋŋi ti ni nirilim pam. Ba daa nyoaai bugum n‑kyɛ ka ti zaa kyɛ na n‑wuusi, saa maa ŋɔn buuri ti zugu, ni waari maa ŋɔn daa niŋi ti seem.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Ka Pɔla daa boori daari na n‑niŋni bugum maa ni, ka lala saha ka waafu daa bɛ daari maa sunsukkuni n‑gbisira, ka bugum maa tuulim daa neaai u, ka u yi na nti dum Pɔla nuu maa.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Tiŋŋa maa biisi ŋɔn daa ti nya ka waafu maa kpalim n‑kyɛ'ai u nuu maa, ba daa yɛli taaba, ni, Mma! Ninvugkuura n nyɛ u. U tuligi n‑yi teeku koom maa ni na, amaa di bu sumni ni u kpalim m‑bɛ ni.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Pɔla mun daa kpaasi waafu maa m‑basi bugum maa ni, ka kur kpalim m‑mari alaafɛa.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Niriba maa daa teesi, ni, di ku yuui ka u fuusi, bee, ka u lu tiŋŋa ya'abi ka kpi. Ka ba guusiri lala n‑yuui, ka sɛseela bu niŋi u, ka ba naayi takki ba teesa n‑yɛli, ni, wunsɔ n nyɛ u.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ ni ŋɔn daa su tingbaŋŋu fukumsi maa, ka u yuuri daa boonni Publiyusi. Ŋɔn daa su puseesi an daa bɛ tin daa bɛ luginseaa la ni. Ka u daa booli ti ni ti kpɛ u yiri m‑bɛ bɛni n‑niŋi daba ata, ka gɔsi ti yɛla suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Di saha maa ka Publiyusi ba maa daa ka alaafɛa n‑dɔya - u puu n daa beera ka u niŋŋa zaa bii. Ka Pɔla daa kpɛ u saani n‑susi Naawunni ka zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu ka tibi u.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Ka din daa niŋi lala maa, tingbaŋŋu maa ninvugseeba ban kpalim ka kɔŋŋi alaafɛ daa kyɛ na, ni ba ti nya tippu.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Ba daa ti ti piini pam; ka tin daa nya soori ni ti yi bɛni maa, ba daa ti ti la bunseelli kam kan daa pɔsigi ti saani.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Tɔ; ti daa niŋi gɔya ata bɛni, ka naayi nya nyarinseaa kan daa pun zɛ tingbaŋŋu maa lugri n‑guri waari saha baasigu. Alɛkzandiriya nyariŋŋu n daa nyɛ ka, ka ba daa puri ka yuseaa kan nyɛ ba Wungyaasi yuuri.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Ti daa du n‑lɔŋŋi m‑paai Sairakuusi tiŋŋa n‑niŋi daba ata bɛni;.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gyili halli nti paai Rɛgyeoo tiŋŋa. N‑neaai beoo ka pɛsisuŋŋu daa piili n‑zeeri n‑yi ti nyaaŋŋa, ka ti daa gaari yooma n‑niŋi daba ayi kyɛnni ka naayi paai Putiyooli tiŋŋa.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Bɛni ka ti daa nya ti nyaandɔlitaaba, ka ba daa mukki ti ni ti kpalim m‑bɛ ba saani n‑niŋi daba ayɔpɔi. Dizugu ti daa niŋi lala pɔi, ka naayi gbaai soori ni ti kyaŋŋi ti Room maa.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Ka Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa bɛ Room maa daa wum ti kyɛnni na lahabaari, ka daa yi na ni ba ti tuusi ti soori; ka paai ti Apiiya Daa ni na, ka baseeba mun daa paai ti Yirikpeera Ata tiŋŋa na. Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa ti nya ba maa, u daa puusi Naawunni taari, ka u sufu daa maai.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti kpɛ Room timpuuni maa, ba daa ti Pɔla soori ni u bɔ yiri seaa u mammaŋŋa n‑kpɛa; see ka ba kyɛ ka langyinsɔ daa kpalim u saani n‑guri u.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Ka Pɔla daa niŋi daba ata bɛni, ka naayi booli Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa bɛ bɛni toondaandima maa, ni ba kyɛ u saani na. Ka ban daa ti paai na maa, u daa yɛli ba, ni, M mabiisi, man bu tum taaliseaa n‑ti ti butiri, bee, ti yaadima kaya maa; amaa di zaa yɔri, ba daa gbaai ma Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa maa ni, n‑lɔ ma n‑niŋi Room dima nuusi ni.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Room dima mun daa viisi n yɛla ka daa boori ni ba kyɛ ma m‑basi, dama ba daa bu nya taaliseaa n saani kan sumsi ni kum.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima daa bu sakki lala, ka dizugu n daa bu nya soori see ka n ti susi, ni, ba zaŋŋi n yɛttɔ'a maa n‑zaali NaaTitaari saani na. Di pa la man mari la yɛttɔ'aseaa ni n ti galimni n Yɛhudiyataaba.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Ka dizugu m boonni ya, ni ya kyɛm na ka ti ti di azama; ka ya nin ti nya ka baŋŋi din m bɛ seem, ni, Izraɛli dima ban mari tama seaa zugu ka n kpalim n‑lɔ kyɔrimi wa.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, Ti na bu deaai gbana zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla, Gyuudiiya dima ŋɔn sɔbi seesi n‑ti ti na, ka ti mabisɔ na bu paai ti na ni u ti tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑galim i.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Amaa ti boori ni ti ti wum din bɛ i saani, dama ti mi, ni, niriba zaa galimni la wɔligri soori wa yɛla tinsi zaa puuni.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Ka ba daa zaali dabsiri, ka daa kyɛ u yirikpeetiri na ni niriba pampam. Ka Pɔla daa wulisi ba ni nimmoosi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla. U daa tɔ'asiri Musa sɔppu yɛla, ni u ti kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi Yisa yɛla a puuni n‑niŋi u yɛdda - halli anabidima sɔppu gba ka u daa wulisi ba lala - wuntaŋŋa saha halli nti paai yuŋŋu.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Tɔ; ka ba mun seeba daa niŋi u yɛligu yɛdda, baseeba mun daa za'asi yɛdda niŋbu.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Ka ban daa ka nangbanyinni ni taaba maa, ba daa doaai ni ba bakki n‑kuli; ka Pɔla daa yɛli ba yɛligu yinni m‑paasi, ni, Yɛlimaŋni ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa tɔ'asi ya yɛla; u daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya yɛli ya yaadima.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: ni, Naawunni yɛliya#:- Kyɛma butiri wa niriba saani n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ya nin wum, m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ya ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Ya nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ya ku nya di.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Dama n niriba wa muna - Ba sufu ni teesa bu zulima, Ba ligi la ba tuba, Ba mumni ba nini: Dama di yi pa lala Ba nini naa n‑nyara, Ba tuba naa n‑wumna, Ba sufu ni teesa naa n‑neaara, Ka ba ti labi n saani na Nti nya tippu n saani.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Tɔ; ya baŋŋma! Dizugu ba tɔlim tuliginsim wa lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa; ban nin ti wum ka.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala halli n‑niŋi yuuma ayi bɛni. U daa yoori u maŋtoori ligri n‑kuuri u bukaata, ka daa deaari sɔkam zaa ban daa kyɛ u saani na.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ka daa moonni Naawunni naam lahabaari ka wulisiri ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yɛla ni nimbiri moaa, ka sɔsɔ daa bu za'asi u.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Man Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya. Mani n nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu la, ka laa n‑gaŋŋi ma n‑zaali u lahabasuŋŋu tuntumna.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Lala lahabasuŋŋu wa zugu ka Naawunni daa pun daŋŋi n‑tisi noori zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ka yɛla, ka kyɛ ka u anabidima daa sɔbi a n‑dooli ŋɔn Naawunni Bukku maa puuni n‑wulisi ti.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Di nyɛ la lahabaari kan wulisiri u Bii maa yɛla: ni, ba daa dɔ'ai u NaaDauda naam tuŋŋu za'anɔri ni, ka di zugu u siri nyɛ la ninsaala ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Naawunni Siiya beebu muna - Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u yiiko n‑kyɛ ka u neaai kum ni, ka lala ka u daa gaŋŋi u ka ti baŋŋi, ni, u siri nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya. Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya nla.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Ŋɔn zugu n kyɛ ka Naawunni niŋi bunsuma n‑niŋi ti ni, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka tirima leebi u tuumu. Ka u tum ti, ni ti ti kyɛ ka tingbana zaa niriba yi niŋi yɛdda u yuuri zugu ka deaai u noori.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Ka ya Room dima gba bɛ la niriba wa ni, ka Naawunni booli ya, ni ya ti leebi Yisa Masiiya dinni.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɔ: yarima ka n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya, ya zaa ban bɛ Room tiŋŋa ka Naawunni wuri ya ka booli ya nti kyɛ ka ya leebi u niriba maa. Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, tim ya bunsuma ni alaafɛ zaa.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Tɔ: din daŋŋi tooni nyɛ la: ni, n teaari ya yɛla m‑puusiri Naawunni, Yisa Masiiya zugu, dama ya yɛdda niŋbu yɛla moonni n‑gyinni duniya zaa.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: N kur gyeemni Naawunni ni n sufu zaa, ka tumni u Bii maa lahabasuŋŋu tuma ni nimbirimoaa. Ka ŋɔn Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi man ŋɔn kpalim n‑susiri u n‑teaari ya yɛla seem la saha kam.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: N yi ti bɛ wumpuusigu ni saha kam zaa maa ka n niŋni lala. N susiri Naawunni, ni, u sakkma, ni, n naayi nya soori ni n ti kyɛ ya saani na.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Dama di kur mugsiri ma, ni n ti nya ya; ni n mari piiniseelli kan yi Naawunni Sii maa saani n‑ti ya, ka ya nin kpaŋŋi di zugu.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Di nyɛ la: ti kpamsiri taaba sufu, man ni yarima zaa, ti zaa ŋɔn niŋni yɛdda seem zugu.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: M mabiisi, m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, n daa pun gbaai ya saani kyɛnni na sahaseesi, amaa n kɔŋŋi la soori halli ni zuna. M boori m mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa ya saani n‑nya seeba ban leebi Naawunni niriba, hoo man daa pun nya seem tinseesi ni la - dama n tuma maa antaam nla.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Ba zaali ma, ni, n suŋŋma sɔkam zaa: ban mi beesigu soori maa, ni ban zi soori maa m‑paasi; ban mi bukku maa, ni ban zi bukku maa m‑paasi#:- ba zaa yɛla dɔli ma.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Lala zugu n kyɛ ka m boori nimmoaa ni n kyɛ ya saani na m‑mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti ya Room timpuuni dima gba.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Vi bu mari ma lahabasuŋŋu wa zugu: dama lahabasuŋŋu maa nyɛ la Naawunni yiiko din kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda la ti nya tuliginsim; Izraɛli dima daŋŋni la tooni, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Lahabaari maa wulisi la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori ka di bɛ la yɛdda zugu ka laa n‑taasiri la yɛdda paasigu ni, ka ba naayi nya taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi. Hoo din sɔbi seem Naawunni Bukku maa ni, ni, Yɛlimaŋni daana nin nya nyɔvuri u yɛdda niŋbu zugu.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Dama Naawunni bɛ la arizana ni, ka u nin ti wulisi u sufu ŋɔn yisigi seem n‑tiri seeba ban mi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa ka bu boori ni di yi paalu. Ba za'asiri Naawunni ka tumni ba putoo tuma, ni ba tumbeeri tuma.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Naawunni yɛla bamsim siri bɛ la ba saani, dama ŋɔn Naawunni mammaŋŋa daa kyɛ ka di yi paalu n‑ti ba.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Dama sɔsɔ nini ku toaai n‑nya Naawunni yiiko ni u beebu an ka naari maa: amaa di zaa yɔri, ŋɔn daa naam duniya maa zugu n kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi anŋɔ zaa yɛla: a bamsim maa daa yi paalu lala saha maa, halli ni zuna. Dizugu niriba wa ka noori ni ba ti yiisi ba mansi ba taalli ni.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Halli ban daa mi Naawunni yɛla maa zaa yɔri, ba daa za'asi ni, ni, ba ku gyeem u ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni zugu, bee, ba puusi u barika di zugu. Ba teesi, ni, ba mari la yɛm, amaa ba ka; ka lala maa kyɛ ka ba yɛm maa baligi zaa.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ban daa boonni ba mansi baŋŋniba seem la, ba daa kur leebri la gyɛra.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: halli ka ba daa ti kyɛ Naawunni gyirima din ka naari maa, n‑takki n‑gyeemni bunseesi an ŋmanni la ninsaala, bee, bunyigra, bee, bunkɔbri, bee, bunvuura.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi ba m‑basi ba halibeoo tunfiisa maa puuni, ka ba naayi tumni din bu tu n‑sa'amni taaba ningbina.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Dama ba za'asi la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa ka boori ziri, ka naayi gyeemni bunnaamna ka kyɛ buna Naamna maa mammaŋŋa; ŋɔn Naawunni Naamna sumni la pɛ'abu saha din ka baasigu; ammi .

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ba m‑basi ba vi kɔrisi tuma ni; halli ka ba pɔ'aba kyɛ doo ni pɔ'a la'aŋŋu ka takki m‑boori ba pɔ'ataaba.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Ka ba dappa gba kyɛ doo ni pɔ'a la'ansuŋŋu ka dɔli ba sufu ni kɔribeeri ka kpɛ zugfa'asim tuma ni ba dabtaaba: ba boori bunseesi an ka soori, ka naayi deaari yɛlibeeri an sum ni ba lala tuma.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Ban daa bu sakki ni ba baŋŋi Naawunni maa zugu n kyɛ ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi ba m‑basi ba toori teesiyɔya ni, nti tumni tunseesi an bu tuusi.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Lala maa zugu ba yi bɛ la tumpoosa ni, ni tumbeeri ni beem tuma, ni ala'akyi tuma ni; ba yɛla zaa nyɛ la nyuuri zappu, ni ninvugkuubu; ba boori la zaba, n‑yɔmsiri niriba, m‑mari halibeoo, ka zuuri la niriba nyaaŋŋa;.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: ba tuuri la niriba ka yaai Naawunni gba; ka nyari ba mansi, m‑mari karimbaani, n‑nyuuri bara, ba kur boori la ba maali n‑nya yɛttɔ'abeeri sɔpaala; ba za'asiri ba ba ni ba ma noori;.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: ba ka yɛm, bee yɛdda, bee wurilim, bee namboo.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ba mi halli, Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lala tuma maa, ni, di sum ni ban tumni lala maa kpi. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba sini ka tumni a ni, ka din gaari zaa nyɛ la ba suŋŋni la seeba ni ba gba tumni lala.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna, n zɔa, i yi zaali zaaligu m‑bugsiri niriba yɛla, i ka sɔseaa nyin nin zaŋŋi n‑wulisi i yɛlimaŋni. Dama yɛttɔ'aseaa zaa ka i ya tɔ'asi n‑galim i taraana, lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu ka i galimni i mammaŋŋa, nyin ŋɔn bugsiri ba ka na tumni lala tuma maa zugu.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Ti mun mi, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn katti sariya ka galimni ban tumni lala tuma maa n‑lusiri ba, ni, u sariya karibu wa dɔli la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Dizugu nyin ninsaala kur bugsiri ban tumni lala tuma yɛla hoo sarikatta ka galimni ba, ka nyin gba tumni lala. Ammi, i teesi, ni, i nin nyaŋŋi n‑yi galinsi ni n‑liisi Naawunni sariya karibu di zugu?.
