eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Bee, i bu baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni zɔri i la namboo ni u ti zi i n‑taasiri ziseaa ka i ŋmaligi ka kyɛ i tumbeeri ni i teesibeeri yɛla? Lala zugu ka i zeemni ŋɔn Naawunni nambɔzoaa, ni u veelim, ni u sugru, an galisi n‑tiri i: bee, m ma'ai ni?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Ay! I tuppuwumni zugu ka i ku sakki n‑ŋmaligi ka kyɛ i yɛlibeeri maa, ka lala ka i la'asiri toom yɛla n‑zaantiri n‑tiri i maŋŋa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni suuri daari; sahanseaa ka u laa n‑nyɛ la ŋɔn katti sariya ni yɛlimaŋni ka sɔkam zaa nya.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Di saha maa ka Naawunni nin ti kyɛ ka sɔkam deaai bunseesi an sum ni ŋɔn daa pun tum seem la.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Baseeba ban kpa n‑tum suŋŋa m‑boori veelim, ni gyirima, ni nyɔvuri din bu sa'amni la ziiya, ban ka Naawunni nin tisi nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Ban mun mari halibeoo n‑kur boori ba mansi ka za'asiri yɛlimaŋni ka dɔli yɛlibeeri, ban ka Naawunni sugyeaa nin kpeesi namsigu ni toom ni.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Nira ŋɔn tumni tumbeeri zaa nin nya wahala ni sufusa'aŋŋu: Izraɛli dima nin daŋŋi tooni n‑nya, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Amaa veelim, ni gyirima, ni sufumaasim nin bɛ ni n‑tiri ban niŋni din tuusi: Izraɛli dima nin daŋŋi tooni n‑deaai, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Dama Naawunni bu wulisiri ninvuggaŋŋsim.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Bankam ka zaaligu maa, ka tumni ala'akyi, ba nin ti baasi yɔri ka zaaligu maa nuu ka di ni; ban mun mari zaaligu maa, ka tumni ala'akyi, ban ka ba nin zaŋŋi zaaligu maa n‑kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Dama di pa la ban tuba wumni zaaligu maa yɛligu la n nin nya yɛlimaŋni Naawunni saani, amaa ban tumni zaaligu tuma maa ka Naawunni deaai, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima n nyɛ ba.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, di pa seem ban pa Izraɛli dima la - ban ka Musa zaaligu maa - ba ninvugseeba yi tumni din tu n‑dɔli zaaligu maa soori, ba maŋtoori sufu ni teesisuma zugu: di yi bɛ la lala, halli ni ban ka Musa zaaligu maa zaa, ba siri zaali la zaalimaŋni n‑ti ba mansi.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Dama ba wulisiri, ni, zaaligu maa tunsuma bɛ la ba sufu ni, ka ba sufu yɛm kyɛ ka lala zaaligu yi paalu, ba mammaŋŋa teesa yi diri sɛhara n‑galimni ba tunseesi ka laa n‑sakkri ni aseesi yaasa, ni, ana n tuusi.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Tɔ: anŋɔ zaa nin niŋi la dabsiri seaa Naawunni ŋɔn ya kari saalidima yɛlisɔ'ara sariya la, ka di yi Kirisita Yisa saani na. Lala ka man kur wulisiri niriba, n lahabasuŋŋu mooligu maa ni.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Tɔ, n zɔa, wumma - i yi ti booli i maŋŋa, ni, Izraɛli nira, ka yɛtti, ni, i zaaziiya nyɛ la Naawunni zaaligu yɛla, ka nyuuri bara zaŋŋi n‑kpa i Naawunni saani beesigu ziiya.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: Ka laa n‑yɛtti, ni, i mi Naawunni yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka sakki n‑ti din veela halli nti yakki zaa, ka pun bamsi zaaligu kariŋŋu n‑naai.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: Ka i laa n‑teesi, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔn datti zoomma, n‑nyɛ neesim n‑ti ban bɛ zibsim ni la;.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: ni, i wulisiri ban kɔŋŋni yɛm; ni, biisi karimba n nyɛ i. I niŋni i mammaŋŋa yɛdda anŋɔ zaa zugu, dama i mari la zaaligu maa, ka yɛm ni yɛlimaŋni zaa gbinni bɛ di ni.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Tɔ, nyin ŋɔn wulisiri niriba lala maa, i ku wulisi i maŋŋa? Nyin ŋɔn moonni n‑tiri ba, ni, ba di zuura la - i mammaŋŋa zuuri, bee?.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Nyin yɛtti, ni, niriba di niŋni zina la, i niŋni zina i mammaŋŋa? Ka yɛtti, ni, i bu boori buga yɛla, i zuuri buga duri buna?.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Halli i mari la zaaligu maa ka nyuuri bara dizugu, i burigri zaaligu maa ni, m‑boaari Naawunni gyirima?.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli la, ni, Yarima zugu ka Naawunni yuuri sa'amni ban pa Izraɛli dima saani.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gunibu yɛla muna - i yi dɔli zaaligu maa tuma suŋŋa, dinŋɔna ka i gunibu mari la anfaani; amaa i yi burigi zaaligu maa ni, i labgi la hoo ban bu guni sɔ la.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Ban bu guni la mun yi dɔli zaaligu maa deede, ba ku nya ba la hoo ban guni maa? Ay! - ba nin nya ba lala.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Ŋɔn bu guni ka dɔli zaaligu maa nin galim nyin ŋɔn mari zaaligu gbaŋŋu ka guni ka mun burigi zaaligu maa ni.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Dama ŋɔn nyɛ Izraɛli nira la paalu ni kɔkɔa pa la Izraɛli nira halaali; ka gunibu maŋni mun pa la din niŋi ningbina paalu ni kɔkɔa.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Ay! U sufu ni ka nira n nyɛ Izraɛli nira halaali, ka gunibu maŋni gba nyɛ la sufu ni yɛttɔ'a. Di yi la Naawunni Sii maa ni na, pa gbana sɔppu saani. Lala nira pɛ'abu bu yiiri la ninsaala saani, amaa Naawunni saani.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna bɔ anfaani m bɛ ni n‑ti nira ŋɔn nyɛ Izraɛli nira zugu? Bee, bɔ ka gunibu paasira?.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Ay! Anfaani pam m bɛ ni, walla! Tuuri, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u yɛligu maa n‑niŋi la Izraɛli dima nuu ni, ni ba ti gura.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ, ba seeba ka yɛdda: tɔ, ka ba yɛdda kaalinsi maa wulisi, ni, Naawunni gba ka yɛdda, bee?.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Ay! di pa lala. Naawunni kur kpalim n‑zɛ u yɛlimaŋni ni, halli ni ninsaala zaa ŋɔn nyɛ ziri daana. Kaman di pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Lala ka i yɛlimaŋni yi paalu ni, Ni, i yɛla zaa mari yɛdda; Ba yi ya kari i sariya I yɛttɔ'a nin zɛ suŋŋa n‑nyaŋŋi ba.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Tɔ, ka lala ka ti tumbeeri yɛla wulisiri Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem la; ka ti yɛtti la wula di zugu? - ni, Naawunni yi ti gyɛ suuri tumbeeri zugu, u bu dɔli la soori? Ay! di pa lala. Ninsaala yɛttɔ'ayɔna nla. Ya bu nya - Naawunni yi bu dɔli la soori, u ya niŋi la wula n‑kari duniya zaa sariya suŋŋa?.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Ni, N yi ti ma'ai ziri ka n ziri kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛlimaŋni yiiri paalu, tɔ, m paasiri Naawunni pɛ'abu di zugu. Ka dinŋɔna bɔ zugu ka ba galim ma hoo tumbeeri daana.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Ni, Dinzugu ya kyɛ ka ti tum tumbeeri, ka lala ka bunsuŋŋu nin yi di ni na. Halli ninvugseeba zuuri n nyaaŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni, man tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti lala maa zugu. Ninvugseeba ban tuuri ma n‑yɛtti lala maa siri sumni ni tubdarigri.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Tɔ: ti yɛtti la wula? Ti Izraɛli dima maa muna - ti yɛttɔ'a sɔa, bee, a bu sɔa? Ay! ti yɛla bu sɔa; dama ti pun wulisiya, ni, sɔkam zaa bɛ la tumbeeri ni - ban nyɛ Izraɛli niriba ni ban pa Izraɛli dima zaa.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Kaman hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Sɔsɔ ka yɛlimaŋni, Pa yinni kɔkɔ gba.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Sɔ mun ka ni m‑mari yɛm, Sɔsɔ bu boori Naawunni ziiya.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Sɔkam zaa burigri soori ni, Sɔkam zaa leebi la bunkyɔ'aŋŋu. Sɔ n ka ni n‑tumni din veela, Pa yinni kɔkɔ gba.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ni#:- Ba lɔŋŋu ŋmanni la gballi noori - Gbaliseaa kan na bu pi: Ba yɛttɔ'a zaa nyɛ la yɔmsigu. Ni#:- Ba mari la gyɛriŋŋa toom n‑sɔ'ari ba nangbana ni.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Ni#:- Ba zulimbeeri toom yiiri ba nɔya ni na.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi, ni#:- Ba zɔri n‑kyɛnni soori ni Ni ba ti ku niriba yooma.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Ba sa'amni la buna n‑gaari, N‑taasi la sufusa'aŋŋu beerim m‑paasi.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Ba mun bu mi sugru soori.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Ni#:- Ba mun bu tiri Naawunni gyirima.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Tɔ; ka ti mi halli, ni, zaaligu sɔppu ŋɔn yɛli seem la, ka yɛtti n‑tiri la zaaligu niriba la - di nyɛ la, Izraɛli dima maa. Lala zugu ka ba ku laa n‑nya kukɔri, ka baŋŋi, ni, duniya niriba zaa mari la yɛttɔ'a ni ba ti tɔ'asi Naawunni tooni.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Din laa m‑paasi, ninsaala zaa ku toaai n‑dɔli zaaligu soori ka naayi yi Naawunni sariya karibu ni na, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana n nyɛ u. Dama zaaligu maa tuma nyɛ la lala: di kur kyɛ ka taalli yɛla yi paalu.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ muna ka Naawunni wulisi u yɛlimaŋni soori kan bu dɔli la zaaligu sɔppu la, halli ka zaaligu gbaŋŋu ni anabidima yɛla daa pun mooli, ni, di nin niŋi lala.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn tiri yɛlimaŋni seem wa dɔli la Yisa Masiiya yɛdda niŋbu, ka dinŋɔ nyɛ la lala n‑ti sɔkam zaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda gba. Wɔligri ka ni.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: dama sɔkam tum la taalli ka kɔŋŋi soori ni ba paai Naawunni gyirima ziiya.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn tisiri ba piini yɔri, samni ka ni, la zugu, u yiisi ba taalli ni, ka zaali ba yɛlimaŋni, Kirisita Yisa ŋɔn fa ba m‑basi zugu.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa muna - Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u n‑tisi hoo maaligu, ni u ti nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn ziim mari kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi, ka di mun dɔli la yɛdda. Lala zugu Naawunni n wulisiri u mammaŋŋa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem, dama sahanseaa la u daa di sugru zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban daa daŋŋi n‑tum taalli.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: U boori la u niŋi lala n‑wulisi u yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem saha kanŋɔa; dizugu u mammaŋŋa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka laa n‑nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn deaari sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi Yisa yɛdda la gba, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Tɔ, ka ti nin nyu bara dizugu, bee? Ay! Barinyuubu ka ni. Wula zugu? Dama di zaa bu dɔli la zaaligu maa dɔligu, amaa di dɔli la sɔseaa - di nyɛ la yɛdda niŋbu soori.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Dinŋɔna ti mari teesa, ni, niriba bɛ la yɛlimaŋni dima ba yɛdda niŋbu zugu; di pa la zaaligu la tuma zugu ka ba nya yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Ammi, Naawunni nyɛ la Izraɛli dima kɔkɔ Naawunni? - ka u ba'a ka ban kpalim la ni? Ay! di pa lala - ban pa Izraɛli dima la gba Naawunni n nyɛ u.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Yɛlimaŋni u nyɛ la sɔkam zaa Naawunni, dama Naawunni yinni kɔkɔ m bɛ ni; u deaari Izraɛli dima, ban guni la, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima ba yɛdda niŋbu zugu, ka laa n‑deaari ban pa Izraɛli dima ka bu guni la gba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima ba yɛdda zugu.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu, ti kyiisiri, ni, anfaani ka zaaligu la ni, halli beela? - dama ti boori yɛdda niŋbu yɛla. Ay! pa lala: ti ya zaŋŋi zaaligu maa n‑zaali la suŋŋa di zugu.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Tɔ: ka Ibrahim muna? - sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ti butiri zaa yaaba la: bɔ ka ti yɛ n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔna n daa baŋŋi?.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Ka di yi daa nyɛ la tunsuma zugu ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, dinŋɔna u da mari seelli ni u kuuli di zugu, amaa pa Naawunni saani ka u daa kuuli.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Litaafi yɛliya, ni, Ibrahim daa sakki n‑niŋi Naawunni yɛdda, ka u yɛdda wa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana. - bee, di pa lala?.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Nira ŋɔn tumni maa, see ka u deaai u yoori: u tuma samni dɔli la u tuma daana la: di pa la ba yɛli, ni, ba nin zaŋŋi n‑ti u piini.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Amaa ŋɔn bɛ ni u yɛdda zugu la, ka pa u tuma zugu, dama u niŋni Naawunni yɛdda, ni, u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋni tumbeeri daana taalli yɛla zaa m‑pɛŋŋni u, di daama mun yɛttɔ'a maa ni ka ba yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Halli NaaDauda gba daa pun yɛli lala nira yɛla - di nyɛ la: ninvugsɔ Naawunni ŋɔn yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka di pa la u tuma zugu. U daa tɔ'asi di daama zugsuŋŋu yɛla, ni#:-.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ba - Ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn kyɛ ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi la, Ka ba tumbeeri laa n‑ka teaari u saani.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Zugsuŋŋu gba n‑ti seeba Naawunni sariya ŋɔn bu lusi ba n‑yɛli, ni, ba galim n‑tum la taalli.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Tɔ: ka NaaDauda ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi zugsunseaa wa nyɛ la ban guni la kɔkɔ dinni? Bee, ban bu guni su ka gba? Ay! - ban bu guni la gba nin mara, dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni, U yɛdda zugu ka Naawunni daa yɛli Ibrahim, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Ka wula saha ka Naawunni daa yɛli lala? Di saha maa ka Ibrahim guniya, bee, u daa na bu guni? - Ay! di saha ka u na bu guni.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa naayi guni u, ka u gunibu wa daa wulisi la, ni, Naawunni pun yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana u yɛdda zugu; yɛdda wa mun ka u daa pun niŋi ka ba daa na bu guni u. Dinzugu ŋɔn Ibrahim nyɛ la yɛdda niŋniba zaa ba - baseeba ban niŋi Naawunni yɛdda ka na bu guni la - ka di nin kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni dima.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Ka u laa n‑nyɛ la seeba ban guni la zaa ba m‑paasi - baseeba ban pa la ban guni kɔkɔa, amaa ba yɛdda niŋbu gba dɔli la ti zaa ba, Ibrahim, soori maa, dama ŋɔn daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda pɔi, ka naayi guni.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɔ; ka dizugu tin niŋi yɛdda la zugu kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ti, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima. Ka lala kyɛ ka nangbanyinni bɛ ni tin ni Naawunni sunsukkuni, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti u nangbambasiri n‑ti Ibrahim ni u nyaaŋŋa maa, u daa yɛli, ni, u nin kyɛ ka u di duniya wa hoo u faari; u daa niŋi lala maa, pa la Ibrahim ŋɔn daa dɔli zaaligu la zugu, amaa ŋɔn daa niŋi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛdda la zugu.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Dama ŋɔn Yisa zugu daa kyɛ ka ti nya yɛdda niŋbu soori nti kpɛ n‑zɛ tin bɛ zaazisunseaa wa ni. Di nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum lala tunsuma n‑ti ti maa. Dizugu ka ti mari sufupeelli, dama ti mari tama, ni, ti nin nya Naawunni gyirima veelim.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Ka di yi nyɛ la zaaligu zugu ka Naawunni nangbambasiri paari ninvugseeba ban dɔli ka, dinŋɔna yɛdda nyɛ la yɔri, ka Naawunni nangbambasiri gba nyɛ yɔri.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Din paasi maa, ti mari sufupeelli halli ti wahala diibu puuni gba, dama ti mi, ni, wahala diibu paasiri ti sugru ni sufukpeemni.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Zaaligu la tumni la sufuyisigri zugu, amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, zaaligu galiŋŋu gba ku bɛ ni.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Sugru mun paasiri ti beesisuŋŋu, ka beesisuŋŋu mun paasiri ti tama.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Nangbambasiri maa daa kyɛ na la yɛdda zugu, ka ka zɛ la deede, ni ka ti leebi piini n‑tiri Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa zaa, pa la seeba ban dɔli zaaligu la kɔkɔa, amaa ni seeba ban niŋni yɛdda hoo Ibrahim gba. Ka ŋɔn Ibrahim nyɛ la ti zaa ba.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Ka tama maa paasigu ku kyɛ ka ti di vi, dama Naawunni zaŋŋi u Siiya n‑ti ti ka ti baŋŋi u wurilim ŋɔn zoaai seem di zugu.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: Litaafi yɛliya, ni, N nin kyɛ ka i leebi bura pam yaaba. - ŋɔn daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda maa zugu nla. U daa niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni tisiri la nyɔvuri, ka u yɛligu daa kyɛ ka din daa ka ni la yi ti bɛ ni.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Kirisita muna - sahanseaa ti daa na ka yaa la, lala saha maa deede ka u daa kpi n‑ti tirim tumbeeri dima maa.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Ibrahim daa niŋi yɛdda ka mari tama, halli din daa ti paai ni u ti koaai tama maa zaa u daa kpalim m‑mari tama, ka di zugu u daa leebi bura pam yaaba, dama Litaafi daa pun yɛli lala, ni, Lala ka i nyaaŋŋa nin ti bɛ ni.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Ya bu nya? - Di tɔ pam ni sɔ sakki n‑kpi n‑ti ninvugsɔa - halli n‑ti yɛlimaŋni daansɔ gba. Amaa di bu ya'ai ka nira yi mari sufukpeemni n‑sakki n‑kpi ninvugsuŋŋu zugu.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Halli ŋɔn daa paai yuunkɔbga maa zaa yɔri, u yɛdda daa bu baligi ŋɔn daa kurigi la zugu, u ningbina ŋɔn daa gyaari seem la, ka u pɔ'a Zaraatu gba ningbina gyaari nti kɔŋŋi puuwa gbaari yaasa.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔna - ŋɔn daa niŋi seem n‑wulisi ti, ni, u wuri ti pam nŋɔa#:- tin daa na bɛ tumbeeri ni sahanseaa la, Kirisita daa kpi n‑ti ti.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Ii - anŋɔ zaa yɔri u yɛdda daa bu basi u, ka u daa bu birisi Naawunni nangbambasiri la; u yɛdda wa daa ti u la yiiko, ka u daa pɛ'ai Naawunni.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Tɔ, ka di yi bɛ lala maa, ka Naawunni pun deaai ti, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima, Yisa Masiiya ziim maa zugu, dinŋɔna ti mi halli, ni, ŋɔn Yisa zugu ka ti nin laa n‑yi Naawunni sugyeaa ni gba.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: U daa maali n‑kpamsi u sufu ni yɛdda, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn basi u nangbambasiri maa seem la, di siri nin niŋi lala.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Dama tin daa nyɛ Naawunni dindaandima sahanseaa la, u Bii Yisa kum maa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ti taalli sindaani maa. Di zugu ti mi halli, ni, Yisa nyɔvuri zugu nin kyɛ ka ti nya tuliginsim.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Ka dinŋɔna, ŋɔn Ibrahim yɛdda maa zugu ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Amaa pa dinŋɔ kɔkɔ gba. See ka ti mari sufupeelli pam Naawunni saani, ka gyeemni u ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu dama ŋɔn Yisa n daa zɔ sindaani n‑ti ti ka u Ba deaai.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Ni, u yɛtti, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana - ba daa bu sɔbi lala ŋɔn Ibrahim zugu kɔkɔa.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Dinzugu ya teesima Adam yɛla: ŋɔn nira yinni zugu ka tumbeoo ni ala'akyi daa kpɛ duniya wa na, ka tumbeoo zugu muna ka kum bɛ ni. Lala zugu ka kum daa pun laai m‑paai sɔkam, dama sɔkam zaa tum la tumbeoo.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: Ay! - ba daa sɔbi di lala maa n‑ti la tirima, dama ba nin yɛli ti gba lala, ti seeba ban niŋi yɛdda n‑tiri Naawunni, ni, u daa yisigi ti Dugma Yisa kum ni la.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Tumbeoo siri daa pun bɛ la duniya wa puuni pɔi, ka Musa na bu deaai zaaligu maa yɛla Naawunni saani. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri tumbeoo galinsi yɛla, zaaligu la ŋɔn daa na ka ni la zugu.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Ti tumbeeri zugu ka ba daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑tisi ni u kpi; ka u mun yisigi kum ni, ni ti ti yi na hoo yɛlimaŋni dima.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Amaa kum daa pun su ba zaa fukumsi n‑yi Adam saha halli nti paai Musa. Sɔkam daa kpi, halli ban daa bu tum tumbɛseelli kan ŋmanni Adam dinni la nti za'asi Naawunni noori zaaligu. Ŋɔn Adam muna - u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni n‑wulisiri sɔ ŋɔn nin naayi kyɛ na la yɛla ŋɔn ti ya bɛ seem.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Amaa Naawunni piini yiiko bu ŋmanni tumbeoo dinni. Ay! - di gaari lala zugu pam. Ti mi halli, ni, ninvugyinni taalli zugu ka niriba pam daa kpi; amaa Naawunni piini zoaari m‑paasiri n‑suŋŋni niriba pam, ka lala gba yiiri la ninvugyinni zugu ni na - di nyɛ la: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Din laa m‑paasi: Naawunni piini siri bu ŋmanni la ninvugyinni maa tumbeoo la yɛla: dama tumbeoo yinni kɔkɔ zugu ka Naawunni daa kari sɔkam sariya n‑lusi ba, amaa Naawunni piini zugu daa kyɛ ka tumbeeri pam yɛla daa kpiisi, ka ba yiisi niriba maa, ni, ba mari yɛlimaŋni.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Ya bu nya? - Tumbeoo yinni taalli zugu daa kyɛ ka kum su sɔkam fukumsi, ka di daa yi lala tumbɛdaana yinni maa saani na. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ti mi halli, ni, seeba ban deaai Naawunni yɛlimaŋni u piinisuŋŋu pam zugu la nin ti su nyɔvuri naam fukumsi, ka di mun nyɛ la tunsuŋŋu daana yinni zugu, ŋɔn nyɛ Yisa Kirisita.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, nira yinni tumbeoo zugu kyɛ ka niriba zaa yɛttɔ'a lu Naawunni saani, ka ba zaa nya taalli u saani. Lala yaasa ka nira yinni maa tunsuŋŋu zugu kyɛ ka niriba zaa yi taalli ni, ka ba mari yɛlimaŋni Naawunni saani nti nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Ninvugyinni maa daa za'asi Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa nya taalli, ni, ala'akyi dima n nyɛ ba. Lala yaasa ka ninvugyinni ŋɔnla daa sakki Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa yi taalli ni, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima n nyɛ ba.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Zaaligu muna - ba daa mari ka na ni ba ti kyɛ ka taalli tumpu maali m‑paasira. Amaa taalli ŋɔn maali m‑paasiri seem la, Naawunni piini gba daa paasi n‑gaari lala zugu.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Tumbeoo zugu n‑daa la, dama tumbeoo daa mari kum yiiko n‑su niriba fukumsi, amaa lala ka Naawunni piinisuŋŋu mari yɛlimaŋni yiiko n‑su ba fukumsi, halli ka ba ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Tɔ; dinŋɔna ti ya yɛli la wula? Ni, ti kpalimma n‑tumni tumbeeri ni ti ti kyɛ ka Naawunni piinisuma zoaari m‑paasira?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Tɔ. M mabiisi, ya kyɛ ka n tɔ'asi n‑ti yaseeba ban baŋŋi zaaligu la. Ya na zi zaaligu yɛla ŋɔn nyɛ seem deede, bee? Ni, nira ŋɔn na kpalim m‑bɛ u nyɔvuri ni sahanseaa maa kɔkɔ ka zaaligu maa dɔli u: bee bɔa?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Ay! - di pa lala. Ti nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban pun kpi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko yɛla ni. Ka wula zugu ka ti nin laa m‑bɛ tumbeeri tumpu ni?.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Ya bu nya? - Zaaligu wulisiya, ni, pɔ'a yi kuli doo, di kpa ni, ni, u sira maa su u yɛla sahanseaa lala doo maa yi na bɛ u nyɔvuri ni. Amaa u sira maa kum nyaaŋŋa, zaaligu basi pɔ'a maa taalli ka u su u maŋŋa, halli ni u nin toaai n‑kuli siripaalli. Pɔ'a wa yi bɔ dɔsɔ, ka u sira na bɛ u nyɔvuri ni, ba boonni u, ni, pɔ'agoorli; amaa u sira maa yi kpi, zaaligu maa bu laa n‑dɔli u, ka u pa pɔ'agoorli, ni ŋɔn kuli siripaalli maa zaa.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Bee, ya bu mi kɔsuubu gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem? Tirim ban daa deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu la zaa, dina n wulisi, ni, ti bɛ la Yisa Kirisita ni. Ka di laa n‑wulisi, ni, ti paasi la u zugu n‑kpi gba.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Tɔ, ka di daa nyɛ la lala n‑ti ya, m mabiisi. Ba daa kyɛ ka ya paasi Kirisita ningbina zugu ka ku ya, di zugu ya laa n‑ka ni n‑ti zaaligu yɛla. Ka dizugu ya nya soori ni ya ti leebi sɔ dinni, ŋɔn yisigi kum ni - di nyɛ la Yisa; ni di niŋi anfaani n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Din laa m‑paasi, ban daa su ti koom ni ti ti paasi Yisa zugu n‑kpi maa, ba daa zaŋŋi ti m‑paasi u zugu n‑sɔ'ai. Ka di gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: ni, ti Ba Naawunni gyirima yiiko daa kyɛ ka Yisa doaai kum ni; ka di zugu lala kyɛ ka ti gba doaai nti bɛ beesipaalli.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Sahanseaa la, ti beesigu daa dɔli ti ningbinkɔrisi yɛla. Di saha ka tumbeeri tumpu boorimma daa dɔli zaaligu maa n‑nya yaa n‑tumni ti ningbina luga zaa ni; ka ti daa bu nya anfaani di zugu - ay! kum kɔkɔ ka ti daa nya.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Dama ti yi ŋmanni la ti Dugma Yisa n‑kpi u kum wa taabu, di kpa, ni, ti nin ŋmanni hoo ŋɔna u kum doaari ni gba.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ti kpiya ni n‑ti zaaligu maa, ka di zugu zaaligu wa bu laa n‑su ti yɛla. Lala yi kyɛ ka ti bu laa n‑dɔli sɔkurigu kan nyɛ zaaligu sɔppu maa dinni la, amaa sɔpaalli kan nyɛ Sii maa dinni.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Ka ti mi, ni, ti beesikura la ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑la'am m‑paasi Masiiya zugu n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, ni lala kyɛ ka ti beesikura ningbina nin sa'am m‑basi, Tumbeoo ŋɔn su a yɛla zaa maa zugu. Dinŋɔna ti ningbina ku laa n‑nyɛ seesi Ala'akyi ŋɔn su hoo ban su daaba seem maa.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Dinzugu, ti ya yɛli la wula? - ni, Naawunni zaaligu maa nyɛ la ala'akyi, bee? Ay! pa lala. Amaa di yi da pa la zaaligu la zugu, n naa n‑ku baŋŋi ala'akyi ŋɔn nyɛ seelli. Dama zaaligu maa yi da bu yɛli, ni, Di kyɛ ka i sufu kɔrisi kpɛ i kpeaa buna zugu. n naa n‑ku baŋŋi niriba neema boobu yɛla.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Dama ŋɔn kpi zaa pun yi ala'akyi ni.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Ala'akyi daa dɔli zaaligu la n‑nya zaazisuŋŋu pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka neema boobu teesibeeri balibu pam kpɛ n sufu ni. Amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, Ala'akyi mun gba kpi ni.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Tɔ, ka tirim ŋɔn pun paasi Kirisita zugu n‑kpi maa, dinŋɔna ti mari la yɛdda, ni, ti nin paasi u zugu m‑mari nyɔvuri gba.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Sahanseaa la, n daa mari nyɔvuri, ka na zi zaaligu yɛla maa. Amaa zaaligu la ŋɔn daa ti paai ma na, ka daa kyɛ ka Ala'akyi neaai ni.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Ti mi, ni, Kirisita daa pun yisigi kum ni, ka di zugu u ku laa n‑kpi, dama kum laa n‑ka yiiko u saani.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: ka man kpi. Zaaligu la gbinni nyɛ la nyɔvuri, amaa man saani ka daa zi la kum na.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Ŋɔn daa kpi maa, u daa kpi yummu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko saani: ka ŋɔn mari nyɔvuri maa muna, u mari n‑ti la Naawunni saani.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Zaaligu maa zugu ka Ala'akyi daa nya soori n‑yɔmsi ma, n‑zaŋŋi zaaligu maa yɛla n‑kyɛ ka n kpi.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya gba teesima ya mansi yɛla lala. Ni, ya gba pun kpi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko ni, ka mari nyɔvuri n‑ti Naawunni, yan la'am m‑bɛ Kirisita Yisa saani zugu.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Ka di zaa wulisiri, ni, Naawunni zaalikpana maa nyɛ la kasi, ka zaaligu kam zaa yinniyinni nyɛ la bunveelli; a zaa mari la yɛlimaŋni kasikasi.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Ya ningbina nyɛ la seesi an kpiira, ka di zugu yan di kyɛ ka Tumbeoo su a fukumsi nti kyɛ ka a dɔli Tumbeoo kɔribeeri yɛla.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Ka bunsuŋŋu dinŋɔ n kyɛ ka kum paai ma la, bee wula? - Ay, pa lala! Ala'akyi n nyɛ din ziiri kum na, ka ka daa dɔlisi bunsuŋŋu wa n‑tum lala maa; ka di zugu ka ti baŋŋi ala'akyi beeri ŋɔn nyɛ seem la. Lala zugu ka zaaligu la kyɛ ka tumbeeri zaa yiiri paalu.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Ya di laa n‑zaŋŋi ya ningbina luga zaa n‑tiri Tumbeoo, ka a ti leebi Tumbeoo tuma neema. Amaa ya zaŋŋma ya ningbina luga zaa n‑tiri Naawunni ka a ti leebi u yɛlimaŋni tuma neema, dama ya nyɛ la seeba ban pun neaai kum ni.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Ti mi halli, ni, zaaligu la nyɛ la seaa kan dɔlisi ni siiya; amaa man mun nyɛ duniya wa ni ninsaala, ka ba koosiri ma ni hoo daabri n‑ti Ala'akyi.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Dinŋɔna Ala'akyi ku laa n‑nyɛ ya dugma, dama zaaligu maa bu su ya yɛla, amaa ka Naawunni piinisuma tuma n su ya.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Halli n ku toaai m‑baŋŋi man tumni seelli. Dama m bu tumni man boori tunseesi. Ay! - tunseesi man bu boori la zaa ka n tumna.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Bɔ yaasa? Zaaligu bu su ti, na'ala Naawunni piini n su ti: ammi, ti tumma tumbeeri lala zugu? - Ay! pa lala.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Lala zugu, n yi tum taaliseaa ka yɛli, ni, m bu boori lala, n yɛttɔ'a wa wulisiya, ni, zaaligu la veela, ka n sakki lala.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Ya bu mi daaba yɛla ŋɔn bɛ seem? Ya yi zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑ti sɔ ni ya leebi u daaba ka wumni u noori, ŋɔnŋɔ n leebi ya dugma ka ya kur nyɛ la yan deaai sɔ noori wa daaba. Sɔya ayi m bɛ ni#:- bee, ya nin deaai Tumbeoo noori nti nya kum; bee, Naawunni noori wumpu soori ka ya nin deaai nti paai yɛlimaŋni zaaziiya.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Ka dinŋɔ pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m bɛ n saani n‑tumna.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Amaa ti puusiri Naawunni barika! Dama ni ya daa bɛ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni n‑wumni Tumbeoo yɛligu hoo daaba maa zaa, ya daa wum yɛlimaŋni mooligu la ka doaai n‑takki ya sufu ni teesa ka sakki n‑deaai wulisigu maa.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: M mi, ni, bunsunseelli ka m beesigu ni, halli beela - di nyɛ la n duniya wa ni ningbina la ni beesigu ni. M boori tunsuma tumpu, amaa n kɔŋŋi la a tumpu.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: Ka ya daa laa n‑yi Ala'akyi suulinsi ni na, ka leebi Yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn su seeba.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: M boori la tunsuŋŋu tumpu n‑kɔŋŋi, ka laa n‑tumni tumbɛseelli man bu boori maa.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: Tɔ, n tɔ'asiri la ninsaala duniya wa puuni beesigu yɛla n‑tiri ya, dama a gbinni bamsim bu tɔa; ka yan bɛ la duniya wa puuni la zugu n kyɛ ka ya na ka yaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlizulima yɛla. Ya daa kyɛ ka ya ningbina luga zaa leebi hoo daaba n‑ti da'ari tuma ni zaaligu za'asigu. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya zaŋŋi ya ningbina n‑leebi la daaba n‑ti Naawunni yɛlimaŋni la, n‑tumni tunsuma kasikasi.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: N yi tum taaliseaa ka yɛli, ni, m bu boori lala, dinŋɔ pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m bɛ n saani n‑tumna.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Yan daa bɛ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni hoo daaba sahanseaa la, Yɛlimaŋni maa daa bu su ya fukumsi.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: N yi baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni nŋɔa: ni, n yi kur boori ni n ti tumni din veela, lala saha maa ka tumbeoo tumpu bɛ n saani.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Ka anfaani bɔ ka ya daa nya di zugu? Ay, zaalim! Lala tumbeeri maa zugu daa kur kyɛ ka ya di vi, ka yɛla wa zaa naari nyɛ la kum.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Dama n sufu ni teesa zaa kur sakkri ni n‑tiri Naawunni zaaligu tuma.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba fa ya m‑basi n‑yiisi Ala'akyi fukumsi ni na, ni ya leebi Naawunni suulinsi niriba. Dinŋɔ zugu ka ya nyari anfaani pam ka leebi la Naawunni dinni, din mun naari nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: amaa m baŋŋiya, ni, zaaliseaa n bɛ n ningbiŋŋu luga ni. Ka lala zaalibeoo tuusiri tɔppu n‑niŋni n sufu ni teesa zaalisuŋŋu la, ka nyaŋŋi ma n‑gbaai ma nti kpari n ningbiŋŋu luga ni teesibeoo ziiya.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Dama Ala'akyi yoori yoori n‑tiri ka tuntumniba, ka lala yoori nyɛ la kum. Amaa Naawunni mun tiri la u piini yɔriyɔri, ka lala piini nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga, Kirisita Yisa ti Dugma saani.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Waay, waay, waay! Man wahala daana wa! Ŋɔni n lee n‑ya fa ma m‑basi, n‑yiisi ma duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu wa ni, din taasiri ma kum ni?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Ay! M pɛ'ari Naawunni ka puusiri u barika, dama ŋɔn pun fa ma m‑basi, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ti Dugma zugu. Ah, tɔ! M mammaŋŋa sufu ni teesa puuni ka n dɔli la Naawunni zaaligu sɔsuŋŋu, amaa n duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu puuni ka n na dɔli la Ala'akyi sɔbeoo.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Man ŋɔn ya tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, man ŋɔn bɛ Kirisita saani maa zugu; m bu ma'ari ziri, halli Naawunni Sii Kasi kur kyɛ ka n sufu ni teesa wulisiri lala gba.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Ni, m mari la sufusa'aŋŋu pam, ni beerim din bu naara; ka di zaa nyɛ la m mabiisi, Yɛhudiyadima maa, zugu. Dama di yi naa n‑suŋŋi bana, man naa n‑sakki, ni, Naawunni ti za'asi ma, ka kari ma Kirisita saani.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Ba nyɛ la Izraɛli dima: Naawunni daa deaai ba hoo u biisi ka wulisi ba u gyirima veelim; u daa pɔ noori n‑ti ba nangbanyinni zugu, ka daa zaali ba u zaaligu, ni ba wungyeeŋŋu soori yɛla; ka u daa ti ba noori zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsuma tisibu.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Ban su kurimni sa yaadima yɛla; halli ba butiri maa ni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u ninsaala ningbina yɛla maa, ka Kirisita Masiiya daa yi na. Ka ŋɔn mun nyɛ la Naawunni, ka u gaari bunseelli kam zaa, ka ba pɛ'ari u ka gyeemni u saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi .

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: Amaa di pa la kaman Naawunni yɛttɔ'a luya. Dama di pa la ban nya ba dɔ'am Izraɛli ni la zaa n nyɛ Izraɛli niriba ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Pa ba zaa n nyɛ Ibrahim biisi, kulla ban yi u butiri la ni zugu. Ay! - ni, Yisahaaku saani ka i biisi nin yi na.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Dinŋɔ n wulisi, ni, di pa la ban dɔ'ai seeba zaa n nyɛ Naawunni biisi maa, amaa ban dɔli la Naawunni nangbambasiri soori n‑dɔ'ai seeba la n nyɛ biisi ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Dama nŋɔ ka Naawunni nangbambasiri maa daa yɛli#:- Nyama! Di yi ti paai yuumni n nin labi na, ka lala saha maa ka Zaraatu ti ya mari u biiya. - Naawunni yɛligu deede nla.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Ka di pa la lala kɔkɔa, amaa Rɛbɛkka mun daa mari doo yinni puuwa - di nyɛ la: ti yaaba Yisahaaku dinni.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Amaa u daa na bu dɔ'ai u gyeesi maa, ka di zugu u biisi wa na bu tum tunseelli zaa - bunsuŋŋu, bee, bumbeoo - ka lala saha ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, Bikpeema la nin ti gyeemni u tunzɔ. U daa niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni yuubu ti zɛ suŋŋa, dama u boori ni u ninvuggamsa yiiri na, pa ba tuma zugu, amaa ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn boonni ba la zugu.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Yaakubu ka n daa wura, Iisɔ mun ka n daa yaai.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Wula ka ti ya yɛli yaasa? Ni, Naawunni daa bu niŋi din tuusi, bee? - Ay! di ku toaai m‑bɛ lala.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Dama u daa yɛli Musa, ni#:- Mani n mari namboo n‑tiri man ya zɔri seeba namboo la; Ka niŋi sufubaalli n‑tiri n sufu ŋɔn baali n‑tiri seeba la.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Dizugu di zaa pa la ninsaala yuubu, bee, ninsaala ŋɔn moori n‑tumni seem la zugu, amaa di bɛ la Naawunni nambɔzoaa maa zugu.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Dama Litaafi maa yɛli n‑ti NaaFirawuna, ni, Mani n kyɛ ka i doaai na ni n ti zaŋŋi i n‑wulisi n yiiko ŋɔn nyɛ seelli maa, ka kyɛ ka n yuuri du duniya wa zaa ni.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Dinzugu u siri mari namboo n‑tiri ninvugseeba, hoo u yuubu ŋɔn bɛ seem la; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka seeba mari tuppuwumni, ka di gba dɔli la u yuubu.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Tɔ: di pa seem ya ninvugsɔ nin ti yɛli ma, ni, Ka dinŋɔna, di niŋi la wula ka Naawunni kpalim n‑nyari ti galinsi? Dama sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑ta'ai ŋɔn Naawunni yuubu ŋɔn boori seem.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Tɔ, ka n ya labsi u, ni, Eeh, n zɔa! Ka nyini n nyɛ ŋɔni? Nyin ninsaala nin boosiri Naawunni boosa, bee wula? Dukku ku toaai n‑doaai m‑boosi ŋɔn mɛ ka la, ni, Bɔ zugu ka i mɛ ma lala?: bee m ma'ai ni.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Ammi, dugmeera ka soori ni u zaŋŋi ya'ari m‑mɛ ŋɔn boori a seem, bee? Ay! - u nin toaai n‑gbeaai ya'ari maa m‑mɛ laveelli, ka laa n‑gbeaai bɛni m‑mɛ laseaa ban zaŋŋi n‑tumni da'ari tuma.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Di bu ya'ai ka Naawunni yi boori u ti wulisi u yiiko, ni u sugyeaa, ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ka dizugu u daa niŋi sugru pam n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban ŋmanni la da'ari tuma laasi, seesi ban daa maali ni a ti sa'am m‑basi, ŋɔn Naawunni sugyeaa zugu.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Ka di bu laa n‑ya'ai ka u boori u ti wulisi u gyirima ŋɔn veeli pam seem. Ka dizugu u daa niŋi lala n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban ŋmanni la veelim tuma laasi la, ka u zɔ ba namboo, dama u daa daŋŋi m‑maali ba ni ba ti nya gyirima.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Halli tirima gba ka u daa booli ti, ni ti nya lala gyirima; pa Yɛhudiyadima la kɔkɔa, amaa ka ban nyɛ buriseesi niriba la m‑paasi.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Hoo din sɔbi Hɔseaa bukku maa ni, ni#:- Ban daa pa n niriba la, N nin booli ba n niriba. Man daa bu wuri sɔ la, N nin booli u, ni, n wurilim nira.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Ka ya'aseaa ban daa yɛli ba, Ni, Ya pa la n niriba. la, Bɛni ka ba nin boonni ba, Ni, Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana biisi. .

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Ka Aizaaya mun daa kaasi n‑yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Izraɛli dima yɛla, ni, Halli Izraɛli niriba yi ti zoaai hoo teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu gba, di zaa yɔri, ba beela nin ti kpalim n‑nya tuliginsim.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Dama ti Dugma maa nin kari u sariya m‑basi duniya ni na, ka din ya naai la yooma.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Hoo Aizaaya ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yɛli seem la, ni#:- Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa yi daa bu kyɛ biisi wa Ni ba kpalim n‑ti ti, Di daa naa n‑niŋi ti hoo Sɔdoom ni Gɔmɔra - Tinseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa sa'am maa.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Ka ti ya yɛli la wula yaasa? Ni, ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa bu boori Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ziiya, amaa ba daa nya ka. Yɛdda zugu yɛlimaŋni beebu ziiya ka ba daa paai.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Ka Izraɛli dima mun daa dɔli zaaligu soori m‑bɔa, nti gyɛa.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Wula zugu n‑daa la? - Dama ba daa bu dɔli yɛdda soori, amaa zaaligu tuma tumpu dinni ka ba daa dɔla. Ba daa tuui n‑lu kugri maa zugu.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni#:- Ya nyama! N doonni la kugri, Buntitaari, n‑niŋni Zayɔn tiŋŋa ni. Kugseaa kan nin kyɛ ka niriba tuui n nyɛ ka, Ni tampiiya kan kyɛ ka ba lu tiŋŋa. Amaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda muna - Ŋɔna n ku di vi, halli beela.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Dinŋɔna ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑kari Kirisita Yisa niriba sariya n‑lusi ba, halli beela.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Dama zaalipaalli bɛ la Yisa Kirisita saani; Naawunni Sii maa zaaligu n nyɛ ka, ka ka tiri nyɔvuri. Zaaligu wa n kyɛ ka i yi zaalikurigu la suulinsi ni na - Ala'akyi ŋɔn su zaaliseaa maa nla, kan tiri kum.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Ban daa sɔbi zaaligu seaa n‑dooli la muna, di daa kɔŋŋi yiiko, duniya wa ni ninsaalidima ningbina ŋɔn boori tumbeoo maa zugu. Amaa Naawunni mammaŋŋa daa zaŋŋi u yiiko n‑tumna. U daa kyɛ ka u toori Biiya kyɛ ti saani na nti leebi ŋɔn mari duniya wa ni ningbina, hoo tumbeeri dima ŋɔn mari seem la - halli tumbeeri zugu ka u daa kyɛ na gba. Lala ka Naawunni daa nyaŋŋi n‑kari sariya n‑lusi Ala'akyi; dama duniya wa ni ningbina maa ni ka tumbeeri bɛ ni n‑tumna.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Di zaa gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: ni, Naawunni zaaligu ŋɔn ya zaali ti yɛlimaŋni maa, lala yɛlimaŋni naayi bɛ ti beesigu ni zaa - tiseeba ban bu dɔli ningbina kɔribeeri sɔkura la, amaa sii maa sɔsuŋŋu, bumpaalli, ka ti dɔla.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Dama seeba ban bɛ duniya wa ningbinkɔrisi ni la, ba zikki zaa bɛ ningbinkɔrisi yɛla ni; amaa seeba ban bɛ sii maa beesisuŋŋu ni la, ban mun zikki kur bɛ sisuŋŋu yɛla ni.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Ningbinkɔrisi teesa tiri la kum, amaa sii maa beesigu teesa tisiri la nyɔvuri ni sufudoaa.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Duniya wa ni ningbinkɔrisi teesa kur zaŋŋi la Naawunni dimni; a bu sakkri n‑tiri Naawunni zaaligu - ka mun pun ku toaai n‑sakki lala.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Lala zugu ka ninvugseeba ban beesigu dɔli ningbinkɔrisi soori la ku toaai n‑tigi Naawunni nini.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Yarima muna - duniya wa ningbinkɔrisi bu su ya, amaa Sii maa beesisuŋŋu ni ka ya bɛ ni. Halli sɔ yi ka Kirisita Siiya, u pa la Yisa dinni gba.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Ya duniya wa ni ningbina muna - kum su a, tumbeeri zugu. Amaa di zaa yɔri, Kirisita yi bɛ ya ni, ya siisi mari la nyɔvuri, Naawunni ŋɔn deaai ya hoo yɛlimaŋni dima maa zugu.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Naawunni siri daa yisigi Yisa kum ni. Dizugu ŋɔn Naawunni Sii maa yi bɛ ya ni, u nin kyɛ ka ya gba ningbina nin ti yisigi kum ni - halli ni a kpiiri ni gba - hoo ŋɔn daa yisigi Yisa seem la; ka di nyɛ la u Sii maa ŋɔn bɛ ya ni la zugu.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Dizugu, m mabiisi, di ŋmanni la hoo ti diri samni seem la. Amaa pa duniya wa ningbiŋŋu samni ka ti di, ni ti ti dɔli ningbinkɔrisi beesigu yɛlibeeri;.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: dama ya yi ti dɔli ningbina beesibeoo soori ya nin kpi. Amaa ya beesigu yi dɔli la Sii maa soori, lala yi kyɛ ka ningbinkɔrisi kpi, ka yarima mun nin neaai.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Dama sɔkam ŋɔn dɔli Sii maa beesigu la nyɛ la Naawunni biiya.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Yan daa deaai Sii maa fukumsi maa, di pa la sisɔ ŋɔn nin kyɛ ka ya laa n‑leebi daaba, ni ya zɔri dabeem di zugu. Amaa yan daa deaai Sii la zugu, lala yi kyɛ ka ya leebri Naawunni biisi: ka ŋɔn Sii Kasi maa zugu ka ti tasiri n‑yɛtti, Abba ! - ka di gbinni nyɛ la, M Ba.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Halli Sii maa mammaŋŋa paasiri la ti toori siisi zugu n‑wulisiri, ni, ti siri nyɛ la Naawunni biisi.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Tɔ, ka ti yi nyɛ la Naawunni biisi, ti laa n‑nyɛ la u faari dittiba. Ti nin di faari Naawunni saani, halli tin ni Kirisita nin la'am n‑di ka. Tin ni ŋɔn yi siri ti la'am n‑di u wahala, dinŋɔna ti ya la'amni n‑nya u gyirima gba.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Man ŋɔn nya seem maa nŋɔa#:- pampaŋŋɔ namsigu wa ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni gyirima veelim seaa ban nin kasigi n‑ti ti la.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Dama Naawunni daa naam duniya zaa, ka ka na bɛ ni n‑guusiri sahanseaa ŋɔn ya yiisi u biisi paalu ni na maa.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Dama duniya wa zaa ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑kpeesi yɛttɔ'ayɔŋŋu ni, ka di pa la duniya yuubu zugu, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali lala maa zugu. Dama u daa mari tama.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: ni, duniya wa zaa nin nya fa-m‑basi ka ku laa m‑bɛ sa'aŋŋu suulinsi ni; di saha ka ka nin paai mansuulinsi ni gyirima ziiya, hoo Naawunni biisi zaa ŋɔn nin ti mari seem la.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Dama ti mi halli, ni, duniya wa zaa nyuusiri ni ka diri la wahala hoo pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn bɛ dɔ'am wahala maa ni ŋɔn nyuusiri seem la. Ka di bɛ la lala halli ni zuna.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Ka di pa la duniya wa kɔkɔa, amaa ti mammansi gba, tirim ban daŋŋi n‑deaai Sii maa, ti gba nyuusiri ni ti sufu ni n‑guusiri sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn nin deaai ti hoo u biisi, ka fa ti ningbina m‑basi la.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Dama ti pun deaai tuliginsim, ka di nyɛ la tin mari lala tama maa ziiya. Tɔ, ka ti nini yi pun nya bunseelli, lala pa la tama marigu; dama sɔsɔ ku laa n‑yɛli, ni, u mari tama, ka mun pun nya.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Amaa ti yi mari tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti nini ŋɔn bu nyari bunseaa, ti nin guri di la ni sugru.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Lala ka Sii maa suŋŋni ti, tin ka yaa maa zugu. Dama ti bu mi din da sum ni ti susi Naawunni seela, amaa Sii maa zɔri ti sindaani n‑tiri ti ni nimmoaa, halli ka sɔ ku toaai n‑yɛli u nyuusigu gbinni zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem gba.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Naawunni mun viisiri m‑baŋŋi din bɛ ninsaalidima sufu ni, ka laa m‑baŋŋi din bɛ Sii maa teesa ni, dama Sii maa zɔri la Naawunni niriba sindaani n‑tiri ba, ka ŋɔn niŋni seem maa dɔli la Naawunni yuubu soori.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Ti mi halli, ni, seelli kam zaa ni, Naawunni suŋŋni la ninvugseeba ban wuri u maa - di nyɛ la: baseeba ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑pii maa, u yuubu la zugu.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Dama u siri daa daŋŋi m‑mi u niriba, ka laa n‑zaali ba, ni ba ti ŋmanni u Bii la. Ka lala kyɛ ka u Bii maa nin nyɛ Zuuwa u babiisi pam saani.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑zaali seeba lala maa, ban ka u daa yiisi; ka daa yiisi ba, n‑deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima. Ka ŋɔn daa deaai ba lala maa, u daa laa n‑ti ba gyirima.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna wula ka ti ya yɛli di zugu? Naawunni yi bɛ ti saani, ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑zabri ti? Ay! sɔ ka ni.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Ŋɔna ŋɔn daa bu za'asi, ni, u toori Bii ku kpi, amaa u daa zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ti zaa zugu; ka dinŋɔna, wula zugu ka u ku ti ti bunsuma kam zaa m‑paasi u zugu? - U ku toaai m‑mɔŋŋi.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Ŋɔni n lee n‑nya galinsi ni u ti tɔ'asi n‑galim Naawunni ninvuggamsa maa? - ay! sɔ ka ni. Dama Naawunni pun deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Bee, ŋɔni n nin lee n‑kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba? Dama di nyɛ la Kirisita ŋɔn daa kpi, ka din gaari lala, u nyɛ la ŋɔn yisigi kum ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ziya Naawunni nudirigu ya'ari la, ka laa n‑zɔri ti sindaani Naawunni saani.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Ka ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑wɔligi tin ni Kirisita wurilim? Daaŋŋu bee? - bee mugsigu? Ban tumni ti putoo nin niŋi? Kɔm ŋɔn ti lu, bee, ti ti kɔŋŋi kyinkyina? Dabeem yɛla nin niŋi, bee tɔppu?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: - Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Nyin, Naawunni, zugu ka ba kuuri ti, Beoo kam zaa ka ba kuuri ti. Dizugu ti ŋmanni la peesi - Peesi ban ya kɔri seeba.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: - Ay! Anŋɔ zaa puuni ti nin nya nyaŋŋni, Yisa ŋɔn wuri ti maa zugu.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Dama n niŋi yɛdda, ni, seelli ku toaai n‑wɔligi tin ni Naawunni wurilim#:- kum ku toaai n‑niŋi, ka nyɔvuri mun bu ya niŋi. Pa malaakadima, pa yiiko daandima. Bunseesi an bɛ duniya wa ni pampaŋŋɔ ku toaai n‑niŋi lala, halli an naayi ya kyɛ na gba. Bunseesi an bɛ saazugu, bee, bunseesi an bɛ bɔ'azuliŋŋu ni ku toaai. Halli bunseelli kam zaa kan bɛ la duniya wa ni m‑paasi - seelli ku toaai n‑wɔligi tin ni Naawunni wurilim din bɛ ti Dugma Kirisita Yisa Masiiya saani maa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: M mabiisi, di nyɛ la n sufu yuubu ni n Yɛhudiyataaba ti nya tuliginsim; lala zugu ka m puusiri Naawunni n‑tiri ba sahakam.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: M mi halli, ka yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, ba boori la Naawunni yɛla ni nimmoosi, amaa ba boobu maa mun bu dɔli bamsim soori.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Dama ba bu mi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori, ka di zugu ba boori la ba zaali ba mammaŋŋa yɛlimaŋni soori. Lala zugu ka ba daa za'asi Naawunni soori ka bu paai ya'aseaa Naawunni ŋɔn naa n‑deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Kirisita Masiiya nyɛ la zaaligu la gbinni, ni kan wa saala gba. U kyɛ ka seeba ban niŋi u yɛdda maa zaa yi paalu ni, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Musa daa sɔbi zaaligu la yɛlimaŋni soori yɛla n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔnkam tumni zaaligu tuma maa zaasa nin nya nyɔvuri di zugu.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlimaŋni din yi yɛdda niŋbu ni na, ni, Di birisiri i sufu ni, ni, Ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑du saazugu m‑paai arizana ni?, - di nyɛ la: ni, u du m‑paai bɛni nti zaŋŋi Kirisita n‑sigi na -.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: bee, Ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑sigi n‑kpɛ bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa ni?; - di nyɛ la: n‑zaŋŋi Kirisita n‑duusi n‑yi kum ni na.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Ka di yɛli la wula? - ni#:- Yɛligu maa bu wɔ'a ya saani, Halli ka bɛ la ya nɔya ni, Ka bɛ la ya sufu ni gba. - di nyɛ la yɛligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛdda niŋbu, yɛliseaa tirima ŋɔn moonni maa.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Ni, ya nɔya, dama i yaari la i noori n‑sakki, ni, Yisa nyɛ la i Dugma; ni, ya sufu, dama i niŋni i sufu ni yɛdda, ni, Naawunni daa neaai Yisa kum ni. Ka anŋɔ abayi zaa zugu ka ya nin nya tuliginsim.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Dama nira niŋni yɛdda u sufu ni, ka di zugu Naawunni deaai u, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana. Ka u laa n‑yaai u noori n‑sakki n‑wulisi paalu ni, ka nya tuliginsim di zugu.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, Ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda la muna - ŋɔna n ku di vi halli beela.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Kpa wa ka wɔligri ka ni - Yɛhudiyadima ku bɛ ba kɔkɔa ka kyɛ Griika dima. Naawunni nyɛ la ba zaa Dugma, ka ŋɔn mun zaŋŋi bunsunseelli kam n‑tiri baseeba ban mi u yuuri n‑susiri u maa.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Ni, Ŋɔnkam mi ti Dugma yuuri ka susiri u la nin nya tuliginsim.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑susiri u, ka bu daŋŋi n‑niŋi u yɛdda? Bee, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑niŋi u yɛdda, ka na bu wum u yɛla? Ka ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑wum u yɛla, ka di pa la sɔ pun mooli u lahabaari maa n‑ti ba?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ban moonni maa muna - ba ku toaai m‑mooli, see ka sɔ yi tum ba ni ba ti niŋi lala. Hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni, Di kur niŋni i la nya'asim ni i ti nya seeba ban mari lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑kyɛnni na.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Amaa pa sɔkam zaa n daa sakki n‑ti lahabasuŋŋu maa; hoo Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la, ni, Ti Dugma, ŋɔni n niŋi ti kukɔri la yɛdda? - Ay, sɔ zaa bu niŋi.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Dinŋɔna yɛdda niŋbu yiiri la mooligu maa wumpu ni, ka mooligu wumpu mun yiiri la Kirisita yɛligu ni.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yɛhudiyadima maa yɛla ka m boosiya, ni, Ba siri daa bu wum, bee bɔa? - Ay! Dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni#:- Ba kukɔya gyili duniya lugri kam zaa, Ka ba yɛligu paai tingbaŋŋu zaa tɔriga.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Dizugu n laa n‑ya m‑boosi, ni, Izraɛli dima siri daa bu baŋŋi? Ay! - Halli Musa daa daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- N nin zaŋŋi ban pa butiri ni yɛlimaŋni n‑kyɛ ka ya zabi ba nyuuri: Ka laa n‑zaŋŋi ninvugseeba ban kɔŋŋi yɛm n‑kyɛ ka ya suuri yisigi.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Ka Aizaaya mun daa niŋi sufukpeemni n‑gaari lala, halli ka u daa ti yɛli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- Seeba ban daa bu boori n zii la - Ban ka n daa kyɛ ka ba nya ma: Ka daa zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti seeba ban bu boosi n yɛla la.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Ka tin na kpalim n‑viisiri anabi Aizaaya yɛla maa, u daa laa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti Izraɛli dima, ni, Beoo kam ka n daa teaai n nuusi n‑ti n niriba, amaa ba daa nyɛ tuppuwumni dima ni zabbooriba.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Ka dinŋɔna m boosiri ya, ni, ammi Naawunni za'asi u niriba m‑basi, bee? - Ay! di pa lala. M mammaŋŋa nyɛ la Izraɛli nira, Ibrahim tuŋŋu ni ka n yi na, halli Bɛngyamin za'anɔri bii n nyɛ ma.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Naawunni bu za'asi u niriba, seeba ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑baŋŋi la. Ammi, ya bu mi Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli Ilaygya yɛla seem la? - Ni, ŋɔn Ilaygya daa susiri Naawunni n‑tuusiri Izraɛli dima seem maa.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: ni, N Dugma, ba daa ku i anabidima la, ba mun daa sa'am i gyeeŋŋu ziisi la, ka man kɔkɔ n kpalim, halli ka ba boori ni ba ku ma gba.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Ka Naawunni daa labsi u la wula? - ni, N pun kyɛ ka niriba tusa ayɔpɔi n kpalim m‑bɛ ni, baseeba na zi n‑gbaanni Ba'ali maa.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Tɔ - ka lala gba zugu ka pampaŋŋɔ ninvugyiisa kpalimiya - kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi piini maa ŋɔn yiisi ninvugseeba la nla.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Ka di yi nyɛ la piini maa zugu ŋɔna, di bu laa n‑dɔli la tunsuma tumpu. Di yi pa lala, kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi piini maa naa n‑ku nyɛ piini.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Bɔ yaasa? Izraɛli dima daa bu nya ban daa boori bunseelli nimmoaa la, amaa ninvugyiisa maa daa nya di. Ban kpalim maa mun daa mari tuppuwumni.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: hoo din daa sɔbi seem la, ni#:- Naawunni daa ti ba sibugsiga - U kyɛ ka ba nini ku nya, U kyɛ ka ba tuba ku wum, Halli nti paai zuna.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Ka NaaDauda gba daa yɛli, ni#:- Ba la'amni n‑diri diibu pam - Kyɛ ka di leebi la bɛriga n‑gbaasi ba! Di leebi la kyɛ'ariga n‑gba ba, Ni bɔ'azuliŋŋu ka ba lu n‑sigi ka puuni. - Lala nin yɔ ba samni n‑ti ba.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Kyɛ ka ba nini zibgi Ka ba ku toaai n‑nyari, halli beela! Kyɛ ka ba kuntiri ni n‑ziiri zitibsa halli saha kan ka tɔriga.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Dizugu m boosiri ya, ni: ammi, ba tuya ni ba ti lu tiŋŋa? - Ay! di pa lala. Amaa di nyɛ la ban burigri la zugu ka fa-m‑basi paai ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani na, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ban Yɛhudiyadima maa ti zabri nyuuri.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Dizugu, ba burigri maa kyɛ ka bunsuma paari la duniya wa dima maa zaa, halli ba pɔsigri zugu ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima leebi hoo bundaandima. Tɔ, ka di yi bɛ lala, Yɛhudiyadima zaa ŋɔn ti la'am m‑paasi maa, di ya niŋi la wula? - di siri nin veela n‑gaari zaa!.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Tɔ: ka pampaŋŋɔ n ya tɔ'asiri n‑tiri yaseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la. Ka man ŋɔn nyɛ la tuumu n‑ti yan ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa, n nin tum n tuma maa zaa ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: N teesiri, ni, di pa seem n Yɛhudiyataaba nin ti zabi nyuuri, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba ninvugseeba ti nya fa-m‑basi.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Dama sahanseaa ka Naawunni daa za'asi ba la, lala zugu daa kyɛ ka u ti la'asi duniya dima zaa na. Ka di yi bɛ lala, u yi laa n‑deaari ba na, di ya bɛ la wula? - Pa nyɔvuri n‑yi kum ni na nla?.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Ba yi daŋŋi n‑gbeaai maasibeeri beela nti zaŋŋi n‑tisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi kyɛ ka maasa maa zaa nyɛ la Naawunni dinni. Ba yi zaŋŋi tinya'ari n‑tisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi kyɛ ka timunni maa nyɛ la u dinni.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Di ŋmanni la hoo ban ŋmaai tiseaa kan wanni wula m‑basi ka zaŋŋi moo ni tiwulli n‑tugi n‑tabli. Lala ka ba zaŋŋi i n‑kpeesi n‑tabli Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni. Ka tiwulipaalli wa deaari la sum ni bunsuma zaa an yiiri tii maa nya'a ni na.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Ka di yi bɛ lala, nyin bumpaalli ku toaai n‑nyu bara n‑zeemni bunkura la. I yi boori i nyu bara, kur teaama, ni, di pa la nyini m‑mari tii la nya'a maa, amaa nya'a maa n mari i.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Di pa seem i ya yɛli, ni, ba daa ŋmaai wulikura maa m‑basi ni ba ti tugi i n‑tabli.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Tɔ, yɛlimaŋni nla. Lala wula maa ka ba daa ŋmaai m‑basi a yɛdda kaalinsi maa zugu; ka nyini ka ba daa tugi n‑tabli, i yɛdda niŋbu zugu. Dizugu nyin di mari karimbaani, see ka i zɔri dabeem.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: dama Naawunni daa bu niŋi sugru n‑ti wulimaŋa la, ka u ku ti di sugru n‑ti nyini gba.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: I bu nya Naawunni ŋɔn zɔri namboo seem? - ka laa n‑darigri tuba gba. U darigri ban lu la tuba, amaa ka zɔ nyini namboo, i yi ti kpalim m‑bɛ u nambɔzoaa maa ni. Di yi pa lala, u nin ŋmaai i gba m‑basi.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Ka banla gba yi ti kyɛ ba yɛdda za'asigu, ba nin laa n‑tugi n‑kpeesi yaasa, dama Naawunni mari la yiiko ni u ti niŋi lala.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: I bu nya? U daa pun zaŋŋi nyini, sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ moo ni tii maa wulli, ni u ti takki i n‑tugi n‑kpɛ tisuŋŋu ni n‑tabli. Tɔ: lala n nyɛ di; ka dinŋɔna labsim ma: ammi, i teesiri, ni, u ku toaai n‑zaŋŋi tisuŋŋu wulimaŋa la ka laa n‑tugi n‑tabli? U nin toaai, walla!.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
