local: M mabiisi, yɛlisɔ'ara nŋɔa, amaa m bu boori ni ya kɔŋŋi a bamsim, ka naayi zaŋŋi ya toori yɛm m‑boori a gbinni. Kulla Izraɛli dimseeba daa mari zugkpeeŋŋu ni tuppuwumni, see ka ban pa Izraɛli dima ti kpɛ na ka ba kaalli maa ti paai.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Ka di naayi kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima zaa ti nya tuliginsim hoo din daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Tuligra maa nin yi Zayɔn na; U nin yiisi din bu tuusi zaa m‑basi, Ka kyɛ ka Yaakubu dima ti leebi kasi zaa.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Lala ka n ti ba noori, n‑zaali nangbanyinni zaaligu n‑ti ba: Di saha ka n nin kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ba.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Ban saani ka di kur bɛ la lala: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla, ban ni Naawunni mari la dimni, ka di nyɛ la yarima zugu. Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni yiisigu piini zugu muna, ba mari la wurilim, ka di nyɛ la ba yaadima zugu.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Dama Naawunni yi ti ya zaŋŋi u fa-m‑basi piini n‑tisi seeba, bee, u yi ti booli seeba u saani na, u bu ya takki u lala teesa maa.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Yarima daa bu wum n‑ti Naawunni, amaa ka yi nya u namboo pampaŋŋɔa, ka di nyɛ la ban ŋɔn bu sakkri Naawunni wumpu zugu.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu ban bu wumni n‑tiri Naawunni pampaŋŋɔa ka di nyɛ la namboo din kyɛ ya saani na zugu. Ka dinŋɔna di gbinni nyɛ la: ba gba nin naayi nya nambɔzoaa.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Dama Naawunni kpeesi sɔkam tuppuwumni zii ni, hoo ban kpatti la tumbeeri dima dansarika duu ni seem la. Ka u niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa naayi nya u nambɔzoaa.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Naawunni bunsuma n zulima, yee! U katti la sariya ka di gbinni sɔ'aya; u sɔya zaa mun ka baŋŋni! Ni#:-.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Ka ŋɔni n na mi m‑baŋŋi Naawunni teesa gbinni? Bee, ŋɔni n na mi n‑kpaam u.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Ni#:- Halli ŋɔni n‑daŋŋi n‑ti Naawunni piini, Ni Naawunni ti labsi n‑ti u yaasa.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Dama bunseelikam zaa yi la u saani na, ka ŋɔn zugu ka a bɛ ni, ka a laa m‑paari u saani. Ŋɔna n sum ni pɛ'abu saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi .

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Dizugu n susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, Naawunni nambɔzoaa zugu, ni, ya zaŋŋma ya mansi m‑maali sara n‑ti Naawunni ka u nini tigi. Di nyɛ la: yan na nɛ wa, ya nin leebi Naawunni dinni, dama dina n nyɛ gyeeŋŋu maŋni n‑tiri u.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka duniya wa dima beesigu mugsi ya, ni ya gba bɛ la lala; amaa ya kyɛ ka ya beesigu takki n‑dɔli ya nini ŋɔn neaai n‑nya sɔpaalli la. Ya tumma Naawunni yuubu tuma nti baŋŋi a gbinni, ni, a veela ka laa n‑nyɛ bunsuma zaa ka Naawunni nini n tigri a.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Tɔ; Naawunni ŋɔn pun zaŋŋi u suŋŋsim piini n‑ti ma la zugu, n kpaamni ya zaa yinniyinni nŋɔa: ni, yan di gɔsi ya mammansi yɛla n‑teesi, ni, a veela, halli ka ya lala teesa gaari din siri nyɛ yɛlimaŋni maa. Ay! - Ya kyɛ ka ya teesa dɔli la yam maŋni, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛdda n‑ti ya sɔkam yinniyinni seem.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Ya nyama - ningbiŋŋu maa nyɛ la bummunni yinni, amaa tuga pam bɛ ka ni, ka a seelikam zaa mari la ka tumni, ka tumni.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Di nyɛ la lala n‑ti ti zaa: ti zoaaya halli, amaa ti nyɛ la hoo ningbimmunni, tin ŋɔn bɛ Yisa Kirisita ni la zugu. Ka ti zaa ŋmanni la u ningbiŋŋu tuga n‑suŋŋni taaba.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Ya bu nya? Naawunni pun zaŋŋi u piini n‑ti ti zaa, ka a nyɛ la kookooba. Sɔ yi nyɛ anabi, Naawunni piini maa zugu, ŋɔn tɔ'asima u yɛdda ŋɔn yi ti ti u, ni u ti yɛli seem la.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Sɔ mun yi gyeemni la niriba, Naawunni piini zugu, ŋɔn zɛ yummu u lala tuma maa: ka sɔ mun yi tumni karimba tuma, ŋɔn gba tumma ni nimmoaa.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Yaasa, sɔ yi kpamsiri niriba, di sum ni u kpamsiri ba suŋŋa; sɔ mun yi tɔtti tɔra, ŋɔn tumma ni u sufu zaa; sɔ mun yi su toondaana fukumsi, nimmoosi ka u tumni u tuma; halli sɔ yi ya suŋŋi faradima, ŋɔn mari sufupeelli n‑suŋŋi ba.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Kyɛ ka ya siri wuri la niriba yɛlimaŋni ni, ka di yɔmsira. Ya yaama Sintaani yɛla, ka gyi din nyɛ bunsuŋŋu.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi mabitirigu wurilim, see ka ya wuri taaba suŋŋa. Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gyirima mun tisibu, see ka ya gyeemni taaba nimmoosi.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya tuma ni, yan di mari zinnya'ari; zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya sufu ni yɛla, ya kyɛ ka ya nini moaai; ti Dugma ka ya gyeemni sahakam.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Yan mari tama maa zugu, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu maai; yan mun nya namsigu sahanseaa maa, ya kur mari sugru di puuni; ka yan susiri Naawunni maa, ya susima nimbirimoaa.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Ya kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa sakki n‑ti tinkpamma, ni ban su fukumsi la zaa. Dama kpamni ni fukumsi n ka ni m‑bu yi Naawunni saani na. Ŋɔn Naawunni pun zaali naam fukumsi dima an bɛ ni zaa.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Ya suŋŋma ya nyaandɔlitaaba ban bɛ wahala ni la, ka sɛ'asi n‑deaari saamma n‑kpeesiri ya yiya ni.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Ka ŋɔnkam za'asi fukumsi dima, di daama za'asi la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seela. Ka seeba ban za'asi ba la muna - ba sumsi la tubdarigri.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Ya susim bunsuma Naawunni saani n‑tiri seeba ban mugsiri ya: ii! - ya susim bunsuma, ka pa la bumbeoo n‑tiri ba.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ŋɔni n zɔri fukumsi dima dabeem? - Di pa la seeba ban tumni tunsuma la, amaa ban tumni tumbeeri la. I yi boori ni i bɛ ni ka ku zɔri fukumsi dima dabeem, nyin tumma tunsuma ka ba nin pɛ'ai i.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Baseeba ban mari sufupeelli maa, ya paasima ba zugu m‑mari sufupeelli; ban mun sufu sa'am, ya paasima ba zugu sufusa'aŋŋu ni.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Ŋɔn su naam fukumsi la nyɛ la Naawunni tuntumna, i suŋŋsim zugu. Amaa i yi ti tum din bu tuusi, nyin zɔma u, dama u bu mari naam yiiko maa yɔri; dama u nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali sɔ ni u ti zaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni sufuyisigri n‑darigri tumbeeri dima tuba.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Ya la'amma m‑bɛ ni taaba ni sugru. Yan di duusi ya mansi amaa ka sakki n‑tabli tarimma saani. Yan di boori ya mansi, bee, n‑yɛtti ni, yɛm daandima n nyɛ ya.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Dizugu di sum ni ti sakkri n‑tiri naam fukumsi dima, pa dabeem zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ti sufu ni teesa ŋɔn mi, ni, lala veela maa zugu.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Yan di tum tumbeeri n‑labsi sɔa, ŋɔn tum tumbeoo n‑niŋi ya la zugu. Ay! - ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑tumni tunseesi niriba zaa ŋɔn gɔsiri n‑yɛtti, ni, a veela.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Lala zugu gba ka ya yoori lampoo ni faarigu, dama ban deaari maa tumni n‑tiri Naawunni ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Ya kyɛ ka yan ni sɔkam mari la nangbanyinni, halli ya yi ti nyaŋŋi lala.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Halli ya tima sɔkam zaa din sum ni u deaai seem. Di yi zaani toosigu, ya toosima; ka di yi kpa ya ni ya yɔ lampoo bee faarigu, ya yooma; halli ŋɔnkam sum gyirima tisibu, yan tim u gyirima ni tibsiri.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: M mabiwutta, yan di yɔ sɔ tumbeeri samni, amaa ya kyɛ u yoobu yɛla n‑ti la Naawunni, dama ŋɔna n su tubdarigri yɛla zaa. Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni, labsigu n nyɛ u dinni, ni, ŋɔna nin labsi n‑yɔa.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Yan di di sɔ zaa samni. Sanseaa bu dɔli ya, di yi pa la, ni, ya wuri taaba; dama ŋɔnkam wuri u taraana pun dɔli la zaaligu zaa.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: N‑laa m‑paasi, ni, Kɔm yi mari i dindaana, nyin tim u diibu ka u di; kɔnyuuri mun yi mari u, nyin tim u koom: i yi niŋi nŋɔ di nin ŋmanni la i moaari la bugunsaala n‑ziinni u zugu.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ya mi zaaligudima la yɛla: ni, Di tum zina. ni, Di ku ninsaala. Di zu. ni, Di kyɛ ka i sufu kɔrisi kpɛ i kpeaa buna zugu. - halli ni an kpalim zaa m‑paasi: tɔ, a zaa bɛ la yɛlli yinni wa puuni, ni, Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Di kyɛ ka tumbeoo nyaŋŋi i; see ka i mari tunsuŋŋu n‑nyaŋŋi tumbeoo.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Wurilim daana ku toaai n‑sa'am u taraana yɛla, lala zugu ka wurilim lu n‑ti zaaligudima la zaa.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Tɔ, ya tumma anŋɔa, dama ya mi saha maa ŋɔn paai seem la. Saha paaya: yan neaam goom ni, dama ti tuliginsim saha maa ku laa n‑yuui hoo din daa yuui seem tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi Naawunni yɛdda sahanseaa la.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Yuŋŋu kyɛnni n‑gatta, ka beoo ya neaai. Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti za'asi tunseesi an tumni zibsim ni la zaa, ka zaŋŋi la neesim ni tuma hoo tɔbbiisi ŋɔn zaŋŋi ba neema ni ba ti tuusi tɔppu seem la.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Wuntaŋŋa ni ka ti bɛ ni, dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti beebu dɔli la soori, ka pa la dimaŋŋa ni daam nyuubu, bee, pɔ'agoorilim ni dɔgoorilim ni tumbeeri zaa, bee, zaba ni nyuuri zappu. Ay, ya kyɛl anŋɔ zaa m‑basi.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: ka zaŋŋi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya n‑niŋi ya beesigu ni, hoo nira ŋɔn yi yɛ u sutura seem la. Ka yan di dɔli ya ningbinkɔrisi yuubu tumbeeri maa, halli beela.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ni ka u yɛdda bu kpeem Naawunni dɔligu ni, yan deaam u ya saani, amaa yan di ŋmɛ u nangbankpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u kyisigu yɛla.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Dama seeba mari la yɛdda din kyɛ ka ba diri bunseelikam zaa; seeba mun yɛdda bu kpeem, ka ba diri la zɛvaari kɔkɔ di zugu.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑di bunseelikam zaa la di galim ŋɔn bu diri la, ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn diri zɛvaari kɔkɔ la gba di galim ŋɔnla, dama Naawunni deaai di daama gba.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: I nyɛ la ŋɔni n‑katti sɔ daabri sariya? Di ti nyɛ la ŋɔn su u maa n nin baŋŋi u nyaŋŋiya, bee, u luya. Halli u nin nyaŋŋi n‑zɛ yummu, dama u dugma nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka u nyaŋŋi.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: M‑paasi yaasa, seeba saani dabsiseesi veela n‑gaari taaba, ka seeba mun saani ka a zaa nyɛ la yimmu. Sɔkam zaa zaanma suŋŋa u toori teesa puuni.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Sɔ yi gamsiri dabsa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u niŋni lala; sɔ mun yi diri seelli zaa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u dira, dama u puusiri la Naawunni bundirigu zugu; halli sɔ yi bu sakki n‑diri bundiriseesi, Naawunni yuuri ka ŋɔn gba niŋni lala, ka ŋɔn gba puusiri la Naawunni u bundirigu zugu.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Dama ti sɔ ka ni m‑bɛ u toori zugu, sɔ mun ka ni n‑kpiiri u kɔkɔ zugu.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Ti yi bɛ ni, ti Dugma zugu ka ti bɛ ni; ti mun yi kpi, ti kpi la ti Dugma zugu. Dinzugu ti yi bɛ ni, bee, ti kpi ni, ti Dugma n su ti yɛla zaa.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Kirisita daa kpi ka laa n‑yisigi kum ni: ni, u leebi ban pun kpi, ni ban na bɛ ni maa zaa, Dugma.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: I nyɛ la ŋɔni? - nyin ŋɔn galimni i mabiiya n‑zeemni u lala? Ti zaa nin ti zaani Naawunni maa tooni ka u kari ti sariya.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Mani m bɛ ni, - Lala ka ti Dugma yɛli - Ka m pɔ la noori di zugu, ni, Sɔkam zaa nin gbaani n tooni, Sɔkam zaa nin baŋŋi ka sakki, ni, Mani n nyɛ Naawunni. .

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Dizugu ti sɔkam zaa nin tɔ'asi u toori beebu yɛttɔ'a n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Dinzugu ti di laa n‑galimni taaba, amaa ya kyɛ ka ti gɔsi tin nin niŋi seem ka ti mabiisi di burigi, bee, n‑lu tumbeeri ni.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Man ŋɔn bɛ Kirisita ni maa kyɛ ka man bu nya ka bundiriseaa kyisiri ka toori; amaa sɔ yi teesi u sufu ni, ni, ka kyisira, dinŋɔna ka siri kyisiri n‑ti di daama.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: I yi daamni i mabiiya nyin diri seelli zugu, dinŋɔna i bu tumni la wurilim zugu. Kirisita daa kpi u zugu, ka nyin di kyɛ ka i bundirigu diibu zugu ti sa'am u yɛla.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka bunsunseaa yan mari maa ti nya yubeoo.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Dama Naawunni naam maa pa la diibu, ni nyuubu; amaa naam maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni sufudoaa, ni nya'asim piiniseaa Naawunni Sii maa ŋɔn tisira.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Ka sɔ yi dɔli la soori wa n‑gyeemni Kirisita, Naawunni nini tigi di daama, ka u yuuri du ninsaalidima saani.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Lala zugu, ya kyɛ ka ti gɔsiri la bunseesi an nin mari sufudoaa ka kyɛ ka ti suŋŋni taaba ka kyɛnni tooni.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka bundirigu zugu ti sa'am Naawunni ŋɔn maali seelli. Bundiriseelli bu kyisira, amaa di bu veeli ni i di seelli ka di nin kyɛ ka i mabii maa lu tumbeoo ni.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Ti yi ŋɔbi numni, bee, n‑nyu daam, bee, anŋɔ taabu zaa, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ti mabii lu tumbeeri ni, dinŋɔna ti di ŋɔbi, bee, n‑nyu a, halli fiiŋ. Lala n sɔa.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Kur mari i yɛdda wa yɛla suŋŋa, nyin ni Naawunni saani. Zugsuŋŋu nin bɛ ni n‑ti ŋɔn baŋŋi u sufu ni, ni, ŋɔn niŋni seem wa ka galinsi.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Amaa ŋɔn birisiri ŋɔn di seelli maa zugu, ka naayi di, Naawunni nin galim di daama di zugu, dama u bu dɔli la u yɛdda soori. Ka seelli zaa din bu dɔli yɛdda nyɛ la tumbeoo.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Tɔ; ka dizugu di sumsi ni tirima ban mari yaa ti yɛdda zugu la ti mari sugru n‑tiri ban ka yaa la: see ka ti maari ba sufu, ka ti di boori ti maari ti mammansi sufu.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Sɔkam niŋma din nin kyɛ ka u taraana sufu maai ka u tumni tunsuma, ka kyɛnni tooni Naawunni saani.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Halli Kirisita gba daa bu maai la u mammaŋŋa sufu, amaa di daa bɛ la hoo din daa sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, Ban n daa boori la Naawunni tuubu la daa tu la mani.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Dama an daa pun sɔbi maa zaa daa sɔbi la ni di wulisi tirima, ni lala zugu ti kyɛ ka ti diri sugru, ka nya kpamsima Litaafi maa ziiya zugu nti mari tama.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Dama Naawunni siri su sugru ni kpamsigu yɛla, ka u nin kyɛ ka ya dɔli Yisa Kirisita ka kpalim m‑mari nangbanyinni ni taaba.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: Ka ya nin la'am n‑zaŋŋi nangbanyinni m‑pɛ'ai Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Dinzugu ya deaama taaba hoo Kirisita ŋɔn pun deaai ya seem la, Naawunni pɛ'abu zugu.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Dama n yɛtti ya, ni, Kirisita daa leebi la Yɛhudiyadima maa daabri ni u ti wulisi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni u ti zaali nangbambasiri maa deede - nangbambasiseesi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun basi n‑ti ti yaadima la.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: Ka laa n‑kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la mun pɛ'ari Naawunni u nambɔzoaa zugu, hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni#:- Dinzugu n nin pɛ'ai nyin Naawunni, N nin pɛ'ai i ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani. N nin yuumni n‑tiri i yuuri.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi yaasa, ni#:- Ya mari sufupeelli, yan pa Yɛhudiyadima wa, Ya paasim u niriba zugu m‑mara.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Yaasa, ni#:- Ya pɛ'ama ti Dugma, yan pa Yɛhudiyadima wa, Ka kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa tiri u gyirima.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Ka Aizaaya gba daa yɛli, ni#:- Gyɛsi tuŋŋu ni dɔsɔ nin ti bɛ ni n‑doaai na n‑su ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la naam; Ŋɔna zugu ka ba nin ti nya tama.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Naawunni siri su tama yɛla, ka n susiri u, ni, u kyɛ ka sufumaasim ni sufudoaa ti paali ya sufu, yan niŋni yɛdda maa zugu. Ka Sii Kasi la yiiko nin kyɛ ka ya mari tama pam halli nti yakki.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: M mabiisi, m mi halli, ni, ya mari yɛlisuma zaa, ka bamsim kur paali ya ni, ka ya nyaŋŋi n‑kpaamni taaba.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Amaa yɛttɔ'aseesi ziiya ka n niŋi sufukpeemni n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya na beela n‑teaari ya. Di yi la n yɛlimaŋni piini maa ni na, piiniseaa Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: U ti ma, ni, n niŋi Yisa Kirisita tuntumna ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa saani. N tuma mun nyɛ la Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu tuma, nti kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ti la'asi na ka Naawunni Sii Kasi kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ba la kasikasi. Ka tuma wa ŋmanni la ŋɔn maanni sara tuma, hoo ban la'asi la puri ni antaam na, m‑maali n‑ti Naawunni seem la.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Dizugu Yisa Kirisita zugu ka m mari soori ni n nyu bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni yɛla.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Dama n ku ma'asi ni n tɔ'asi seelli di yi pa la Kirisita ŋɔn zaŋŋi ma n‑tum tunseesi la; ka laa n‑yɛli yɛttɔ'aseesi la, ni di kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa ti sakki n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: U zaŋŋi lala tunkara ni alahazibu tuma yiiko n‑tum tuma wa n nuu ni, ni Sii Kasi yiiko. Ka di nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm zaŋŋi n‑gyiligi wa'aligu sa, halli nti paai Illirikum tingbaŋŋu ni, ka n mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla zaa.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Ya bu nya? - M ba'a kur bɛ la timpaala ni, ni n ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa luginseaa niriba ban na bu wum Kirisita yuuri la ni. Lala ka n ku meeri n‑tɔmni sɔ nyeebri zugu.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: Amaa di bɛ la hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- Ban na zi n‑wum u lahabaari la, Ban nin nya ka baŋŋi: Ban na zi n‑wum u yɛla maa, Ban nin baŋŋi di gbinni.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka di yuui ka m boori soori ni n ti kyɛ ya saani na n‑guui.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ n laa n‑ka tunseelli luga wa puuni, ka n yi boori ni n kyɛ ya saani na yuuma seem nŋɔa.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: m mari tama, ni, n yi ya gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Sipeen tingbaŋŋu ni, n nin nya ya. Ka n yi ti kaai ya n‑niŋi daba ayi pɔi, ka ti la'am m‑maai taaba sufu, ya nin naayi toaai n‑zaali ma soori ni ka n kyaŋŋi n kyɛnni la.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ŋɔna, n wa la Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, ni n ti suŋŋi Naawunni niriba bɛni.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Dama Masidoonya dima ni Akaaya dima daa gbaai toosigu ni sufupeelli, ni, ba ti toosi n‑suŋŋi fara dimseeba ban bɛ Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni la.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Ba daa gbaai lala ni sufupeelli, halli Gyɛrusalɛm dima samni n dɔli ba gba. Dama Gyɛrusalɛm dima daa zaŋŋi ba Naawunni bunsuma n‑tɔri n‑ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa nti suŋŋi ba siisi, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka di sumsi ni ban ti zaŋŋi ba duniya wa bunsuma n‑tɔri n‑ti Yɛhudiyadima wa n‑suŋŋi ba ningbina.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Dizugu n yi tum dinŋɔ maa, n‑zaŋŋi ban bɔ seelli maa nti ti Gyɛrusalɛm dima maa n‑naai, n nin dɔli ya saani n‑gaari n‑kyaŋŋi n Sipeen la.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: M mi halli, ni, n yi kyɛ ya saani na, n ya mari Kirisita bunsuma zaa n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: N susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni Sii Kasi wurilim zugu gba, ni, ya suŋŋi ma ka ti susi Naawunni nimmoosi man yɛla zugu.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: Ya susima, ni, n nin yi Gyuudii dimseeba ban bu niŋi yɛdda la nuusi ni na, ka Gyɛrusalɛm puuni Naawunni niriba la nini nin tigi n suŋŋsim maa.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Ka lala zugu Naawunni nin ti ma soori ni n kyɛ ya saani na ni sufupeelli, ka man ni yarima nin la'am taaba m‑maai taaba sufu.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Naawunni siri su sufudoaa yɛla; ŋɔn Naawunni bɛ ya zaa saani. Ammi .

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: Tɔ, ka m boori ni ya ti deaai ti tunzɔpɔ'a wa ŋɔn yuuri boonni Fiibi. U nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn tumni Naawunni tuma n‑tiri u niriba ban la'amni Kyɛnkyiriiya tiŋŋa puuni.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: Ya deaam u hoo din sumsi ni ya deaai ya mabii seem la, ti Dugma yuuri zugu. U yi ti boori seelli ya saani, ya suŋŋma u yan nin toaai seem zaa, dama ŋɔn gba suŋŋi niriba pam halli ni man gba.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Ya puusima Prisila ni Akwila n‑ti ma, dama ba n nyɛ n tuntuntaaba la, Kirisita Yisa saani.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi ba nyɔvuya m‑pa talima man zugu, ka di pa la man kɔkɔ m pɛ'ari ba, see ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ka la'amni ti Dugma yuuri zugu la gba - ban zaa paasi man zugu m‑pɛ'ara.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Ya puusima ban mun la'amni ban Akwila ni Prisila yiri maa puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni maa. Ka puusi n zɔsuŋŋu Ɛpeenɛtusi; ŋɔna n nyɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi ba zaa n‑leebi Yisa nyaandɔla la.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Ka puusi Mariama, dama u tum pam n‑ti ya.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Ni Andirɔnikusi ni Gyuniyasi: n dɔ'ariba n nyɛ ba, ka man ni ban n daa la'am m‑bɛ dansarika ni. Yisa tuumudima maa tiri ba gyirima, halli ni ba daa daŋŋi ma n‑leebi Yisa nyaandɔliba gba.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Ka laa m‑puusi Ampiliyatusi n‑ti ma, dama n wuri u hoo Naawunni biiya:.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: ni Yuribanusi ti tuntunkpeaa Kirisita Masiiya zugu, ni Siteekyusi n zɔsuŋŋu.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Apɛliisi gba n nyɛ gyirima daana Kirisita yuuri zugu, ya puusima u n‑ti ma; ŋɔn ni Arisitɔbulusi yiri dima la zaa.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Ka puusi n dɔ'ara Hɛroodiyɔni n‑ti ma, ni Naasisusi yiri dima, ban nyɛ ti Dugma niriba la, m‑paasi.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Tirifeena ni Tirifoosa n nyɛ pɔ'aseeba ban tum tunkara n‑ti ti Dugma, ya puusima ba n‑ti ma; ka puusi Pɛrisisi man wuri sɔ la - ŋɔn gba tum tunkara n‑ti ti Dugma.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Ka puusi Ruufusi dama ti Dugma pun yiisi u: ka laa m‑puusi u ma la, ŋɔn nyɛ man gba ma.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Ka puusi Asinkyiratusi, ni Filɛgɔni, ni Hɛrimiisi, ni Patirɔbaasi, ni Hɛrimaasi, ni ti mabiisi ban bɛ ba saani maa zaa.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Ka laa m‑puusi Filɔlɔgusi, ni Gyuliya, ni Neeriyusi ni u tunzɔpɔ'a la, ni Ɔlimpaasi ni Yisa nyaandɔliba ban bɛ ba saani zaa.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Ya puusima taaba suŋŋasuŋŋa ti wurilim puusigu ni. Kirisita Masiiya nyaandɔliba la zaa puusiri ya na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: N kpaamni ya ni, ni, ya baŋŋma seeba ban wulisiri wulisiseesi an sa'amni yan pun bamsi wulisigu la; ba kur taasiri la nangbankpeemni na, ni ba ti lusi ya. Ya yim lala niriba maa yɛla ni.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Dama lala niriba bu gyeemni ti Dugma Kirisita, amaa ba maŋtoori kɔrisi yɛla ka ba gyeemna. Ka ba zaŋŋi ba yɛttɔ'anya'asa ni nangbammalisim la n‑yɔmsiri ninvugseeba ban teesa bu zulima.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Yarim muna - sɔkam mi, ni, ya deaari Naawunni noori, ka dizugu n sufu maasi ya yɛla zugu. M boori ni ya teesa ti zulima zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsuma, amaa ka laa m‑boori ni a ti kɔŋŋi bumbeeri bamsim, halli fiiŋ.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Naawunni n su sufudoaa yɛla zaa, ka di ku yuui ka u nin kyɛ ka ya nɔ n‑tɔm Sintaani zugu n‑ŋmirigi u. Ti Dugma Kirisita Masiiya wurilim kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Timɔti, n tuntunkpeaa la, puusiri ya suŋŋa, ni n dɔ'ariba m‑paasi - Luusiyusi, ni Gyeesɔni ni Soosipata nla.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gayusi gba puusiri ya ni; n yirikpɛdaana n nyɛ u, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka Yisa niriba la'amni u yiri maa ni. Ɛrasitusi gba puusira - ŋɔn su ti tiŋŋa wa ligri yɛla fukumsi la - ni ti mabii Kuwaatusi m‑paasi.

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ari Naawunni! Ŋɔna nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka ya nya yaa, ka di yi la man wulisiri lahabasuŋŋu maa ni na, ni Yisa Kirisita yɛla mooligu ni na. Di nyɛ la din wulisi yɛlisɔ'ara gbinni n‑tiri ya la. Ba daa sɔ'ai yɛla wa, piiligu ni halli ni zuna.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba kasigi a gbinni n‑wulisi ti. Hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, lala lahabaari maa ka Naawunni, saha din ka naari la Daana, yɛtti, ni, ba yiisima na n‑wulisiri ya'ari kam, ni duniya zaa niriba ti deaai u noori n‑niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Naawunni siri bɛ la u kɔkɔ m‑mari yɛm; gyirima zaa nyɛ la u dinni halli ni saha din ka tɔriga. Ti pɛ'ama u, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu! Ammi .

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Tɔ: m mabiisi, man daa kyɛ ya saani na maa, ni n ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛligu n‑tiri ya, ni ya ti baŋŋi Naawunni yɛlisɔ'ara - lala saha n daa bu kyɛ na ni n ti zaŋŋi yɛlinya'asa an mari bamsim n‑tɔ'asira.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Ay! - n daa yɛli, ni, man ŋɔn bɛ ya saani la m bu ya sakki m‑baŋŋi seelli na'ala Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yɛla kɔkɔ ka n nin baŋŋi - halli ban daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu gba.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: N daa bɛ ya saani ka daa kɔŋŋi yaa ni yiiko, ka n daa zɔri dabeem halli nti gyigi di zugu.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: N yɛligu maa, ni man daa mooli lahabaari la gba, daa pa la yɛm yɛttɔ'anya'asa, amaa a daa wulisiri la Naawunni Sii maa yiiko yɛla.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: ni lala ti kyɛ ka ya yɛdda niŋbu ku bɛ saalidima yɛm zugu, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Yɛm yɛla ka ti siri yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ban gbaai kpamni la saani, amaa di pa la pampaŋŋɔ saha yɛm maa, pa din yi ban su pampaŋŋɔ duniya fukumsi la saani na - ban kur naari yɔri.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Ay! Naawunni yɛm ka ti tɔ'asira - bunsɔ'arigu din daa bu yi paalu na. Lala yɛm ka Naawunni daa zaali pɔi ka naayi naam pampaŋŋɔ duniya wa; ka niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka tirima nin nya gyirima.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Yɛm wa muna - ban su pampaŋŋɔ duniya fukumsi la sɔsɔ daa bu baŋŋi di gbinni; halli ba yi daa baŋŋi, ba naa n‑ku kpaasi ti Dugma gyirima daana n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Ba siri daa bu baŋŋi, hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Bunseesi sɔ nimbiri ŋɔn na bu nya, Bunseesi sɔ tubri ŋɔn na bu wum, Bunseesi ninsaalisɔ sufu ŋɔn na bu teesi a yɛla - Anŋɔ ka Naawunni pun maali siri, U nin zaŋŋi a n‑ti ban wuri u maa.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Tirim muna - ŋɔn Naawunni zaŋŋi anŋɔ taabu n‑wulisi ti; u Sii maa zugu ka u wulisiri a ni, dama Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, halli Naawunni yɛlizulima gba.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Ka saalidima saani, ŋɔni m mi u saalitaraana yɛla deede? - Ay, sɔsɔ bu mi, na'ala di daama maŋtoori sufu ni sii maa m mi u yɛla. Lala ka sɔ na zi Naawunni yɛla deede, na'ala Naawunni Siiya.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Pa duniya wa siiya ka ti daa deaai; amaa Sisɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na. Ka lala n kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ti ti piiniseesi la zaa.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Tɔ: lala ka ti wulisira. Di bu yi la duniya wa yɛm wulisigu ni na, amaa hoo Sii maa ŋɔn wulisiri ti seem; ka ti mari n‑suŋŋni Sii maa niriba, ni ba ti baŋŋi u yɛla.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Ninvugseeba ban ka Sii maa bu deaari yɛliseesi an yiiri Naawunni Sii maa ni na, dama a nyɛ la gyɛrilim ba saani. Ba bu baŋŋi a gbinni, dama sɔ ku toaai m‑baŋŋi, di yi pa la u dɔli Sii maa soori n‑viisiri a.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Nira ŋɔn mari Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, amaa sɔ mun bu viisiri ŋɔna.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Ni#:- Dama ti Dugma sufu ni teesa muna - Ŋɔni n na mi m‑baŋŋi a? Ŋɔni mun ŋmeeri u saawara? Tirima mun mari la Kirisita Masiiya sufu ni teesa.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Man Pɔla n‑sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti ya, man ni ti mabii Sɔsitɛniisi. Man nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu, dama lala ka u sufu yuubu daa wulisi.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: N sɔbri n‑tiri yarima, yaseeba ban la'amni Kɔrinti tiŋŋa maa ni n‑gyeemni Naawunni: yan bɛ Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani maa zugu n kyɛ ka ya nyɛ la Naawunni maŋtoori niriba. Lala zugu ka u boonni ya u saani, ka laa m‑paasiri ya zugu ninvugseeba ban leebi Naawunni dinni tiŋŋa kam zaa ni, ka ba zaŋŋi la Yisa Kirisita yuuri m‑puri ba, dama u nyɛ la ba Dugma, ni ti gba Dugma.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, zaŋŋma u piinisuma ni alaafɛ zaa n‑tisi ya.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: M puusiri Naawunni barika ya zugu sahakam, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u piinisuma n‑ti ya la zugu, ka di nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Dama ŋɔn saani ka ya pun deaai bunsuma pam gba - di nyɛ la yɛttɔ'a zaa, ni bamsim zaa, ziiya.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Lala zugu ka ya beebu kur wulisiri, ni, lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Kirisita yɛla maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni deede.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: M mabiisi, n ku toaai n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya hoo ban mi Sii maa yɛla la, amaa hoo ban mi ninsaala yɛla la kɔkɔa, dama ya na nyɛ la bileesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Kirisita yɛla.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Dinzugu ya bu kɔŋŋi Naawunni piinisuma, halli yinni gba, ka kpalim n‑guusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yiibu na saha.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Dizugu n daa mɔ'asiri ya la bisim, pa dikpeema; dama ya daa bu sa'ai ditibsa diibu. Halli ni zuna ya na ku toaai n‑deaai ninvugkura diibu.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: U siri nin mari ya suŋŋa halli nti paai di tɔriga, ka ya nin zɛ yummu ti Dugma Yisa dabsiri daari maa ni, galinseelli ka ni.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: dama ya na zi Sii maa yɛla. Nyuuri zappu ni suuri yisigri ŋɔn na bɛ ya saani, pa duniya wa saalidima yɛla ka ya dɔla?.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Naawunni nyɛ la yɛdda daana, ka ŋɔna zugu ka ba daa booli ya, ni ya yi na n‑la'amni m‑bɛ u Bii Yisa Kirisita ti Dugma saani.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Ya bu nya? - sɔ yɛtti, ni, Pɔla ka u dɔla; ka sɔ mun yɛtti, ni, Apɔloosi ka u boora: pa duniya wa ninsaalidima yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa?.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: Tɔ: ka n susiri ya, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni, ya zaa mari nangbanyinni ni taaba; yan di ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑wɔligri ka kyɛri taaba. See ka ya kpamsiri taaba ka mari sufu yinni ni teesiyinni.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Apɔloosi nyɛ la ŋɔni? Bee, Pɔla nyɛ la ŋɔni? Ti zaa nyɛ la Naawunni tuntumniba, ka di kyɛ ka yarima niŋni yɛdda dima, amaa bunseelikam yi la Naawunni saani na n‑ti ti.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: M mabiisi wumma! N wumni ya yɛla Kloowi yiri dima maa saani, ni, zaba m bɛ ya saani.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya yɛdda yɛla, mani n daa buri bumbura la, ka Apɔloosi mun daa yɔ'ai bumbula la koom, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu ka a zoaai na n‑niŋna.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Man tɔ'asiri seem maa nyɛ la: ni, ya ninvugsɔ n yɛtti, ni, u dɔli la man Pɔla, ka sɔ yɛtti, ni, u dɔli la Apɔloosi, sɔ mun gba yɛtti, ni, Siifasi Piita ka u dɔla, halli ka sɔ yɛtti yaasa, ni, Man mun dɔli la Kirisita.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Lala zugu ka ŋɔn butti la pa seela, ka ŋɔn yɔ'ari koom la gba pa seela; din kpa la talasi nyɛ la Naawunni tuma, dama ŋɔna n kyɛ ka di zoaari na n‑niŋna.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Ammi, ba purigi Kirisita zii bunaasi? Ba daa kpaasi Pɔla n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu ni ya ti nya tuliginsim, bee? - Ay! pa lala. Bee, ba daa su ya Pɔla koom? - Ay, halli fiiŋ gba!.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Ŋɔn butti ni ŋɔn yɔ'ari koom la zaa tumni la tumni yinni, ka ti Dugma nin yɔ ba zaa hoo ban tumni seem la.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: Di niŋi ma nya'asim Naawunni zugu, ni, n daa bu su ya ninvugsɔ koom, na'ala Krispusi ni Gayusi kɔkɔa.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Tirima zaa la'am n‑tumni Naawunni tuma: yan mun nyɛ la Naawunni puu ka ti koora, ka laa n‑nyɛ u yiri ka ti meera.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Dama lala zugu ya ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, n daa su ya koom m mammantoori yuuri zugu.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Mani n nyɛ tammeera ni yɛlimaŋni, hoo man deaai piiniseesi Naawunni saani la, ka n daa nyeebi nyeebri maa; ka sɔ mun deaai m‑meeri n‑tɔmna: tɔ! see ka sɔkam mari u maŋŋa ka baŋŋi ŋɔn tumni u meebu tuma seem.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Ya bu nya? - di daa pa la koom suubu yɛla zugu ka Kirisita daa tum ma ya saani na, amaa lahabasuŋŋu mooligu zugu; ka m bu zaŋŋi ninsaala yɛm m‑moonna, dama lala kyɛ ka Kirisita dɔpulli zugu kum yɛla ti basi yɔri.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Ba daa pun nyeebi nyeebri la suŋŋa, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑nyeebi seaa m‑paasi ka zugu - Yisa Kirisita yɛla ka n tɔ'asiri wa.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Dama dɔpulli maa lahabaari muna - ninvugseeba ban bɛ baasiyɔri soori ni maa yi ti wum di, ba yɛtti, ni, gyɛrilim; amaa tirima mun ban bɛ tuliginsim soori maa ni la saani ka di mari la Naawunni yiiko.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Amaa sɔ zaŋŋi salima m‑mɛ m‑pa nyeebri wa zugu, ka sɔ mun zaŋŋi anzulifa m‑pa, bee kugveela; ka seeba gba zaŋŋi daari, bee moori ni kika'a m‑pa.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- N nin sa'am yɛm dima bamsim maa; N nin gbarigi ba miilinsi.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Amaa sariya karibu daari yi paai na, di nin yiisi sɔkam tuma paalu ni na, n‑wulisi an bɛ seem, dama bugum n ya zamsi sɔkam zaa tuma.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Tɔ, ka yɛm daana la lee? Bamsim daana la lee? Ŋɔn viisiri m‑baŋŋni pampaŋŋɔ duniya beesigu yɛla la bɛ la yani? Naawunni n zaŋŋi duniya wa yɛm n‑leebi gyɛrilim, bee, m ma'ari ni?.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Tɔ; ka bugum yi bu ti nyaŋŋi sɔ tuma, di daama nin deaai u laara sanyoaa.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Naawunni mammaŋŋa yɛm zugu daa kyɛ ka duniya wa dima daa zaŋŋi ba toori yɛm ni ba ti baŋŋi u yɛla n‑kɔŋŋi. Ka u sufu yuubu zugu ka baseeba ban wum lahabaari mooligu din ŋmani hoo gyɛrilim la n‑niŋi yɛdda la muna - ban nyari fa-m‑basi.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Amaa bugum yi di sɔ tuma zaa, di daama nin kɔŋŋi marigu; u mammaŋŋa ku baasi yɔri, amaa u nin ŋmanni la hoo ŋɔn kpeeri bugum ni n‑gatta.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Tɔ: Yɛhudiya dima maa boori la alahazibu tuma nyaabu, ka Griika dima maa mun boori yɛm yɛla wumpu;.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Ya bu baŋŋi, ni, yarima n nyɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu, bee? Halli Naawunni Siiya bɛ ya ni gba.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: amaa tirima ŋɔn moonni lahabaseaa maa nyɛ la Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yɛla: ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu. Ka Yɛhudiya dima maa yɛtti, ni, din kyisiri nla; ka Griika dima maa mun yɛtti, ni, gyɛrilim n nyɛ di.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi sa'am Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziiya, Naawunni gba nin sa'am di daama. Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu nyɛ la u dinni, kan nyɛ bunkasikasi: ka yarim n nyɛ u Gyeeŋŋu Duu wa.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn booli seeba la ŋɔna, u siri nyɛ la Masiiya n‑ti ba, ka laa n‑nyɛ Naawunni yɛm ni u yiiko gba - halli ba yi nyɛ Yɛhudiya dima, ni Griika dima zaa.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Tɔ: yan di kyɛ ka ya sɔ yɔmsi u maŋŋa; ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ya saani ka teesi, ni, u nyɛ la yɛm daana duniya wa saani, kyɛ ka di daama leebi gyɛrigu; ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka u leebi yɛm daana ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Dama Naawunni gyɛrilim mari yɛm n‑gaari saalidima yɛm, ka Naawunni bugsim mari yaa n‑gaari saalidima yaa.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Dama duniya wa yɛm nyɛ la gyɛrilim Naawunni saani. Lala zugu ka di pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, Yɛm daandima maa muna - Naawunni zaŋŋi ba toori yɛmgɔliŋŋa m‑bɛtti n‑gbaari ba.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: M mabiisi, ya teesima yan daa bɛ seem ka ba daa booli ya. Ya sunsukkuni ya ka niriba pam ban mari duniya wa yɛm; ya ka niriba pam ban mari yiiko, bee, n‑yi naam zuliya ni.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi, ni, Ti Dugma m mi yɛm dima teesa zaa, ka a nyɛ la yɔri.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Amaa Naawunni yiisi la bunseesi an mari gyɛrilim duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‑diisiri yɛm dima maa vi: Naawunni yiisi la an nyɛ bumbugsa duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‑diisiri yaa dima maa vi.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu, di kyɛ ka ya sɔ nyuuri bara ninsaalidima yɛla zugu. Dama bunseelikam zaa nyɛ la ya dinni.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: U yiisi la tarimma, ni niriba ŋɔn zeemni bunseesi, halli bunseesi an ka ni gba, ka mari a n‑kyɛ ka bunseesi an bɛ ni maa ti basi yɔri.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Ii! - Pɔla nyɛ la ya dinni, ni Apɔloosi, ni Siifasi Piita; yarima n su beesigu ni kum fukumsi; din bɛ ni pampaŋŋɔ ni din nin niŋi zaa - anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la ya dinni.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: Ka lala kyɛ ka sɔ ku toaai n‑nyu bara ŋɔn Naawunni saani.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: Ka ya mun nyɛ la Kirisita Masiiya ŋɔn su ninvugseeba, ka ŋɔn Kirisita nyɛ la Naawunni dinni.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni zugu ka ya bɛ Yisa Kirisita ni; ŋɔna ka Naawunni kyɛ ka u leebi yɛm n‑ti ti, ni yɛlimaŋni, ka u laa n‑kyɛ ka ti leebi Naawunni mammaŋŋa niriba kasi kasi, ka laa n‑nya fa-m‑basi.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Dizugu - hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli - Ŋɔn ya n‑nyu bara zaa, kyɛ ka u nyuuri a ni Naawunni yuuri zugu kɔkɔa.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna see ka ba nyari ti, ni, Kirisita tuntumniba, ni seeba Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi u yɛlisɔ'ara fukumsi n‑niŋi ba nuu ni.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Din kpalim ka ba boori tuntumniba saani nyɛ la, ni, ba zɛ yummu n‑tumna.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Man mun ba'a ka ni, ni, yarima yi katti n sariya, bee ninsaalidima kootu sɛseelli yi katta; halli m mammaŋŋa gba - man bu katti n yɛla sariya.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: M bu nya teesibeeri n sufu ni, amaa lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka n niŋi yɛlimaŋni daana; ti Dugma kɔkɔ nin ti kari ma sariya.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Lala zugu yan di daŋŋi n‑kari sariya ka saha maa na bu paai na; halli ni ti Dugma kyɛnni na. Ŋɔna nin kyɛ ka din sɔ'ai zibsim ni zaa nin ti yi paalu na, ka wulisi saalidima sufu ni teesa an bɛ seem. Di saha ka sɔkam zaa nin ti nya pɛ'abu din sumsi u, Naawunni saani.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: M mabiisi, yarima zugu ka n zaŋŋi man ni Apɔloosi yɛla wa n‑wulisiri ya, ni ya baŋŋi yɛligu wa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni#:- See ka ya ti dɔli din sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ka ku yakki Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seem. Ni, yan di gyeemni sɔ ka laa n‑zeemni u taraana.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Bɔ n wulisi, ni, ya wɔligiya ni ka kyɛ ya taaba? Bɔ ka ya mara, di yi pa la ya daa deaai ka la piini? Ka ya yi deaai piini maa, bɔ n kyɛ ka ya nyuuri bara, hoo yan bu deaai di zaa?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Ni, bundaandima n nyɛ ya, fa! Ni, ya tigiya ka bu kɔŋŋi seela. Ni, ya pun di naam ka kyɛ ti. Tɔ: ya yi siri di naam, di veela! - dama ti gba naa n‑la'am n‑di naam maa.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: N teesi, ni, Naawunni zaŋŋi tirima, u tuumudima maa, ka labsi ti yɛla nyaaŋŋa. Ti ŋmanni la hoo ninvugseeba ban kari ti sariya n‑lusi ti, ni, ti sum la kum; ka ti diri lala wahala ni sɔkam ti gɔsira - halli malaakadima ni ninsaalidima zaa gba.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Tirima siri nyɛ la gyɛra, Kirisita zugu; ka yarima mun wulisiri ninneesim Kirisita saani, fa! Bumbugsa n nyɛ ti, ka ya mun mari kpeeŋŋu. Ya nyari gyirima, ka ti mun nyari fiiliŋŋu.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Halli ni zuna ti kpiiri la kɔm ni kɔnyuuri, n‑ka bunyeera, ka laa n‑nyari mugsigu ti gyiliŋŋu la puuni.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Ti namsiri n‑tumni tuma ni ti nuusi. Ka niriba tuuri ti, ka ti mun kaari kasuma n‑tiri ba; ba namsiri ti ni, ka ti mun bu za'asiri ba yuubu.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Ba tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑niŋni ti, amaa ti kpaŋŋi ti maŋŋa n‑sakki n‑ti ba baalimm. Ti ŋmanni la duniya zaa sa'ari, hoo ninsaalidima maa sa'atampuuri, halli ni zuna.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: M bu sɔbri wa ni n ti diisi ya la vi, amaa n kpaamni ya ni hoo m biwutta.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Dama halli ya yi mari ya karimbadima tusa tusa zaŋŋi n‑kpa Kirisita ziiya gba, ya ka badima pam, see yinni kɔkɔa. Yɛlimaŋni, mani n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai ya zaŋŋi n‑kpa lahabasuŋŋu beesigu ziiya ka di nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Lala zugu ka n susiri ya, ni, ya dɔli n beesigu soori.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n tum Timɔti ya saani na; u nyɛ la m biwuttigu ni yɛlimaŋni, ka laa n‑nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn zɛ yummu ti Dugma yɛla zugu. Ŋɔna nin toaai n‑teaari ya man Kirisita beesigu sɔya yɛla, hoo man ŋɔn wulisi seem lugri kam man la'amni ni Naawunni niriba zaa.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Man na bu kyɛ ya saani na zugu ka ya seeba mari karimbaani.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Amaa di siri ku yuui ka n nin kaai ya na, Naawunni yi ti ti ma lala soori. Di saha n nin baŋŋi karimbaani dima maa gbinni, pa ba yɛttɔ'a yɛla kɔkɔa, amaa ni ba yiiko ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Dama Naawunni naam pa la yɛttɔ'a kɔkɔa, amaa yiiko ni gba.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Bɔ ka ya boora? - N kyɛm na nti darigi ya tuba? - bee, n zaŋŋi Sii maa wurilim ni nirilim n‑kyɛ ya saani na?.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Ya sɔ yi mari yɛttɔ'a ni u taraana, ka u zaŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a maa n‑kyaŋŋi ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la saani ka kyɛ Naawunni niriba maa - lala mari soori? - Ay! lala ka soori.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, Naawunni niriba maa nin ti kari duniya wa zaa sariya? Ka ya yi ya kari duniya wa sariya, ya ku toaai n‑kari sarabisi?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ti nin kari halli malaakadima sariya gba? Ka ku toaai n‑kari saalidima maa yɛla, fa?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Amaa yarima yi mari yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ taabu, ya kyɛnni seeba saani ni ba kari n‑ti ya, ka ba pa la ninvugseeba Naawunni niriba ŋɔn tiri gyirima.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Lahabaari siri yi na, ni, zina m bɛ ya saani - ka ka taabu ka ni, halli ban bu dɔli Naawunni soori la saani gba: ni, dɔsɔ n di u ba pɔ'a.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: N yɛtti dinŋɔ la, ni n ti diisi ya vi. Ammi, sɔ ka ya saani ŋɔn mari yɛm n nin toaai m‑maali ya yɛla maa? - halli ninvugu yinni gba?.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Ka ya mun nyuuri la bara, fa! Di da bu sum ni ya sufu sa'am di zugu? - ka ya yi da zaŋŋi ŋɔn tumni lala maa n‑yiisi ya la'aŋŋu ni, di da sɔa.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Ka ya ziiri taaba n‑kyɛnni kootu ni, halli seeba ban ka yɛdda saani.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Tɔ, halli ni man ka ya saani maa zaa, n siiya bɛ la ya saani, ka m pun kari di daama sariya hoo man bɛ ya saani n‑wumni lala doo wa yɛttɔ'a seem.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Yan zaŋŋni taaba n‑kyɛnni kootu ni maa kur wulisi, ni, ya pun lu. Ni, ba tum la taalli ya saani; tɔ, ka wula zugu ka ya ku kyɛ taalli maa m‑pɛŋŋi ba? Ni, ba fa ya buna yɔri; tɔ, ka wula zugu ka ya ku kyɛ di yɛla m‑basi?.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Ya yi ti la'am taaba ti Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu, ka n gba sii bɛ la ya saani, ti Dugma Yisa yiiko kur bɛ la bɛni.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Amaa yarima n tumni taalli ka faari buna - halli ya taaba saani la gba!.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: Di saha ya zaŋŋma lala doo maa n‑ti Sintaani ka u sa'am u ningbina, ka di yi pa seem lala nin kyɛ ka u sii maa nin nya tuliginsim, ti Dugma dabsiri daari.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni taalli la ku nya Naawunni naam maa? Yan di yɔmsi ya mansi: see ka ya baŋŋi, ni, zina dima ku nya Naawunni naam, ka ba'amaanniba gba ku nya, bee gyɛ'amiru dima ni dɔppa ban tumni lala, bee ban zaŋŋni ba dɔtaaba n‑doontira;.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Ay! ya bara nyuubu wa bu veela! Ya bu mi paanu dabina yɛla - ni, ba zaŋŋi a beela n‑niŋi boroboro zum puuni, ka a nin nyaŋŋi n‑tum m‑pitti paanu n‑fuusiri a zaa gba.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: ni na'ayigsi, ni beem dima, ni danyuuriba, ni minaafigsi, ni ban yɔmsiri niriba - banŋɔ sɔsɔ ku nya Naawunni naam; ba nuu ka lala faari maa yɛla ni, halli beela.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ya yiisim dabinkura la m‑basi, ka ya leebi boroboropaalli, an ka dabina. Halli, ya siri pun bɛ la lala, dama ba pun kɔrigi Kirisita, hoo ti Yakki-n‑Gaari pɛbila maaligu la - pa paanu kan ka dabina n nyɛ Yakki-n‑Gaari dinni?.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Ya seeba mun daa bɛ la lala. Amaa ya daa nya koom suubu; ba daa kyɛ ka ya leebi Naawunni niriba kasikasi; ba daa deaai ya, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yuuri zugu, ni ti Ba Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la saani.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti maali Kyuu wa suŋŋa: ti di mari dabinkura paanu an kyisira, bee, tumbeeri ni putoo buna; amaa bumpaala, an ka dabina, an nyɛ la yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni dina.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Ni, M mari la seelikam yiiko. - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŋŋsim. Ni, M mari la seelikam yiiko - tɔ, amaa di kyɛ ka seelikam zaa nyaŋŋi ma.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: N daa pun sɔbi n‑ti ya n gbaŋŋu la ni, ni, yan di la'am m‑bɛ ni ni zina dima.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Ni, Diibu nyɛ la puuri dinni, ka puuri mun su diibu yɛla. - tɔ, amaa Naawunni nin kyɛ ka abayi wa zaa ti baasi yɔri. Pa zina n su ningbina fukumsi, amaa ti Dugma n su a; ningbina maa Dugma n nyɛ u.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Tɔ - pa la duniya wa niriba ban tumni zina la yɛla ka n daa tɔ'asi, bee, beem dima, bee, ban yɔmsiri n‑faari niriba, halli baseeba ban gyeemni buga la gba: di yi da nyɛ la lala, ya naa n‑yi la duniya wa ni zaa.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Naawunni yiiko ka u daa zaŋŋi n‑yisigi ti Dugma kum ni, ka u nin yisigi ti gba lala.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Tɔ: ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi yan daa sɔbi a yɛla la: di veela ni doo bɛ ni ka bu di pɔ'a.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Amaa man yi sɔbri n‑tiri ya wa, ni, ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ni ka boonni u maŋŋa, ni, ya mabiiya Yisa yuuri zugu, ka tumni zina m‑mari beem, halli n‑gyeemni buga gba, bee, n‑zuuri niriba nyaaŋŋa, bee n‑nyuuri daam m‑bugra, bee, n‑yɔmsiri n‑faari niriba - lala tataabu daana ka n yɛtti ya, ni, yan di la'amni ni u n‑diri diibu.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina nyɛ la Kirisita ningbinluga? Ka n ya niŋi la wula n‑zaŋŋi Kirisita ningbinlugri n‑la'am n‑leebi gyɛ'amiru ningbiŋŋu lugri? Ay! - lala ka soori.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Amaa zina kɔrisi zugu, ya kyɛ ka doo zaa mari u pɔ'a, ka pɔ'a zaa mari u sira.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Ammi, m ba'a bɛ la ban pa ti niriba yɛla ni, bee? - ni n ti kari ba sariya. Ay, pa lala! Yan mun katti la ti toori niriba yɛla, bee m ma'ari ni?.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Kyɛ ka doo maa tiri u pɔ'a din sumsi u, ka pɔ'a maa gba tiri u sira, hoo din tuusi seem.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ŋɔn la'am n‑tabli ni gyɛ'amiru, ni, babayi maa zaa leebi la yummu? Dama di yɛliya, ni, Babayi maa zaa nin la'am m‑bɛ ni kaman bunni yinni.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Seeba ban pa ti niriba muna - Naawunni nin kari ba sariya. Ni, Ya karima putoo daana n‑yiisi ya puuni.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Dama pɔ'a maa ningbiŋŋu pa la u toori dinni kɔkɔa, amaa u sira gba dinni; ka lala doo maa ningbiŋŋu pa la u toori dinni kɔkɔa, amaa u pɔ'a gba dinni.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Ŋɔn mun la'am u maŋŋa ni ti Dugma, ba leebi la sii yinni.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Ya zɔma n‑yi zina tuma ni; dama tumbeeri zaa pa la nira ningbiŋŋu puuni yɛttɔ'a, na'ala zina tuma nyɛ lala; amaa ŋɔn niŋi zina tuma zaa niŋni lala tumbeeri u toori ningbiŋŋu ni.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Yan di za'asi taaba, ka di yi pa la ya niŋi la nangbanyinni, ni, ya zaŋŋi lala saha maa n‑susiri Naawunni; ka laa n‑la'am taaba yaasa. Ka di kyɛ ka Sintaani zamsi ya n‑nyaŋŋi, yan ku toaai m‑mari ya mansi zugu.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina maa nyɛ la Sii Kasi gbeebu ziiya? - u Gyeeŋŋu Duu. Dama ya daa deaai Sii Kasi maa Naawunni nuu ni, ka u bɛ la ya saani. Ya bu su ya maŋtoori yɛla;.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Man yɛtti la nŋɔa, di pa la n zaali ya lala zaaligu, amaa ya yi tumni lala, n nin sakki.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: ay! - ba daa da ya ni. Dizugu ya zaŋŋma ya ningbina n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Man saani, n da boori la sɔkam dɔli la n toori soori. Amaa sɔkam mari la u toori soori Naawunni piini zugu: yinni mari la piini kanŋɔa, ka sɔ mari la seaa.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Tɔ: ka n yɛtti n‑tiri seeba ban na bu di pɔ'a, bee, n‑kuli doowa, ni pukɔya gba, ni, ba yi kpalim m‑bɛ hoo man bɛ seem wa, di sɔa.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Amaa ba yi ku toaai n‑gbaai ba mansi, ba dim pɔ'aba, bee, n‑kuli dɔppa. Ya yi niŋi lala di veela n‑gaari ya kyɛ ka pɔ'aba bee dɔppa yɛla ti mugsi ya.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Ban mun mari pɔ'aba, bee, siridima ka n yɛtti wa - amaa pa mani n‑yɛtta, amaa ti Dugma zaaligu nŋɔa - ni, pɔ'a di yi u sira yiri:.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: amaa u yi yi, ŋɔn di laa n‑kuli dɔsɔa, bee, ŋɔn labma u sira maa yiri. Ka doo mun di yiisi u pɔ'a.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Ban kpalim muna ka n yɛtti wa - mani n‑yɛtta, pa ti Dugma zaaligu nŋɔa - ni, Kirisita bisɔ yi mari u pɔ'a ka pɔ'a maa pa la yɛdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‑bɛ u saani, tɔ, ŋɔn di yiisi u pɔ'a maa.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Ka pɔ'a mun yi mari u sira ka sira maa pa la yɛdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‑bɛ u saani, tɔ, ŋɔn di yi.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Dama doo ŋɔn ka yɛdda maa leebi la Naawunni dinni u pɔ'a la zugu, ka pɔ'a sɔ ŋɔn ka yɛdda la gba leebi la Naawunni dinni u sira la zugu: di yi pa lala, ya biisi naa m‑pa la Naawunni dinni, amaa ka ba siri nyɛ la u dinni.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Amaa ŋɔn ka yɛdda la yi ya bakki, kyɛ ka u bakki. Yɛdda daana la ka taalli dinŋɔ ni: Naawunni m booli ti ni ti bɛ sufudoaa ni.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: I bu nya, pɔ'a? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i sira m‑basi. Bee nyini bu nya, doowa? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i pɔ'a m‑basi.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Di zaa yɔri, ya kyɛ ka sɔkam kpalim m‑bɛ beesiseaa ti Dugma ŋɔn daa zaali u, sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u maa. N zaaligu n‑kur la, man yi bɛ Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu kam zaa ni.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Dɔsɔ m bɛ ni ka ba daa guni u pɔi ka Naawunni naayi booli u? - Tɔ, u di kpiisi gunibu maa. Sɔ mun bɛ ni ka ba daa bu guni u ka Naawunni booli u? - Tɔ, u di laa n‑kyɛ ka ba guni u.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Gunibu bee bu guni zaa nyɛ la yɔri: din kpa talasi nyɛ la Naawunni zaaligu zaa dɔligu.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Kyɛ ka sɔkam kpalim m‑bɛ ŋɔn daa bɛ seem sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: I yi daa nyɛ la daabri i booligu saha maa, nyin di kyɛ ka di mugsi i - amaa i yi ti nya soori ni i ti yi daabtiri maa na, kur dɔla ka n‑yi.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ŋɔn daa nyɛ daabri u booligu maa saha zaa mari la yɔlisim ti Dugma saani, ka ŋɔn mun daa su u maŋŋa u booligu maa saha leebi la daabri n‑ti Kirisita.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Ba daa da ya ni n‑yɔa; dizugu yan di leebi daaba n‑ti saalidima.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Dizugu sɔkam ŋɔn daa bɛ seem zaa ka Naawunni daa booli u, u kpalimma m‑bɛ lala, Naawunni zugu.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Tɔ; ka ban na bu di pɔ'aba muna, ni pɔ'aseeba ban na bu kuli dɔppa gba - n ka ti Dugma zaaligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba yɛla; amaa man ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn zɔ namboo n‑ti ma la, ka u kyɛ ka n niŋi yɛdda daana di zugu, m mari la saawara n‑ti ba.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Ni, n teesiya, ni, zuna mugsigu ŋɔn bɛ seem zugu, ba yi kpalim m‑bɛ lala, di sɔa.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Ni, i pun di pɔ'a? - tɔ, di yiisi u. I na ka pɔ'a - tɔ, di bɔ pɔ'a.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Amaa i yi di pɔ'a, i bu tum taalli; ka pɔ'asariga yi kuli doowa, u gba bu tum taalli. Amaa ban mari pɔ'aba, bee siridima, nin di wahala duniya wa puuni, ka m boori ni n kyɛ ka ya ku di lala wahala.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Man wulisiri seelli maa nŋɔa, m mabiisi: saha maa ku yuui. Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑wa, see ka ban mari pɔ'aba gba bɛ hoo ban ka.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: Lala yaasa, kyɛ ka ban kumni la bɛ hoo ban bu kumna, ka ban mari sufumaasim bɛ hoo ban mari sufusa'aŋŋu, halli ban daari neema gba bɛ hoo ban bu su marigu.
