eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: M‑paasi lala, ban mari duniya buna n‑tumna, ba di kyɛ ka ba ba'a zaa bɛ a ni. Dama duniya kanŋɔ gatti ni maa.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: M bu boori ni seelli ti mugsi ya. Dakoori ba'a zaa bɛ la ti Dugma yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka ti Dugma sufu maai.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Pɔ'adaana mun ba'a bɛ la duniya yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka u pɔ'a sufu maai;.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: ka u ba'a puri buyi. Lala ka pukoori, bee, pɔ'asariga ba'a zaa bɛ la ti Dugma yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka bɛ kasi n‑ti ti Dugma, u ningbiŋŋu ni, ni u sii ni gba. Pɔ'a sɔ ŋɔn mari u sira muna - u ba'a bɛ la duniya yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka u sira sufu maai.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: N yɛtti dinŋɔ la ya suŋŋsim zugu; pa la man ya ta'ari ya yɛla. N ya wulisiya ya ya nin niŋi seem ka ya teesa ku puri, amaa ka ya tumni din tuusi, ka kur gyeemni ti Dugma la suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Tɔ: ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔsɔ ŋɔn su pɔ'asariga yɛla maa: pɔ'a maa yuuma yi paai doo kulibu ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn su u yɛla maa teesi, ni, u bu tumni din tu seem n‑tiri u, kyɛ ka u niŋi u sufu ŋɔn boori seem: ba yi ti niŋi amriya di ka taalli.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Amaa lala doo maa yi niŋi u sufu ni yɛdda, ka sɔ bu mugsiri u, u yi nyaŋŋi u maŋŋa ka gbaai gbaari kirikiri - tɔ, u yi kyɛ ka pɔ'a maa kpalim la pɔ'asariga, lala gba veela.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Dinŋɔna pɔ'a maa yi ti kuli doowa, lala dɔli la soori amaa u yi bu kuli doowa, di sɔa.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Pɔ'a sɔ yi kuli doowa, di sum ni u bɛ la u sira maa saani, halli ni doo maa nyɔvuri tɔriga. Amaa u sira maa yi ti kpi, u mari soori ni u kuli dɔsɔ ŋɔn boora; amaa see ka doo maa nyɛ la ti Dugma nira.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Di zaa yɔri, u yi kpalim la pukoori u nin mari sufumaasim n‑gaari lala - man teesi seem nŋɔa, ka m mi, ni, n gba mari Naawunni Siiya.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Tɔ; ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi ban zaŋŋi m‑maali ba'a la yɛla: ni, Ti zaa mari la bamsim. - tɔ, ti siri mi lala halli, amaa bamsim lala taabu zugu ka niriba kur duusiri ba mansi; wurilim mun nyɛ la din paasiri niriba beesisuŋŋu.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Ka nira ŋɔn teesi, ni, ŋɔn mi la seelli la - di daama na ka miilinsi din dɔli soori.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Amaa nira ŋɔn wuri Naawunni, Naawunni mi u yɛla.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Tɔ: zaŋŋi n‑kpa ba'a bundira diibu ziiya, ti mi, ni, ba'ari pa la bunseelli kam duniya wa ni, ka wunni ka ni di yi pa la Naawunni kɔkɔa.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Ba yi tɔ'asi ba'a ni wuna yɛla duniya wa puuni, saazugu bee tingbaŋŋu ni - halli ka ba siri tɔ'asiri la wuna ni dugmadima pam yɛla -.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: di zaa yɔri, dama tirima saani ka Naawunni yinni bɛ ni. Ti Ba Naawunni n nyɛ u, ka bunseelli kam yi la u saani na, ka ti gba bɛ ni n‑tiri u. Ka ti Dugma yinni gba bɛ ni: Yisa Kirisita n nyɛ u, ka bunseelli kam bɛ ni ŋɔn Yisa zugu, halli ŋɔn zugu ka ti gba bɛ ni.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Yɛlimaŋni nla: amaa pa sɔkam zaa m mari bamsim wa. Seeba na kpalim m‑miili la ba'a yɛla, halli ka ba yi ti di lala diibu ba teesiri, ni, ba diri a ni n‑tiri ba'ari maa. Lala ka ban bu mi taalli ni din tu la yɛla galimiya ni.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Pa diibu zugu ka ti pɔtti Naawunni saani na: ti yi bu di, ti ku pɔsigi seelli; ti mun yi di, lala ku paasiri ti seelli.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Amaa ya baŋŋma ka ya yuubu niŋbu di kyɛ ka ban yɛdda bu zɛ deede la ti burigi n‑lu.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Ya bu nya? - ŋɔn bu mi taalli ni din tu la yi ti nya ka nyini bamsim daana wa bɛ la ba'a duri ni n‑diri diibu, ammi, lala ku kyɛ ka u di ba'a diibu? - Ii, u nin di.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ŋɔn na zi yɛla suŋŋa wa baasi yɔri nyin bamsim maa zugu - halli ni u nyɛ la i mabiiya, ka ŋɔn gba zugu ka Kirisita daa kpi.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Ya yi tum taalli wa taabu n‑niŋi ya mabiisi, ka kyɛ ka ba burigi ban na zi soori deede zugu, ya tumni la taalli n‑niŋi Kirisita.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Dizugu n numni wa ŋɔppu yi nin kyɛ ka n taraana burigi, dinŋɔna n ku laa n‑ŋɔbi numni yaasa, dama di nin kyɛ ka n taraana burigi n‑lu.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Man bu su m mammaŋŋa? Pa tuumu n nyɛ ma? Bee, m pun bu nya ti Dugma Yisa? Ay! - n daa nya u ka leebi u tuumu, ka ŋɔna n ti ma yɔlisimma: halli n tuma zugu ka yarim gba leebi Naawunni niriba.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Dizugu n yi pa la tuumu n‑ti seeba, n nyɛ la tuumu n‑ti yarima - man zugu ka ya nyɛ la Naawunni niriba, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, tuumu n nyɛ ma.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: N labsigu nŋɔa, n‑tiri ban ya boosi ma boosa.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Ammi, tirima ka soori ni ti nya ti bundira ni bunnyuura?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Halli tirima kɔkɔ n ka soori ni ti di pɔ'a ŋɔn nyɛ yɛddaniŋna, ka kyɛ ka u dɔli ti ti gyiliŋŋu ni, bee? - hoo tuumuseeba la ŋɔn tumni seem, ni ti Dugma tunzɔppa la, ni Siifasi gba.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Bee, kulla man ni Baanabasi kɔkɔ n ka soori ni ti ku ti bukaata, see ka ti tumni ti toori nuusi tuma?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Sɔ m bɛ ni m‑mari u toori samni n‑tumni soogya tuma? - Ay, ba yoori ba zaa! Bee, sɔ bɛ ni n‑sa tiisi ka ku di a wala? - Ay, ŋɔn saari zaa n deaari wala! Yaasa, sɔ m bɛ ni n‑guri bunkɔbri ka ku nyu ba bisim? - Ay, ba zaa nyuura!.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: M bu tɔ'asiri lala n toori ninsaala teesa zugu, amaa Naawunni zaaligu gba yɛtti la lala. - Bee, m ma'ari ni?.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli Musa zaaligu sɔppu ni, ni, Nigi yi tumni kyi buubu tuma, yan di lɔ ba nɔya. - Tɔ, nigi yɛla m mugsi Naawunni, bee?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Ay! - U yɛtti dinŋɔ zaa n‑ti la tirima, bee wula? Ii! - tirima zugu ka ba daa sɔbi a zaa, ni lala kyɛ ka ŋɔn koora ni ŋɔn buuri kyi zaa nin la'am m‑mari tama, ni, ba nin deaai ba tɔra.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Tɔ; ka tin daa tum ya saani seem la ŋmanni la hoo kyi koobu tuma ka di bɛ la ya siisi ziiya; ka di yi bɛ lala, ammi, ti ka soori ni ti nya anfaani ya saani duniya wa ziiya?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Soori mun yi bɛ ni ka baseeba nyari lala anfaani ya saani ka di bu sum ni tirima gba nya? Amaa di zaa yɔri, ti daa bu zaŋŋi lala soori maa. Ti daa yɔ ti toori samni zaa, dama ti daa bu boori ni Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu tuma labi nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: M‑paasi lala, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni maa nyari ba diibu la lala tuma zugu? Ii! - ban deaari bunkɔbri niriba saani m‑maanni n‑tiri ba kur mari ba tɔra.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Lala taabu ka ti Dugma zaali, ni, ban tumni u lahabasuŋŋu tuma maa nin nya lala tuma maa yoori.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Soori bɛ ni, amaa man mun daa bu deaai lala anfaani. M bu sɔbri yɛla wa ni ya ti niŋi lala n‑ti ma. Ay! - n yi da kpi di naa n‑sɔa! M bu boori ni ba ta'ari m bara nyuubu.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Ya bu nya? - n yi mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla, lala bu ti ma soori ni n ti nyuuri bara. Yɛlimaŋni, di nyɛ ma la talasi - halli n yi bu moonna, n nin di wahala.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: N yi tumni lala tuma maa m mammaŋŋa sufu yuubu zugu, n nyari anfaani di ni; amaa di yi pa la n yuubu zugu, n kur kpalim n‑tumni tunseesi ban zaali ma la.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Ka n yoori anfaani nyɛ la bɔa? Dinŋɔ kɔkɔa: ni, n yi moonni lahabasuŋŋu maa ka ba deaari yɔri, man ŋɔn bu boori ligri zugu, anfaani nla n‑ti ma.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: Tɔ: ni man su n toori maa zaa, n sakki, ni, n leebi la daabri n‑ti sɔkam, ni lala ti kyɛ ka n nya niriba pam ka ba ti tuligi.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Yɛhudiya dima maa saani ka n daa leebi hoo Yɛhudiya doowa, ni lala nin kyɛ ka n nya ba ka ba ti tuligi; n siri pa la zaaligu ŋɔn su sɔ fukumsi, amaa n leebi la hoo lala nira n‑tiri zaaligu ŋɔn su seeba fukumsi la, ka lala ti kyɛ ka zaaligu niriba wa ti nya tuliginsim.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Ban mun ka zaaligu la saani ka n yi leebi ba taraana n‑gba kɔŋŋi zaaligu: n siri pa la sɔ ŋɔn bu dɔli Naawunni ŋɔn zaali soori la, dama Kirisita zaaligu su n yɛla zaa, amaa lala kyɛ ka n nya ban ka zaaligu la ka ba ti tuligi.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Halli ban ka yaa la saani n daa sakki n‑leebi ŋɔn gba ka yaa; yɛlimaŋni, saalidima zaa ŋɔn bɛ seem man gba daa sakki, ni, n ti leebi lala; ka lala ti kyɛ ka n nyaŋŋi n‑fa seeba m‑basi.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Anŋɔ zaa ka n niŋna, Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu zugu, ni man gba nin toaai n‑la'am n‑di ka bunsuma.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: M‑paasi, ya bu mi zoaa yɛla? - ni, ban zɔri zoaa ni la saani, zɔzɔttiba maa zaa zɔra, amaa yinni kɔkɔ n yi zɔ n‑di. Dizugu ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑zɔ suŋŋa din nin kyɛ ka ya zɔ n‑di.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Ŋɔn ya zɔ zoaa ni, bee, n‑ŋmɛ bɔla muna - ba zaa yi mugsiri la ba mansi ni nimmoaa m‑bamsiri deema maa. Ba niŋni la lala ni ba ti di n‑deaai yaanni kan bu yuura; tirima mun ya di seaa kan bu naara.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Dizugu man mun bu zɔri zoaa la ni hoo ŋɔn zɔri yɔriyɔri; m bu zabri hoo ŋɔn buuri pɛsigu.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Ay! - n mari n toori ningbiŋŋu ka gbaari ka ni nimmoaa, ni lala zugu kyɛ ka m pa la sɔ ŋɔn yiisi wa'azu n‑ti seeba, ka m mammaŋŋa kɔŋŋi nyaabu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: M mabiisi, m bu boori ya kpalim ziilinsi ni, see ka ya baŋŋi, ni, ti badima zaa daa la'am m‑bɛ sangbaŋŋu la lɔŋni, ka ba zaa daa la'am n‑kpɛ teeku maa puuni n‑lɔŋŋi.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Tɔ, lala n daa nyɛ di: ka di ŋmanni la hoo ban daa su ba zaa koom n‑ti Musa, sangbaŋŋu ni teeku la puuni.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Ba zaa daa la'am n‑di diseebu din daa pa duniya wa diibu.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: ka daa nyu bunnyuuriseebu bun daa pa duniya wa dinni. Ni, tampii maa daa dɔli ba - pa duniya wa tampii n‑daa la - ka koom daa yi ka ni na; ka lala tampii maa daa nyɛ Kirisita Masiiya.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Di zaa yɔri, ba pam n daa nyɛ seeba Naawunni sufu ŋɔn daa bu maai ba zugu; ka ba daa kpi n‑kpalim n‑dɔya yoo maa ni.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Tɔ, ka anŋɔ n nyɛ an kpaamni tirima n‑wulisiri ti, ni, ti di dɔli kɔribeeri hoo ban daa dɔli seem.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Halli yan di leebi ban maanni ba'a, hoo ban seeba daa maanni la - hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, Niriba maa daa zinni ni ba ti di ka nyu, ka naayi doaai na nti deemna.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Ti di laa n‑niŋi zina tuma, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa niŋi seem, ka ba niriba tusa pisi ni ata daa kpi dahinyinni la.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Ka ti di zamsiri ti Dugma, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa zamsira ka wigi daa ku ba.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Yaasa, ti di nyurimna, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa nyurimni maa ka malaaka kum daana daa kyɛ ka ba baasi yɔri.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Tɔ: anŋɔ zaa daa paai ba na, ka a wulisiri ti yɛla, ka ba daa sɔbi a n‑dooli ni ba ti kpaamni ti a zugu - tirima ban kpalim nti nya duniya naari saha paari na.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Dizugu, i yi teesi, ni, i zɛ la deede, nyin baŋŋma ka di lu!.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Yan nya zamsigu seem, di bu yakki n‑gaari ninsaalidima ŋɔn nyari seaa. Yɛdda daana n nyɛ Naawunni, u ku kyɛ ka zamsigu seaa kan gaari ya paai ya. Halli zamsigu yi ti paai ya na, u nin bɔ sɔseaa kan nin kyɛ ka ya nyaŋŋi.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Dinŋɔna, m mabiwutta, ya zɔm ka yi ba'a ni buga gyeeŋŋu ni.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: Ya nini n neaai, ka di zugu ka n tɔ'asiri lala n‑tiri ya. Ya mammansi kur baŋŋma n yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Ti yi mari bunnyuura kɔbeaa la m‑puusi Naawunni barika, pa la tin la'amni n‑tɔtti Kirisita ziim la zugu? Ti mun yi zaŋŋi diibu la n‑ŋmaari n‑tiri taaba, pa la tin la'amni n‑tɔtti Kirisita ningbina zugu?.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Tɔ, ka diibu yinni m bɛ ni, ka tirim ban zoaai la'amni n‑ŋɔbra, ka ti ti leebi ninvugyinni ningbina, dama ti zaa la'am n‑diri diibu yinni.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Ya bu ya teesi Izraɛli dima maa yɛla? Ban nyɛ Izraɛli dima duniya wa soori zugu la, niriba zaa ban la'amni m‑maanni sara la, ba laa n‑la'amni n‑tɔtti numni maa n‑ŋɔbra.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Bɔ ka n wulisiri wa? - Ni, ba'ari numni nyɛ seelli? bee, ni, ba'ari nyɛ seelli?.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Ay! - amaa ba'amaanniba bu maanni Naawunni, arizimbeeri ka ba maanna. Ka m bu boori ni ya la'amni n‑deaari arizimbeeri yɛla tɔra.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ya ku toaai n‑nyu ti Dugma bunnyuura kɔbeaa ni, ka laa n‑nyu arizimbeeri dinni m‑paasi - lala ka soori. Ya ku toaai n‑di ti Dugma laa ni, ka laa n‑di arizimbeeri dinni ni - lala gba ka soori.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Bee, ti ya kyɛ ka ti Dugma nyuuri yisigi? Ammi, ti mari yaa n‑gaari u? Ay - ti ka, walla!.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Ni, M mari la seelikam yiiko. - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŋŋsim. Ni, M mari la seelikam yiiko. - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri niriba beesisuŋŋu.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka sɔkam bɔ u mammaŋŋa yɛla, amaa u taraana yɛla ka u boora.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Ya ŋɔbma nakoosidima numkoosa zaa, amaa di boosiri numseesi yɛla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Dama di sɔbiya, ni, Duniya wa, ni din bɛ ka ni maa - a zaa nyɛ la ti Dugma dinni.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Tɔ. Sɔ ŋɔn bu niŋi yɛdda yi ti zuusi ya u bundirigu ka ya boori kyɛnni, yan dima ban zaali seelli na zaa; yan di boosiri a yɛla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Amaa sɔ yi ti yɛli ya, ni, Ba'ari numni nŋɔa. dinŋɔna yan di di, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yɛli ya lala maa zugu, ni yan ku ti galim sɔ sufu ni teesa zugu.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: - Tɔ, lala nira maa sufu ni teesa yɛla ka n tɔ'asiri maa, pa ya mammaŋŋa dina. Mani n su m mammaŋŋa yɛla, ka dizugu n ku kyɛ ka sɔ zaŋŋi u sufu ni teesa n‑galim ma ka di mugsi ma.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: N yi puusi Naawunni ka di diibu maa, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑galim ma, diibu wa zugu? - dama m pun puusi Naawunni di zugu.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Dizugu ya yi diri diibu, bee, n‑nyuuri bunnyuura, bee, ya yi niŋni seelli zaa, ya kyɛ ka ya tumni lala n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ burigi n‑lu ya yɛla zugu - pa Izraɛli dima, bee ban pa Izraɛli niriba, halli Naawunni niriba gba.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Lala ka man moora, ni n kyɛ ka sɔkam sufu maai n yɛla zugu. M bu boori m mammaŋŋa anfaani yɛla, see din mari anfaani n‑tiri sɔkam - di nyɛ la: ni, ba nya tuliginsim.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Ya dɔli n soori maa, hoo man gba dɔli Kirisita Masiiya soori seem la.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Tɔ: ka m pɛ'ari ya, dama ya teaari n yɛla sahakam ka kpalim n‑dɔli wulisigu maa zaa, hoo man daa zaali ya seem la.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Amaa m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, dɔppa saani sɔkam zaa zuguraana n nyɛ Kirisita Masiiya, pɔ'a mun zuguraana nyɛ la u sira, hoo Kirisita zuguraana ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Dɔsɔ ŋɔn pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, di daama diisiri u maŋŋa vi.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Amaa pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn mun bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, lala pɔ'a diisiri u maŋŋa vi. U yi ti niŋi lala, di ŋmanni la hoo ŋɔn laa m‑puni u zugu ka doaai ni zugkoaa n‑tɔ'asira.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Dama pɔ'a yi ku sakki, ni, u bɔbi u zugu, dinŋɔna ŋɔn kyeema, bee, m‑puni ka doaai n‑zaani zugkoaa. Ka lala mun yi nyɛ la vi, dinŋɔna see ka u bɔbi u zugu.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Doo bu sum ni u pili u zugu, dama Naawunni ŋmaari n nyɛ u, n‑tisiri Naawunni gyirima; pɔ'a mun tisiri doo maa gyirima.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Dama ba daa bu zaŋŋi pɔ'a m‑maali doowa, amaa ba daa naam doowa pɔi ka naayi yiisi pɔ'a u saani na.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Dizugu pa pɔ'a zugu ka doo daa bɛ ni, amaa doo maa zugu ka ba daa maali pɔ'a maa.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Dinŋɔna see ka pɔ'a maa bɔbi u zugu suŋŋa, ban su u fukumsi zugu, ni malaakadima la zugu.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Ni dinŋɔ zaa, ti Dugma saani pɔ'a pa u kɔkɔa ka kyɛ doowa, ka doo gba pa u kɔkɔa ka kyɛ pɔ'a.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Ba daa naam doo ka yiisi pɔ'a u saani na, ka pɔ'a mun yi laa n‑dɔ'ari doowa. Ka seelikam yi la Naawunni saani.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Ya mammansi teesim din sumsi ni: pɔ'a yi bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni - di mari soori?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Duniya wa beesigu kaari wulisiri ti lala - ni, doo yi mari zɔbwɔ'ala, di mari la vi.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Pɔ'a mun yi mari u zɔbwɔ'ala, di mari la gyirima n‑ti u. Ba ti u lala zɔbri ni a ti leebi bɔbsuŋŋu n‑ti u.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Sɔ yi ya laa n‑ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dinŋɔ zugu, kyɛ ka n yɛli ya, ni, tirima bu dɔli lala soori, halli ya'aseelikam Naawunni niriba ŋɔn la'amni n‑gyeemna.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Tɔ: ka man ŋɔn ya tɔ'asi yɛliseesi n‑ti ya wa, di pa la pɛ'abu zugu ka n nin tɔ'asi. Dama yan la'am taaba sahanseaa la, di bu kyɛ ka ya yɛla maa kyɛnni tooni: ay! - amaa a labri la nyaaŋŋa.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Dama, daŋŋni, n wum ya lahabaari, ni, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya gyeem Naawunni, ya mari la nangbanwala - ka n sakki n sufu ni, ni, di bu ta'ari ni ya bɛ lala.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Dama see ka nangbanwala bɛ ya ni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka Naawunni nini ŋɔn tigi seeba la yɛla ti yi paalu ni.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya yi la'am taaba, di pa la ti Dugma diibu ka ya dira.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Dama ya yi diri ni, sɔkam diri la u mammaŋŋa diibu n‑daŋŋni u taraana; ka kɔm mari seeba, halli ka seeba mun nyuuri nti bugra.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Wula? Ya ka yiya n‑nin toaai n‑di ka nyu a puuni, bee? Bee, Naawunni niriba gyeeŋŋu la'aŋŋu maa ka ya gɔsi yɔri, halli nti zeemni ban ka, ka diisiri ba vi? Ammi, n nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? - Ay! n ku pɛ'ai ya, walla!.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Dama man daa deaai seelli ti Dugma saani la ka n daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisi ya: ni, yunseaa ban daa figsi ti Dugma Yisa maa, u daa daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi diibu la.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: m‑puusi Naawunni pɛ'abu n‑yɛli, ni, N ningbina nŋɔa n‑ti yarima; ya niŋma lala ka teaari n yɛla.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Ka ban daa di n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi kɔbeaa gba lala, ni, Naawunni nangbanyinni zaalipaalli maa nŋɔa; n ziim nin maali lala zaaligu maa n‑ti ya. Ya yi kur ti nyu kanŋɔ sahanseaa zaa, yan teaama n yɛla.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Tɔ, ka sahanseaa kam ya yi ti di bundira wa ka nyu kɔbeaa wa bunnyuura, ya moonni la ti Dugma kum yɛla, halli ni u lappu na.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Dizugu ŋɔn mi, ni, u bu dɔli soori, ka laa n‑di bundira wa, bee, n‑nyu bunnyuura wa, u galimni la ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: See ka nira viisi u maŋŋa suŋŋa pɔi ka naayi di bundira maa ka nyu kɔbeaa maa bunnyuura.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Dama ŋɔnkam di ka nyu ka bu baŋŋi di gbinni, ni, ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa nŋɔa, di daama bɔ la sariya karibu m‑basi u maŋŋa zugu.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Lala zugu kyɛ ka ya niriba pam kɔŋŋi yaa ka beera; halli ya seeba mun pun ka ni.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Amaa ti yi viisi ti mansi ka kari ti toori sariya, ba ku ti laa n‑kari ti sariya n‑lusi ti.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Amaa ti Dugma yi viisiri ti yɛla ka kari ti sariya, u wubsiri ti n‑zaanni ti soori ni, ni lala ti kyɛ ka ba ku la'am ti ni duniya wa dima n‑kari ti sariya n‑lusi ti.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Ka dinŋɔna, m mabiisi, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya ti di, see ka ya guri taaba.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Kɔm yi mari sɔa, ŋɔn dima u yiri ni pɔi, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ya yi la'am taaba ya ku nya galinsi di zugu. Tɔ: n yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, n nin laa n‑wulisi ya din kpalim la zaa.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: N yi nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri ninsaalidima yɛttɔ'a kam zaa, halli ni malaakadima yɛttɔ'a gba, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŋɔna n ŋmanni la gbiliŋŋa bee feeŋŋa n‑kumni yɔri.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: N yi niŋi anabitiri, ka laa m‑mari bunsɔ'ara zaa bamsim, m‑mari yɛdda halli nti kyɛ ka zɔya vuui m‑paai seela, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŋɔna m pa seelli.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Halli n yi zaŋŋi n ligri ni n neema zaa n‑tɔri fara dima, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi n ningbiŋŋu n‑kyɛ ka ba nyɔ ka bugum, ka mun ka wurilim, n ka anfaani anŋɔ zaa zugu.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Wurilim mari la sugru ni nambɔzoaa; wurilim bu zabri nyuuri; ka mun bu nyuuri bara, bee, m‑mari karimbaani.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Wurilim bu tumni gyɛrilim; di bu boori di mammaŋŋa yuubu, bee, n‑gyɛ suuri yooma: wurilim bu teaari taalli n‑guri ni di ti boosi.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Wurilim sufu bu maara ninvugseeba tumbeeri zugu; amaa ka mari la sufumaasim yɛlimaŋni zaa zugu.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Wurilim ta'ari ni, m‑mari yɛdda seelikam puuni, ka laa m‑mari tama ka diri sugru seelikam puuni.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Wurilim bu naara: amaa anabitiri yɛla yi bɛ ni, a nin paai a tɔriga; ka ba yi ti tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ba nin naa n‑sini ba noori; bamsim gba nin paai di naari.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Dama ti bamsim maa pa la bamsimunni, ka ti anabitiri gba pa la bummunni.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Ka din nyɛ bummunni deede la yi ti paai na, lala saha ka an pa bummuna la nin gaari n‑naai.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Ya bu nya? man daa nyɛ bibila sahanseaa la, n daa mari la biilim n yɛttɔ'a puuni, ni n teesa ni n yɛm yɛla zaa ni. Amaa man daa leebi bunkurigu maa, n daa zaŋŋi biilim yɛla maa zaa m‑basi.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Dama pampaŋŋɔ ti nyari liisaliisa hoo mansɔ'asi ni, amaa ka ti nin nya kasi hoo nini ŋɔn kpa taaba, din daari maa. Pampaŋŋɔ n na bu mi yɛla zaa suŋŋa, a seesi kɔkɔ ka m mi; amaa din daari maa n nin ti baŋŋi di zaa kasi, hoo ban baŋŋi ma seem la.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Dizugu an kpalim m‑bɛ ni n nyɛ yɛdda, ni tama, ni wurilim, abata wa; amaa din gaari a zaa nyɛ la wurilim.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Tɔ; ka n ya tɔ'asi Sii Kasi piini yɛla n‑tiri ya: m mabiisi, m bu boori ni seelli ti kpalim ka ya bu baŋŋi di.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ya mi halli, ni, yan daa na bu niŋi tuuba la, ba daa yɔmsi ya ka ya dɔli ba'a an bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asira.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Dizugu n yɛtti ya, ni, Naawunni Sii ŋɔn bɛ sɔ saani zaa, di daama ku toaai n‑yɛli Yisa baasima yɔri! Ka di yi pa Sii Kasi maa zugu, sɔ mun ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, Yisa nyɛ la ti Dugma.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Tɔ: ka piini balibubalibu m bɛ ni, amaa Sii yinni maa bɛ ni n‑tisira.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Gyeeŋŋu sɔya balibubalibu m bɛ ni, amaa ti Dugma yinni ka ti gyeemna.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Tuma balibubalibu gba bɛ ni, amaa Naawunni yinni maa n tumni a zaa sɔkam puuni.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Sii maa wulisiri u maŋŋa n‑tiri sɔkam zaa, ni di zaa ti maali ka kyaŋŋi tooni.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Sii maa zugu ka ninvugsɔ mari yɛm yɛttɔ'a; ka sɔ mun mari bamsim, lala Sii yinni maa zugu.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Sɔ yaasa mari la yɛdda lala Sii maa zugu, ka sɔ mun bɛ ni ka u deaai baaridima tippu, Sii yinni maa zugu.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Seeba yaasa deaari la alahazibu tuma yiiko, bee, anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, bee, m‑baŋŋi siisi yɛla viisigu, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, bee, m‑baŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni n‑wulisira.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la Sii yinni maa tuma, ka u mari n‑tisiri u sufu ŋɔn boori seem.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Di ŋmani la hoo ninsaala ningbiŋŋu ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ka nyɛ la bunyinni, amaa ka mari la luga pam; ka luga mun zaa, halli ni an zoaai seem maa zaa, a la'amni n‑niŋi ningbiŋŋu yinni. Tɔ: ka Kirisita Masiiya bɛ la lala.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Sii yinni maa zugu ka ti zaa daa su Naawunni koom, ka di daa kyɛ ka ti la'am n‑niŋi ningbiŋŋu yinni; halli ni ti seeba daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima ka seeba mun daa nyɛ Griikadima gba; ka seeba daa nyɛ daaba, seeba mun daa su ba mansi; ti zaa daa la'am n‑nyu Sii yinni maa.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Tɔ, ka ningbiŋŋu muna - pa lugri yinni kɔkɔ nla, amaa luga pam.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ya bu nya? - Sɔ nɔbri yi ti yɛli, ni, Tɔ, m pa la nuu, dizugu m pa ningbiŋŋu maa lugri, halli fiiŋ gba!, lala maa ku kyɛ ka ka siri pa ningbiŋŋu maa luginseaa.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Bee, sɔ tubri yi ti yɛli, ni, Tɔ, m pa la nimbiri, dizugu m pa ningbiŋŋu lugri kɔtaa!, lala maa ku kyɛ ka ka siri pa ningbiŋŋu maa luginseaa.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Ya bu nya? - ningbiŋŋu zaa yi da nyɛ la nimbiri, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑wumna? Bee, ka zaa yi da nyɛ la tubri, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑wumni nyuu?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Amaa din nyɛ seem maa, Naawunni daa niŋi luga maa zaa la a lugri a lugri, hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, a zaa yi da nya lugri yinni kɔkɔa, yani ka ningbiŋŋu da ya bɛ ni?.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Din nyɛ seem maa, luga maa zoaai ni, amaa a la'amni n‑niŋi ningbimmunni.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Nimbiri ku toaai n‑yɛli nuu, ni, I ka bukaata man saani!, bee, zugu yɛli nɔba, ni, Ya ka ma bukaata.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Ay! - halli luginseesi an ka yaa mari la bukaata gba.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Ka luginseesi tin yɛtti, ni, an ka gyirima la n nyɛ a, ana ka ti gɔsiri suŋŋa; ka a seesi an bu veela la, ana ka ti mari suŋŋa vi zugu.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: - halli ka a bunveela la bu boori lala suŋŋsim. Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn maali ningbiŋŋu seem maa, u zaŋŋi la gyirima n‑ti luginseesi an yɛla bu mugsi pam la.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: Naawunni tum lala ni u ti kyɛ ka nangbankpeemni ni wɔligri ka ningbiŋŋu maa ni, amaa ka luga maa zaa ti suŋŋni m‑mari taaba.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Lugri yinni yi ti namsi, a zaa yi la'am n‑namsi ni; lugri yinni mun yi nya pɛ'abu, sufu maari n nyɛ di n‑tiri a zaa.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Tɔ: yarima mun n la'am n‑nyɛ Kirisita ningbiŋŋu, ka ya zaa yinni yinni n nyɛ u ningbinluga.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Ka Naawunni daa yiisi u niriba ka ba tumni tuma balibubalibu n‑tiri taaba: u daa daŋŋi n‑zaali seeba ni ba ti niŋi u tuumudima maa; ka laa n‑zaali seeba m‑pa anabidima; nta karimbadima, m‑paasi ban tumni alahazibu tuma, ban tibri baaridima, ban suŋŋni niriba, ni Naawunni niriba toondaandima, ni ban tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Sɔkam zaa nyɛ la tuumu? - Ay! Sɔkam nyɛ la anabi, bee, karimba? Sɔkam n toaai n‑tumni alahazibu tuma? - Ay! di pa lala.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: M‑paasi yaasa: sɔkam tibri la baaridima? Sɔkam n toaai n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu? Bee, m‑baŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a gbinni? - Ay!.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna ya booma piiniseesi an tibsa la. Halli ya kyɛ ka n wulisi ya sɔseaa kan veeli n‑gaari zaa#:-.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Ka dinŋɔna ya dɔli wurilim sɔya, ka bɔ Sii maa piini; ka kan daŋŋi tooni la nyɛ la anabitiri yɛla piini.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Dama ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu maa bu tɔ'asiri n‑tiri saalidima, amaa Naawunni ka u tɔ'asira. Halli sɔsɔ bu baŋŋni a gbinni, ka u kpalim n‑yɛtti yɛlisɔ'ara u siiya puuni.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Ŋɔn mun tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a maa yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri niriba, di paasiri ba beesigu ka kpamsiri ba.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Ii - ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu maa paasiri u maŋtoori beesigu, ŋɔn mun yɛtti anabitiri yɛla la paasiri Naawunni niriba zaa beesigu.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Tɔ, n da boori la ni ya zaa toaai n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ka laa m‑boori ni ya yɛtti anabitiri yɛla, ka lala yi sɔa. Anabi gaari ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu, di yi pa la sɔ bɛ ni n‑wulisiri yɛttɔ'a balibu maa gbinni ka lala nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Tɔ, m mabiisi: n yi kyɛ ya saani na n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, n suŋŋni ya la wula? Ay, zaalim! - ka di yi pa la n laa m‑mari yɛlisɔ'ara kasigri, bee, bamsim yɛla, bee, anabitiri yɛla, bee, wulisigu n‑ti ya na.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Halli bunseesi an ka nyɔvuri saani di kur bɛ la lala: ya bu nya? - bunŋmeera hoo yiiya bee goongyi yi bu ŋmɛ n‑gɔligi suŋŋa, wula ka nira ya baŋŋi ŋmeebu maa gbinni?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Ka bunseaa ban ŋmeeri m‑moonni tɔppu yɛla la muna - ka yi bu ŋmɛ n‑gɔligi, wula ka ba ya baŋŋi n‑niŋi tɔppu siri?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: Ya mammansi yɛttɔ'a ni gba, ya yi bu kyɛ ka ya yɛla yiiri suŋŋa, wula ka sɔ ya niŋi m‑baŋŋi yan yɛtti seelli maa gbinni? Ay! - lala kur ŋmanni la yan tɔ'asiri n‑tiri pɛsigu.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Yɛttɔ'a balibu pam siri bɛ duniya wa puuni, ka a seelikam zaa mari ka gbinni.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Amaa sɔ yi ti tɔ'asi n‑ti ma ka m bu wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, u nin gɔsi ma, ni, tinzunnira, ka man mun gɔsiri u gba lala.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Tɔ: dizugu ya mansi ŋɔn boori Sii maa piini la, ya kpaŋŋma n‑nya an paasiri la Naawunni niriba beesigu, ka ya zaa ti kyaŋŋi tooni.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Dinŋɔna, kyɛ ka ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu zaa la ti susiri Naawunni ni u kyɛ ka u toaai n‑wulisiri di gbinni gba.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Dama n yi tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu m‑puusi Naawunni, n sii maa puusiri ni, amaa n yɛm ka anfaani di puuni.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Dinŋɔna - n ya niŋi la wula? N nin zaŋŋi n sii maa m‑puusi Naawunni, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi n yɛm m‑puusi u; n nin zaŋŋi n sii maa n‑yuum yuuma n‑ti Naawunni, ka zaŋŋi n yɛm n‑yuumni m‑paasi.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Bee, i yi puusiri Naawunni i sii puuni, wula ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ bɛni nin niŋi m‑baŋŋi n‑niŋi Ammi! n‑ti i puusigu maa? - dama ŋɔn bu wumni nyin yɛtti seelli wa.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Nyin ŋɔn puusiri suŋŋa maa zaa yɔri, di ku toaai m‑paasi i taraana beesigu.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: Halli man tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu n‑gaari ya zaa, ka m puusi Naawunni di zugu.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Ni di zaa yɔri, n yi bɛ la Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, m boori ni n zaŋŋi n yɛm n‑tɔ'asi beela ni n ti wulisi n taaba, ka di gaari n yi tɔ'asi pam yɛttɔ'azuna ni ka ba bu wumna.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: Tɔ, m mabiisi: yan di teesiri hoo bibisi: yɛlibeeri ziiya ka ya siri nin bɛ ni hoo bileesi, amaa yɛm ziiya see ka ya nin bɛ ni hoo ninvugkura.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Di sɔbiya, Naawunni zaaligu maa ni, ni#:- N nin zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'azuna Ni yan bu mi seeba maa yɛligu N‑tɔ'asi n‑ti butiri wa niriba, Ni di zaa ba ku wum n‑ti ma. - Lala ka ti Dugma yɛli.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Dinŋɔna yɛttɔ'azuna bulisiri yɛla n‑tiri ban ka yɛdda la, pa ban mara; anabitiri yɛla mun bulisiri yɛla n‑tiri ban mari yɛdda, pa ban ka.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Ya bu nya? - Naawunni niriba yi la'am n‑gyeemna, ka ba zaa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ka saamma bee ban ka yɛdda dima kpɛ na, ba ku yɛli, ni, ya kpɛ la yinyaari?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Amaa ya zaa yi tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛla ka lala nira ti kpɛ na, ya zaa zugu ka u nin ti baŋŋi u taalli ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni, ba kari u sariya ka lusi u.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Di saha ka u nin yɛli u sufu yɛlisɔ'ara an bɛ seem zaa, ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem Naawunni ka yɛli, ni, Naawunni siri bɛ ya saani.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Bɔ n yi kpalim, m mabiisi? - Ya yi la'am taaba, sɔkam mari u yuumni, bee, u yɛliwulisira, bee, u bunsɔ'ara kasigri yɛla, bee, u tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu bee n‑wulisiri lala yɛttɔ'a gbinni. Tɔ - ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑kyɛ ka anŋɔ zaa nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Seeba yi ya tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a balibu yɛla, kyɛ ka di nyɛ la babayi-ata kɔkɔa, ka ba tɔ'asi yinniyinni n‑deaai taaba; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka sɔ bɛ ni n‑wulisiri a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Amaa sɔ yi ka ni n‑wulisiri a gbinni, kyɛ ka yɛttɔ'a balibu daana sini Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu ni, ka naayi tɔ'asi n‑tiri u maŋŋa ni Naawunni.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Anabidima maa muna - ya kyɛ ka ba gba ti tɔ'asi ba niriba ayi-ata, ka ban kpalim la kpasi ba yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Ka yɛttɔ'a yi kyɛ sɔ ŋɔn ziya saani na, kyɛ ka ŋɔn pun tɔ'asiri la ti tɔ'asi n‑naai pɔi, ka ŋɔnla naayi deaai yɛttɔ'a.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Dama ya zaa nin toaai n‑tɔ'asi ya anabitiri yɛla n‑deaari taaba yinniyinni, ka sɔkam bamsi ka di kpamsi ba.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Anabidima maa n su ba mammaŋŋa anabitiri yɛla fukumsi.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: dama Naawunni pa la ŋɔn burimna, amaa ŋɔn maanna. See ka ya dɔli ban la'amni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la zaa kaari.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: ka kyɛ ka pɔ'aba kpalim m‑bɛ surimm ya la'aŋŋu maa ni. Ba ka soori ni ba tɔ'asira, see ka ba dɔli soori hoo zaaligu maa ŋɔn yɛli seem la.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Pɔ'a yi boori ni u baŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseaa gbinni, see ka ba naayi kuli yiri ni ka u ti boosi u sira. Pɔ'a yi ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu ni, di mari la vi.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Wula! Naawunni yɛligu daa piili yarima saani, bee? Bee, ya kɔkɔ saani ka di paai?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Sɔ yi yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la anabi, bee, ŋɔn mi Sii maa yɛla, di daama nin sakki, ni, man ŋɔn sɔbri seelli n‑tiri ya wa nyɛ la ti Dugma zaaligu.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Sɔ mun yi bu wumni n‑tiri yɛligu wa, yan di wumni n‑tiri di daama gba.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna, m mabiisi, ya boom suŋŋa anabitiri yɛla, amaa ka di ta'ari yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu yɛla.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: See ka bunseelikam niŋi suŋŋa ka dɔli soori deede.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Tɔ, m mabiisi, n ya teaai ya la man ŋɔn daa mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti ya seem. Lahabaari wa ka ya daa deaai, ka lahabaari maa zugu ka ya kpalim n‑zɛ yummu.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Halli lala lahabasuŋŋu zugu ka ya nya tuliginsim, ya yi kpalim n‑dɔla wulisiseaa man daa mooli n‑ti ya la suŋŋa. Di yi pa lala, ya yɛdda bɛ la yɔri.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Lahabaari wa ka n daa deaai seeba saani ka laa n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti ya, ka di yɛttɔ'akpanni nŋɔa: ni, Kirisita Masiiya daa kpi ti tumbeeri zugu, hoo Litaafi maa ŋɔn daa pun wulisi seem.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: Ka di laa n‑wulisi, ni, ba daa sɔ'ai u, ni, dabsa ata daari ka u daa neaai kum ni, hoo Litaafi maa ŋɔn daa pun wulisi seem.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: Ni, u daa kyɛ ka Siifasi Piita nya u pɔi, ka u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi gba daa naayi nya u.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka u daa kyɛ ka ti mabiisi bɔbgu daa nya u, ka ba daa zoaai n‑gaari ninvugkɔbsinu. Niriba wa pam na bɛ ba nyɔvuya ni, amaa baseeba laa n‑ka ni.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑kyɛ ka Gyeemsi nya u, ka u tuumudima zaa gba daa nya u.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Anŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa, u daa naayi kyɛ ka n gba nya u, hoo ban gbirigi n‑dɔ'ai sɔa.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Dama mani n kpalim tuumudima maa zaa nyaaŋŋa, halli m bu sum ni ba booli ma Naawunni tuumu gba, dama n daa katti Naawunni niriba n‑namsiri ba.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Amaa Naawunni piini zugu kyɛ ka m bɛ la man bɛ seem; ka lala piini maa bu basi yɔri, dama n daa tum tuma n‑gaari ba zaa - halli di pa la mani n‑tumna, amaa Naawunni piini ŋɔn mari ma n‑tumna.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Tɔ, di yi nyɛ la mani bee bana, lala ka ti moonna, ka lala ka ya daa niŋi yɛdda.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Tɔ: ti moonni n‑yɛtti, ni, Kirisita daa neaai kum ni; ka wula ka ya seeba yɛtti, ni, kum neaari ka ni?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Kum neaari yi ka ni, dinŋɔna Kirisita daa bu neaai.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Kirisita mun yi daa bu neaai kum ni, dinŋɔna ti moonni la lahabaari maa yɔri, ka ya yɛdda niŋbu gba nyɛ la yɔri.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: M‑paasi lala, ba nin nya ti la, ni, zirima'ariba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni yɛla. Dama ti yɛtti, ni, Naawunni n daa neaai Kirisita kum ni: amaa ban kpi la yi ku toaai n‑neaai kum ni, dinŋɔna u daa bu neaai u.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Ii! - ban kpi yi ku toaai n‑neaai kum ni, dinŋɔna Kirisita gba daa bu neaai.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Kirisita mun yi bu neaai kum ni, dinŋɔna ya yɛdda ka gbinni ka ya na kpalim m‑bɛ ya tumbeeri ni.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Ka lala naa n‑kyɛ ka Kirisita niriba ban ka ni la gba baasi yɔri.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Di yi nyɛ la: ni, ti ka tama Kirisita ni, di yi pa la duniya wa ni kɔkɔa, dinŋɔna ti wahala gaari sɔkam zaa dinni.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Amaa yɛlimaŋni ka Kirisita daa siri neaai n‑yisigi kum ni; u pun daŋŋi ban kpi la zaa lala - hoo ban daŋŋi n‑kyɛ naara pɔi ka naayi nya puri antaam maa zaa seem la.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Ya bu nya? - ninsaalisɔ zugu ka kum daa paai ti zaa na, ka dizugu kum ni neaari gba daa paai ti na ninsaalisɔ zugu.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Ii! - Adam zugu ka sɔkam kpiira; Kirisita mun zugu ka sɔkam nin neaai kum ni.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Amaa sɔkam ni u saha: Kirisita daŋŋi hoo naara ŋɔn daŋŋi seem la, ka ban pa nyɛ la Kirisita niriba - ba saha nyɛ la u lappu na dabsiri daari.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka di nin naai: Kirisita nin sa'am yiiko dima ni fukumsi dima zaa yɛla ka kyɛ ka ba baasi yɔri pɔi, ka naayi zaŋŋi naam maa yɛla n‑niŋi u Ba Naawunni nuusi ni.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Dama see ka u di naam halli nti nyaŋŋi u dindaandima zaa.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Ka u dindaansɔ ŋɔn ti ya kpalim nyaaŋŋa ka u sa'am u yɛla nyɛ la kum.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Ni, U zaŋŋi bunseelikam n‑kyɛ ka u nyaŋŋi ka su a fukumsi. Tɔ, ka ŋɔn Naawunni yi ti zaŋŋi bunseelikam n‑ti u, di wulisi, ni, di pa la ŋɔn Naawunni fukumsi ka u su.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Ka Naawunni Bii maa muna - u yi ti nyaŋŋi n‑naai, u mammaŋŋa gba nin kpɛ u Ba maasim ni, sɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi seelikam n‑ti u ni u ti nyaŋŋi; ka lala nin kyɛ ka Naawunni naayi leebi seelikam sɔkam saani.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Tɔ: ka ba yi yɛtti, ni, kum ni neaari ka ni, ka ban suuri Naawunni koom n‑tiri ban pun kpi la ya niŋi la wula? Ban kpi maa yi bu neaari fass, wula zugu ka ba suuri koom n‑tiri ba?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Tirima muna - wula zugu ka ti diri wahala sahakam?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: N kpiiri ni beoo kam - lala n siri nyɛ di, m mabiisi - hoo man mari sufumaasim n‑duusiri m maŋŋa ya zaa yɛla zugu seem la, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita saani la.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Halli n daa bɛ la Ɛfɛsusi timpuuni n‑zabri ni mooni bunkɔbbeeri. Tɔ, di yi nyɛ la kulla ninsaala beesigu wa yɛla zugu kɔkɔa, n ku nya anfaani di zugu. Ban kpi yi bu neaara, ni, Ya kyɛ ka ti diri ka nyuura, Dama beoo ti sa nin kpi.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya: ni, Zɔbeoo sa'amni la halli beesisuŋŋu gba soori.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Ya maalima n‑teesi yɛlimaŋni yɛla, ka di laa n‑tumni tumbeeri. Yaseeba bɛ ni m‑bu mi Naawunni yɛla. Ya vi yɛttɔ'a ka n yɛtti wa.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Tɔ: di yi pa seem sɔ nin boosi n‑yɛli, ni, Kpiimma neaari la wula n‑doaari na? Wula, ningbimbɔ ka ba ya zaŋŋi n‑yisigi.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Nyin gyɛrigu wa! I yi buri bumburiseelli, ka ku toaai m‑buli na n‑niŋi di yi pa la ka daŋŋi n‑kpi.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: I yi buri, pa kan ya buli n‑yi na la ka i butta, kulla bumburli hoo kawanni, bee, kyi bumburiseaa.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Amaa Naawunni n yi tiri ka ningbinseaa ŋɔn boori maa; bumbutiri zaa ni ka u tisiri ka ningbiŋŋu.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Ningbina zaa pa yummu: ninsaala ningbiŋŋu m bɛ ni, ni bunkɔbri dina ŋɔn bɛ ni, niinsi gba dina bɛ a kɔkɔa, ni zimi gba dina.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Saazugu ningbina bɛ ni, ka kyɛ duniya wa ni ningbina, ka a zaa mari a veelim, ka a nyɛ la a kookooba.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Wuntaŋŋa mari ka veelim, ka namboaa mun mari ka dinni, ni saŋmariwuusi gba mara; ka saŋmariwuseaa veelim bu ŋmanni la ka taraana dinni.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Tɔ: ka kum ni neaari nin nyɛ la lala. Ba zaŋŋi bunseesi an sa'amni ni la n‑sɔ'ai, ka bunseesi an nin bɛ ni sahakam la nin doaai na.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Ban zeemni seelli la ka ba sɔ'ara, ka ka nin doaai na n‑nya gyirima. Bumbugsiri m butti tiŋŋa, bunkpeeŋŋu mun nin buli n‑doaai na.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Ba zaŋŋi duniya wa ni ningbina n‑sɔ'ara; ka ka nin neaai na hoo ningbinseaa Sii maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi nyɔvuri n‑tiri ka maa. Duniya wa ni ninsaala ningbina siri bɛ ni, ka ningbina laa m‑bɛ ni seesi Sii maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi nyɔvuri n‑tisiri maa.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, Daŋŋni nira la, Adam, daa leebi la ninsaala. sɔ ŋɔn vu la: halli ka Adam sɔ naayi kyɛ na nti leebi siiya n‑tiri sɔkam nyɔvurimaŋni.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Amaa sii maa daana daa bu daŋŋi na: ay! - duniya dinni la n daa daŋŋi n‑kyɛ na ka sii maa daana la naayi pa.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Ba daa zaŋŋi tanni m‑maali daŋŋni ninsaala maa, amaa ŋɔn mun pa buyi la ŋɔna, u yi la arizana ni na.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Hoo tanni daana ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la, lala ka u duniya wa taaba gba bɛ ni; ka arizana niriba mun bɛ la hoo ba mun toondaana, arizana ni doo la.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Dizugu ti pun mi tanni doo wa beesigu beebu, n‑ŋmanni hoo ŋɔna ŋɔn bɛ seem; ka lala yaasa ti nin ŋmanni hoo arizana doowa nti bɛ u gba beesigu.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: M mabiisi, n yɛtti ya wa, ni, duniya wa ningbina kur nyɛ la numni ni ziim, a ku toaai n‑nya Naawunni naam beebu; halli ka kan sa'amni ni la ku toaai n‑di faari kan bu naara.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Ya wumma! N yɛtti ya la yɛlisɔ'ara yɛla: pa ti zaasa n nin kpi, amaa ba nin takki ti zaa.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: Ba nin takki ti yoomyooma, hoo nimbiri kamsigu saha. Lala saha ka ba nin kyɛ ka kikaa ti kum n‑wulisi duniya naari saha paari na. Dama ba nin peebi, ka ban kpi la zaa nin doaai ka ku laa n‑kpi; ka tirima mun nin takki.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: See ka kan sa'amni la ti deaai beesigu din bu naara, hoo ban deaari sutura n‑yeeri seem la. Ka kan kpiiri la nin deaai beesigu din bu kpiira n‑yɛa.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Lala saha maa ka kan sa'amna ka kpiiri la ti deaai kan bu laa n‑kpiira n‑yɛ, ka di siri nin niŋi hoo ban daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- Ba vɔli kum n‑nyaŋŋi.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: ni#:- Ha! Kum, i nyaŋŋsim bɛ la yani? Ha! Kum, i dumpu yiiko lee.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Dama dumpu seaa kum ŋɔn mari n‑dumni ti la nyɛ la tumbeeri taalli; ka taalli maa yiiko bɛ la zaaligu la ni.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Dinŋɔna ti puusiri Naawunni barika! Dama ŋɔna n kyɛ ka ti nyaŋŋi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Lala zugu, m mabiwutta, see ka ya bɛ yummu deede; yan di bɛ kpa, ka bɛ kpa. Ya zaŋŋma ya mansi zaa n‑ti ti Dugma tuma maa sahakam, dama ya mi halli, ni, ya yi tumni pam n‑tiri ti Dugma, ya bu tumni la yɔri.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Tɔ: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi toosigu n‑ti Naawunni niriba la yɛla: man daa wulisi Galɛseaa dima maa seem la, ya gba niŋma lala.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Di nyɛ la: ni, bakwai piiligu dabsiri daari zaa, see ka ya sɔkam yinniyinni yiisiri beela n‑zaali m‑mara, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn lɔrigi u yɛla seem maa; lala nin kyɛ ka man yi ti kyɛ na sahanseaa la, toosigu yɛla ku laa m‑bɛ ni yaasa.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: N yi ti paai na, n nin sɔbi gbana n‑zaŋŋi a n‑ti yan yiisi seeba la, ni ya piini la m‑paasi, ka tum ba ni ba ti zi a n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Di mun yi sum ni man gba kyaŋŋi, ba nin beeli ma ka ti kyaŋŋi.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: N yi dɔli Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu n‑gaari, n nin kyɛ ya saani na. Ii, n siri ya n‑dɔli Masidoonya pɔi.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: ka di bu ya'ai ka n niŋi dabsa ayi ya saani, bee, n nin kpalim ya saani halli ni waari saha ti naai. Lala ka ya nin nyaŋŋi n‑zaali ma soori ni, ni n ti kyaŋŋi n kyɛnni maa, di yi nyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm bee seela zaa.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Dama m bu boori ni n nyɛ ya ka n nɔba n zɛ saazugu ka gaari yooma; m boori ni Naawunni yi sakki, ni, n niŋi la dabsa ayi ya saani.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Amaa n nin kpalim m‑bɛ Ɛfɛsusi halli ni DaPisinu Daari Pɛntikoosi Kyuu la saha.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Dama ba lɔrigi soori n‑ti ma ni n tum tunsuma pam, amaa ka niriba pam ta'ari ma di ni.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Timɔti yi ti paai ya na, yan kyɛ ka u bɛ ya saani ka dabeem ka u, dama u tumni la ti Dugma tuma maa hoo man gba ŋɔn tumni seem la.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Ya sɔ di zeem u. Ya zaalima u soori zugu ni sufu maasim, ka u ti labi n saani na. Dama n guri la u kyɛnni na, ŋɔn ni ti mabiisi zaa.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Ti mabii Apɔloosi ŋɔna - n daa susi u nimbirimoaa, ni, u kpaŋŋma u maŋŋa n‑dɔli ti mabiisi wa n‑kyɛ ya saani na; amaa u daa bu boori ni u kyɛ na pampaŋŋɔ, halli fiiŋ: ni, u yi ti nya sahasuŋŋu, u nin kyɛ na.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna ya baŋŋma suŋŋa! Ka zɛ deede ya yɛdda ni, m‑mari sufukpeemni ka kpamsiri ya mansi.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: See ka ya niŋi bunseelli kam wurilim zugu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Ma mabiisi, ya pun mi Sitɛfanasi yɛla: ni, ŋɔn ni u yiri dima maa daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda n‑daŋŋi Akaaya dima zaa; ka ba zikki zaa bɛ la yɛdda dima la suŋŋsim ni. Dizugu ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi, m mabiisi.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: ni ya wumni n‑tiri lala niriba, ni ba nimmoosi tuntuntaaba zaa.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Sitɛfanasi ŋɔn daa paai ma na, ŋɔn ni Fɔtunaatusi ni Akaayakusi, n sufu daa maai, dama ba nin zɛ ya zaazii maa ni, yan ka n saani maa zugu.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Dama ba daa labsi n sii na, ni ya gba siisi m‑paasi. Yan di tam lala niriba yɛla.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Ti kpa wa mabiisi m puusiri ya ni - Naawunni niriba ban la'amni m‑bɛ ni Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu tinsi ni zaa. Akwila gba puusiri ya na, ni u pɔ'a Prisila, ni ban la'amni ba yiri maa ni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Halli ti mabiisi zaa puusira. Ya puusima taaba suŋŋasuŋŋa ti wurilim puusigu ni.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Ya nyam sɔppu wa - man sɔbri m‑puusiri ya n toori nuu ni sɔppu: Pɔla .

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Sɔ yi bu wuri ti Dugma, mbusu bɛ di daama zugu. Marana ta! , ni, Kyɛm na, ti Dugma!.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa piinisuma bɛ ya saani.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: N wurilim bɛ ya saani, Kirisita Yisa Masiiya saani.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Dinzugu n daa teesi n‑gbaai n sufu ni, ni, n bu ya kyɛ ya saani na n‑laa n‑nya sufusa'aŋŋu yaasa.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Dama man yi ti sa'am ya sufu, ŋɔni m bɛ ni n‑ya kyɛ ka man mun sufu maai? - ka di pa la man sa'am seeba sufu maa, di nyɛ la yarima.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: N daa sɔbi lala maa, ni n ti kyɛ ka man yi kyɛ ya saani na maa, ya ku sa'am n sufu maa; dama yarima n nyɛ ban sum ni ya kyɛ ka n sufu maai. Ay! - n siri daa mari yɛdda, ni, ya zaa nin la'am n‑nya n sufumaasim maa.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Dama sufusa'aŋŋu ni beerim ni nintam zugu n daa kyɛ ka n sɔbi n‑ti ya maa. Ka di pa la ni n kyɛ ka ya sufu ti sa'am, amaa ni ya ti baŋŋi man wuri ya pam seem.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa taasi sufusa'aŋŋu na maa, pa man sufu ka u daa sa'am amaa ya zaa sufu - bee, n tɔ'asiri lala n‑yakkri sɔsuŋŋu?.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Ya daadam bɔbgu ŋɔn pun darigi u tubri seem la - tɔ di sakkiya!.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Dinzugu ya kyɛri u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, ka suŋŋi u ka u ti nya sufumaasim: di yi pa lala, di pa seem sufusa'aŋŋu nin nyaŋŋi u.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Di zugu n kpamsiri ya ni, ni, ya kur kyɛ ka u baŋŋi, ni, ya wuri u ni.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Halli din daa kyɛ ka n daa sɔbi n‑ti ya daa nyɛ la: ni, n ti zamsi ya yɛdda yɛla, m‑baŋŋi ya nin deaai n noori seelikam puuni deede, bee, ya bu ya deaai?.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Ya yi pun kyɛ sɔ taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, man gba kyɛ di daama taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u: man mun daa kyɛ taaliseaa m‑pɛŋŋi u la - taalli yi siri daa bɛ ni - n daa kyɛ m‑pɛŋŋi yarima zugu, Kirisita Masiiya tooni.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Ammi, dinŋɔ wulisi la, ni, ti maali n‑tibgri ti mansi, bee? Ti nyɛ la seeba ban boori ni ba sɔbi gbana n‑kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ti, fa? Bee, ya sɔbri lala gbana n‑ti ti gba? - hoo seeba ŋɔn boori a seem la.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Ka di nin kyɛ ka Sintaani ku nyaŋŋi ti. Dama ti pa la seeba ban zi u teesibeeri yɛla an nyɛ seem la.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Ay! Ti gbana maa n siri nyɛ yarima: ban sɔbi gbanseesi n‑niŋi ti sufu ni n nyɛ ya. Sɔkam nin toaai n‑karim ti lala gbana maa m‑baŋŋi a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Tɔ, man ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa, ni n ti mooli Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu yɛla maa, n daa nya ka ti Dugma pun yoaai soori n‑ti ma.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Di kasigi n‑wulisi, ni, Yisa Kirisita mammaŋŋa n sɔbi lala gbaŋŋu maa, di nyɛ la yarima; ka tirima muna - ba tum ti ni ti mari ka na. Ba bu zaŋŋi inki n‑sɔbi ka, amaa Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana Sii Kasi la ka ba zaŋŋi n‑sɔbra. Halli ba bu boaai n‑sɔbi ka kuglama ni, hoo Musa zaaligu ŋɔn daa sɔbi seem la, amaa ninsaalidima sufu ni.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Amaa n daa na kpalim n‑ka sufudoaa dama n daa bu nya m mabii Taituusi la bɛni. Lala zugu ka n daa kyeoosi ba ka gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Ti yɛtti dinŋɔ, dama ti niŋi yɛdda ka bu birisiri Naawunni tooni, ka di bɛ la Kirisita zugu.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Amaa ti puusiri Naawunni dama u suŋŋni ti sahakam. Ka Kirisita ŋɔn nyaŋŋi zaa ka kyɛnni u gyirima gyingaari ni kyɛnni sahanseaa la, ŋɔn Naawunni n kyɛ ka ti dɔli la Kirisita nyaaŋŋa. Halli u kyɛ ka tirima zugu ka ba baŋŋi u bamsim maa seela zaa, hoo nyunya'asiri ŋɔn kpeeri yiri ya'akam zaa puuni la ka niriba zaa wumna.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Seelli ka ti ni din nin wulisi, ni, tirim mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑tumni tuma maa. Din kyɛ ka ti nin toaai n‑tumni a maa yi la Naawunni saani na.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Ii, ti siri ŋmani la hoo Kirisita turaali n‑tiri Naawunni, ka di nyɛ la ban nyari tuliginsim maa saani, ni ban bɔrigri la gba saani.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni n kyɛ ka ti sa'ai ni ti tum u nangbanyinni zaaligu sɔpaalli tuma wa. Ka ka pa la sɔseaa kan dɔli sɔppu la, amaa Sii maa soori n nyɛ ka. Sɔppu zaaligu soori mari la kum, amaa Sii maa soori tisiri la nyɔvuri.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Ban mun saani ti ŋmani la kum nyupoosiri, banla saani muna ti ŋmani la nyɔvuri nyunya'asiri. Ka ŋɔni n nin nyaŋŋi lala tumni maa?.
