eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Tɔ; amaa zaalikurigu la muna - ba daa boaai ka n‑sɔbi n‑niŋi kuga ni, ka ka daa mari kum na; amaa ka tuma soori daa mari gyirima pam. Halli di daa kyɛ ka Musa nindaa la daa nya'ari nyain, ka Izraɛli dima daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑gɔsira. U nindaa neesim daa baligri ni; amaa di zaa yɔri, gyirima n‑daa siri la.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Tirim bu ŋmanni daa biisi, ti bu zaŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu n‑diri daa n‑koosiri m‑boori ligri di zugu. Ay - ti tɔ'asiri yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni yɛla: ti tɔ'asiri hoo Naawunni ŋɔn tum seeba la, ka di nyɛ la ŋɔn Naawunni ni Kirisita tooni ka ti tɔ'asira.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: Ka di yi bɛ lala, gyirima pam nin ti dɔli Sii maa zaaligu soori tuma, halli nti yakki: bee, m ma'ai ni?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Daŋŋni soori wa n daa kari niriba sariya n‑lusi ba; halli ni di zaa yɔri, ka daa mari gyirima. Ka sɔpaalli la muna - sɔseaa kan deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima - lala soori bu ya mari gyirima pam n‑gaari ka? Ay, ka nin mara!.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Sɔkurigu gyirima bu laa n‑ŋmani la gyirimamaŋni, dama sɔpaalli gyirima gaari ka halli nti yakki.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Daŋŋni soori neesim daa baligri m‑basira, halli ka di daa piili ni gyirima; dizugu sɔseaa kan kpalim m‑bɛ gyirima ni ka bu naari la mari la gyirima n‑gaari ka.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Lala zugu tin mari tama kanŋɔ maa zugu, ti gbaari ti mansi n‑tumna, ka vi ka ni.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Tirima bu ŋmani la hoo Musa, dama ŋɔna n daa zaŋŋi saariga n‑ligi u nindaa, ni Izraɛli niriba di ti nya gyirima neesim la ŋɔn daa baligri n‑naari seem maa.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Amaa di zaa yɔri, Izraɛli dima maa yɛm na kpalim n‑ligi ni; ba yi kur karimni gbankurigu la, saariga maa na kpalim m‑pili la ba yɛm. Ka bu piligi m‑basi, dama Yisa kɔkɔ n nyɛ ka piligra.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Halli ni zuna, ba yi ti karimni Musa zaaligu gbaŋŋu la, saariga maa na kpalim m‑pili la ba yɛm.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Amaa ba nin toaai m‑basi ka, hoo Litaafi la ŋɔn yɛli Musa yɛla seem la, ni, Ŋɔn daa ti ŋmaligi n‑kpa Naawunni saani maa, di saha ka saariga maa daa piligi.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Tɔ: ti Dugma n nyɛ Sii maa, ka ti Dugma Sii yi bɛ seelli zaa, bɛni ka sumaŋŋa nin bɛ ni.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Tirima muna - ti ka saariga kan ligi ti nini: ti kur gɔsiri ti Dugma gyirima maa ka nya'asiri n‑labsiri na. Di yiiri la ti Dugma, ŋɔn nyɛ Sii maa, saani na, ka takkri ti ni ti kyaŋŋi tooni n‑ŋmani hoo ŋɔn mammaŋŋa ŋɔn nyɛ seem; ti siri yiiri la gyirima zii ni, m‑paari gyirima din zoaai n‑gaari lala.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Man Pɔla n‑sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa - man ŋɔn nyɛ Yisa Kirisita Masiiya tuumu, Naawunni yuubu zugu - man ni ti mabii Timɔti: ti sɔbri n‑tiri ya, yan bɛ Kɔrinti tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ti Dugma la, ni Naawunni niriba zaa ban bɛ Akaaya tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, zaŋŋma u piinisuma ni alaafɛ zaa n‑ti ya.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ai Naawunni, ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la, ti Ba Nambɔzoaa Daana, ka ŋɔn Naawunni n su sufumaasim n‑kpamsiri sɔkam.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Ŋɔna n siri kpamsiri ti sufu ti yɛlibeeri zaa puuni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti gba nin toaai n‑kpamsi ban mari yɛlimugsa zaa sufu, hoo tin gba ŋɔn daa deaai sufumaasim Naawunni saani seem la.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Hoo tin kur la'am n‑diri Kirisita wahala wa, lala ka ti nin la'am n‑diri u sufumaasim kpamsigu gba, halli nti yakki.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Ti yi nya toom, di nyɛ la ya sufumaasim ni ya fa-m‑basi zugu; ti mun yi ti nya ti sufu kpamsigu, di bɛ la lala ka di nin kyɛ ka ya gba sufu kpamsi, halli ka ya nin bamsi sugru di zugu n‑zɛ yummu, sahanseaa yan nya namsi hoo ti dina wa la.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Dizugu ti mari tama ya zugu ka ka birisigu, dama ti mi halli, ni, yan la'am n‑diri ti wahala wa seem la, lala ka ya nin la'am n‑nya ti sufumaasim gba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Dama ti bu boori ni ya kpalim n‑zi ti yɛla, ti mabiisi, tin ŋɔn daa di wahala Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu wa ni seem: ti siri daa namsi ni halli nti yakki, ka ti daa kɔŋŋi tama zaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti beebu gba.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Ti daa teesiya, ni, kum kɔkɔ n kpalim ti. Amaa dinŋɔ daa niŋi ti, ni di ti wulisi ti, ni, ti di niŋni ti mammansi yɛdda, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn toaai n‑neaari ban kpi la.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: U daa fa ti m‑basi yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu ni, ka u nin laa n‑fa ti m‑basi yaasa. Halli ti niŋi tama, ni, u nin laa n‑yiisi ti yɛlibeeri ni.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: yan ŋɔn suŋŋni ti ya wunsusigu zugu la. Lala nin kyɛ ka niriba pam ti puusi Naawunni tirima zugu, dama ba nin nya Naawunni ŋɔn wum niriba pam wunsusigu seem la ka suŋŋi ti di zugu.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna tin nyuuri bara yɛttɔ'aseaa zugu la nyɛ la: ni, ti sufu ni bamsim wulisiri ti, ni, ti bɛ la beesisuŋŋu duniya wa ni. Ni, di pa la duniya wa yamgɔliŋŋu soori ka ti dɔla, amaa sɔsuŋŋu ka ti dɔli n‑tiri Naawunni kasikasi. Naawunni ŋɔn daa suŋŋi ti seem la, lala ka ti bɛ ni, halli yarima saani ka ti beesigu siri daa pun bɛ lala.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Ti bu sɔbri bunseelli yan ku toaai n‑karim ka baŋŋi di gbinni. Ka m mari tama, ni, ya nin baŋŋi di zaa halli ni di tɔriga.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: dama ya pun baŋŋi ti yɛliseesi gbinni. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai n‑nyu bara tirima zugu, hoo tin ya n‑nyu bara yarima zugu seem wa, ti Dugma Yisa dabsiri daari maa.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: N daa pun niŋi yɛdda, ni, di bɛ la lala: ka dizugu n daa gbaai gbaari, ni, n ya n‑daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ya saani na pɔi, ka lala ti kyɛ ka ya nya sufumaasim buyi.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: N daa yɛli, ni, n ya n‑dɔli ya saani na n‑gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu ni, ka laa n‑yi bɛni n‑labi ya saani na yaasa, ka ya zaali ma soori ka n kyaŋŋi Gyuudiiya.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Tɔ; man ŋɔn daa yɛtti lala maa, n daa birisiri n‑yɔmsira, bee? Bee, n daa gbaai n yɛla hoo duniya wa nira, n‑yɛli Ii! ka labi n‑yɛli, Ay.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Ay! - Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ yɛdda daana zugu ka ti na zi n‑yɛli ya, Ii! ka labi n‑yɛli, Ayi.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Dama Naawunni Bii Yisa Kirisita yɛla ka ti daa mooli n‑ti ya - man ni Sailivanisi ni Timɔti - ka ŋɔn mun Yisa pa la Ii! ni Ay! daana, amaa ka u seelikam kur sakki, ni, Ii.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Dama nangbambasiseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi la zaa ka ŋɔn Yisa sakki, ni, Ii! n‑zaali a ni. Dizugu ŋɔn Yisa zugu ka ti niŋi Ammi ! la, n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Tɔ: ka Naawunni n zaŋŋi tirima ni yarima gba n‑zaali deede n‑ti Kirisita; ŋɔna n daa diisi ti naam gba.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u daalimni m‑pa ti; ka kyɛ ka u Sii la bɛ ti sufu ni, ni di ti wulisi, ni, di zaaniya ka di siri ya kyɛ na.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Ay! - di daa pa la birisigu zugu ka n daa bu kyɛ ya saani na. M boonni Naawunni ni u ti di sɛhara n‑ti n sufu yɛlimaŋni yɛla: namboo zugu ka n daa bu kyɛ Kɔrinti na, dama n daa bu boori ni ya nya yɛla.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Pa la ti su la ya yɛdda yɛla m‑mugsira, amaa tin ni yarima la'amni n‑suŋŋni la taaba ya sufumaasim zugu; dama yɛdda zugu ka ya zɛ deede maa.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Tɔ: ka ti baŋŋi ti duniya wa ningbina yɛla: ni, a ŋmani la hoo suga, bee, tanti leensi; ni, a yi ti wurim m‑basi, sɔkam mari u dusuŋŋu, Naawunni ŋɔn mɛ seaa n‑ti ti, pa ninsaala; ka lala duu maa zɛ la arizana ni, ka ka nin ti yuui halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Ka di nyɛ la Naawunni namboo zugu ka ti tumni wa, ti ku toaai n‑kyɛ tama.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Tin ŋɔn kpalim m‑bɛ kpa wa, ti na nyuusiri ni, dama ti sufu kur boori la ti paai dupaala wa n‑kpɛ a ni, bee, ti nya suturipaala Naawunni saani n‑yɛ a.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Ay! - di pa lala. Ti za'asi yɛlisɔ'ara seesi m mari vi la, ka bu goori n‑yɔmsira, ti mun bu sa'amni Naawunni yɛligu la. Amaa tin yɛtti yɛlimaŋni maa paalu ni maa zugu kyɛ ka sɔkam baŋŋi ti yɛla suŋŋa u sufu teesa ni, zaŋŋi n‑kpa Naawunni saani.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Ya bu nya? - ti yi nya sutura n‑yɛa, dinŋɔ wulisiri, ni, ti ku laa n‑kyɛnni yɔri.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Halli ni ti lahabasuŋŋu maa ŋɔn ligi la saariga seeba saani maa zaa yɔri, ban baasiri yɔri la saani ka ka ligi.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Tin mun na kpalim n‑gbeeri kpa wa sugri la ni, ti kur nyuusiri ni ka fabinna; di pa la tin boori la ni ti kyɛnni yɔri, amaa tin boori ni ti yɛ suturiveela, bee, n‑kpɛ ti duseesi an zɛ arizana ni la. Di saha ka ti bunseesi an kpiiri la nin ti basi zaa, ka nyɔvuri nin nyaŋŋi a zaa.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Dama Sintaani n kyɛ ka duniya wa dima nya u n‑gyeemni u, ni, ba Naawunni. U siri kyɛ ka ban ka yɛdda la sufu zoomna, ka bu nyari neesimaŋni: lala neesim maa mun yi la lahabasuŋŋu mooligu ni na - lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Kirisita gyirima, ka ŋɔn Kirisita mun ŋmani la Naawunni deede.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Halli lala zugu ka Naawunni daa naam ti m‑maali ti siri; ka u wulisi ti lala, dama u pun daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u Siiya n‑ti ti.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Pa ti mammansi yɛla ka ti moonna, amaa Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yɛla, ni, u nyɛ la ti Dugma: ka laa m‑moonni, ni, tirima n nyɛ ban gyeemni ya zaa, ŋɔn Yisa zugu.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna ti kpamsiri ti sufu, dama ti mi halli, ni, ti yi na kpalim m‑bɛ ningbina wa puuni, di wulisi, ni, ti na ka Naawunni saani.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, Kyɛ ka neesim neaai zibsim ni!: tɔ, ŋɔn Naawunni kyɛ ka u neesim neaai ti sufu ni gba, ni di ti ti ti bamsim zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni gyirima din bɛ Yisa Kirisita nindaa ni la.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ saha ti mari yɛdda n‑kyɛnna: pa ti nini ka ti mari n‑nyari deede.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Naawunni tuntuntaaba n nyɛ ti, ka Naawunni pun zaŋŋi u piini n‑ti ti, halli ka ti bu sumsi lala; tɔ, ka di zugu ti kpamsiri ya, ni, yan di kyɛ ka Naawunni piini wa basi yɔri.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Ti siri mari arizikki anŋɔ zaa, amaa ti saani a bɛ la hoo buna ŋɔn bɛ sinsi puuni seem. Ka lala zugu nin wulisi yaa din zoaai pam la yɛla - ni, pa tirima saani ka di yi na, amaa di yi la Naawunni saani m‑paai ti na.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Ii, ti siri kpamsiri ti sufu: tin ŋɔn mun boori ni ti yi ningbina wa ni, ni ti ti bɛ Naawunni saani.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Dama u yɛliya, ni#:- Sahasuŋŋu maa deede ka n daa wum n‑ti ya, Halli tuliginsim dabsiri daari ka n daa suŋŋi ya. ka mani n yɛtti ya wa, ni, pampaŋŋɔ n nyɛ Naawunni sahasuŋŋu wa, ka tuliginsim daari wa n nyɛ zuna.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Di mugsi ti ya'ari kam, amaa ti bu ŋmirigi. Ti burimna, amaa ka bu basi tama.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Ka dinzugu ti kpaŋŋi ti mansi n‑tumni din kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni nini tigra, halli tin bɛ duniya wa ningbina puuni maa, bee, ti ka a ni.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Ti bu ta'ari sɔ soori, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba ku galim ti tuma soori wa.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Ba niŋni ti putoo, amaa ti bu basi yɔri; ba ŋmɛ ti n‑lusi, amaa ti bu sa'am zaa.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Dama Kirisita nin ti zi u sariya karibu bimbinni zugu, ni u ti boosi ti zaa boosa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti ningbina wa ni tuma, a yi ti nyɛ bunsuma, bee, bumpoosa. Soori ka ni ni sɔ za'asi: ti sɔkam zaa nin zɛ u tooni n‑deaai ti laara sanyoaa hoo ti tuma wa ŋɔn sumsi seem.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Amaa bunseelikam zaa puuni ti ya n‑wulisiri, ni, Naawunni tuntumniba n nyɛ ti. Halli di yi nyɛ wahala diibu puuni gba, ti bɛ la lala n‑diri sugru, ni toom ni yɛlimugsa puuni gba.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Tin kyɛnni n‑goori seem wa, sahakam ka Yisa kum yɛla kpalim m‑bɛ ti ningbina ni, ni lala ti kyɛ ka Yisa nyɔvuri beesigu gba bɛ ti ningbina ni, ka niriba ti nya.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Ka dinŋɔna ti mi Naawunni yɛla ŋɔn mari dabeem seem: lala zugu ka ti kpaŋŋni ti maŋŋa ni ti moonni Naawunni lahabaari n‑kyɛ ka niriba ti sakki. Ti beebu bu sɔ'ai Naawunni saani, ka ti laa m‑mari tama, ni, di bu sɔ'ai ya gba sufu teesa saani.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Ba bu ti, ka kpari ti sarika duri ni; niriba mun daa zabri ti yɛla zugu; ti daa tumni tunkpeema, ka ganni gɔgala, ka kpalim m‑bɛ kɔm ni. A zaa puuni ka ti tumni la Naawunni tuma.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Ii - tirima ban nɛ wa, ba kur zaŋŋni ti n‑niŋni kum ziiya, Yisa yɛla zugu; dama lala nin kyɛ ka ŋɔn Yisa nyɔvuri beesigu ti yi paalu, halli ti ningbina an kpiiri la puuni gba.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Di pa la ti maali m‑boori ti tibgi ti maŋŋa ya saani yaasa; ay! - ti ya n‑ti ya la soori ni ya ti nyuuri bara ti yɛla zugu. Ka dinŋɔna, ninvugseeba yi ya nyu bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi an nyari wa yɛla, ka pa sufu ni yɛlimaŋni yɛla, ni ba ti zeem ya - tɔ! yan mun nin nyaŋŋi n‑labsi ba labsisuŋŋu.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu kum tumni ti puuni, amaa yarima muna - nyɔvuri tumni ya puuni.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Ti kur zɛ yummu Naawunni saani, m‑mari yɛm, ni sugru, ni nirilim; ti kpalim m‑bɛ Naawunni Vuusim Kasi saani, ka wuri niriba ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Ni, yinyaari mari ti - tɔ, di yi bɛ la lala, di nyɛ la Naawunni zugu ka ti kɔŋŋi yɛm. Tin mun yi nyɛ ninvugmaŋa, di nyɛ la yarima zugu ka ti mari ninneesim.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, N daa niŋi yɛdda; dizugu ka n daa tɔ'asi. Tɔ, ka tirima gba niŋi yɛdda, ka lala zugu ka ti tɔ'asira.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Ti mari la yɛlimaŋni yɛligu n‑tumna, ni Naawunni yiiko; ti mari ti tɔppu neema an nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni dina, ka a bɛ la ti nudirigu ni ti nuzaa ni zaa.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya wurilim bɛ ni m‑mugsiri ti: ti ku toaai n‑yɛli seela, na'ala, ninvugyinni ŋɔn daa kpi n‑ti sɔkam la zugu, lala wulisi, ni, sɔkam mun kpiya.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Dama ti mi halli, ni, Naawunni daa neaai ti Dugma Yisa kum ni. Ka laa m‑mi, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin kyɛ ka ti gba neaai, tin ni Yisa ŋɔn la'am m‑bɛ ni maa, ka nin taasi tin ni yarima zaa m‑paai u saani na.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Anŋɔ zaa puuni ka ba pɛ'ari ti, ka laa n‑fiilim ti; ba gyeemni ti ka laa n‑zeemni ti; ba yɛtti, ni, ti yɔmsira, ka laa n‑yɛtti, ni, ti tɔ'asiri yɛlimaŋni.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa kpi maa gbinni nyɛ la: ni, ban kpalim m‑bɛ ni la, pa ba mammaŋŋa zugu ka ba bɛ ni, amaa ŋɔn Kirisita zugu ka ba bɛ ni, dama u daa kpi n‑ti ba ka laa n‑yisigi.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la yarima anfaani zugu; di gbinni nyɛ la: ni, niriba pam ŋɔn deaari Naawunni piinisuma an zoaai nti yakki la, ba nin puusi Naawunni pampam di zugu, ka Naawunni gyirima ti paasira.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Sɔsɔ bu mi ti yɛla, amaa ti yuuri duya; ti nyɛ la hoo ban kpiira, ka kpalim n‑nɛa; ba namsiri ti, amaa ti bu kpi zaa.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Ka di zugu pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ti ku laa n‑gɔsiri niriba hoo duniya wa ninsaadima maa ŋɔn gɔsiri ba seem m‑bugsiri ba yɛla: halli ti yi daa gɔsiri Kirisita mammaŋŋa gɔsibu wa, ti ku laa n‑niŋi lala.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Ka dinŋɔna ti ku basi tama. Halli ni ti ningbina dɔŋŋni maa zaa yɔri, ti mun sufu ni ti maanni n‑leebri bumpaala beoo kam.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Halli ti bɛ la sufusa'aŋŋu ni, amaa ka mari sufupeelli sahakam; ti bɛ la fara ni, amaa ka kyɛ ka daadam pam leebi la bundaandima; ti ka seela, amaa ka mari bunseelikam.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Dinŋɔna ninvugsɔ yi bɛ Kirisita saani, ba naam u la bumpaalli: u beesikurigu la gaariya, ka bunseelikam leebi la beesipaalli.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Yɛlimugsa mari ti, amaa a bu tibsa ka a mun ku yuui. Ka anŋɔ zaa nin kyɛ ka ti nya tibsim ni gyirima an gaari zaa ka laa n‑ka naari.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Ya bu nya, ti zɔdima? - ti tɔ'asi n‑ti ya Kɔrinti dima wa ka bu mɔŋŋi ya, halli ti yoaai la ti sufu ni teesa n‑ti ya.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Ka anŋɔ zaa yi la Naawunni saani. U daa kyɛ ka Kirisita zɔ ti sindaani u saani m‑maali yɛttɔ'a zaa, ka daa laa n‑zaŋŋi sindaani zoobu tuma wa n‑ti ti ni ti ti tumna.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Ti siri bu zaŋŋi ti nini n‑kpa la bunseesi an nyari la, amaa an bu nyari maa ka ti gɔsira. Dama bunseesi an nyari ni nini bu ya n‑yuui, amaa an bu nyari muna - anŋɔ nin ti kpalim m‑bɛ ni sahakam.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Di pa la ni tirima n mɔŋŋni ya ti wurilim; ay! - amaa yarima n mɔŋŋni ti ya wurilim.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Ni, ŋɔn Naawunni daa bɛ la Kirisita saani n‑zɔri sindaani, ni u ti maali u yɛttɔ'a zaa n‑ti duniya wa dima, ni u ku laa n‑teesi ba tumbeeri yɛla halli beela. Ka u zaŋŋi sindaani zoobu wa yɛla n‑ti ti ni ti ti moonna.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Dinŋɔna see ka ya dɔli soori n‑labsi n‑ti ma hoo man pun tisi seem - n tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya hoo m biisi la - ka ya gba yoaai ya sufu ni teesa n‑ti ma.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Dama Naawunni tum ma, hoo ŋɔn tum bisɔ ni u ti mari ŋɔn Naawunni kukɔri n‑kyɛ na n‑susira. Lala ka u tum ti zaa gba. Ti susiri niriba nimmoosi Kirisita zugu, ni, ba labi Naawunni saani na n‑niŋi nangbanyinni.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka yan ni ban ka yɛdda la ti la'amni n‑tumni hoo na'akoora ŋɔn tumni seem. Ammi, yɛlimaŋni ni tuppuwumni la'amni n‑suŋŋni taaba? - Ay! Bee, neesim ni zibsim ya niŋi la wula n‑la'amna? - a ku toaai, halli beela!.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Ŋɔn Kirisita mun daa ka bumbɛseelli u ni, ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u ti leebi bumbeoo, ka di daa nyɛ la tirima zugu. Lala ka u daa kyɛ ka tirim tumbeeri dima wa ti leebi yɛlimaŋni dima n‑ti ŋɔn Naawunni.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Kirisita siri ku toaai n‑lɔ nangbanyinni ni Sintaani; bee, yɛdda daana ku toaai n‑nyɛ ŋɔn za'asi yɛdda niŋbu la taraana.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri siri bu ŋmanni ba'ari bee bugri ŋɔn bɛ seela. Amaa tirima n nyɛ gyeeŋŋu yiri n‑ti Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana maa. Hoo ŋɔn Naawunni daa yɛliya#:- N nin kpɛ ba saani n‑gbɛa; Man ni ban nin kyɛnni n‑gyinna; N nin nyɛ Naawunni n‑ti ba Ka ba mun nin nyɛ n niriba.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Dinzugu, ni#:- Ya yim na ka kyɛ ba, n‑wɔligi ya mansi m‑bɛ ya kɔkɔa. - Lala ka ti Dugma yɛtta. Ni#:- Ya di siisi bunda'ariga kan kyisira, Ka n nin deaai ya.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Ka n nin leebi ya Ba, Ka yan mun nin leebi m biisi. - Lala ka ti Dugma Yiiko Zaa Daana yɛtta.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Man, Pɔla, n‑sɔbri wa: ka n susiri ya, Kirisita sugru zugu, ni u nirilim zugu. Halli ban yɛtti n yɛla seem maa yɔri ka n susiri ya - dama ba yɛtti, ni, n yi bɛ ya saani n nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn zɔri vi ni dabeem, amaa n yi ka ya saani muna m mari la sufukpeemni ni dablim.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: N susigu wa gbinni nyɛ la: ni, n di kyɛ ya saani na nti niŋi sufukpeemni, dama n teesiri, ni, di nin kpa la talasi ni n zaŋŋi n sufukpeemni m‑boosi ninvugseeba boosa; dama ba yɛtti, ni, man nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn tuusi la tɔppu hoo duniya wa dima ŋɔn tuusiri seem maa.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Ni ti bɛ la duniya wa ni maa zaa yɔri, ti bu zabri hoo duniya wa tɔppu ŋɔn tuusiri seem.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Neenseesi tin mari n‑zabra maa pa la duniya wa tɔppu neema: ay! - a mari la Naawunni yiiko, n‑nyaŋŋi n‑sa'amni kpeeŋŋu ziziisi. Ti sa'amni la nangbankpeemni yɛla gba.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: m‑paasi bunseelikam zaa kan duusiri kamaŋŋa ka za'asiri Naawunni yɛla bamsim. Ti mun zabri ni ti ti gbaasi sufu ni teesa zaa, n‑kyɛ ka a zaa ti wum n‑ti Kirisita.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Ka ya yi ti sakki wumpu n‑naai, ti nin maali siri ni ti darigi ban ku wum la zaa tuba.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Ya nini n gɔsiri yɛla ka ya teesiri, ni, a gɔsibu siri wulisiri la a gbinni zaa ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ay! - ŋɔnkam niŋi yɛdda, ni, u nyɛ la Kirisita nira, see ka u maali n‑teesi, ni, tirim gba nyɛ la Kirisita niriba lala.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Di yi pa seem n nin nyu bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti Dugma ŋɔn zaŋŋi yiikoseaa n‑ti ti la, halli nti yakki sɔsuŋŋu gba; ŋɔn Naawunni siri daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ti, ni ti ti duusi ya beesigu, pa ti sigsi ka. Amaa vi ku mari ma bara nyuubu wa zugu.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: M bu boori di ŋmanni hoo man sɔbri n gbana wa ni n ti vɔlisiri ya.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Dama ninvugseeba ban yɛtti n yɛla, ni, n gbana n nyɛ buntibsa ka mugsira, amaa m mammaŋŋa yi ti bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la n nyɛ la ninvugyɔri, ningbimbaaliga daana; ni, Ka u yɛttɔ'a wa muna - a ka yaa halli beela.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: See ka lala niriba baŋŋma, ni, tin nyɛ seem ti gbana sɔppu maa ni, sahanseaa tin ka ya saani la, lala ka ti nin nyɛ ti tuma maa ni, sahanseaa tin bɛ ni maa.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Niriba wa n nyari ba mansi kɔkɔa, amaa tirima ku toaai n‑zaŋŋi ba yɛla m‑ma'asi ti mansi, bee n‑la'am m‑bɛ ba saani. Ba zaŋŋi ba mansi yɛla n‑zamsiri taaba; ba zaŋŋi ba mansi m‑ma'asiri la taaba. Ba siri ka yɛm halli fiiŋ gba!.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Tirim mun ku ma'asi ti mansi n‑nyu bara n‑yakki din mari soori. Ti nin sɛ'ali ti ma'asigu ni zamsigu yɛla ni ya'aseaa Naawunni ŋɔn zaali ti - halli nti paai ya saani na gba.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Ti yi daa bu paai ya na, ti barinyuubu naa n‑ku dɔli soori; amaa di pa lala, dama ti siri daa pun daŋŋi ya saani na n‑taasi Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ya.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Ti mun bu yakkri n‑gatti ti tɔriga n‑nyuuri bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugseeba tuma m‑paasi ti maŋtoori dina zugu. Amaa ti mari tama, ni, ya yɛdda nin zoaara, ka lala ka ti tuma zii gba nin yɛligi ya saani.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Halli ni ti nin toaai m‑mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti ya tooni sa tingbanseesi niriba. Ti bu boori ti kpɛ sɔ tuma ya'ari ni n‑nyuuri bara tunseesi ŋɔn pun tum n‑naai la zugu.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Ni, Kyɛ ka ŋɔn ya nyu bara zaa nyuuri a ti Dugma yɛla zugu.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Di siri pa la ŋɔn pɛ'ari u maŋŋa la yuuri ka ba duusira, amaa ti Dugma ŋɔn pɛ'ai sɔ la.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Tɔ, m mabiwutta, ba pun basi nangbambasiri wa n‑ti ti na, ka di zugu ya kyɛ ka ti leebi Naawunni niriba kasikasi. Ka kyɛ ka da'ari ka ti ni, ti ningbina ni ti siisi zaa; ka laa n‑zɔri Naawunni, ni u ti su ti beesigu yɛla zaa.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Ya ku deaai ti suŋŋa? Ti bu tum sɔ taalli, ti bu sa'am sɔ soori, ti mun bu yɔmsi sɔ n‑fa u.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Man ŋɔn yɛtti lala, di pa la ni n ya n‑wulisi, ni, ya pa la niriba: ya siri bɛ la ti sufu ni, halli ni tin ni yan yi la'am n‑kpi, bee, ti la'am n‑nɛ gba - ti pun yɛli ya lala - bee, ti daa bu yɛli?.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: N siri niŋi ya yɛdda ka tɔ'asiri pam n‑nyuuri bara ya zugu. N nya n sufu maasim zaa di zugu; ni ti wahala diibu zaa yɔri, n sufu pɛligiya pam.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Dama tin daa paai Masidoonya sahanseaa maa, ti daa bu nya vuusim, ka daa nyari yɛla pam ya'ari kam zaa. Daadam saani ka ti daa mari zaba, ka ti sufu puuni ka dabeem daa kur bɛ ni.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Amaa ni di zaa yɔri, Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn gɔsiri ban ka yaa yɛla n‑tiri ba maasim, ka u daa maai ti sufu, ka kyɛ ka Taituusi kyɛ ti saani na.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Ka di pa la u kyɛnni na maa kɔkɔa, amaa yan ni ŋɔna ŋɔn daa mari sufumaasim ni taaba seem la zugu. U daa yɛtti yan boori ti yɛla seem la, ni yan nyari sufusa'aŋŋu seem, taalli maa zugu; m‑paasi yan zaŋŋi ya sufu zaa n‑niŋni na: ka lala daa kyɛ ka n sufu maai halli nti yakki.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Ni man daa sɔbi gbaŋŋu ka ya sufu sa'am maa zaa, m bu ya takki n‑labi nyaaŋŋa. Halli n siri daa boori ni n takki, dama n daa nya ka gbaŋŋu kana daa niŋi ya sufusa'aŋŋu, halli ni di daa bu yuui pam gba.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ muna n yi mari la sufupeelli, pa la ya sufusa'aŋŋu la zugu, amaa, ni, ya sufusa'aŋŋu daa kyɛ ka ya takki ya sɔbeeri, ka taasi ya tooni. Naawunni zugu sufusa'aŋŋu n‑daa la, ka dizugu ya daa bu labi nyaaŋŋa ti tuma zugu.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Naawunni zugu sufusa'aŋŋu wa zugu siri kyɛ ka ti takkri ti sɔbeeri nti nya tuliginsim, ni ti sufu ku laa m‑miligi; duniya wa sufusa'aŋŋu mun taasiri ti kum ni.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Ya bu nya Naawunni zugu sufusa'aŋŋu wa ŋɔn suŋŋi ya seem? - ya boori yooma m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa, ka bu boori galiŋŋu di zugu. Ya gyɛ la suuri n‑niŋi tumbɛtumna la, ka zɔri tumbeoo la yɛla dabeem; ya boori nimmoosi ni ya maai yɛlli maa, ni ya kari yɛttɔ'a maa sariya ni yɛlimaŋni. Bunseelli kam puuni ya daa wulisi, ni, ya yi yɛla wa ni, ka ka taalli di zugu.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Man daa sɔbi n‑ti ya na maa gbinni muna, di pa la ŋɔn daa niŋi taalli la zugu, bee, ban daa tum sɔ taalli la zugu gba. Ay! - n daa sɔbi ni, ni n ti kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi yan boori ti yɛla nimmoosi maa seem, ka di yi paalu ni, Naawunni tooni.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa zugu ti sufu kpamsiya. Ka n‑zaŋŋi m‑paasi ti mammaŋŋa kpamsigu zugu, ti mari sufupeelli Taituusi yɛla zugu, dama u sufu daa maaya, yan daa ti u sufudoaa la zugu.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: N daa pun tɔ'asi n‑ti u n‑nyuuri bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya yɛla, ka ya mun daa bu diisi ma vi. Tin daa yɛli seelli zaa n‑ti ya la daa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ka lala ka tin daa nyu bara seesi n‑ti Taituusi ya zugu la gba siri daa niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Din paasiri u wurilim n‑ti ya nyɛ la: ni, u teaari la ya zaa ŋɔn daa wum n‑ti u seem la, ni, ya daa deaai u ka zɔri dabeem pam tumbeeri la zugu.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: N nin toaai n‑niŋi ya yɛdda zaa seelikam puuni, ka di niŋi ma nya'asim pam.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Tɔ, m mabiisi, m boori la ya baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi piiniseaa n‑suŋŋi Masidoonya dima maa.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Ni, u daa kyɛ ka ba ti zoaai zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba tisibu ziiya halli nti yakki: ni ba yɛlimugsa zamsigu maa zaa, ni ban bɛ fara puuni zaa yɔri, ba pun wulisi ba sufumaasim ni ba nirilim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Dama mani nŋɔ n‑diri sɛhara, ni, ba daa tisi ban daa nyaŋŋi seem - halli n‑yakki ba kpeeŋŋu tɔriga. Ba mammaŋŋa yuubu ni.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: ka ba daa bɛlim ti pam, ni, ti kyɛ ka ba la'am n‑tumni tunsuŋŋu wa.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Ba mun daa bu niŋi tin daa teesi seem la, amaa ba daa daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑ti la Naawunni, ka naayi ti ti gba, Naawunni yuubu zugu.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Tɔ: Taituusi daa pun daŋŋi m‑piili lala tuma maa, ka dizugu ti daa susi u, ni, u tumma tunveelli wa n‑naai yarima gba saani.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Ka dinŋɔna yarima ŋɔn kur niŋni seelikam suŋŋa la zugu - di yi nyɛ yɛdda ni, bee yɛttɔ'a ni, bee yɛm, bee nimmoosi tumpu ni, bee wurilim ni, yan wuri ti suŋŋasuŋŋa seem la - kur kpaŋŋma ya mansi m‑moori n‑niŋi suŋŋa n‑gba tisiri lala piini maa.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Di pa la n tiri ya la wadda, amaa m boori la n zamsi ya wurilim ŋɔn paai seem n‑nya, din nin kyɛ ka n zaŋŋi seeba ban wuri nimmoosi maa wurilim m‑ma'asiri ya dinni.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Dama ya mi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita tunsuŋŋu veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa: ni, u daa su arizikki zaa, amaa yarim zugu ka u daa leebi fara daana, ni lala ti kyɛ ka ya mun leebi bundaandima.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Ka dinŋɔna n saawara maa nŋɔa: ni, yarima n nyɛ seeba ban daa piili lala yɛttɔ'a maa yuumni la sa; ka di daa pa la yan daa daŋŋi n‑tum la kɔkɔa, amaa ya daa daŋŋi m‑piili n‑teesi lala tuma maa yɛla.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ see ka ya tum tuma maa halli nti naai; ka lala nin kyɛ ka ya sufu ni teesa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tuma maa, ni ya tuma maa gba, nin dɔli la taaba halli nti naai; ka ya nin tum ya marigu tɔriga.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Ya bu nya? - ninvugsɔ yi ti boori ni u tisi, piini maa deaari dɔli la ŋɔn mari seem, pa ŋɔn ka seelli la.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Di pa la ni ti kyɛ ka seeba vuusiri ka yarim mun nya mugsigu; ay! amaa ni di ti sa'asi n‑ti sɔkam.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ya mara, ka seeba mun ka; ka ya nin suŋŋi ba ban pɔsigi seela maa. Ban mun yi ti mara, ka ya ka, ba gba nin suŋŋi ya: lala ka ya nin sa'asi taaba.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- Ban daa va pam la dina daa bu zoaai n‑yakki, ka ban mun daa va beela maa dina n daa bu pɔsigi ba.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: M puusiri Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka Taituusi gba nini moaai zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya yɛla zugu.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Dama ŋɔn Taituusi daa wum ti susigu maa ni sufumaasim pam, ka laa n‑gbaai u kyɛnni ya saani na la ni u toori zigizigi boorimma nimmoosi.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: M‑paasi u zugu, ti ya kyɛ ka ti mabisɔ gba ti kyɛ ya saani na. Ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn tumni lahabasuŋŋu maa tuma, ka Naawunni niriba ban la'amni lugri kam zaa ni kur pɛ'ari u di zugu.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: M‑paasi lala, ŋɔna ka ba daa yiisi ni u ti dɔli ti n‑suŋŋi ti, tin taasi piini maa sahanseaa la; piini mun tisibu wa wulisiri ti Dugma gyirima paalu ni, ka laa n‑wulisi tin boori ti suŋŋi niriba nimbirimoaa seem la.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Dama ti bu boori ni sɔ galim ti zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ligri pam wa yɛla.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Dizugu ti kpaŋŋi la ti mansi n‑niŋni din tu, pa la Naawunni tooni kɔkɔa, amaa saalidima tooni gba.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Ti laa n‑ya tum la ti mabisɔ m‑paasi ba zugu; ti daa pun nya ka doo wa tumni nimmoosi sahakam, ka pampaŋŋɔ u niŋi ya yɛdda pam, ka dizugu u ya tum n‑gaari lala.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Taituusi muna - u nyɛ la n tuntuntaraana ya saani. Ka ti mabiisi wa mun zɛ la ba taaba zaaziiya, ba taaba mun n nyɛ baseeba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni Naawunni tinsi zaa puuni; ka ban laa n‑nyɛ seeba ban kyɛ ka Naawunni nya pɛ'abu.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Dinzugu ya kyɛ ka niriba wa ti baŋŋi ya wurilim ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka laa m‑baŋŋi din kyɛ ka ti nyuuri bara ya zugu, ka Naawunni niriba zaa ti nya.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Ay! - Man ŋɔn tɔ'asiri seem wa nyɛ la gyɛrilim, amaa n susi ya, ni, ya di sugru n‑sakki n‑wum. Halli ya pun sakki lala gba.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: N gɔsiri ya yɛla suŋŋasuŋŋa, ka a dɔli la Naawunni soori. Di ŋmani la hoo m bii n nyɛ ya, pɔ'asariga, ka m pun basi noori, ni, ya nin kuli sira yinni - di nyɛ la Kirisita. Lala zugu ka m boori la ni ba taasi ya u yiri hoo pɔ'asarisuŋŋu ŋɔn ka galinsi.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Amaa dabeem mari ma, dama n teaari Hawa yɛla, ni, waafu daa zaŋŋi u putɔyam n‑yɔmsi u n‑nyaŋŋi u; man mun n zɔri, ni, di yi pa seem ya nin burigi n‑yi yɛlimaŋni sɔseaa yan nin mari n‑dɔli Kirisita wa ni.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Dama sɔ yi kyɛ na m‑moonni Yisa sɔ yɛla, ka di pa la tirim Yisa wa, ya nin sakki n‑deaai lala; bee, yan deaai la sisɔ ŋɔn pa yan pun deaai Sisɔ wa, bee, lahabasuŋŋu kan pa yan deaai seaa wa, anŋɔ zaa ka ya nin sakki n‑deaai lala ni nimbirimoaa.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Ba yɛnni, ni, tuumudima tibsim daandima n nyɛ ba, amaa m bu teesi man yɛla, ni, m pɔsigi ka bu paai ban sa'ai seem maa na.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Halli ni n yɛttɔ'a pa la yɛttɔ'azulima maa zaa, man lee m‑mari la bamsim. Ti daa pun wulisi anŋɔ zaa kasi n‑ti ya, ka di dɔli la sɔya zaa.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Ya bu nya? - n daa sigsi m maŋŋa ni n ti duusi ya, dama n daa mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ya la yɔri, samni ka ni: tɔ, taalli ka n daa tum maa?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: N daa fa la Naawunni niriba ban bɛ tinseesi la ni, dama n daa deaai la suŋŋsim ba saani ni n ti suŋŋi ya.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Ka man ŋɔn daa bɛ ya saani nti pɔsigi seelli maa, n daa bu zaŋŋi n yɛla m‑mugsi ya sɔa; dama ti mabiseeba ban daa yi Masidoonya na la n daa tiri ma man daa pɔsigi seelli zaa. N daa bu sakki n‑kyɛ ka n yɛla mugsi ya sɔa, ka n ya kpalim m‑bɛ lala.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Kirisita yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn saligi m‑bɛ n saani wa ŋɔna, ya Akaaya tingbaŋŋu ni sɔ ku toaai n‑ta'ai ma bara wa nyuubu ni.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Ka bɔzugu? - ni, man bu wuri ya zugu, bee? Ay! - Naawunni m mi, ni, n wuri ya!.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Ka dinŋɔna n ya kpalim n‑tumni la man tumni seem wa. Dama ninvugseeba m bɛ ni ban boori ba nya soori n‑zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑wulisi, ni, tin ni ban sa'ai taaba: tɔ, n tuma gbinni nyɛ la: ni, n ti nyaŋŋi n‑ta'ai ba, ba barinyuubu ziiya.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Lala niriba nyɛ la ziri tuumudima. Ba tuma zii la muna, yɛlimaŋni ka di ni. Ba gyinni m‑ma'ari ziri, ni, ba nyɛ la Kirisita tuumudima.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Ka lala ka alahazibu, dama Sintaani mammaŋŋa gba ma'ari ziri, ni, u nyɛ la malaaka neesim daana.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Dizugu di ka alahazibu, ni, u nyaandɔliba gba yi ma'ari ziri, ni, ba dɔli la yɛlimaŋni nyaaŋŋa. Amaa ba naari nin nyɛ din dɔli la ba tuma maa.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: N laa n‑yɛtti ya, ni, yan di kyɛ ka ya sɔ nya ma la gyɛrigu. Di yi pa lala, see ka ya di sugru n‑deaai ma hoo gyɛrigu, ka dinŋɔna n nin nyu bara beela.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Nyamaŋŋa bara wa ziiya, m bu tɔ'asiri an dɔli la soori ti Dugma saani; n tɔ'asiri gyɛrilim yɛttɔ'a.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Ni, niriba pam n nyuuri bara ba duniya wa yɛla zugu: tɔ, lala ka man gba ya n‑nyu bara.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Ya bu nya? - ya diri sugru n‑tiri ban kɔŋŋni yɛm, yan mari yɛm pam maa zugu.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti zaŋŋi ya hoo u daaba n nyɛ ya, tɔ - ya nin di sugru n‑sakki lala: sɔ yi diri ya gba, ya nin sakki, bee, sɔ yi fa ya, bee, sɔ yi ti zaali u maŋŋa ya tooni, bee, sɔ yi lɔbi ya tampɛkka - anŋɔ zaa puuni ya nin di sugru n‑sakki lala.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Man muna, n daa ka yaa ni n ti tum anŋɔ zaa n‑niŋi ya - di yi pa seem n yaa pɔsigri wa nin diisi ma vi. Amaa nira ŋɔn nyaŋŋi n‑nyu bara seelikam zaa zugu la muna - tɔ, man gba nin toaai n‑nyu di bara: ya nyama, n siri tɔ'asiri la gyɛrilim; bee m ma'ai ni?.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Ni, ba nyɛ la Hibruu niriba? - tɔ, man gba. Ni, ba nyɛ la Izraɛli dima? - man gba n nyɛa. Ni, ba nyɛ la Ibrahim ya'ansi? Lala ka man gba n nyɛa.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Ni, ba tumni la Kirisita tuma? - ay! mani n gaari lala. Dama man daa tum n‑gaari ba, n‑kpɛ sarika n‑gaari; ba daa bu ma, halli ka n daa ti ya kpi, ka di daa maali m‑maali n‑niŋi ma n‑gaari ba zaa. Tɔ, pa sɔ ŋɔn zugu n labgi yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa? - Ay, gyɛrilim n siri nyɛ di!.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Napɔmbunu ka Yɛhudiyadima maa daa feebi ma kpaasa pisinaasi yinni ka ni.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Napɔmbuta muna ka ba daa bu ma daari, ka zaŋŋi kuga n‑lɔbi ma yimmu: napɔmbuta ka n daa bɛ nyarintitaari ni ka ka sa'am n‑sim teeku koom ni, halli n daa kpalim m‑bɛ teekuzuliŋŋu ni, n‑yuui yuŋŋu n‑neaai beoo nti paai zaanoori yaasa ka n naayi nya fa-m‑basi.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: M bɛ la kyɛnni zugu sahakam. Bunseelikam zaa ziiya ka yɛttɔ'abeeri yɛla daa ya mugsi ma - n daa lɔŋŋni mɔ'a ka di daa bɛ la lala; ni fafaariba nuusi ni ka n daa nya lala, ni n toori butiri niriba nuusi ni, ni tinzundima gba nuusi ni; halli n daa bɛ la timpuuni, bee, mooni, bee, teeku zugu gba ka di daa mugsi ma; ninvugseeba daa ma'ai ziri, ni, ba n nyɛ ti Kirisita nyaandɔlitaaba, ka daa vɔlisi ma lala gba.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: N tumni tunkara n‑namsira, halli sahaseaa m bu gbisira; kɔm m mari ma, ni kɔnyuuri, ni nangbantoo; waari gba mari ma, ka n kyɛnni n‑gyinni yɔri.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Kan paasi anŋɔ zaasa, din mugsiri ma pam dabsa zaa la nyɛ la: ni, n teesiri Naawunni niriba zaa yɛla yɛttɔ'a maa.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Sɔ nin toaai m‑bugsi ka man mun bu la'am m‑bugsi? - ay, sɔ ka ni! Bunseelli mun nin toaai n‑lusi Naawunni nira n‑kpeesi u tumbeeri ni ka m bu gyɛ suuri di zugu? - ay! di ku toaai n‑niŋi!.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna n yi ya nyu bara, see ka n nyu a man ŋɔn bugsi seem la zugu.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Naawunni m mi, ni, m bu ma'ari la ziri, ka u nyɛ la ti Dugma Yisa Ba la, ka su pɛ'abu sahakam zaa.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: M‑paasi n namsigu la zaa zugu, man ŋɔn daa bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa la ka Gɔmna sɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka NaaArɛtasi daa zaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa fukumsi n‑ti u; ka lala Gɔmna maa daa kyɛ ka ba guri tiŋŋa maa sɔya, ni ba gbaai ma.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Amaa ba daa zaŋŋi ma n‑kpeesi kpangyɔkku ni, n‑lɔ ka la miisi, n‑dɔlisi takoro n‑sigsi ma hoo guuga, tiŋŋa maa goomni nyaaŋŋa. Lala ka ba daa fa ma m‑basi Gɔmna wa nuu ni, ka u daa bu nya ma, bee, n‑gbaai ma.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: See ka n kpalim n‑nyuuri n bara wa. Ni anfaani seaa ka di ni maa zaa yɔri, n nin tugi m‑paai zii nyaabu yɛla, ni ti Dugma ŋɔn kasigi bunseesi n‑ti ma la.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: Kirisita ninvugsɔ m bɛ ni ka m mi u yɛla: di daama ka ba daa taasi n‑duusi nti paai arizana zii buta daana ziiya. Di yuuma pii ni anaasi an gaari n‑daa la; tɔ - di daa nyɛ la u maŋtoori ningbiŋŋu n daa kyaŋŋi, bee, u daa yi u ningbiŋŋu m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi, n ku toaai m‑baŋŋi, kulla Naawunni m mi.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Ka n laa m‑mi di daama yɛla - di daa nyɛ la u maŋtoori ningbiŋŋu n‑daa la, bee, u daa yi u ningbiŋŋu m‑basi maa, n ku toaai m‑baŋŋi, kulla Naawunni m mi -.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: ni, ba daa gbaai u nti du n‑kpɛ Naawunni zisuŋŋu, nti wum yɛliseesi an ku toaai n‑yɛli; soori ka ni ni ninsaala ti tɔ'asi a taabu, halli beela.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Tɔ: lala doo taabu yɛla zugu ka n nin nyu bara; n ku nyu bara m mammaŋŋa yɛla zugu, ka di yi pa la n nyu a man ŋɔn bugsa seem la zugu.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Halli n yi ya nyu bara, n ku tumni gyɛrilim di zugu, dama n ya n‑tɔ'asiri la yɛlimaŋni. Amaa n nin kyɛ ni, ni sɔ di teesi n yɛla pam, n‑gaari ba n nin toaai m‑baŋŋi a seem, ban gɔsiri n tuma ka wumni n yɛligu la sahanseaa la.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Zii ni kasigri yɛla wa daa nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ma; dinzugu Naawunni bu boori ni karimbaani mari ma a zugu. Ka di zugu n daa nya Sintaani tuumu tuma n ningbina wa ni nti mugsi ma hoo goaa ŋɔn yi bɛ ni n‑kusiri ma.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Napɔmbuta ka n daa susi ti Dugma, ni, u nin kyɛ ka ka yi ka kyɛ ma.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Amaa u daa yɛli ma, ni, M piini la bɛ ni, halli nyin bu sumni di la, ka di nin sa'asi n‑ti i. Dama n yiiko zoaari m‑paari ka tɔriga n‑tiri bugsim daandima maa. Dinzugu n nin maali n‑nyu bara man bugsa seem la zugu. Lala nin kyɛ ka Kirisita yiiko nin kpalim m‑bɛ n saani sahakam.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu n nin nya sufudoaa man yi bugsa sahanseaa la, ni ban tuuri ma sahanseaa, ni wahala diibu saha gba; ka laa n‑nya sufudoaa namsa ni yɛlimugsa ni, di yi nyɛ Kirisita zugu. Man bukki ni maa, dina ka m mari la yaa.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: Tɔ: n leebi la gyɛrigu ka tɔ'asi gyɛrilim n‑naai. Yarim mun kyɛ ka n niŋi lala: yan daa yi zɛ n nyaaŋŋa di naa n‑sɔa. Ban boonni seeba, ni, tumudima ban gaari zaa la muna - halli ni man pa seelli maa zaa, m bu pɔsigi ba ni.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Bunseesi an wulisiri tuumumaŋni yɛla la ka n daa tumni ya saani, ana ni sugru diibu zaa: an mari alahazibu la, ni makkri yɛla, ni yiiko tuma nla.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Bumbɔ saani ka ya daa fiilim m‑bu paai Naawunni niriba ban bɛ tinseesi la zaa maa ziiya? - ni, man daa bu zaŋŋi n yɛla m‑mugsi ya la zugu? Eeh, m badima, ya kyɛ n taalli kanŋɔ m‑pɛŋŋi ma!.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ n laa n‑niŋi siri ni n ti kyɛ ya saani na, din paasiri n kyɛnni buta zugu; ka kyɛnni wa saha gba n ku zaŋŋi n yɛla m‑mugsiri ya. Dama di pa la m boori ya neema, bee, ya ligri, amaa ya mammansi ka m boora. Ya bu nya? - di pa la biisi n ya m‑bɔ n‑zaali n‑ti ba badima ni ba madima, amaa ba badima ni ba madima nin bɔ n‑zaali n‑ti ba.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Dizugu n nin di man mari seelli maa zaa, halli ka seelli laa n‑kpalim n saani gba, ni n ti suŋŋi ya ya beebu ni; lala m mari sufupeelli n‑ti ma. Ammi, man ŋɔn wuri ya pam la zugu ka yan mun wuri ma beela? - ay, lala ka soori!.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Ni, tɔ! halli m bu zaŋŋi n yɛla m‑mugsi ya gba, amaa ni dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, man ŋɔn nyɛ yɔmsa zaa daana wa, n daa yɔmsi ya n‑gbaai ya.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Ni, ka dinŋɔna n daa tum ninvugseeba ya saani na. Tɔ! - ba sɔ n daa fa ya n‑ti ma? Ŋɔni n‑daa la? - Ay, ba sɔ zaa daa bu fa ya!.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: N daa bɛlim la Taituusi, ni, u kyɛ ya saani na, ka daa tum ti mabisɔ ni u dɔli u na m‑paasi. Tɔ, Taituusi daa fa ya neema? - Ay! u daa bu fa. Pa Sii yinni ni soori yinni maa ka ti dɔla la - m mammaŋŋa ni ban zaa?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Ka dinŋɔna ya yi teesiri, ni, ti tɔ'asiri ti yɛttɔ'a wa ni yarima ti kari ti yɛlli sariya? Ay, di pa lala, n zɔsuma! Ti bɛ la Kirisita saani ka ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni tooni. Ka tin niŋni seelli zaa nyɛ la din nin kpamsi ya.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Dama n zɔri la dabeem, man ŋɔn teesi, ni, di yi pa seem n nin kyɛ na ka ku paai ya hoo man boori yan nyɛ seem la. Ka ya gba ku nya ma yan boori m bɛ seem maa. Ii, dabeem mari ma, ni, nangbankpeemni nin bɛ ni, ni nyuuri zappu, ni nyigbu, ni suuri, ni wɔligri, ni nyaanzum, ni galagala, ni karimbaani, ni basabasa zaa tuma an bu dɔli soori.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: Dabeem m mari ma yaasa, ni, n yi kyɛ na ka Naawunni nin laa n‑sigsi ma ya nini ni yaasa, ka n nin nya sufusa'aŋŋu pam ban pun tum tumbeeri la zugu, ka ba ku sakki n‑takki n‑yi lala yɛla ni. Ba tumbeeri wa bɛ la hoo da'ari tuma la, ni zina, ni kɔribeeri tuma an bu dɔli soori halli zaa.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: N kyɛnni kanŋɔ nin paasi buta: ni, Yɛlli kam zaa nin zaani suŋŋa, niriba ayi-ata yi di di sɛhara n‑dɔli la taaba.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: N buyi kyɛnni maa saha ka n daa pun bɛ ya saani nti daŋŋi n‑yɛli n yɛttɔ'a wa, ka man ŋɔn ka ya saani pampaŋŋɔ n ya maali n‑yɛli yaasa. Ni, n yi labi na n ku kyɛ ban daa pun tum tumbeeri la, ka ku laa n‑kyɛ baseeba ban paasi ba zugu la pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Di gbinni nyɛ la: ya daa boori la n wulisi ya ka ya baŋŋi, ni, Kirisita tɔ'asiri man yɛttɔ'a wa puuni. Tɔ: ya baŋŋma, ni, u pa la bumbugsiga n‑ti ya: ay! - u siri mari la yiiko ya saani.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: U siri daa bukki ni ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu; amaa ni lala maa zaa yɔri, pampaŋŋɔ u mari la nyɔvuri m‑bɛ ni Naawunni yiiko zugu. Lala taabu ti gba la'am u saani m‑bukki, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu ti nin bɛ u ni n‑tumni n‑tiri ya.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Ya viisim ya mansi n‑nya yan bɛ la yɛdda niŋbu maa ni, bee? - Ya zamsima ya mansi. Bee, ya na bu mi, ni, Yisa Kirisita bɛ la ya saani? Ya yi pa la seeba ban lu zamsigu ni, u bɛ ni.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: M mari tama, ni, ya nin ti baŋŋi, ni, tirima bu lu zamsigu ni.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Tɔ: ti susiri Naawunni, ni, ya ku tum din bu tu. Pa la ni niriba ti nya tirima, ni, ti nyaŋŋi zamsigu maa ni. Ay! - tin boori seem nyɛ la: ni, yarima nin tumni din tu, halli ni tirim bɛ la hoo ban lu zamsigu ni gba.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Dama ti ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli n‑galim yɛlimaŋni maa, see ka ti dɔli la yɛlimaŋni soori n‑tumna.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Ti yi ti bukki ka yan mun kpeem, ti mari sufupeelim di zugu. Ti kur susiri Naawunni, ni, ya nin nyaŋŋi seelikam suŋŋa.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Lala zugu ka n sɔbri anŋɔ man na ka ya saani maa, ni n yi ti kyɛ na n ku mari kukɔri nyiim n‑zaŋŋi n yiiko m‑mugsiri ya. Ti Dugma zaŋŋi yiiko wa n‑ti ma, ni m maali ya yɛla ka a kyaŋŋi tooni ka ku labi nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Yɛttɔ'a maa naari, m mabiisi, nyɛ la: ni, ya kpalim suŋŋa. Ya kyɛ ka ya kpaŋŋi ya mansi, kyɛ ka ya sufu kpamsi, ka mari nangbanyinni n‑ti taaba, ni sufudoaa zaa. Naawunni siri nyɛ la Sufudoaa ni Wurilim Daana, ka u nin bɛ ya saani.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Ya puusima taaba suŋŋasuŋŋa ti wurilim puusigu ni. Tiŋŋa wa Naawunni niriba puusiri ya zaa na.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya piinisuma bɛ ya zaa saani, ni Naawunni wurilim, ni Sii Kasi la suŋŋsim zaa.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Man Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya: mani ka ba booli ma nti leebi tuumu la, ka di daa bu yi ninsaala saani, bee, ninsaala kpaŋmantiri puuni, amaa ka yi la Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani, ni ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa doaai u kum ni saani na.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: Man ni ti mabiseeba ban bɛ kpa m‑paasi n zugu n‑sɔbri n‑tiri yarima - ya Naawunni la'aŋŋu dima zaa ban bɛ la Galɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya nin ti ya piinisuma ni alaafɛa zaa.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: Din yɛ n‑kyɛ ka ti tuligi n‑yi zuna tumbeeri sahabeoo puuni nŋɔa#:- ŋɔn Yisa Kirisita daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi, ti tumbeeri zugu; ka di daa dɔli ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa boori seem.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Tibgri bɛ la ŋɔn Naawunni saani halli saha din ka tɔriga! Ammi .

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: Di gyɛri ma pam ya yɛla zugu! Dama di na bu yuui ka ya zaa daa ŋmaligi n‑za'asi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa booli ya u saani na, u piinisuma la zugu, ka ya daa dee n‑sakki n‑tiri lahabasunseaa.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, lahabasunseaa laa n‑ka ni, amaa n dee n‑yɛtti la lala, dama ninvugseeba bɛ ni n‑gyɛtti ya yɛm ka kpaŋŋni ba mansi ni ba takki Yisa Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu maa.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Amaa di yi ti nyɛ tirima - bee, halli malaaka ŋɔn yi arizana ni na gba - n yi mooli lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ya ka ka bɛ ka kɔkɔ ka kyɛ tin daa mooli lahabasunseaa n‑ti ya la, ŋɔn sa'amma m‑basi bugum puuni halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Ti daa pun yɛli lala, ka n laa n‑yɛtti ya wa yaasa: nira kam yi mooli lahabaseaa n‑ti ya ka ka bɛ ka kɔkɔ ka kyɛ yan daa pun deaai seaa maa, di daama ŋɔn sa'amma m‑basi halli saha kan ka naari!.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Kala, dinŋɔ ŋmani kaman n boori la ni ninsaalidima nini tigi ma? Bee, Naawunni nintigri ka m boora? Ni, n kpaŋŋni n maŋŋa la ni sɔkam ti baŋŋi n yɛla, fa! Ay! - N da naa m‑boori n niŋi lala, n da naa n‑ku niŋi Kirisita tuntumna, walla!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: Ya kyɛ ka n yɛli ya, m mabiisi, ni, lahabasunseaa man daa mooli n‑ti ya maa pa la ninsaala dinni.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: N daa bu deaai ka sɔsɔ saani, bee, sɔ n daa zaŋŋi ka n‑wulisi ma. Di daa nyɛ la Yisa Masiiya mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kasigi ka n‑ti ma.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Ya daa pun wum sahanseaa man daa na dɔli la Yɛhudiyadima addiini soori la yɛla. Ni, n daa kur namsiri la Naawunni la'aŋŋu dima ka daa bu zɔri ba namboo, ka daa laa n‑kpaŋŋi m maŋŋa ni n sa'am ba yɛla zaa.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: N daa bɛ la Yɛhudiyadima addiini soori ni nimbirimoaa n‑gaari n taaba maa, ka kur zaŋŋi saha n‑dɔla ti yaadima addiini kaya deede.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Amaa Naawunni piinisuma zugu, u daa pun yiisi mani pɔi ka ba daa naayi dɔ'ai ma, ka daa ti booli ma ni n tum n‑ti u. Ka sahanseaa ŋɔn daa yi ti boori maa.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: u daa kasigi u Bii n‑wulisi ma, din yɛ n‑kyɛ ka n moonni yɛttɔ'amaŋa maa lahabaari n‑tisi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa. Lala saha maa n daa bu kyaŋŋi ninsaalisɔ saani nti deaai noori kpaaŋŋu.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: n mun daa bu kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm nti nya tuumudima ban daa bɛ ni n‑daŋŋi n tooni tuumu tuma puuni la. Amaa n daa na'a kyaŋŋi la Arabii tingbaŋŋu la ni, ka naayi labi Damasikusi timpuuni na.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Tɔ: ka di daa niŋi yuuma ata nyaaŋŋa pɔi ka n daa kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm nti nya Piita ni ti ti baŋŋi taaba; ka man ni ŋɔn daa bɛ ni hoo dapii ni anu saha.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: N daa bu nya tuumusɔ yaasa ka di pa ni Gyeemsi kɔkɔa, ti Dugma tunzɔ la.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Man sɔbi seelli wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Naawunni gba mi, ni, n bu ma'ari ziri!.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa n daa kyaŋŋi la Siriiya ni Silisii tingbana ya'aseesi.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: Lala saha maa ka Kirisita la'aŋŋu ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ Gyuudiiya tingbaŋŋu ni la daa na zi n‑nya ma.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Ba daa na'a kur wumni la lahabaari, ni, Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa namsiri ti saha din gaari la - ŋɔna yi moonni la yɛdda yɛla pampaŋŋɔa, halli ŋɔn daa kur kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa ni u sa'am a m‑basi la.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Ka dizugu daa kyɛ ka ba daa pɛ'ai ka tibgi Naawunni man zugu.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Yarim Galɛseaa dima wa! Yan gyɛra dima wa! Ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi u tiima n‑kyirim ya wa? Ya mammantoori nini n daa nya m‑baŋŋi Yisa Masiiya kum maa yɛla, ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu!.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Ya yɛlim ma yɛlli yinni wa: ammi, ya daa deaai Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi yan daa tum la zaaligudima ŋɔn wulisi seem tuma zugu, bee, yan daa wum lahabasuŋŋu la ka daa niŋi ka yɛdda zugu?.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Wula zugu ka ya lee n‑nyɛ gyɛra lala? Ya daa piili la ni Naawunni Sii Kasi maa; ya boori la ya ti naai ni ya mammantoori yiiko?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Ammi, yan daa di wahala seem wa, di daa ka gbinni halli beela? Di yi ti nyɛ la, ni, di siri daa ka gbinni!.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi u Sii Kasi n‑ti ya la, ka tum alahazibu tuntumsa n‑ti ya, zaaligudima tumpu zugu n‑daa la? bee, yan daa wum la Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu la ka niŋi ka yɛdda la zugu?.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Ibrahim muna - kaman Litaafi ŋɔn pun yɛli, ni, U daa sakki n‑niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda, ka u yɛdda maa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Dinŋɔna ya baŋŋma, ni, Ibrahim ya'ansi ni yɛlimaŋni n nyɛ ninvugseeba ban mari yɛdda la.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Litaafi wulisiya, ni, Naawunni nin ti deaai ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa hoo yɛlimaŋni dima ba yɛdda niŋbu maa zugu. Ka dizugu ka Litaafi daa daŋŋi m‑mooli lahabasuma maa n‑tisi Ibrahim, ni, Nyin zugu ka duniya wa niriba zaa nin nya zugsuŋŋu.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Ibrahim daa nyɛ yɛdda daana ka daa nya bunsuma nangbambasiri maa di zugu, ka lala ka ŋɔn kam zaa niŋi yɛdda nin ti nya bunsuma la, kaman Ibrahim ŋɔn daa nya seem maa taabu.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Ban kur zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑sakki n‑tiri zaaligudima tumpu la yɛla, ban m bɛ la mbusu yɛla puuni. Dama Litaafi pun yɛli, ni, Ŋɔn kam bu kpalim n‑sakkri n‑deaari bunseelli kam din sɔbi n‑dooli zaalikpana gbaŋŋu la puuni n‑niŋni a ni, mbusu bɛ di daama zugu.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: Dinŋɔna di wulisiya, ni, sɔsɔ ku yi taalli Naawunni saani hoo yɛlimaŋni daana, fukumsi zaaligudima zugu: dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni, Yɛlimaŋni daana nin nya nyɔvuri u yɛdda niŋbu zugu.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Amaa zaaligu yɛla bu dɔli la yɛdda soori; ay! - di nyɛ la hoo Litaafi ŋɔn pun yɛli la, ni, Ŋɔn kam zaa tum bunseela zaa zaaligu ŋɔn wulisi seem maa nin ti vu.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Amaa Kirisita Masiiya mammaŋŋa daa leebi la mbusu tuuri, ni u ti suŋŋi ti ka yiisi ti mbusu galinsi ni na - seesi zaaligu ŋɔn daa mari ti a puuni la; dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni, Ban zaŋŋi ninvugsɔ zaa n‑yuli doo zugu ni u ti kpi la - Naawunni mbusu bɛ la di daama zugu.
