eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Ŋɔn Kirisita daa niŋi lala, ni u kyɛ ka di ti paai ban kam pa Yɛhudiyadima la - Naawunni zugsuŋŋu bunseesi ŋɔn daa pun basi nangbambasiri n‑tisi Ibrahim la nin paai ba na. Ŋɔn Yisa Masiiya zugu ka ba ti deaai a. Dinzugu ti yɛdda puuni ti nin toaai n‑deaai Vuusim Kasi maa, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun basi nangbambasiri maa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: M mabiisi, n yɛ zaŋŋi la ti beoo kam yɛla n‑ŋmansi n‑wulisi ya#:- tɔ, niriba ayi yi ti zaali yɛttɔkka ka sakki n‑ti taaba, sɔ ku toaai n‑kpiisi ka, bee, m‑paasi ka seela.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa basi nangbambasiri n‑tisi, ni, Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa. Litaafi bu tɔ'asiri la nyaaŋŋa dima pam yɛla amaa, ni, nyaaŋŋa - ninvugyinni kɔkɔa. Di nyɛ la: ni, Kirisita Masiiya maa.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Man ŋɔn boori n yɛli seem la nyɛ la nŋɔa#:- Naawunni daa pun lɔ la nangbanyinni yɛliseaa n‑ti Ibrahim ka daa basi nangbambasiri n‑zaali ka. Di nyaaŋŋa di daa yuui m‑paai yuunkɔbsinaasi ni pisita pɔi, ka zaaligu daa yi na. Tɔ! Lala zaaligu ku toaai n‑nyaŋŋi n‑sa'am daŋŋni yɛlli maa ka kpiisi Naawunni nangbambasiri la.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Dama Naawunni piini yi da dɔli fukumsi zaaligudima maa, dinŋɔna di daa bu naa n‑dɔli u nangbambasiri maa yaasa. Amaa u nangbambasiri basibu zugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi piini maa n‑tisi Ibrahim.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Tɔ, ka fukumsi zaaligu maa gbinni? Ka daa paasiya, ni ka yɛ n‑wulisi tumbeeri ŋɔn nyɛ seela, ka laa n‑wulisi, ni, ka yɛ m‑bɛ ni n‑guusira, halli ka Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa la ti yi na, ban daa basi nangbambasiri maa n‑ti sɔ la. Malaakadima saani ka lala fukumsi zaaligu maa daa paai na ka di bɛ la sindaanzɔtta nuu ni.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Tɔ: ka sindaani daana yɛttɔ'a pa la ninvugyinni kɔkɔ saani; Naawunni mun n nyɛ yinni.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Kala, di wulisiya, ni, lala fukumsi zaaligudima maa galim la Naawunni nangbambasiri maa? Ay, halli beela! Dama ninsaala yi daa nyaŋŋi n‑deaai zaaligu seaa kan mari nyɔvuri, dinŋɔna sɔkam siri da naa n‑niŋi yɛlimaŋni daana, zaaligu maa zugu.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Amaa Litaafi yɛliya, ni, duniya wa zaa bɛ la ala'akyi tumbeeri yiiko suulinsi puuni; ka dizugu kyɛ ka piiniseesi an bɛ nangbambasiri maa puuni, zaŋŋi n‑kpa yɛdda zugu la, nin nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban mari yɛdda la dina.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Amaa yɛdda saha maa ŋɔn daa na bu paai na, fukumsi zaalikpana maa daa kur guri ti kaman dansarika dima, halli ka yɛdda yɛla kyɛnni na ti yi paalu.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Dinzugu zaaligu maa daa su ti yɛla zaa kyappi, halli ka Kirisita ti paai na, din ti yɛ kyɛ ka ti yi Naawunni taalli puuni hoo yɛlimaŋni dima, ti yɛdda niŋbu zugu.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ yɛdda saha ŋɔn yi paai na wa, ti bu laa m‑boori fukumsi nti su ti n‑gura.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Di nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa yɛdda la zugu ka yarima zaa n nyɛ Naawunni biisi.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Ya ninvugseeba zaa ban daa su Naawunni koom la Yisa Masiiya puuni, ya daa zaŋŋi u n‑yɛ hoo ban yeeri sutura la.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Ka dinzugu Yisa saani ka Yɛhudiyadima ni ban pa Yɛhudiyadima yɛla laa n‑ka ni, bee, daaba ni ban su ba mansi yɛla, bee, dappa ni pɔ'aba yɛla - anŋɔ zaa laa n‑ka ni. Ya zaasa nyɛ la bunyinni Kirisita Yisa Masiiya puuni.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Ya yi nyɛ la Kirisita ŋɔn su seeba, tɔ, ka dinzugu ya nyɛ la Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa dima, ka ya nin ti deaai Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiseesi maa.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Tɔ: ka ligri toosigu n‑tiri Naawunni niriba la yɛla muna - di pa la yɛlimugsa man saani ni n sɔbi di yɛla n‑ti ya.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Dama m pun mi halli yan boori ni ya suŋŋi lala seem. Halli n pun tibgi ya yuuri Masidoonya dima saani, ni, ya Akaaya tingbaŋŋu dima wa n pun maali ya siri ni ya tisi ka di yuui, halli yuumni la sa. Ka ya zigizigi la yɛla daa kyɛ ka ba niriba pam gbaai tuma wa m‑paasi.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna n tumni la ti mabiisi wa ni ba daŋŋi n tooni n‑kyɛ ya saani na, ni tin nyu bariseesi ya yɛla wa zugu ku basi yɔri. Ti daa yɛli ba, ni, ya daa pun maali ya siri.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: ka m bu boori Masidoonya niriba ti dɔli ma na n‑nya ka ya bu niŋi siri zaa. Lala nin diisi tirima vi, ka laa n‑diisi ya gba vi, tin daa niŋi ya yɛdda seem wa zugu.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Lala zugu ka n daa teesi, ni, di kpa la talasi ni n kpamsi ti mabiisi wa ka ba daŋŋi ma na n‑suŋŋi ya ni ya ti sɛ'asi ya piiniseesi yan daa pun yɛli, ni, ya nin tisi la. Lala nin kyɛ ka a siri nyɛ la wurilim piini, pa ban mugsi ya seesi zugu.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Ya teaama dinŋɔa: ŋɔn butti n‑ya'ara ti ya n‑ŋmaari ni n‑ya'ara; ka ŋɔn mun n butti n‑zoaara ti ya n‑ŋmaari ni n‑zoaara.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Amaa see ka sɔkam ti tisi u sufu ŋɔn teesi, ni, u tisima seem, pa la mugsigu zugu, dama Naawunni wuri la ŋɔn tisiri sufupeelim zugu.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Ka ŋɔn Naawunni nin kyɛ ka u piini zaa paari ya saani na, ni ya ti mari yan boori seelli zaa sahakam, ka laa m‑mari m‑paasi ni ya ti zaŋŋni n‑suŋŋni niriba.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tisiri la, ni#:- U zaŋŋi piini n‑wurim n‑tisi fara dima, Ka kpalim la yɛlimaŋni daana sahakam.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Ka Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi bumbura n‑tiri ban butti la, ka laa n‑tiri bundira ni ba ti di. Ka u siri nin zaŋŋi bumbura n‑tisi yarima, ka laa m‑paasi ya antaam zaa - di nyɛ la: yɛlimaŋni puuni beesigu.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Ya nin bɛ ni hoo seeba bam mara, bunseelikam zaa puuni; ka di nin kyɛ ka ya tisiri ya yuubu sahakam: ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba puusi Naawunni ya tuma wa zugu, tirim mun saani.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu yan tumni suŋŋsim wa gbinni pa la, ni, ya nin suŋŋi Naawunni niriba ban pɔsigi bunseesi zii kɔkɔa, amaa ka di laa m‑mari la wulisigu pam ka naayi paasiri Naawunni puusigu.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Ya yi tum suŋŋsim tuma wa n‑naai, di nin wulisi ya beesisuŋŋu beebu yɛla. Ka niriba nin ti nya; ka pɛ'ai Naawunni, ni, ya deaari ŋɔn Naawunni noori n‑tɔ'asiri n‑wulisiri Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu yɛla yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ka din paasi lala zugu, ni, ya yiisiri ya sufu yuubu piini n‑tiri ba, ni sɔkam.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Ka ba nin laa n‑susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ya, ka nin boori ya yɛla ni nimbirimoaa, dama Naawunni ti ya u piinisuma pam gba, halli nti yakki.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Pɛ'abu siri bɛ ni n‑ti Naawunni u piini tisibu zugu, halli ni tin ku toaai n‑wulisi u piini wa zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem la.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Amaa n yɛtti ya wa, ni, bisɔ yi di u ba faari ka na nyɛ bipɔlli, lala saha maa u yi bɛ la kaman daabri ŋɔn bɛ seem la, halli ŋɔn kur su bunseelli kam maa zaa yɔri.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Ŋɔn yi na nyɛ bipɔlli sahaseaa maa, seeba n yi su u fukumsi ka maanni u yɛla kam zaa n‑tiri u, halli ka u ba maa ŋɔn daa zaali sahaseelli maa yi ti paai.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Lala maa taabu n daa nyɛ di n‑tiri tirima, ka ti gba daa bɛ hoo daaba n‑tisi siseeba ban su duniya wa fukumsi pɔi, ka ti daa ti zoaai na n‑sa'ai m‑paai niriba ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: Amaa nimbirimoaa saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, Naawunni daa tum la u Bii na. U daa kyɛ na kaman ninsaala ka u ma daa dɔ'ai u ni u ti bɛ Yɛhudiyadima zaalikpana la suulinsi ni.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: U daa niŋi lala ni u ti suŋŋi ban daa bɛ zaalikpana maa suulinsi puuni la, ni di kyɛ ka tirima ti leebi Naawunni biisi.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Dinŋɔ n wulisi, ni, ya siri nyɛ la u biisi, ni, Naawunni daa tum u Biiya Sii Kasi na n‑kpeesi ti sufu ni. Ŋɔna n nyɛ Sisɔ ŋɔn kaasiri m‑boonna, ni, Abba! , bee M Ba, m Ba.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Ka dinzugu pampaŋŋɔ i laa m‑pa la u daabri amaa u biiya. Ka nyin ŋɔn nyɛ u bii maa zugu, Naawunni nin zaŋŋi ŋɔn mari bunseela ni u tisi u biisi la n‑ti i.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Daadaa la ya daa na bu mi Naawunni, ka dizugu ya daa nyɛ la daaba, halli ka ban daa su ya daa pa la Naawummaŋni.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ, yan yipa baŋŋi Naawunni wa - bee, ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn baŋŋi ya yɛla maa - ka di kyaŋŋi la wula ka ya laa m‑boori ya maali n‑leebi daaba? Ammi, ya yɛ laa n‑labi duniya fukumsi siisi wa saani? - siseeba an ka kpeeŋŋu wa, bumbugsa wa, fa! Bumbɔ n taasi ka ya laa m‑boori lala?.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Ya kur dɔli la kaari sɔkurigu, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kyuusi yɛla, ka ya tibgri dabseesi, ni gɔriseesi, ni yuunseesi, ni sahaseesi.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: Di mugsi ma pam ya zugu! Ni, ammi, di pa seem man ŋɔn daa tum tunseesi zaa n‑ti ya maa basi la yɔri?.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: M mabiisi, n bɛlimni ya ni, ni, ya bɛ ni hoo man bɛ seem wa, dama man gba daa bɛ la hoo yan bɛ seem maa. Ya bu tum ma taaliseaa zaa.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ya teaama man ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti ya sahanseaa la; di daa kur nyɛ la dooru ŋɔn daa mari ma la zugu.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Halli ni n ningbina yɛla ŋɔn daa mugsi ya pam maa zaa yɔri, ya daa bu yi n nyaaŋŋa, bee, n‑kari ma. Amaa ya daa deaai mani kaman yan nin deaai arizana ni malaakasɔ seem, bee, hoo yan daa deaai Yisa Masiiya seem la.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Ya daa mari sufupeelli pam! Ka bɔ n niŋi? Man mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑yɛli, ni, ya yi daa naa n‑toaai n‑fugsi ya toori nimbiya n‑ti ma gba, ya daa naa n‑niŋi lala.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Tɔ, ka man yi leebi la ya dindaana, man daa tɔ'asi yɛlimaŋni maa zugu?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Ninvugseeba wulisiri la ba wurilim n‑ti ya nimmoosi, amaa ka ba sufu ni teesa bɛa. Ban boori ba niŋi ya seem kur nyɛ la, ni, ba wɔligi ya ka kyɛ ma, din nin kyɛ ka ya gba wurilim nimmoaa ti leebi ba dinni.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Tɔ, di sumsi ni ya mari wurilim ni nimbirimoaa, kan nin mari gbinni din veela: ka mari lala saha kam zaa, ka pa la man ŋɔn yi ti bɛ ya saani maa kɔkɔa.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: M bisuma! Kaman pɔ'a ŋɔn yi bɛ dɔ'am beerima puuni seem la, lala taataabu beerima ni ka m bɛ ni n‑tiri ya, ka n guusiri halli ni Kirisita Masiiya ti kyɛ ya saani na nti bɛ ya ni.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: N daa kur boori la n da bɛ ya saani pampaŋŋɔa, din da naa n‑kyɛ ka n takki man ŋɔn tɔ'asiri seem n‑tiri ya. Di mugsi ma pam ya zugu!.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Kyɛ ka n boosi yarim ninvugseeba ban boori ba bɛ zaaligu suulinsi ni la: ya daa bu wum fukumsi zaaligudima maa ŋɔn wulisi seem, bee?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, Ibrahim daa mari la bidibsi ayi; ka yinni daa nyɛ daabpɔ'a biiya, ka ŋɔnla mun daa nyɛ birikyimpɔ'a biiya.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Ba daa kur dɔ'ai u bisɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ pɔ'adaabri bii maa la hoo pɔ'a ŋɔn kur dɔ'ari bii kam zaa seem maa; amaa u bisɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ birikyimpɔ'a la bii maa ŋɔna, ba daa dɔ'ai ŋɔnla Naawunni nangbambasiri basibu puuni.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Yɛttɔ'a wa nyɛ la hoo yɛttɔ'anyɛsa, ka a mari la gbinni n‑tiri ya: pɔ'aba ayi wa zɛ la nangbanyinni loobu yɛttɔ'a ayi zaani. Pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai biisi daabtiri puuni la nyɛ la Hagaara, ŋɔna n zɛ Sainayi zoori la zaani.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Tɔ: Sainayi zoori wa zɛ la Arabii tingbaŋŋu la ni - Hagaara nla. Ka ka mun zɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa zaani - pampaŋŋɔ Gyɛrusalɛm la - dama ŋɔn Hagaara daa nyɛ la daabri, ka Gyɛrusalɛm wa niriba kur bɛ la daabtiri ni.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Yɔlisimma nyɛ la ti dinni, Kirisita Masiiya ŋɔn daa yɔlisi ti ni! Dinzugu ya zaanma kaman birikyindima ban su ba mansi, ka di laa n‑zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑leebi daaba yaasa.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Amaa arizana ni Gyɛrusalɛm la su ka maŋŋa; ti ma maa nla.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Ya wumma! Man Pɔla n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi sakki ka kyɛ ka ba guni ya, dinŋɔna Kirisita ka bukyi n‑ti ya halli beela.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni#:- Nyin pɔ'akundɔ'ara wa, Na kur mari sufupeelim! Kaasima ka tasiri ni sufumaasim, Nyin ŋɔn na zi n‑nya bidɔ'am beerim! Pɔ'aziiliŋŋa nin ti mari biisi pam n‑gaari pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn bɛ doo yiri.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: M‑paasi yaasa, man laa n‑yɛtti ya, ni, dɔsɔ ŋɔn sakki ka kyɛ ka ba guni u, see ka u dɔli la fukumsi zaalikpana la zaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Yarim muna, m mabiisi - ya yi nyɛ la Naawunni biisi nangbambasiri basibu zugu, kaman Yisahaaku gba ŋɔn daa nyɛ Ibrahim biiya seem la.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Yarima ban boori ya yiisi ya taalli Naawunni saani zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi fukumsi zaaligudima dɔligu zugu la, ya wɔligri ya mansi ka kyɛ Masiiya. Ya ku laa n‑nya anfaani Naawunni saani.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Lala saha maa ka bisɔ ban daa dɔ'ai kaman pɔ'a ŋɔn kur dɔ'ari seem maa daa niŋi la alaka n‑niŋi bisɔ ban daa dɔ'ai Naawunni Sii nangbambasiri puuni la; ka pampaŋŋɔ di kur nyɛ la lala taataabu maa.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Amaa tirim ŋɔna, ti mari tama, ni, Naawunni nin yiisi ti taalli u saani; dinŋɔ ka ti guusira ti yɛdda niŋbu zugu, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la puuni.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Amaa wula ka Litaafi yɛtta? - Ka yɛliya, ni, Karim pɔ'adaabri maa ni u bii la m‑basi kpaŋŋa; dama pɔ'adaabri bii maa bu ti yɛ la'am birikyimpɔ'a bii maa saani n‑di ba ba maa faari.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Ka tin ni Yisa yi la'am n‑niŋi yinni, sɔ yi guni, bee, u bu guni, a zaa ka yaa halli beela; kulla yɛdda mara, din mun tumni wurilim zugu.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Ka dinzugu, m mabiisi, tirim pa la pɔ'adaabri la biisi amaa ti lee n‑nyɛ la birikyimpɔ'a maa biisi.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Ya daa tumni la din tuusi! Ŋɔni n ta'ai ya ni ya di laa n‑wum n‑ti yɛlimaŋni maa?.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Di siri pa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli ya la n‑ta'ai ya lala!.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Ni, Di kur nyɛ la dabina beela kɔkɔ zugu ka boroboro pitta.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Amaa man niŋi ya yɛdda ti Dugma zugu, ni, ya gba nin sakki lala. Tɔ: ninvugsɔ ŋɔn kyirimni ya wa muna - di daama yi nyɛ la sɔ zaa, u tumbeeri taalli nin kpalim n‑dɔli u.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: M mabiisi, amaa man ŋɔna - ni, n na kur kpalim n‑gyinni m‑moonni, ni, gunibu nyɛ la bunseela din kpa talasi: tɔ, ka di yi bɛ la lala, wula zugu ka n laa m‑bɛ galinsi ni wahala puuni? Dama man yi siri moonni lala, Yisa kum dɔpulli yɛla ku laa m‑bɛ n saani ni ba ti yaai ma di zugu.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Ban kyirimni ya muna - man teesa puuni, ba yi boori lala taabu yɛla, see ka ba foaai ba lana m‑basi!.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: M mabiisi, yarima ŋɔna - ba daa booli ya yɔlisimma zii na, ka mugsigu zaa ka ya saani. Amaa yan di kyɛ ka mugsigu kaalinsi din bɛ ya saani wa leebi soori n‑ti ya, ka ningbina ni kɔribeeri ti deaai ya beebu zaasa. Amaa ya lee n‑kyɛ ka di nyɛ la wurilim zugu ka ya tumni n‑tiri taaba.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Dama fukumsi zaaligudima maa la'am n‑niŋi zaalikpanni yinni, ni#:- Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Amaa ya bɛ ni hoo mooni bunkɔbri, ka dumni taaba m‑paari taaba dansi; ya baŋŋma, miri ka ya ti sa'am taaba n‑naai zaa zaa.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Man ŋɔn yɛtti seem nŋɔa#:- ya kyɛ ka Sii Kasi maa wulisiri ya ya beebu zaa soori, ka ya ku tum la ninsaala kɔribeeri boorimma tuma.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Dama ninsaala kɔribeeri ŋɔn za'asiri bunseela ka Siiya Kasi boora, ka Siiya Kasi mun ŋɔn bu boori bunseelli la ka kɔrisi gba boora. Sɔya ayi wa mari taaba dimni, ka dinŋɔ wulisiya, ni, ya ku toaai n‑niŋi yan boori seem.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Ka ya mun yi dɔli la Sii Kasi maa wulisigu la, dinŋɔna ya ku laa m‑bɛ fukumsi zaaligudima maa suulinsi ni.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Ninsaala kɔrisi tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem kur yi paalu ni. Zina da'ari tuma n nyɛ a, ni kyɔ'ansi beebu, ni ban bu mari ba mansi suŋŋa tuma zaa:.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: m‑paasi buga gyeeŋŋu, ni soo, ni tiima tuma. Niriba leebri la dindaandima ka zabri taaba; ba zabri zinyuu, ka kyɛ ka ba suuri yisigra, ka mari tumbeeri teesa ni ba ti duusi ba mansi. Ba laa m‑boori wɔliginsim.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: Halli ba zabri nyuuri; ka laa n‑nyuuri daam m‑bugra; ka tumni tunkyɔ'ama, ni tumbeeri wa taabu baligu kam zaa. N yɛtti ya ni, kaman man daa pun daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya seem la: ni, ban kam zaa bɛ anŋɔ taataabu beesigu maa ni ku ti di Naawunni naam faari.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Amaa Siiya Kasi beebu ni bɛ la hoo tiseaa kan wanni walisuma: wurilim, ni sufupeelim, ni sufudoaa n‑nyɛ a, m‑paasi sugru, ni nirilim, ni tunsuma tumpu, ni yɛdda niŋbu.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: Niriba sigsiri ba mansi, ka mari ba mansi suŋŋa. Zaalisɛseaa ka ni n‑galimni yɛla wa taataabu.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Ka ban kam zaa nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa dina pun zaŋŋi ninsaatiri teesa n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku - ana ni kɔribeeri boorimma zaa.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Siiya Vuusim Kasi maa tiri ti la nyɔvuri; see ka u laa n‑su ti nyɔvuya maa sɔya zaa gba.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Faasee ka ti kyɛ ti karimbaani m‑basi; ka ti di laa n‑yisigri taaba sufu, n‑zabri nyuuri ni taaba m‑paasi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: M mabiisi, sɔ yi tumni taalli ka ya baŋŋi lala, see ka yarima ban mari Siiya Kasi maa nin zaŋŋi u n‑zaali n‑wulisi u din tu; amaa faasee ka ya niŋi lala maa baalimm baalimm. Nyin gɔsim i mammaŋŋa suŋŋa, din nin kyɛ ka nyin gba di kpɛ zamsigu puuni.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Ya suŋŋma n‑zi taaba zitibsa, ka ya yi tumni lala, ya nin dɔlisi Kirisita Masiiya zaaligudima zaa.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Sɔ yi teesi, ni, u nyɛ la bunseelli ka mun siri pa bunseelli, di daama kur yɔmsiri la u mammaŋŋa.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa zamsi m‑baŋŋi u mammaŋŋa tuma an nyɛ seem; an dee n‑yi nyɛ la bunsuma, dinŋɔna u nin toaai n‑nyu bara di zugu: ka mun ku zaŋŋi u yɛla n‑ŋmamsi u taraansɔ gba ŋɔn tum seela.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Dama sɔkam zaa ziiri la u mammantoori ziiri.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Ba yi zaŋŋni Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑wulisiri sɔa, ŋɔn purigma u bunsuma n‑tisi u karimba maa.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Yan di yɔmsiri ya mammansi, ka Naawunni pa la ban niŋni sɔ ansarisi; ka nira yi buri bunseela, ana ka u nin ti kyɛa.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Sɔ yi buri ningbina kɔrisi beesigu bumbura, u nin ti nya sa'aŋŋu antaam di zugu; u mun yi buri la Siiya Kasi bumbura, lala nin kyɛ ka u ti nya nyɔvuriseaa kan ka naari.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Dizugu ti di kyɛ ka ti gyɛ tunsuma tumpu zugu; ka ti yi bu kyɛ tuma maa m‑basi, saha kyɛnni na ka ti nin nya ti antaam.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Ka dinzugu, soori ŋɔn kur yoaai n‑ti ti maa zugu, faasee ka ti tumni suŋŋa n‑tiri sɔkam zaa, ka di mun di tam la ti yiri dima maa ban nyɛ yɛdda niŋniba la.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Kur gɔsima man ŋɔn zaŋŋi n mammaŋŋa nuu n‑sɔbri bakyi kɔbkarakara seem n‑ti ya na#:-.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Ninvugseeba ban kur mugsiri ya ya gunibu zugu maa n nyɛ ban kur boori ni ba wulisi ba mansi ka nyu kafuga zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ningbina yɛla. Ban niŋi seem gbinni kur nyɛ la, ni, din nin kyɛ ka niriba ku namsi ba Kirisita dɔpulli maa zugu.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: Halli ninvugseeba ban guni maa bu wumni n‑tiri fukumsi zaaligudima maa; ba boori la ya guni ka ba yi nyu bara, ni, ya daa wum n‑ti ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ningbina yɛttɔ'a wa yɛla.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: Amaa man ŋɔna, m bu boori n nyu la bara bunseelli kam zugu, di yi pa la ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya dɔpulli la zugu kɔkɔa. U dɔpulli wa zugu ka duniya wa nyɛ la bunkpiiŋŋu n‑ti ma, ka man gba nyɛ bunkpiiŋŋu n‑ti duniya.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: Di pa la mugsigu kaman nira guni, bee, u bu guni; din kur mugsi nyɛ la i kur maali n‑niŋi bumpaalli.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Ninvugseeba ban kur dɔli soori wa, alaafɛ ni sufudoaa nin bɛ ba saani: halli ka laa m‑bɛ niriba zaa saani ban nyɛ Izraɛli dima halaali n‑ti Naawunni la!.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: Din baasi nyaaŋŋa: sɔ di laa n‑ti ma yɛlimugsa m‑paasi, dama kyisiseesi an bɛ n ningbina ni wa wulisiya, ni, n nyɛ la Yisa Kirisita nira.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: M mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zaŋŋma u piinisuma n‑niŋi ya zaa sufu ni! Ammi .

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Man Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya. Mani n nyɛ Yisa Kirisita Masiiya tuumu, Naawunni wurilim zugu. N sɔbri n‑tiri yarima, Naawunni niriba, yaseeba ban bɛ Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa n‑niŋi Kirisita Yisa yɛdda.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita nin ti ti ya piinisuma, ni sufudoaa zaa.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Pɛ'abu bɛ ni n‑ti Naawunni, sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la: ŋɔn Naawunni tiri ti u bunsuma zaa an yi u Sii maa saani arizana puuni na, tirim ŋɔn bɛ Yisa saani maa zugu.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: U daa yiisi ti ni, n‑niŋi ti u maŋtoori dina pɔi, ka naayi naam duniya wa. U daa yiisi ti lala, ni ti ti leebi u dina, m‑bɛ kasi, ka laa n‑ka taalli u saani. U wurilim zugu.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: ka u daa pun daŋŋi n‑niŋi din nin kyɛ ka ti leebi u biisi, Yisa Masiiya zugu, ka di dɔli la u yuubu maa ŋɔn boori seem la.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
local: Lala zugu ka pɛ'abu zaa sum la ni ŋɔna, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u gyirima piini n‑ti ti la zugu, ka daa niŋi lala u Bii maa saani, dama u wuri la u Bii maa pam.

eng: We have been ransomed through his Sons blood, and we have forgiveness for our failures based on his overflowing grace.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni biwuttigu puuni ka ti mari fa-m‑basi u ziim la zugu; tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi ka ti mara, ka di zaa nyɛ la Naawunni piiniseaa tin bu sum ni ka la, ka u ti ti.

eng: which he poured over us with wisdom and understanding.
local: Halli u daa ti ti lala piini maa zaa, ka laa n‑ti ti bamsim ni miilinsi zaa.

eng: God revealed his hidden design to us, which is according to his goodwill and the plan that he intended to accomplish through his Son.
local: U daa laa n‑wulisi u yuubu yɛla m‑paasi, nti kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi a yɛlisɔ'ara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u beesisuŋŋu la. Yɛla wa zaa ka u daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi n‑dɔlisi Kirisita.

eng: This is what God planned for the climax of all times: to bring all things together in Christ, the things in heaven along with the things on earth.
local: ka daa maali anŋɔ zaa siri, ni a ti naayi tum n‑naai. Lala nin niŋi, a saha yi ti paai la. Ka laa m‑maali siri, ni arizana ni tingbaŋŋu buna zaa ti la'am taaba, ka Yisa naayi su a zaa fukumsi.

eng: We have also received an inheritance in Christ. We were destined by the plan of God, who accomplishes everything according to his design.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔn Yisa saani ka ba daa yiisi ti gba, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun zaali di seem la. Dama u maanni la bunseelikam ni di dɔli u sufu yuubu ŋɔn boori seem.

eng: We are called to be an honor to Gods glory because we were the first to hope in Christ.
local: Ka di nin kyɛ ka tirima nin leebi hoo pɛ'abu ni gyirima n‑ti u - tirim ban daŋŋi m‑mari tama Kirisita saani la.

eng: You too heard the word of truth in Christ, which is the good news of your salvation. You were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit because you believed in Christ.
local: Ka yarima gba daa wum yɛlimaŋni yɛttɔ'a, Naawunni yɛligu tuliginsim lahabasuŋŋu la. Ka yan daa niŋi yɛdda la, ya daa deaai Naawunni Sii Kasi la hoo daalimni din wulisiri, ni, Yisa su ya yɛla. Halli Naawunni daa pun basi noori, ni, u nin niŋi lala.

eng: The Holy Spirit is the down payment on our inheritance, which is applied toward our redemption as Gods own people, resulting in the honor of Gods glory.
local: Lala Sii Kasi maa bɛ la ti saani n‑wulisiri, ni, u nin kyɛ ka ti di faari; ni, u nin kyɛ ka ba yɔ ti samni n‑fa ti m‑basi; ni, u nin kyɛ ka ti yɛla kyɛ ka pɛ'abu bɛ ni n‑tiri Naawunni.

eng: Since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all Gods people, this is the reason that.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu man gba bu kyɛ pɛ'abu ya zugu, man ŋɔn daa wum, ni, ya niŋi ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda sahanseaa la, halli ni zuna. Ka n laa n‑wum yan wuri Naawunni niriba zaa seem la. Dinzugu n puusiri Naawunni n‑tiri ya saha kam, ka teaari ya yɛla n wunsusigu ni.

eng: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, will give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation that makes God known to you.
local: Ti Ba Gyirima Zaa Daana! - Naawunni n nyɛ u n‑ti ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita; ti mun susiri u, ni, u tim ya u Siiya, ka u wulisi ya yɛm ka kasigi u yɛlitibsa n‑ti ya, ka kyɛ ka ya maali m‑baŋŋi u yɛla m‑paasi.

eng: I pray that the eyes of your heart will have enough light to see what is the hope of Gods call, what is the richness of Gods glorious inheritance among believers.
local: Ka n laa n‑susiri, ni, ya sufu nin nya ninneesim ka ya baŋŋi tama seaa zugu ŋɔn booli ya la, ka ya laa m‑baŋŋi ya faari ŋɔn ya zoaai seem m‑mari gyirima la, n‑tiri yaseeba ban nyɛ ŋɔn Naawunni dina maa.

eng: and what is the overwhelming greatness of Gods power that is working among us believers. This power is conferred by the energy of Gods powerful strength.
local: M‑paasi, ya nin baŋŋi u yiiko ŋɔn kpeem seem halli n‑ti yakki, n‑tiri tiseeba ban niŋi u yɛdda la.

eng: Gods power was at work in Christ when God raised him from the dead and sat him at Gods right side in the heavens.
local: U lala yiiko wa ka u daa zaŋŋi n‑tumni tunkpeema n‑tiri Kirisita, ka neaai u kum ni. Ka daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u n‑ziili ŋɔn Naawunni nudirigu zizii maa ni, arizana naam gyirima puuni.

eng: far above every ruler and authority and power and angelic power, any power that might be named not only now but in the future.
local: Ka di gaari naam zaa, ni yiiko zaa, ni kpamma ni gɔmnanti zaa fukumsi marigu. Yuseaa ka ni ka ba putti sɔ n‑gaari ŋɔn Yisa yuuri; ka di pa la beesigu kanŋɔ ni kɔkɔa, amaa ni kan na ya kyɛ na la ni gba.

eng: God put everything under Christs feet and made him head of everything in the church.
local: Naawunni siri zaŋŋi bunseelikam n‑niŋi ŋɔn Yisa napɔna ni, ni u ti noora; ka yiisi u, ni u leebi zuguraana nti su yɛttɔ'a zaa fukumsi n‑ti baseeba ban la'am n‑dɔli Naawunni la.

eng: which is his body. His body, the church, is the fullness of Christ, who fills everything in every way.
local: Ba lala la'aŋŋu maa nyɛ la u ningbiŋŋu; ŋɔn Yisa paali la seelikam, ya'ari kam, ka u niriba la'aŋŋu maa puuni ka u bɛ ni, ka paali ka zaa.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa zugu ka man Pɔla gbaantiri n‑susira - man ŋɔn bɛ dansarika duu ni Kirisita Yisa zugu. Ka di nyɛ la yaseeba ban pa Yɛhudiya dima suŋŋsim maa zugu ka n susira.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: - N siri mi halli, ni, ya pun wum Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi tunseaa n‑zaali ma, ni, yarima zugu ka u dɔli la u wurilim soori ka kyɛ ka n suŋŋni ya.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: Ka laa m‑mi, ni, kana n nyɛ yɛlisɔ'ariseaa kan yi paalu n‑ti ma, dama m pun sɔbi lala beela n‑ti ya.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ya karim man ŋɔn daa sɔbi gbanseaa la, nti baŋŋi man ŋɔn mari bamsim seem zaŋŋi n‑kpa Kirisita yɛlisɔ'ara maa.

eng: Earlier generations didnt know this hidden plan that God has now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit.
local: Yɛla wa daa bu yi paalu n‑ti ninsaalidima saha din gaari la ni; ay! - di daa pa la hoo Naawunni Sii maa ŋɔn zaŋŋni a pampaŋŋɔ n‑wulisiri n‑tiri Naawunni tuumudima ni anabidima seem la.

eng: This plan is that the Gentiles would be coheirs and parts of the same body, and that they would share with the Jews in the promises of God in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Yɛla wa gbinni muna - di nyɛ la: ni, lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu nin kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiya dima maa ni Yɛhudiya dima maa zaa nin la'am n‑di faari maa. Ba nin la'am n‑labgi ningbiŋŋu yinni, ba nin la'am n‑nyari bunsuma, bunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn basi noori, ni, u nin tisi la zaa.

eng: I became a servant of the gospel because of the grace that God showed me through the exercise of his power.
local: Lala lahabasuŋŋu tuma ka man tumna: Naawunni wurilim piini zugu ka n deaai tuma wa ka leebi u tuntumna, pa m mammaŋŋa zugu; ŋɔn Naawunni n daa zaŋŋi u yiiko n‑kpeesi ma, ni n ti tumni lala.

eng: God gave his grace to me, the least of all Gods people, to preach the good news about the immeasurable riches of Christ to the Gentiles.
local: Man muna - halli ni Naawunni niriba zaa ŋɔn gaari ma zaa yɔri, u daa zaŋŋi piini wa n‑ti ma na, ni n ti moonni lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri ban pa Yɛhudiya dima la. Ka lala ka ba ti baŋŋi Kirisita nya'asim ŋɔn tibsa seem, halli ni sɔ ku toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni zaa yɔri.

eng: God sent me to reveal the secret plan that had been hidden since the beginning of time by God, who created everything.
local: Naawunni n daa naam duniya maa zaa, ka yɛla wa soori daa sɔ'ai u saani sahakam: ka yɛlisɔ'ara wa ka n yɛtti paalu ni, ni ya nini ti neaai.

eng: Gods purpose is now to show the rulers and powers in the heavens the many different varieties of his wisdom through the church.
local: Naawunni daa niŋi lala, ni u ti kyɛ ka pampaŋŋɔ u yɛm nin ti dɔli la u nyaandɔliba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni u la, m‑paai saazugu naama ni yiiko dima.

eng: This was consistent with the plan he had from the beginning of time that he accomplished through Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Di zaa nyɛ la hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun gbaai seem, ka ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita puuni ka u lala sufuyuubu yɛla yiiri paalu na.

eng: In Christ we have bold and confident access to God through faith in him.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa zugu, ni ti yɛdda ŋɔn bɛ u ni la zugu, ka ti nya soori ni ti nin toaai m‑paai Naawunni saani na ka bu zɔri dabeem, ka vi ka ti.

eng: So then, I ask you not to become discouraged by what Im suffering for you, which is your glory.
local: Anŋɔ zaa zugu ka n yɛtti ya, ni, yan di gbarigri beesigu ni, man ŋɔn namsiri ya zugu la zugu, dama lala nyɛ la duusigu n‑ti ya.

eng: This is why I kneel before the Father.
local: - Tɔ, ka di nyɛ la anŋɔ zaa zugu ka n gbaantiri n‑susiri ti Ba Naawunni.

eng: Every ethnic group in heaven or on earth is recognized by him.
local: Ti Ba n siri nyɛ u, ka di zugu ba boonni ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn su sɔkam zaa maa lala: arizana ni, ni tingbaŋŋu ni gba, ka ba boonni ba, ni, dɔ'am yinni babiitiri.

eng: I ask that he will strengthen you in your inner selves from the riches of his glory through the Spirit.
local: N susiri u ni, ni, u nin kyɛ ka u Sii Kasi maa ti kpamsi ya ya sufu ni, hoo u maŋtoori gyirima ŋɔn tibsa n‑veela seem.

eng: I ask that Christ will live in your hearts through faith. As a result of having strong roots in love.
local: Ka n laa n‑susiri, ni, Kirisita nin bɛ ya sufu ni la, ya yɛdda niŋbu zugu; ka lala nin kyɛ ka wurilim nin kyɛ ka ya zɛ yummu, hoo tiiya kan mari nya'asuma, bee duu kan mari nyeebkpeema.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Yarim muna - ya galinsi ni ya tumbeeri taalli daa ku ya.

eng: I ask that youll have the power to grasp loves width and length, height and depth, together with all believers.
local: Ni, ya nin mari yiiko, yan ni Naawunni niriba zaa, n nin nyaŋŋi m‑baŋŋi Yisa wurilim ŋɔn yɛlima seem, n‑wa'a seem, n‑du seem, n‑zulim seem zaa, halli ni di siri yakki n‑gaari ti bamsim zaa zugu gba. Lala bamsim nin kyɛ ka Naawunni beesigu zaa paali ya, ka ya mun beesigu nin wulisi Naawunni yɛla zaasa.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: Anŋɔ zaa puuni ka ya daa bɛ ni, yan daa dɔli duniya wa yɛla soori maa, n‑gyeemni Sibɛsɔ ŋɔn su saazugu ni pɛsigu fukumsi. Pampaŋŋɔ ka ŋɔna na tumni ban bu wumni Naawunni noori la sufu ni.

eng: Glory to God, who is able to do far beyond all that we could ask or imagine by his power at work within us.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna Naawunni mari yiiko din nin toaai n‑tumni n‑gaari tin susi seem, bee, tin teesi seem maa zaa, ka u lala yiiko m bɛ ti ni n‑tumna.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Halli tirim zaa gba daa bɛ la ban puuni sahaseaa, n‑tumni la ti tumbeeri beesigu ŋɔn daa boori seem, n‑dɔli ka boorimma ni ka teesa. Hoo banla, duniya wa ala'akyi dima n daa nyɛ ti gba. Sɛseelli daa bu sumni ti, na'ala Naawunni sugyeaa ni tubdarigri.

eng: glory to him in the church and in Christ Jesus for all generations, forever and always. Amen.
local: Lala gyirima ni duusigu ka u nyaandɔliba la'amni n‑tiri u, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya puuni, beoo kam, yuumni kam, halli ni saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi .

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Amaa Naawunni wurilim daa galisi ka u nambɔzɔrigu daa zoaai, ka u mari n‑ti ti.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Ka lala zugu u daa kyɛ ka ti la'am Masiiya saani n‑neaai kum ni, halli ni ti tumbeeri daa ku ti maa zaa yɔri. Piiniseaa tin bu sumni ka la zugu ka ya nya tuliginsim.

eng: And God raised us up and seated us in the heavens with Christ Jesus.
local: Ka ŋɔn Naawunni daa kyɛ ka tin ni Kirisita Yisa yisigi, n‑zaŋŋi ti n‑ziili ti, ni tirim ni ŋɔn zaa zi ŋɔn Naawunni saani u naam zizii maa ni saazugu.

eng: God did this to show future generations the greatness of his grace by the goodness that God has shown us in Christ Jesus.
local: U niŋi lala ni di ti kyɛ ka yuuma an kyɛnni na la dima nin ti baŋŋi u piiniseesi an ka taabu la yɛla, ni, ŋɔn zɔ ti namboo seem, Kirisita Yisa zugu.

eng: You are saved by Gods grace because of your faith. This salvation is Gods gift. Its not something you possessed.
local: Dama piiniseaa tin bu sumni ka la zugu ka ya nya tuliginsim, ya yɛdda niŋbu zugu. Ka di pa la ya mammantoori yiiko zugu: piini maa yi la Naawunni saani n‑ti ya na.

eng: Its not something you did that you can be proud of.
local: Pa ya tuma zugu; dinŋɔna sɔ di nyu bara dizugu.

eng: Instead, we are Gods accomplishment, created in Christ Jesus to do good things. God planned for these good things to be the way that we live our lives.
local: Dama Naawunni nuusi tuma n nyɛ ti, ka u maali ti la Kirisita Yisa saani. U maali ti, ni ti tumni la tunsuma - tunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn pun daŋŋi n‑zaali ni ti ti tumni maa.

eng: So remember that once you were Gentiles by physical descent, who were called uncircumcised by Jews who are physically circumcised.
local: Ya teaama, ni, sahanseaa sa ya daa pa la Naawunni niriba, ka ninvugseeba ban boonni ba mansi, ni, gunibu dima daa tuuri ya, ni, yɔkɔla dima. Ya ningbiŋŋu yɛla n‑daa la, hoo ba gunibu la gba nyɛ la ninsaala nuusi ŋɔn guni ba seem la.

eng: At that time you were without Christ. You were aliens rather than citizens of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of Gods promise. In this world you had no hope and no God.
local: Ka laa n‑teaai, ni, lala saha maa ya daa ka Masiiya, ya daa pa Izraɛli butiri niriba, ka pa seeba ban daa su faari diibu yɛla. Faari wa ka Naawunni daa basi noori, ni, u ti ti bana. Ay! - ya daa bɛ la duniya wa ni hoo seeba ban ka tama, ka laa n‑kɔŋŋi Naawunni.

eng: But now, thanks to Christ Jesus, you who once were so far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
local: Ya siri daa wa'a ni Naawunni, amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya bɛ la Kirisita Yisa puuni, pampaŋŋɔ ya yi pɔtti Naawunni saani na, ŋɔn Masiiya ziim maa zugu.

eng: Christ is our peace. He made both Jews and Gentiles into one group. With his body, he broke down the barrier of hatred that divided us.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Izraɛli dima ni ban pa Izraɛli niriba yɛla muna - ŋɔn Yisa mammaŋŋa nyɛ la ti sufumaasim ni ti alaafɛa, ŋɔna la'asi babayi maa taaba n‑niŋi nangbanyinni. U daa sa'am din daa waligi ba ni taaba hoo goomni m‑bɛ ba sunsukkuni la, ka zaŋŋi u maŋtoori ningbiŋŋu m‑maai dimni la.

eng: He canceled the detailed rules of the Law so that he could create one new person out of the two groups, making peace.
local: U daa zaŋŋi u ningbina n‑tum lala ni u ti baasi zaalikurigu maa yɔri, dama ka daa dɔli la zaalikpana ni kaya sɔya. U daa laa n‑tum lala ni u ti la'am zuliya buyi wa u saani na, m‑maali n‑naam ninvugyinni, bumpaalli, ka lala daa zi sufudoaa ni alaafɛ na.

eng: He reconciled them both as one body to God by the cross, which ended the hostility to God.
local: Ka lala ningbiŋŋu yinni wa ni ka u daa kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli maa zugu, ka daa zɔ babayi la sindaani Naawunni saani, dama u daa zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa n‑kpiisi dimni maa.

eng: When he came, he announced the good news of peace to you who were far away from God and to those who were near.
local: U siri daa kyɛ na m‑mooli lahabasuŋŋu, ni, sufudoaa bɛ ni n‑tiri yaseeba ban daa wa'a la, ni ban daa bu wa'a la gba.

eng: We both have access to the Father through Christ by the one Spirit.
local: Ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔseaa ti bayi zaa ŋɔn la'amni n‑dɔla m‑paari ti Ba Naawunni saani la, ka ŋɔn Naawunni Siiya nyɛ la Sii yinni ka suŋŋni ti.

eng: So now you are no longer strangers and aliens. Rather, you are fellow citizens with Gods people, and you belong to Gods household.
local: Dizugu ya laa m‑pa tinzundima, bee, tinseaa saamma: ay! - ya yi la'am n‑nyɛ la Naawunni niriba, halli u yiri dima n nyɛ ya gba.

eng: As Gods household, you are built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
local: U yiri wa mun mɛ n‑tɔm nyeebsuŋŋu zugu, di nyɛ la u tuumudima ni anabidima maa; ka ka sunsukkugri kan gbaari ka zaa la nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa mammaŋŋa.

eng: The whole building is joined together in him, and it grows up into a temple that is dedicated to the Lord.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa zugu ka yiri maa zaa gbaai taaba n‑du n‑leebi gyeeŋŋu yiri kasikasi, n‑tiri ti Dugma.

eng: Christ is building you into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
local: Ka ŋɔn saani yaasa ka yarima gba la'amni n‑leebri la ya'aseaa Naawunni ŋɔn nin kpɛ m‑bɛ ni, u Sii Kasi maa zugu.

eng: Therefore, imitate God like dearly loved children.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ya beebu ma'asiri la Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ seem la, dama u wuri ya hoo u biisi, ka biwuttigu kam ma'asiri la u ba.

eng: Live your life with love, following the example of Christ, who loved us and gave himself for us. He was a sacrificial offering that smelled sweet to God.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya beesigu zaa kur nyɛ la wurilim, hoo Kirisita ŋɔn daa wuri ti ka zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑maali n‑tisi Naawunni, nti kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni daa wum u sara wa nya'asim.

eng: Sexual immorality, and any kind of impurity or greed, shouldnt even be mentioned among you, which is right for holy persons.
local: Amaa yan di kyɛ ka zina bɛ ya ni, bee da'ari tuma bee zinyuu - halli yan di kyɛ ka ba tɔ'asiri m‑boonni anŋɔ taabu yuya ya saani, dama anŋɔ bu sumni Naawunni niriba.

eng: Obscene language, silly talk, or vulgar jokes arent acceptable for believers. Instead, there should be thanksgiving.
local: Ya yɛttɔ'a muna - yan di kyɛ ka gyɛrilim bɛ a ni, bee yɛttɔ'abarima, bee deenyɔya - anŋɔ zaa gba bu sumsi ya, amaa pɛ'abu ni puusigu yɛla ka ya yɛtta.

eng: Because you know for sure that persons who are sexually immoral, impure, or greedy—which happens when things become gods—those persons wont inherit the kingdom of Christ and God.
local: Ya baŋŋma dinŋɔa: zinyuu daana n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn gyeemni la buga, ka ŋɔna ni zina nira ni da'ari tuntumna - ba sɔkam ku la'am n‑di faari n‑kpɛ Naawunni naam ni, din nyɛ Kirisita dinni la.

eng: Nobody should deceive you with stupid ideas. Gods anger comes down on those who are disobedient because of this kind of thing.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna yan di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ zaŋŋi u yɛttɔ'ayɔya n‑yɔmsi ya; dama lala zugu ka Naawunni sufuyisigri paari ban bu wumni u noori la.

eng: So you shouldnt have anything to do with them.
local: Dinzugu yan di la'amni lala niriba saani.

eng: You were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord, so live your life as children of light.
local: Dama ya daa bɛ la zibsim ni saha din gaari la, amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya yi bɛ la ti Dugma neesim ni. Ya kyɛ ka ya beesigu nyɛ la neesim dima dinni.

eng: Light produces fruit that consists of every sort of goodness, justice, and truth.
local: Neesim din bɛ ya ni zugu, ya tumni la din veela, ka din mari la yɛlimaŋni ka tu la.

eng: Therefore, test everything to see whats pleasing to the Lord.
local: Ka ya booma m‑baŋŋi din pɛligri ti Dugma sufu.

eng: and dont participate in the unfruitful actions of darkness. Instead, you should reveal the truth about them.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ya la'am banŋɔ saani n‑tumni din taasiri zibsim yɛliyɔya na la, amaa ya yiisim tuma wa paalu ni n‑wulisi a beeri ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: Its embarrassing to even talk about what certain persons do in secret.
local: Dama tunseesi niriba wa ŋɔn sɔ'ari n‑tumni la muna - halli sɔ yi tɔ'asi a yɛla, di nyɛ la vi.

eng: But everything exposed to the light is revealed by the light.
local: Amaa seelli zaa neesim ŋɔn neaai n‑niŋi maa bɛ la paalu ni.

eng: Everything that is revealed by the light is light. Therefore, it says, Wake up, sleeper! Get up from the dead, and Christ will shine on you. .
local: dama neesim n kyɛ ka ti nyari seelikam. Dizugu ka ba yɛtti, ni#:- Nyin gɔgbisira wa, doaama! Neaam kum ni! - Ka Kirisita nin ti neaai na n‑ti i.

eng: So be careful to live your life wisely, not foolishly.
local: Anŋɔ zaa zugu, ya baŋŋma yan bɛ seem suŋŋasuŋŋa; di tumni gyɛrilim, amaa ka ya tumni yɛm tuma.

eng: Take advantage of every opportunity because these are evil times.
local: Zuna muna - dabsibeeri saha nla, ka dizugu yan zaŋŋma ya saha zaa n‑niŋni suŋŋa.

eng: Because of this, dont be ignorant, but understand the Lords will.
local: Dizugu yan di leebi gyɛra, amaa ya baŋŋma ti Dugma ŋɔn boori seem maa ka tumni lala.

eng: Dont get drunk on wine, which produces depravity. Instead, be filled with the Spirit in the following ways.
local: Yan di nyuuri daam m‑bugra, dama galagala yɛla nŋɔa, amaa ya kyɛ ka Naawunni Siiya paali ya.

eng: speak to each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; sing and make music to the Lord in your hearts.
local: Ya yuumni n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba - Naawunni Litaafi la yuuma, ni pɛ'abu yuuma, ni yuunseesi Naawunni Siiya ŋɔn wulisi ya maa ka ya yuumna; ya sufu ni ka ya yuumni lala n‑tiri ti Dugma.

eng: always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Naawunni nyɛ la ti Ba; ya puusim u taari bunseelikam zugu, ni sahakam, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yuuri ni.

eng: and submit to each other out of respect for Christ.
local: Ya tim Kirisita gyirima, ka wumni n‑tiri taaba#:-.

eng: For example, wives should submit to their husbands as if to the Lord.
local: Pɔ'aba, ya wumni n‑tiri ya siridima kpaaŋŋu la, hoo yan wum n‑ti ti Dugma seem.

eng: A husband is the head of his wife like Christ is head of the church, that is, the savior of the body.
local: Kirisita bɛ la u niriba saani hoo zugu ŋɔn bɛ ningbiŋŋu saani seem la, ka u nyɛ la ningbiŋŋu maa tuligra: lala ka doowa n nyɛ hoo zugu n‑ti u pɔ'a.

eng: So wives submit to their husbands in everything like the church submits to Christ.
local: Hoo u niriba ŋɔn wumni n‑ti Kirisita seem, lala ka di sumsi ni pɔ'aba gba wumni n‑ti ba siridima seelikam puuni.

eng: As for husbands, love your wives just like Christ loved the church and gave himself for her.
local: Ya dɔppa muna - ya wuri ya pɔ'aba hoo Kirisita ŋɔn daa wuri u niriba seem, n‑zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi ba zugu.

eng: He did this to make her holy by washing her in a bath of water with the word.
local: U daa niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka ba leebi Naawunni niriba kasikasi, ka su ba koom m‑peaai ba, yɛligu maa zugu.

eng: He did this to present himself with a splendid church, one without any sort of stain or wrinkle on her clothes, but rather one that is holy and blameless.
local: Yaasa, u daa niŋi lala ni u ti zaŋŋi ba n‑ti u maŋŋa: ba nyɛ la hoo u maŋtoori pɔ'a, pɔ'aveelli ŋɔn ka lasim, bee da'ari, bee lala yɛlibeoo zaa, nti bɛ u dinni ka ka galiŋŋu.

eng: Thats how husbands ought to love their wives—in the same way as they do their own bodies. Anyone who loves his wife loves himself.
local: Lala gba ka di sum ni dɔppa wuri ba toori pɔ'aba, hoo ba mammaŋŋa ningbina. Ŋɔnkam wuri u pɔ'a wuri la u maŋŋa.

eng: No one ever hates his own body, but feeds it and takes care of it just like Christ does for the church.
local: Ya bu nya? - sɔ n ka ni n‑yaai u mammaŋŋa ningbiŋŋu: ay! - ba yi lee m‑mari ka la suŋŋa, hoo Kirisita ŋɔn mari u niriba suŋŋa seem la.

eng: because we are parts of his body.
local: Dama tirima, u niriba maa, nyɛ la u ningbiŋŋu tuga, ni ka ya'a.

eng: This is why a man will leave his father and mother and be united with his wife, and the two of them will be one body .
local: Ni, Dinŋɔ zugu ka doo nin kyɛ u ba, ni u ma, ka di pɔ'a ka ba la'am m‑bɛ ni kaman bunni yinni.

eng: Marriage is a significant allegory, and Im applying it to Christ and the church.
local: Dinŋɔ baŋŋni tɔ ni; man mun yɛtti, ni, di wulisiri la Kirisita ni u niriba yɛla, ni, ba nyɛ la bunni yinni.

eng: In any case, as for you individually, each one of you should love his wife as himself, and wives should respect their husbands.
local: Amaa yarim gba, see ka ya sɔkam zaa wuri u pɔ'a hoo ŋɔn wuri u mammaŋŋa seem la, ka u pɔ'a maa mun nin ti u sira gyirima.

eng: As for children, obey your parents in the Lord, because it is right.
local: Biisi, ya wumni n‑tiri ya ba ni ya ma dima, ti Dugma zugu; dama dinŋɔ n nyɛ din tu.

eng: The commandment Honor your father and mother is the first one with a promise attached.
local: Ni, Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima. - ya yi dɔlisi Litaafi zaalikpana n‑ti paai kanla, kana n di toonni ka mari nangbambasiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi laara yɛla.

eng: so that things will go well for you, and you will live for a long time in the land .
local: Dama di laa n‑yɛtti, ni, Lala ka i nin kpalim i nyɔvuri ni n‑yuui tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

eng: As for parents, dont provoke your children to anger, but raise them with discipline and instruction about the Lord.
local: Yarim biisi badima muna - yan di galimni ka tuuri ya biisi ka ba ti gyɛ suuri, amaa ya mari ba la suŋŋa n‑wulisi ba n‑kpaamni ba ti Dugma zugu.

eng: As for slaves, obey your human masters with fear and trembling and with sincere devotion to Christ.
local: Daaba, ya wumni n‑tiri ya duniya wa dugmadima maa, ni gyirima ni ninzoaa. Yan zaŋŋma ya sufu zaa n‑wumni n‑tiri ba, hoo yan nin wum n‑ti Kirisita seem la.

eng: Dont work to make yourself look good and try to flatter people, but act like slaves of Christ carrying out Gods will from the heart.
local: Ya gyeemni ba, pa la ban yi gɔsiri ya kɔkɔa, hoo ninvugseeba ban boori ni ba nya duusigu ninsaalidima saani, amaa yɛlimaŋni ni, hoo daaba ban nyɛ Kirisita dinni; ya tumni la Naawunni tuma ya sufu ni.

eng: Serve your owners enthusiastically, as though you were serving the Lord and not human beings.
local: Ya tumni nimmoosi, hoo yan tumni n‑tiri ti Dugma, pa la saala.

eng: You know that the Lord will reward every person who does what is right, whether that person is a slave or a free person.
local: Dama ya mi, ni, ti Dugma nin yɔ sɔkam u tunsuma zugu, di daama yi nyɛ la daabri, bee, u pa daabri gba.

eng: As for masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Stop threatening them, because you know that both you and your slaves have a master in heaven. He doesnt distinguish between people on the basis of status.
local: Yaseeba ban mari daaba la muna - hoo man yɛnni, ni, ba tumma n‑tiri ya seem la, lala ka ya gba tumni ni n‑tiri ba; ya kyɛli tulisigu ni mugsigu. Ya baŋŋma, ni, ya gba nyɛ la Kirisita daaba; yan ni ban zaa mari ya dugma yinni: u bɛ la arizana ni, ka nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn bu gaŋŋni nira ni nira.

eng: Finally, be strengthened by the Lord and his powerful strength.
local: Di zaa nyaaŋŋa, ya kpamsim ya mansi ti Dugma saani, ka kyɛ ka u yiiko titaari bɛ ya saani.

eng: Put on Gods armor so that you can make a stand against the tricks of the devil.
local: Ya yeema Naawunni gba'anu ka deaai u tɔppu neema zaa. Anŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ya nyaŋŋi Sintaani yɔmsigu putɔyam zaa.

eng: We arent fighting against human enemies but against rulers, authorities, forces of cosmic darkness, and spiritual powers of evil in the heavens.
local: Dama ti bu moori ni ninsaalidima, amaa saazugu yiiko dima ka ti zabra, ni saazugu naam dima, ni seeba ban su duniya wa fukumsi ka bɛ la zibsim ni la - halli siseeba ban bɛ saazugu m‑mari yɛlibeeri yiiko la zaasa.

eng: Therefore, pick up the full armor of God so that you can stand your ground on the evil day and after you have done everything possible to still stand.
local: Dinzugu ya deaam Naawunni tɔppu neema zaa, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ya tuusi dabsibeoo daari tɔppu la n‑zɛ deede; ka di zaa yi ti naai, ya nin nyɛ la ban nin kpalim n‑zɛ ya gbaya zugu.

eng: So stand with the belt of truth around your waist, justice as your breastplate.
local: Dizugu ya zɛ deede. Ya looma ya seaa suŋŋa - yɛlimaŋni maa nyɛ la ya lala sɛlooriga; ka zaŋŋi gba'anu n‑ta'ai ya nyaari - ya beesigu ŋɔn tuusi seem la nla.

eng: and put shoes on your feet so that you are ready to spread the good news of peace.
local: Ya pirima mugrikpeema - di nyɛ la: ya maalim siri ni ya mooli sufudoaa lahabasuŋŋu maa.

eng: Above all, carry the shield of faith so that you can extinguish the flaming arrows of the evil one.
local: M‑paasi, Sintaani nin tɔ ya bugum piima, see ka ya mari bunni n‑ta'ai a n‑kpiisi a - ya yɛdda niŋbu mun nla.

eng: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is Gods word.
local: Ka laa m‑pili soogyadima kutti zupiliga - di nyɛ la: tuliginsim din ta'ari ya; ka zaŋŋi takoobi n‑kyeera - Naawunni yɛligu maa ŋmanni la u Sii Kasi suu wa n‑ti ya.

eng: Offer prayers and petitions in the Spirit all the time. Stay alert by hanging in there and praying for all believers.
local: Ya mari wunsusigu sahakam, n‑susiri m‑bɛlimni Naawunni yan boori seelikam, Sii Kasi maa soori ni. Halli ya galima gɔgala gba, ni ya ti susira; yan bɛlimma u, ka teaai u u maŋtoori niriba zaa yɛla.

eng: As for me, pray that when I open my mouth, Ill get a message that confidently makes this secret plan of the gospel known.
local: Ya laa n‑susiri n‑ti ma gba, n‑yɛli Naawunni ka u ti ma yɛttɔ'a, man ŋɔn tɔ'asi saha kam gba, ni n ti zaŋŋi n sufukpeemni n‑wulisi lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛlisɔ'ara ka a ti yi paalu.

eng: Im an ambassador in chains for the sake of the gospel. Pray so that the Lord will give me the confidence to say what I have to say.
local: Lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu ka n nyɛ ti Dugma tuumu n‑zɛ u zaani n‑tiri niriba, ka ba lɔ ma la kyɔrimi di zugu. Amaa ya susima n‑ti ma, ni, n tɔ'asi paalu ni ka dabeem ka ma, hoo din sumsi ni n tɔ'asi seem.

eng: Tychicus, my loved brother and faithful servant of the Lord, can inform you about my situation and what Im doing.
local: Tikyikusi nyɛ la m mabisuŋŋu, ka laa n‑nyɛ yɛdda daansɔ ŋɔn suŋŋni ma ti Dugma zugu, ka n wuri u pam. U yi ti paai ya na, u nin yɛli ya din bɛ ni maa zaa, ka ya gba baŋŋi n lahabaari - man ŋɔn nya seem la, ni man tumni seem maa zaa.

eng: Ive sent him for this reason—so that you will know about us. He can reassure you.
local: Lala zugu ka n tumni u ya saani na, ni u ti kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ti yɛla, ka kpamsi ya sufu.

eng: May there be peace with the brothers and sisters as well as love with the faith that comes from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: M puusiri ti mabiisi zaa: Naawunni paasim ba alaafɛa, ni wurilim ni yɛdda - anŋɔ zaa ka ti Ba Naawunni tisira, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya.

eng: May grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ forever.
local: Naawunni paasim u piinisuma n‑ti ya zaa ban wuri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ka ya wurilim bu kpiira.

eng: Therefore, as a prisoner for the Lord, I encourage you to live as people worthy of the call you received from God.
local: Man bɛ sarika ni ti Dugma yɛla zugu, ka n bɛlimni ya ni, ni, ya mari beesiseaa din sumsi ya, dama ŋɔn Naawunni booli ya u saani na.

eng: Conduct yourselves with all humility, gentleness, and patience. Accept each other with love.
local: Ya bɛ baalimm n‑sigsi ya mansi, m‑mari sugru ni taaba, ka kyɛri taaba taalli wurilim zugu.

eng: and make an effort to preserve the unity of the Spirit with the peace that ties you together.
local: Ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi m‑mari nangbanyinni, hoo Naawunni Sii maa ŋɔn yi zaŋŋi u bansi m‑ba ya ni taaba.

eng: You are one body and one spirit, just as God also called you in one hope.
local: Ti zaa nyɛ la ningbiŋŋu yinni niriba, sii yinni dima, hoo ban daa booli ya na la ni ya ti mari tama yinni.

eng: There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism.
local: Ya Dugma yinni ka ya gyeemna, yɛdda yinni ka ya niŋna, Naawunni kɔsuubu yinni ka ba daa su ya.

eng: and one God and Father of all, who is over all, through all, and in all.
local: Naawunni yinni mun n nyɛ ti zaa Ba, ka u gaari sɔkam, ka tumni sɔkam ni, ka bɛ sɔkam zaa ni.

eng: God has given his grace to each one of us measured out by the gift that is given by Christ.
local: Amaa ti sɔkam deaari u piini u piini, hoo Kirisita ŋɔn putti a n‑tisiri seem la.

eng: Thats why scripture says, When he climbed up to the heights, he captured prisoners, and he gave gifts to people .
local: Lala zugu ka di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Ŋɔna n daa du saazugu; Di saha ka u daa gbaasi niriba pam, Ka daa zaŋŋi piini n‑tisi niriba.

eng: What does the phrase he climbed up mean if it doesnt mean that he had first gone down into the lower regions, the earth?.
local: Tɔ. Daŋŋni, ni, U daa du saazugu: di gbinni nyɛ la bɔa? - di pa la, ni, u daa daŋŋi n‑sigi la duniya wa tiŋni na gba?.

eng: The one who went down is the same one who climbed up above all the heavens so that he might fill everything.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa sigi na maa, di daama n daa nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa laa n‑du saazugu n‑gaari halli nti kpɛ arizana puuni la, ni u ti paali ya'ari kam zaa.

eng: He gave some apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers.
local: Din pa, ni, u daa zaŋŋi piini n‑tisi: di mun gbinni nyɛ la bɔa? - ni, ŋɔna n daa ti seeba tuumudima tuma, ka laa n‑ti seeba anabi tuma, seeba mun moonni la lahabasuŋŋu maa, seeba yaasa n niŋi kpamma ni karimbadima.

eng: His purpose was to equip Gods people for the work of serving and building up the body of Christ.
local: Ba deaari la piini wa, ni ba ti sɛ'asi Naawunni niriba ni ba tumna, ka Kirisita ningbiŋŋu maa nin ti mɛa.

eng: until we all reach the unity of faith and knowledge of Gods Son. Gods goal is for us to become mature adults—to be fully grown, measured by the standard of the fullness of Christ.
local: Halli nti paai sahanseaa ti zaa ŋɔn ti ya niŋi nangbanyinni ti yɛdda niŋbu maa ni, ka naayi baŋŋi Naawunni Bii maa yɛla. Lala ka ti nin kyɛ biilim n‑la'am n‑leebi ninvugkurigu m‑bɛ hoo Naawunni ŋɔn boori zaa, ka naayi ŋmanni la Kirisita ŋɔn bɛ seem la zaa.

eng: As a result, we arent supposed to be infants any longer who can be tossed and blown around by every wind that comes from teaching with deceitful scheming and the tricks people play to deliberately mislead others.
local: Ka ti ku laa n‑nyɛ bileesi, ti ku laa n‑dɔli wulisigu balibubalibu hoo pɛsigu ŋɔn zɛ ti yɔriyɔri seem; ti ku laa n‑kyɛ ka yɔsimyɔmsiriba nin ma'ai ti ziri n‑yɔmsiri ti.

eng: Instead, by speaking the truth with love, lets grow in every way into Christ.
local: Ay! - Ti nin lee n‑yɛtti yɛlimaŋni, wurilim zugu. Ti nin zoaai na m‑paai ŋɔn Kirisita ŋɔn sa'ai seem, dama u nyɛ la ti zaa zuguraana.

eng: who is the head. The whole body grows from him, as it is joined and held together by all the supporting ligaments. The body makes itself grow in that it builds itself up with love as each one does its part.
local: Ŋɔn siri nyɛ zugu maa, ka tirim mun nyɛ la u ningbiŋŋu tuga. Ŋɔn zugu ka ti zaa la'am n‑nyɛ yimmu, dama u siri nyɛ la ti zugu. Ŋɔn saani ka ti zoaari m‑meeri taaba ka nyari yaa ni kpeeŋŋu di zugu. Ningbiŋŋu wa ya'ari ni tugri kam zaa mari yiiko n‑tumni ka tuma ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Ka di zaa nyɛ la lala wurilim zugu.

eng: So Im telling you this, and I insist on it in the Lord: you shouldnt live your life like the Gentiles anymore. They base their lives on pointless thinking.
local: N yɛtti ya la nŋɔa#:- n kukɔri ka n yɛtti ni, Naawunni zugu, ni, yan di kyɛ ka ya beesigu na bɛ la hoo ban bu mi Naawunni la ŋɔn bɛ seem, ba sufu ni teesiyɔya ziilinsi zugu.

eng: and they are in the dark in their reasoning. They are disconnected from Gods life because of their ignorance and their closed hearts.
local: Dama ban bu mi din m bɛ ni; ba bɛ la yɛla ni, ka na zi Naawunni nyɔvuri ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ba ziilinsi ni ba tuppuwumni zugu.

eng: They are people who lack all sense of right and wrong, and who have turned themselves over to doing whatever feels good and to practicing every sort of corruption along with greed.
local: Ba ba'a laa n‑ka ba soori ni, ka yi tuusi, bee, ka bu tuusi? Ba zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑ti yɛlibeeri balibu kam n‑kpalim m‑boori m‑paasi, dama ba sufu boorimma nla.

eng: But you didnt learn that sort of thing from Christ.
local: Amaa pa lala ka yarima n daa bamsi Kirisita soori maa!.

eng: Since you really listened to him and you were taught how the truth is in Jesus.
local: Ay! - Yan daa wum u yɛla ka daa deaai u wulisigu maa, yɛlimaŋni din bɛ Yisa saani la zugu n‑daa la.

eng: change the former way of life that was part of the person you once were, corrupted by deceitful desires.
local: Ba daa wulisi ya, ni, yan daa nyɛ ninvugseeba sahanseaa la, lala niriba daa bɛ la baasiyɔri soori ni, kɔribeeri ni yɔmsa zugu; ni, ya zaŋŋma lala ninvugkura beesigu maa m‑basi.

eng: Instead, renew the thinking in your mind by the Spirit.
local: Ni, ka kyɛ ka ya teesa leebi teesipaala.

eng: and clothe yourself with the new person created according to Gods image in justice and true holiness.
local: Ni, ka lala ya nin maali n‑naam n‑leebi ninvugpaala Naawunni zugu. Halli ya nin leebi ninvugveela, yɛlimaŋni dima kasikasi.

eng: Therefore, after you have gotten rid of lying, Each of you must tell the truth to your neighbor because we are parts of each other in the same body.
local: Dinzugu see ka ya kyɛli ziri; ni, sɔkam yɛtti yɛlimaŋni n‑tiri u taraana, dama ti zaa nyɛ la ningbiŋŋu yinni tuga.

eng: Be angry without sinning. Dont let the sun set on your anger.
local: Ni, Halli ni ya sufu yi yisigi, yan di tum putoo.; yan di kyɛ ka ya sugyeaa yuui halli ka wuntaŋŋa ti lu.

eng: Dont provide an opportunity for the devil.
local: Yan di tiili dariga n‑ti Sintaani.

eng: Thieves should no longer steal. Instead, they should go to work, using their hands to do good so that they will have something to share with whoever is in need.
local: Ŋɔn yi zuuri zaa kyɛli zuubu ka zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑tumni tunsuma: di nin kyɛ ka u mari bunseesi ka u nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi n‑suŋŋi ban kɔŋŋni la.

eng: Dont let any foul words come out of your mouth. Only say what is helpful when it is needed for building up the community so that it benefits those who hear what you say.
local: Yan di tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'abeeri; ya tɔ'asim yɛttɔ'asuma an nin suŋŋi yan tɔ'asiri n‑tiri seeba maa, ni seelli din nin niŋi ba anfaani.

eng: Dont make the Holy Spirit of God unhappy—you were sealed by him for the day of redemption.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka Naawunni Sii Kasi maa sufu sa'am ya beesiyɔri zugu, dama ŋɔn daa daalim ya n‑wulisi, ni, ya nyɛ la Naawunni dinni, ni, ya maali la siri nti fa-m‑basi dabsiri daari.

eng: Put aside all bitterness, losing your temper, anger, shouting, and slander, along with every other evil.
local: Ya yim putoo ni, ni sufu yisigri ni sufugyeaa zaa ni, ni yɛttɔ'abeeri ni nyaanzum ni; halli ya kyɛli yɛlibeeri zaa yɛla.

eng: Be kind, compassionate, and forgiving to each other, in the same way God forgave you in Christ.
local: Amaa ka ya wuri taaba, ka mari nirilim ni taaba, n‑zɔri taaba namboo, ka kyɛri taaba taalli hoo Naawunni ŋɔn kyɛ ya taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ya seem la, Kirisita zugu.

eng: From Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus. To all those in Philippi who are Gods people in Christ Jesus, along with your supervisors and servants.
local: Gbaŋŋu wa yi la man, Pɔla, saani na - man ni Timɔti gba: tirim mun nyɛ Yisa Kirisita Masiiya daaba, ka ti sɔbri n‑tiri ya Naawunni niriba, yaseeba ban bɛ Filipaai tiŋŋa ka bɛ ni ni Yisa Kirisita taaba, yan ni ya gyeeŋŋu kpamma ni ban suŋŋni ba zaa.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni tim ya sufudoaa ni alaafɛa, ŋɔn ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita.

eng: I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: N pɛ'ari la m Ba Naawunni saha kam zaa, n yi ti teaari ya yɛla sahanseaa maa.

eng: Im thankful for all of you every time I pray, and its always a prayer full of joy.
local: Man yi puusi u saha kam, n susiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya zaa yɛla; ka n lala susigu bɛ la ni sufumaasim.

eng: Im glad because of the way you have been my partners in the ministry of the gospel from the time you first believed it until now.
local: yan daa la'am n‑suŋŋi ma zugu - ya suŋŋni ma zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu tuma ziiya, piiligu ni halli ni zuna.

eng: Im sure about this: the one who started a good work in you will stay with you to complete the job by the day of Christ Jesus.
local: Dama n niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn pun piili tunsunseesi ya saani la, u nin kyɛ ka a na kyɛnni tooni lala halli ni a naari, Kirisita Masiiya Dabsiri daari saha.

eng: I have good reason to think this way about all of you because I keep you in my heart. You are all my partners in Gods grace, both during my time in prison and in the defense and support of the gospel.
local: Ya bɛ la n sufu ni saha kam, ka di zugu di tuya, ni, n niŋi lala yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya yɛla. Ya la'amni n‑suŋŋni ma n‑nyari Naawunni piinisuma. Di nyɛ la lala man ŋɔn lɔ la kyɔrimi seem ziiya, ni man ŋɔn gyɛrigi sarikatta maa tooni ka wulisiri lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn zɛ seem ziiya m‑paasi.

eng: God is my witness that I feel affection for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
local: Halli Naawunni mammaŋŋa nin sakki, ni, n sufu siri boori ya yɛla hoo Yisa Kirisita sufu ŋɔn boori ya yɛla nimmoosi seem la.

eng: This is my prayer: that your love might become even more and more rich with knowledge and all kinds of insight.
local: Ka man susiri Naawunni seem maa nŋɔa: ni, u kur kyɛ ka ya na maali n‑wuri sɔkam pam, ka di dɔli la ya yɛm ni ya sufu ni bamsim an zoaai seem maa.

eng: I pray this so that you will be able to decide what really matters and so you will be sincere and blameless on the day of Christ.
local: Lala nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai m‑baŋŋi din tu, halli nti paai Kirisita Dabsiri daari la; ka lala saha ya nin ti zaani, galinsɛseelli ka ni.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Yuumpii ni anaasi nyaaŋŋa, man ni Baanabasi daa laa n‑labi Gyɛrusalɛm, ka n daa zaŋŋi Taituusi m‑paasi ka ti daa kyaŋŋi.

eng: I pray that you will then be filled with the fruit of righteousness, which comes from Jesus Christ, in order to give glory and praise to God.
local: Di saha ka yɛlimaŋni tuma zaa nin paali ya, ka a taabu yi la Yisa Kirisita kɔkɔ saani na: di nin kyɛ ka pɛ'abu ni tibsim ti bɛ ni n‑tiri Naawunni.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Din daa kyɛ ka n kyaŋŋi daa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn daa kasigi n‑ti ma, ni, n kyɛma. N daa zaŋŋi n lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla n‑wulisi ba, Naawunni lahabasunseaa man daa moonni n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la. N daa wulisi lala lahabaari m mammaŋŋa, n‑ti baseeba ban daa nyɛ ninvugtibsa la'aŋŋu dima puuni la kɔkɔa, dama n daa bu boori man daa pun tum tunseesi la, bee, man daa na tumni tunseesi maa, daa yi leebi tunyɔya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the things that have happened to me have actually advanced the gospel.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna, m mabiisi, m boori ya baŋŋi, ni, yɛttɔ'aseesi an paai ma na zaa - ay! a naari nyɛ la: ni, lahabasuŋŋu yɛla kur kyɛnni tooni.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Amaa n dɔlinkpeaa Taituusi, halli ni ŋɔn daa nyɛ Griika doo maa zaa yɔri, u daa bu nya mugsi gunibu zugu.

eng: The whole Praetorian Guard and everyone else knows that Im in prison for Christ.
local: Dama dinŋɔ n kyɛ ka sɔkam baŋŋi, ni, di nyɛ la Yisa Kirisita zugu ka ba lɔ ma kyɔrimi lala: halli Naaba Kambɔnsi maa ni ban kpalim zaa ti baŋŋi lala.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Ay! - di daa nyɛ la yɔmsigu dima maa zugu, baseeba ban daa ma'ai ziri, ni, ba nyɛ la ban niŋi yɛdda: niriba wa daa kpɛ ti saani na, ni ba ti sɔ'ai m‑baŋŋi ti yɔlisimma yɛla maa - tin mari yɔlisinseaa Yisa Masiiya dɔligu zugu la - dama ba daa boori la ba zaŋŋi ti n‑leebi daaba.

eng: Most of the brothers and sisters have had more confidence through the Lord to speak the word boldly and bravely because of my jail time.
local: Ka ban daa lɔ ma maa zugu n kyɛ ka ti mabiisi pam maali m‑mari sufukpeemni nti moonni Yɛligu maa paalu ni, dabeem ka ni: baseeba ban niŋi Yisa yɛdda pam niŋni lala.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Amaa ti daa bu kyɛ nini n‑ti ba, halli beela, ni ti ti kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛlimaŋni lahabasuŋŋu maa kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani n‑ti ya.

eng: Some certainly preach Christ with jealous and competitive motives, but others preach with good motives.
local: Tɔ, seeba m bɛ ni n‑niŋni lala karimbaani ni nyamaŋŋa zugu, ni ba yuuri ti du n‑gaari ba taaba; ka seeba mun moonni Kirisita yɛla ba sufu ni yɛlimaŋni ni yɛdda zugu.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Amaa ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ ni kaman zuguraandima la muna - man mun ba'a ka ba zuguraantiri yɛla ni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban daa nyɛ seem; dama Naawunni bu gamsiri n‑tibgri niriba - zuguraandima wa daa bu zaŋŋi yɛlipaaliseaa m‑paasi ma.

eng: They are motivated by love, because they know that Im put here to give a defense of the gospel.
local: Ii, lala niriba siri moonni ka la wurilim zugu, dama ba baŋŋi, ni, di nyɛ la lahabasuŋŋu zugu ka n zɛya wa, ni n ti wulisi di yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem la.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Amaa ba daa baŋŋiya, ni, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi la lahabasuŋŋu mooligu n‑ti ma, ni n deaai n‑zaŋŋi m‑moonni n‑tiri ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa, kaman ŋɔn daa pun zaŋŋi mooligu wa n‑tisi Piita, ni u zaŋŋi m‑mooli n‑ti Yɛhudiyadima seem la.

eng: the others preach Christ because of their selfish ambition. They are insincere, hoping to cause me more pain while Im in prison.
local: Banla muna moonni la Kirisita yɛla nyuuri zappu zugu; ba sufu ni teesa bu dɔli soori, ka ba yɛtti, ni, man ŋɔn kpalim n‑lɔ zugu ba nin kyɛ ka n sufu sa'am.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u yiiko puuni ka Piita daa niŋi tuumu n‑ti ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima maa, ka Ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka man gba daa leebi tuumu lala n‑tisi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa.

eng: What do I think about this? Just this: since Christ is proclaimed in every possible way, whether from dishonest or true motives, Im glad and Ill continue to be glad.
local: Amaa m ba'a ka ba yɛla maa ni: n nya ka Kirisita yɛla yi paalu, ni niriba ti wum, ka n sufu maai di zugu. Di yi nyɛ ba sufu ni yɔmsigu zugu ka ba moonna, bee, ba sufu yɛlimaŋni zugu gba, di ka taalli. Ii, n sufu nin maai.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Ba daa baŋŋiya, ni, Naawunni zaŋŋi la lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ma, u wurilim zugu - di nyɛ la Gyeemsi, ni Siifasi Piita, ni Gyɔni, ninvugseeba niriba ŋɔn gɔsiri hoo ti la'aŋŋu duu zaantɔlisi. Ka lala maa zugu ka ba daa gbaai n nuu - man ni Baanabasi zaa - n‑wulisi, ni, tin ni ban nyɛ la bunyinni: ka daa sakki, ni, mani ni Baanabasi yɛ n‑tumni n‑tiri la ban pa Yɛhudiyadima, ka ban mun tumni n‑tisiri ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima maa.
