local: Yan di kyɛ ka sɔ yɔmsi ya seelli ni, dama dabsiri maa ku toaai m‑paai na, di yi pa la niriba pam daŋŋi n‑la'am taaba n‑za'asiri Naawunni noori pɔi, ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Zaaliguza'asira ni Baasiyɔri Daana maa naayi yi paalu.

eng: He is the opponent of every so-called god or object of worship and promotes himself over them. So he sits in Gods temple, displaying himself to show that he is God.
local: Ŋɔnŋɔ nin ti za'asi bunseelikam din zɛ Naawunni zugu, ni Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, ka duusi u maŋŋa n‑gaari seelikam. U nin ti kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔna n‑nyɛ Naawunni.

eng: You remember that I used to tell you these things while I was with you, dont you?.
local: Ya bu teaai man ŋɔn daa bɛ ya saani ka yɛli ya seem la?.

eng: Now you know what holds him back so that he can be revealed when his time comes.
local: Ka pampaŋŋɔ ya pun mi din goaari u, ni, di kyɛ ka u ku ti yi paalu see sahasuŋŋu maa deede.

eng: The hidden plan to live without any law is at work now, but it will be secret only until the one who is holding it back is out of the way.
local: Yɛlisɔ'ara an nyɛ zaaligu za'asigu yɛla pun piili a tuma, amaa ŋɔn goaari a maa kur nin bɛ ni, halli nti paai sahaseaa ban nin yiisi u soori ni.

eng: Then the person who is lawless will be revealed. The Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath from his mouth. When the Lord comes, his appearance will put an end to him.
local: Di saha ka Zaaliguza'asira maa nin yi paalu. Di saha ka ti Dugma Yisa nin ti zaŋŋi u vuusim pɛsiŋŋu din yiiri u noori ni na n‑ku u, ka u lappu na gyirima nin ti sa'am u yɛla zaa.

eng: When the person who is lawless comes, it will happen through Satans effort, with all kinds of fake power, signs, and wonders.
local: Ŋɔn Zaaliguza'asira maa kyɛnni na nin ti dɔlisi la ni Sintaani yiiko soori, nti mari kpeeŋŋu ni alahazibu tuma balibu kam, ni sisa, ka a zaa nyɛ la ziri.

eng: It will happen with every sort of wicked deception of those who are heading toward destruction because they have refused to love the truth that would allow them to be saved.
local: Ka u nin ti zaŋŋi ala'akyi tuma n‑yɔmsiri ninvugseeba ban baasiri yɔri. Ba nin baasi yɔri dama ba za'asiya, ni, ba ku wuri yɛlimaŋni maa ka nya tuliginsim.

eng: This is why God will send them an influence that will mislead them so that they will believe the lie.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Naawunni tum la kyiriŋŋu din mari yiiko nti ba, ka ba niŋi ziri maa yɛdda.

eng: The result will be that everyone will be judged who is not convinced by the truth but is happy with injustice.
local: Ka di naari ti ya nyɛ la: ba kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba - ninvugseeba ban ku sakki, ni, ba niŋi yɛlimaŋni maa yɛdda, ka ba sufu boorim nyɛ la ba tum ala'akyi.

eng: But we always must thank God for you, brothers and sisters who are loved by God. This is because he chose you from the beginning to be the first crop of the harvest. This brought salvation, through your dedication to God by the Spirit and through your belief in the truth.
local: Amaa tirima muna - di kpa la talasi ni ti pɛ'ai Naawunni ya zugu, yaseeba ban nyɛ ti mabiisi. Ka ti Dugma wuri ya, dama piiligu ni ka u daa pun yiisi ya, ni ya ti nya tuliginsim Naawunni Sii Kasi tuma puuni, ni yan niŋi yɛlimaŋni maa yɛdda zugu.

eng: God called all of you through our good news so you could possess the honor of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: U daa kyɛ ka ti wulisi ya u lahabasuŋŋu, ka lala ka u daa booli ya ni ya ti nya lala tuliginsim; ni yan ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita nin ti la'am n‑nya gyirima.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold on to the traditions we taught you, whether we taught you in person or through our letter.
local: Dizugu, m mabiisi, ya zɛ yummu ka mari wulisigu seelli tin daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisi ya la, di yi daa nyɛ yɛttɔ'a maa ni, bee, ti daa sɔbi nti ya.

eng: Our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and a good hope.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya mammaŋŋa kpamsima ya sufu, ŋɔn ni Naawunni ti Ba zaa, dama Naawunni wuri ti, halli nti tisi ti sufudoaa din ka naari, ni tama suŋŋu wa. Ka ti Dugma Yisa ni ti Ba Naawunni nin laa n‑kpaŋŋi ya, ya tunsuma ni ya yɛttɔ'asuma kam zaa ni.

eng: First of all, then, I ask that requests, prayers, petitions, and thanksgiving be made for all people.
local: Din daŋŋi tooni ka n kpamsiri sɔkam nyɛ la nŋɔa#:- Yan susim Naawunni n‑tiri ninsaalidima zaa, ka puusiri u ba zugu, ka zɔri ba sindaani u saani.

eng: Pray for kings and everyone who is in authority so that we can live a quiet and peaceful life in complete godliness and dignity.
local: Ka ya laa m‑puusiri u lala n‑tiri nadima ni bankam su fukumsi la. Dina nin kyɛ ka ti bɛ baalimm ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka ti, ka ti kpalim n‑dɔli la Naawunni soori n‑gyeemni u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: This is right and it pleases God our savior.
local: Dinŋɔ n veela ka Naawunni nini n tigri di. Ka ŋɔn Naawunni n nyɛ ti tuligra.

eng: who wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
local: ka boori ni sɔkam zaa ti tuligi ka baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seelli.

eng: There is one God and one mediator between God and humanity, the human Christ Jesus.
local: Dama Naawunni yinni m bɛ ni, ka sindaantumna yinni kɔkɔ mun bɛ Naawunni ni ninsaalidima sunsukkuni; ŋɔna n nyɛ Yisa Kirisita, ka u gba nyɛ la ninsaala.

eng: who gave himself as a payment to set all people free. This was a testimony that was given at the right time.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑pa talima sɔkam zaa zugu, ni u yɔlisi ba. Dinŋɔa n daa niŋi sahasuŋŋu maa deede nti wulisi, ni, di zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: I was appointed to be a preacher and apostle of this testimony—Im telling the truth and Im not lying! Im a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
local: Ka lala zugu ka Naawunni daa gaŋŋi ma ka n leebi u tuumu sɔ ŋɔn tum ni m moonni di lahabaari n‑wulisiri n‑tiri ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa. U daa niŋi lala ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni soori maa. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i maa; m bu ma'ari la ziri!.

eng: Therefore, I want men to pray everywhere by lifting up hands that are holy, without anger or argument.
local: M boori la dɔppa ban bɛ lugri kam zaa n nyɛ Naawunni niriba maa ti wukki ba nuusi saazugu m‑puusiri Naawunni, ka sufugyeaa bee nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu ka di puuni.

eng: In the same way, I want women to enhance their appearance with clothing that is modest and sensible, not with elaborate hairstyles, gold, pearls, or expensive clothes.
local: N laa m‑boori la pɔ'aba gba yeeri suturiveela an sumni ba, seesi an ka galinsi, bee, vi - pa la zugri paaŋŋu, bee salima ni anzulifa nangɔkɔrisi yeebu, bee, kyinkyina an mari ligri pam soobu.

eng: They should make themselves attractive by doing good, which is appropriate for women who claim to honor God.
local: Ay! See ka ba zaŋŋi tunsuma m‑maanni ba mansi: lala ka di sum ni pɔ'aba ban gyeemni Naawunni bɛ ni.

eng: A wife should learn quietly with complete submission.
local: Ya kyɛ ka pɔ'aba maa sini baalimm n‑sigsi ba mansi ka bamsira.

eng: I dont allow a wife to teach or to control her husband. Instead, she should be a quiet listener.
local: M bu ti pɔ'aba soori ni ba leebi karimbadima, bee, ba su dɔppa fukumsi. Di kpa la talasi ni ba sini la baalimm.

eng: Adam was formed first, and then Eve.
local: Dama Adam ka Naawunni daa daŋŋi n‑naam, ka naayi naam Hawa.

eng: Adam wasnt deceived, but rather his wife became the one who stepped over the line because she was completely deceived.
local: Ka di pa la Adam ka Sintaani daa yɔmsi, amaa Hawa ka u daa yɔmsi ka u leebi ŋɔn burigi Naawunni zaaligu.

eng: But a wife will be brought safely through childbirth, if they both continue in faith, love, and holiness, together with self-control.
local: Amaa pɔ'aba yi kur kpalim m‑mari yɛdda, ni wurilim, ka bɛ kasi, ka mari ba mansi suŋŋa, ba nin nya tuliginsim, biisi dɔ'am puuni.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us so that the Lords message will spread quickly and be honored, just like it happened with you.
local: Ti mabiisi, din kpalim nyɛ la nŋɔa: ya susim Naawunni n‑ti ti, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti Dugma yɛligu moonni n‑kyɛnni tooni yooma, ka ba deaai di ni gyirima, hoo yarima ŋɔn daa deaai seem la.

eng: Pray too that we will be rescued from inappropriate and evil people since everyone that we meet wont respond with faith.
local: Ka laa n‑susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ti, ni, u yiisi ti putoo dima ni ninvugbeeri nuusi ni, dama pa sɔkam zaa n‑niŋi ti lahabaari maa yɛdda.

eng: But the Lord is faithful and will give you strength and protect you from the evil one.
local: Amaa ti Dugma nyɛ la ŋɔn zɛ yummu, ka u nin ti ya yaa, ka ta'ai ya Yɛlibeoo Daana maa nuusi ni.

eng: We are confident about you in the Lord—that you are doing and will keep doing what we tell you to do.
local: Halli ti siri niŋi ti Dugma maa yɛdda zaa, ni, ya tumni hoo tin daa wulisi ya seem la, ka kur nin niŋni lala sahakam.

eng: May the Lord lead your hearts to express Gods love and Christs endurance.
local: Ti Dugma kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi u wurilim ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka ya diri sugru hoo Kirisita Masiiya ŋɔn diri di seem la zaa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we command you in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to stay away from every brother or sister who lives an undisciplined life that is not in line with the traditions that you received from us.
local: Ti mabiisi, ti kpaamni ya ni, ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya zugu, ni, ya gbaam ya maŋŋa ka kyɛ ya mabiseeba ban nyɛ gbinnya'ari dima, baseeba ban bu dɔli wulisigu seelli tin daa wulisi ba la.

eng: You yourselves know how you need to imitate us because we were not undisciplined when we were with you.
local: Ya mammaŋŋa mi, ni, di sum ni ya niŋi hoo tin daa niŋi seem la. Ti daa pa gbinnya'a dima, tin daa bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la.

eng: We didnt eat anyones food without paying for it. Instead, we worked night and day with effort and hard work so that we would not impose on you.
local: Ti daa bu di diseelli sɔ saani ka bu yɔ di samni. Amaa ti daa yi tumni ti tuma halli ka di wum ti; ti daa tumni la sahakam zaa, ni ti di ziili ya zitibsiri.

eng: We did this to give you an example to imitate, not because we didnt have a right to insist on financial support.
local: Pa la ti daa ka la soori ni ti deaai suŋŋsim ya saani zugu ka ti daa tumni lala maa, amaa ti daa niŋi lala ni ya nya, ka dɔli ti soori maa.

eng: Even when we were with you we were giving you this command: If anyone doesnt want to work, they shouldnt eat.
local: Tin daa bɛ ya saani, ti daa yi yɛtti ya ni, ni, Ŋɔn za'asi, ni, u ku tum, ba di kyɛ ka u di.

eng: We hear that some of you are living an undisciplined life. They arent working, but they are meddling in other peoples business.
local: Ti wumiya, ni, ninvugseeba ban bɛ ya saani n‑nyɛ gbinnya'a dima: ba bu boori ni ba tum seelli, ka na'a gyinni n‑kpeeri la niriba yɛla ni.

eng: By the Lord Jesus Christ, we command and encourage such people to work quietly and put their own food on the table.
local: Lala taabu niriba ka ti kpaamni ka susiri ba ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya zugu, ni, ba zinnma baalimm ka tum ba tuma n‑gɔsi ba maŋŋa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont get discouraged in doing what is right.
local: Ti mabiisi, yan di gyɛ ya tunsuma tumpu ni.

eng: Take note of anyone who doesnt obey what we have said in this letter. Dont associate with them so they will be ashamed of themselves.
local: Ka sɔ yi bu deaai ti wulisigu seelli tin sɔbi wa, yan kpasim u ka di laa m‑puusiri u; lala nin kyɛ ka vi gbaai u.

eng: Dont treat them like enemies, but warn them like you would do for a brother or sister.
local: Yan di zaŋŋi u ka u leebi ya dindaana, amaa ya kpaam u hoo ya mabiiya.

eng: May the Lord of peace himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
local: Ti Dugma nyɛ la sufudoaa daana: ŋɔn mammaŋŋa tim ya sufudoaa sahakam zaa, ni yɛlikam puuni. Ti Dugma nin bɛ ya zaa saani.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand. This verifies that the letter is from me, as in every letter of mine. This is how I write.
local: Tɔ, nyama sɔppu wa - man sɔbri m‑puusiri ya n toori nuu ni sɔppu: Pɔla . Lala ka n daalim n gbaŋŋu kam. N nuu sɔppu n‑siri la.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya wurilim nin bɛ ya zaa saani.

eng: This saying is reliable: if anyone has a goal to be a supervisor in the church, they want a good thing.
local: Yɛttɔkku wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Ninvugsɔ sufu yi boori nimmoaa, ni, u leebi Naawunni niriba toondaana, ŋɔn boori seelli maa nyɛ la tunsuŋŋu.

eng: So the churchs supervisor must be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, sober, modest, and honest. They should show hospitality and be skilled at teaching.
local: Ka di kpa la talasi ni Naawunni niriba toondaana bɛ la beesisuŋŋu ka ka galiŋŋu. Di sumni ni u mari la u pɔ'a yinni, ka laa m‑mari u maŋŋa suŋŋa, m‑mari mangbaari, ka niriba tiri u gyirima. Di sum ni u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn deaari saamma ka niŋni ba saanni, ka nyɛ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑wulisi niriba suŋŋa m‑paasi.

eng: They shouldnt be addicted to alcohol or be a bully. Instead, they should be gentle, peaceable, and not greedy.
local: Di bu sumni ni u nyɛ ŋɔn nyuuri daam m‑bugra, bee ŋɔn nyɛ zabboora; amaa di sumni ni u nyɛ la ŋɔn bɛ baalimm ka pa ŋɔn ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni, bee, ŋɔn sufu wuri ligri nti yakki.

eng: They should manage their own household well—they should see that their children are obedient with complete respect.
local: Di kpa la talasi ni u nyɛ la ŋɔn mari u maŋtoori yiri suŋŋa, ka u biisi deaari u noori ka tiri u gyirima bunseelli kam zaa ni.

eng: because if they dont know how to manage their own household, how can they take care of Gods church?.
local: Dama nira yi bu nyaŋŋi m‑mari u toori yiri maa, u ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai m‑mari Naawunni niriba maa n‑gɔsiri ba yɛla?.

eng: They shouldnt be new believers so that they wont become proud and fall under the devils spell.
local: Di mun bu sum ni di daama nyɛ la ŋɔn na yooli n‑niŋi tuuba; di yi pa lala u nin nyu bara n‑duusi u maŋŋa ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ba kari u sariya n‑lusi u, hoo Sintaani ŋɔn daa lu seem la.

eng: They should also have a good reputation with those outside the church so that they wont be embarrassed and fall into the devils trap.
local: Ka din paasi dinŋɔ zaa zugu nyɛ la, di kpa la talasi ni u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn yuuri du ban bɛ kpaŋŋa saani: di yi pa lala, u nin ti tum vi tuma, ka Sintaani kyɛ'ariga gbaai u.

eng: In the same way, servants in the church should be dignified, not two-faced, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
local: Di bɛ la lala gba ban suŋŋni ya toondaandima saani. Di kpa la talasi ka ba mari la beesisuŋŋu ka niriba tiri ba gyirima. Di laa n‑sum ni ba zɛ la yummu ka tɔ'asiri yɛlimaŋni. Ba di nyuuri daam m‑bugra, bee, ba sufu wuri ligri nti yakki.

eng: They should hold on to the faith that has been revealed with a clear conscience.
local: Di laa n‑kpa la talasi ni ba sufu ni teesa bɛ deede, ka ba deaai yɛttɔ'azulima an bɛ ti wulisigu puuni la m‑mari suŋŋa.

eng: They should also be tested and then serve if they are without fault.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ba ma'asi lala niriba pɔi, ka ba beesigu yi wulisi, ni, ba ka galinsi, yan naayi kyɛ ka ba tum toondaandima suŋŋniba tuma maa.

eng: In the same way, women who are servants in the church should be dignified and not gossip. They should be sober and faithful in everything they do.
local: Ka di laa m‑bɛ la lala pɔ'aba saani gba; di kpa la talasi ka ba mari beesisuŋŋu ka niriba tiri ba gyirima. Di bu sum ni ba nyɛ seeba ban zuuri niriba nyaaŋŋa, amaa ban gbaari ba mansi suŋŋa ka zɛ yummu bunseelikam zaa ni.

eng: Servants must be faithful to their spouse and manage their children and their own households well.
local: Di sum ni ŋɔn suŋŋni ya toondaandima maa mari la pɔ'a yinni, ka nyaŋŋi m‑mari u biisi ni u yiri dima suŋŋa.

eng: Those who have served well gain a good standing and considerable confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Ninvugseeba ban tumni tuma wa suŋŋa nin nya yusuma, ka nyaŋŋi m‑mari sufukpeemni n‑tɔ'asira, ban niŋi Yisa Masiiya yɛdda seem la zugu.

eng: I hope to come to you quickly. But Im writing these things to you so that.
local: M mari tama, ni, di bu ya n‑yuui ka n ti kyɛ i saani na nti kaai i. Amaa n sɔbri anŋɔ zaa n‑tiri i.

eng: if Im delayed, youll know how you should behave in Gods household. It is the church of the living God and the backbone and support of the truth.
local: ni lala kyɛ ka, halli n kyɛnni na maa yi ti yuui gba n yɛla wa nin wulisi i Naawunni niriba ŋɔn sumni ni ba bɛ seem, n‑gɔsiri Naawunni yiri maa yɛla n‑tiri u. Ŋɔn Naawunni n nyɛ nyɔvuri daana, ka u lala yiri nyɛ la u toori niriba, ka laa n‑nyɛ zaantɔliga kan mari yɛlimaŋni maa, ka laa n‑nyɛ nyeebri ka ka tɔmni ka zugu n‑zɛ yummu.

eng: Without question, the mystery of godliness is great: he was revealed as a human, declared righteous by the Spirit, seen by angels, preached throughout the nations, believed in around the world, and taken up in glory.
local: Sɔ ku toaai n‑kyiisi, ni, ti Naawunni soori yɛlisɔ'ara wa bu galisi. Dina n nyɛ, ni#:- U daa leebi ninsaala n‑yi paalu, Naawunni Sii Kasi mun daa zɛ u nyaaŋŋa: Malaakadima gba daa nya u. Ba daa mooli u yɛla n‑tiri bura kam zaa, Ka niriba daa niŋi u yɛdda duniya wa ni. Naawunni daa ti duusi u saazugu N‑taasi gyirima zaa ziiya arizana ni.

eng: We dont need to write to you about the timing and dates, brothers and sisters.
local: Ti mabiisi, di bu laa n‑kpa talasi ni ti laa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dabsa ni saha seelli din ti ya n‑niŋi.

eng: You know very well that the day of the Lord is going to come like a thief in the night.
local: Dama ya mammansi mi halli, ni, ti Dugma lappu na saha dabsiri maa nin ti kyɛ na hoo ta'ayiga ŋɔn yi kyɛ na yuŋŋu seem la.

eng: When they are saying, There is peace and security, at that time sudden destruction will attack them, like labor pains start with a pregnant woman, and they definitely wont escape.
local: Ni, sahaseaa niriba yi ti yɛtti, ni, sufudoaa ni goom bɛ ni, di saha ka basiyɔri ya m‑paai ba na, ka ba ku toaai n‑fa ba mansi m‑basi. Di nin ti piili katiŋŋa, hoo pɔ'a ŋɔn yi wulisiri, ni, u dɔ'ai ka beerim seelli mari u la.

eng: But you arent in darkness, brothers and sisters, so the day wont catch you by surprise like a thief.
local: Amaa, ti mabiisi, ya pa la ninvugseeba ban bɛ zibsim ni ka dabsiri maa nin ti libgi ya hoo ta'ayiga ŋɔn libgri niriba seem la.

eng: All of you are children of light and children of the day. We dont belong to night or darkness.
local: Ya nyɛ la neesim ni wuntaŋŋa ni niriba. Ti pa la yuŋŋu bee zibsim ni niriba.

eng: So then, lets not sleep like the others, but lets stay awake and stay sober.
local: Dizugu di bu sum ni ti gbisira, hoo ninvugseeba ŋɔn niŋni seem la, amaa ya kyɛ ka ti neaai goom ni ka mari ti mansi suŋŋa.

eng: People who sleep sleep at night, and people who get drunk get drunk at night.
local: Dama yuŋŋu ka gɔgbisiriba gbisira, ka danyuuriba mun nyuuri m‑bugri la yuŋŋu gba.

eng: Since we belong to the day, lets stay sober, wearing faithfulness and love as a piece of armor that protects our body and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
local: Amaa tirima n nyɛ wuntaŋŋa ni niriba: di zugu ya kyɛ ka ti mari ti maŋŋa. Ya kyɛ ka ti zaŋŋi yɛdda ni wurilim n‑leebi ti nyɔta'arigu m‑pa ti nyaari zugu, ka zaŋŋi ti tuliginsim tama n‑leebi tɔbtuusira kutti zupiliga la m‑pili.

eng: God didnt intend for us to suffer his wrath but rather to possess salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Naawunni daa bu maali ti ni u sufuyisigri tubdarigri ti paai ti na, amaa u zaali ti ni ti nya tuliginsim ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya zugu.

eng: Jesus died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with him.
local: Ŋɔna n daa kpi ti zugu, ni di kyɛ ka ti zaa ti la'am m‑bɛ u saani, di yi nyɛ la ti bɛ la ti nyɔvuri ni, bee, ti kpi ni.

eng: So continue encouraging each other and building each other up, just like you are doing already.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ya kpamsima taaba sufu ka niŋni din nin suŋŋi ya taaba ka ba zoaai Naawunni dɔligu ni: halli ya siri pun niŋni lala pampaŋŋɔ gba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we ask you to respect those who are working with you, leading you, and instructing you.
local: Ti mabiisi, ti susiri ya, ni, ya tim ya toondaandima maa gyirima - ban nyɛ ban tumni Naawunni tuma n‑tiri ya, ka kpaamni ya suŋŋa.

eng: Think of them highly with love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
local: Ya gyeemni ba ka tiri ba gyirima, ka laa n‑wuri ba pam ban tumni seelli la zugu; ka mari nangbanyinni ni taaba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are disorderly. Comfort the discouraged. Help the weak. Be patient with everyone.
local: Ti mabiisi, ya kpaamni gbinnya'a dima. Ka ban sufu miligi la muna - ya maari ba sufu n‑tiri ba: halli ya suŋŋma ban gbarigi la, ka mari sugru ni sɔkam zaa.

eng: Make sure no one repays a wrong with a wrong, but always pursue the good for each other and everyone else.
local: Sɔ di boosiri u kpeaa beeri, amaa ya niŋma nirilim n‑tiri taaba - halli n‑tiri sɔkam zaa.

eng: Rejoice always.
local: Ya mari sufupeelli sahakam;.

eng: Pray continually.
local: ya susima Naawunni sahakam;.

eng: Give thanks in every situation because this is Gods will for you in Christ Jesus.
local: ka pɛ'ari Naawunni seelikam zaa puuni. Dama lala ka Naawunni boori ni ya niŋni ya beebu ni, Yisa Masiiya zugu.

eng: Dont suppress the Spirit.
local: Yan di kpiisi Naawunni Sii Kasi yɛla hoo bugum ŋɔn kpiisiri seem la;.

eng: Dont brush off Spirit-inspired messages.
local: yan di zeem anabitiri yɛttɔ'a yɛla.

eng: but examine everything carefully and hang on to what is good.
local: Amaa ya bugsima bunseelikam m‑baŋŋi di yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem; ka mari din veela suŋŋasuŋŋa;.

eng: Avoid every kind of evil.
local: ka naayi yi bunseelli kam din nyɛ bumbeoo ni.

eng: Now, may the God of peace himself cause you to be completely dedicated to him; and may your spirit, soul, and body be kept intact and blameless at our Lord Jesus Christs coming.
local: Naawunni n tiri sufudoaa: kyɛ ka u maali ya ka ya bɛ kasi zaa, ka mari ya siisi ni ya sufu ni ya ningbina ka a ku ti mari taalli ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita lappu na saha.

eng: The one who is calling you is faithful and will do this.
local: Ŋɔn booli ya maa nyɛ la ŋɔn nin niŋi di, dama yɛdda daana n nyɛ u, ka zɛ yummu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
local: Ti mabiisi, ya susima Naawunni n‑tiri ti gba.

eng: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
local: Ya puusima ti mabiisi la zaa mabiitiri puusigu suŋŋa n‑ti ti.

eng: By the Lords authority, I order all of you to have this letter read aloud to all the brothers and sisters.
local: Ya wumma yaa! Ti yɛtti ya ti Dugma zugu, ni, ya kyɛ ka ba karim gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti ti mabiisi la zaa.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya piinisuma kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani.

eng: Dont correct an older man, but encourage him like hes your father; treat younger men like your brothers.
local: Di tasi ninvugkurigu n‑nyikki u; amaa kpamsim u kaman i ba. Ka laa n‑deaai na'akyimbisi la hoo i tunzɔppa.

eng: treat older women like your mother, and treat younger women like your sisters with appropriate respect.
local: ka kyɛ ka pɔ'akura la leebi i madima, ka pɔ'asara mun leebi i tunzɔpɔ'aba, halli ka i dɔli sɔsuŋŋu n‑tiri ba.

eng: Take care of widows who are truly needy.
local: Gɔsim pukɔya ban siri boori suŋŋsim la suŋŋa.

eng: But if a particular widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to respect their own family and repay their parents, because this pleases God.
local: Amaa pukɔsɔ yi mari biisi bee ya'ansi, nyin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, see ka ba daŋŋi n‑gɔsi ba toori yiri dima suŋŋa. Ba yi niŋi lala ba nin labsi n‑yɔ sanseaa ba badima ni ba yaadima maa ŋɔn daa tum tunseelli zaa n‑ti ba la. Lala n kyɛ ka Naawunni nini tigra.

eng: A widow who is truly needy and all alone puts her hope in God and keeps on going with requests and prayers, night and day.
local: Amaa pukɔsɔ ŋɔn bɛ u kɔkɔ ka ka suŋŋna nyɛ la ŋɔn siri boori suŋŋsim. Lala pukoori maa zaŋŋi la u tama zaa n‑niŋi Naawunni nuusi ni, ka kur puusiri Naawunni n‑susiri u, yuŋŋu ni wuntaŋŋa zaa.

eng: But a widow who tries to live a life of luxury is dead even while she is alive.
local: Pukɔsɔ mun ŋɔn zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑ti u ningbinkɔrisi tuma la, ŋɔna pun kpi ni halli ni ŋɔn na nɛ maa zaa yɔri.

eng: Teach these things so that the families will be without fault.
local: Dizugu zaalima yɛla wa gba n‑tiri niriba, ka ba yi ti dɔli a, sɔsɔ ku galim ba.

eng: But if someone doesnt provide for their own family, and especially for a member of their household, they have denied the faith. They are worse than those who have no faith.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi bu gɔsiri u dɔ'ariba, halli m‑bu gɔsiri u toori yiri dima gba, u kyiisiri la u yɛdda niŋbu soori, halli ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pa Yisa nyaandɔla n nyɛ bunsuŋŋu n‑gaari u.

eng: Put a widow on the list who is older than years old and who was faithful to her husband.
local: Di zaŋŋi pukɔsɔ n‑sɔbi u yuuri m‑paasi pukɔya ni, di yi pa la u pun paai u yuuma pisiyoobu, ka kuli doo yinni kɔkɔa.

eng: She should have a reputation for doing good: raising children, providing hospitality to strangers, washing the feet of the saints, helping those in distress, and dedicating herself to every kind of good thing.
local: See ka sɔkam mi u tunsuma yɛla - tunsuma wa nyɛ la: u wulisi u biisi suŋŋa, n‑deaari saamma, n‑tumni n‑tiri Yisa nyaandɔliba, n‑suŋŋni ban bɛ wahala ni, ka zaŋŋi u maŋŋa zaa n‑tum la tunsuma balibu kam.

eng: But dont accept younger widows for the list. When their physical desires distract them from Christ, they will want to get married.
local: Di bu laa n‑sumni ni i zaŋŋi pukɔya ban na bu kurigi la yuya n‑sɔbi m‑paasi pukɔya maa ni. Dama ba kɔrisi yi ti bɛ dɔppa kulibu ni, ba nin ŋmaligi ka kyɛ Kirisita yɛla;.

eng: Then they will be judged for setting aside their earlier commitment.
local: ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka Naawunni kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba, dama ba za'asiri ban daa pun basi nangbambasiseela n‑ti Naawunni la.

eng: Also, they learn to be lazy by going from house to house. They are not only lazy, but they also become gossips and busybodies, talking about things they shouldnt.
local: Din paasi dinŋɔ zugu nyɛ la#:- ba bamsiri la gbinnya'ari sɔya, ka sa'amni la ba saha n‑gyinni n‑kpeeri yiya, ka laa n‑zuuri niriba nyaansi, ka kpeeri ba yɛla ni yɔri, ka tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an bu sumni ni ba tɔ'asi.

eng: So I want younger widows to marry, have children, and manage their homes so that they wont give the enemy any reason to slander us. .
local: Dinzugu n kpamsiri pukɔya ban na bu kurigi la, ni, ba kulima dɔppa, n‑dɔ'ai biisi, n‑gɔsiri ba toori yiya. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti dindaandima ku nya soori ni ba tuuri ti.

eng: Some have already turned away to follow Satan.
local: Halli seeba pun burigi n‑dɔli Sintaani gba.

eng: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should take care of them and not burden the church, so that it can help other widows who are truly needy.
local: Ka pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔla yi mari pukɔya u yiri puuni, ŋɔn suŋŋma ba, ka di kyɛ ka mugsigu bɛ Yisa niriba zugu; lala nin kyɛ ka ba nin toaai n‑suŋŋi pukɔseeba ban siri boori suŋŋsim la.

eng: Elders who lead well should be paid double, especially those who work with public speaking and teaching.
local: Kpamseeba ban tumni toondaana tuma suŋŋa sumni la ba nya yoori buyi, ka di siri bɛ la lala n‑tiri ban tumni suŋŋa n‑yiisiri wa'azu ka wulisiri Naawunni yɛligu.

eng: The scripture says, Dont put a muzzle on an ox while it treads grain , and Workers deserve their pay .
local: Dama Naawunni yɛligu yɛliya, ni, Di lɔ na'akoorigu noori, u yi tumni puu tuma sahanseaa. Ni, Tuntumna zaa sumni u deaai u tuma yoori.

eng: Dont accept an accusation made against an elder unless it is confirmed by two or three witnesses.
local: Sɔ yi galim toondaansɔ i saani, nyin di deaai yɛttɔ'a maa, na'ala niriba ayi bee babata n la'am n‑di di sɛhara.

eng: Discipline those who are sinning in front of everyone so that all the others will be afraid.
local: Kpaasima ban tumni ala'akyi la zugri paalu ni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka dabeem gbaai ban kpalim la.

eng: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to follow these practices without bias, and without playing favorites.
local: N tɔ'asiri wa ni Naawunni ti wumna, ni Yisa Masiiya, ni malaaka seeba ŋɔn gamsi maa m‑paasi. N ya zaali i, ni, i dɔli wulisigu wa ka di daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni, ninvugsɔ galimiya pɔi, ka naayi wum u yɛttɔ'a; bee, n‑gaŋŋi sɔ n‑wuri, ka kyɛ sɔa.

eng: Dont rush to commission anyone to leadership, and dont participate in the sins of others. Keep yourself morally pure.
local: Di boori yooma ni i zaŋŋi i nuusi m‑pa sɔ zugu ni u tum Naawunni tuma. I mun di kyɛ ka i nuu bɛ i taaba tumbeeri tumpu ni. Kur mari i maŋŋa m‑bɛ kasi.

eng: Dont drink water anymore, but use a little wine because of your stomach problems and your frequent illnesses.
local: I puu ŋɔn beera, ka i ningbina bu tigi alaafɛ sahaseesi la zugu, di laa n‑nyuuri koom kɔkɔa, amaa nyuuma daam beela m‑paasira.

eng: The sins of some people are obvious, and the sins are judged before the people must face judgment, but the sins of other people show up later.
local: Ninvugseeba tumbeeri yi yi paalu n‑daŋŋi ba tooni m‑paai sariya karibu ziiya; amaa seeba mun dina dɔli la ba nyaaŋŋa.

eng: In the same way, the good that people do is also obvious and cant be hidden.
local: Lala ka tunsuma gba yiiri paalu ni, ka halli tunsunseaa yi bɛ ni kan bu yi paalu yooma, ka mun kur ku naayi n‑kpalim n‑sɔ'ai.

eng: The Spirit clearly says that in latter times some people will turn away from the faith. They will pay attention to spirits that deceive and to the teaching of demons.
local: Naawunni Sii maa daa pun wulisi ti n‑yɛli, ni, sahanseesi an ti paari nyaaŋŋa, ninvugseeba nin ti kyɛ ba yɛdda niŋbu soori. Ni, ba nin ti dɔli siseeba ban yɔmsiri niriba, ni bunseesi arizimbeeri ŋɔn wulisiri maa.

eng: They will be controlled by the pretense of lying, and their own consciences will be seared.
local: Lala wulisigu maa yiiri la ninvugyɔmsiriba saani na, baseeba ban nyɛ ziri dima, ka ba sufu ni teesa bu laa n‑teesiri bunsuma, hoo nira yi ti zaŋŋi kurituulli n‑vuuli u ningbina seem.

eng: They will prohibit marriage and eating foods that God created—and he intended them to be accepted with thanksgiving by those who are faithful and have come to know the truth.
local: Niriba wa taabu wulisigu yɛtti la, ni, di bu tuusi ka nira di pɔ'a, bee, pɔ'a kuli doowa. Ni, bundiriseesi kyisiri n‑tiri niriba, ka ba di diri a. Amaa Naawunni mun daa naam bundira maa zaa n‑tisi niriba, ni ba ti deaai m‑puusi u a zugu, dama ba laa n‑niŋi u yɛdda ka baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni maa.

eng: Everything that has been created by God is good, and nothing that is received with thanksgiving should be rejected.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn naam bunseelli kam zaa veela. Ka di bu sum ni ti za'asiri a ni; amaa ti puusima Naawunni ka deaari a ni;.

eng: These things are made holy by Gods word and prayer.
local: dama Naawunni yɛligu ni wunsusigu nin kyɛ ka a bɛ kasi Naawunni saani.

eng: If you point these things out to the believers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus who has been trained by the words of faith and the good teaching that youve carefully followed.
local: I yi zaŋŋi yɛla wa zaa n‑wulisiri ti mabiisi maa, i nin leebi Kirisita Yisa tuntunsuŋŋu. Lala ŋmanni la hoo nyin diisiri i mammaŋŋa n‑kpamsira; nyin ŋɔn daa pun wum yɛlisuma zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛdda soori, ka laa n‑wum yɛlimaŋni wulisigu ka dɔli ka.

eng: But stay away from the godless myths that are passed down from the older women. Train yourself for a holy life!.
local: Amaa zɔma n‑yi ka kyɛ lahabayɔya ni sɔlinseesi an bu sum ni Naawunni nyaandɔliba. Kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa m‑bamsi Naawunni soori dɔligu.

eng: While physical training has some value, training in holy living is useful for everything. It has promise for this life now and the life to come.
local: Dama sɔ yi ti kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑kpamsi u ningbiŋŋu, di mari la anfaani beela n‑tiri u; amaa u yi ti kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa Naawunni soori dɔligu ziiya, lala mari la anfaani zaa n‑tiri u, dama dinŋɔ zugu n kyɛ ka u mi, ni, u nin nya bunsuma pampaŋŋɔa, ka laa n‑nya bunsuma sahanseaa din ti ya kyɛ na m‑paasi.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves complete acceptance.
local: Yɛttɔkku wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ka sum ni nira deaai ka zaa, ka niŋi ka yɛdda.

eng: We work and struggle for this: Our hope is set on the living God, who is the savior of all people, especially those who believe.
local: Dinŋɔa zugu ka ti diri wahala ka tumni ni ti yaa tɔriga, dama ti zaŋŋi ti tama zaa n‑niŋi Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana wa ni. Ŋɔn Naawunni n nyɛ sɔkam zaa tuligra, halli ni u tuligri ninvugseeba ban niŋi u yɛdda la zaa, halli nti yakki.

eng: Command these things. Teach them.
local: Zaŋŋma anŋɔa zaa n‑wulisi ba, ka zaali ba, ni, ba dɔli a.

eng: Dont let anyone look down on you because you are young. Instead, set an example for the believers through your speech, behavior, love, faith, and by being sexually pure.
local: Di kyɛ ka sɔ zeemni i, nyin ŋɔn bu kurigi zaa zugu. Amaa kyɛ ka i yɛttɔ'a wulisi yɛddaniŋniba maa din sum ni ba bɛ seem - i yɛttɔ'a, ni i beebu, ni i wurilim, ni i yɛdda niŋbu, ni i tuma kasikasi tumpu la zaa.

eng: Until I arrive, pay attention to public reading, preaching, and teaching.
local: Kur kpalimma n‑karimni Naawunni yɛligu maa n‑tiri niriba ni nimbirimoaa, ka kpamsiri ba sufu, ka wulisiri ba, halli ni n ti kyɛ na.

eng: Dont neglect the spiritual gift in you that was given through prophecy when the elders laid hands on you.
local: Di tam nyin daa deaai Naawunni piiniseelli la, see ka i kyɛ ka i ba'a bɛ ka ni: ni, ba daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla, ka toondaandima daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa i zugu ka i daa deaai ka.

eng: Practice these things, and live by them so that your progress will be visible to all.
local: Dɔli anŋɔ zaa ka zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑tiri a. Dina nin kyɛ ka sɔkam nya ka baŋŋi, ni, i tuma maa kyɛnni tooni.

eng: Focus on working on your own development and on what you teach. If you do this, you will save yourself and those who hear you.
local: Kur mari i maŋŋa n‑gɔsi i beebu ni i wulisigu suŋŋa. Kpalimma n‑niŋni anŋɔ dima, dama i yi niŋi lala i nin tuligi i maŋŋa, ni ninvugseeba ban wumni i wulisigu maa gba.

eng: From Paul, who is an apostle of Jesus Christ by the command of God our savior and of Christ Jesus our hope.
local: Man Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu ŋɔa: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya tuumu n nyɛ ma, ka Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti tuligra wa daa zaali ma lala tuma, ŋɔn ni Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn nyɛ ti tama la.

eng: To Timothy, my true child in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: N sɔbri ka n‑tiri nyini, Timɔti, m bimaŋni ti yɛdda niŋbu soori maa ni. Ti Ba Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita nin zɔ i namboo ka niŋi bunsuma n‑niŋi i ni, ka ti i sufudoaa.

eng: When I left for Macedonia, I asked you to stay behind in Ephesus so that you could instruct certain individuals not to spread wrong teaching.
local: Tɔ, teaama man daa gatti n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya ka yɛli i seem la. Ni, see ka i kpalim m‑bɛ Ɛfɛsusi timpuuni sa: ni, ninvugseeba m bɛ bɛni n‑wulisiri ziri wulisigu. Ka dizugu m boori ni i yɛli ba, ka ba kyɛ lala wulisigu maa.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to myths and endless genealogies. Their teaching only causes useless guessing games instead of faithfulness to Gods way of doing things.
local: Ni, ba kyɛli daadaa yɛla bamsigu - lahabaseesi an ka yɛlimaŋni, ni dɔ'am kpamma yuya kaaligu din ka tɔriga la. Dama a kur taasiri la nangbankpeemni kɔkɔ na, ka bu wulisiri Naawunni yuubu soori, dama dinŋɔ nyɛ la yɛdda niŋbu puuni.

eng: The goal of instruction is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
local: Naawunni yuubu wa daliiri nyɛ la, ni, di kpamsi wurilim; wurilim mun yiiri sufu kasi ni na, ni sufu ni teesa an ka galiŋŋu, ni yɛddamaŋni niŋbu ni na.

eng: Because they missed this goal, some people have been distracted by talk that doesnt mean anything.
local: Ninvugseeba mun ŋmaligi ni, ka kyɛ anŋɔa zaa; ka bɔrigi n‑kpalim la yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama tɔ'asigu ni.

eng: They want to be teachers of Law without understanding either what they are saying or what they are talking about with such confidence.
local: Ba sufu kur yɛnni, ni, ba leebi Naawunni zaaligu karimbadima, amaa ka bu baŋŋi ban mari yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑tɔ'asiri maa gbina: halli ni bunseesi ban tɔ'asiri a yɛla maa ni nimmoaa - ba bu mi a gba gbina.

eng: Now we know that the Law is good if used appropriately.
local: Ti mi halli, ni, Naawunni zaaligu maa veela, nira yi dɔli ka, n‑tumni kan wulisiri seem.

eng: We understand this: the Law isnt established for a righteous person but for people who live without laws and without obeying any authority. They are the ungodly and the sinners. They are people who are not spiritual, and nothing is sacred to them. They kill their fathers and mothers, and murder others.
local: Ka di kpa la talasi ni ti baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni bu zaali u zaaligu dima maa ninvugsuma zugu, amaa u zaali a la zaaligu za'asiriba zugu, ni tuppuwumni dima, ni ban bu gyeemni Naawunni, ni ala'akyi dima zugu. U laa n‑zaali a la ban beebu bu bɛ kasi ka ba ba'a ka Naawunni yɛla ni la zugu, ni ban nyɛ ninvugkuuriba n‑kuuri ba badima ni ba madima la.

eng: They are people who are sexually unfaithful, and people who have intercourse with the same sex. They are kidnappers, liars, individuals who give false testimonies in court, and those who do anything else that is opposed to sound teaching.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑tabli ban tumni ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi tuma, ni dɔppa ban doontiri dɔppa bee pɔ'aba ban doontiri pɔ'aba, ni ban zaŋŋni mugsigu n‑gbaasiri niriba, ni ziri ma'ariba, ni ban diri ziri sɛhara - banŋɔ zaa zugu ka Naawunni na zaali u zaaligu dima wa. A nyɛ la bankam niŋni din kyiisiri wulisimaŋni la zugu.

eng: Sound teaching agrees with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that has been trusted to me.
local: Wulisigu wa mun bɛ la lahabasuŋŋu ni ka mari gyirima, lahabaseelli Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ Alibarika Daana wa ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑niŋi man nuusi ni, ni m moonni ka.

eng: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength because he considered me faithful. So he appointed me to ministry.
local: M puusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita ŋɔn tiri ma yaa ni n tum tuma wa. M puusiri u ŋɔn nya ka yɛdda daana n nyɛ ma, ka dizugu di sumni ni u gaŋŋi ma ka zaali ma u tuma wa ni maa.

eng: even though I used to speak against him, attack his people, and I was proud. But I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and without faith.
local: Halli ni man daa daŋŋi n‑yɛtti u zulima, ka katti u niriba m‑mugsiri ba, ka tuuri u la zaa yɔri, ŋɔn Naawunni daa zɔ ma namboo, dama n daa niŋni di la ziilinsi zugu, ni man daa na bu niŋi yɛdda la zugu.

eng: Our Lords favor poured all over me along with the faithfulness and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Di saha maa ka ti Dugma daa zaŋŋi u wurilim piinisuma, an galisi nti yakki la, n‑ti ma; ka ti ma yɛdda ni wurilim din bɛ Yisa Masiiya ni la.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—and Im the biggest sinner of all.
local: Yɛttɔkku wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ka sumni ni nira deaai ka zaa; ni, Yisa Kirisita daa kyɛ duniya wa na ni u ti tuligi tumbeeri dima. Man muna n nyɛ ŋɔn tum tumbeeri wa n‑gaari sɔkam.

eng: But this is why I was shown mercy, so that Christ Jesus could show his endless patience to me first of all. So Im an example for those who are going to believe in him for eternal life.
local: Amaa lala zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni daa zɔ ma namboo, ni Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn yi ti kyɛ n taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ma, halli ni man ŋɔn tum tumbeeri n‑gaari sɔkam zaa yɔri. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka niriba zaa ti nya u sugrudiibu zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Ka di ti daŋŋi n‑leebi sɔseaa din nin kyɛ ka ninvugseeba ban na yɛ n‑niŋi u yɛdda nin naayi dɔli ka, ka ba nya nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga la.

eng: Now to the king of the ages, to the immortal, invisible, and only God, may honor and glory be given to him forever and always! Amen.
local: Naawunni n nyɛ saha kam zaa naaba, ka ŋɔn kɔkɔ n nyɛ ŋɔn kur bɛ saha zaa ka bu kpiira, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑nya u: ti tim u tibsim ni gyirima, saha din ka naari. Ammi !.

eng: Timothy, my child, Im giving you these instructions based on the prophecies that were once made about you. So if you follow them, you can wage a good war.
local: M bii Timɔti, n zaŋŋi zaaligu wa n‑niŋi i nuusi ni ka di mun dɔli la anabitiri yɛttɔ'a ŋɔn daa yɛli seem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla: ni, lala ti kyɛ ka i zaŋŋi ana, ni a ti leebi hoo i tɔppu neema ka i mari a n‑tuusi tɔppu suŋŋa.

eng: because you have faith and a good conscience. Some people have ruined their faith because they refused to listen to their conscience.
local: Mari i yɛdda niŋbu ni i sufu ni teesa kasi, ka galiŋŋu ka ni. Ninvugseeba mun pun za'asi sufu ni teesa wa taabu ŋɔn wulisiri seem, ka ba yɛdda niŋbu zaa baasi yɔri di zugu.

eng: such as Hymenaeus and Alexander. Ive handed them over to Satan so that they can be taught not to speak against God.
local: Ba puuni seeba n nyɛ Haamɛneesi ni Alɛkzanda. Ban ka n zaŋŋi n‑niŋi Sintaani nuusi ni, ni di kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, di bu sum ni ba mɔ Naawunni.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, to promote the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Mani Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti i, man ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita Yisa tuumu, ka Naawunni daa yiisi ma ni u ti diisi ma lala kpamni maa. Ŋɔn Naawunni mun daa pun basi nangbambasiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nyɔvuri din bɛ Kirisita Yisa saani yɛla.

eng: To Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: N sɔbri n‑tiri nyini, m biwuttigu Timɔti. Ti Ba Naawunni ni ti Dugma Kirisita Yisa nin zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑ti i ka zɔ i namboo, ka ti i sufudoaa ni alaafɛa!.

eng: Im grateful to God, whom I serve with a good conscience as my ancestors did. I constantly remember you in my prayers day and night.
local: N gyeemni la Naawunni kaman n yaadima ŋɔn daa gyeemni u seem la, ka n sufu ni teesa bɛ la u saani ka ka galiŋŋu: ka n pɛ'ari ŋɔn Naawunni ka laa n‑teaari i yɛla n wunsusigu ni, yuŋŋu ni wuntaŋŋa zaa.

eng: When I remember your tears, I long to see you so that I can be filled with happiness.
local: N yi ti teaai i yɛla, ni, nyin daa kum nintam seem la, n yi kur boori la n nya i, ka di kyɛ ka n sufu maai.

eng: Im reminded of your authentic faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice. Im sure that this faith is also inside you.
local: Dinzugu n teaai nyin ŋɔn mari yɛdda seem maa, ka ziri ka di ni. Halli i yaapɔ'a Loosi daa daŋŋi m‑mari yɛdda wa taabu, ni i ma Yuunisi la; ka m mi, ni, nyin gba mara.

eng: Because of this, Im reminding you to revive Gods gift that is in you through the laying on of my hands.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu m boori la n teaai i, Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti i piiniseaa la yɛla, ni, i tigsima di n‑niŋi hoo nira ŋɔn yi tigsi bugum ka di ti diri seem la. Dama ka bɛ i ni halli man daa zaŋŋi n nuusi m‑pa i zugu sahaseelli la n‑kyɛnna.

eng: God didnt give us a spirit that is timid but one that is powerful, loving, and self-controlled.
local: Dama Naawunni daa bu zaŋŋi la u Siiya n‑ti ti, ni u ti kyɛ ka ti zɔri dabeem; amaa u Sii maa nin kyɛ ka ti mari yiiko ni wurilim, ka mari ti mansi suŋŋa.

eng: So dont be ashamed of the testimony about the Lord or of me, his prisoner. Instead, share the suffering for the good news, depending on Gods power.
local: Dizugu di kyɛ ka vi mari i, nyin tɔ'asiri ti Dugma yɛla zugu, bee, man zugu gba, man ŋɔn bɛ dansarika duu wa ni maa. See ka Naawunni yiiko kyɛ ka man ni nyin la'am n‑di wahala lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu.

eng: God is the one who saved and called us with a holy calling. This wasnt based on what we have done, but it was based on his own purpose and grace that he gave us in Christ Jesus before time began.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni n daa tuligi ti ka booli ti, ni di kyɛ ka ti bɛ kasi. Di pa la ti tunsuma zugu, amaa di nyɛ la u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa pun gbaai seem: dama u daa daŋŋi n‑yiisi u bunsuma, ni u ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti ti Kirisita Yisa zugu pɔi, ka sahaseelli din daa pun gaari la daa na bu piili.

eng: Now his grace is revealed through the appearance of our savior, Christ Jesus. He destroyed death and brought life and immortality into clear focus through the good news.
local: Halli nti paai zuna, ka pampaŋŋɔ di yi kasigi n‑wulisi ti, Kirisita Yisa Masiiya, ŋɔn nyɛ ti Tuligra, kyɛnni na la zugu. Ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn daa nyaŋŋi kum zaa, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka ti nini neaai. U lahabasuŋŋu puuni u wulisi ti nyɔvuri yɛla, nyɔvuri seaa kum ŋɔn laa n‑ka ni la.

eng: I was appointed a messenger, apostle, and teacher of this good news.
local: Lahabasuŋŋu wa zugu ka Naawunni daa yiisi ma n‑leebi lahabaari maa tuumu, ni n ti moonni ka n‑wulisiri n‑tiri niriba.

eng: This is also why Im suffering the way I do, but Im not ashamed. I know the one in whom Ive placed my trust. Im convinced that God is powerful enough to protect what he has placed in my trust until that day.
local: Ka lala zugu ka n diri wahala wa. Amaa vi bu mari ma, dama n mi man ŋɔn niŋi sɔ yɛdda, ka mi, ni, u nin toaai mari bunseelli man ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑niŋi u nuu ni la, halli nti paai Zaadaari.

eng: Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching that you heard from me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: I pun wum yɛttɔ'asuma man saani. Kur kyɛ ka a wulisi i bunseesi an mari alaafɛ ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ka kpalim m‑mari yɛdda ni wurilim, dama lala ka Kirisita Yisa boori ni ti tumna.

eng: Protect this good thing that has been placed in your trust through the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
local: Naawunni Sii Kasi ŋɔn bɛ ti ni la pun zaŋŋi yɛlimaŋni bunsuŋŋu n‑niŋi i nuu ni; kur kpalimma n‑gura suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: You know that everyone in Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
local: I mi halli, ni, Faagyilusi ni Hɛmɔgyiniisi daa bakki ka kyɛ ma, ban ni ban bɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu zaa m‑paasi ba zugu.

eng: May the Lord show mercy to Onesiphorus household, because he supported me many times and he wasnt ashamed of my imprisonment.
local: Amaa n susiri ti Dugma Naawunni, ni, u zɔma Ɔneesifɔru ni u yiri dima namboo, dama u daa kur nyɛ la ŋɔn kpamsiri n sufu sahakam; ka vi bu mari u ban kpari ma dansarika duu wa zugu.

eng: After I arrived in Rome, he quickly looked for me and found me.
local: Halli ŋɔn daa paai Room na sahanseaa la, u daa bɔ n ziiya ni nimmoaa, halli nti nya ma.

eng: May the Lord allow him to find his mercy on that day .
local: Ti Dugma kyɛ ka u ti nya namboo ŋɔn ti Dugma saani Zaadaari maa! Ka nyin mun mi ŋɔn daa tum tunsunseesi Ɛfɛsusi n‑suŋŋi ma.

eng: Those who are under the bondage of slavery should consider their own masters as worthy of full respect so that Gods name and our teaching wont get a bad reputation.
local: Di kpa la talasi ka ninvugseeba ban nyɛ daaba la tisiri ba duniya wa dugmadima gyirima, lala ka ya ku kyɛ ka sɔ tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseaa din bɛ n‑sa'amni Naawunni yuuri, bee, ti wulisigu wa yuuri.

eng: And those who have masters who are believers shouldnt look down on them because they are brothers. Instead, they should serve them more faithfully, because the people who benefit from your good service are believers who are loved. Teach and encourage these things.
local: Halli daaba ban duniya wa dugmadima ŋɔn nyɛ yɛddaniŋniba maa gba - di sum ni ba tiri ba lala gyirima; ba di zeemni ba, ban nyɛ mabiisi ni taaba zugu. Ay! see ka ba tumni n‑tiri ba halli n‑gaari ban dugmadima pa yɛddaniŋniba maa ŋɔn gyeemni ba seem la; dama ban diri ba tuma maa anfaani nyɛ la yɛddaniŋniba ban wuri seeba. Wulisima ba ka kpamsi ba ka ba niŋni anŋɔ dima zaa.

eng: If anyone teaches anything different and doesnt agree with sound teaching about our Lord Jesus Christ and teaching that is consistent with godliness.
local: Ka ŋɔnkam wulisiri wulisigu din bɛ di kɔkɔa, ka bu dɔli ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita yɛligu din mari alaafɛ la, bee, Naawunni soori wulisigu tin dɔli seaa la.

eng: that person is conceited. They dont understand anything but have a sick obsession with debates and arguments. This creates jealousy, conflict, verbal abuse, and evil suspicions.
local: di daama nyɛ la karimbaani daana ka na zi bunseela. U naa n‑nyɛ la kɔribeeri daana ŋɔn sufu kur bɛ la nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu ni, ni zabkyɔ'ama zappu zugu. Dinŋɔ taasiri la nyuuri zappu na, ni taaba nyaanzum, ni wɔligri, ni zulisigu.

eng: There is constant bickering between people whose minds are ruined and who have been robbed of the truth. They think that godliness is a way to make money!.
local: Niriba wa taabu teesa sa'amiya zaa, ka ba daamni la taaba dizugu; ba kɔŋŋni la yɛlimaŋni zaa gba, ka teesiri, ni, Naawunni soori zugu nin kyɛ ka ba nya arizikki n‑niŋi bundaandima.

eng: Actually, godliness is a great source of profit when it is combined with being happy with what you already have.
local: Tɔ: Naawunni soori siri nyɛ la arizikki titaari n‑tiri ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nini tigi ŋɔn mari seela.

eng: We didnt bring anything into the world and so we cant take anything out of it.
local: Dama ti daa bu zi bunseelli n‑kyɛ duniya wa ni na; ti mun ku laa n‑zi bunseelli n‑labi.

eng: well be happy with food and clothing.
local: Ka ti yi mari bundirigu ni sutura, dinŋɔna di sakkiya n‑ti ti.

eng: But people who are trying to get rich fall into temptation. They are trapped by many stupid and harmful passions that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
local: Ninvugseeba ban sufu kur boori ni ba leebi bundaandima maa pun zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑kpeesi zamsigu ni, hoo kyɛ'ariga ni. Ba laa n‑zaŋŋni ba mansi n‑kpeesiri la gyɛrilim ni, ni kɔriseesi an lusiri daadam ka basiri ba yɔri.

eng: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. Some have wandered away from the faith and have impaled themselves with a lot of pain because they made money their goal.
local: Dama ligri boobu nyɛ la bumbeeri kam zaa nya'ari. Ka ninvugseeba ban sufu daa kur bɛ ligri nyaabu zugu daa burigiya ni, ka kyɛ ba yɛdda halli nti zaŋŋi sufusa'aŋŋu pam n‑kusi ba mansi hoo piima la.

eng: But as for you, man of God, run away from all these things. Instead, pursue righteousness, holy living, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
local: Amaa nyin ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni nira wa, kur gbaam i maŋŋa ka kyɛ anŋɔ zaa. Kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑dɔli yɛlimaŋni soori maa; ka zaŋŋi yɛdda, ni wurilim, n‑gyeemni Naawunni ka laa n‑zɛ yummu m‑bɛ baalimm.

eng: Compete in the good fight of faith. Grab hold of eternal life—you were called to it, and you made a good confession of it in the presence of many witnesses.
local: Kur bari n‑zaani m‑mɔ suŋŋasuŋŋa. Gbaam nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga yɛla gba kirikiri. Naawunni daa booli i, ni i ti mari lala nyɔvuri maa, ka nyin mun daa tɔ'asi n‑wulisi lala paalu ni, ka niriba pam daa wum.

eng: I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when testifying before Pontius Pilate.
local: Naawunni zaŋŋi nyɔvuri n‑tisiri bunseelli kam zaa; Yisa Masiiya mun daa zaani Gɔmna Pɔntiyusi Pailiti tooni n‑tɔ'asi n‑wulisi Naawunni yɛla paalu ni: dizugu ka man zaantiri zaaligu wa n‑tiri i.

eng: Obey this order without fault or failure until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ni, i dɔli soori maa ni beesikasi ka galiŋŋu ka ni, halli ka ti Dugma Yisa ti yi paalu na.

eng: The timing of this appearance is revealed by God alone, who is the blessed and only master, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Naawunni nin wulisi u n‑ti ti na, sahaseelli ŋɔn ti gbaai, ni, sahasuŋŋu deede nla. Ŋɔn kɔkɔ nyɛ la Naawunni Alibarika Daana, ŋɔn su bunseelli kam; u nyɛ la nadima zaa Naaba ni dugmadima zaa Dugma.

eng: He alone has immortality and lives in light that no one can come near. No human being has ever seen or is able to see him. Honor and eternal power belong to him. Amen.
local: Ŋɔn kɔkɔ n laa n‑nyɛ ŋɔn bu kpiira, ka bɛ la neesim ni, halli ka sɔsɔ ku toaai m‑pɔri m‑paai u na, neesim maa gyirima zugu. Ninsaalisɔ na zi n‑nya u, ka sɔ mun ku toaai nya u. Ŋɔna n sumni ni ba gyeemni u, ka u su yiiko halli ni saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi .

eng: Tell people who are rich at this time not to become egotistical and not to place their hope on their finances, which are uncertain. Instead, they need to hope in God, who richly provides everything for our enjoyment.
local: See ka i kpaam duniya wa bundaandima maa, ni, ba di mari karimbaani, bee, n‑zaŋŋi ba tama n‑niŋi arizikki din bu ya yuui saha zaa. Amaa ba zaŋŋma ba tama n‑niŋi Naawunni ŋɔn tiri ti bunseelli kam din nin nya'asa n‑ti ti.

eng: So, my child, draw your strength from the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu m biiya, piinisuma m bɛ la Kirisita Yisa ni; kur kyɛ ka a kpamsi i, ni kpeeŋŋu zaa.

eng: Tell them to do good, to be rich in the good things they do, to be generous, and to share with others.
local: See ka i kpaam ba yaasa, ni, ba tumma din veela; ka nyɛ bundaandima tunsuma tumpu ziiya. Di nyɛ la: ba putti ban mari bunseela la n‑tiri niriba.

eng: Take the things you heard me say in front of many other witnesses and pass them on to faithful people who are also capable of teaching others.
local: I pun wum Naawunni yɛttɔ'asuma man saani, ka niriba pam gba daa la'amni i saani n‑wumna. Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna zaŋŋma anŋɔ n‑wulisiri ninvugseeba ban zɛ yummu ka nyɛ ban gba nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi a n‑wulisiri ba taaba.

eng: When they do these things, they will save a treasure for themselves that is a good foundation for the future. That way they can take hold of what is truly life.
local: Ba yi ti niŋi lala, ba la'amni la arizikki ni ba zaali ni di ti ku ba bukaata, sahaseaa kan kyɛnni na maa. Lala nin ŋmanni hoo ban dooli nyeebsuŋŋu pɔi ka naayi mɛ ka zugu seem la. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ba nyaŋŋi n‑nya nyɔvuri din nyɛ nyɔvurimaŋni la.

eng: Accept your share of suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini n nyɛ Kirisita Yisa tɔbbiiya ka zɛ yummu; dizugu kur paasima ti zugu nti diri wahala.

eng: Timothy, protect what has been given to you in trust. Avoid godless and pointless discussions and the contradictory claims of so-called knowledge.
local: M bii Timɔti, mari Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑niŋi i nuu ni, ni i guri maa. Ŋmaligma ka kyɛ yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama an bu sum ni Naawunni yɛla ni, ka za'asi nangbankpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi ninvugseeba ŋɔn kyirimni m‑boonni bansim la.

eng: Nobody who serves in the military gets tied up with civilian matters, so that they can please the one who recruited them.
local: Tɔ, ka tɔbbisɔ ŋɔn bɛ u tɔppu tuma ni bu zaŋŋni u nuu n‑kpeesiri dabsa zaa yɛla ni, dama u ba'a ka la bunseelli ni, di yi pa la u tum din kyɛ ka u tɔppu toondaana maa nini tigra.

eng: When some people adopted this false knowledge, they missed the goal of faith. May grace be with you all.
local: Dama, ninvugseeba ban yɛtti, ni, ba mari bamsim wa burigiya ka kyɛ yɛdda niŋbu soori. Naawunni piinisuma nin kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani.

eng: Also in the same way, athletes dont win unless they follow the rules.
local: Halli ninvugsɔ ŋɔn zɔri zoaa gba ku deaai zoaa laara na'ala u dɔli la zoaa maa zaaligu soori ŋɔn wulisi seem la.

eng: A hardworking farmer should get the first share of the crop.
local: Ka pukpaara ŋɔn tumni suŋŋa nyɛ la ŋɔn sumni ni u daŋŋi n‑deaai bundiripaala maa seesi n‑di.

eng: Think about what Im saying; the Lord will give you understanding about everything.
local: Teesima man yɛtti i seem wa yɛla, dama ti Dugma nin kyɛ ka i baŋŋi seelikam zaa gbinni.

eng: Remember Jesus Christ, who was raised from the dead and descended from David. This is my good news.
local: Teaama Yisa Masiiya yɛla#:- ni, u daa yi NaaDauda za'anɔri ni na, ka daa kpi n‑neaai kum ni. Lala ka n daa wulisi n‑ti i, n lahabasuŋŋu mooligu la yɛla ni.

eng: This is the reason Im suffering to the point that Im in prison like a common criminal. But Gods word cannot be imprisoned.
local: Lahabasuŋŋu wa zugu ka n diri wahala, ka ba ba ma bansi kaman tumbɛtumna n nyɛ ma la. Amaa sɔ ku toaai m‑ba Naawunni yɛligu bansi, halli beela!.

eng: This is why I endure everything for the sake of those who are chosen by God so that they too may experience salvation in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
local: Dizugu ka n niŋni sugru, wahala yɛla zaa puuni. N niŋni lala ni n ti suŋŋi ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn daa gamsi la, ni di kyɛ ka ba gba nin toaai n‑nya tuliginsim din bɛ Kirisita Yisa saani, ka mari gyirima din ku naai la.

eng: This saying is reliable: If we have died together, we will also live together.
local: Yɛttɔ'a wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni#:- Tin ni ŋɔna yi daa la'am n‑kpi, Ti nin ti la'am m‑bɛ ni yaasa.

eng: If we endure, we will also rule together. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
local: Ti yi kpalim n‑niŋni sugru, Tin ni ŋɔna nin ti la'am n‑di naam. Ti mun yi za'asi u, Ŋɔn gba nin za'asi ti.

eng: If we are disloyal, he stays faithful because he cant be anything else than what he is.
local: Ti yi bu zɛ yummu, Ŋɔn kur zɛ la yummu, Dama u ku toaai n‑za'asi u mammaŋŋa.

eng: Remind them of these things and warn them in the sight of God not to engage in battles over words that arent helpful and only destroy those who hear them.
local: Tɔ'asima n‑tiri i niriba la, n‑teaari ba anŋɔ zaa yɛla. See ka i kpaam ba, Naawunni zugu, ni, ba zɔma n‑yi yɛttɔ'ayɔya ni. A ka bukyi; a laa n‑sa'amni la ninvugseeba ban wumni a maa.

eng: Make an effort to present yourself to God as a tried-and-true worker, who doesnt need to be ashamed but is one who interprets the message of truth correctly.
local: Zaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni n‑wulisi, ni, i nyɛ la tuntumnisɔ ŋɔn ku di vi u tuma ni, ka tumni suŋŋa n‑wulisiri Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Avoid their godless discussions, because they will lead many people into ungodly behavior.
local: Zɔma ka kyɛ yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama wa seesi an bu tiri Naawunni gyirima la. A nyɛ la an kyɛri ka niriba maali m‑burigri ka kyɛri Naawunni soori.

eng: and their ideas will spread like an infection. This includes Hymenaeus and Philetus.
local: Niriba wa wulisigu maa ŋmanni la nɔriseaa kan diri n‑kpeeri ningbiŋŋu ni la. Ba seeba n‑nyɛ Haamɛneesi ni Filituusi.

eng: who have deviated from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already happened. This has undermined some peoples faith.
local: ban kyɛ yɛlimaŋni soori m‑burigi: ba yɛtti, ni, kpiimma kum ni neaari saha pun gaari. Lala ka ba niŋni n‑gbarigri seeba yɛdda.

eng: Gods solid foundation is still standing with this sign: The Lord knows the people who belong to him, and Everyone who confesses the Lords name must avoid wickedness. .
local: Amaa Naawunni pun zaali nyeebri, ka ka zaani suŋŋa. Ba sɔbi yɛttɔ'asuma m‑pa ka zugu, ka a nyɛ daalimni: ni, Ti Dugma mi u niriba ŋɔn nyɛ seeba. ni Ŋɔnkam zaa yɛli, ni, u dɔli la ti Dugma, ŋɔn zɔma ka kyɛ tumbeeri.

eng: In a mansion, there arent just gold and silver bowls but also some bowls that are made of wood and clay. Some are meant for special uses, some for garbage.
local: I bu nya? - Yirititaari puuni ka laasi balibubalibu bɛ ni: pa salima ni anzulifa buna kɔkɔa, amaa a seesi bɛ ni an nyɛ la ban zaŋŋi daari m‑maali, ka seesi mun nyɛ ban zaŋŋi ya'ari m‑mɛa. Aseesi ka ba mari n‑tumni gyirima tuma, ka mari seesi mun n‑tumni din pa gyirima tuma.

eng: So if anyone washes filth off themselves, they will be set apart as a special bowl. They will be useful to the owner of the mansion for every sort of good work.
local: Ka ninvugsɔ yi mari u sufu kasi, ka yi man pun yɛli bumpoosiseesi zaa yɛla ni, dinŋɔna u nin leebi hoo laseaa kan mari gyirima, ka maali siri ni ka tum tunsuma. Kana n veela, ka ka yiridaana nin zaŋŋni ka n‑tumni u tuma.

eng: Run away from adolescent cravings. Instead, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace together with those who confess the Lord with a clean heart.
local: Dizugu gbaam i maŋŋa ni na'akyimbisi kɔribeeri tuma; ka kpaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑dɔli yɛlimaŋni soori, ni yɛdda, ni wurilim, ni sufudoaa. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka i paasi ban dɔli ti Dugma ka ba sufu ka da'ari la zugu.

eng: Avoid foolish and thoughtless discussions, since you know that they produce conflicts.
local: Kur yiisima i nuu ka kyɛ gyɛrilim zaa, ni boosa boosigu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama yɛla. I mi halli, ni, a yi taasiri la nangbankpeemni na.

eng: Gods slave shouldnt be argumentative but should be kind toward all people, able to teach, patient.
local: Ka ti Dugma tuntumna mun bu sum ni u ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni. Di laa n‑sumni ni u mari nirilim zaŋŋi kyaŋŋi sɔkam zaa saani, ka mi wulisigu suŋŋa, ka nyɛ la ŋɔn diri sugru halli bumbeeri puuni gba.

eng: and should correct opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will change their mind and give them a knowledge of the truth.
local: Di laa n‑sumni ni u bɛ la baalimm n‑wulisiri ban ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni, ni, ba ti baŋŋi din tuusi. Dama di pa seelli ka Naawunni ti kyɛ ka ba takki ba teesibeeri ni ba ala'akyi soori nti baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seelli.

eng: They may come to their senses and escape from the devils trap that holds them captive to do his will.
local: Sintaani daa pun bɛri u kyɛ'ariga n‑gbaai ba, ka kyɛ ka ba tum u sufu yuubu zaa; amaa di yi pa seem Naawunni nin ti kyɛ ka ba labi n‑teesiri teesisuma yaasa.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: Naawunni zugu ka n ya n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑kpaam i, Kirisita Yisa zugu; dama ŋɔn Yisa nin ti kari ban nɛ ni ban kpi zaa sariya. Ŋɔn Yisa Masiiya lappu na zugu, ni u naam diibu la zugu, ka n ya n‑yɛli i ni, ni, i niŋma nŋɔa#:-.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: Moolima Naawunni yɛligu; kur yirigma di yɛla sahasuŋŋu, halli ni sahaseaa din pa sahasuŋŋu gba. Sɛ'asim niriba, kpaasim ba zugri ni; kpaamni niriba suŋŋa; kur dim sugru n‑wulisiri n‑wulisiri ba.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Dama saha kyɛnni na ka niriba ku ti laa n‑deaari yɛlimaŋni wulisigu, amaa ka nin dɔli la ba mammaŋŋa kɔrisi soori. Ba nin bɔ m‑bɔ karimbadima bɔbgu, seeba ban nin wulisiri wulisiseesi an niŋni ba nya'asim m‑paasira.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Ba nin za'asi yɛlimaŋni wumpu, ka zaŋŋi ba ba'aba zaa n‑niŋi kurimbu ni sa lahabaya an ka yɛlimaŋni.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: Amaa nyin muna - kur gbaama i maŋŋa yɛlli kam zaa puuni, m‑mari sugru wahala diibu ni; tumni lahabasuŋŋu mooligu tuma, ka laa n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn yiisi i, ni i tum tunseaa la.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Man muna - saha maa paaya ni n nyɔvuri leebi hoo sara maaligu: n duniya wa ni yiibu saha pɔtta.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: N zabi zabsuŋŋu n‑naai; n pun zɔ nti paai zoaa maa tɔriga; n daa mari n yɛdda niŋbu suŋŋa.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Seela bu laa n‑kpalim n‑ti ma, di yi pa la n yɛlimaŋni tuma wa laara deaari. Ti Dugma Yɛlimaŋni Daana nin ti kari sariya Zaadaari maa, ka ŋɔn saani ka n nin deaai ka. Laara n dɔya n‑guri ma, ka di pa la man kɔkɔ ka u ti ya n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi, amaa u nin ti zaŋŋi n‑ti bankam zaa sufu boori u lappu na.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Tɔ: ka kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ n saani na yoomyooma.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: Dama ba zaa bakki ka kyɛ ma: Diimasi daa kyaŋŋi Tɛsalɔnika, ŋɔn wuri duniya wa buna zugu; Karɛseensi mun daa kyaŋŋi Galɛseaa, ka Taituusi kyaŋŋi Dalimɛseaa.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Luuki kɔkɔ n kur kpalim m‑bɛ n saani. Zaŋŋma Maaki m‑paasi i maŋŋa zugu n‑kyɛ na, dama u nyɛ la ŋɔn nin toaai n‑suŋŋni ma suŋŋa.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: Man mun daa tum la Tikyikusi, ni u kyaŋŋi Ɛfɛsusi.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: Ka i yi ti kyɛnni na, kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑zaŋŋi m bunyeerigu la - buntibsiseaa man daa kyɛ m‑basi Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa maa ni la - ka bɛ la Kaapusi saani: zaŋŋma ka m‑mari na. Ka zaŋŋi litaafidima la gba m‑paasi na, an sɔbi gbansuma la zugu mari la anfaani n‑gaari zaa.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Alɛkzanda ŋɔn nyɛ mankyeeri la daa tum ma taalli pam: ti Dugma nin boosi u beeri, n‑labsi ŋɔn tum seem maa zugu.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: See ka nyini gba mari i maŋŋa u saani, dama u daa zabi n‑kyiisi ti yɛligu la nimbirimoaa.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Man daa pun daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a la kootu ni, amaa sɔ daa bu zaani n nyaaŋŋa; sɔkam zaa daa zɔ ni ka kyɛ ma. Naawunni di zaŋŋi di taalli n‑ziili ba!.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Amaa ti Dugma daa zaani n nyaaŋŋa, ka ti ma yaa, nti kyɛ ka lahabasuŋŋu maa mooligu ti yi paalu, ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa zaa wum di. Ti Dugma daa yiisi ma gbugimni noori ni na ni alaafɛa.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Ti Dugma nin yiisi ma yɛlibeoo kam zaa ni na, ka nin mari ma halli nti paai u arizana naam ni. Ŋɔna n sum ni tibgri halli ni saha kan ka tɔriga! Ammi !.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: Puusima Prisila ni Akwila ni Ɔneesifɔru yiri dima n‑ti ma.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Ɛrasitusi daa kpalim m‑bɛ Kɔrinti. Ka n daa kyɛ Tirɔfimusi mun Miliitusi timpuuni, ŋɔn daa ka alaafɛ zugu.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ na ka waari saha na bu piili. Yubulusi puusiri i ni, ŋɔn ni Pudɛnsi ni Linusi ni Klɔdiya, zaŋŋi n‑tabli ti mabiisi ban kpalim la zaa.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Ti Dugma kpamsim i sufu! Naawunni piinisuma nin kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni!.

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Gbaŋŋu wa yi la man Pɔla saani na. Man mun nyɛ daabri n‑ti Naawunni, ni tuumu n‑ti ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya. Naawunni n daa gaŋŋi ma ka tum ma ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpamsi u ninvuggamsa la yɛdda. U daa laa n‑tum ma ni n kyaŋŋi nti kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni soori ni.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: Naawunni n daa daŋŋi m‑basi ti nangbambasiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nyɔvuri kan ka baasigu la pɔi ka duniya naayi piili. Ka lala nyɔvuri tama zugu ka ti mari ti yɛdda ni bamsim maa n‑zɛ yummu.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: Ka saha maa deede ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa kasigi u yɛligu maa n‑tiri niriba. Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti tuligra la n daa zaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑niŋi n nuusi ni, ka m moonni ka: ka lala lahabaari puuni ka u wulisiri u yɛligu yɛla.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: N sɔbri n‑tiri la nyini, Taituusi, dama i nyɛ la m bimaŋni, ti zaa ŋɔn mari yɛdda yinni zugu. Ti Ba Naawunni ni Kirisita Yisa nin niŋi bunsuma zaa n‑niŋi i, ka ti i alaafɛa.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n daa kyɛ i, ni i kpalim m‑bɛ Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu ni, ni i dɔlisi tin daa bu naai tunseesi la ka maali n‑zaanni a. N daa laa m‑boori la i yiisi niriba n‑diisi toondaandima tinkam zaa ni, kaman man ŋɔn daa wulisi i seem maa.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: I yi diisi sɔ toondaana, see ka di daama mari la beesisuŋŋu ka ka galiŋŋu. See ka u mari la pɔ'a yinni, ka u biisi n nyɛ seeba ban niŋni yɛdda, ka niriba ku nya taalli ba saani, ni, ba bu gbaari ba mansi suŋŋa, bee, ba bu deaari ba badima maa noori.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Toondaana ŋɔn su Naawunni yiri dima tuma fukumsi zugu, di niŋi la kamaata ka u mari la beesisuŋŋu ka ka galiŋŋu. Di bu sumni ni u nyuuri bara, bee, u sufu yisigi yoomyooma. Di mun bu laa n‑sumni ni u nyɛ danyuura, bee, zabboora bee, ŋɔn ba'a zaa bɛ la ligri boobu ni.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: See ka u deaari la saamma u yiri ni, ka wuri din veela. Toondaana sumni la u nyɛ yɛm daana ni yɛlimaŋni daana, ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ kasi ka mari u maŋŋa suŋŋa.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: Di laa n‑niŋi kamaata ka u mari Naawunni yɛligu din nyɛ yɛlimaŋni maa kirikiri, hoo ban daa wulisi ti seem la. Lala zugu nin kyɛ ka u nin toaai n‑kpamsi u taaba ka wulisi ba yɛm ni alaafɛ sɔya ŋɔn bɛ seem; ka dinŋɔna u nin wulisi ban kyiisiri ti yɛla ba buriginsim ŋɔn nyɛ seelli.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: Dama niriba pam siri bɛ ni ban mari tuppuwumni ka kur tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama ni ba ti yɔmsiri niriba. Seeba ban na kur dɔli la Yɛhudiyadima addiini kaya soori maa bɛ la lala halli.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Ba yɛttɔ'a ka soori - see ka i kyɛ ka ba sina, dama ba wulisiri la wulisiseelli din da bu sumni ni ba wulisi. Lala wulisigu maa ŋmaari la yiya. Nyoori zugu ka ba niŋni lala maa.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: Kyiriti dima maa muna - halli ba maŋtoori anabisɔ gba daa yɛli ba yɛla, ni#:- Kyiriti dima kur nyɛ la ziri dima saha zaa; Ka nyɛ ninvugbeeri, ka nyɛ gbinnya'adima: Beem dima ban wuri bundirigu pam yɔri.

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Yɛlimaŋni n‑kur la. Dinzugu kpaasim ba zugri ni suŋŋa. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ba yɛdda maali n‑kpaŋŋi suŋŋa.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: I yi niŋi lala, ba ku laa n‑wumni sɔlinseesi Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn sɔlimni n‑tisiri ba maa; bee, ninsaalidima ban za'asi yɛlimaŋni soori la ŋɔn zaali zaaliseesi maa.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Bunseelli kam zaa nyɛ la kasi n‑ti ninvugseeba ban nyɛ kasi; amaa ban tumni da'ari tuma ka bu niŋni yɛdda la, bunseelli n ka ni n‑nyɛ kasi n‑ti ba. Ba sufu, ni ba sufu ni teesa zaa nyɛ la da'ari.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Ba yɛliya, ni, ba mi Naawunni, amaa ka ba tuma zaa kyiisiri u ni. Ba tuma zaa nyɛ la din kyisira, ni noori za'asigu. Dinzugu ba ku nyaŋŋi n‑tum tunsuŋŋu zaa.

eng: Understand that the last days will be dangerous times.
local: M biiya, kur baŋŋma, ni, duniya wa yi ti ya naai sahanseaa, wahala ni sufusa'aŋŋu pam nin bɛ duniya wa ni.

eng: People will be selfish and love money. They will be the kind of people who brag and who are proud. They will slander others, and they will be disobedient to their parents. They will be ungrateful, unholy.
local: Niriba nin ti mari beem, ka wuri ligri n‑gaari ninsaalidima. Ba nin nyuuri bara n‑duusiri ba mansi ka tuuri Naawunni ni niriba. Biisi nin ti za'asi ba badima ni ba madima yɛligu ka ba bu ya puusi ban tum tunsuma n‑ti ba. Ba nin za'asi Naawunni beesikasi yɛla.

eng: unloving, contrary, and critical. They will be without self-control and brutal, and they wont love what is good.
local: Ba bu ti ya boori taaba, bee, n‑kyɛ taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni. Ba nin zuuri niriba nyaaŋŋa, amaa ka ku mari ba mansi. Ba nin tumni taaba putoo gba. Ba ku laa m‑boori bunsuma halli beela.

eng: They will be people who are disloyal, reckless, and conceited. They will love pleasure instead of loving God.
local: Ba nin ti yi taaba nyaaŋŋa; ba nin luuri n‑tumni basabasa; ba nin tibgri ba mansi ba yɛttɔ'a ni. Halli ka sɔkam boori la u sufuyuubu bunnya'asa ka za'asi Naawunni yɛla wumpu.

eng: They will look like they are religious but deny Gods power. Avoid people like this.
local: Niriba ti ya n‑dɔli la addiini soori ka niriba ti nya, amaa ka bu dɔli Naawunni yiiko soori ni yɛlimaŋni. Zɔma lala niriba.

eng: Some will slither into households and control immature women who are burdened with sins and driven by all kinds of desires.
local: Niriba wa taabu yi kur yɔmsiri n‑kpeeri yiya ni, ni ba ti la'ari pɔ'aseeba ban ka yaa n‑gbaasiri ba ni ba ti leebi ba nyaandɔliba. Lala pɔ'aba mun dɔli ba, ba maŋtoori tumbeeri yɛla zugu, ni ba kɔribeeri yɛla zugu m‑paasi.

eng: These women are always learning, but they can never arrive at an understanding of the truth.
local: Lala pɔ'aba maa yi kur wumni wulisigu, amaa ba bu ya nyaŋŋi m‑paai yɛlimaŋni yɛla bamsim.

eng: These people oppose the truth in the same way that Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. Their minds are corrupt and their faith is counterfeit.
local: Tɔ, ka niriba wa ŋmanni la Gyansi ni Gyambirisi, ban daa nyɛ Igyipti dɔseeba ban daa kyiisi Musa yɛla la; lala ka niriba wa yi kyiisiri yɛlimaŋni maa. Lala niriba zaa mari la teesibeeri, ka kɔŋŋi yɛdda soori dɔligu.

eng: But they wont get very far. Their foolishness will become obvious to everyone like those others.
local: Amaa ba yɛla ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi tooni. Lala niriba daa kyiisi Musa, ka ba gyɛrilim daa yi paalu ka sɔkam nya, lala ka sɔkam nin ti nya niriba wa dinni paalu ni.

eng: But you have paid attention to my teaching, conduct, purpose, faithfulness, patience, love, and endurance.
local: Nyin mun daa pun dɔli man ŋɔn daa wulisi sɔseelli. I daa pun bamsi n beebu soori, ka mi man ŋɔn daa gbaai gbaari seem la. I pun mi n yɛdda yɛla, ni n sugru diibu yɛla. I dɔli la n wurilim soori, ka laa n‑zɛ yummu hoo man daa zɛ seem la.

eng: You have seen me experience physical abuse and ordeals in places such as Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I put up with all sorts of abuse, and the Lord rescued me from it all!.
local: Halli i laa m‑mi mugsigu ni wahala seesi an daa paai ma na: i mi n dindaandima ban daa niŋi ma seem Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa, ni Aikoonyum ni Lisitira tinsi ni gba. Amaa ka ti Dugma daa yiisi ma a zaa ni.

eng: In fact, anyone who wants to live a holy life in Christ Jesus will be harassed.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ninsaala kam ŋɔn boori ni u dɔli sɔsuŋŋu ka gyeemni Kirisita Yisa nin nya namsigu.

eng: But evil people and swindlers will grow even worse, as they deceive others while being deceived themselves.
local: Halli ka teesibeeri dima ni ziri dima zaa nin maali n‑gbarigi ba tumbeeri zugu. Ba yɔmsiri niriba, ka niriba mun yɔmsiri ba gba.

eng: But you must continue with the things you have learned and found convincing. You know who taught you.
local: Amaa nyin muna - see ka i kur kpalim n‑dɔli ban daa pun wulisi i bunseesi la, seesi nyin daa pun niŋi a yɛdda; dama i mi halli ban saani ka i daa bamsi a.

eng: Since childhood you have known the holy scriptures that help you to be wise in a way that leads to salvation through faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Ka laa m‑mi, ni, i daa baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu, nyin daa pun nyɛ bibila sahanseaa la. Lala yɛligu maa teesa zugu ka i nin ti nya bamsim, ka di zugu i nin niŋi Kirisita Yisa yɛdda nti nya tuliginsim u zugu.

eng: Every scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for showing mistakes, for correcting, and for training character.
local: Litaafi yɛligu maa sɔppu zaa ka Naawunni vuusim daa ti ti na; a zaa mari yiiko nti wulisi ti, ka kyiisi ziri, ni ti ti baŋŋi tumbeeri ni din bu dɔlisi yɛla; ka a laa n‑wulisiri ti ŋɔn Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: so that the person who belongs to God can be equipped to do everything that is good.
local: Dizugu Naawunni nira nin toaai n‑tumni ŋɔn Naawunni sufuyuubu ka laa m‑mari yɛm ni yiiko ni u ti tum tunsuma zaa.

eng: But you should talk in a way that is consistent with sound teaching.
local: Amaa nyin mun kur wulisima wulisigu seelli din dɔlisi kan nyɛ yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Tell the older men to be sober, dignified, sensible, and healthy in respect to their faith, love, and patience.
local: Wulisima ninvugkura la, ni, ba bɛ baalimm, ka ba beebu maa nin sum ni gyirima tisibu. Wulisima ba ka ba mari yɛddamaŋni ni wurilimmaŋni, ka kur kpalim n‑zɛ yummu, dama anŋɔ zaa tisiri alaafɛa.

eng: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in their behavior, teaching what is good, rather than being gossips or addicted to heavy drinking.
local: Wulisima pɔ'akura la gba ka ba beebu mari gyirima; amaa ka pa ni ba leebi ban zuuri niriba nyaaŋŋa, bee, daam ŋɔn su seeba. Kyɛ ka ba wulisiri la din veela.

eng: That way they can mentor young women to love their husbands and children.
local: Ba yi niŋi lala, ba nin toaai n‑wulisi pɔ'aseeba ban na bu kurigi la ka ba wuri ba siridima ni ba biisi.

eng: and to be sensible, morally pure, working at home, kind and submissive to their own husbands, so that Gods word wont be ridiculed.
local: Ni, m‑paasi lala, ni ba mari mangbaari ka bɛ kasi, n‑tumni ba yiya tuma suŋŋa, ka sigsiri ba mansi n‑tiri ba siridima. Dinŋɔ zaa nin kyɛ ka sɔ ku maali Naawunni yɛligu maa ansarisi.

eng: Likewise, encourage the younger men to be sensible.
local: Di sumni, ni, i kpamsi na'akyimbisi la gba ka ba mari mangbaari.

eng: in every way. Offer yourself as a role model of good actions. Show integrity, seriousness.
local: Bunseelli kam zaasa puuni i maŋtoori tunveela nin wulisi ba din sum ni ba bɛ seem. I yi wulisiri ba, nyin zaanma deede; ka kyɛ ka i nini m moaai.

eng: and a sound message that is above criticism when you teach, so that any opponent will be ashamed because they wont find anything bad to say about us.
local: ni i yɛttɔ'a zaa bɛ suŋŋa ka sɔ ku toaai n‑galim i. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ban bu sakki i yɛligu maa di vi, dama ba ku nya yɛttɔ'abeoo ni ba ti zaŋŋi n‑galim ti beebu soori.
