eng: Tell slaves to submit to their own masters and please them in everything they do. They shouldnt talk back.
local: Wulisim daaba la gba ka ba sigsiri ba mansi n‑tiri ba dugmadima bunseelikam zaa ni. Wulisim ba ka ba tumni din nin kyɛ ka ba dugmadima nini tigra, ka bu ŋmeeri ba nangbankpeemni.

eng: or steal. Instead, they should show that they are completely reliable in everything so that they might make the teaching about God our savior attractive in every way.
local: Ka ba di zuuri ba dugmadima neema, amaa ba kyɛ ka ba beebu kasigi n‑wulisi, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛdda dima ka zɛ yummu ni yɛlimaŋni. Dina nin kyɛ ka Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti tuligra maa yɛla wulisigu la ti nya gyirima, ban n niŋni bunseelikam zaa zugu.

eng: The grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
local: Dama Naawunni n siri wulisi u wurilim paalu ni, ni, u piinisuma zugu ka sɔkam nin toaai n‑nya tuliginsim.

eng: It educates us so that we can live sensible, ethical, and godly lives right now by rejecting ungodly lives and the desires of this world.
local: U wurilim piini wa lahabaari n wulisiri ti, ni, ti za'asima din bu dɔli Naawunni soori, ni din nyɛ duniya wa kɔribeeri, amaa ka kur mari mangbaari, ka zɛ deede, ka laa m‑mari beebu seelli din dɔli la Naawunni soori, saha kanŋɔna.

eng: At the same time we wait for the blessed hope and the glorious appearance of our great God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Ti niŋma dinŋɔ ka guusiri ti tama paalu yiibu na - Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn ti ya zaŋŋi u gyirima n‑yi paalu ni na nla, ka u nyɛ la ti Naawuntitaari, ni ti Tuligra.

eng: He gave himself for us in order to rescue us from every kind of lawless behavior, and cleanse a special people for himself who are eager to do good actions.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa n daa zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri n‑tisi, ni u yɔlisi tirima ni ti ti leebi u toori niriba maa. Ka u laa n‑yiisi ti tumbeeri kam zaa ni, ka nyeesi ti ka ti leebi kasi, ka ti sufu kur nin boori la ti tumni din veela.

eng: Talk about these things. Encourage and correct with complete authority. Dont let anyone disrespect you.
local: Anŋɔ n nyɛ bunseesi din sumni ni i wulisiri n‑tiri ba. Mari yiiko zaa n‑kpamsiri ba, ka kpaasiri ba zugri ni. Nyin mun di kyɛ ka sɔ gɔsi i yɔri.

eng: Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities. They should be obedient and ready to do every good thing.
local: Teaama i niriba n‑yɛtti ba, ni, ba kyɛ ka ba sufu bɛ nadima ni, ni ba tinkpamma ni, ka wumni ba noori yɛligu ka tumni tunsuma saha kam zaa.

eng: They shouldnt speak disrespectfully about anyone, but they should be peaceful, kind, and show complete courtesy toward everyone.
local: Ka i laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, ba di sa'amni ba kpeaa yuuri, bee, m‑boori zaba; amaa ba mari nangbanyinni ni sɔkam zaa ka sigsiri ba mansi niriba zaa saani.

eng: We were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, and slaves to our desires and various pleasures too. We were spending our lives in evil behavior and jealousy. We were disgusting, and we hated other people.
local: Tirima mun gba daa zi Naawunni yɛla. Ka ti daa niŋni gyɛrilim, ka daa dɔli ti mammaŋŋa sufu yuubu ni kɔribeeri, ka burigri n‑yi sɔsuŋŋu ni. Ti daa za'asi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛligu ka daa sa'amni ti taaba yuya ka laa n‑zabri ba nyuuri. Ka niriba daa yaai ti, ka ti mun gba daa bu boori ba yɛla.

eng: But when God our saviors kindness and love appeared.
local: Amaa Naawunni ti tuligra ŋɔn daa ti wulisi u sufupeelli maa soori, ni u wurilim.

eng: he saved us because of his mercy, not because of righteous things we had done. He did it through the washing of new birth and the renewing by the Holy Spirit.
local: ti daa nya tuliginsim. Di daa pa la ti tunsuma zugu, amaa ŋɔn Naawunni daa zɔ ti namboo zugu. U daa zaŋŋi u Sii Kasi maa n‑niŋi ti ni, ka zaŋŋi u kɔsuŋŋu n‑su ti, nti peaai ti tumbeeri m‑basi, ni ti ti maali n‑dɔ'ai n‑nya nyɔvuri paalli.

eng: which God poured out upon us generously through Jesus Christ our savior.
local: Ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u Sii Kasi kyɛ ti saani na n‑suŋŋi ti halli, ka daa niŋi lala, Yisa Kirisita ti Tuligra zugu.

eng: So, since we have been made righteous by his grace, we can inherit the hope for eternal life.
local: U daa niŋi lala ni u ti deaai ti hoo yɛlimaŋni dima u lala wurilim piini la zugu, ka kyɛ ka ti nin mari tama, ni, ti siri nin di nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga hoo ti faari tɔrli.

eng: This saying is reliable. And I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God might give careful attention to doing good. These things are good and useful for everyone.
local: Yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni ka i nin toaai n‑niŋi ka yɛdda. M boori la i wulisiri yɛla wa zaa ni nimbirimoaa. Ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ninvugseeba ban pun niŋi Naawunni yɛdda la kur nin teesiri tunsuma tumpu yɛla saha kam. Dama lala nin suŋŋi niriba ka niŋi sɔkam zaa anfaani.

eng: Avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, and fights about the Law, because they are useless and worthless.
local: Amaa di kyɛ ka i ba'a bɛ yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama nangbankpeemni wa ni, bee, yaadima yuya dɔlisigu ni, bee zaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi zaaligu kaari sɔya. Anŋɔ zaa ka gbinni, ka bɛ la yɔri.

eng: After a first and second warning, have nothing more to do with a person who causes conflict.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn boori wɔligri muna - kur kpamsim u napɔmbuyi pɔi, ka u yi na za'asira, nyin naayi kyɛli u ka di laa m‑puusiri u.

eng: because you know that someone like this is twisted and sinful—so they condemn themselves.
local: Lala nira maa nyɛ la bungɔliŋŋu ka kur tumni la tumbeeri: u katti la u mammaŋŋa sariya ka lusi u maŋŋa.

eng: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, try to come to me in Nicopolis, because Ive decided to spend the winter there.
local: Tɔ: ka n gbaai la gbaari, ni, n nin kpalim m‑bɛ Nikɔpɔlisi tiŋŋa see ka waari saha ti naai: dizugu n yi ti tum Aritimasi bee Tikyikusi i saani na, nyin kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa ka kyaŋŋi ni i ti paai ma bɛni.

eng: Help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with enthusiasm so that they wont need anything.
local: Kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑suŋŋi Looya Ziinasi ni Apɔloosi ka zaali ba soori, ka di kyɛ ka ba bunseelli ti pɔsigi.

eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: Kur kyɛ ka ti niriba maa ti bamsi tunsuma nimmoaa tumpu, ni ba ti ku ba dabsa zaa bukaata ka ku kpalim m‑bɛ yɔri.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: Baseeba ban bɛ kpa maa puusiri i ni. Ka nyin mun puusima ti zɔdima ban niŋi yɛdda maa zaa n‑ti ma. Naawunni piinisuma bɛ ya zaa saani.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Man Pɔla, ŋɔn daa kpɛ sarika Kirisita Yisa Masiiya yɛla zugu, ni ti mabii Timɔti - ti puusiri i ni. Mani Pɔla n‑sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri nyini Failiimɔn, tin la'am n‑tumni Kirisita Yisa tuma la zugu.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: Ti laa m‑puusiri i pɔ'a Siisita Afiiya, ni i bii Akyipusi, ni Yisa nyaandɔliba ban la'amni i yiri puuni la. Akyipusi gba nyɛ la ŋɔn moori n‑dɔla Yisa suŋŋa.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ti Dugma Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa suŋŋma i ka ti i sufumaasim.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Tɔ: m mabii Failiimɔn, man ŋɔn susiri wunsusigu sahanseaa zaa, n yi puusiri Naawunni pam i zugu.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: Dama m mabiisi yɛtti ma, ni, i wuri ti Dugma Yisa pam, ka laa n‑niŋni u yɛdda, ka boori ban nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba zaa.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: N laa n‑susiri Naawunni, ni, u kyɛ ka nyin ni ban niŋi yɛdda ka la'am n‑gyeemni Naawunni maa zaa ti mari nangbanyinni. Lala maa nin kyɛ ka i baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi bunsunseesi zaa n‑ti ti Kirisita zugu.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: Nyini Failiimɔn n nyɛ m mabisuŋŋu, ka i boori la Naawunni nyaandɔliba zaa yɛla ka kpamsiri ba sufu n‑tiri ba. Lala zugu ka man gba nya sufupeelli ni sufudoaa.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Tɔ: ka man ŋɔn su fukumsi seelli Kirisita tuma zugu, m mari soori ni n ti zaali i noori, ni, i tumni din tuusi.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: Amaa man ŋɔn wuri i la zugu ka n susiri i ni, ni, i suŋŋi ma. Ii: man Pɔla, ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita Yisa kpambari la - halli ka pampaŋŋɔ ba gbaai ma n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpari Yisa yɛla zugu -.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: man Pɔla yi susiri i n‑tiri Ɔneesima. Ŋɔn daa nyɛ i daabri ka zɔ la zugu ka n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri i na. N ya yɛli i, ni, u wum n yɛligu, halli man bɛ la dansarika ni zaa yɔri, ka yi niŋni Yisa yɛdda, di zugu u leebi la hoo m biiya.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: Ŋɔn Ɔneesima yuuri maa gbinni nyɛ la anfaani, amaa u daa ka anfaani n‑ti i; ŋɔn mun yi niŋi tuuba wa, u mari la anfaani n‑tiri mani ni nyini zaa.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: Di zugu ka man Pɔla tumni u, ni u labi i saani na, halli ni n wuri u pampam.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: Halli m boori u kpalim n saani n‑tumni Naawunni tuma maa; lala ka u laa n‑zɛ i zaazii ni n‑suŋŋni ma, ban daa kpari ma lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu la.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: Amaa m bu boori ni n niŋi seelli, see ka nyin mammaŋŋa baŋŋi di gbinni. I yi ti baŋŋi, i nin tum din tuusi i toori sufu yuubu zugu, pa la man zaali i lala maa zugu.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: Di yi pa seem ka lala zugu ka ŋɔn daa zɔ ka kyɛ i beela maa: ni, u yi ti labi na, i nin mari u halli saha din ka tɔriga.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ u yi leebi la ti mabiwuttigu, u laa m‑pa daabri, ka lala veela n‑gaari zaa. U nyɛ la m mabiiya, amaa see ka nyin ni ŋɔn lɔ la mabiitiri. N‑gaari lala, ti wuri u hoo dɔsuŋŋu, ka laa n‑wuri u hoo ti Dugma biiya m‑paasi.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Man ni nyin la'am n‑tumni Naawunni tuma. Di zugu deaama u hoo nyin deaai ma seem maa.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: U yi sa'am la i bunseelli kam, bee, u di la i samni, nyin yɛlima ma ka n yɔa.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: I bu nya? - man Pɔla n sɔbri wa n toori nuu ni: man nin yɔ u samni. Halli man yɛligu ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka i niŋi Naawunni yɛdda maa, ka di zugu i pun di n samni i maŋtoori nyɔvuri zugu.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Di zugu kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka n nya anfaani dinŋɔ zugu: kur kyɛ ka n sufu maai, dama ti zaa nyɛ la Yisa nyaandɔliba.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: N sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri i, dama m baŋŋiya, ni, i nin wum n noori wa, halli ka tum n‑gaari man yɛtti seem wa.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Din paasi, n susiri i ni, ni, i maalima duu siri n‑zaali ma. Dama m mari tama, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba yiisi ma n‑ti ya na, dama ya susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ma.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Ɛpafraasi gba puusiri i ni. Ŋɔn gba ka ba daa kpari duu, Kirisita Yisa zugu.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: Maaki, Arisitaakusi, Diimasi, ni Luuki dima zaa puusiri i ni. Man ni ban n la'amni n‑tumni Yisa tuma maa.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya piinisuma bɛ ya sufu zaa ni.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Sahanseaa din gaari la, Naawunni daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ti yaadima la; u daa niŋi lala anabidima nɔya ni, dama ba niriba pam daa tɔ'asiri ba saha ba saha, ni zii baligubaligu.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: Amaa zuna sahanseaa din baasi nyaaŋŋa wa ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ti la u Bii maa noori ni. U Bii maa ka u daa gaŋŋi, ni duniya wa naari saha yi ti paai na, u nin ti leebi bunseelli kam faditta, dama ŋɔn kɔbri ni gba ka Naawunni daa naam duniya zaa.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: Bii maa n wulisiri Naawunni gyirima ŋɔn ŋmanni seem la; ka na mari Naawunni mammaŋŋa beebu, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ seem la, ŋɔn gba bɛ la lala. U yɛligu kan mari yiiko pam maa ka u zaŋŋni m‑mari bunseelli kam. Ŋɔn daa ti maali soori ni daadam nya ala'akyi kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi n‑naai, u daa zinni la Naawunni Ŋɔn Galisi maa nudirigu zizii maa arizana ni.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Dinzugu Naawunni daa zaali u la zaazititaari ni, n‑gaari malaakadima; kaman ŋɔn daa pun ti u yuuri kan gaari ba yuya seem la.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Naawunni daa yɛli Yisa, ni#:- Nyini n nyɛ m biiya; Zuna n leebi i ba. m‑paasi yaasa: Naawunni daa yɛli Yisa, ni#:- N nin leebi i ba, Ka i mun leebi m biiya. - U daa na mi n‑yɛli malaakasɔ lala? - Ay, halli beela!.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Din paasi yaasa, Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti yɛ n‑tum u bikpeema maa ni u kyɛ duniya wa ni na, u daa yɛliya, ni#:- Ya kyɛ ka Naawunni malaakadima zaa ti gyeem u.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Ka ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri malaakadima maa yɛla maa, u daa yɛli la nŋɔa#:- U maali u malaakadima maa la hoo pɛsiŋŋu, Ka kyɛ ka u tuumudima bɛ hoo bugum peelim.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Bii maa yɛla muna ka u daa yɛli, ni#:- Naawunni, i naam zizii la kpalimna, Ka nin bɛ ni halli ni saha kam. I naam zangbeaa bɛ la i nuu ni - Sɔsuŋŋu ni yɛlimaŋni naam zangbeaa!.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: Nyin wuri din tuusi Ka yaai din bu tuusi. Di zugu ka Naawunni pun diisi i naam - Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ i Dugma maa - Ka i sufupeelim gaari i taaba dinni zaa.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: U naa n‑yɛli yaasa#:- Piiligu maa ni, n Dugma, Ka nyini n daa nyeebi duniya wa; Sangbana la gba nyin, Naawunni, daa maali.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: Anŋɔ zaa nin ti gaari, Amaa nyin kur ya kpalim m‑bɛ ni; A zaa nin ti kpi Hoo sutura ŋɔn kpiiri seem la.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: Ka i nin ti kpabi a hoo kparikurigu la, Ka a takki hoo kparigu ŋɔn takkri seem maa. Amaa nyin kur nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala ka ka takkri, I mun kur ka naari.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Tɔ: ka malaakadima maa ni bandima ka Naawunni daa yɛli lala? Bee, n‑yɛli, ni#:- Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, Ni, Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni, Halli ka n zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni. Ay! - ba sɔ ka u daa bu yɛli lala!.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Di zugu malaakadima maa yi dee nyɛ la bɔa? - Ba nyɛ la siseeba ban gyeemni Naawunni ka laa n‑nyɛ ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn tumni seeba ka ba ti suŋŋni u niriba - ninvugseeba ban ti ya nya tiliginsim.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Dinzugu di kpa la talasi ka ti sufu kpalim m‑bɛ tin daa pun wum yɛlimanseelli la suŋŋa; din nin kyɛ ka bunseelli ku zaŋŋi ti m‑miligi ka kyɛ di.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Dama yɛttɔ'aseesi malaakadima ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi la daa zɛ la deede ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ŋɔnkam daa bu dɔli a, bee, n‑deaai zaaligu la, di daama siri daa nya tubdarigri din sumni u.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: Ka dinŋɔna wula ka tirima nin toaai n‑zɔ n‑yi tubdarigri maa ni, ti ba'a yi ka tiliginsim din galisi wa ni? Ti Dugma mammaŋŋa n daa daŋŋi m‑mooli tiliginsim wa yɛla, ka baseeba ban daa wum lala daa wulisi ti, ni, di siri nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Ka Naawunni gba daa zaŋŋi makkri tuma ni alahazibu tuntumsa n‑di lala sɛhara, ni alahazibu tuma baligu pam, ka daa tɔri Sii Kasi piini la, hoo u yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Pa malaakadima ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi duniyaseaa kan ti ya kyɛ na suubu fukumsi n‑niŋi ba nuusi ni: lala duniya la n nyɛ tin tɔ'asiri seaa yɛla wa.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Amaa sɔ n daa sɔbi Naawunni yɛligu luginseaa ni, ka yɛli, ni#:- Ti Dugma Naawunni, ninsaala nyɛ la bɔa? - Halli ka i teesiri u yɛla? Bee, daadam bii dee n‑nyɛ la bɔa? - Ka i ba'a bɛ u ni?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: I daa naam u ka u gyirima poaai malaakadima gyirima, Ka di niŋi lala saha beela: Amaa ka daa kyɛ ka u mari pɛ'abu ni gyirima pam.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: Ka zaŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuusi ni. Ni, Naawunni zaŋŋni bunseelli kam zaa n‑niŋni ninsaala fukumsi ni maa; tɔ - u daa bu kyɛ halli bunni yinni din wulisi, ni, ŋɔn ninsaala bu su di. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ti bu nya ka ninsaala su bunseelli kam zaa fukumsi pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Kulla Yisa ka ti nya, ka u siri daa poaai malaakadima saha beela, ni u ti di wahala n‑kpi. Ka ti laa n‑nya u, ka u mari gyirima pampaŋŋɔa. Dama Naawunni wurilim zugu ka u daa sakki, ni, u ti baŋŋi kum toom ŋɔn nyɛ seem, n‑niŋi lala daadam kam zugu.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa naam bunseelli kam, ka u mari bunseelli kam: ka mun daa yɛnni, ni, u nin zaŋŋi biisi pam ni ba ti la'am n‑nya gyirima. Di zugu di daa tuusiya ni u kyɛ ka Yisa bɛ beesikasi zaa ka ka galiŋŋu, wahala diibu puuni. Dama ŋɔn Yisa nyɛ la biisi wa toondaana ŋɔn ziiri ba n‑taasiri tiliginsim ziiya.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka niriba leebi la Naawunni niriba m‑bɛ kasi, ŋɔn ni ŋɔn suŋŋni seeba lala maa - ba zaa la'am m‑mari ba dɔ'am yinni. Di zugu ka Yisa bu zɔri vi ni u booli ba, ni, u mabiisi.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: Dama ŋɔn daa yɛli n‑ti Naawunni seem nŋɔa#: N nin mooli i yuuri maa n‑ti m mabiisi maa; Ka yuum yuuma m‑pɛ'ai i, ti la'aŋŋu puuni.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli, N nin zaŋŋi n yɛdda zaa n‑niŋi u ni. Ka laa n‑yɛli yaasa, ni, Nnya ma! - Man ni biseeba nyin zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Ka biisi maa ŋɔn nyɛ saalidima m‑mari ningbiŋŋu ni ziim la zugu, ŋɔn Yisa gba daa leebi ba taabu, ka ŋɔn ni ban la'am m‑mari ningbiŋŋu yinni. U daa niŋi la lala ni u kum zugu ti kyɛ ka u sa'am Sintaani ŋɔn mari kum yiiko la.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: Ka lala ka u daa yiisi daaba maa m‑basi - baseeba ban daa kpalim m‑bɛ daabtiri ni ba beebu zaa, kum dabeem zugu la.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: Di pa la malaakadima maa zugu ka u daa niŋi lala, ni u ti suŋŋi bana, amaa Ibrahim ya'ansi ka u daa suŋŋi.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka di daa kpa talasi ni u bɛ kaman u mabiisi ŋɔn bɛ seem seelikam puuni. Dina n nin kyɛ ka u toaai n‑leebi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaansɔ ŋɔn zɔri niriba namboo, ka zɛ la yummu u tuma tumpu ni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni saani; ka naa n‑kyɛ ka u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑zɔri sindaani n‑kpiisiri niriba tumbeeri taalli ni ba ti nya ala'akyi kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Ka ŋɔn mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa di wahala u zamsigu nyaabu puuni zugu, u nin toaai n‑suŋŋi ninvugseeba ban nyari zamsigu pampaŋŋɔa.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: M mabiisi, ya nyɛ la Naawunni dinni ka nyɛ kasi; ka ya gba ka Naawunni daa booli ka ya sakki. Tɔ! - ya teaama sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ti toondaana yɛla - di nyɛ la: sɔ tin sakki u yɛla, ni, ti tuumu n nyɛ u, ni ti gyeeŋŋu toondaana - Yisa nla.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: U daa zaani yummu n‑ti Ŋɔn yiisi u n‑zaali maa, kaman Musa gba ŋɔn daa zaani yummu u Naawunni gyeeŋŋu duu puuni tuma ni seem la.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Amaa Masiiya nyɛ la ŋɔn sumsi ni u mari gyirima n‑gaari Musa, kaman tammeera ŋɔn mari gyirima n‑gaari yiri seaa ŋɔn mɛ seem la.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Yiri kam mari la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mɛ ka, amaa Naawunni n nyɛ ŋɔn mɛ bunseelli kam zaa.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Ŋɔn Musa daa zaani yummu hoo kpambari Naawunni yiri maa zaa ni, bee, hoo wuddaana, ni u ti niŋi sɛhara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tɔ'asi yɛliseesi la.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Yisa Masiiya mun bɛ la Naawunni yiri ni hoo yiddaana biiya, ka tirima muna - ti yi ti mari sufukpeemni ni nimbirimoaa - tirima mun nyɛ la hoo yiri dima maa, see ka ti laa n‑niŋi tin mari tamaseaa maa yɛdda, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi di baasigu.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Dizugu ka Naawunni Sii Vuusim Kasi teaari n‑yɛtta#:- Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni, Kaman ya Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa mari seem, n‑nya galinsi yoo maa ni sahanseaa la, Yan daa zamsi ma la zugu.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Bɛni ka ya yaadima daa zamsi ma, Halli ni ba daa kur nya man Naawunni tuma maa zaa yɔri -.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: N tuma ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem yuumpisinaasi puuni la. Di zugu n daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi ba zamaani maa ni Halli nti yɛli ba yɛla, ni, Ba sufu teesa ni ka ba kur burigri ni, Ka za'asiri n sɔya dɔligu.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Lala zugu ka n sufu daa yisigi, Ka n daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli#: Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya man saani! .

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: Tɔ: m mabiisi, yan di kyɛ ka ya sɔ mari teesibeeri u sufu ni an birisira ka za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu; lala nin kyɛ ka u yi Naawunni nyaaŋŋa - Naawunni sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Amaa sahanseaa Naawunni Sii Kasi ŋɔn boonni, ni, zuna dabsiri wa la, ya kpamsima taaba sufu dabsiri kam, ka di kyɛ ka ya sɔsɔ mari tuppuwumni ka dɔli tumbeeri yɔmsigu yɛla. -.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Dama ti yi ti kpalim m‑mari tin daa daŋŋi m‑mari tamaseaa la halli nti paai seelikam baasigu maa, lala nin kyɛ ka ti nyɛ la baseeba ban la'am m‑bɛ Masiiya saani, ka ti zaa teesa nyɛ yummu. -.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Hoo tin na pun yɛli seem la, ni#:- Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa, Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni, Kaman ya Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa mari seem, .

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Tɔ, ka bandima n‑lee n‑nyɛ ban n daa wum Naawunni kukɔri maa ka daa mari tuppuwumni? - Pa Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi ninvugseeba n‑yi Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni na zaa la nla?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Ka bandima zugu ka Naawunni daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi ba, yuumpisinaasi puuni la? - Pa ninvugseeba ban daa tum tumbeeri la nla? - halli ka ba daa kpi m‑basi yɔri, yoo maa ni.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Ka bandima ka Naawunni daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli ba, ni, Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya man saani.? - Pa ninvugseeba ban daa mari tuppuwumni maa nla?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Tɔ: ka dizugu ti nya ka di nyɛ la ba yɛdda za'asigu zugu ka ba daa bu kpɛ lala vuusim zii maa ni.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Dizugu vuusim ziseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi ka nangbambasiri maa, lala nangbambasiri maa na kpalim n‑zɛya: dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ti mari ti mansi ka ti sɔ ku kɔŋŋi vuusim zii maa paari na.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa daa paai tirim gba saani na, kaman di daa paai ban saani seem la. Tɔ, ban daa wum lahabaseaa daa bu ku ba bukyi, dama ban ŋɔn daa wum maa, ba daa bu niŋi di yɛdda.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Amaa tirima ban pun niŋi yɛdda la kpɛ vuusim zii maa Naawunni saani. Lala zugu ka u yɛliya, ni#:- Lala zugu ka n sufu daa yisigi, Ka n daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli#: Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya n saani. Naawunni n daa yɛli lala, halli ŋɔn daa pun naam duniya wa, ka naai u tuma maa.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Dama dabsaayɔpɔi daari yɛla ka Naawunni pun yɛli, Litaafi puuni ya'aseaa, ni, Naawunni daa vuusi u tuma zaa puuni dabsaayɔpɔi daari.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya n saani.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Di zugu vuusim zii maa nyaabu na kpalim n‑guri ninvugseeba ni ba ti vuusi, amaa ban daa tuui n‑wum lala lahabasuŋŋu maa daa bu paai vuusim ziiya Naawunni saani, ban daa bu niŋi yɛdda zugu.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Ka Naawunni daa laa n‑zaali dabsiseaa kan boonni, ni, zuna dabsiri wa; ka di daa yuui pɔi, ka u daa kyɛ ka NaaDauda daa yuum n‑yɛli - hoo tin na pun yɛli seem la - ni#:- Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa, Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Gyɔsuuwa yi siri daa kyɛ ka Naawunni niriba nya vuusimaŋni, Naawunni naa n‑ku laa n‑tɔ'asi vuusim dabsiri yɛla yaasa.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Lala zugu, vuusim dabsiri na kur zɛ ni n‑guri Naawunni niriba ni ba vuusi.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Ŋɔnkam kpɛ vuusim ni Naawunni saani pun kyɛ u tuma ka vuusira - hoo Naawunni mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa tum u tuma n‑naai ka vuusi seem la.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti kpaŋŋi ti maŋŋa m‑paai vuusim ziiya Naawunni saani, ka di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ dɔli la banŋɔ tuppuwumni soori nti kɔŋŋi lala vuusim maa, u yɛdda kɔŋŋsim zugu.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Naawunni yɛligu mari la nyɔvuri, ka tumni ka tuma pam. Di ŋmani la suuwa, ka diri n‑gaari takoobi kan mari nɔya ayi; ka ŋmaari ni n‑wɔligri ninsaala sii ni u ningbiŋŋu ziiya, ka laa m‑paari u kɔbtuga ni kɔba kpaam. Lala ka Naawunni yɛligu siri viisiri m‑baŋŋni ninsaala sufu boorim ni u sufu ni teesa zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Bunseelli ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑sɔ'ai Naawunni saani, dama duniya yɛla ni buna kam zaa dɔ la u tooni, ka ti zaa nin ti vii ti beesigu m‑bulisi u.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Tɔ: ka Naawunni Bii Yisa ŋɔn nyɛ ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la n ya kyɛ ka ti gyi ti yɛdda tin sakki seaa maa nuusi ayi. Ti Toondaana wa n galisi, ka du arizana n‑gaari n‑kpɛa.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana wa pa la sɔ ŋɔn zi ti tariŋŋu ni ti kyɔ'ansi zaa - Ay! - u nin toaai n‑zɔ ti namboo dama u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn daa nya zamsigu balibu kam zaa, amaa u daa bu tum ala'akyi halli beela!.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti niŋi sufukpeemni n‑vuui m‑pɔri Naawunni naam ziziiya, dama ŋɔna n nyɛ alibarika daana ka tisiri u piinisuma yɔri. Lala nin kyɛ ka u zɔ ti namboo. Ka laa n‑niŋi ti alibarika din nin toaai n‑suŋŋi ti sahaseaa tin boori suŋŋsim zaa.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana zaa nyɛ la ban yiisi sɔ u ninsaalidima ni n‑diisi, ni u ti gyeemni Naawunni ka zɔri sindaani ba tumbeeri zugu, ka maanni sara n‑kɔrigri bunkɔbri di zugu.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: U nin toaai m‑mari ban bu mi soori, ni ban bɔrigri, la ni sugru, dama u gba nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn ka kpeeŋŋu.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka di mugsi ni u maanni la sara tumbeeri zugu n‑tiri u niriba maa, ka laa m‑maanni u maŋtoori tumbeeri zugu.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Sɔsɔ bu diisiri u maŋŋa lala tuma maa: Naawunni booligu kɔkɔ n nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka nira leebi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana - hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli Aruuna n‑diisi u seem la.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Lala zugu ka Yisa daa bu wukki u maŋŋa ni u leebi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana. Amaa di daa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn daa yɛli u, ni#:- I nyɛ la m biiya: Zuna ka n leebi i ba.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Ka laa n‑yɛli ya'aseaa yaasa, ni#:- I nin di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana nti bɛ ni saha kam, Ka di dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa daa puusiri Naawunni: u duniya wa beebu saha u daa susiri wunsusigu ni kunkuma ni nintam. U daa yɛtti, ni, Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑tuligi u kum ni. Ka Naawunni daa wum n‑ti u, dama u daa sigsi u maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: U daa nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, amaa di zaa yɔri, u daa di wahala ka lala daa kyɛ ka u bamsi u Ba noori deaari.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: Ŋɔn daa ti naai seelikam, u daa ti leebi la tiliginsim kan ka naari daana n‑ti seeba ban sakki u yɛligu la.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Naawunni mun daa yiisi u, ni u ti di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana n‑dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem la.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Yɛla pam ka ti mara ni ti ti tɔ'asi n‑ti ya, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa yɛla; amaa di tɔ pam ni ti ti tɔ'asi di gbinni dama ya bu deaari yɛligu yooma.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Di yuuya ni: di da sum ka ya nyɛ la karimbadima pampaŋŋɔa, amaa ya na boori ka sɔ laa n‑wulisi ya Naawunni yuubu yɛla piiligu, ni daŋŋni yɛttɔ'aseesi ban na pun niŋni tuuba la ŋɔn bamsiri maa. Ya boori la bisim, pa la diibu din tibsa.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Ya bu nya? - ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ bisim zugu na bu mi din tuusi, dama u nyɛ la bileaa.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Amaa diibu din tibsa nyɛ la bunkura diibu, ka ban yam baŋŋi yɛlisuma ni yɛlibeeri n‑gamsiri la dinni.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Dizugu ti kyɛm tooni ka kyɛ ban daa piili n‑wulisi ti yɛliseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa Masiiya yɛla maa, yɛliseesi an bu tɔ la. Ya kyɛ ka ti beebu ŋmanni la bunkura dinni ka bu laa n‑dɔli ti yɛdda piiligu yɛla, hoo ti beebu takkri, tin daa yi ti yɛliyirima sahanseaa la, bee, tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi Naawunni yɛdda seem la.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: Lala piiligu yɛla sunsukkuni bɛ la kpaaŋŋu din wulisiri tin nin peaai Naawunni koom seem, bee, ban zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ninvugseeba ban niŋi tuuba la zugu seem, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba ban nin ti yisigi kum ni, ka Naawunni nin ti kari ba sariyaseaa kan ku naai halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Tɔ; Naawunni yi ti sakki, ti nin niŋi lala n‑kyɛ anŋɔ zaa.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Dama ya teesima ninvugseeba ban pun wum lahabaari maa yɛla ka ba pun lam m‑baŋŋi Naawunni yiri nya'asim din nyɛ seem, ka yi leebi la seeba ban la'am n‑deaai Vuusim Kasi maa Naawunni saani. Ba laa m‑baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu maa nya'asim suŋŋa, ni yiikoseesi an yi la sahaseaa kan kyɛnni na ni gba. Lala niriba yi ti mi neesim yɛla wa zaa pɔi ka naayi za'asi Naawunni yɛdda m‑basi, soori ka ni ni ba nin toaai m‑maali n‑niŋi tuuba yaasa. Ban za'asiri lala maa, ba laa m‑maali n‑kpaasiri Naawunni Bii n‑tabinni dɔpulli zugu yaasa, ka diisiri u vi paalu ni.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Ya bu nya? - saa yi miiri tingbanseaa zugu saha kam, ka ba koori ka ka ba bumbula niŋni suŋŋa n‑tiri kukpaariba maa, lala tingbanni nyari la kasuma Naawunni saani.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Amaa puseaa kan wali goosi bee wubligu an ka fa'ari, lala puu ka Naawunni kabeeri bɛ ka ni, ka ka baasigu nyɛ la: bugum di ka.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Ti tɔ'asiri lala zaa yɔri, ti mabiisi, dama ti mi suŋŋa, ni, di pa lala ya saani; amaa yarima n niŋni yɛdda ni yɛliseesi an mari tiliginsim.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Dama Naawunni ka putoo din nin kyɛ ka u nini yɔ'ali ya tuma ni ya wurilim ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni, ya daa gyeemni Naawunni niriba ka suŋŋni ba, halli ka ya na kpalim n‑suŋŋni ba yaasa.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Ti boori ni ya zaa yinniyinni nin kpalim n‑tumni pupeelim tuma lala ni nimbirimoaa, ka laa m‑baŋŋi tamaseaa tin mara, ka niŋi yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑wa saha naari.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Ya yi ti niŋi lala, ya ku tumni gbinnya'ari. Ay! - ti bu boori lala. Amaa ya nin dɔli ninvugsuma soori, ninvugseeba ban daa niŋi yɛdda ni sugru, halli nti di faariseaa Naawunni ŋɔn pun pɔ noori, ni, u nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ti la.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Tɔ: ka Naawunni ŋɔn daa boori ni u ti pɔ noori m‑basi nangbambasiri n‑ti Ibrahim, ni, u daa pɔ u mammaŋŋa yuuri - dama sɔsɔ ka ni ka u yuuri galisi n‑gaari ŋɔn Naawunni dinni, ni u ti zaŋŋi lala yuuri m‑pɔa.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: M‑basi nangbambasiri n‑yɛli, ni, N nin zaŋŋi bunsuma zaa n‑ti i, ka kyɛ ka i niriba zoaari ni pampam.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Ka Ibrahim ŋɔn daa niŋi sugru seem maa n daa kyɛ ka u naayi di nangbampɔri yɛla maa.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Tɔ; ninsaalidima yi ti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni, ba ninvugsɔ yi ti ya pɔ noori, bunseelli kan gaari u ka u nin ti pɔ n‑wulisi u yɛdda, ka lala ka yɛttɔ'a maa naaya.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Naawunni sufu ŋɔn daa ti bɛ ni, ni, u ti kyɛ ka Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa dima maa baŋŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa, ni, u sufu yuubu bɛ la nangbambasiri maa ni, ka di yɛla ku ti takki halli saha kan ka tariga; dizugu u daa zaŋŋi u nangbampɔri m‑pɔa.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Dinŋɔna yɛla ayi wa m bɛ ni, seesi an ku toaai n‑takki, ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi a n‑wulisi ti, yɛliseesi Naawunni ku ma'ai ziri a zugu; lala nin kyɛ ka ti mari sufukpeemni ti tama puuni. Dama ti pun zɔ n‑kyɛ duniya yɛlibeeri maa nti deaai tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑zaali ti tooni la.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tama kanŋɔ n wulisiri ti nyɔvuya ŋɔn ku baasi yɔri seem, ka ka mari yɛdda ka bu damni halli beela; hoo miseaa ban zaŋŋi n‑loori nyariŋŋu miri ka ka damna. Ti tama maa ŋmanni la hoo miseaa kan ku lɔrigi, dama ba zaŋŋi ka n‑lɔ luginsuŋŋu ni, hoo dusunseaa kan nyɛ Naawunni Beebu Zikasi la, ka ka zɛ Yɛhudiya dima Gyeeŋŋu Zii maa ni, pateesa maa nyaaŋŋa sa.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Ti zugu ka Yisa daa daŋŋi ti n‑kpɛ lala dukasi maa ni. Soori ka ni ni sɔsɔ ku ti kpɛ, di yi pa la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima; amaa ŋɔn Yisa di la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ka ku naai, ka di dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori la.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Tin tɔ'asiri seem maa yɛlikpanni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya wa: ti mari la ti lala Gyeeŋŋu Toondaansɔa, ka u pun la'am n‑zini Naam ziiya, arizana ni.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: Ka u tumni ti Dugma ziziiya Gyeeŋŋu Duu ni, Sugri maa puuni - sugseaa ti Dugma mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti, ka di pa la saalisɔ ŋɔn daa ti la.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Dama Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana zaa tumni nyɛ la: u zaŋŋni piini m‑maali, ka lala zugu ka di sum ka ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana wa gba mari u bunseelli din nin tisi.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: U yi da bɛ duniya wa ni, u da naa n‑ku nyɛ halli maalimaanna gba, dama maalimaanniba pun bɛ ni m‑maanni maaligu hoo zaaligu maa ŋɔn wulisi seem la.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Ban daa mari gyeeŋŋu ziseaa kan daa kur ŋmani hoo bummaŋseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa wulisi ba la. Lala gyeeŋŋu Sugri mun bɛ la saazugu, Naawunni saani. Dama Musa ŋɔn daa ya n‑ti Sugri maa, u daa nya kpaaŋŋu, ni, Gɔsim suŋŋa ka Sugri maa zaa ti hoo nyin daa nya seem zoori zugu la.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Yisa yi deaai la gyeeŋŋu tunseesi an gaari banŋɔ dina. Ka lala maa zugu n kyɛ ka ŋɔna n zɔri sindaani Naawunni saani ni u ti zaali nangbanyinni zaalipaalli, ka u lala zaaligu maa gba gaari ba dinni, ni ka nangbampɔri la zaa.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Dama zaalikurigu la yi daa ka taalli, di naa n‑ku mugsi ni seaa takki.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Naawunni mun daa nya ba taalli, ŋɔn daa yɛli ba, ni#:- Dabsa maa kyɛnni na - Ti Dugma n yɛtti lala - Ka n nin ti zaali nangbanyinni zaalipaalli, Ka man ni Izraɛli dima dɔli ka, Ni Gyuuda yiri dima gba.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: Ka ku ti ŋmani nangbanyinni zaaliseaa Man daa mari ni ba yaadima maa. Dabsiri seaa ka n daa gbaai ba nuu n‑yiisi ba Igyipti tingbaŋŋu na la, Lala dabsiri maa ka n daa zaali ka. Amaa ba na bu kpalim n‑niŋi ka yɛdda Ka dizugu - ti Dugma daa yɛliya - n sufu daa ka ba ni. .

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Ni#:- Dinŋɔ ka n nin basi noori n‑ti ba, ni, n nin suŋŋi ba dabsa an kyɛnni na: - lala ka ti Dugma daa yɛli - Ni, N nin zaŋŋi n zaaligu yɛla n‑sɔ'ai ba sufu ni; Di ya n‑niŋi hoo man zaŋŋi la bunni n‑sɔbi n‑niŋi a zaa ba sufu ni seem la. N nin leebi ba Naawunni, Ka ba gba leebi n niriba.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Ba ku laa n‑wulisiri taaba, bee, ba yɛtti taaba, ni, Baŋŋma ti Dugma la. Dama ba zaa nin ti mi ma, Ba tarimma zaŋŋi m‑paai zuguraandima.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Dama n nin kyɛ ba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi, Ka ku teaai ba tumbeeri maa yaasa. .

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Ŋɔn tɔ'asiri nangbanyinni seaa, ni, bumpaalli maa - tɔ, di wulisiya, ni, daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni la kurigiya ni, ka ku laa n‑yuui.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni zaaligu la muna - halli kan gba daa mari la kyisa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gyeeŋŋu soori, ka laa m‑mari duniya wa ni wumpuusigu ziiya.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Dama suga ka ba daa ti, ka ba daa vii pateesa n‑kpɛ sugseaa: ka Sugri wa puuni bunni daa bɛ ni ka ba daa zaŋŋi firila ayɔpɔi n‑tɔm ka zugu; bɛni ka Naawunni boroboro gba daa bɛ ni, ni a teebuli maa. Sugri wa ka ba daa boonni ziseaa kan tabli Naawunni Beebu Ziiya.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Ka pateesa ayi daana daa bɛ ni, ka ba daa vii n‑gaari ka nyaaŋŋa yaasa, n‑kpɛ Sugseaa. Bɛni muna ka ba daa boonni Ti Dugma Beebu Ziiya.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Sugri wa daa mari salima teebuli, ban daa nyɔ tikubsim turaali seaa zugu; ni nangbanyinni yɛla adakka gba, ka ba zaŋŋi salima n‑tabli ka nyaaŋŋa zaa. Adakka wa puuni ka salima dukku kan mari Manna diibu la daa bɛ ni, ni Aruuna zangbɛseaa kan daa pusi vaari la; m‑paasi kuglama, nangbanyinni yɛla ŋɔn daa sɔbi m‑pa seesi ni la - anŋɔ zaa daa bɛ la adakka maa puuni.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Adakka wa zugu ka buna ayi daa tɔm, ban daa maali seesi ni a ti ŋmani bunvuseesi ban boonni Kyɛruppa la, ka a pɛ'abu gaari zaa; ka a wulisiri, ni, Naawunni gyirima bɛ ba saani; Kyɛruppa wa kpunkpama lɔŋni ka Naawunni namboo zii daa bɛ ni. Amaa ti ku toaai n‑wulisi anŋɔ zaa gbina yinniyinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem pampaŋŋɔ.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Ban daa sɛ'asi lala n‑naai, di daa kyɛ ka maalimaanniba kpeeri daŋdaŋ sugri la ni, n‑tumni ba sahakam tuma.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Amaa sugseaa kan pa abayi la puuni ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana kɔkɔ n daa naa n‑toaai n‑kpɛa; n‑kpɛ bɛni yimmu yuumni ni. Ka daa ka soori ni u kpɛ ka bu mari ziim na; ka u yiisi lala ziim n‑teaari tumbeeri yɛla - u mammaŋŋa ni niriba ŋɔn bu mi ka tum tumbɛseesi la.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Vuusim Kasi maa daa wulisiya, ni, soori daa ka ni ka ba daa naa n‑dɔli n‑kpeeri ti Dugma maa saani, sahanseaa sugseesi ban daa daŋŋi n‑ti la ŋɔn daa na kpalim n‑zɛya maa.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Dinŋɔ wulisiri zuna yɛla ŋɔn bɛ seem la, ni, ba lala maaligu ni ba piini tisibu zaa ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka gyeengyeemniba maa sufu ni teesa di galimni ba.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Amaa di wulisiri la bundira, ni bunnyuura, ni koom suubu deaari: ningbiŋŋu bunkyisira anŋɔ taabu daa zaani halli ka saha ti paai ka yɛlli kam niŋi suŋŋa n‑naai.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Amaa Yisa daa kyɛ na hoo Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana, ka di nyɛ la yɛlisuma la zugu - yɛliseesi an pun bɛ ni la. Dinzugu u daa dɔlisi Sugri kan gaari suga zaa; Sugri wa pa la ban zaŋŋi nuusi n‑ti seaa - halli ka pa la duniya wa dinni, halli beela.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Sugri wa ka u daa dɔli n‑kpɛ ti Dugma Beebu Ziiya. U mun daa bu mari la buusi bee na'abisi ziim n‑kpɛa, amaa u daa mari la u toori ziim, dama u daa pun nya mansuulinsi n‑ti niriba, ka ba ku laa n‑leebi daaba yaasa.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Dama ba zaŋŋi buusi ziim ni nigi ziim m‑miisi n‑niŋi ninvugseeba ban sa'am kyisigu la, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ba ningbina niŋi kasi.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Dinŋɔna Yisa Kirisita ŋɔn daa ka galimni ka zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni hoo ban maanni bunkɔbri seem maa, u lala maaligu gaari ban dinni. Naawunni Siiya daa sakki, ni, u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi; ka u tuma wa kyɛ ka ti sufu ni teesa bu laa n‑galimni ti ti tumbeeri zugu, tunseesi an daa kuuri ti la. Ka ti naayi baŋŋi n‑gyeemni Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka u leebi la nangbanyinni zaalipaalli la sindaanzɔtta, ni u ti kyɛ ka ban booli seeba la nya nangbampɔri seaa kan faari m‑basira, ka bu naara. Dama kum daa kuya, ka u daa da ba n‑ti ba mansuulinsi, ni ba ku laa n‑niŋi daaba n‑ti tumbeeri an daa bɛ daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni zaaligu la ni.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi faari diibu, see ka ba nya kum sɛhara pɔi ka naayi tisi.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Kum nyaaŋŋa ka ba diri faari; nira yi na bu kpi, faari diibu ku toaai m‑bɛ ni.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Nangbanyinni zaalikurigu la gba daa bu zaani deede, di yi pa la ba daa yiisi ziim.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Dama Musa ŋɔn daa ti yɛli niriba maa nangbanyinni zaaligu maa yɛla, hoo Litaafi maa ŋɔn wulisi seem la, u daa zaŋŋi la nigi ni buusi ziim, ni koom, ka zaŋŋi hisɔppu tivaari soo ni gaarizeaa, m‑miisi gbanseaa kan m mari zaaligu dima la, ka miisi niriba maa gba.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: Ka yɛtta, Ziim wa nyɛ la ban yiisi seebu n‑kyɛ ka nangbanyinni zaalipaalli zaani - zaaliseaa Naawunni ŋɔn ti ya, ni, ya dɔla.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: U daa laa n‑zaŋŋi ziim maa m‑miisi n‑niŋi Sugri la, ni bunseesi ka ba daa mari n‑tumni ka tuma la gba.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, zaaligu maa zugu yɛla pam leebi yɛlisuma ziim maa zugu, ka ziim maa yi daa bu yiisi, taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi daa ka ni Naawunni saani.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Dama buna wa ka ba daa maali ni a ti ŋmani arizana ni buna la. Ka di daa tu ni ba mari a m‑maanni a zugu, ni ba ti kyɛ ka a nyɛ kasi. Ka dinŋɔna arizana buna mammansi boori la maaligu kan gaari anŋɔa.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Dama Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ gyeeŋŋu ziiya halli n‑ti paai gyeeŋŋu nimbirimoaa zii maa, ka daa pa la ninsaalidima ŋɔn maali ya'aseaa, ka laa m‑pa ban gɔsi bunseesi an nyɛ la bummaŋa n‑zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑maali buna an nin ti ŋmani lala. Ninsaala ŋɔn maali bunseaa la n kur wulisiri bummaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Ay! - u daa kpɛ la arizana ni, ni u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi Naawunni ti zugu.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Di mun pa hoo Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ŋɔn tumni seem la. Dama u kpeeri n‑kpeeri Naawunni Beebu Ziiya yuumni kam, ka kur mari ziim bun pa u toori ziim. Ay! ŋɔn Yisa bu kpeeri arizana ni n‑zaŋŋni u maŋŋa n‑yiisiri sara sahakam.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: Dama di yi daa nyɛ lala, u daa naa m‑bɛ ni n‑kpiiri n‑kpiira, duniya piiligu ni zaŋŋi m‑paai zuna. Amaa din nyɛ seem pampaŋŋɔa, u kyɛ na yimmu, sahanseaa duniya wa naari ŋɔn pɔtti na la: u kyɛ na ni u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑maali maaliseaa kan nin kyɛ ka Naawunni di sugru n‑ti ti, ti tumbeeri zugu.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Ninsaala ŋɔn kpiiri yummu ka sariya karibu dɔlisiri seem la.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: lala ka Yisa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑maali yummu maa. Ka u nin ti labi na yaasa: ka di pa la u kari tumbeeri sariya zugu, amaa ni u tiligi seeba ban ziya n‑guusiri u ni nimbirimoaa la.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Izraɛli dima zaaligu maa daa bɛ la hoo ŋmansigu, ka teaari ti yɛlisuma an kyɛnni na maa. Ka pa la bunsuma la mammansi. Dama yuumni kam ka ba maanni sara m‑maanni sara, amaa ba ya n‑niŋi la wula n‑suŋŋi ninvugseeba ban kyɛnni Naawunni saani na maa? Ay! - lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka ba leebi hoo din sumsi ni ba bɛ seem deede.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Ya bu nya? - di yi pa lala, ba naa n‑ku laa m‑maanni a, dama ba sufu da naa n‑ku laa n‑galim ba tumbeeri zugu. Ba ala'akyi taalli zaa yi siri daa kpiisi m‑basi, maaligu balim kam zaa naa n‑ku bɛ ni.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Amaa din nyɛ seem maa, ba maanni la maaligu yuumni kam, ka lala ka ba wulisiri niriba n‑teaari ba ba tumbeeri taalli yɛla.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Dama nigi ni buusi ziim ku toaai n‑ti ti tumbeeri taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Dinŋɔ zugu, Yisa ŋɔn daa ya n‑kyɛ duniya wa ni na maa, u daa yɛli, ni#:- Nyin, Naawunni, bu boori sara maaligu, I bu laa m‑boori koom tisibu: See ninsaala ningbina ka i maali n‑ti ma:.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Pa bunkɔbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a, Halli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba: - Anŋɔ zaasa zugu i nini bu tigra.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Di saha ka n yɛli#:- Mani nŋɔa - ni n niŋni nyin boori seem: Ii, mani nŋɔ, m Ba Naawunni - Hoo n yɛla pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, Litaafi gbaŋŋu maa puuni ka a sɔbi. .

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: Tɔ: u daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni, pa bunkɔbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a, ni, halli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba, ni, anŋɔ zaasa zugu ka Naawunni nini bu tigra. Amaa anŋɔ taabu zaa dɔli la zaalikurigu soori.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Ka u naayi yɛli, ni, Mani nŋɔa - ni n niŋni nyin boori seem, m Ba Naawunni. Ka di gbinni nyɛ la: ni, u basi an daŋŋi tooni la ka zaŋŋi bumpaala an pa nyaaŋŋa n‑zaali a zaaziiya.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Tɔ, ka Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem nyɛ la, ni, tirima n yi ti tumbeeri maa ni, n‑leebi ŋɔn Naawunni mammaŋŋa niriba, Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn zaŋŋi u toori ningbiŋŋu n‑tisi yimmu la zugu.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna beoo kam zaa ka maalimaanniba kur zɛ la ba tuma tumpu ni, m‑maanni ba sahakam bunkɔbmaaligu, amaa lala maaligu tuma zaa ku toaai n‑taasi tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi na.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Amaa Yisa Kirisita ŋɔna, u daa maali maaligu yinni kɔkɔ tumbeeri zaa taalli zugu: lala maaligu maa mari yiiko saha kam. U daa maali n‑naai pɔi, ka naayi labi n‑zinni Naawunni nudirigu ziziiya.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Bɛni ka u kpalim n‑ziya, halli ni Naawunni ti zaŋŋi u dindaandima zaa n‑dooli tiŋŋa ka u noora.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, u daa maali maaligu yinni ka tiligi seeba ban bɛ sɔsuŋŋu ni, ni ba ti paai Naawunni saani.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la gba wulisiri ti lala. U daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni#:-.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Ti Dugma yɛliya, ni, Lala ka n daa basi noori n‑ti ba, Ni, n nin suŋŋi ba dabsa an kyɛnni na: Ni, n nin zaŋŋi n zaaligu yɛla n‑sɔ'ai ba sufu ni; Di ya n‑niŋi hoo man zaŋŋi la bunni n‑sɔbi n‑niŋi a zaa ba sufu ni seem la. .

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Ni#:- N ku laa n‑teaai ba tumbeeri yɛla, Bee, ba ala'akyi taalli yaasa.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Tɔ! dinŋɔna anŋɔ zaa ka Naawunni tam m‑basi, ka dizugu sɔsɔ bu laa n‑sum ni ba maali bunkɔbmaaligu u tumbeeri taalli zugu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu, m mabiisi, ti mari soori ni ti kpɛ Dukasi maa, ya'aseela din kyisiri da'ari zaasa; n‑kpɛ ka vi ka ti ni, Yisa kum wa zugu.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: U yoaai la sɔpaalli n‑ti ti, kan nyɛ la nyɔvuri soori ni yɛlimaŋni, ni ti ti gaari n‑kpɛa. Ti gaari la pateesiseaa kan daa ta'ai ti ni ti ku paai ya'asuŋŋu wa, Dukasi la puuni. Soori maa mun nyɛ la u maŋtoori ningbiŋŋu.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Yaasa m‑paasi, ti mari la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana, ka u su Naawunni Yiri maa yɛla.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: Dizugu, ya kyɛ ka ti pɔri na n‑kpɛ Naawunni saani. Ti yi ya kpɛa, see ka ti sufu zɛ yummu, ka ti niŋi yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni. Ya kyɛ ka ti sufu nyɛ sufuveela an bu galimni ti, dama ba pun zaŋŋi kɔsuŋŋu n‑su ti ningbina zaa, ka di ŋmani la hoo ban zaŋŋi ziim n‑niŋi ti, kaman zaalikurigu da'ari peaari soori ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem la.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ti mari tama wa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ti nin toaai n‑niŋi Naawunni ni u nangbampɔri la yɛdda.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ti sufu bɛ taaba yɛla ni, n‑suŋŋni taaba ni ti zaa kyaŋŋi tooni n‑wuri niriba ka tumni tunsuma.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Yan di za'asi taaba la'aŋŋu, hoo seeba ŋɔn za'asiri seem la. Amaa ya kpamsiri taaba sufu. Ka niŋni anŋɔ zaa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ya nya ka ti Dugma dabsiri kyɛnni na saha bu ya n‑yuui.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya bu nya? Ti yi ti mi yɛlimaŋni seaa ban pun wulisi ti pɔi, ka laa n‑kpalim n‑tumni tumbeeri, dinŋɔna bunkɔbmaaligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ala'akyi laa n‑ka ni n‑ti ti.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: Ay! - Din kpalim nyɛ la dabeem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi sariya karibu din kyɛnni na, ni bugum seelli bun nin di baseeba ban kyiisiri Naawunni yɛla.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa za'asi Musa zaaligu yɛla, ka niriba ayi- ata di sɛhara, ni, ba wum lala - tɔ, lala nira daa yi kpi ni di zugu, ka ba daa bu zɔ u namboo halli beela.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Tɔ, ka ninvugsɔ yi ti za'asi Naawunni Biiya, bɔ n ya n‑niŋi ŋɔna? U tu ŋɔn daa kpi u zugu, ka yɛnni ni, u Tiligra ziim pa seela - halli ni lala ziim maa kyɛ ka Naawunni basi nangbanyinni nangbambasiri n‑ti u gba: ka laa n‑zeem Naawunni Sisuŋŋu.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Dama ti mi Naawunni yɛla, ni, u yɛliya#:- Mani n su labsigu, N nin labsi n‑yɔa. Ka yɛli m‑paasi, ni#:- Ti Dugma nin ti kari u niriba sariya.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Di siri mari dabeem pam ni Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana la ti kari i sariya n‑lusi i!.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Ya teaama din daa nyɛ seem ya saani dabsa an gaari la. Din saha maa ya daa pun deaai Naawunni neesim maa, ka daa nya namsibalibu pam, amaa a daa bu nyaŋŋi ya.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Lala saha maa ka ba daa yi namsiri ya ka zeemni ya niriba tooni; bee, ya daa yi ti sakki n‑zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑zɛ la ya taaba nyaaŋŋa, ban pun di namsi anŋɔ taabu sahanseaa la.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Ya daa teesi dansarika dima yɛla n‑suŋŋi ba; halli niriba ban daa fa ya marigu zaa gba, di daa bu niŋi ya sufusa'aŋŋu; dama ya daa mi, ni, ya mari seelli din gaari lala ka mun ka naari.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Dizugu yan di kyɛ ya sufukpeemni m‑basi, dama sufukpeemni nin ti ti ya yoori pam.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: See ka ya mari sugru. Lala nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem ka naayi deaai bunsunseesi Ŋɔn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ti seem maa.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Di kpalim la beela, Ni ŋɔn kyɛnni na maa ti kyɛ na; U bu ya n‑yuui.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: Ni, Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ka galiŋŋu n saani, U niŋni ma yɛdda, Ka dizugu u nin nya nyɔvuri. Amaa, u yi ti labi nyaaŋŋa, N sufu ku maai di daama zugu.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Tirima mun pa la ninvugseeba ban labri nyaaŋŋa nti baasi yɔri, amaa ban niŋni yɛdda nti nya tiliginsim.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Tɔ: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛdda niŋbu wa muna - yɛdda nyɛ la tin na mari seelli tama, ni, ti nin ti nya tin boori seelli maa. Yɛdda wa daana na bu nya, amaa u siri mi, ni, u nin nya.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Yɛdda wa taabu zugu ka ti yaadima yɛlisuma yi paalu#:-.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka ti baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni yɛligu n daa kyɛ ka duniya naam, ka tin nya seesi wa zaa an daa ti bɛ ni maa, bunseesi an bu nyara saani ka a daa yi na.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka Eebli daa yiisi sara n‑ti Naawunni, ka ka anfaani gaari u beeri Kyeen sara. Ka lala daa wulisi, ni, u daa nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana, dama Naawunni daa deaai u piini. Eebli daa kpiya, amaa yɛdda zugu ka u kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri ni.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka kum daa bu paai Inɔkyi, dama Naawunni n daa zaŋŋi u, ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya u, duniya wa ni. Niriba daa pun daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi u yɛlisuma, ni, ŋɔn daa mi Naawunni suŋŋasuŋŋa, ka dɔli u yuubu zaa, ka Naawunni na bu zaŋŋi u.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Tɔ: yɛdda yi ka ni, sɔ ku toaai m‑mi Naawunni n‑dɔli u yuubu; di yi pa la nira ŋɔn kyɛ Naawunni saani na niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni bɛ ni, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, ŋɔn Naawunni wumni n‑tiri ban boori u ziiya.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka Nuhu daa sakki Naawunni kpaaŋŋu halli u nini daa na bu nya koom maa gba, ka daa kpaasi nyariŋŋu maa ka ka daa ti tiligi ŋɔn ni u yiri dima maa. Ka di zugu ba daa kari ninvugseeba ban daa bu niŋi yɛdda lala maa sariya yɛttɔ'a n‑lusi ba, ba yɛdda kaalinsi zugu. Ka ŋɔn Nuhu daa naayi di faari kan nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni yuuri kan yi yɛdda niŋbu ni na.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka Ibrahim daa sakki, sahaseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u n‑wulisi tingbanseaa ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u, ka ka ti leebi u faari la. U daa sakki, ka daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi, ka dinŋɔ zaa daa niŋni maa ka Ibrahim na bu mi ŋɔn wa luginseaa maa.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Yɛdda zugu yaasa ka u daa gɔ n‑gɔ u faari tingbaŋŋu maa puuni, hoo saana bee sɔkyɛnna: u daa gbeeri la suga ni, ka Yisahaaku ni Yaakubu gba daa goori n‑gbeeri lala, halli ni Naawunni daa la'am ba Ibrahim saani m‑basi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin ti ba faari maa.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Ŋɔn Ibrahim ba'a zaa daa bɛ la tinseaa kan mari nyeebsuma ni, ka Naawunni su ka fukumsi ka laa n‑nyɛ ŋɔn meeri ka.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Yɛdda zugu gba ka ŋɔn Ibrahim daa nya yaa ni u ti dɔ'ai biiya, halli ŋɔn daa kurigi n‑gaari dɔ'am maa zaa yɔri, ka u pɔ'a Zaraatu gba daa kɔŋŋi dɔ'am. Dama u daa niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni daa basi noori lala n‑ti u, ka u daa yɛnni, ni, ŋɔn Naawunni n nyɛ yɛdda daana.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Lala zugu ka doo yinni wa, ŋɔn daa yɛtti, ni, u kpiiri ni, ka u dɔ'am daa zoaai hoo saŋmariwuusi ŋɔn zoaai saazugu seem maa, bee, hoo teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu ŋɔn zoaari seem, nti kɔŋŋi kaaligu.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Tɔ: banŋɔ zaa daa niŋi la yɛdda, halli ni ba kum. Ba daa bu deaai Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ba seelli la; ba daa kur nyɛ a ni ka a bɛ la wa'aligu, amaa ba sufu daa gyaari a. Ba daa sakkiya, ni, ba na bu su tingbaŋŋu maa, amaa ba nyɛ la saamma ni sɔkyɛnniba duniya wa puuni.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Niriba ban tɔ'asiri lala maa wulisiya, ni, ba boori la ba toori tiŋŋa.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Ya bu nya? - ba yi daa kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri ban daa yi tingbanseaa ni na la yɛla, ba daa naa n‑nya soori n‑labi bɛni.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Ay! - ba daa boori la tinseaa kan sɔa: saazugu tiŋŋa nla. Dizugu ka Naawunni bu nya vi ni ba ti boonni u, ni, ba Naawunni; dama u daa maanni tiŋŋa siri n‑ti ba.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Yɛdda zugu yaasa ka Ibrahim daa sakki, sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa zamsi u la; u daa maali siri ni u maali u bii Yisahaaku. Ŋɔn Ibrahim ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asi m‑basi noori, ka ŋɔn gba daa zaŋŋi u bigaŋŋa ni u ti maali.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: halli ni ŋɔn Yisahaaku saani ka Naawunni daa pɔ noori ni, u nyaaŋŋa ni u butiri zaa nin ti yi na.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Ibrahim mun daa yɛnni, ni, Naawunni nin toaai n‑neaai kpiima kum ni ka ba vu, ka di siri daa ŋmanni la hoo Yisahaaku daa neaai kum ni n‑labi vuya ni, ka Ibrahim daa deaai u lala.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Ŋɔn Yisahaaku muna, yɛdda zugu ka u daa ka n‑niŋi u biisi Yaakubu ni Iisɔa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi an nin paai ba na.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Yɛdda zugu ka ŋɔn Yaakubu gba daa ka n‑niŋi Yisifu biisi la, ŋɔn daa ya n‑kpi sahanseaa la, ka daa danni u zangbeaa zugu ka gyeemni Naawunni.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka Yisifu kum saha ŋɔn daa ti pɔtta, u daa tɔ'asi Izraɛli dima maa ŋɔn nin ti yi Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni seem, ka daa laa n‑wulisi ba ban nin ti zaŋŋi u kɔba n‑niŋi a seem.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka Musa ba ni u ma ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai u maa, ba daa nya ka u veela pam ka daa sɔ'ai u ka di paai gɔya ata, ba mun daa bu zɔri Naaba zaaligu maa.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Yɛdda zugu ka ŋɔn Musa, ŋɔn daa ti sa'ai nira maa, daa bu sakki, ni, ba boonni u, ni, NaaFirawuna bipuŋŋa biiya.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: U daa sakkiya, ni, u ti la'am n‑di namsi ni Naawunni niriba la, ka za'asi ala'akyi ŋɔn kyɛ ka beebu nya'asa seem la, halli saha beela.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Masiiya zugu ka u daa sakki, ni, u yi di vi, di sɔa: u daa kyɛ Igyipti bunsuma, dama u daa gɔsiri la u dahinseelli yoori.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Yɛdda daa laa n‑kyɛ ka u daa bu zɔ NaaFirawuna sufuyisigri ka daa bakki ka kyɛ Igyipti tingbaŋŋu. Dama u daa zaŋŋi u nini n‑kpa n‑gɔsi Naawunni, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn bu nyara gba.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Yɛdda zugu muna ka u daa kyɛ ka ba di Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la ka daa miisi ziim n‑niŋi maa, ni ŋɔn kuuri Igyipti bikpamma la di siisi Izraɛli dima maa biisi.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: U yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka TeekuZeoo koom daa nyuusi, ka Izraɛli dima maa daa sigi n‑nɔ tankuumni n‑gaari; amaa Igyipti niriba la ŋɔn daa ma'asi n‑sigi teeku maa ni, koom daa di ba.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Niriba ŋɔn daa niŋi yɛdda zugu ka Gyɛrikoo gooma zaa daa lu: ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gyiligi dabsa ayɔpɔi, ka a daa lu.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Halli Reehabu, gyɛ'amiru la, daa niŋi yɛdda; ka di zugu ba daa bu ku u ban daa ka yɛdda la saani, dama ninvugseeba ban daa kyɛ na ni ba ti gɔsi tiŋŋa la daa gbɛ pɔ'a maa saani ka u daa deaai ba suŋŋa.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Tɔ: ka n nin laa n‑yɛli la wula? N ka saha n nin tɔ'asi Gyidiyɔn yɛla, ni Barakku, ni Samsɔn, ni Gyɛfta, ni NaaDauda, ni Samuwɛli, ni anabidima yɛla maa.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Ba zaa daa niŋi la Naawunni yɛdda, n‑tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi nti di naam, ba daa kari sariya ni yɛlimaŋni, ka deaai Naawunni ŋɔn daa pɔ noori, ni, u ti tisi seem la; halli n daa ti kpari gbigma nɔya.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
