local: n‑kpiisi bugum zilima yaa; niriba ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi takoobi ni ba ti kyɛ ba, ba daa nya fa-m‑basi. Ba daa nyɛ la bumbugsa ka laa n‑leebi yaa daandima; ba daa nya kpeeŋŋu tɔppu ni, ka daa ta'ai tinzundima tɔbbiisi n‑labsi ba nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Ba daa kyɛ ka seeba neaai kum ni na, ka labsi ba n‑ti ba madima, bee, ba pɔ'aba. Ba mun daa namsi lala niriba seeba ni ba ti za'asi Naawunni, amaa ka ba daa bu sakki n‑za'asi ni ba ti yi namsigu maa ni. Ay! - Ba daa boori ni ba ti yisigi kum ni n‑nya bunsuma an gaari lala.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Ba daa diisi baseeba vi, ka feebi ba; ba daa ba baseeba gba bansi n‑kpari dansarika duu ni.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Ba daa lɔbi seeba kuga n‑ku, ka zaŋŋi sooyadima ni takoobidima n‑ŋmaai seeba n‑ku ba. Lala niriba seeba mun daa yɛ la peesi ni buusi gbana n‑gyinna; fara daa mari ba, ba daa ka seela; niriba daa namsiri ba ka ba daa di wahala zaa.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Halli ni duniya wa daa bu sum ni lala niriba bɛ ka ni. Ba daa kyɛnni n‑gyinni yoo ni, ni zɔya ziisi ni, n‑gbeeri tampifeeri ni, ni vɔya an bɛ tiŋŋa.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Banŋɔ zaa - yɛdda zugu ka ba tɔ'asi ba yɛlisuma yɛla, amaa ba sɔ daa bu nya nangbampɔri maa seela.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Di gbinni nyɛ la: ni, Naawunni daa pun daŋŋi n‑gbaai gbaari din nyɛ bunsuŋŋu n‑ti tirima: ni, ban ku toaai n‑nya ba bunsuma m‑maali m‑bɛ deede ba kɔkɔa, see ka tirima gba la'am ba saani n‑nya.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Lala Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka wa daa nyɛ la Salɛm tiŋŋa naaba, ka laa n‑gyeemni Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa, ka maanni u maaligu m‑paasi. Ka Ibrahim ŋɔn daa tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi nadima maa n‑naai ka kunna, ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa yi na n‑tuusi u soori ka daa susi Naawunni bunsuma n‑ti Ibrahim.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Din saha ka Ibrahim daa yiisi bunni pii kam zaa puuni bunyinni zakka n‑ti u. Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka mun yuuri gbinni nyɛ la buyi: u mammaŋŋa yuuri gbinni nyɛ la Yɛlimaŋni Naaba, amaa ba yɛnni m‑paasi, ni, u nyɛ la Salɛm Naaba, ka di gbinni nyɛ la Sufudoaa Naaba.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Luginseaa ka ni n‑wulisi, ni, ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa mari u ba, bee u ma, bee u yaadima. Bunseelli mun ka ni n‑wulisi u dɔ'am bee u kum yɛla. Dinzugu u ŋmani la Naawunni Bii maa. U kur ya kpalim n‑gyeemni Naawunni ka maanni sara halli ni saha din ka tariga.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Ya kur gɔsim n‑nya ŋɔn daa nyɛ ninvugtitaari seem! Halli ti yaaba Ibrahim daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn daa deaai bunseelli kam zaa tɔppu la ni n‑yiisi zakka n‑ti Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Tɔ; zaaligu wulisiya, ni, Liivai za'anɔri biisi kɔkɔ nin toaai n‑leebi maaligu maanniba. Ban kɔkɔ ka zaaligu wulisi, ni, ba deaari zakka Izraɛli dima saani. Izraɛli dima mun nyɛ ba mabiisi, amaa ba yiisiri zakka n‑ti ba, halli ni ba zaa yaaba n nyɛ Ibrahim maa yɔri.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa bu yi Liivai za'anɔri ni na, amaa u daa deaai zakka Ibrahim saani. Ibrahim mun nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa deaai Naawunni nangbambasiri, amaa ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa susi Naawunni bunsuma n‑ti u.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Di ka birisigu, ni, ŋɔn galisi n susiri Naawunni bunsuma n‑tiri ninvugsɔ ŋɔn poora.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Liivai za'anɔri ni maalimaanniba n nyɛ seeba ban kpiira, ka ba deaari zakka. Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka mun kum lahabaari ka ni, dinŋɔna u vu. Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli seem la nla.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Tɔ; Ibrahim ŋɔn daa yɔ zakka n‑ti Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka la, di nyɛ la kaman hoo u bii Liivai gba daa yɔa, halli ni ŋɔn ni u biisi n nyɛ ban deaari zakka.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Dama Ibrahim ŋɔn daa tuusi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka soori ni sahanseaa la, ba daa na bu dɔ'ai u ya'aŋŋa Liivai.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Tɔ. Liivai maalimaantiri soori maa zugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi zaaligu n‑tisi u niriba Izraɛli dima. Amaa ba tuma maa yi daa toaai n‑kyɛ ka niriba zaani Naawunni tooni ka ka galimni, dinŋɔna di daa bu kpa talasi ni maalimaannisɔ kyɛ na yaasa. Maalimaanna wa nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn yi la Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya ni na, pa Aruuna za'anɔri nira.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Dama maalimaantiri soori yi ti takki, di kpa la talasi ni zaaligu gba takki.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Ban tɔ'asi maalimaannisɔ yɛla maa n nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa. Amaa u bu yi Liivai za'anɔri ni na, Gyuuda za'anɔri nira n nyɛ u - sɔkam zaa mi lala. Ka lala za'anɔri maa ni sɔ mun na zi n‑leebi maalimaanna m‑maanni sara Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni. Yɛlimaŋni Musa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri maalimaanniba yɛla la u daa bu purigi Gyuuda yuuri.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Ka maalimaannisɔ hoo Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka yi daa yi paalu na, dinŋɔna tin tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseelli maa nin yi kasi ka sɔkam baŋŋi.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Pa ninsaala zaaligu zugu ka lala maalimaanna daa di u maalimaantiri, bee, u yaadima za'anɔri tuŋŋu zugu, amaa nyɔvuri seaa kan ka naari la yiiko zugu.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni#:- I nin di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana nti bɛ ni saha kam, Ka di dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Zaalikurigu la daa ka yaa, ka ku toaai n‑ku ninsaala bukyi. Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka m‑basi.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: Bɔ zugu? - Musa zaaligu maa ŋɔn daa na ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka bunseelli leebi kasi ka ka galimni maa zugu. Dinzugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi tamapaalli n‑zaali zaalikurigu zaani maa ni. Lala tama maa n ya kyɛ ka ti paari Naawunni saani.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Din paasi dinŋɔ zugu, Naawunni daa pɔ la pɔri sɔpaalli wa ni. Zaalikurigu soori ni, ba daa bu pɔ pɔri n‑diisiri maalimaanniba.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa ya diisi Yisa maalimaanna, u daa daŋŋi m‑pɔ pɔri. Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Ti Dugma n pɔ pɔri wa, Ni, I ya nyɛ la maalimaanna Sahaseaa din ka tɔriga. U ku laa n‑takki u sufu yaasa.

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Naawunni ŋɔn daa pɔ pɔseaa wa zugu ka ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa ŋɔn zaali nangbambasipaaliseaa la gaari zaalikurigu la.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Din paasi nŋɔa: maalimaanniba wa pam n daa bɛ ni, dama ba daa kpiiri ni. Sɔ daa yi naai la u tuma ka sɔ daa deaai u.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Yisa mun bɛ la saha din ka tɔriga. Dizugu u pa la sɔ ŋɔn bakki ka sɔ zaani u zaanni ni m‑maali sara.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Lala zugu ka u mari yiiko ka di nin toaai n‑tuligi bankam paai u ka u zi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni saani la, pampaŋŋɔ ni saha zaa. U vu la saha din ka naari n‑zɔri Naawunni sindaani ba zugu.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Yisa ŋɔn yi leebi ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana wa - tɔ! lala kɔkɔ ka ti nin ku ti bukaata zaa. U nyɛ la kasi. U ka taaliseaa bee ala'akyi. U beebu ni ka Naawunni wɔligi u n‑zaali u kɔkɔ n‑kyɛ tumbeeri dima. Halli gba u daa duusi u saazugu n‑yakki sangbana zugu.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: U bu ŋmani la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima wa. Dama dabsiri kam ba yi daŋŋni m‑maanni la sara ba mammaŋŋa ala'akyi zugu, ka naayi maali niriba zugu. Amaa Yisa daa maali sara yinni, u daa maali yimmu n‑ti ninsaalikam, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi la.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Musa zaaligu puuni ba daa gaŋŋni la ninsaala dima, baseeba gbarigi ka mari galimni, n‑diisiri ba Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima. Amaa di nyaaŋŋa Naawunni daa pɔ la pɔri ka diisi u Bii maa. Ŋɔna n ka galimni saha din ka baasigu.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna, sɛharadima wa n zoaai, ka ba zɛya n‑gyiligi ti. Ka di zugu ya kyɛ ka ti yiisi yɛlitibsa an vili n‑tabli ti la zaasa, ni ala'akyi din kpɛ la yooma n‑goaai ti kyɛnni maa. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti nya sufukpeemni n‑zɔri n‑kyɛnni tooni n‑dɔli sɔseaa ban zaali n‑ti ti la.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Ka di zugu ti wukkma ti zugu n‑kyɛ ka ti nini kpa la Yisa. Ŋɔna n nyɛ ti yɛdda yɛla piiligu, ka laa n‑nyɛ a naari gba. Ŋɔna n daa di dɔpulli kum wahala ka bu zaŋŋi di ka di nyɛ vi, dama u daa gɔsiri la sufupeelim seaa din dɔ lala wahala maa tooni la. Ka di zugu pampaŋŋɔ ka u paai la u Naawunni nudirigu zizii maa n‑ziya.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ya siri teaama u yɛla, ŋɔn daa mɔrigi n‑di wahalaseaa ala'akyi dima ŋɔn daa yaai u yɛla seem la. Lala nin kyɛ ka ya yɛdda ku gbarigi, bee, ya sufu n‑kyirim.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Yarima ŋɔn moori situusi moobu ni ya ti za'asiri ala'akyi yɛla la, ya na bu mɔ halli nti paai dansi ni kum nyaabu.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Ammi, ya pun tam Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya ni u ti kpamsiri ya sufu? U ya n‑kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi, ni, ya nyɛ la u biisi maa, ka yɛtti, ni#:- M biiya, ti Dugma yi sa'asi i soori, Di teesi, ni, di nyɛ la gɔsiyɔri. Halli u yi ti darigi i tubri gba, Di kyɛ ka i sufu miligi.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Dama ŋɔn wuri ninvugseeba la ka ti Dugma sa'asiri maa, Ka u feebri ŋɔn zaŋŋi ninvugsɔ n‑leebi u biiya.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Ya deaama lala sa'asigu maa kaman di yi la ya Ba saani na: lala nin bugsi, ni, ya nyɛ la Naawunni biisi. Ka bii ŋɔni n lee m‑bɛ ni ka u ba bu sa'asiri u? Ay! - sɔ ka ni!.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Ya yi bu nya Naawunni sa'asigu kaman u biisi zaa ŋɔn nyari seem maa, dinŋɔna di bugsiya, ni, ya pa la u biisi ni yɛlimaŋni, amaa seegyɛbisi n kur nyɛ ya.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti duniya wa beebu puuni ti badima n sa'asiri ti, ka ti tiri ba gyirima ka gyeemni ba. Amaa din gaari lala ka laa n‑kpa talasi nyɛ la: ni, ti deaai ti Ba Naawunni sa'asigu; dama u su ti siisi yɛla fukumsi, ka u nin kyɛ ka ti nya nyɔvuri.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Ti duniya ni badima sa'asiri ti saha beela, ka di dɔli la ba sufu yuubu ŋɔn wulisiri ba seem. Naawunni mun sa'asiri ti la hoo din sumsi ti, ka di yuui m‑paai saha din ka naari; ni tin ni ŋɔn ti la'am m‑bɛ u beebu kasikasi maa ni.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Tubdarigri saha na mari la beerim, ka ba bu nya di anfaani ŋɔn nyɛ seem; amaa ka di nyaaŋŋa ban deaai lala sa'asigu maa nin nya di nyoori, ka di nyɛ la sufudoaa ni yɛlimaŋni beesigu.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Dizugu ya kpamsima ya nuusi ni ya duna, dama a ka yaa.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Ka kyɛ ka sɔgɔlima maa yi teaai m‑maali suŋŋa. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ban nyɛ wɔbsi maa ku burigri soori ni, amaa ka laa n‑nya tippu.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Tɔ: ya kpaŋŋma ya maŋŋa ka nangbanyinni bɛ yan ni sɔkam zaa saani, ka galinsi ku bɛ ni; amaa ya la'amni m‑bɛ beebu kasi n‑tiri taaba, dama di yi pa lala sɔ ka ni n nin nya ti Dugma maa.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Ya mari ya mansi ka di kyɛ ka sɔ kɔŋŋi Naawunni bunsuma, miri ka putoo yɛla kpɛ na ka taasi galinsi na n‑sa'amni niriba pam yɛla.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Halli yan di kyɛ ka sɔ tumni zina, bee, u bɛ kaman Iisɔ ŋɔn ba'a daa ka Naawunni yɛttɔ'a ni, ka daa zaŋŋi u dɔ'am kpamni n‑takki sa'aŋmanni yinni la.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Ka ya mi, ni, di nyaaŋŋa Iisɔ daa ti boori ni u ba ka kasuma n‑ti u, ka daa bu nya sɔseelli ni u ti dɔli n‑takki ŋɔn pun tum seem la, halli u daa yoolim n‑kum nintam la yɔri, soori daa ka ni.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Tɔ; ka yarima bu paai la luginseelli ban nin toaai n‑siisi na, hoo Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa paai zoori maa seem la, zɔseaa bugum ŋɔn daa dira, ka zibsim ni sangbansabla ni sanzeoo zeebu m bɛ ni.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: ni kikaa kan daa kumni pampam, ni kukɔri kan daa tasiri ka ka yɛttɔ'a mari dabeem, halli ka ban daa wumni maa daa bɛlim Naawunni, ni, u di laa n‑kyɛ ka ba wumni ka taabu yaasa.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Ba daa bu nyaŋŋi ban daa zaali ba zaaligu seaa maa, ni, Halli bunkɔbgu gba yi siisi zoori maa, yan lɔbma u kuga n‑ku.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Lala gɔsibu daa mari dabeem pam, halli ka Musa u mammaŋŋa daa ti fabli, ni, dabeem mari u halli ka u daa gyigra.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Amaa yarima ŋɔn paai zɔseelli na nyɛ la Zayɔn zoori; ya paai la Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana la tiŋŋa na - di nyɛ la arizana ni Gyɛrusalɛm la; ya paai la malaakadima maa saani ka ba tusatusa la'amni la taaba ni sufupeelim pam;.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: halli ya paai la bikpamma la'aŋŋu maa na gba, baseeba yuya sɔbi m‑bɛ arizana ni bukku maa ni. Dinŋɔna ya paai la Naawunni saani na, ka u nyɛ la sɔkam zaa sarikatta: ka naayi paai Naawunni niriba ziiya, ya'aseaa ba nin ti kpalimni m‑bɛ ni, dama ba daa dɔli u soori deede n‑naai.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Ya paai la Yisa saani na gba, ŋɔn nyɛ sindaanzɔtta zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nangbanyinni sɔpaalli yɛla, ka laa m‑paai u ziim zii na, dama ba zaŋŋi u ziim m‑miisi; ka di wulisiri yɛliseesi an mari anfaani n‑gaari Eebli yɛla maa.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Tɔ, ka dizugu yan di za'asi ŋɔn tɔ'asiri ya yɛttɔ'a wa wumpu. Dama ban Izraɛli dima maa daa za'asi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ duniya wa puuni la noori, ka daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tiligi; ka tirima ya niŋi la wula n‑tiligi, ti yi ti za'asi ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni maa noori wumpu?.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Lala saha maa ka Naawunni kukɔri daa kyɛ ka tingbaŋŋu dam, amaa pampaŋŋɔ u basi la noori, ni, N ku laa n‑kyɛ ka tingbaŋŋu kɔkɔ n dam, amaa n nin laa m‑paasi yummu n‑damni saazugu ni sangbana gba.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn daa yɛli, ni, n‑laa m‑paasi yummu la, di wulisiri, ni, bunseesi an bu zɛ deede nin ti damni m‑basi zaa - di nyɛ la: ban daa naam seesi la. Ka bunseesi an ku toaai n‑dam ka zɛ deede la mun nin ti kpalim m‑bɛ ni.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ari Naawunni, dama u zaŋŋi la naam n‑ti ti, ka di ku toaai n‑dam. Ka ya laa n‑kyɛ ka ti zaŋŋi pɛ'abu din mari dabeem ni gyirima n‑gyeemni u, ka lala nin kyɛ ka u nini tigi.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: dama ti Naawunni siri nyɛ la bugum din dira.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya kpalim n‑wuri taaba hoo mabiisi.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Ya deaama saamma ya yiya ni, dama lala maa taabu zugu ka seeba daa ti deaai malaakadima ka bu mi.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Ya teaama ban kpari dansarika duu ni la yɛla, ka kyɛ ka di ŋmanni hoo yarima ni ban ka ba la'am n‑kpari; ka laa n‑teaai ban bɛ namsi ni la yɛla dama ya gba nyɛ la ninsaalidima, ka di pa seem ya gba nin di wahala.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Ya kyɛ ka doo ni u pɔ'a beebu mari gyirima, ka ba la'am m‑bɛ ni ni yɛdda, ka di veela; di kyɛ ka ba goori hoo dɔgoora bee pɔ'agoora. Naawunni nin kari lala niriba sariya, ni zina zaa tumniba dinni, n‑lusi ba.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu zaa ti bɛ ligri ni, amaa ya kyɛ ka ya nini nani yan mari bunseesi la; dama Naawunni yɛliya, ni, N ku bakki ka kyɛ ya, n mun ku za'asi ya.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Dinzugu ti mari sufukpeemni ka yɛtti, ni#:- Ti Dugma n nyɛ n suŋŋna - Dabeem ku mari ma: Bɔ ka ninsaala nin toaai n‑niŋi ma.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Ya teaama ya tooni dima yɛla, ban daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu n‑ti ya la. Ya teesima n‑nya ba beebu ŋɔn daa naayi niŋi seem; ka ma'asiri ba, ya yɛdda niŋbu puuni.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya siri kpalim n‑nyɛ ŋɔn sa nyɛ seem soosila, n‑kpalim n‑nyɛ lala zuna, ka kur nin kpalim n‑nyɛ lala sahakam.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka wulisipaala balibubalibu yɔmsi ya ka ya bɔrigi. Di tuya ni Naawunni piini kpamsi ti sufu, ka pa la bundira kyisigu yɛla, an ka bukaata n‑ti ban dɔli a la.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Dama ti mari maaligu ni ti deaari, ka ban kpalim n‑tumni Gyeeŋŋu Sugri tuma la ka soori ni ba la'am n‑di diibu wa.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Banŋɔ kaari maa ni ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa n yi mari bunkɔbri ziim n‑kpɛ Naawunni Beebu Ziiya; ka di wulisi, ni, tumbeeri zugu maaligu nla. Amaa bunkɔbri wa muna - ba daa zaŋŋi a n‑yiisi ba gbeebu ziiya kpaŋŋa sa n‑nyoaai bugum n‑di a.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Lala zugu ka Yisa gba daa di namsi tiŋŋa maa za'anɔri kpaŋŋa sa, ni u toori ziim kyɛ ka daadam ti leebi Naawunni niriba.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ti doaai tin gbɛ seela, n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa m‑paai u saani, ka tirima ni ŋɔna la'am n‑di vi seesi ŋɔn daa di maa.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Dama ti ka tiŋŋa kan zɛ deede n‑yuui duniya wa ni, na'ala ti boori tinseaa kan kyɛnni na la.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ti maanni pɛ'abu sara n‑tiri Naawunni sahakam, ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu - ti nangbana ŋɔn pɛ'ari u yuuri seem nla.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Yan di tam tunsuma tumpu yɛla, ka tɔtti ya marigu ni taaba, dama lala sara maaligu maari Naawunni sufu.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Ya sakkma n‑tiri ya tooni dima, ka wumni ba noori. Ban gɔsiri ya zugu ni nimmoosi hoo ninvugseeba ban gali gɔgala, dama ba zuguraandima nin boosi ba boosa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba tuma yɛla. Ya wumma n‑tiri ba; ka ba sufu maasa ba tuma ni, ka yɛlimugsa ka ni, dama di yi pa lala di ku suŋŋi ya.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Ya susima Naawunni n‑ti ti. Ti niŋi yɛdda ka zilisigu ka ni, ka mari tama, ni, ti beebu zaa nin sum ni gyirima tisibu.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: Din gaari zaa, man kpamsiri ya ni, ni, ya susima Naawunni, ni, di ku yuui ka n nya soori n‑labi ya saani na.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Naawunni nyɛ la Sufudoaa Daana: nangbanyinni zaaligu ziim zugu ka u daa yisigi ŋɔn nyɛ PɛguriZuguraana la kum ni - ŋɔn nyɛ la ti Dugma Yisa.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni ti ya bunni kam zaa kan nin kyɛ ka ya niŋni u sufu yuubu la, n‑tumni tunsuma zaa. U nin bɛ ya puuni n‑tumni bunseelli kam din nin kyɛ ka u nini tigi, Yisa Kirisita yuuri zugu. Ŋɔna n su pɛ'abu saha kam. Ammi .

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: M mabiisi, n kpamsiri ya, ni, ya sakki n‑wumni n kpaaŋŋu wa, dama man ŋɔn sɔbi gbanseaa n‑ti ya wa kur nyɛ la bumbila fiiŋ.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: M boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba basi ti mabii Timɔti la. U yi paai na pampaŋŋɔa, man ni ŋɔn nin kyɛ na nti nya ya.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Ya puusima ya tooni dima maa, ni Naawunni niriba zaa. Italii dima maa puusiri ya na.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Naawunni tim ya zaa u piinisuma ni alaafɛa.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: M mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya nyɛ la pɛ'abu zaa Daana, ka ti mun niŋi u yɛdda maa; dizugu yan di tibgri niriba n‑gamsiri hoo ya nini ŋɔn nya ba seem maa.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Di pa seem bundaana yi ti piri salima nuntuuwa m‑bɔbi suŋŋa n‑kyɛ ya la'aŋŋu ni na, ka nɔndaana gba bɔbi u neenkyɛra n‑kyɛ na.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: Ya yi ti tisi ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa la gyirima, n‑yɛli u, M Ba, i ku zinni zizisuŋŋu wa ni?, ka yɛli nɔndaana la, Zaanma kpa sa!, bee, Zinma tiŋŋa, n nɔba tooni.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: Dinŋɔna, ya tum taalli ya ninvuggaŋŋni la zugu. Halli ya zaŋŋni ya teesibeeri n‑katti la sariya ka di ka la yɛlimaŋni. Di pa lala?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: N zɔsuma, ya wumma! Naawunni n daa yiisi duniya wa nɔndaandima ni ba ti niŋi u yɛdda, ka dizugu ba nyɛ la bundaandima u saani. Ba nin di faari nti su Naawunni naam beebu, hoo ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiri n‑zaali ban wuri u la seem maa.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Di zaa yɔri, ya na kur zeemni la nɔndaandima! Yaasa - bandima n namsiri ya ka mari ya n‑yiiri sariya kattiba tooni la? Pa bundaandima nla?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Ban n tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'ayɔya an sa'amni ya Dugma yusuŋŋu wa!.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Naawunni Litaafi maa mari la Naaba zaaligu yɛla, ka di yɛliya, ni, Wura i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la! Tɔ, ya yi niŋni lala maa, di veela.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Amaa ya yi tibgri niriba ba bɔbri zugu ya tumni la taalli, ka zaaligu maa wulisi, ni, ya galimiya.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Dama ninvugsɔ ŋɔn dɔli zaaligudima zaa, amaa ka galim zaaligu yinni kɔkɔa, lala nira galim la zaaligu zaa.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Dama Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, Di tum zina!, ka daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, Di ku ninsaala! Dizugu i yi bu tum zina, amaa ka i ku nira, i bu tum la taalli ka galim zaaligu?.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya yɛttɔ'a ni ya tuma ŋmanni la ninvugmaŋni dina - ninvugsɔ ban nin ti zaŋŋi zaaligu seaa din tisiri la mansuulinsi la n‑kari u sariya, ka u ka taalli kam zaa.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Sariya karibu daari, Naawunni ku ti zɔ namboo n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa bu zɔri u kpeaa namboo; amaa ka nambɔzɔtta nin mari sufupeelli, lala dabsiri maa daari.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: Tɔ, m mabiisi, ninvugsɔ yi ti yɛli, ni, u mari la yɛdda, ka mun bu tumni tunsuma, bɔ yɛdda nla? Lala taabu yɛdda nin toaai n‑tiligi sɔa? - Ay!.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Ya mabisɔa, doowa bee pɔ'a, yi ti yɛli, ni, u ka kparigu bee, bundirigu.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: ka ya ninvugsɔ yi ti yɛli u, ni, Bɛ ni suŋŋa ka diri n‑tigra; waari mun di wum i! ka bu ti u ŋɔn boori seelli maa, suŋŋsim bɔ nla?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Tɔ: yɛdda nyɛ la lala: tunsuma yi ka yɛdda ni, ka kpi ni.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Di yi pa seem ka ninvugsɔ ya n‑yɛli, ni, Tɔ; nyin mari la yɛdda, ka man mun mari tunsuma. Di zaa nla! Tɔ! - man nin kyɛ ka n tunsuma wulisi i n yɛdda maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem; ka nyin mun ya niŋi la wula n‑wulisi ma i yɛdda ŋɔn nyɛ seem, halli i yi ka tunsuma?.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Ni, i niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni yinni m bɛ ni? Di veela! Amaa baŋŋma, ni, arizimbeeri gba mari lala yɛdda maa - ka di zugu ba gyigri ni, dabeem zugu.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Nyin yam kaalinsi daana wa! Baŋŋma, ni, yɛdda, ka tunsuma ka ni, nyɛ la yɔri!.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: I baŋŋiya, ni, ti Ba Ibrahim tunsuma zugu daa kyɛ ka u leebi yɛlimaŋni daana, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u bii Yisahaaku ni u maali n‑tisi Naawunni zugu.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Bɛni ka i nin nya yɛdda ni tunsuma ŋɔn tumni n‑dɔla taaba seem. U tunsuma zugu daa kyɛ ka u yɛdda maa daa paai di tariga.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Litaafi maa ni ka di yɛliya, ni, Ibrahim daa sakki n‑niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda, ka u yɛdda maa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana. Lala n kyɛ ka ba booli u, ni, Naawunni zɔa.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ya bu nya? See ka tunsuma paasi ninsaala yɛdda ni, u ku toaai n‑niŋi yɛlimaŋni nira.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Lala yaasa ka Reehabu, gyɛ'amiru la, daa deaai saanseeba ban daa kpɛ na ni ba ti baŋŋi tiŋŋa maa kpeeŋŋu ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la. U daa wulisi ba sɔseaa ka ba yi Gyɛrikoo timpuuni n‑kuli. Pa u tunsuma zugu ka ba yɛnni, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana?.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Ningbiŋŋu maa yi ti wɔligi ka kyɛ siiya, ka kpi ni; ka di bɛ la lala - yɛdda yi wɔligi ka kyɛ tunsuma maa ka gba kpi ni.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Mani, Gyeemsi, n sɔbri wa m‑puusiri ya. N sɔbri n‑tiri ya Izraɛli za'anɔya pii-ni-ayi niriba, yaseeba ban wurim m‑bɛ duniya tingbana la zaa. Man Gyeemsi mun nyɛ Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita daabri la.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: M mabiisi, sahaseaa zaa ya yi ti bɛ zamsigu zaa puuni, ya kyɛ ka ya kpalim m‑mari sufupeelli;.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: ka baŋŋi, ni, ya yɛdda zamsigu maa kyɛ ka ya nyari sufukpeemni.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Ka ya zɛ yummu n‑kyɛ ka ya sufukpeemni maa kpaŋŋi zaa n‑naai, ka di zugu ka ya nin doaai na suŋŋa ka ku kɔŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Amaa ya sɔ yi ti kɔŋŋi yam, ŋɔn susim Naawunni ka u nin deaai. Dama Ŋɔna n tiri sɔkam ka bu za'asira.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Amaa see ka di daama mari yɛdda n‑susira, ka di zulisira, dama ŋɔnkam zulisiri ŋmanni la mɔ'ari, pɛbsigu ŋɔn zeeri ka sahanseaa la, ka ka kɔmiisi laanni n‑kyɛnni ka labri na.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Di daama di teesiri, ni, u nin nya bunseelli ti Dugma saani.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: Teesa ayi daana n nyɛ u, u ku zɛ yimmu, u laanni ni u yɛla zaa puuni.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Kyɛ ka ya mabitarima mari sufupeelli, dama Naawunni kyɛ ka u yuuri du.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Ka bundaana gba mari sufupeelli, ba yi ti sigsi u n‑labsi u nyaaŋŋa, dama u nin ti kuui m‑basi kaman mɔpuum.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Wuntaŋŋa yi pusi nti bii pam, moori maa kuuri ni ka a puum suui, ka a veelim sa'am; lala ka bundaana gba nin ti suui n‑lu u beesigu puuni.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari sufukpeemni n‑zɛ yummu zamsigu puuni la, dama zamsigu nyaaŋŋa u nin deaai nyɔvuri piiniseaa ka ti Dugma basi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin zaŋŋi n‑ti seeba ban wuri u la.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Sɔ yi ti nya zamsigu, ŋɔn di yɛli, ni, Naawunni n zamsiri u, dama bumbeoo ku toaai n‑zamsi Naawunni, u gba mun ku zaŋŋi bumbeoo n‑zamsi sɔ.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Ti mammansi ningbinkɔrisi n kyɛ ka ti nyari zamsigu, sahaseaa ti ningbinkɔrisi yi ti yɔmsi ti n‑kpeesi zamsigu ni maa.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Ningbinkɔrisi mun yi gbaai puuwa, ka dɔ'ari la ala'akyi; ka ala'akyi yi ti zoaai, ka mun dɔ'ari la kum.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: Dinŋɔna yan di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya, m mabisuma!.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Piinisuŋŋu kan veela zaa yi la saazugu na, n‑yiiri ti Ba saani na, Sɔ ŋɔn su bunseesi an neaari saazugu zaa maa. Ŋɔn mun bu takkra, u bu ŋmanni la hoo sisiisi an ta'asira.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Ŋɔna n daa yɛli, ni, u dɔ'ai ti ni, ni ti leebi u yɛlimaŋni yɛligu biisi, ka ti niŋi hoo u puu antaam bundira piiligu.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: M mabisuma, ya kpasim dinŋɔa: sɔkam gbaam wumpu yoomyooma, amaa u di gbaai yɛttɔ'a yooma, bee, u di kyɛ ka u sufu yisigi yooma!.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Dama ninsaala sufuyisigri bu tiri yɛlimaŋni beesiseaa Naawunni ŋɔn boori maa.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Dizugu ya basima dɔgoorilim ni pɔ'agoorilim, ni yɛlibeeri an zoaari pam zuna wa, ka deaai yɛttɔ'aseaa ban pun buri i sufu ni la. Kana nin fa ya m‑basi.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ya wumni la Naawunni yɛligu ka bu tumni hoo din wulisi ya seem: lala maa ya kur yɔmsiri la ya mansi. Ya tumma din yɛli seem maa!.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Ŋɔnkam wumni yɛligu maa ka bu tumni din yɛli seem maa ŋmanni la hoo dɔsɔ ŋɔn gɔsi mansɔ'asi puuni, ni u nya u nindaa.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: ka yi gɔsi ka bakki, u yi tam la yooma u nindaa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Amaa dɔsɔ ŋɔn gɔsi zaalisuŋŋu bunsuma soori maa, ni sɔseaa kan tisiri mansuulinsi, ka kpalimni n‑niŋni lala - halli u bu tamni ŋɔn wum seelli maa amaa ka niŋi lala - di daama nin nya anfaani u tuma ni.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Sɔ yi teesi, ni, u nyɛ la zaaligu sɔdɔla amaa ka bu gbaai u zulimni garigɛ suŋŋa, u yɔmsiri la u maŋŋa, ka sɔseaa ŋɔn dɔla maa ka anfaani.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn sakki sɔseaa, ni, kana n nyɛ bunkasi ka ka lasim nŋɔa#:- ni, ti gɔsim kpibisi ni pukɔya ba wahala ni n‑suŋŋi ba, ka kyɛ ka ti duniya wa yɛla di sa'am ti.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: M mabiisi, yan di kyɛ ka ya niriba pam ti leebi karimbadima; dama ya mi, ni, tirima ban tumni lala tuma maa - ti sariya karibu nin ti kpeem pam.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ti ninsaadima zaa burigri la sɔsuŋŋu ni ka galimna: amaa ka ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ni m‑bu galim u yɛligu maa ni, lala nira nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu kasi, ka nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nyaŋŋi m‑mari u maŋŋa.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Ya bu nya? Ti yi zaŋŋi garigɛ n‑niŋi yoofu noori, ti nin nyaŋŋi u ka u sakki ti yuubu; ti nin mari u n‑tumni hoo tin boori seem.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, nyarinkara an bɛ teeku ni maa, a yi kur karigi pampam seem, pɛbsigu m mari a n‑kyɛnni ni kpeeŋŋu. Amaa ba mari la bumbila fiiŋ kan ŋmaligra, ka nyarintitaari wa dɔli la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusiri maa ŋɔn gbaari soori seem maa.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Zulimni gba nyɛ la dinŋɔa: ka n poora pam ningbina ni, ka mari yɛla pam. M‑paasi yaasa - daari yi zoaai pam n‑dɔya, bugunsaalli yinni kɔkɔ nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka a zaa di.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, ninsaala zulimni ŋmanni la bugum; ka bɛ la u ningbina maa ni kaman duniya yɛlibeoo n nyɛ ka - bumbeoo halli nti yakki. Ka sa'amni la u ningbina zaa, ka kyɛ ka u bɛ wahala ni sahakam zaa, hoo bugum puuni beesigu ŋɔn nyɛ seem; halli ka Sintaani bugum ziiya yiiko ni ka zulimni moaari ka dinni.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Bunkɔbri balibu zaa, ni niinsi balibu zaa, ni bunvuura ni koom ni bunni zaa, saala nyaŋŋi ba zaa, ka ba zaa yiiko bɛ la u nuusi ni.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: Amaa zulimni ŋɔna, saala ka ka yiiko; ka nyɛ la bumbeoo ka bu vuusira; kan mari lɔkku seebu n kuura.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Ti zaŋŋni la kana m‑pɛ'ari ti Ba Naawunni, ka laa m‑mari n‑tuuri ka galimni ninsaala, halli ka Naawunni daa naam saala ka u ŋmanni la ŋɔn Naawunni.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Nangbanni yinni maa ni ka yɛttɔ'asuma yiiri na, ni yɛttɔ'abeeri m‑paasi. M mabiisi, lala ka soori halli beela.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Kɔbuliga nimbiri yinni ka kɔsuŋŋu yiiri na, ka kɔbeoo nin laa n‑yi ka ni na m‑paasi, bee? - Ay!.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: M mabiisi, kinkaŋŋa nin toaai n‑wali taama? - bee, gaa nin toaai n‑wali kinkama? Ay, a ku nyaŋŋi! Ka dinŋɔna kɔbuliga ku toaai n‑yiisi kɔsuŋŋu ni kɔbeoo m‑paasi saha yinni.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Tɔ; ka ŋɔni m bɛ ya sunsukkuni n nyɛ yamdaana ni baŋŋna? - Kyɛ ka di daama ti tumni tunsuma n‑sigsiri u maŋŋa, lala nin kyɛ ka ti nya u yam maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Amaa ya yi mari nyuuri bee putoo ni zinyuu din yiiri ya sufu ni na, yan di nyuuri bara di zugu ka leebi ban kyiisiri yɛlimaŋni maa.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Dama dinŋɔ taabu pa la yam seelli din yiiri la arizana ni na, amaa di nyɛ la duniya wa yam, din yi la daadam beesigu ni na; halli Sintaani n su di.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Yɛlimaŋni, nyuuri zappu ni zinyuu ŋɔn bɛ seela, bɛni ka kyiisigu ni tumbeeri kam tuma gba bɛ ni.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Amaa yam din yi arizana ni na nyɛ la halaali; ka din pa dinŋɔ zugu nyɛ la din kpamsiri sufudoaa ni baalimm beebu. Lala yam kyɛ ka nira deaari wulisigu, ka laa n‑nyɛ din kyɛ ka u zɔri la namboo zaa, ka tumni la tunsuma zaasa. U nin zɛ yummu n‑niŋi yɛdda daana, ka ka birikɔnsi halli beela.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ la sindaani zoobu tuma ni ŋmanni la hoo ŋɔn butti bumbura sufudoaa zugu; lala nira nin ti ŋma u puu maa bundira, ka a nyɛ la taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi Naawunni saani.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Tɔ; Bɔzugu ka zaba ni nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu bɛ ya sunsukkuni? Pa la ya kɔribeeri zugu, ni teesibɛseesi an zabri taaba ya sufu ni?.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Ya boori m‑boori ya sufu yuubu, n‑gyɛa, ka dizugu ya kuuri niriba. Ya zabri niriba nyuuri, ka dizugu ya ŋmeeri taaba nangbankpeemni n‑tuusiri tɔppu. Amaa ya bu susiri Naawunni ka lala zugu ka ya kɔŋŋna.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Halli ya yi susiri gba, ya bu deaara, dama ya susigu bu dɔli soori; ya boori bunseesi yan nin zaŋŋi n‑di ya maŋtoori kɔrisi nya'asim.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Ya za'asiri Naawunni yɛdda niŋbu hoo pɔ'agoorli ŋɔn za'asiri u sira seem. Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, duniya wa zoori loobu nyɛ la Naawunni dimni? N yɛliya, ni, sɔkam zaa ŋɔn boori duniya wa zoori, ŋɔna n leebi Naawunni dindaana.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Bee, ya teesiya, ni, Naawunni Sɔppu la tɔ'asi la yɔri? Ni, Naawunni kyɛ ka u Siiya bɛ ti saani; ka u ku sakki, ni, ti za'asi u ka wuri sɔa.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Halli u tumni wurilim tuma n‑tiri ti n‑gaari lala gba, ka dizugu ba daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Naawunni za'asiri karimbaani dima, Ka u tumni la wurilim n‑suŋŋni ban sigsi ba mansi.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Dizugu, ya sakkma n‑ti Naawunni. Ya za'asim Sintaani ka u nin zɔ ka kyɛ ya.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Ya vuum m‑pɔri Naawunni saani, ka u gba nin ti pɔri ya saani na. Yaseeba ban nyɛ tumbeeri dima, ya peaam ya nuusi da'ari m‑basi; yaseeba ban nyɛ birikɔ'anu dima, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu zɛ yimmu n‑niŋi kasi.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am. Ya kumma ka kaasira: ya takkma ya laari maa ka di leebi kunkuma, ka ya sufumaasim wa leebi sufusa'aŋŋu!.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Ya sigsim ya mansi ti Dugma saani, ka u nin wukki ya saazugu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: M mabiisi, yan di tɛrisiri taaba. Nira yi tɛrisiri u taraansɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita biiya, bee, u yi kari u sariya, u tɛrisiri Naawunni zaaligu ka katti zaaligu sariya. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna i yi ti katti zaaligu sariya, i laa m‑pa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn deaai zaaligu maa n‑tumna, amaa i leebi la di sarikatta.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Naawunni kɔkɔ n tisiri zaaligu, ka laa n‑nyɛ sariya katta. Ŋɔn kɔkɔ n nin toaai n‑tuligi nira, bee, u sa'am u yɛla. Nyin mun teesiya, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni, n nin toaai n‑kari i ninsaalikpeaa sariya?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ, ya wumma man ya n‑yɛli ya seelli wa: yaseeba ban yɛtti, ni, Zuna bee beoo ti ya gɔ ni n‑kyaŋŋi tinyaakaza ni, m‑bɛ bɛni ka di paai yuumni, n‑tumni dabiiligu tuma n‑nya ligri pam.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Halli ya bu mi yan sa ya nya yɛttɔ'aseaa beoo gba! Ya ŋmanni la pa'ari din bɛ asuba saha; ka di ku yuui, ka di nin wurimni yooma ka sɔ ku laa n‑nya di.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Ay! See ka ya yɛli nŋɔa: ni, Di yi siri nyɛ ti Dugma sufuyuubu, ti nin kpalim m‑bɛ ni n‑tumni nŋɔ, bee, nŋɔa.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Amaa din bɛ seem maa, ya mari la karimbaani, ka kuunna; kuula anŋɔ zaa bu veela.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Dizugu, ninvugsɔ yi mi din tuusi ni u tum, ka bu niŋi di, di daama tumni la tumbeoo.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ, ya wumma man ya n‑yɛli ya seelli wa, yaseeba ban nyɛ bundaandima maa: ya kumma ka kaasira, dama wahala kyɛnni ya zugu na.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Ya marigu maa zaa poaai, ka leelisi ŋɔbi ya neema maa zaa n‑yoosi.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Ya salima ni ya anzulifa maa zaa nyamiya, ka a nyaŋŋu maa nin ti wulisi ya galiŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ seem. Di nin kpɛ ya ningbina ni n‑vuunni n‑dira hoo bugum. Ya boori la buna ni nimmoaa n‑zaanni a ni, amaa naari dabsa maa pun paai ti na.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Tuntumniba daa tum ya tuma, amaa ya daa nyɛri ba; ya wumma! - ya lala tumbeeri damni ŋɔn paai saazugu seem! Ba daa la'asi ya puri antaam n‑ti ya na, ka bu deaai sɛseela: ban kumni seem n‑niŋni ya, ya baŋŋma ka ba kunkuma maa lu la ti Dugma Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa tuba ni.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Duniya wa puuni ya daa bɛ la beesisuŋŋu ka daa diri ya sufu yuubu bundira. Ya diri n‑karigri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya kɔribu daari.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Halli ya daa kari ban ka galiŋŋu sariya n‑lusi ba, ka ku ba, ka ba daa bu ta'ai ya sufu ni teesa.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: Dizugu, m mabiisi, ya mari sugru ka gbaai ya mansi n‑guri ti Dugma kyɛnni saha na. Ya teaam pukpaara tuma soori, ni, u mari sugru n‑guri u puu bundira ŋɔn ya niŋi seem. Ni, u mari saha n‑guri siga saa maa, ni u ti buri; ka laa n‑guri bundira niŋbu saha saasi maa gba.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Lala zugu ya gba mari sugru ka di koaai tama, dama ti Dugma nin kyɛ na, u bu ya yuui.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: Dizugu yan di fabinni ni taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'abisibisi. Ya baŋŋma, ni, ba nin kari ya sariya di zugu. Sariya Katta ni yɛlimaŋni la nin paai ti na - halli u pun bɛ la za'anɔri ziiya!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: M mabiisi, ya baŋŋma anabidima ban daa tɔ'asi ti Dugma yuuri zugu la yɛla. Ba daa zɛ yummu wahala pam puuni. Lala nin ti wulisi ya sugru ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Ya teaama, ni, di nyɛ la seeba ban mari ba maŋŋa maa ka ti yɛtti, ni, ba nya zugsuŋŋu Naawunni saani. Ya pun wum Ayuuba ŋɔn daa gbaai u maŋŋa n‑di sugru seem maa; ka ya mi halli, ti Dugma Naawunni ŋɔn daa naayi tum seem n‑ti u. Dama ti Dugma Naawunni maa nyɛ la wurilim zaa daana ka zɔri niriba namboo.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: Di zaa nyaaŋŋa, m mabiisi, yan di poori noori: di pɔ arizana, bee, tingbaŋŋu, bee, seelli kam zaa. Ya kyɛ ka ya Ii nyɛ la Ii! ka ya Ay! nyɛ la Ay!: di yi pa lala, ba nin kari ya sariya n‑lusi ya.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Ya sɔ bɛ mugsima ni, bee? U susim Naawunni! Ya sɔ mari sufumaasim, bee? U yuumma pɛ'abu yuuma n‑ti Naawunni!.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Ya sɔ beera? U boolim Naawunni niriba kpamma ka ba susi n‑ti u, ka niŋi kpaam n‑soosi u zugu, ti Dugma yuuri maa ni.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Ka wunsusigu yi susi la ni yɛdda, di nin kyɛ ka dooru daana maa nya alaafɛa; ti Dugma nin doaai u. Halli u yi tum la tumbeeri, u nin nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Dizugu ya yɛlim yan tum taalli seem yɛla n‑ti taaba, ka susi Naawunni n‑ti taaba, ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nya alaafɛa. Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ka taalli ka susi ni nimmoaa, u susigu mari yaa pam ka u nin nya.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Ilaygya daa kur nyɛ la ninsaala hoo tirima. U daa susi ni sufu yinni, ni, saa di mi, ka saa siri daa bu mi tingbaŋŋu maa ni, ka di daa niŋi yuuma ata ni purigri.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Ka u daa laa n‑susi ka sangbana daa ti tisi saa na, ka tingbaŋŋu maa daa yiisi diibu na.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: M mabiisi, di yi pa seem ya sɔ nin ti bɔrigi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlimaŋni ziiya, ka u taraansɔ zi u n‑labsi soori ni na.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Ya kyɛ ka lala nira baŋŋi dinŋɔa: ni, ŋɔnkam nyaŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka ala'akyi daana kyɛ u tumbeeri maa m‑basi, u yiisi tumbeeri daana wa kum yɛla puuni, ka sɔ'ai la tumbeeri pam taalli.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Gbaŋŋu wa yi la man Piita saani n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban yi ba toori tiŋŋa n‑wurigi m‑bɛ Pɔntusi tingbaŋŋu, ni Galɛseaa, ni Kappadɔsii, ni Ɛseaa, ni Bitinii tingbana m‑paasi la.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Yarima ka Naawunni ti Ba daa pun baŋŋi ya yɛla, ka yiisi ya, n‑kyɛ ka ya leebi u dinni, m‑bɛ kasi u Sii Kasi zugu la, ni ya sakki n‑ti Yisa Kirisita, ka u ziim zugu ka ya nin niŋi kasi. M puusiri ya zaa suŋŋa, ni, Naawunni ti ya alaafɛ ni sufudoaa zaa.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ai Naawunni, ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la, u namboo la zugu. Ŋɔn daa yisigi Yisa Kirisita kum ni la zugu, u daa yiisi nyɔvuri paalli na n‑ti ti. Ka lala zugu ti mari tama, ni, ti ti nya beesigu paalli ti kum nyaaŋŋa.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: Lala ka ti nin di faari. Faari wa mun nyɛ la ban zaali seaa arizana ni n‑guri ti la. Ka ku toaai n‑sa'am, bee, m‑baasi, bee, n‑suui hoo moori ŋɔn suuri seem.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Dama ya yɛdda niŋbu nin kyɛ ka Naawunni yiiko mari ya, halli ka tiliginsim din pun maali siri la ti paai ya na, duniya wa naari saha.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Di sumni ni ya mari sufupeelli di zugu, halli ni ya yi na kpalim n‑nyari namsigu ni wahala beela - ay! di bu ya yuui.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Wahala balibubalibu wa nin kyɛ ka ya yɛdda nin nya zamsigu kaman ban yi zamsiri salima bugum ni seem la. Amaa salima ŋɔn nin ti naai, ya yɛdda mun nin zamsi n‑yi bummaŋni, ka mari anfaani pam. Di nin ti ti ya pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni tibsim, Yisa Kirisita yi ti yi paalu sahanseaa la.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Ya na bu nya u, ka wuri u; halli ni yan bu nyari u zaa yɔri ya niŋi u yɛdda, ka nyari sufupeelli pam ni sufumaasim zaa.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Dama ya yi nyari la ya yɛdda nyoori, di nyɛ la ya siisi tiliginsim.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Tiliginsim dinŋɔ ka anabidima daa guri la, saha an gaari la ni, ban daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi u wurilim piini n‑ti yarima la yɛla; dizugu ba daa viisi m‑boori di gbinni suŋŋa.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Yisa Kirisita Sii Kasi daa bɛ ba ni n‑daŋŋi n‑wulisiri ba ŋɔn Yisa Kirisita wahala yɛla - ni u gyirima din nin dɔli nyaaŋŋa na yɛla gba. Ka ba daa boori nimmoosi ni ba ti baŋŋi dinŋɔ saha, ni din ti ya n‑nyɛ seem.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Amaa di daa kasigi n‑ti ba, ni, ba bu tumni n‑tiri la ba mansi, amaa ba tumni n‑tiri la yarima. Lala zugu, pampaŋŋɔ saha wa ka ba tum Sii Kasi ka u yi arizana n‑sigi na ni u ti wulisi lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ya - alahazibu seesi malaakadima ŋɔn guusiri ni ba nya maa.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Anŋɔ zaa zugu, see ka ya maali siri ya sufu ni, ka mari ya mansi. Ya zaŋŋma ya sufu ni teesa ba'aba zaa n‑niŋi ya tama maa yɛla ni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti ya, Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn ti ya yi paalu na dahinseaa la.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Ya n nyɛ ban deaai Naawunni noori la hoo u bisuma, ka di zugu yan di kyɛ ka kɔrisi su ya sufu yɛla, hoo din daa niŋi yan daa na zi sɛseela sahanseaa la seem maa.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Dizugu ya beebu zaa puuni, see ka ya bɛ kasi hoo Naawunni ŋɔn booli ya maa ŋɔn bɛ kasi seem la.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: Tɔ; di sɔbi n‑dooli Naawunni Bukku ni, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, Ya bɛ kasi dama m bɛ la kasi.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Ya puusiri Naawunni, ni, ya Ba: ka ŋɔn Naawunni mun bɛ la sɔ ŋɔn katti sɔkam sariya hoo nira maa tuma ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka bu deaari sɔ nini. Di yi ti bɛ lala, see ka ya zɔ dabeem, yan na bɛ duniya wa ni sahanseaa la ka na bu kuli Naawunni saani maa.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Dama ya mi halli, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa tiligi ya n‑yiisi ya ya badima yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama tuma maa ni, pa anzulifa ka u daa zaŋŋi m‑pa talima n‑yiisi ya, bee, salima, bee lala bunseelli zaa kan sa'amna.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: Ay! - Yisa Masiiya ziim ka u daa zaŋŋi n‑niŋi, hoo ban zaŋŋi pɛbilisɔ ŋɔn bu pɔrisi, bee, m‑mari da'ari zaa, m‑maali seem. Ka lala ziim mari la anfaani pam; din ka ba daa zaŋŋi m‑pa talima n‑tiligi ya.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Naawunni daa pun daŋŋi m‑pii u pɔi, ka naayi naam duniya wa. Halli ka ŋɔn Yisa daa ti yi paalu zɛmaana baasigu saha wa. Yarima zugu n‑daa la.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni, ka ti u gyirima. Ŋɔnŋɔ zugu ka ya niŋni Naawunni yɛdda, ka ya kur kyɛ ka ya yɛdda niŋbu ni ya tama zaa bɛ la Naawunni saani.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Ya pun dɔli la yɛlimaŋni soori, ka deaai Naawunni noori n‑leebi u dinni. Ka lala yi kyɛ ka ya siri wuri ya mabiisi ka yɔmsa ka ni. Dizugu ya na wuri ba, ya sufu ni, ni nimmoaa.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Naawunni yɛligu kan mari nyɔvuri ka ka naari la daa pun maali n‑dɔ'ai ya buyi dɔ'am. Lala dɔ'am maa pa la duniya wa dɔ'am, ka i dɔ'ai m‑bɛ ni n‑yuui beela ka naayi naai; di nyɛ la dɔ'am seebu ka ba dɔ'ai i n‑kpɛ nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Di nyɛ la hoo din daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- Ninsaala ŋmanni la moori, Ba mun gyirima zaa ŋmanni la mɔpuum. Moori kpiiri ni, Ka a puum maa suui m‑basi.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: Amaa ya Dugma yɛligu ku ti basi, Amaa din nin bɛ ni Halli ni saha din ka naari. Lala yɛligu maa nyɛ la lahabasunseaa ban daa moonni na n‑ti ya la.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Ya pɔ'aba, di bɛ la lala maa taabu ya saani gba: ya wumma n‑ti ya siridima. Ka di yi nyɛ la ba sɔ m bu niŋi Naawunni yɛligu maa yɛdda, u pɔ'a beesigu nin kyɛ ka u niŋi tuuba ka di nin nyɛ la ya beesisuŋŋu zugu, amaa pa la ya yɛttɔ'a zugu.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: Dama ba nin nya ya beesisuŋŋu, ni, ya dɔli la sɔseesi an mari gyirima ni veelim n‑tiri ba.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Yan di kyɛ ka ya veelim yiiri la ya sutura bɔbri ni, hoo zugu paaŋŋu bee salima bansi ni nangɔkɔrisi ni kyinkyinveela soobu;.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: amaa di sum ni di nyɛ la ya sufu ni beesigu bunsɔ'ara zugu, ka ya kpalim n‑sigsiri ya maŋŋa baalimm. Lala sufuveelli maa ku toaai n‑sa'am, ka nyɛ la din mari gyirima Naawunni saani.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Dama lala taabu ka pɔ'aseeba daa bɛ ni, sahanseaa din gaari la. Ba daa nyɛ kasi ka zaŋŋi ba tama n‑niŋi Naawunni ni, ka daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala n‑wulisiri ba veelim ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem. Ba daa wumni n‑tiri ba toori siridima.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Hoo Zaraatu ŋɔn daa wum n‑ti Ibrahim, ka boonni u n daana la seem. Ya nin leebi la u bipunsi, ya yi ti tum din sumsi seem ka di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Ya pɔ'a daandima, see ka ya gba bɛ lala maa taabu: ya teesim pam zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yan ni ya pɔ'aba beesigu ziiya. Ya niŋni ba hoo ya suŋŋniba ban yaa bu paai, amaa ka yan ni ban nin la'am n‑di nyɔvuri hoo ya faari - di nyɛ la Naawunni piini zaa - Ŋɔn tiri ya bunseaa u wurilim zugu. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka seelli ku ta'ai ya wunsusigu.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa, ya zaa la'am m‑bɛ ni taaba suŋŋa. Ya zɔm taaba namboo. Ya wuri taaba hoo mabiitiri. Ya mari namboo ni taaba ka di duusiri ya mansi.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Yan di zaŋŋi beeri n‑yɔ beeri, bee, tuuri n‑labsi tuuri; amaa kyɛ ka ya labsigu nyɛ la: ya susi bunsuma n‑tiri ba. Dama dinŋɔ zugu ka ba daa booli ya, ni ya ti di Naawunni bunsuma maa faari.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Sɔkam ŋɔn nin wuri duniya wa beesigu Ni u ti nya dabsisuma - Di sumni u gbaai la u zilimni ka kyɛ yɛttɔ'abeoo, Ka u noori mun di mi ziri yɛla.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: U yim tumbeeri ni Ka tumni la tunveela; U boom beesisuŋŋu n‑dɔli ka.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Dama Naawunni nini gɔsiri la yɛlimaŋni dima yɛla, Ka u tuba wumni la ba wunsusigu. Amaa u nindaa ŋmaligi ni Ka kyɛ u kpaari m‑basi tumbeeri dima la.

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Ya nini yi moaai tunsuma tumpu ni, ŋɔni nin daam ya?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: Halli ya yi ti nya wahala ya tunsuma maa zugu, ya mari la zugsuŋŋu. Ni, Ya di zɔ dabeem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban ŋɔn zɔri bunseesi la zugu; di kyɛ ka ba kpeesi ya dabeem.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Ya sufu ni teesa puuni ya tim Kirisita gyirima hoo ya Dugma. Sɔkam yi ti boosi ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yan mari tama seem maa, ya kyɛ ka ya pun sɛ'asi ni ya labsi n‑wulisi lala nira di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: Amaa ya tɔ'asim yɛttɔ'anya'asa n‑ti u baalimm, ka ti u gyirima. Ka di kyɛ ka ya tum taalli ka ya sufu daamni ya; lala nin kyɛ ka ninvugseeba ban zaŋŋi ya Kirisita puuni beesisuŋŋu n‑galimni ya la nin ti di vi dizugu.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: Ya yi ti tum tunsuma ka ba namsi ya dizugu, Naawunni yuubu yi ti zaali ya lala, di sɔ ni ya tum tumbeeri ka nya namsigu dizugu.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Ya bu nya? Kirisita daa kpi yimmu ti tumbeeri zugu, ka a yɛla naai; ŋɔna yɛlimaŋni zaa daana daa kpi tirim halibeoo dima zugu, ni u ti zaŋŋi ti n‑taasi Naawunni saani. Ba daa ku u ningbiŋŋu, amaa u siiya daa doaai n‑na vu.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: Ka lala ka u daa kyaŋŋi nti mooli yɛligu n‑ti ninvugseeba ban kpari dansarika ni la siisi.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: Ban n nyɛ seeba ban daa za'asi Naawunni noori sahanseaa din gaari la, Nuhu saha maa; halli ni Naawunni daa di sugru n‑yuui, Nuhu ŋɔn daa maanni nyarintitaari maa dahinseesi la. Ka lala nyariŋŋu puuni ka niriba beela kɔkɔa - di nyɛ la babanii - daa nya fa m‑basi, ka koom maa daa bu di ba.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: Tɔ, ka lala koom wulisi ya ya Naawunni kɔsuubu yɛla, din fa ya m‑basi seem. Di pa la ya su koom m‑peaai ya ningbina da'ari m‑basi, amaa ya ŋmaligi Naawunni saani ni u ti peaai ya sufu ni taalli m‑basi. Ka u niŋi lala Yisa Kirisita kum yisigri zugu.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa daa pun paai arizana ni nti bɛ Naawunni nudirigu ziiya, ka u su malaakadima fukumsi, ni naama ni kpamni zaa dinni m‑paasi.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Ya yiisim din bu veela zaa m‑basi - yɔmsa, ni ziri, ni nyuuri, ni nangbammalisim.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Ya nyɛ la hoo bileesi ka ba dɔ'ai ya Naawunni niriba maa ni. Dizugu ya kyɛ ka Naawunni Sii bisim kɔkɔ kɔm mari ya, dama buna wa n nin suŋŋi ya ka ya zoaai na n‑nya tiliginsim.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ya pun nyu bu, ka baŋŋi ti Dugma ŋɔn veela seem.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Ya vuum ya mansi m‑pɔri u. Ŋɔna n nyɛ hoo kugri, halli ni u nɛ ni maa - kugseaa niriba ŋɔn za'asi, amaa kana ka Naawunni yiisi ka u nya ka ka veela.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Ka yarima gba ŋmani la u kuga, ka ya gba n nyɛ seeba ban nɛa. Ba zaŋŋni ya m‑meeri Sii Kasi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri. Ka ya laa n‑nyɛ u gyeeŋŋu toondaandima la, ka mari maaligu na - bunkasiseesi ban maali n‑tiri Naawunni ka u sakki n‑deaari a la, Yisa Kirisita zugu.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Litaafi yɛliya#:- Gɔsim man ŋɔn yiisi sunsukka kugri n‑ti Zayɔn, Bunveelli ka n gamsi n‑tisi: Ka sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda muna - Ŋɔna n ku di vi, halli beela.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔ wulisiya, ni, ya yɛdda dima maa saani, di nin veeli n‑ti ya; amaa ban ka yɛdda dima maa ŋɔna, u yɛ n‑ŋmani la hoo din sɔbi seem, ni#:- Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la - Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: Ni#:- Kugseaa kan nin kyɛ ka niriba tuui n nyɛ ka, Ni tampiiya ŋɔn kyɛ ka ba lu tiŋŋa. Ba nin ŋmɛ ba nɔba n‑tuui ka saani n‑lu, dama ba daa bu niŋi yɛligu maa yɛdda. Di daa pun bɛ la lala n‑guri ba.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Yarima gba ka ba daa sɔbi ya yɛla, ni, zuliyaseaa ban yiisi maa, naam gyeeŋŋu toondaandima, Naawunni niriba butiri, ban daa yiisi ninvugseeba n‑zaali kpaŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni ni ba ti mooli Naawunni gyirima yɛla - dama ŋɔn Naawunni daa booli ya n‑yiisi ya zibsim ni, ni ya ti kpɛ u alahazibu neesim ni la.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Ya yɛla gba ka u daa tɔ'asi, ni#:- Sahanseaa sa la ya daa pa u niriba, Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya yi nyɛ la Naawunni niriba. Sahanseaa sa maa Naawunni sugru daa ka ya ni, Amaa pampaŋŋɔ u sugru maa paai ya na.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: N kpaamni ya ni, m mabiisi, dama ya kur nyɛ la saamma m‑bɛ ya kyɛnni ni, duniya wa puuni, ni, ya gbaam ya mansi ka kyɛ ningbina kɔri kan zabri n‑sa'amni ya siiya yɛla.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Ka bɛ beesisuŋŋu ban bu dɔli Naawunni maa saani. Ka halli ba yi daŋŋi n‑tuuri ya m‑boonni ya, ni, ninvugbeeri, lala nin kyɛ ka ba nin laa n‑nya ya tunsuma ka mari pɛ'abu n‑tiri Naawunni, u kyɛnni na dabsiri daari maa.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Ya zaŋŋma ya mansi n‑sakki n‑ti fukumsi yiiko kam zaa an bɛ saalidima saani, ti Dugma maa zugu. Di yi nyɛ la Naaba.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: bee, u tuumuseeba ŋɔn daa tum ni ba darigi ban niŋi taalli la tuba ka pɛ'ai ban niŋi suŋŋa maa.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Dama lala n nyɛ Naawunni yuubu: ni, ya ti niŋi suŋŋa ka dizugu ya nin kyɛ ka ban ka bamsim la kpalim la surimm, ka ku laa n‑tɔ'asiri ba yɛttɔ'ayɔya la.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Ya bɛ ni hoo ban su ba maŋŋa la, amaa yan di zaŋŋi ya mansuulinsi maa n‑ligi ya mansi ka tumni tumbeeri. Kur kyɛ ka ya bɛ ni hoo Naawunni tuntumniba.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Ya tim sɔkam gyirima hoo din sum ni u seem. Ya yɛddaniŋniba maa - ya wuri taaba; ya zɔm Naawunni; ya gyeemni NaaTitaari maa.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Ya seeba ban nyɛ daaba, ya zaŋŋma ya mansi n‑wumni n‑tiri ya dugudima ni gyirima, pa la ban niŋni ya suŋŋa la kɔkɔa, amaa halli ban mari ya ni putoo la gba.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Dama di mari la pɛ'abu, ninsaala yi bu tum taalli ka deaai putoo tubdarigri ni sugru, ŋɔn mi Naawunni zugu.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Ya bu nya? - ya yi tum taalli ka ba bu ya di zugu, ya yi deaai di ni sugru, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu, bee? - Ay, pa lala! Amaa ya yi nya namsigu ya tunsuma zugu ka deaai di ni sugru, dina m mari pɛ'abu Naawunni saani.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: Dinŋɔ zugu ka ba daa booli ya, dama Kirisita daa nya namsigu ya zugu, ka lala u daa kyɛ n‑zaali n‑wulisiri, ni, ya gba dɔli u soori maa.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: Ni#:- Ŋɔna daa bu niŋi taaliseaa, Ka ziri bee yɔmsa mun daa zi n‑yi u noori ni na.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
