local: Ban daa tu u maa, u daa bu tu n‑labsi; ban daa namsi u la, u daa bu vɔlisi sɔa. Amaa u daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑ti la Ŋɔn katti sariya ni yɛlimaŋni maa.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: U daa deaai ti tumbeeri taalli n‑niŋi u saani u kum dɔpulli maa zugu; ni di ti kyɛ ka ti kpi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ala'akyi ni, ka laa n‑doaai na nti bɛ yɛlimaŋni soori ni. U dansi maa zugu kyɛ ka ti nya alaafɛa.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Ya daa ŋmanni la hoo peesi ban bɔrigi; amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya labi ya pɛgura saani na, pɛgurisɔ ŋɔn guri ya nyɔvuya suŋŋa.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Mani Saimoon Piita, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya tuumu, ni u daabri - mani n sɔbri n‑tiri ya. Yarima ka Naawunni Yɛlimaŋni Daana ni ti Tiligra Yisa Kirisita, daa kyɛ ka ya nya soori ni ya niŋi yɛdda - yɛdda tibsiri din ŋmanni ti dinni wa.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Naawunni wurilim piini ni u sufudoaa bɛ ya saani, n‑kyɛ ka ya mi ti Dugma Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yɛla wa, halli zaa.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Ŋɔn Naawunni yiiko tiri ti tin boori seelli zaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti beesigu ni ti gyeeŋŋu yɛla. Dama ti baŋŋi u, ni, u booli ti ni ti ti paai u maŋtoori gyirima ni u veelim saani na.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Ka dizugu ka ti deaai u nangbambasiri la an galisi ka tibsa zaa. Anŋɔ zugu ka ya mari tɔrli u beesigu la ni, ka ya za'asi duniya wa kɔribeeri ni Sintaani yɔmsa an mari sa'aŋŋu la.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: Lala zugu ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi ka ya zaŋŋi beesisuŋŋu m‑paasi ya yɛdda zugu; ka laa n‑zaŋŋi bamsim m‑paasi ya beesisuŋŋu wa zugu;.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: ka mari ya mansi m‑paasi bamsim zugu; ka laa m‑paasi sugru ni Naawunni gyeeŋŋu sɔsuŋŋu anŋɔ zaa zugu.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: M‑paasi ya gyeeŋŋu zugu, ya mari nirilim n‑tiri taaba, ka din paasi bunseelli kam zaa nyɛ la wurilim.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ya yi mari anŋɔ zaa ka a yi bɛ ya saani n‑zoaai, ya ku zi yɔri n‑ka bukaata; ya nin mi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa bu mi anŋɔa, u nyɛ la zooma ka u nini bu neaai suŋŋa; ka u laa n‑tam, ni, u daa su koom n‑yi kasi u tumbɛseesi ŋɔn daa pun tum la puuni.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Lala zugu, m mabiisi, ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi sahakam zaa n‑sakkri ti Dugma Naawunni booligu, dama ŋɔna n yiisi ya. Ya kyɛ ka u yɛla zɛ suŋŋa ya saani. Ya yi niŋi lala ya ku laa n‑labi nyaaŋŋa.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ya nin nya soori n‑kpɛ Naawunni yiri naam maa ni, Yisa Kirisita ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma ni ti Tiligra zugu. Ka u mun naam ka naari.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: Lala zugu man na kpalim n‑teaari ya anŋɔ yɛla - halli ni ya mun pun mi a ni, ka kpalim n‑zɛ yummu n‑kpamsi yɛlimaŋni zugu, yɛlimaŋni seaa yan mari pampaŋŋɔ la.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: N teesiya, ni, di tuya, ni, sahanseaa man ŋɔn na kpalim m‑bɛ n nyɔvuri ni la, ka bɛ n ningbiŋŋu wa puuni, n nin kpalim n‑teaari ya sahakam zaa.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: M mi halli, ni, di ku yuui ka n nin ti ka ni, hoo ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn wulisi ma seem la.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: Amaa n nin kpaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka ya na kur nin nyari teaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛla wa ni - halli n yi ti laa n‑ka ni.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Ya bu nya? - Ti daa bu dɔli sɔlinseesi ninsaala ŋɔn zaŋŋi u putɔyam n‑tɔ'asi, ni a ti yɔmsi niriba. Ay! Ti daa yɛli ya yɛlimaŋni lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yiiko, ni u lappu na yɛla. U gyirima yɛla ka ti nini daa nya, ka ti mammansi diri la a zaa sɛhara.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: Dama u daa deaai gyirima, ni naam tibsim, ti Ba Naawunni saani; kukɔri la ŋɔn daa yi Naawunni Yiiko Daana saani m‑paai ti na n‑yɛli, ni, M Bii nŋɔa; n wuri u ni. N nini n tigi u pam.

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Ka lala kukɔri daa yi sangbana ni na ka tirima mammaŋŋa daa wum ka, tin ni ŋɔna n daa la'am m‑bɛ Naawunni zoori zugu la.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
local: Lala ka ti nin toaai m‑paasi ti yɛdda niŋbu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi anabidima yɛttɔ'a maa yɛlimaŋni la. Dizugu ya nin kpaŋŋi ya mansi ka wum n‑ti ba yɛla - hoo yan yi nya neesim ŋɔn yi neaai zibsim ni seem la, halli ka beoo ti neaai ka bɛŋmariga maa ŋɔn ya pusi n‑neaari ya sufu ni la.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: See ka ya daŋŋi m‑baŋŋi dinŋɔa: Litaafi yɛttɔ'azaala gbinni bamsim pa la ninvugyinni kɔkɔ yɛttɔ'a - sɔ ku toaai n‑su a.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: Dama anabi sɔsɔ bu zaŋŋi u maŋtoori sufu yuubu n‑yiisiri u yɛttɔ'azaala maa na; Naawunni saani ka ninsaalidima wa daa deaari yɛttɔ'a maa, sahanseaa Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko ŋɔn daa wulisiri ba a yɛla.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Amaa ziri anabidima daa bɛ Naawunni niriba maa ni lala saha maa. Dizugu di yi pa seem lala ziri karimbadima nin ti bɛ ya gba sunsukkuni. Ba nin zi sa'aŋŋu na, ni ziri yɛttɔ'a an wɔligri niriba. Ka ba nin kyiisi, ni, ba bu mi ba Daana sɔ ŋɔn daa fa ba m‑basi la, ka za'asi u yɛla maa. Dizugu ba nin sa'am ba maŋtoori yɛla m‑basi yooma.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: Lala maa zugu, niriba pam gba nin ti dɔli ba tunyɔya maa, ka lala maa zugu nin kyɛ ka seeba nya soori ka ba tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑tuuri yaseeba ban dɔli yɛlimaŋni soori maa.

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
local: Ba zinyuu maa puuni ka ziri karimbadima wa nin sɔlim ziri sɔlima ni ba ti nya nyoori ya saani; amaa m‑mari nti paai zuna, Ŋɔn ya kari ba sariya gba siri biiya; ka din sa'amni ba maa gba bu gbisira.

eng: God didnt spare the angels when they sinned but cast them into the lowest level of the underworld and committed them to chains of darkness, keeping them there until the judgment.
local: Naawunni daa bu kyɛ halli malaakadima galiŋŋu n‑ti ba - baseeba ban daa tum taalli maa - amaa u daa zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi la mugsigu ni, ka ba ba ba zibsim ni. Bɛni ka u guri ba halli ni ba sariya karibu daari.

eng: And he didnt spare the ancient world when he brought a flood on the world of ungodly people, even though he protected Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
local: M‑paasi lala, Naawunni daa bu kyɛ duniyakurigu dima taalli, amaa u daa zi saa koom na ka bu ti di ninvugyɔya wa zaa; ŋɔn daa kyɛ sɔ maa mun daa nyɛ la Nuhu, dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa mooli Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori la. Nuhu daa tiligi - ŋɔn ni niriba ayɔpɔi kɔkɔa, seeba ban daa zɛ kasi.

eng: God condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to total destruction, reducing them to ashes as a warning to ungodly people.
local: Naawunni daa laa n‑sa'am Sɔdoom ni Gɔmɔra tinsi, ka daa zaŋŋi bugum n‑nyɔ a zaa. Lala ka u daa wulisi bunseelli din nin niŋi ninvugseeba ban za'asi u soori maa.

eng: And he rescued righteous Lot, who was made miserable by the unrestrained immorality of unruly people. .
local: Amaa u daa fa Lɔti m‑basi. Bɔ zugu? Lɔti daa nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu. U daa nyari ninvugseeba ban daa za'asi kaari ni sɔsuma zaa la, ka nya ba tumbeeri an daa nyɛ seem, ka u sufu daa sa'am di zugu.

eng: While that righteous man lived among them he felt deep distress every day on account of the immoral actions he saw and heard.
local: Dɔsuŋŋu wa daa kur bɛ la ba saani dabsiri kam zaa. U daa bɛ ni n‑nyari ba tunkyɔ'ama ka wumni ba ala'akyi damni, ka di daa diisi u wahala pam.

eng: These things show that the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from their trials, and how to keep the unrighteous for punishment on the Judgment Day.
local: Anŋɔ zaa zugu ka ya ti baŋŋi, ni, ti Dugma siri mi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑fa ninvugsuma m‑basi; ka laa m‑mi sariya karibu yɛla, ka kyɛ ka ninvugseeba ban bu dɔli soori la bɛ wahala puuni halli ni ba sariya karibu daari.

eng: This is especially true for those who follow after the corrupt cravings of the sinful nature and defy the Lords authority. These reckless, brash people arent afraid to insult the glorious ones.
local: Ka niriba wa seeba gaari ba zaa - di nyɛ la: baseeba ban zaŋŋi ba zikki zaa n‑niŋi ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi ni, ka za'asi kpamma nɔya wumpu. Lala niriba ba'a ka sɔsɔ yɛla ni, saazugu gyirima daandima ka ba bu zɔra, ka laa n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'abeeri ka zaŋŋi ba sufukpeemni n‑tuuri ba.

eng: yet angels, who are stronger and more powerful, dont use insults when pronouncing the Lords judgment on them.
local: Halli malaakadima gba - ka ba mari yaa ni kpeeŋŋu n‑gaari ziri karimbadima wa - malaakadima maa bu tuuri n‑figsiri gyirima daandima wa, see ka ba boonni ba Naawunni sariya karibu zii na n‑fabinni n‑wulisiri ba taalli ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: These false teachers are like irrational animals, mere creatures of instinct, born to be captured and destroyed. They slander what they dont understand and, like animals, they will be destroyed.
local: Niriba wa tataabu kur ŋmanni la hoo moo ni bunkɔbri. Ba dɔ'am ka anfaani see ka niriba nin ti gbaai ba n‑kɔri. Ba nyɛ la ban zeemni n‑tuuri bunseesi ban bu mi a gbinni la. Ba nin ti baasi yɔri.

eng: In this way, they will receive payment for their wrongdoing. They even enjoy unruly parties in broad daylight. They are blots and blemishes, taking delight in their seductive pleasures while feasting with you.
local: Ba nin ti di wahala, ba n daa niŋi niriba wahala maa zugu. Ba nini ku ti tigi, di yi pa la ba tumni ba ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi tumbeeri paalu ni. Ka halli ba yi paasi ya zugu, ya bundira diibu la'aŋŋu ni, ba laa n‑kpalim n‑nyɛ da'ari dima ban tumni vi tuma: ka ba sufu maai, ban yɔmsiri ya la zugu.

eng: They are always looking for someone with whom to commit adultery. They are always on the lookout for opportunities to sin. They ensnare people whose faith is weak. They have hearts trained in greed. They are under Gods curse.
local: Ba nini kur gɔsiri niriba pɔ'aba ka boori ba; ba dɔli la tumbeeri nimbirimoaa. Ba bɛtti ba yɛla hoo kyɛ'ariga ni ba ti gbaasi ninvugseeba ban bu zɛ yummu. Ba moori ni ba ti bamsi tumbeeri sɔya zaa an nyɛ seem. Mbusu bɛ ba zugu!.

eng: Leaving the straight path, they have gone off course, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the payment of doing wrong.
local: Lala niriba pun kyɛ sɔsuŋŋu m‑burigi, ka dɔli la Beelɛm soori. Ŋɔn Beelɛm daa nyɛ Beoori biiya. Anabi n daa nyɛ u, amaa u daa boori la ligri yoori - bunseaa ka u ya n‑deaai taalli tuma zugu.

eng: But Balaam was rebuked for his wrongdoing. A donkey, which has no voice, spoke with a human voice and put a stop to the prophets madness.
local: Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka - halli ni buŋŋa bu tɔ'asiri maa zaa yɔri - u buŋŋa maa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u dirindaari maa. Buŋŋa daa tɔ'asi hoo ninsaala n‑ta'ai anabi maa yinyaari tuma.

eng: These false teachers are springs without water, mists driven by the wind. The underworld has been reserved for them.
local: Yaasa, lala niriba ŋmanni la hoo kuliseesi an ka koom, bee, hoo sangbanseesi sanzeoo ŋɔn zeeri ka a kyɛnni la. Ba gbeebu ziiya pun maali siri ni ka ti deaai ba, zibɛseaa kan zibsa libilibi.

eng: With empty, self-important speech, they use sinful cravings and unrestrained immorality to ensnare people who have only just escaped life with those who have wandered from the truth.
local: Ba tɔ'asiri la karimbaani yɛttɔ'aseesi an mari gyɛrilim. Ka ba zaŋŋni ba ningbinkɔrisi tuma n‑yɔmsiri niriba - ninvugseeba ban na yi ziri yɛla ni n‑niŋni tuuba ka di bu yuui.

eng: These false teachers promise freedom, but they themselves are slaves of immorality; whatever overpowers you, enslaves you.
local: Ba yɛtti, ni, ba nin wulisi lala niriba sumaŋŋa soori, halli ni ba mammansi bu su ba mansi. Ay! - ba nyɛ la daaba n‑tiri yɛlikyɔ'ama. Yɛlimaŋni nla, dama bunseelli kam ninvugsɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka ka nyaŋŋi u, u nyɛ la kanŋɔ daabri.

eng: If people escape the moral filth of this world through the knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ, then get tangled up in it again and are overcome by it, they are worse off than they were before.
local: Ka dinŋɔna, ba nyɛ la seeba ban daa daŋŋi n‑zɔ n‑yi duniya wa yɛla ni, hoo sɔkyɛnniba ŋɔn yi pa'ari ni m‑paai neesim zii seem maa. Ka ba daa baŋŋi ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya, ti Tiligra la, yɛla pɔi, amaa ka naayi labi nyaaŋŋa ka kyɛ ka duniya yɛlibeeri laa n‑nyaŋŋi ba. Di yi bɛ lala, ba naari nyɛ la bumbeoo, ka gaari ba piiligu beeri ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem la.

eng: It would be better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having come to know it, to turn back from the holy commandment entrusted to them.
local: Ba yi daa bu mi Naawunni sɔseaa kan basiri la taalli ni galiŋŋu, di naa n‑sɔa. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba pun mi soori pɔi, ka laa n‑ŋmaligi n‑labi ka za'asi zaalisunseaa ban daa deaai Naawunni nuusi ni la.

eng: They demonstrate the truth of the proverb: A dog returns to its own vomit, and a washed sow wallows in the mud.
local: Yɛlimaŋni ka ti kpamma yɛliya, ni#:- Baa tiira, ka laa n‑ŋmaligi n‑diri di. ka laa n‑yɛtti, ni#:- I yi peaai kurikyuu, u nin labi n‑sigi baya'ari ni. Niriba wa yɛla wulisiri ti lala nyɛsa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Ti sakkiya, ni, Yisa Kirisita daa namsiya pam, ŋɔn daa bɛ ni hoo ninsaala sahaseaa la: dinŋɔna ya gba sɛ'asim ya mansi n‑di namsigu; dama ŋɔnkam sakki n‑ti ningbiŋŋu namsigu pun yiisi u maŋŋa tumbeeri yɛla ni.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Lala nin kyɛ ka u tumni Naawunni yuubu halli ni u nyɔvuri naari saha, ka pa la ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi tuma.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Ya daa yuui pam, dabsa an gaari la, ka ya beesigu daa ŋmani la hoo ban bu mi Naawunni beesigu la, m‑bɛ ni ya yuubu zugu, m‑mari ningbiŋŋu kɔribeeri, n‑nyuuri daam, n‑deemni deenyɔya, ka gyeemni ba'ayuya an bu dɔli soori.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: Di yi niŋi ba alahazibu pam, yan bu laa n‑la'amni ba saani m‑bɛ ban bɛ beesiseaa ni maa, kan nyɛ bumbeoo zaa maa zugu; ka ba tuuri ya di zugu.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Amaa ba nyɛ la seeba ban nin ti wulisi ba duniya wa beesigu yɛla ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem n‑ti Naawunni, dama u pun maali siri ni u ti kari ninvugvuya ni kpiimma zaa sariya la.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: Lala zugu ka ba daa yiisi lahabasuŋŋu wa'azu n‑ti ninvugseeba ban pun kpi la gba. Ban daa bɛ ninsaalidima beebu sahanseaa la, ba daa kari ba sɔkam duniya wa ni sariya maa, di nyɛ la kum sariya. Amaa Sii Kasi yiiko zugu ba nin ti bɛ nyɔvuri ni Naawunni saani.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Bunseelli kam zaa naari ni maa, dizugu ya mari ya mansi n‑guri suŋŋa, ka susiri Naawunni.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Din gaari a zaa nyɛ la, ya zaŋŋma ya sufu zaa n‑wuri taaba ni yɛlimaŋni, dama wurilim sɔ'ai la tumbeeri pam taalli.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Ya deaam taaba ya yiya ni hoo saamma, ka di fabli di zugu m‑mɔŋŋi taaba.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Dama Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi piiniseesi n‑ti ya la, ya sɔkam zaŋŋma ŋɔn deaai piiniseaa n‑tumni n‑suŋŋi u taaba - hoo yiri dima ŋɔn suŋŋni taaba n‑tumni ba yiri tuma zaa seem.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Dinŋɔna ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a, ŋɔn tɔ'asim Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑ti u la; ka ŋɔn tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba, ŋɔn zaŋŋma yaseaa Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti u la n‑tumna. Lala zugu ka Naawunni nin nya pɛ'abu Yisa Kirisita zugu, dama ŋɔn Yisa nyɛ yiiko daana ni gyirima daana, n‑su a ni, halli ni saha din ka naari. Ammi .

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: M mabiwutta, ya yi nyari wahala hoo bugum ŋɔn vuuli ŋɔn paari ya seem la, yan di teesiri, ni, din na zi n‑niŋi sɔ n niŋi ya maa.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Amaa ya mari sufupeelli, yan ni Masiiya maa ŋɔn la'am n‑namsi la zugu, dama ya nin mari sufumaasim pam sahanseaa u gyirima ŋɔn ti yi paalu na la.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ba yi galim ya ka tuuri ya Kirisita yuuri zugu, ya mari la zugsuŋŋu, dama Naawunni Sii Kasi ni u gyirima bɛ ya ni.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Tɔ; amaa ya sɔ di leebi ŋɔn kuuri niriba, bee n‑zuura, bee n‑tumni tumbeeri zaa, halli yan di kpeeri niriba yɛla ni gba, ka nya namsigu lala taalli zugu.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Amaa nira yi nya namsigu ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita dinni zugu, ŋɔn di kyɛ ka vi mari u, amaa ŋɔn pɛ'am Naawunni ba n zaŋŋi Kirisita yuuri m‑boonni u la zugu.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Saha paaya ni sariya karibu piili. Naawunni niriba mun ya daŋŋi tooni sariya maa ni: tɔ - ka di yi piili ti saani, wula ka di ti ya n‑naai ban bu sakki n‑wum Naawunni yɛligu lahabasuŋŋu la saani?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: Di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- Di yi tɔa, ni ban dɔli Naawunni la n‑nya tiliginsim: Tɔ, ka ban za'asi u yɛla, ni ala'akyi dima muna - Di ya n‑niŋi ba la wula.

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Dinzugu ninvugseeba ban nyari namsigu Naawunni yuubu la zugu. Tɔ - di sumni ni ba kur tumni din tuusi ka zaŋŋi ba nyɔvuri n‑niŋi ba Naamna nuusi ni, Sɔ ŋɔn kur zɛ u nangbambasiri nyaaŋŋa la.

eng: We announce to you what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have seen and our hands handled, about the word of life.
local: Ti sɔbri n‑tiri ya la nyɔvuri yɛligu maa zugu. Yɛligu maa daa bɛ ni piiligu ni sa. Tin daa wum bunseesi maa ka ti ya n‑sɔbi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u yɛla maa, ni tin daa gɔsi seesi, ka ti nini nya, ka ti daa zaŋŋi ti nuusi n‑siisi.

eng: The life was revealed, and we have seen, and we testify and announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us.
local: Nyɔvuri maa n daa yi paalu na, ka ti daa nya; di zugu ka ti tɔ'asiri m‑moonni lahabaari n‑tiri ya. Ti lahabaari nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka naari wa yɛla, nyɔvuridaansɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ Naawunni ti Ba saani la, ka pampaŋŋɔ u yi paalu na ka ti nya.

eng: What we have seen and heard, we also announce it to you so that you can have fellowship with us. Our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
local: Tin nya ka wum seelli maa ka ti moonni n‑tiri ya gba, ni ti ti kyɛ ka tin ni yan nin la'am m‑bɛ ni. Halli ti beebu nin ti bɛ ti Ba saani, ni u Bii Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani.

eng: We are writing these things so that our joy can be complete.
local: Ti sɔbi kanŋɔ ni ti zaa sufumaasim leebi bummunni zaa.

eng: This is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light and there is no darkness in him at all.
local: Tɔ; lahabaseaa tin daa wum Naawunni Bii maa saani ka moonni n‑tiri ya nŋɔa#:- Naawunni nyɛ la neesim, ka zibsim halli fiiŋ ka u saani.

eng: If we claim, We have fellowship with him, and live in the darkness, we are lying and do not act truthfully.
local: Dinzugu, ti yi yɛtti, ni, ti bɛ la u beesigu maa taabu amaa ka na kpalim m‑bɛ zibsim ni lala saha maa, ti ma'ari la ziri ti yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni ti beesigu maa ni m‑paasi.

eng: But if we live in the light in the same way as he is in the light, we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from every sin.
local: Amaa ti yi bɛ neesim ni - hoo Ŋɔn bɛ neesim ni seem maa - dinŋɔna tin ni ŋɔn beesigu maa sakkri n‑tiri taaba. Ka Naawunni Bii Yisa ŋɔn daa kpi n‑ti ti maa zugu, u ziim nin ti peaari ti n‑yiisiri ti tumbeeri zaa taalli ni.

eng: If we claim, We dont have any sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
local: Ti yi ti yɛli, ni, ti ka la tumbeeri, ti yɔmsiri la ti maŋŋa ka yɛlimaŋni ka ti ni.

eng: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from everything weve done wrong.
local: Amaa ti yi ti yiisi ti tumbeeri yɛla n‑yɛli Naawunni, u nin niŋi hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la, ka tum din nyɛ deede; u nin kyɛ ti tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ti, ka nyeesi ti galiŋŋu yɛla ni a da'ari zaa m‑basi.

eng: If we claim, We have never sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.
local: Amaa ti yi yɛtti, ni, ti bu tum tumbeeri, lala gbinni n nyɛ, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la ziri daana; dizugu ti yi yɛli lala di wulisi, ni, u yɛligu ka ti ni.

eng: My little children, Im writing these things to you so that you dont sin. But if you do sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
local: Tɔ, m biisi, dinŋɔ ka n sɔbri n‑tiri ya na, ni ya di tum tumbeeri. Amaa sɔ yi ti tum tumbeeri, u mari la sɔ ŋɔn zɔri sindaani u zugu n‑tiri Naawunni ti Ba maa - Yisa Kirisita nla, sɔ ŋɔn tumni yɛlimaŋni tuma deede.

eng: He is Gods way of dealing with our sins, not only ours but the sins of the whole world.
local: Ka ŋɔn Kirisita leebi la maaligu kan ti ti soori n‑kyɛ ka ti nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi ti tumbeeri zugu. Ka di pa la tirima kɔkɔ tumbeeri, amaa sɔkam zaa dina.

eng: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
local: Tin dɔli Naawunni zaaligu zugu nin wulisi, ni, ti siri mi u.

eng: The one who claims, I know him, while not keeping his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in this person.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi yɛli, ni, M mi u. ka bu dɔli u zaaligu maa, lala nira nyɛ la ziri daana ka yɛlimaŋni ka u ni.

eng: But the love of God is truly perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know we are in him.
local: Amaa ŋɔn dɔli u yɛligu maa nyɛ la sɔ Naawunni wurilim maa ŋɔn siri paali u zaa. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti niŋi yɛdda, ni, ti la'am m‑bɛ Naawunni saani.

eng: My dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. I have written both letters to stir up your sincere understanding with a reminder.
local: Tɔ, n zɔsuma, gbaŋŋu kanŋɔ paasi la gbana ayi man pun sɔbi n‑ti ya. Gbana maa zaa puuni ka n boori ni ya yam ti neaai yɛlisuma zaa puuni.

eng: The one who claims to remain in him ought to live in the same way as he lived.
local: Ŋɔnkam yɛnni, ni, u la'am m‑bɛ la Yisa saani, di sumni ni di daama bɛ la hoo Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

eng: I want you to recall what the holy prophets foretold as well as what the Lord and savior commanded through your apostles.
local: M boori la ya teaai Naawunni anabisuma maa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi sahanseaa sa la; ka laa n‑teaai Naawunni tuumudima maa ŋɔn daa yɛli yɛliseesi n‑ti ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti Dugma, ti Tiligra, zaaligu la.

eng: Dear friends, Im not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the message you heard.
local: N zɔdima: di pa la n sɔbri la zaalipaaliseaa n‑tiri ya na, amaa n teaari ya la bunkurigu yɛla, seaa yan daa deaai piiligu ni la. Zaalikurigu wa kur nyɛ la lahabaseaa yan daa pun wum la.

eng: Most important, know this: in the last days scoffers will come, jeering, living by their own cravings.
local: Tɔ: man ya n‑daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya seem nŋɔa: ni, n boori la ya baŋŋi, ni, naari dabsa yi ti paai na, ninvugseeba nin yi na ban nin dɔli ba sufu ni teesibeeri n‑niŋni ansarisi ka fiilimni bunsuma.

eng: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.
local: Halli zaalipaalli n nyɛ ka gba, ka n tiri ya; dama ka nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni Yisa Masiiya zugu, ni yɛlimaŋni yarima saani gba. Ka nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala n‑tiri ya gba; dama zibsim la viiri ni, ka yɛlimaŋni neesim neaara.

eng: and saying, Where is the promise of his coming? After all, nothing has changed—not since the beginning of creation, nor even since the ancestors died.
local: Ka ba nin boosi, ni, U daa basi noori, ni, u nin kyɛ na. Ammi, u kyɛ na? Ka u lee? Ti badima pun ka ni, ka seelli kam zaa na kpalim n‑nyɛ la hoo din daa pun nyɛ seem duniya piiligu ni la.

eng: The one who claims to be in the light while hating a brother or sister is in the darkness even now.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi ti yɛli, ni, u bɛ la neesim maa ni, ka yaai u mabiiya, u na kpalim m‑bɛ zibsim ni.

eng: But they fail to notice that, by Gods word, heaven and earth were formed long ago out of water and by means of water.
local: Ba mi halli, ka za'asiri di yɛlimaŋni maa, ni, sahanseaa sa la Naawunni daa yɛli la u yɛligu ka arizana ni duniya daa naam; koom ka u daa zaŋŋi m‑maali lala duniya maa, ka ka yi koom ni na.

eng: The person loving a brother and sister stays in the light, and there is nothing in the light that causes a person to stumble.
local: Ŋɔn mun wuri u mabiiya kpalim m‑bɛ la neesim maa ni, ka seelli ka u ni kan nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka u taraana ti tuui n‑tum taalli.

eng: And it was through these that the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
local: Di mun daa nyɛ la koom ni ka lala duniyakurigu maa daa sa'am n‑naai.

eng: But the person who hates a brother or sister is in the darkness and lives in the darkness, and doesnt know where to go because the darkness blinds the eyes.
local: Amaa ŋɔn mun yaai u mabiiya bɛ la zibsim ni n‑kyɛnna. U bu mi ŋɔn kyɛnni m‑paari seela, dama zibsim maa kyɛ ka u nini bu nyara.

eng: But by the same word, heaven and earth are now held in reserve for fire, kept for the Judgment Day and destruction of ungodly people.
local: Amaa sangbana ni duniya seesi an bɛ ni wa muna - Naawunni daa yɛli u yɛligu yaasa, ni, u ti mari a n‑guri ni a ti nyɔ bugum. Ba mari a n‑guri la ninvugbeeri maa sariya karibu daari, ni ba kari ba sariya ka sa'am ba.

eng: Little children, Im writing to you because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus name.
local: M biisi, man sɔbri n‑tiri ya wa, yan pun deaai ya tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi zugu - ya deaai di Yisa yuuri zugu.

eng: Dont let it escape your notice, dear friends, that with the Lord a single day is like a thousand years and a thousand years are like a single day.
local: M mabiisi, yan di tam yɛlli yinni wa, ni, wɔligri ka ni Naawunni saani: dabsiri yinni ni yuuma tusiri - Ŋɔn saani a zaa nyɛ la yummu.

eng: Parents, Im writing to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, Im writing to you because you have conquered the evil one.
local: M badima, n sɔbri n‑tiri ya gba, Yan mi Sɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ ni zugu - duniya piiligu saha ka u daa bɛ ni. Na'akyimbisi, n sɔbri n‑tiri ya gba, yan pun zabi Sintaani n‑nyaŋŋi u la zugu.

eng: The Lord isnt slow to keep his promise, as some think of slowness, but he is patient toward you, not wanting anyone to perish but all to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ti Dugma Naawunni bu maasi ni u nangbambasiri, hoo seeba ŋɔn teesiri seem maa. Ay! - u kur mari la sugru n‑tiri ya, dama u bu boori sɔ sa'aŋŋu, amaa u boori la sɔkam zaa ti ŋmaligi ka kyɛ u tumbeeri.

eng: Little children, I write to you because you know the Father. Parents, I write to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, I write to you because you are strong, the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
local: M‑paasi yaasa#:- M bibisi, n laa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya, yan mi Naawunni ti Ba la zugu. M badima, n laa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya gba, Yan mi Sɔ Ŋɔn Daa Bɛ ni la zugu - duniya piiligu saha ka u daa bɛ ni. Na'akyimbisi, n laa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya gba, yan mari yaa zugu; ka Naawunni yɛligu ŋɔn bɛ ya ni zugu, ka yan pun zabi Sintaani n‑nyaŋŋi u la.

eng: But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will pass away with a dreadful noise, the elements will be consumed by fire, and the earth and all the works done on it will be exposed.
local: Amaa ti Dugma dabsiri maa siri ya sɔ'ai ni n‑kyɛ na hoo na'ayiga ŋɔn yi sɔ'ari n‑tumni seem la. Lala dabsiri maa ka saazugu nin ti tasi n‑niŋi vuuri pam ka gaari; saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu zaa tuga, ni a neema kam zaa, nin ti di bugum ka lakki m‑basi. Tiŋŋa maa, ni bunsɔ'ara zaa an bɛ ka ni la, nin ti yi paalu.

eng: Dont love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in them.
local: Ya wumma! Yan di wuri duniya wa, bee, bunseesi an bɛ ka ni la. Sɔ ŋɔn boori duniya wa yɛla, ti Ba Naawunni wurilim ka u ni.

eng: Since everything will be destroyed in this way, what sort of people ought you to be? You must live holy and godly lives.
local: Dizugu, m mabiisi, a zaa yi ya sa'am lala, n ya m‑boosi ya n‑nya#:- wula niriba ka di sumni ni ya n nyɛa? Ay! - See ka ya beebu nyɛ kasi ka ya zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑ti Naawunni kɔkɔa!.

eng: Everything that is in the world—the craving for whatever the body feels, the craving for whatever the eyes see and the arrogant pride in ones possessions—is not of the Father but is of the world.
local: Dama bunseelli kam kan bɛ duniya wa ni, hoo ningbinkɔrisi ni bunveela zaa ti nini ŋɔn nya ka boora, ni saalidima ŋɔn gyeemni bunseelli zaa - anŋɔ zaa bu yi la ti Ba Naawunni saani na, amaa duniya buna n nyɛ a, ka yi la duniya wa ni na.

eng: waiting for and hastening the coming day of God. Because of that day, the heavens will be destroyed by fire and the elements will melt away in the flames.
local: Yan guri ti Dugma Naawunni kyɛnni dabsiri na maa, ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi ka ka kyɛ na yooma. Di daari maa ka sangbana nin di bugum n‑sa'am, ka saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu tuga ni a neema zaa nin nyɛligi m‑basi.

eng: And the world and its cravings are passing away, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
local: Ka duniya wa paari ka naari ni, kan ni ka kɔrisi yɛla zaa. Amaa ban tumni Naawunni ŋɔn boori ni ba tum seem la - lala niriba nin kpalim m‑bɛ ni halli saha kan ka naari.

eng: But according to his promise we are waiting for a new heaven and a new earth, where righteousness is at home.
local: Amaa tirima n guri la Naawunni ŋɔn basi noori, ni, u ti tisi bunseelli kam zaa maa: ni, sangbampaala ni duniyapaalli ka u nin ti tisi, bɛni ka yɛlimaŋni nin ti mari ka yiri n‑gbeeri sahakam.

eng: Little children, it is the last hour. Just as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. This is how we know it is the last hour.
local: M biisi, saha maa nŋɔa. Ba daa pun yɛli ya, ni, Masiiya Dindaana maa nin ti kyɛ na; tɔ, ka u dindaandima pam pun kyɛ na. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, saha maa nŋɔa.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, make every effort to be found by him in peace—pure and faultless.
local: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna, m mabiwutta, ya mi halli, ni, lala Naawunni dabsiri siri nin kyɛ na. Ka di zugu ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑kyɛ ka u nin nya ya yɛlimaŋni beesigu ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni, di ka taalli bee da'aseesi; m‑paasi yan ni ŋɔn Naawunni nangbanyinni ni beebu la.

eng: They went out from us, but they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But by going out from us, they showed they all are not part of us.
local: Ba daa yi ti saani m‑bakki, amaa ba daa pa la ti niriba ni yɛlimaŋni; ba yi siri daa nyɛ ti niriba, ba naa n‑kpalim ti saani. Ban yi m‑bakki seem la zugu ka ba wulisiri, ni, ba zaa pa la ti niriba.

eng: Consider the patience of our Lord to be salvation, just as our dear friend and brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.
local: Kur teaama, ni, di nyɛ la ti Dugma ŋɔn di sugru zugu ka ninsaalidima nin nya tiliginsim. Lala ka ti zɔsuŋŋu Pɔla gba daa pun sɔbi n‑wulisi ya. Ti Dugma zaŋŋi bamsim n‑ti u, ka suŋŋi u ni u ti wulisiri yɛlimaŋni yɛla.

eng: But you have an anointing from the holy one, and all of you know the truth.
local: Amaa yarim zaa pun deaai Naawunni kpaam soosigu, lala zugu ya zaa baŋŋiya.

eng: speaking of these things in all his letters. Some of his remarks are hard to understand, and people who are ignorant and whose faith is weak twist them to their own destruction, just as they do the other scriptures.
local: U sɔbri anŋɔ taabu n‑niŋi u gbana zaasa puuni. Amaa u gbana maa ni ka ya'aseesi m bɛ ni an bamsim tɔ ni: ninvugseeba ban ka yam ka ku toaai n‑zɛ yummu bɛ ni, ka lala niriba zaŋŋni Pɔla yɛla n‑takkri n‑gyɛtta - hoo ban takkri Naawunni yɛliseesi zaa gba - ka dizugu ba sa'amni ba maŋtoori yɛla m‑basira.

eng: I dont write to you because you dont know the truth but because you know it. You know that no lie comes from the truth.
local: Din kyɛ ka n sɔbri yɛla wa n‑tiri ya maa, di pa la n teesi, ni, yan ka bamsim zugu. Ay! - n sɔbri n‑tiri ya yan pun mi la zugu, ka ziri ni yɛlimaŋni ku toaai n‑kpɛ laa yinni ni.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, since you have been warned in advance, be on guard so that you arent led off course into the error of sinful people, and lose your own safe position.
local: Yarima muna, m mabiisi - ya pun wum yɛla wa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Dizugu ya mari ya mansi ka di kyɛ ka ninvugbeeri wa daamni ya. Ba za'asiri wumpu, yan di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya n‑lusi ya, ka ya yi ya zaazisuŋŋu ni.

eng: Who is the liar? Isnt it the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This person is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
local: Ŋɔni n nyɛ ziri daana? - Pa ŋɔn kyiisiri, ni, Yisa pa la Masiiya maa? Di daama nyɛ la Masiiya Dindaana maa; u nyɛ la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn za'asiri ti Ba Naawunni ni u Bii Yisa zaa yɛla.

eng: Instead, grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ. To him belongs glory now and forever. Amen.
local: Ya kyɛ ka ya kur kyɛnni tooni, yan bɛ ni n‑deaari piinisuma ti Dugma ni Tiligra Yisa Kirisita saani sahanseaa la, m‑paasiri bamsim zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u yɛla gba. Ŋɔnŋɔ nin mari gyirima Zuna, ni saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi .

eng: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father, but the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn za'asiri Naawunni Bii la, u Ba Naawunni gba ka u za'asira. Ŋɔn mun sakki n‑ti Bii maa, u sakkri n‑tiri u Ba maa gba.

eng: As for you, what you heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you will also remain in relationship to the Son and in the Father.
local: Ya kyɛ ka yan daa daŋŋi n‑wum seem la kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Yan daa daŋŋi n‑wum seela la yi kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani, ya gba nin kpalim m‑bɛ Bii maa saani, ni u Ba maa gba saani.

eng: This is the promise that he himself gave us: eternal life.
local: Tɔ, dinŋɔ ka Naawunni daa basi noori n‑ti ti maa - nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: See what kind of love the Father has given to us in that we should be called Gods children, and that is what we are! Because the world didnt recognize him, it doesnt recognize us.
local: Ya baŋŋma Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi wurilim n‑ti ti la, ni, din galisi seem! U kyɛ ka ba boonni ti, ni, Naawunni biisi. Ka u biisi n siri nyɛ ti gba. Din kyɛ ka duniya wa na zi ti la nyɛ la: ka daa bu mi Ŋɔn gba.

eng: I write these things to you about those who are attempting to deceive you.
local: Lala ka n sɔbri n‑tiri ya, ka teesiri la seeba ban boori ba burigi ya la yɛla.

eng: Dear friends, now we are Gods children, and it hasnt yet appeared what we will be. We know that when he appears we will be like him because well see him as he is.
local: N zɔsuma, pampaŋŋɔ ti nyɛ la Naawunni biisi, ka ti na bu baŋŋi tin nin nyɛ seem. Amaa tin mi bunseelli nŋɔa: ni, u yi ti yi paalu na, ti nin ti nya u hoo ŋɔn nyɛ seem ni yɛlimaŋni, ka dizugu ti nin ŋmanni hoo ŋɔn bɛ seem maa.

eng: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains on you, and you dont need anyone to teach you the truth. But since his anointing teaches you about all things , remain in relationship to him just as he taught you.
local: Yarima muna - ya daa pun deaai Naawunni kpaam soosigu. Lala piini maa nin ti kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Di zugu di pa la ya boori ninsaalisɔ ni u ti wulisi ya yam yɛla, dama Naawunni Siiya soosigu wulisiri ya yɛla kam zaa bamsim ŋɔn nyɛ seem; ka yɛtti ya yɛlimaŋni, ka bu ma'ari ziri. Dinzugu ya kpalimma m‑bɛ u saani, hoo ŋɔn daa wulisi ya seem maa.

eng: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves even as he is pure.
local: Nira kam ŋɔn mari lala tama maa maanni la u maŋŋa kasi hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ kasi seem la.

eng: And now, little children, remain in relationship to Jesus, so that when he appears we can have confidence and not be ashamed in front of him when he comes.
local: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ, m biisi, ya kpalimma m‑bɛ u saani; ka u yi ti labi n‑yi paalu sahanseaa na, ti nin nya soori m‑paai u saani na, ka ti ku ti di vi.

eng: Every person who practices sin commits an act of rebellion, and sin is rebellion.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔnkam zaa tumni tumbeeri tuma, u galimni zaaligu maa, dama tumbeeri tumpu ni zaaligu galiŋŋu nyɛ la yummu.

eng: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that every person who practices righteousness is born from him.
local: Ya mi halli, ni, Ŋɔna n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana. Lala zugu ya baŋŋma, ni, sɔkam zaa ŋɔn tumni yɛlimaŋni muna - Naawunni nyɛ la u Ba.

eng: You know that he appeared to take away sins, and there is no sin in him.
local: Amaa ya mi halli, ni, Kirisita daa yi na nti zaŋŋi ti tumbeeri taalli m‑basi. Ka u mammaŋŋa saani ka tumbeeri sɛseelli ka ni.

eng: Every person who remains in relationship to him does not sin. Any person who sins has not seen him or known him.
local: Sɔ ka ni n bɛ u ni ka kpalim n‑tumni taalli tuma; ŋɔn mun kpalim n‑tumni taalli tuma zaa na zi n‑nya u, bee, m‑mi u.

eng: Little children, make sure no one deceives you. The person who practices righteousness is righteous, in the same way that Jesus is righteous.
local: M biisi, yan di kyɛ ka sɔ yɔmsi ya ka ya burigi. Ŋɔn tumni din tu zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

eng: The person who practices sin belongs to the devil, because the devil has been sinning since the beginning. Gods Son appeared for this purpose: to destroy the works of the devil.
local: Ŋɔn mun kpalim n‑tumni taalli tuma nyɛ la Sintaani nira, dama ŋɔn Sintaani kur tumni la taalli zaŋŋi n‑yi piiligu ni sa na. Lala zugu ka Naawunni Bii maa daa yi na; u daa kyɛ na nti sa'am ŋɔn Sintaani tuma maa.

eng: Those born from God dont practice sin because Gods DNA remains in them. They cant sin because they are born from God.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni biiya ku kpalim n‑tumni tumbeeri, dama Naawunni nyɔvuri bɛ u ni; u ku toaai n‑kpalim n‑tumni taalli tuma, dama Naawunni soori ni ka ba dɔ'ai u.

eng: This is how Gods children and the devils children are apparent: everyone who doesnt practice righteousness is not from God, including the person who doesnt love a brother or sister.
local: Dinŋɔ n kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi ban nyɛ Naawunni biisi ni Sintaani biisi: ni, ŋɔn tumni din bu tuusi zaa pa Naawunni biiya, ka ŋɔn bu wuri u taraana gba pa Naawunni biiya.

eng: This is the message that you heard from the beginning: love each other.
local: Dama lahabaari kanŋɔ ka ba daa wulisi ya piiligu ni, ni, ti kyɛ ka ti wuri taaba.

eng: Dont behave like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he kill him? He killed him because his own works were evil, but the works of his brother were righteous.
local: Yan di dɔli Kyeen soori - Sintaani ŋɔn daa su sɔ sufu teesa la, ka u daa ku u sunzɔ maa. Wula zugu ka u daa ku u? - U toori tuma n daa nyɛ tumbeeri, ka u sunzɔ mun dina daa nyɛ bunsuma.

eng: Dont be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
local: Ti gba, m mabiisi, ka duniya dima n yaai ti ni; dinŋɔna yan di kyɛ ka di niŋi ya alahazibu.

eng: We know that we have transferred from death to life, because we love the brothers and sisters. The person who does not love remains in death.
local: Ti wuri ti mabiisi, ka lala kyɛ ka ti mi, ni, ti yisigi la kum ni n‑lɔkki n‑kpɛ nyɔvuripaalli puuni. Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn za'asiri wurilim la mun kur kpalim m‑bɛ kum ni.

eng: Everyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know that murderers dont have eternal life residing in them.
local: Ŋɔnkam yaai u mabiiya zaa nyɛ la ninvugkuura; ka ya mun mi halli, ni, nyɔvuri kan ka tariga ku kpalim m‑bɛ ninvugkuura saani.

eng: This is how we know love: Jesus laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
local: Nŋɔ zugu ka ti baŋŋi wurilim ŋɔn nyɛ seela: ni, tirima zugu ka ŋɔn Yisa daa bu za'asi ka ba ku u. Tirim gba, di sumni ti di za'asi kum ti mabiisi zugu.

eng: But if someone has material possessions and sees a brother or sister in need but refuses to help—how can the love of God dwell in a person like that?.
local: Ninvugsɔ yi mari bunseela duniya wa ni, ka u nya ka u mabisɔ bɛ la fara ni, amaa u ku sakki, ni, u zɔ faradaana wa namboo, ka mɔŋŋi, ni, u ku suŋŋi u - wula ka Naawunni wurilim bɛ di daama saani? Ay! - di ku bɛ ni.

eng: Little children, lets not love with words or speech but with action and truth.
local: M biisi, yan di kyɛ ka ti taaba wurilim bɛ la noori ni yɛttɔ'a kɔkɔa. Ay! - ya kyɛ ka ti wuri taaba n‑tumni tunsuma n‑tiri taaba ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts in Gods presence.
local: Tɔ; lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, ti nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni maa niriba, ka ti teesa nya sufudoaa Naawunni saani.

eng: Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts and knows all things.
local: Dama sahaseaa ti sufu ni teesa ŋɔn yi ti galimni ti, ti baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni yiiko gaari ti teesa zaa, ka u mi bunseelli kam zaa.

eng: Dear friends, if our hearts dont condemn us, we have confidence in relationship to God.
local: Amaa ti teesa yi bu galimni ti, n zɔdima, dinŋɔna ti teesa zaa nin bɛ la Naawunni saani, vi ka ni.

eng: We receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
local: Di yi ti bɛ lala, ti yi ti susiri seelli zaa u saani, ti nin nya; dama ti dɔli la u zaaligu yɛla ka tumni u sufu yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

eng: This is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love each other as he commanded us.
local: U lala zaaligu maa nŋɔa: ni, ti niŋi Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda, Yisa Kirisita, ka laa n‑wuri taaba hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn wulisi ti seem la.

eng: Those who keep his commandments dwell in God and God dwells in them. This is how we know that he dwells in us, because of the Spirit he has given us.
local: Ŋɔnkam dɔli u zaaligu yɛla zaa nyɛ la ŋɔn bɛ ŋɔn Naawunni saani, ka Naawunni mun gba bɛ u saani. Din kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, u kpalim m‑bɛ ti saani nyɛ la: ni, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u Siiya n‑ti ti maa zugu.

eng: Dear friends, dont believe every spirit. Test the spirits to see if they are from God because many false prophets have gone into the world.
local: M mabiisi, yan di deaai sɔkam zaa ŋɔn yɛnni, ni, u mari Naawunni Sii maa, n‑niŋi u yɛligu yɛdda; amaa ya zamsim di daama n‑nya u yɛligu yiiko yi la Naawunni Sii maa saani na, bee? - Dama ziri anabidima pam pun yi duniya wa na.

eng: This is how you know if a spirit comes from God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come as a human is from God.
local: Yan nin niŋi seem m‑baŋŋi yiikoseaa din yi la Naawunni Sii ni na, ni din pa Naawunni dinni, nŋɔa#:- ŋɔnkam zaa sakkri, ni, Yisa Masiiya daa leebi ninsaala n‑kyɛ na, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn mari Sisɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na.

eng: and every spirit that doesnt confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and is now already in the world.
local: Sɔ mun yi bu sakki lala, di daama yɛligu yiiko bu yi la Naawunni Siiya saani na. U sii maa yiiko yi la Masiiya Dindaana la saani na. Ya daa pun wum, ni, lala Dindaana ya kyɛ na; u mun pun bɛ duniya wa puuni.

eng: You are from God, little children, and you have defeated these people because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
local: M biisi, yarima ŋɔna - Naawunni n su ya; ya mun nyaŋŋi ziri anabidima wa, dama ŋɔn bɛ ya saani maa mari yiiko n‑gaari ŋɔn bɛ duniya wa dima saani maa.

eng: They are from the world. So they speak from the worlds point of view and the world listens to them.
local: Ban muna - duniya wa yiiko n su ba yɛla, ka ba tɔ'asiri la duniya wa yɛttɔ'a; di zugu duniya wa dima wumni n‑tiri ba.

eng: We are from God. The person who knows God listens to us. Whoever is not from God doesnt listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
local: Tirima ŋɔna, Naawunni n su ti yɛla: ŋɔnkam mi Naawunni wumni n‑tiri ti. Ŋɔn mun pa Naawunni nira ku wum n‑ti ti. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti baŋŋni yɛlimaŋni yiiko ni ziri dinni.

eng: Dear friends, lets love each other, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born from God and knows God.
local: Tɔ, n zɔdima, ya kyɛ ka ti wuri taaba, dama wurilim yi la Naawunni saani na. Ŋɔnkam zaa yi mari wurilim, u nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, ka mi Naawunni.

eng: The person who doesnt love does not know God, because God is love.
local: Wurilim yi ka sɔ zaa ni, di daama zi Naawunni, dama Naawunni nyɛ la wurilim.

eng: This is how the love of God is revealed to us: God has sent his only Son into the world so that we can live through him.
local: Dinŋɔ ka Naawunni daa tum n‑wulisi ti u wurilim soori: u daa tum u Bii yinni duniya wa na, ni ti ti nya nyɔvuri u zugu.

eng: This is love: it is not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that deals with our sins.
local: Wurilim mun ŋɔn nyɛ seem nla#:- pa la tirima n wuri Naawunni, amaa Ŋɔna n wuri ti ka tum u Bii la na ni u ti leebi maaligu kan ti ti soori n‑kyɛ ka ti nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi ti tumbeeri zugu.

eng: Dear friends, if God loved us this way, we also ought to love each other.
local: N zɔdima, Naawunni ŋɔn siri wuri ti lala, dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ti wuri taaba.

eng: No one has ever seen God. If we love each other, God remains in us and his love is made perfect in us.
local: Sɔsɔ na zi n‑nya Naawunni; amaa ti yi ti wuri taaba, ŋɔn Naawunni nin la'am m‑bɛ ti ni, ka u wurilim maa zaa siri nin bɛ ti saani.

eng: This is how we know we remain in him and he remains in us, because he has given us a measure of his Spirit.
local: Ti mi halli, ni, ti la'amni m‑bɛ la Naawunni ni, ka ŋɔn Naawunni mun la'am m‑bɛ ti ni, dama u pun zaŋŋi u Sii maa n‑ti ti.

eng: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the savior of the world.
local: Ka ti gba nya ka yɛtti n‑wulisiri tin ŋɔn nya seem maa, ni, ti Ba maa tum la u Bii la na ni u ti leebi duniya wa Tiligra.

eng: If any of us confess that Jesus is Gods Son, God remains in us and we remain in God.
local: Ŋɔnkam zaa yɛnni, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya, di daama la'am m‑bɛ ni ni Naawunni, ka Naawunni nin la'am ni u m‑bɛ ni.

eng: We have known and have believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who remain in love remain in God and God remains in them.
local: Ka ti mammansi mi wurilim seaa Naawunni ŋɔn mari n‑tiri ti maa, ka ti niŋni lala wurilim yɛdda. Naawunni nyɛ la wurilim, ka sɔ yi bɛ wurilim beesigu ni, di daama la'am la ni Naawunni m‑bɛ ni, ka Naawunni gba la'am ni u m‑bɛ ni.

eng: This is how love has been perfected in us, so that we can have confidence on the Judgment Day, because we are exactly the same as God is in this world.
local: Wurilim paari ka tariga ti saani, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti mari sufukpeemni ka ku ti di vi, sariya karibu daari maa; dama ti duniya wa ni nyɔvuri ni ti Dugma Yisa dinni nyɛ la yummu.

eng: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear expects punishment. The person who is afraid has not been made perfect in love.
local: Dabeem ka wurilim ni; wurilim seaa din nyɛ bummaŋni zaa, di katti dabeem zaa m‑basira. Tubdarigri zugu ka dabeem bɛ ni, ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn zɔri dabeem maa na ka wurilim ni yɛlimaŋni.

eng: We love because God first loved us.
local: Tirim mari la wurilim maa, Naawunni ŋɔn daŋŋi n‑wuri ti la zugu.

eng: Those who say, I love God and hate their brothers or sisters are liars. After all, those who dont love their brothers or sisters whom they have seen can hardly love God whom they have not seen!.
local: Sɔ yi yɛli, ni, u wuri Naawunni, ka na yaai u mabiiya - ziri daana n nyɛ u. U nini pun nya u mabiiya ka u bu wuri u, dizugu u ya niŋi la wula n‑wuri Naawunni, sɔ u nini ŋɔn na zi n‑nya?.

eng: This commandment we have from him: Those who claim to love God ought to love their brother and sister also.
local: Zaaligu seaa Yisa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ti maa nyɛ la nŋɔa#:- ŋɔnkam zaa wuri Naawunni, di sumni ka u wuri u mabii gba.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: N tɔ'asiri n‑tiri yaseeba ban nyɛ kpamma Yisa nyaandɔliba la'aŋŋu puuni, dama man gba nyɛ la kpeema. N nini daa nya Yisa ŋɔn daa namsi seem, ka u gyirima maa yi ti yi paalu ni sahanseaa, man nin mari n tɔrli.

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: Ya niŋi hoo bunkɔbguriba n‑guri Naawunni peesi n‑ti u: ba bɛ la ya saani, dizugu see ka ya guri ba suŋŋa hoo Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem, pa la sɔ ŋɔn mugsi ya zugu amaa ya sufu yuubu. Ya tumma ya tuma suŋŋa, pa la ligri boobu zugu, amaa ya maŋŋa ŋɔn boori nimmoosi ka ya tumni zugu.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Yan di leebi ban diisi ya mansi nadima n‑su ba fukumsi, amaa ya kyɛ ka ya beebu maa nyɛ bunveelli, ka ba nin nya ka dɔla.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: Ka Bunkɔbgura Zuguraana maa yi ti yi paalu na, u nin zaŋŋi ban nyaŋŋi la laara piini n‑ti ya, ka a ku baasi sahakam.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Ya na'akyimbisi la, ya sakkma n‑tisi ban gaari ya la. Ya zaa kyɛ ka mansigsigu bɛ ya saani, ka tumni n‑tiri taaba, dama Litaafi ni ka di yɛli#:- Naawunni za'asiri karimbaani dima, Ka u tumni la wurilim n‑suŋŋni ban sigsi ba mansi.

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: Lala zugu ya sigsim ya mansi Naawunni Yiikodaana tooni, ka u nin ti duusi ya u toori sahasuŋŋu.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Ya zaŋŋma ya yɛla zaa n‑niŋi u nuu ni, dama u ba'a zaa bɛ la ya yɛla ni.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa sahakam, dama Sintaani ŋɔn nyɛ ya dindaana maa kur goori hoo gbigimni la n‑kaasiri m‑boori sɔ ni u gbaai n‑ŋɔbi.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Ya za'asim u yɛla, ka zɛ yimmu ya yɛdda maa puuni. Ya mi halli, ni, di nyɛ la lala n‑ti ya nyaandɔlitaaba maa, duniya wa ni lugri kam zaa.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Ka ya yi sakki n‑namsi beela, ti Dugma Naawunni nin mari ya suŋŋa n‑ti ya yaa, ka seelli ku daam ya. Ŋɔn Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn tiri piinisuma zaa maa, ka u daa booli ya ni ya laa n‑di u nya'asim din ku naai, Yisa Kirisita zugu.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: Yiiko zaa nyɛ la u dinni sahakam, sahakam. Ammi .

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Mani n sɔbi gbambila wa n‑zaŋŋni n‑tiri Sailivanisi ni u mari na n‑ti ya. U nyɛ la m mabiiya, ka n niŋi u yɛdda. N sɔbi ka, ni n ti suŋŋi ya ka kpamsi ya, ka kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni wurilim yɛla siri bɛ la lala, ka ana n nyɛ an nin kyɛ ka ya zɛ yummu.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban la'amni la tiŋŋa wa puuni, ni, Babɛlɔn la, - ba zaa puusiri ya: m bii Maaki gba puusiri ya.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Ya puusima taaba suŋŋasuŋŋa, ti wurilim puusigu ni. Sufudoaa ni alaafɛ bɛ ya saani, ya zaa ban bɛ Yisa puuni.

eng: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born from God. Whoever loves someone who is a parent loves the child born to the parent.
local: Tɔ: ŋɔnkam zaa niŋi yɛdda, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Masiiya maa, di daama nyɛ la Naawunni biiya. Ka ŋɔnkam wuri ninvugsɔ, see ka u wuri di daama bii maa gba.

eng: This is how we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep Gods commandments.
local: Dinŋɔ n kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, ti wuri Naawunni biisi maa: tin wuri Naawunni ka tumni u zaaligu tuma zugu nla.

eng: This is the love of God: we keep Gods commandments. Gods commandments are not difficult.
local: Ti Naawunni wurilim nŋɔa: ti yi tumni u zaaligu tuma maa. Ka u zaaligu tuma bu tɔ ni.

eng: because everyone who is born from God defeats the world. And this is the victory that has defeated the world: our faith.
local: dama Naawunni bii zaa nyaŋŋni la duniya wa. Ka lala nyaŋŋni din nyaŋŋi duniya wa nyɛ la bɔa? - Ti yɛdda niŋbu nla.

eng: Who defeats the world? Isnt it the one who believes that Jesus is Gods Son?.
local: Ka ŋɔni n nin nyaŋŋi duniya wa? - Pa la ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya? - Ii, ŋɔna!.

eng: This is the one who came by water and blood: Jesus Christ. Not by water only but by water and blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
local: Ŋɔn Yisa Masiiya Kirisita n nyɛ ŋɔn daa dɔli koom soori, ni ziim soori m‑paasi, n‑kyɛ na. Pa koom kɔkɔ n‑daa la, amaa koom ni ziim zaa. Ka Naawunni Sii maa n nyɛ ŋɔn diri di sɛhara, dama ŋɔn Naawunni Sii maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: The three are testifying.
local: Dizugu sɛhara dima ata m‑bɛ ni:.

eng: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and the three are united in agreement.
local: Naawunni Sii Kasi m bɛ ni, ni koom suubu yɛla bɛ ni, ni ŋɔn daa kpi ka u ziim yi na yɛla m‑paasi: ka babata wa sakkri n‑tiri la taaba.

eng: If we receive human testimony, Gods testimony is greater, because this is what God testified: he has testified about his Son.
local: Ti deaari ninsaala sɛhara n‑niŋni di yɛdda; amaa Naawunni sɛhara gaari lala zaa. Dama Naawunni sɛhara nla, ŋɔn di sɛharaseaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Bii maa yɛla.

eng: The one who believes in Gods Son has the testimony within; the one who doesnt believe God has made God a liar, because that one has not believed the testimony that God gave about his Son.
local: Nira kam ŋɔn niŋi Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda mari la lala sɛhara maa u maŋtoori sufu ni. Ŋɔn kam mun ku sakki, ni, u niŋi Naawunni yɛla yɛdda, di daama n kur yɛtti, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la ziri daana, dama u bu niŋi Naawunni ŋɔn di sɛharaseaa yɛdda, sɛhara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Bii maa yɛla maa.

eng: And this is the testimony: God gave eternal life to us, and this life is in his Son.
local: Ka sɛhara maa nŋɔa: Naawunni ti ti la nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka lala nyɔvuri wa bɛ la u Bii maa saani.

eng: The one who has the Son has life. The one who doesnt have Gods Son does not have life.
local: Nira kam ŋɔn mari Naawunni Bii maa mari la nyɔvuri; ka ŋɔn mun ka Naawunni Bii maa ka nyɔvuri.

eng: I write these things to you who believe in the name of Gods Son so that you can know that you have eternal life.
local: Tɔ: n sɔbri anŋɔ n‑tiri yaseeba ban niŋi Naawunni Bii maa yuuri yɛdda la, ni ya ti baŋŋi, ni, ya mari nyɔvuri kan ka naari.

eng: This is the confidence that we have in our relationship with God: If we ask for anything in agreement with his will, he listens to us.
local: Lala sufukpeemni ka ti mari m‑paari Naawunni saani, ka vi ka ti: ni, ti yi ti susi bunseelli kam u saani, ka di dɔli la u yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem, u nin wum n‑ti ti.

eng: If we know that he listens to whatever we ask, we know that we have received what we asked from him.
local: Ka ti yi mi, ni, u wumni ti susigu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa, ti laa m‑mi, ni, ti nin nya di u saani.

eng: If anyone sees a brother or sister committing a sin that does not result in death, they should pray, and God will give life to them—that is, to those who commit sins that dont result in death. There is a sin that results in death—Im not saying that you should pray about that.
local: Sɔ yi ti nya ka u mabisɔ tum tumbɛseelli kan ku taasi u kum ni, ŋɔn susima Naawunni u mabii wa zugu, ka Naawunni nin kyɛ ka tumbeeri daana maa laa n‑nya nyɔvuri. Tumbɛseesi m bɛ ni n‑taasiri niriba kum ni - pa ana ka n yɛtti maa.

eng: Every unrighteous action is sin, but there is a sin that does not result in death.
local: Tunseesi an bu tuusi zaa maa nyɛ la taalli tumbeeri, amaa taalli tumbɛseesi m bɛ ni an bu taasiri kum ni.

eng: We know that everyone born from God does not sin, but the ones born from God guard themselves, and the evil one cannot touch them.
local: Tɔ: ti mi halli, ni, ninvugsɔ zaa ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni biiya - u bu kpalim n‑tumni tumbeeri; dama Yisa, Naawunni ŋɔn dɔ'ai sɔ, nin ti gɔsi u yɛla; ka Sintaani ŋɔn su tumbeeri fukumsi la ku niŋi u seela.

eng: We know we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
local: Ti laa m‑mi, ni, Naawunni n su ti yɛla, ka duniya wa zaa mun bɛ la ŋɔn su tumbeeri fukumsi maa nuusi ni.

eng: We know that Gods Son has come and has given us understanding to know the one who is true. We are in the one who is true by being in his Son, Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
local: Ti mi, ni, Naawunni Bii maa daa kyɛ na ni u ti ti ti bamsim, ni ti baŋŋi Naawummaŋni maa. Ka ti bɛ la Naawummaŋni maa saani - di nyɛ la: ti bɛ la u Bii Yisa Kirisita saani. Ŋɔna n nyɛ Naawummaŋni, ka laa n‑nyɛ nyɔvuri kan ka naari maa.

eng: Little children, guard yourselves from idols!.
local: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna, m biisi, ya mari ya mansi ka di gyeem ba'a bee buga.

eng: From the elder. To the chosen gentlewoman and her children, whom I truly love.
local: Mani ŋɔn sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa - man kpeema maa nla: n sɔbri n‑tiri nyini, m ma: Naawunni pun yiisi i n‑niŋi u dinni, ni i biisi maa m‑paasi i zugu. N wuri ya ni n sufu zaa, yɛlimaŋni maa zugu - halli di pa la man kɔkɔ n wuri ya, amaa seeba zaa ban mi yɛlimaŋni la gba wuri ya ni.

eng: because of the truth that remains with us and will be with us forever.
local: Ti wuri ya lala yɛlimaŋni maa zugu; yɛlimaŋni maa mun na kur kpalim m‑bɛ ti saani saha din ka tariga.

eng: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, will be ours who live in truth and love.
local: M puusiri ya zaa, ni, ti Ba Naawunni, ŋɔn ni Yisa Kirisita u Bii maa zaa, nin suŋŋi ti ka zɔ ti namboo, ka kyɛ ka ti nya sufudoaa. Anŋɔ zaa ka ti nin nya, ka kpalim yɛlimaŋni ni wurilim puuni.

eng: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as we had been commanded by the Father.
local: Di daa niŋi ma nya'asim pam, man ŋɔn daa nya ka i biisi la seeba dɔli la yɛlimaŋni soori maa, kaman ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali ti seem la.

eng: Now, dear friends, I am requesting that we love each other. Its not as though Im writing a new command to you, but its one we have had from the beginning.
local: Tɔ, m ma, wumma! Pampaŋŋɔ n susiri i, ni, ti kyɛ ka ti wuri taaba. Di pa la n sɔbri la zaalipaalli n‑tiri i, amaa di nyɛ la zaaliseaa tin daa deaai piiligu ni la maa.

eng: This is love: that we live according to his commands. This is the command that you heard from the beginning: live in love.
local: Ka wurilim wa nyɛ la: ni, ti dɔli Naawunni zaaligu yɛla zaa. Ya daa pun wum lala n‑yi piiligu ni, halli ni zuna - ni, zaaligu mun nyɛ la: ni, ti dɔli wurilim soori.

eng: Many deceivers have gone into the world who do not confess that Jesus Christ came as a human being. This kind of person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
local: Dama pampaŋŋɔ ka ninvugseeba ban yɔmsiri niriba yiiri la duniya wa na, ba bɔbgu gba. Ba nyɛ la seeba ban ku sakki, ni, Yisa Masiiya daa leebi ninsaala n‑kyɛ duniya wa ni na. Sɔ ŋɔn yɛli lala zaa nyɛ la ŋɔn yɔmsira, halli n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ la Masiiya Dindaana maa.

eng: Watch yourselves so that you dont lose what weve worked for but instead receive a full reward.
local: Dizugu ya mari ya mansi suŋŋa, ka di zaŋŋi yan pun tum tunseesi maa anfaani la m‑basi ka ka yi ya nuusi ni; ay! - see ka ya nya a sanyoaa ka bu pɔsigi seela.

eng: Anyone who goes too far and does not continue in the teaching about Christ does not have God. Whoever continues in this teaching has both the Father and the Son.
local: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bu dɔli Yisa wulisigu yɛla, amaa ka yakkri a maa, di daama ku kpalim m‑bɛ Naawunni saani; amaa ŋɔnkam sakki n‑ti Yisa wulisigu ka kpalim m‑bɛ lala, di daama mari la ti Ba Naawunni u saani, ni Naawunni Bii maa m‑paasi.

eng: Whoever comes to you who does not affirm this teaching should neither be received nor welcomed into your home.
local: Dizugu sɔ yi ti kyɛ ya saani na ka bu mari wulisigu wa yɛla na, yan di sakki ka u kpɛ ya yiya ni, halli yan di puusi u gba.

eng: because welcoming people like that is the same thing as sharing in their evil actions.
local: Dama halli ninvugsɔ ŋɔn gyaari di daama gba, di ŋmanni hoo ŋɔn paasi u zugu ka ba la'am n‑tumni tumbeeri maa seem.

eng: I have a lot to tell you. I dont want to use paper and ink, but I hope to visit you and talk with you face-to-face, so that our joy can be complete.
local: Tɔ. N na mari yɛla pam ni n ya yɛli n‑ti ya, amaa ni lala zaa yɔri, n bu boori n sɔbi a m‑pa gbaŋŋu zugu n‑ti ya na. Dama m mari tama, ni, n nin kyɛ ya saani na n‑kaai ya, ka ti nini nya taaba ka ti di taaba azama, dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti nya sufupeelli pam.

eng: Your chosen sisters children greet you.
local: Tɔ: i sunzɔ bɛ la n saani; ŋɔn gba ka Naawunni yiisi n‑niŋi u dinni ka ŋɔna ni u biisi gba puusiri i ni suŋŋasuŋŋa.

eng: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I truly love.
local: Mani ŋɔn sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa - man kpeema maa nla: n sɔbri n‑tiri nyini, Gayusi. N wuri i ni n sufu zaa, yɛlimaŋni maa zugu.

eng: Dear friend, Im praying that all is well with you and that you enjoy good health in the same way that you prosper spiritually.
local: N zɔsuŋŋu, m puusiri i ni, ka susiri Naawunni, ni, bunseelli kam zaa niŋi suŋŋa n‑ti i, ka i mari alaafɛa, hoo man mi suŋŋa, ni, i sii maa mari alaafɛ zaa seem la.

eng: I was overjoyed when the brothers and sisters arrived and spoke highly of your faithfulness to the truth, shown by how you live according to the truth.
local: Ti Kirisita mabitaaba ŋɔn daa paai na ka yɛli ma i yɛdda ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni, i na dɔli la yɛlimaŋni sɔsuŋŋu ni nimbirimoaa, ka di daa niŋi ma nya'asim pam.

eng: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are living according to the truth.
local: Dama seelli ka ni m‑maari n sufu n‑gaari n yi wum, ni, m biisi maa bɛ la yɛlimaŋni beesigu.

eng: Dear friend, you act faithfully in whatever you do for our brothers and sisters, even though they are strangers.
local: N zɔsuŋŋu, i tumni yɛdda tuma n‑suŋŋni ti Kirisita mabitaaba la, halli ni ba daa pa ya timbiisi gba.

eng: They spoke highly of your love in front of the church. You all would do well to provide for their journey in a way that honors God.
local: Ba daa pun tɔ'asi i wurilim yɛla n‑wulisi n‑ti ti kpa wa mabiisi maa. Kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑suŋŋi ba ba kyɛnni maa ni, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem.

eng: because they left on their journey for the sake of Jesus Christ without accepting any support from the Gentiles.
local: Dama Yisa yuuri zugu ka ba yi soori, ka dizugu ba ku sakki ni ba deaai sɛseelli ninvugseeba ban bu dɔli Yisa maa saani.

eng: Therefore, we ought to help people like this so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
local: Di sumni ni ti deaai niriba wa taabu ti yiya ni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti zaa la'amni taaba n‑tumni yɛlimaŋni maa tuma.

eng: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, doesnt welcome us.
local: Tɔ: n daa pun sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ya bɛni sa niriba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la, amaa Diyɔtirifiisi ŋɔn boori u su ba fukumsi maa ku sakki n‑deaai n noori.

eng: Because of this, if I come, I will bring up what he has done—making unjustified and wicked accusations against us. And as if that were not enough, he not only refuses to welcome the brothers and sisters but stops those who want to do so and even throws them out of the church!.
local: N yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, n nin kyɛ ka u baŋŋi ma zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn niŋi seem maa zaa - halli yɛlibɛseesi ni ziriseesi ŋɔn ma'ari n‑tiri ma la. Ni lala maa zaa di na bu sakki u: u ku sakki ni u deaai ti Kirisita mabitaaba ban yi kyɛnni ya saani na. Halli seeba yi boori ni ba deaai ba, u bu sakkra, ka katti lala niriba n‑yiisiri gyeemniba la'aŋŋu maa ni.

eng: Dear friend, dont imitate what is bad but what is good. Whoever practices what is good belongs to God. Whoever practices what is bad has not seen God.
local: N zɔsuŋŋu, di dɔli ban tumni din bu dɔli soori nyaaŋŋa. Ay! - see ka i dɔli ban tumni din tuusi soori maa. Dama ŋɔnkam tumni din tuusi yi la Naawunni saani na, ŋɔn mun tumni din bu tuusi maa na zi n‑nya Naawunni.

eng: Everyone speaks highly of Demetrius, even the truth itself. We also speak highly of him, and you know that what we say is true.
local: Sɔkam zaa tɔ'asiri Dimitiriyusi yɛla, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu; yɛlimaŋni maa gba tɔ'asiri u yɛla la suŋŋa. Tirima gba zaŋŋi tin mi seem m‑paasi n‑wulisi u veelim ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka i mi, ni, tin yɛtti seem maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

eng: I have a lot to say to you, but I dont want to use pen and ink.
local: Tɔ. N na mari yɛla pam ni n ya n‑yɛli n‑ti i, amaa di zaa yɔri, n bu boori n sɔbi a n‑niŋi gbaŋŋu ni n‑ti i na.

eng: I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face-to-face.
local: Dama m mari tama, ni, di bu ya n‑yuui ka n nin kyɛ i saani na n‑kaai i, ka ti nini nya taaba ka ti di taaba azama. Alaafɛ ni sufudoaa bɛ i saani. I zɔdima m puusiri i ni. Puusima ti zɔdima zaa yinniyinni.

eng: Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James. To those who are called, loved by God the Father and kept safe by Jesus Christ.
local: Man Gyuuda, Gyeemsi sunzɔ la, n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya; man ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn tumni n‑tiri Yisa Kirisita Masiiya hoo u daabri la. Yarima ka n sɔbri gbaŋŋu maa n‑tiri ya, dama n wuri ya ti Ba Naawunni yɛla zugu, ka ba pun booli ya Naawunni saani na ka Yisa Kirisita mari ya suŋŋa.

eng: May you have more and more mercy, peace, and love.
local: Ya nin nya namboo Naawunni saani, ni alaafɛ ni wurilim zaa.

eng: Dear friends, I wanted very much to write to you concerning the salvation we share. Instead, I must write to urge you to fight for the faith delivered once and for all to Gods holy people.
local: M mabiisi, n wuri ya pam ka di zugu n da yi boori la n sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man ni yan zaa ŋɔn la'am n‑nya tiliginsim seem maa; amaa di kpa la talasi ni n daŋŋi n‑sɔbi yɛttɔ'a n‑susiri ya, ni, ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑zaŋŋi ya kpeeŋŋu zaa m‑moora, ni ya ti zɛ yɛdda yɛla maa nyaaŋŋa - yɛdda yɛliseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun zaali n‑ti u niriba la.

eng: Godless people have slipped in among you. They turn the grace of our God into unrestrained immorality and deny our only master and Lord, Jesus Christ. Judgment was passed against them a long time ago.
local: Dama ninvugseeba pun sɔ'ai n‑kpɛ ya saani na, ka za'asiri Naawunni gyeeŋŋu yɛla. Halli ba daa pun sɔbi lala niriba yɛla, ni, ba nin kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba. Ninvugbeeri wa n kur zeemni ti Dugma Naawunni piinisuma, halli nti takki Naawunni yɛla ka a leebi la da'ari tuma an bu dɔli soori; ka ba za'asiri ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yɛla, halli ni ŋɔn kɔkɔ ŋɔn su ti yɛla zaa gba.

eng: I want to remind you of something you already know very well. The Lord, who once saved a people out of Egypt, later destroyed those who didnt maintain their faith.
local: M boori ni n teaai ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun tum seem, halli ni yan pun wum di lahabaari gba: ni, Naawunni daa daŋŋi n‑tiligi u niriba n‑yiisi ba Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni na, amaa di zaa yɔri, u daa naayi kyɛ ka baseeba ban daa za'asi yɛdda niŋbu la daa ti baasi yɔri.

eng: I remind you too of the angels who didnt keep their position of authority but deserted their own home. The Lord has kept them in eternal chains in the underworld until the judgment of the great day.
local: Halli malaakadima maa gba - baseeba ban daa bu sakki, ni, ba ti kpalim ba maŋtoori naam fukumsi yɛla ni, ka daa kyɛ ba beebu ziiya m‑bakki la - ban ka Naawunni daa lɔ la kyɔrimseesi an ku toaai n‑lɔrigi sahakam, n‑kpari zibsim zii maa ni, halli n‑guri sariya karibu daari maa.

eng: In the same way, Sodom and Gomorrah and neighboring towns practiced immoral sexual relations and pursued other sexual urges. By undergoing the punishment of eternal fire, they serve as a warning.
local: Lala gba ka Sɔdoom dima, ni Gɔmɔra dima, ni tinseesi an daa gyiligi bɛni niriba m‑paasi daa bɛ ni. Ba daa tumni la zina tuma ka dɔli la kɔrisi yɛliseesi an bu dɔli soori; ka lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba nya tubdarigri saha din ka naari, bugum maa ni; halli ka ba yɛla ti leebi la wulisigu n‑kpamsiri sɔkam zaa.

eng: Yet, even knowing this, these dreamers in the same way pollute themselves, reject authority, and slander the angels.
local: Tɔ, ka lala deede ka niriba wa zaasiri zaasibeeri, ka di zugu ba sa'amni la ba maŋtoori ningbina, ka za'asiri la fukumsi ka tuuri la gyirima daandima.

eng: The archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil about Moses body, did not dare charge him with slander. Instead, he said, The Lord rebuke you.
local: Ya bu nya, halli malaakadima zuguraana maa, Maikyɛli la, daa bu tu Sintaani gba, Musa ŋɔn daa kpi ka ba daa mari nangbankpeemni n‑ti taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u ningbiŋŋu yɛla. Ay! - u daa kur yɛli u, ni, Ti Dugma nin boosi i.

eng: But these people slander whatever they dont understand. They are destroyed by what they know instinctively, as though they were irrational animals.
local: Amaa niriba wa muna - ba yi bu baŋŋi bunseelli gbinni, kana ka ba tuura. Halli ba yi ti baŋŋi bunseesi, ba mari la yam hoo bunkɔbri ŋɔn mari seem m‑baŋŋi a, ka bamsim din bu tuusi wa mun kyɛ ka ba basi la yɔri.

eng: They are damned, for they follow in the footsteps of Cain. For profit they give themselves over to Balaams error. They are destroyed in the uprising of Korah.
local: Waai, waai, waai! Ba yɛla basi la yɔri! Dama ba dɔli la Kyeen sɔbeoo la. Ba mun sufu kpɛ Beelɛm yɔmsigu yɛla ni, ka ba nin nya tubdarigri hoo ŋɔn Beelɛm ŋɔn daa nya seem la. Halli ba za'asiri yɛlimaŋni soori hoo Koora ŋɔn daa za'asi seem, ka ba nin nya baasiyɔri hoo ŋɔna.

eng: These people are like jagged rocks just below the surface of the water waiting to snag you when they join your love feasts. They feast with you without reverence. They care only for themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; fruitless autumn trees, twice dead, uprooted.
local: Ya yi ti la'amni n‑diri bundirigu wurilim zugu, lala niriba kpeeri na n‑sa'amni ya yɛla, ka vi bu kpɛ ba di zugu. Ba ŋmanni la pɛguriseeba ban bu guri peesi maa, amaa ba maŋtoori yɛla ka ba gɔsira; bee, sangbana an ka saa ka pɛsiŋŋu kur zeeri a ka a kyɛnni kpa, n‑labri kpa; bee, tiseesi an bu wanni wala halli ni waligu saha ŋɔn paai maa zaa yɔri - a pun kpi napɔmbuyi, ka ba nin foaai a m‑basi.

eng: wild waves of the sea foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the darkness of the underworld is reserved forever.
local: Ba laa n‑ŋmanni la teeku kooma; a damni la kɔmiisi n‑tumni la toom n‑yiisiri la fufukka - ba vi tuma nla; bee, saŋmariwuusi n nyɛ ba, seesi an goori n‑goora, halli ka zibsim din ka naari ti deaai ba m‑mara.

eng: Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, prophesied about these people when he said, See, the Lord comes with his countless holy ones.
local: Inɔkyi daa nyɛ la Adam ya'aŋŋa, sɔ ŋɔn daa yi badima ayɔpɔi Adam nyaaŋŋa ni na: ŋɔna n daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba wa yɛla, ni, Ya nyama! Naawunni kyɛ na, ŋɔn ni u nyaaŋŋa - u toori niriba tusatusa la;.

eng: to execute judgment on everyone and to convict everyone about every ungodly deed they have committed in their ungodliness as well as all the harsh things that sinful ungodly people have said against him.
local: u kyɛ na ni u ti kari sɔkam sariya. U nin darigi ba zaa tubri ba tumbeeri zugu, ni ban daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑za'asi ŋɔn Naawunni maa zugu - ala'akyi dima n nyɛ ba ka ba za'asiri Naawunni noori.

eng: These are faultfinding grumblers, living according to their own desires. They speak arrogant words and they show partiality to people when they want a favor in return.
local: Niriba wa nyɛ la yɛttɔ'adɔ'asa daandima, ba kur fabinni la yɔriyɔri; ba dɔli la ba kɔribeeri sɔya, n‑tɔ'asiri n‑nyuuri bara; ba bu tiri niriba gyirima, amaa ba ya n‑yɔmsi ba n‑kyɛ ka ba nini tigi ba yɛla.

eng: But you, dear friends, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Yarima muna, m mabiseeba man wuri suŋŋa wa, ya teaama yɛttɔ'aseesi yan daa pun wum la, aseesi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita tuumudima ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya la.

eng: They said to you, In the end time scoffers will come living according to their own ungodly desires.
local: Ni, ba daa yɛli, Saha nin ti kyɛ na din nyɛ naari saha ka ninvugseeba nin ti bɛ ni n‑niŋni ansarisi, n‑dɔli la ba kɔribeeri, ka za'asiri Naawunni yɛla.

eng: These people create divisions. Since they dont have the Spirit, they are worldly.
local: Lala niriba n nyɛ baseeba ban purigi ya ka kyɛ ka ya wɔligi taaba. Ba dɔli la ba duniya wa yam yɛla, ka kɔŋŋi Naawunni Sii maa zaa.

eng: But you, dear friends: build each other up on the foundation of your most holy faith, pray in the Holy Spirit.
local: Yarima muna, m mabiisi, ya kpalimma n‑zɛ yummu ya yɛdda maa ni, dama di zaa nyɛ la Naawunni dinni. Ya puusima Sii Kasi maa yiiko ni.

eng: keep each other in the love of God, wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, who will give you eternal life.
local: ka kur kpalimma Naawunni wurilim la puuni. Ya dim sugru n‑guusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita nambɔzoaa saha la, dama lala saha maa ka ya nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

eng: Have mercy on those who doubt.
local: Ya zɔm namboo n‑ti ninvugseeba ban birisira.

eng: Save some by snatching them from the fire. Fearing God, have mercy on some, hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful urges.
local: Ninvugseeba muna - ya faama ba m‑basi bugum ni na. Seeba yaasa ya zɔm ba namboo, amaa ka zɔ ba dabeem gba, halli nti kyisi ba bunyeera da'ari siisigu gba, dama ba ningbinkɔrisi yɛla sa'amni a ni.

eng: To the one who is able to protect you from falling, and to present you blameless and rejoicing before his glorious presence.
local: Naawunni kɔkɔ n nyɛ ti tiligra, ka ŋɔn nin toaai n‑guri ya n‑kyɛ ka ya kpalim m‑bɛ kasi. U nin zaali ya u gyirima zii tooni, ka da'ari ka ni, ka kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi zaa di zugu. Ya kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni nya gyirima ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya maa zugu, ka nya duusigu, ni kpeeŋŋu, ni yaa. Ka di daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ la lala, ka na bɛ la lala zuna, ka di nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala saha din ka naari. Ammi .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn gyi la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi u nudirigu ni la yɛligu nŋɔa; ŋɔn kyɛnni salima firilatɔmna sunsukkuni maa. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
