local: M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni, ya tumni tunkara ka diri sugru; ka ya bu boori tumbeeri tumniba, halli ninvugseeba ban ma'ari ziri n‑yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la Yisa tuumudima: ban ka ya viisiri suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi, ni, ba pa la lala.

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: Ka ya diri sugru, ka zaŋŋni sufukpeemni n‑tumni n‑tiri ma, ka bu gyeera.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseaa n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ya daa daŋŋi n‑wuri ma suŋŋa, amaa ya bu laa n‑wuri ma lala.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: Dizugu ya teaama yan daa du saazugu seem ka baŋŋi yan lu n‑sigi seem; ka takki ya teesa ni ya beesigu, ka laa m‑maali n‑tumni hoo yan daa daŋŋi n‑tumni seem maa. Ya yi bu niŋi lala, n nin kyɛ na n‑yiisi ya firilatɔmnigu la ka zaazii maa ni m‑basi.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: Halli bunsuŋŋu na kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Dama ya ku sakki Nikɔlaati dima yɛla, hoo man gba ŋɔn ku sakki ba yɛla seem.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin kyɛ ka u di nyɔvuri tii maa wala, tiseaa kan zɛ Naawunni zisuŋŋu la puuni maa. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Simeena tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn n nyɛ piiligu ka laa n‑nyɛ naari yɛligu nŋɔa: u daa kpiya, ka laa n‑yisigi n‑nɛa. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: M mi ya wahala ni ya fara beesigu ŋɔn bɛ seem: amaa - ya bu nya? - ya nyɛ la bundaandima. M mi niriba wa ŋɔn tuuri ya seem maa - ninvugseeba ban yɛtti, ni, Izraɛli dima n nyɛ ba, halli ni ba pa la lala, amaa Sintaani la'aŋŋu duu niriba n nyɛ ba.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Yan di zɔ dabeem yan yɛ n‑nya namsigu seem zugu. Ya bu nya? - Sintaani nin kyɛ ka ba gbaasi yaseeba n‑kpari sarika duu ni n‑namsi ya, ka ya nin nya wahala dabsipiiya. Ya kur zɛ yummu m‑mari ya yɛdda niŋbu kirikiri, halli nti paai kum, ka mani nin zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑ti ya - di nyɛ la nyɔvuri.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, kum din paasiri buyi maa ku toaai n‑niŋi u seelli. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Peegamɔn tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn mari takoobi maa yɛligu nŋɔa. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: M mi yan bɛ seela maa, ni, Sintaani naam zizii bɛ la bɛni. Ya bu nya? - ka halli ni dinŋɔ zaa, ya na mari n yuuri yɛla suŋŋa, ka bu za'asi yan niŋi ma yɛdda seem, halli Antipasi saha gba - Antipasi sɔ ŋɔn daa mooli n yɛla ka ba daa ku u ya tiŋŋa maa puuni, kan nyɛ Sintaani ŋɔn bɛ seela.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseesi ni n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ninvugseeba bɛ ya saani ban dɔli la Beelɛm wulisigu yɛla. Dama ŋɔn Beelɛm daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa wulisi NaaBalakku ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima tum tumbeeri. Ka lala daa kyɛ ka ba daa burigi soori, n‑tɔtti maaligu numni n‑dira, ka a daa yi la ziri wuna la sara ni na; ka u daa laa n‑kyɛ ka ba tumni zina m‑paasi.

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: M‑paasi yaasa, ya mari ninvugseeba ban dɔli Nikɔlaati dima wulisigu la.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: Dizugu ya takkma ya teesa ni ya beesigu la! Di yi pa lala, n nin kyɛ ya saani yooma na, n‑zaŋŋi n noori ni takoobi maa n‑tuusi ya tɔppu.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin zaŋŋi manna disɔ'ara n‑ti u, ka laa n‑ti u kugpeelli, ka ba sɔbi yupaalli n‑niŋi ka, ka sɔ ku mi lala yuuri maa na'ala ŋɔn deaai kugri maa. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: Ni, Ka dinŋɔ ka i sɔbi n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Tayatayira tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Naawunni Bii yɛligu nŋɔa; sɔ ŋɔn mari u nini hoo bugunsaala maa, ka u nɔba nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni ya wurilim, ni ya yɛdda maa; m mi yan tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba seem, ka ya sufu ŋɔn laa n‑kpeem seem maa. Halli ya naayi tumni tunkara ka a gaari yan daa daŋŋi n‑tum seesi la.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseaa n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ya kyɛ ka pɔ'a wa, Gyɛzibɛla, wulisiri n‑yɔmsiri n niriba la. U ma'ai la ziri n‑yɛtti, ni, pɔ'a-anabi n nyɛ u, ka yɔmsiri ba ni ba ti tumni zina, ka diri ziri wuna sara.

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: N pun ti u saha ni u takki u teesibeeri ni u beesigu la, amaa u bu sakki, ni, u kyɛ u zina tuma la.

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices.
local: Dizugu n nin zaŋŋi u m‑basi namsigu gadu zugu, ŋɔn ni ban tumni zina u saani la m‑paasi, ka ba yi bu takki ba teesibeeri ni tumbɛseesi pɔ'a wa ŋɔn wulisi ba la zaa, ba nin di wahala pam.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: N nin kyɛ ka u biisi maa ti kpi zaa. Lala saha maa ka ninvugseeba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni ma tinsi tinsi puuni maa nin ti baŋŋi, ni, man nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn viisiri niriba sufu ni teesa, ka nin ti tisi ya sɔkam hoo u maŋtoori tuma ŋɔn sumsi ni u deaai seem maa.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: Amaa kyɛ ka n tɔ'asi n‑ti ya Tayatayira dimseeba ban kpalim la, yan bu deaai wulisigu wa zaa. Ya bu bamsi ban boonni yɛlisɔ'ara, bunzulima la - Sintaani yɛlisɔ'ara n kur nyɛ a! Tɔ, yarima muna - n ku ziili ya ziseaa m‑paasi.

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: di yi pa la ya kpalim n‑gyi yan na mari bunseesi maa, halli ka n ti ya kyɛ ya saani na.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, sɔkam ŋɔn mari n tuma tumpu suŋŋa halli nti paai yɛla zaa naari maa#:- N nin zaŋŋi fukumsi n‑ti u Ka u nin ti su niriba kam zaa.

eng: to rule the nations with an iron rod and smash them like pottery.
local: U nin guri ba hoo bunkɔbgura, U nin zaŋŋi kutti damni n‑guri ba. U yi ti zaŋŋi ka n‑ŋmeera, Ba nin ŋma hoo dukku ŋɔn ŋmaari seem la.

eng: just as I received authority from my Father. I will also give them the morning star.
local: Tɔ: lala fukumsi ka m Ba pun zaŋŋi n‑ti ma. Ka n nin zaŋŋi bɛŋmariga n‑ti di daama.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Saadisi tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn su Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi yɛttɔ'a, ka u su la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi gba. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:- M mi ya tuma an bɛ seem, ni, ba yɛtti ya yɛla, ni, ya neaai ni maa, amaa ya kpi ni.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Ya neaama! ka kpamsi din kpalim ya saani, halli an ya n‑kpi gba, dama m bu nya ya tuma maa ka a nya tummuna Naawunni saani.

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Di zugu ya teaama yan daa deaai seelli, ni yan daa wum seelli maa; ya wumsima anŋɔ zaa ka kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am ya tumbeeri taalli zugu, ka takki ya beebu di zugu. Dama ya yi ku neaai, n nin kyɛ na hoo na'ayiga ŋɔn kyɛ na seem, ka ya ku baŋŋi sahaseaa man nin kyɛ ya saani na.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: Amaa ya Saadisi dima maa seeba bu sa'am ya situra ni da'ari tuma. Ban nin ti yɛ situripeela ka man ni ban nin la'am n‑kyɛnna, dama ba sum la lala.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, ŋɔn gba nin ti yɛ situripeela ka man ku kyɛ ka ba nyeesi u yuuri n‑yiisi nyɔvuri bukku maa ni m‑basi. N nin di u sɛhara n‑zɛ u nyaaŋŋa m Ba Naawunni saani ni u malaakadima saani.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Filadɛlifiya tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn n nyɛ Naawunni dinni, yɛlimaŋni daana maa yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa. Ŋɔna m mari la NaaDauda saafibii maa: ka u yi ti yoaai seelli, sɔ n ku toaai n‑yɔa; ka u mun yi ti yɔ seelli, sɔ n ku toaai n‑yoaai. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem: ya bu nya? - za'anɔri ka n yoaai m‑basi ya la, ka sɔ ku toaai n‑yɔ ka. Dama m mi halli, ni, ya yaa bu zoaai, amaa ya deaari n noori maa ka bu yi n nyaaŋŋa.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Sintaani la'aŋŋu duu dima maa muna - baseeba ban yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la Izraɛli dima maa, amaa ba ma'ari ni, ba pa lala - n nin kyɛ ka ban nin kyɛ ya saani na n‑gbaani ya tooni ka baŋŋi, ni, n wuri ya.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Ya daa deaai n noori ka di sugru n‑dɔla, di zugu n nin deaai ya n‑guri ya suŋŋa, ka wahalaseaa kan ti ya kyɛ na la ku niŋi ya seela - ka nin ti kyɛ na sahaseaa n‑zamsi duniya wa ni niriba zaa.

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na. Ya gbaama yan mari seelli la kpeeŋŋa, ka di kyɛ ka sɔ ti fa ya naam zupiliga ya saani.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u leebi hoo zaantɔliga ka ba sa m Ba Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni, ka u ku laa n‑yi bɛni na yaasa. N nin sɔbi m Ba Naawunni yuuri m‑pa n‑niŋi u, ni ŋɔn Naawunni timpaalli la yuuri, di nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm bumpaalli la. Tiŋŋa wa ya n‑yi saazugu, m Ba saani, n‑sigi na. Ka n nin sɔbi m mammantoori yupaalli m‑pa di daama ni m‑paasi.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la. .

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: Ni, Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Leoodisiiya tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- Ŋɔn yuuri boonni, ni, Ammi yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa: ŋɔna n nyɛ yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni daana, sɔ ŋɔn diri sɛhara suŋŋa. Ŋɔna n daa piili Naawunni ŋɔn daa naam bunseelli kam, ka su di zaa fukumsi. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem: ni, ya bu maasa, ya mun bu tuula - man da boori ka ya nyɛ la a yinni.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: Dinŋɔna yan bu maasa ka mun bu tuula zugu, ka kur kpalim n‑nyɛ bunŋmamm, n ya n‑ti ya ni n‑yiisi n noori ni m‑basi.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Halli ya yɛtti, ni, Bundaandima n nyɛ ti - ti mari la marigu pam ka bu kɔŋŋi seelli! - amaa ya bu baŋŋi, ni, ya siri nyɛ la faradima, ka wahala mari ya, ka ya kɔŋŋi bunseelli kam; ya laa n‑zoom zaa ka kyɛnni beelli, situra ka ni.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Mani n kpaamni ya, ni, ya kyɛm n saani na n‑da salima - ban zaŋŋi bugum n‑kuri seesi ka a ti leebi kasikasi maa - ka ya siri nin leebi bundaandima: ya daama situripeela n‑yɛa, ka lala nin ligi ya beelli kyɛnni vi maa: halli ka da nini mirigri gba, ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nini neaara.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Ya bu nya? - Man ŋɔn wuri seeba zaa, ban ka n sa'asiri ka kpamsiri, ni ba ti bamsi yam. Dinzugu ya kyɛ ka ya nini moaai ka takki ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri soori.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Nnya ma! N zɛ la za'anɔri maa ni n‑kpaasiri m‑boonna; ka ŋɔnkam yi wum n kukɔri maa ka yoaai n‑ti ma, n nin kpɛ na n‑di diibu u saani, halli man ni ŋɔna nin ti la'am n‑di.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin ti u soori ni u zinni man saani n naam gbana zugu, hoo man gba daa pun nyaŋŋi ka zinni m Ba naam gbana zugu seem la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la. .

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Tɔ: anŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa nya, ka arizana ziiya ka ba yoaai hoo kyɛburu: ka n daa wum man ŋɔn daa pun wum kukɔseaa la, ka ka bɛ hoo kikaa kunkuma maa. Kukɔri maa yɛtti nŋɔa#:- Duuma kpa na, ka n nin wulisi i din ti ya niŋi dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa paali ma, ka n nya naam ziziiya arizana puuni ka sɔ n ziya.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Ŋɔn daa ziya maa gɔsibu daa veela hoo mansɔ'asi kugveela ban boonni gyasipa ni Sadiisi kugri la ŋɔn veela seem. Ka sasuu veelim daa gyiligi naam zizii maa hoo mansɔ'asi veelim ŋɔn bɛ seem.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Ka ziziisi pisi ni anaasi gba daa bɛ ni n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa, ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi zi a ni: ba daa yɛ bunyeeripeela n‑ziya, ka pili salima naam zupilisi ba zugri ni.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Ka saa daa nya'asiri ka tasiri m‑mari vuuri pam naam zizii maa ni na, ka bugum mɔkpala ayɔpɔi neaari zizii maa tooni - Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi n kur nyɛ ana.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Ka bunseelli daa dooni naam zizii maa tooni hoo mɔ'ari, ka ka gɔsibu ŋmanni la hoo mansɔ'asi. Ka bunvuya anaasi n daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa sunsukkuni, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‑gɔsira.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Gbaŋŋu wa lahabaari wulisiri la Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ti kasigi n‑wulisi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba la. Di nyɛ la lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi duniya yɛliseesi an ya n‑niŋi pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna. Yɛla wa zugu ka Masiiya maa daa tum u malaakasɔ man Gyɔni saani, man ŋɔn nyɛ u daabri maa.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Ka bunvusɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni maa daa ŋmanni la gbigimni, ka ŋɔn pa nyaaŋŋa ŋmanni la na'araa, ka ŋɔn paasi buta mari nindaa hoo ninsaala dinni, ka ŋɔn paasi bunaasi mun ŋmanni la wubga n‑yikkra.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Dizugu man Gyɔni nini ŋɔn daa nya yɛliseesi maa zaa m‑bɛ gbaŋŋu wa puuni. A zaa nyɛ la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni yɛla ka Yisa Masiiya daa kasigi a n‑dooli paalu ni.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Ka babanaasi maa zaa daa mari kpunkpama ayooyoobu, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‑gɔsira. Ba daa kur yuumni yuuma sahakam, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu, ni#:- Kasi! kasi! kasi! Ti Dugma Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa Nyɛ la ŋɔn bɛ kasi maa! Ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni, Ka u kpalim m‑bɛ ni pampaŋŋɔa, Halli ŋɔn ya kyɛ na.

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Dizugu zugsuŋŋu pam bɛ nira zaa ŋɔn karim gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti u taaba. Ninvugseeba ban nin wum gbaŋŋu wa yɛligu ka sakki n‑ti ka yɛla gba mari zugsuŋŋu pam. Dama saha bu ya yuui ka gbaŋŋu wa puuni yɛla wa zaa nin yi paalu ka niriba nini nya.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Ka bunvuya maa daa yi yuumni lala, n‑tibgri Ŋɔn zi naam zizii maa ni, ka tiri u gyirima, ni, u nin bɛ saha din ka naari. Ka lala saha maa.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: Pampaŋŋɔ man Gyɔni m puusiri yaseeba ban dɔli Yisa ka la'amni taaba gyeeŋŋu ziisi ayɔpɔi seesi an bɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni la. Yɛttɔ'a wa ka n ya n‑yɛli ya: Naawunni daa bɛ ni, ka na bɛ ni, halli u nin bɛ ni sahakam: u nin zɔ ya zaa namboo ka ti ya sufudoaa, yaseeba ban dɔli Yisa. Ka u siiya buyɔpɔi bɛ u naam zizii tooni maa, ka ŋɔn gba nin niŋi lala n‑ti ya.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi maa gbaantiri la tiŋŋa n‑tiri Ŋɔn zi naam zii maa, ka bɛ ni saha din ka naari; ka ba zaŋŋi ba naam zupilisi m‑piligri m‑basiri tiŋŋa, naam zizii maa tooni. Ka ba yɛtti, ni#:-.

eng: and from Jesus Christ—the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and freed us from our sins by his blood.
local: Yisa Kirisita n daa tuui n‑neaai kum ni, ka nyɛ yɛdda daana ni sɛharaditta maa; ŋɔn gba nin zɔ ya namboo ka ti ya sufudoaa. Dama ŋɔna n nyɛ duniya zaa nadima Naaba. Ŋɔn Yisa wuri ti pam, ka lala zugu ka u daa kpi n‑yiisi ti ti tumbeeri taalli ni.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: Nyini n nyɛ ti Dugma, Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni, I sumni tibgri ni gyirima, I sumni yiiko marigu! Dama nyini n daa naam bunseelli kam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa naam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa ti bɛ ni.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Ka u laa n‑kyɛ ka ti leebi nabiisi ka gyeemni u Ba Naawunni hoo gyeeŋŋu toondaandima. Gyirima ni yiiko pam bɛ ŋɔn Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani, halli saha din ka tariga! Ammi .

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Ya gɔsima n‑nya! U bɛ sangbana ni n‑kyɛnni na; Di saha ya zaa nini nin nya u - Halli ninvugseeba ban daa kusi u maa gba: Dizugu ninsaala bura zaa niriba Nin kum ka fabli u zugu. Lala n nyɛ di. Ammi .

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Ti Dugma Naawunni yɛliya: Mani n nyɛ piiligu, ka n mun nyɛ naari. Mani n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn na bɛ ni, ka daa pun bɛ ni, halli n nin ti kyɛ na: Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa.

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: Tɔ: mani n nyɛ ya mabii Gyɔni n‑sɔbri wa; ka man ni yarima zaa la'amni n‑diri wahala ni sugru, Yisa yuuri zugu, ni ti Dugma Naawunni naam la zugu. Tɔ, ka n daa bɛ la Patimɔsi tingbaŋŋu ni, tingbanseaa kan bɛ teeku kooma sunsukkuni. N daa bɛ bɛni Naawunni yɛligu zugu, man daa moonni u yɛla n‑wulisiri niriba maa zugu.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: Di daari maa daa nyɛ la ti Dugma dabsiri maa, Alahari; ka n Dugma Sii maa daa paali ma ni, halli ka n ti wum kukɔri n nyaaŋŋa, ka ka kumni pampam hoo kikaa ŋɔn kumni seem.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: Ni, Sɔbma i nini ŋɔn nya bunseesi ka i tuba gba wum yɛttɔ'aseesi maa zaa. Sɔbma a n‑tɔlim n‑ti Yisa niriba maa seeba ban la'amni taaba n‑gyeemni Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziisi ayɔpɔi, tinsi ayɔpɔi puuni: ka lala tinsi yuya nŋɔa#:- Ɛfɛsusi, Simeena, Peegamɔn, Tayatayira, Saadisi, Filadɛlifiya ni Leoodisiiya.

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: Di saha maa n daa gyeaai, ni n ti nya ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri maa; ka daa nya ka salima buna ayɔpɔi n zɛya, ka firiladima tɔm n‑tɔm a zugu.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: Ka n daa nya firiladima maa sunsukkuni, ka sɔ n daa zɛya n‑ŋmanni hoo ninsaala, ka yɛ zalabia ka u sɛlooriga nyɛ salima bunni.

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: U zugu ni u zɔbri daa pɛligi la hoo gumni, bee, hoo la'apeeliga, ka u nini nya'asiri ni hoo bugunsaala.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: U nɔba nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa, ka u kukɔri daa ŋmanni la hoo kɔpɔbra pam damni.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: Di saha maa u daa gyi la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi u nudirigu ni, ka takoobi dittigu yiiri u noori ni na. Ka u nindaa mun kur nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn yi bii n‑nya'ari seem la.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: Man daa nya u sahanseaa la, n daa lu la tiŋŋa m‑pɔbri u toonni hoo man kpi ni la; ka u zaŋŋi u nudirigu m‑pa n zugu ka yɛli ma, ni, Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, dama mani n nyɛ ŋɔn nyɛ piiligu ni naari.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Di saha ka u laa n‑yɛli, Mani n nyɛ ŋɔn nɛ maa! N daa kpi ni, ka neaai nti kpalim saha din ka tariga; di zugu kum ni kpiimma tiŋŋa fukumsi zaa bɛ la n saani.

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: Dinzugu sɔbma yɛliseesi i nini ŋɔn nyari wa zaasa, dama a nyɛ la yɛliseesi an niŋni pampaŋŋɔ ni yɛliseesi an nin kyɛ na di nyaaŋŋa.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Nyin ŋɔn nyari saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi ni firila tɔmpu ziisi wa - tɔ, a zaa nyɛ la yɛlisɔ'ara ka laa m‑mari a gbinni, a gbinni: saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi maa nyɛ la malaakaseeba ban guri Naawunni niriba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni u, tinsi ayɔpɔi maa puuni la. Ka firiladima ayɔpɔi la gba nyɛ la lala Naawunni niriba maa.

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Ka n daa gɔsi nti nya bukkubila, ka Sɔ ŋɔn daa zi naam zizii maa ni la daa mari ka u nudirigu ni. Ka sɔppu daa bɛ ka puuni, ni ka nyaaŋŋa m‑paasi; ka ba zaŋŋi ka n‑kpabi, ka tabli buntabinna ayɔpɔi.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Ka n daa nya malaaka kpeeŋŋu daansɔa, ka u tasiri m‑boonni pampam, ni, Ŋɔni n sum ni u ti lakki buntabinna maa n‑yoaai bukku wa.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Amaa sɔsɔ daa ka ni n‑nin nyaŋŋi n‑yoaai bukku maa, halli n‑gɔsi ka gba; saazugu sɔ ka ni, tingbaŋŋu gba sɔ ka ni, halli tingbanlɔŋŋu ni gba sɔ ka ni.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Man mun daa kum pam, dama sɔ daa ka ni n‑sumsi ni u yoaai bukku maa, halli n‑gɔsi ka gba.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Amaa kpambaya pisi ni anaasi sɔ daa yɛli ma, ni, Di kumna! Dama Gbigimni maa nyaŋŋiya, Gyuuda za'anɔri Gbigimni la; NaaDauda dɔ'am tuŋŋu maa Nya'ari gba n nyɛ u. Ŋɔna n nin toaai n‑lakki buntabinna ayɔpɔi n‑yoaai bukkubila maa.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Di saha ka n daa nya pɛbilisɔa. U daa zɛya naam zizii la puuni, ka bunvuya anaasi ni kpambaya maa daa gyiligi u, ka di daa bɛ la hoo ban pun kɔrigi u seem la. U daa mari yiila ayɔpɔi ni nini ayɔpɔi, an wulisi Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi la, ka u mari a n‑tumni ni a kyaŋŋi duniya wa zaa puuni la.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Ka Pɛbila wa daa kyɛ na n‑deaai bukku maa, Ŋɔn daa zi naam zizii maa ni saani.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: Ka ŋɔn daa deaai bukku la sahanseaa maa, bunvuya anaasi la daa gbaani tiŋŋa u tooni; ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi gba daa niŋi lala, ka daa mari sɔkam zaa yinniyinni u goongyi, ni u salima laa - laasi mun paali ni turaali, di nyɛ la Naawunni niriba wunsusigu.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Ka ba daa yuumni n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Nyin n sumsi ni i deaai bukku maa, I mun sumsi ni i lakki ka buntabinna! Dama nyini ka ba daa pun kɔrigi, Ka i lala kum zugu daa yɔ sɔkam samni N‑da ba n‑ti Naawunni na: Zuliya pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, Ni tinsi pam niriba, ni bura pam.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: I daa kyɛ ka ba di naam Naawunni saani, Ka diisi ba Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima n‑ti u - Ban mun nin ti di duniya wa ni naam.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Di saha ka n daa nya, ka daa wum malaakadima pam damni - malaaka tusatusa, bee miiliyɔnmiiliyɔn. Ba daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa, ni bunvuya ni kpambaya maa ziiya.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: M‑boonni pampam n‑yɛtta#:- Pɛbila maa sumni seelikam deaari! Ba daa kɔrigi u, ka u sumni anŋɔ zaa#: U sum ni u deaai kpeeŋŋu ni arizikki, U sum ni u deaai yam ni yaa, U sum ni u deaai pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni yɛlisuma.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Di saha ka n daa wum, ka ban daa naam bunseelli kam zaa gba daa tɔ'asira - an bɛ saazugu, ni an bɛ tingbaŋŋu ni, ni an bɛ tingbanlɔŋŋu ni, halli an bɛ teeku ni zaa - n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Pɛ'abu ka ba zaŋŋi n‑ti u: Ba tisi Ŋɔn zi naam zizii maa ni, Pɛbila maa gba ka ba tisira - Pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni fukumsi Halli ni saha din ka naari.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Ka bunvuya anaasi maa daa sakki, ni, Ammi! ; ka kpambaya maa daa gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeemna.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: N daa nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinna ayɔpɔi la yinni; ka n daa ti wum bunvuya anaasi maa yinni ŋɔn tasiri kaman satasiŋŋa n‑yɛtti, Kyɛm na.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wuripeelli, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa mari taroo, ka ba zaŋŋi naam zupiliga n‑ti u, ka u daa yi na ni u ti tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyi la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi buyi daana la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, Kyɛm na.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Ka wurizeaa daa yi na, ka sɔ ŋɔn daa ba u maa, ka ba zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti u ni u ti fa duniya zaa sugru ni nangbanyinni ba saani, din nin kyɛ ka daadam kuuri taaba. Ka ba zaŋŋi takoobi titaari n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buta la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi buta la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, Kyɛm na! Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wurisabilli, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa daa mari gbarigbinni kan ma'asiri kyi u nuu ni.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Ka n daa wum hoo kukɔri n‑tasiri pampam bunvuya anaasi la sunsukkuni, ni, Kyɛ ka ba tumni dabsimunni n‑kur nyari kasuma gbarigbinni yinni, bee, kabeeri gbarigbina ata - a ligri nla! - amaa di sa'am kpaam maa, bee, daam maa.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri bunaasi la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi bunaasi la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, Kyɛm na.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wuripirisiri, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa yuuri boonni la Kum, ka u taraana Kpiimma Tiŋŋa daa dɔla. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi duniya purigri bunaasi zugu yinni niriba, ni ba zaŋŋi takoobi fukumsi n‑kuuri ba, ni kɔm fukumsi, ni dooru ni mooni bungbaara dinni m‑paasi.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri bunu la; ka n daa gɔsi nti nya ninvugseeba ka ba daa pun ku ba, ban daa mooli Naawunni yɛligu la zugu, ni ban daa wulisi yɛliseesi ban daa nya ka wum Naawunni saani la zugu; banŋɔ zaa siisi daa bɛ la wungyeeŋŋu teebuli maa lɔŋni.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Ka ban zaa n daa tasiri kpeeŋŋakpeeŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni Ti Dugma, nyin kur nyɛ la Yɛlimaŋni Daana kasikasi! - ka bɔ saha ka i nin ti kari ti sariya n‑ti ti, ka zaali duniya dima samni, ti kum zugu.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Di saha ka ba daa zaŋŋi bunyeeripeela n‑ti ba zaa, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, ba vuusima beela, halli ka ba taaba zaa saha gba ti paai, ba mabiseeba ban gba nyɛ Naawunni daaba maa - seeba duniya niriba ya n‑ku m‑paasi ba zugu la.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyoobu la; ka n daa gɔsi nti nya ka tingbaŋŋu daa dam pampam. Ka wuntaŋŋa maa daa sɔbgi libilibi, ka namboaa daa moaai zimizimi zaa.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: halli saŋmariwuusi gba daa luri tingbaŋŋu ni na, hoo sahanseaa sunzɔpiliŋŋa ŋɔn zeeri n‑zɛ'asiri kinkaŋŋa ka ka wala luri tiŋŋa ni seem maa.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Ka sangbaŋŋu maa daa kpabi hoo sɔŋŋu m‑bakki, ka tingbaŋŋu ni zɔya la daa yigsi a beesigu ziiya, ni tingbanseesi an bɛ teeku ni la gba.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Di saha ka n daa nya duniya nadima ni ninvugkara ni kpambaya, m‑paasi bundaandima ni ban mari kpeeŋŋu la, ni sɔkam zaa, daaba ni sumaŋdima zaa: ka ba zaa daa zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑sɔ'ari la zɔya ni feeri ni tampiisi lɔŋni.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Ka ba daa boonni n‑yɛtti zɔya ni tampiisi maa, ni, Ya luma ti zugri na n‑sɔ'ai ti, ka Sɔ ŋɔn zi naam zizii la di nya ti, halli Pɛbila wa suuri di paai ti na!.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Dama ba suuri yisigri dabsiri maa paai ti na, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑kpalim n‑zɛ ba tooni.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Ka sahanseaa ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyɔpɔi la, arizana puuni zaa ka di daa fɔ surimm n‑yuui beela.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Di nyaaŋŋa ka n daa nya malaakadima ayɔpɔi seeba ban zɛ Naawunni tooni maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi kikaasi ayɔpɔi n‑ti ba.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Ka malaakasɔ daa yi n‑kyɛ na nti zaani gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa tooni, m‑mari salima turaalinyooriga. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi turaali zum pam n‑ti u ni u ti nyɔa, ni, lala ka u nin ti maali Naawunni niriba wunsusigu zaa n‑tisi, salima gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa zugu - kan zɛ naam zizii maa tooni la.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Ka turaali la nyoobu nyoosi daa yi malaaka maa nuu ni n‑du Naawunni tooni, ni Naawunni niriba maa wunsusigu zaa m‑paasi.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Ka di nyaaŋŋa ka malaaka maa daa gbeaai bugum teebuli maa zugu m‑paali u bunni, ka zaŋŋi n‑lɔbi tingbaŋŋu ni na; ka saa daa nya'asi ka tasi m‑mari vuuri pam, ka tingbaŋŋu mun daa dam.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Di saha ka malaakadima ayɔpɔi, kikaasi daandima maa, daa niŋi siri ni ba peebi#:.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Malaaka tooni daana maa ŋɔn daa peebi, ka sakuga ni bugum daa gyɛri ni ziim n‑kpaari n‑luri tingbaŋŋu ni na. Ka duniya wa daa puri buta, ka purigri yinni daa di bugum, ni ka tiisi, ka duniya mɔmaasa zaa gba daa di bugum.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyi zugu daa peebi, ka bunseelli hoo zɔtitaari kan diri bugum daa lɔbi n‑lu teeku koom ni na. Ka teeku maa yi puri buta, ka ka purigri yinni daa leebi ziim;.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: ka teeku koom ni bunvuya purigri buta gba daa kpi, ka nyarima purigri buta daa sa'am.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buta daa peebi, ka saŋmariwutitaari daa yi sangbana ni n‑lu na, ka ka yuuri boonni Toom. Ka daa diri bugum hoo mɔkpalli ŋɔn di bugum seem. Ka kulisi ni kɔbulisi daa puri buta ka a purigri yinni ka Toom daa lu a koom ni, ka bu ti leebi la bun tɔ pam, halli ka niriba pam daa nyu lala koom ka daa kpi di zugu.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa bunaasi daa peebi, ka wuntaŋŋa purigri buta daa ŋmɛa, ni namboaa gba dinni, ka saŋmariwuusi gba daa puri buta ka a purigri yimmu daa ŋmɛa. Ka wuntaŋŋa saha yi puri buta, ni yuŋŋu gba, ka a purigri buta yinni yinni daa kɔŋŋi neesim di zugu.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Tɔ: ka di saha maa ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka wubga daa yigri saazugu agbaa ziiya; ka n daa wum ka u tasiri pampam n‑yɛtti, Yɛla! Yɛla! Yɛla! Dabeem yɛla nin paai ban bɛ duniya puuni, sahanseaa malaakadima ata ban kpalim la yi ti peebi ba kikaasi.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa bunu zugu la daa peebi. Ka n daa nya saŋmariwuseaa: ka ka daa lu n‑yi saazugu m‑paai tingbaŋŋu ni na, ka daa deaai saafibii kan kparigri bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa noori la.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Ka u daa yoaai bɔ'azuliŋŋu noori maa, ka nyoosi pam daa yi na, halli n‑ligi wuntaŋŋa ni saazugu zaa neesim, hoo bugum ŋɔn diri moo puuni n‑yiisiri nyoosi seem.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Ka bunyigra hoo toori daa yi nyoosi maa ni n‑sigi na, ka ba daa kyɛ ka toori maa mari yiiko hoo naŋŋa dumpu taabu.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Ba daa yɛli ba, ni, ba di niŋi moori, bee bumbula, bee tiisi seelli, amaa ninsaaliseeba ban ka Naawunni daalimni ba gbeeri ni la ka ba daamna.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Toori maa daa ka soori ni ba ku lala niriba maa, amaa ni ba namsi ba gɔya anu. Ba namsigu maa beerim ŋmanni la naŋŋa dumpu beerim.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Lala gɔya anu maa puuni ka ba nin bɔ kum ziiya n‑kɔŋŋi: ba nin boori ni ba kpi, amaa kum nin zɔ ka kyɛ ba.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Toori wa muna - ba daa ŋmanni la wuriseeba ban maanni siri ni ba kyaŋŋi tɔppu ni; ba zugri pili la bunseesi hoo salima naam zupilisi, ka ba nindaasi ŋmanni ninsaala nindaa.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Ba zɔbri daa ŋmanni la pɔ'a zɔbwɔ'ala la, ka ba nyina ŋmanni gbigma nyina.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Ba nyaari daa ligi la bunseesi an ŋmanni kutti dawɔ'ari, ka ba mari n‑ta'ari piima ni kuga toobu ni; ka ba kpunkpama vuuri daa ŋmanni la wuri pam ŋɔn datti tarɔkudima n‑zɔri n‑wa tɔppu ni ka a mari vuuri seem la.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Ba zuya daa ŋmanni la nansi zuya, ka ba dumpu daa ŋmanni la nansi dumpu, halli ba zuya puuni ka ba yiiko daa bɛ ni, ni ba ti daamni niriba maa gɔya anu maa.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Ka ba daa mari la naaba ŋɔn gɔsiri ba: ŋɔna n nyɛ malaakasɔ ŋɔn su bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa fukumsi la. U yuuri Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a ni boonni, ni, Abaddɔn , ka Griiktiri yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni, Apɔliyɔn .

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Tɔ: ka daŋŋni dabeem yɛlli maa gaariya; amaa ka nyaaŋŋa ka dabeem yɛla ayi n yɛ n‑dɔla.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyoobu daa peebi, ka n daa wum kukɔri ka ka tɔ'asiri n‑yi gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa luga anaasi maa zaa ni na - salima bunseaa kan zɛ Naawunni tooni maa nla.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Ka kukɔri maa daa booli malaaka maa ŋɔn pa buyoobu zugu ka mari u kikaa maa; n‑yɛli u, ni, Basim malaakadima anaasi ban daa ba seeba n‑dooli Yufreetisi mɔ'atitaari la ni la.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Ka ba siri daa lɔrigi malaaka anaasi maa m‑basi: dama saha kanŋɔa - zuna dabsiri wa, gɔri kanŋɔa, yuumni wa puuni - saha wa zugu ka ba daa zaali ba ni ba gura. Ba daa yiisi ba na ni ba ti ku duniya wa niriba - ba yi ti puri niriba zaa zii buta, ka malaakadima wa nin ku ba purigri yimmu.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Ba daa mari ba tɔbbiisi, ban daa ba wuri n‑tuusira: ba daa zoaai pampam, halli ka ba paai la miiliyɔna ayi - n daa wum ba kaalli, ni, di nyɛ la lala.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Ka n zii nyaabu maa ni ka n daa nya wuri ni ban baari ba ŋɔn bɛ seem maa. Niriba maa daa mari kutti buna n‑yɛ n‑ta'ari ba nyaari, ka a seesi daa moaai la hoo bugum la, ka seesi sɔbgi hoo bulu din nya'ara, seesi mun ŋmanni la farinwuta. Wuri maa mun zugri daa ŋmanni la gbigma zugri, ka bugum nyoosi daa yiiri ba nɔya ni na, ni farinwuta m‑paasi.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Bugum, ni nyoosi, ni farinwuta an daa yiiri wuri maa nɔya ni na maa - anŋɔ abata zaa n daa nyɛ an daa ku niriba purigri buta la yimmu.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Dama wuri maa yiiko daa bɛ la ba nɔya ni ba zuya ni. Ba zuya maa ŋmanni la wugi, m‑mari zugri: anŋɔ ka ba mari n‑daamni niriba maa.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Di saha maa ka ninsaaliseeba daa kpalim, baseeba yɛlibeeri wa daa bu ku ba. Di zaa yɔri ba daa bu takki ba teesibeeri ni ba tumbeeri sɔya beebu, amaa ba daa kpalim n‑gyeemni wunseesi ba mammansi ŋɔn maali la. Ba daa bu kyɛ arizimbeeri gyeeŋŋu, bee bugseesi ban zaŋŋi salima m‑maali la, bee anzulifa, daanya, kuga, bee daari m‑maali, halli ni anŋɔ zaa ku toaai n‑nyara, bee n‑wumna, bee n‑kyɛnna.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Ba mun daa bu takki n‑zɔ n‑yi ba ninvugkuubu la ni, bee ba tiima ni, bee ba pɔ'agoorilim bee dɔgoorilim ni, bee ba na'ayiga tuma zaa ni.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Tɔ: ka di saha maa ka n daa nya malaaka kpeeŋŋu daansɔa, ka u yi arizana ni n‑sigri na. Ka u gɔbi la sangbaŋŋu hoo kyinkyingɔbrigu, ka sasuu daa pili u zugu; u nindaa daa ŋmanni la wuntaŋŋa ka u gbaya ŋmanni langbindaari bugum.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: U daa zali litaafibila fiiŋ, ka ka yoaai ni m‑pa'ai u nuu zugu. Ka u zaŋŋi u nɔba maa n‑tɔm duniya wa ni: u nudirigu dinni mun noori teeku zugu, ka seaa la noori tingbankuumni zugu.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Tɔ: dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa maali n‑nya, ka malaakadima anaasi daa zɛya: duniya luga anaasi ka ba daa bɛ ni nti gyi pɛsigu zii bunaasi zaa n‑ta'ari ni, ni pɛsigu zaa di zeeri tingbaŋŋu maa ni, bee teeku ni; halli ni a di zeeri n‑niŋni tiisi gba.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Ka u tasi m‑booli kukɔzuliŋŋu, kan zulisim ŋmanni gbigimni nyuriŋŋu. Ka ŋɔn daa booli lala maa, satasinsi buyɔpɔi daa zaŋŋi ba gba kunkuma n‑tasi n‑sakki.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Di saha ka n daa nya, ka malaakasɔ daa doaai wuntampusiri zii ni na, ka u daa mari bunseelli kan nyɛ Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana dinni, ka ka daalimni ŋɔn Naawunni maŋtoori buna. Malaaka wa daa tasi pampam n‑tiri u taaba babanaasi seeba ban daa mari yiiko ni ba sa'amni tingbaŋŋu ni teeku maa.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Ban daa tasi n‑naai, n daa ya sɔbi la ban yɛli seem maa, amaa n daa wum kukɔri ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑yiiri arizana ni na n‑yɛtti, ni, Satasinsi buyɔpɔi maa ŋɔn yɛli seesi maa - a nyɛ la yɛlisɔ'ara. Di sɔbi a n‑dooli.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: n‑yɛtti ba, ni, Ya di sa'am tingbaŋŋu maa, bee teeku, bee tiisi la, see ka man daalim Naawunni niriba n‑niŋi ba gbeeri maa n‑naai, ni, Naawunni n su ba.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Ka malaaka sɔ man daa pun nya, sɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ n‑tɔm teeku ni tingbaŋŋu maa zugu maa - ŋɔna daa zaŋŋi u nudirigu n‑wukki saazugu.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Di saha ka n daa wum niriba maa kaaligu ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem: ni, ban daa daalim ninvugseeba zaa maa daa nyɛ niriba tusa kɔbga ni tusa pisinaasi ni tusa anaasi. Ba daa yi Izraɛli za'anɔya zaa ni na#:.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: m‑pɔ Naawunni, Naawunni ŋɔn nɛ m‑bɛ ni saha din ka tariga la, ka daa naam saazugu, ni tingbaŋŋu, ni teeku, ni bunseelli kam din bɛ a ni zaa. U daa pɔ lala n‑yɛli, ni, Guusigu laa n‑ka ni!.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: za'anɔri kam zaa niriba tusa pii-ni-ayiyi. Di nyɛ la#:- Gyuuda za'anɔri niriba tusa pii-ni-ayi, ni Ruubɛn za'anɔri lala, ni Gaddi za'anɔri;.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: See ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyɔpɔi la yi peebi u kikaa maa: lala saha ka Naawunni nin tum u tunsɔ'arigu la n‑naai hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli n‑ti anabidima maa seem, ban daa nyɛ u tuntumniba maa.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Aseeri za'anɔri tusa pii-ni-ayi, ni Naftaali za'anɔri lala, ni Manaasi gba;.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Ka n daa laa n‑wum kukɔseaa kan daa tɔ'asi n‑yi arizana ni na la, ka ka yɛtti n‑tiri ma yaasa, ni, Kyɛma n‑zaŋŋi litaafiseaa kan yoaai m‑bɛ malaaka maa nuu ni - malaakasɔ ŋɔn tɔm teeku ni tingbaŋŋu zugu la.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: Simiyɔn za'anɔri tusa pii-ni-ayi, ni Liivai za'anɔri lala, ni Isakaari gba;.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Tɔ, ka n daa kyaŋŋi nti yɛli malaaka maa, ni, u tim ma litaafibila maa. Ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, Deaam n‑ŋɔbi! Ka nin leebi bunseaa kan tɔ i puuri ni, halli ni i noori ni ka ka ya m‑malisa hoo siiri.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Zɛbulɔn za'anɔri tusa pii-ni-ayi, ni Yisifu za'anɔri lala, ni Bɛngyamin gba. Banŋɔ zaa ka ba daa daalim.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Dinŋɔna n daa deaai litaafibila maa u nuu ni n‑ŋɔbi, ka ka siri daa malisa n noori ni hoo siiri, amaa man daa vɔli n‑naai maa, ka daa leebi buntoori n puuri ni.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Anŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka ninvugbɔbgu daa bɛ naam zizii maa tooni n‑zɛya, ni Pɛbila maa tooni. Niriba wa daa nyɛ la bura pam niriba, ni zuliya pam, ni tinsi pam niriba, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ka ba daa yɛ situripeela n‑zɛya, ka daa mari abe tiisi wula ba nuusi ni.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Ka ba daa yɛli ma, ni, See ka i laa n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛla zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tinsi pam niriba, ni bura pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni nadima pam.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Ka ba daa kaasiri pampam n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Naawunni ti Dugma kpalim n‑ziya, ka ŋɔn ni Pɛbila maa n su tiliginsim yɛla zaa.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Di saha ka malaakadima zaa ban daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa daa gbaani tiŋŋa, ban ni kpambaya maa ni bunvuya anaasi la m‑paasi, ka ba zaa daa gyeemni Naawunni.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: n‑yɛtta#:- Naawunni ti Dugma su la yɛlisuma, ni gyirima, ni yam, ni puusigu, ni gyeeŋŋu, ni yiiko, ni yaa; Ka di bɛ la lala halli ni saha din ka tariga. - Ammi, ammi .

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Ka kpamma maa ninvugsɔ daa boosi ma n‑yɛli, ni, Ninvugseeba ban yeeri situripeela wa - bandima n nyɛ ba? Ni, yani ka ba yi na.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, M Ba, nyini m mi di. Ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, Tɔ; ba nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban yi wahala diibu toom ni na; Pɛbila maa ziim ka ba daa zaŋŋi m‑peaai ba situra, ni a ti pɛligi nyain lala.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Lala zugu kyɛ ka ba bɛ la Naawunni naam zizii maa tooni, ka ba gyeemni u wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa, Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni: Ka Naawunni kpalim m‑bɛ ba saani n‑ziya; U zi la naam zizii maa ni saha kam.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Kɔm ku laa m‑mari ba, kɔnyuuri mun ku laa m‑mari ba gba: Halli wuntaŋŋa ku laa m‑bii n‑niŋi ba, bee, n‑kyɛ ka wɔligu wumni ba.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Dama Pɛbila maa bɛ la naam zizii puuni, ka u nin guri ba suŋŋa hoo pɛgura ŋɔn guri u peesi seem; U nin kyɛ ka ba dɔli kɔsuŋŋu bulisi n‑nya nyɔvuri, ka u laa n‑nyeesiri ba nini nintam n‑tiri ba sahakam.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi kika'ari n‑ti ma, ni n ti mari ka m‑ma'asira; ni, Doaama m‑ma'asi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu wa, ni ka teebuli maa, ni baseeba ban puusiri bɛni la!.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Amaa di ma'asi dundɔŋŋu kan bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa nyaaŋŋa la; kyɛli ka kpaŋŋa sa! Dama ba zaŋŋi ka n‑ti tinzuna dima, ka ba nin kpɛ tinkasi maa puuni na n‑kyɛnni n‑noori ka n‑niŋi gɔya pisinaasi ni ayi.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: N sɛhara dima ayi la muna - n nin tum ba na n‑ti ba soori ni ba ti tɔ'asi m‑moonni n anabitiri yɛla maa; ba nin yɛ la bootu kpari n‑wulisi ba sufusa'aŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka nin tɔ'asiri lala n‑niŋi dabsa tusiri ni kɔbsiyi ni pisiyoobu.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Ban n nyɛ ɔlivii kpaam tiisi ayi maa, ni firilatɔmna ayi an zaantiri ti Dugma Ŋɔn Su Duniya Wa Zaa tooni la.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Ka sɔ yi ti boori u namsi ba, bugum nin yi ba noori ni na n‑di ba dindaandima wa; sɔ yi boori ni u namsi ba, di sumni ni di daama kpi lala.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Niriba ayi wa mari yiiko ni ba kpatti sangbana, ka saa ku mi dabsiseesi ban ya tɔ'asiri ti Dugma yɛla maa. Ba laa m‑mari yiiko kulisi zugu, n‑nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka a koom leebi ziim. Ka ba laa n‑toaai n‑kyɛ ka dabeem yɛla balibu kam ti niŋi duniya maa, ka a maali n‑niŋi sahaseaa sɛharadima wa sufu yi boora.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Ka ba yi ti mooli ba anabitiri yɛla n‑naai, bunkɔbsɔ nin yi bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa ni n‑du na nti tuusi ba tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi ba n‑ku ba.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Ba nin kpi n‑kpalim n‑dɔ tintitaari maa sɔroo la puuni, tinseaa ban daa zaŋŋi sɛharadima wa dugma maa n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu ka puuni maa. Tiŋŋa wa niriba tumbeeri zugu ka ba pa ka yupaasi, ni, Sɔdoom, bee, Igyipti.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Ka tinsi pam niriba ti gɔsi, ni zuliya pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni bura pam. Ba gɔsiri ban kpalim n‑dɔya seem n‑niŋi dabsa ata ni purigri, ka lala saha maa ba ku sakki ni ba sɔ'ai ba.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Ka duniya wa niriba sufu yi maai pam ba kum maa zugu. Ba diri la kyuu ka tiri taaba piini, dama anabidima ayi wa daa nyɛ seeba ban daa diisi duniya dima wahala pam.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Amaa daba ata ni purigri nyaaŋŋa, Naawunni nyɔvuri vuusim daa kpɛ ba na, ka ba daa ti doaai n‑zaani ba gbaya zugu ka dabeem pam gbaai seeba ban nya ba maa.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Di saha ka anabidima ayi maa daa wum kukɔri ka ka yi arizana ni na n‑yɛli ba, Ya duuma kpa na! ka ba siri daa kpɛ sangbaŋŋu ni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi arizana ni; ka ba dindaandima nini daa nya.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Ka di nyaaŋŋa ka tingbaŋŋu daa dam, ka tintitaari maa purigri yinni piiya zugu daa lu. Niriba tusa ayɔpɔi daa kpi tingbaŋŋu dampu maa ni, ka dabeem gbaasi ban kpalim la, ka ba pɛ'ai Naawunni Arizana Daana maa.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Dabeem yɛlli buyi zugu gaariya, ka din pa buta zugu kyɛnni na ka bu ya n‑yuui.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Ka malaaka ŋɔn paasiri buyɔpɔi daa peebi u kikaa, ka kukɔya daa tasiri n‑yiiri arizana ni na n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Duniya zaa naam leebi ti Dugma naam, Ni ŋɔn daa diisi sɔ Masiiya, Yisa, dinni. U naam ku ti mari baasigu Halli saha din ka tariga.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi ban bɛ Naawunni tooni n‑zi ba gyirima ziziisi ni la - ba zaa daa dooni m‑pibli tiŋŋa n‑gyeemni Naawunni.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Ti puusiri i ni, ti Dugma Naawunni, Nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa - Nyin ŋɔn bɛ ni, ka daa pun bɛ ni. I deaari la i yiiko din galisi; Pampaŋŋɔ ka i di naam.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Tinsi niriba suuri yisigiya Amaa nyin gba suuri saha paaya - Sahaseaa nyin zaŋŋi ban kpi la n‑kari ba zaa sariya: Sahaseaa nyin zaŋŋi laara ni piinisuma n‑tiri anabidima ban daa tum n‑ti i la, n‑tiri i niriba, ni ban zɔri i yuuri la, n‑tiri ba zaa, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa. Saha paaya ni i ti zaŋŋi ban daa sa'am duniya maa n‑sa'am ba gba yɛla zaa.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Di saha ka Naawunni arizana puuni Gyeeŋŋu Duu daa yoaai, ka adakka maa bɛ bɛni, Naawunni nangbanyinni yɛla bunni la. Ka saa daa nya'asi ka tasi m‑mari vuuri pam, ka tingbaŋŋu mun daa dam, ka sakuga daa lu na, bunkara ni buntibsa pampam.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Ka n daa nya bunkɔbsɔ yaasa, ka u daa yiiri teeku koom maa ni na. U daa mari yiila piiya ni zugri ayɔpɔi, ka bunveela daa vili u yiila maa yinni yinni zaa. Ka yubeeri m bɛ u zugri ni n‑tuuri Naawunni.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Man daa nya bunkɔbsɔ wa daa ŋmanni la kaliiya, amaa u nɔba ŋmanni la bunkɔbsɔ nɔbkara an nunyeesi wa'a pam; u noori mun ŋmanni la gbigimni dinni. Nyɛbkarigu maa daa zaŋŋi u maŋtoori yiiko ni u naam zizii maa n‑ti bunkɔbbeoo wa, ka zaŋŋi fukumsi pam n‑ti u.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Tɔ, ka bunkɔbgu wa zugri maa yinni daa bɛ la hoo ban daa pun kɔrigi ka; amaa u kum daŋŋa maa daa gaari ni, ka lala daa gyɛri duniya niriba zaa pam, ka ba daa dɔli bunkɔbbeoo maa nyaaŋŋa.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Saalidima daa gyeemni Nyɛbga maa, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi lala yiiko n‑ti bunkɔbbeoo maa zugu, ka ba daa laa n‑gyeem bunkɔbgu maa, ni, Ŋɔni n bɛ ni n‑ŋmani hoo bunkɔbgu wa? Sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑zabi n‑nyaŋŋi u.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Ka ba daa kyɛ ka bunkɔbgu wa mari la noori n‑tɔ'asiri u sufu yuubu yɛlibeeri n‑tuuri Naawunni. Ba daa ti u soori ni u ti su fukumsi n‑niŋi gɔya pisinaasi ni ayi.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Ka u daa yaai u noori n‑tɔ'asiri yɛlibeeri n‑tuuri Naawunni, ni u ti sa'am Naawunni yuuri, ka tuuri u beebu ziiya ni seeba ban bɛ arizana ni maa.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Ba daa laa n‑ti u soori ni u ti tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi Naawunni niriba, ka u su fukumsi m‑mari zuliya pam, ni tinsi pam niriba, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni bura pam.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Ka sɔkam ŋɔn bɛ duniya tingbaŋŋu zugu zaa nin gyeemni u, ŋɔnkam yuuri bu sɔbi n‑dooli nyɔvuri bukku maa ni; lala bukku ka Pɛbila maa mara, Pɛbilisɔ ban daa kɔrigi duniya piiligu ni halli ni zuna.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, ŋɔn wumma!.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Ba yi ya n‑gbaai sɔ daabri, Ban gbaam di daama! Ba yi ya n‑zaŋŋi takoobi n‑ku sɔ, Ban kuum di daama! Dizugu see ka Naawunni niriba mari sufukpeemni, See ka ba zɛ yummu n‑niŋi yɛdda.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Tɔ; ka n daa laa n‑nya bunkɔbsɔ yaasa, ka u yi tingbantiŋni n‑du na. U daa mari la yiila ayi, kaman pɛbila, amaa u daa tɔ'asiri kaman Nyɛbkarigu la.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Ka u daa deaai daŋŋni bunkɔbbeoo maa yiiko, halli nti su u fukumsi tuma zaa n‑tumni n‑tiri u. Ka ŋɔn daa deaai u yiiko maa, u daa kyɛ ka duniya ni niriba zaa gyeemni daŋŋni bunkɔbgu maa, sɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yi na ka u kum daŋŋa nya tippu maa.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: U daa tum makkri tunnyalima pam, halli nti kyɛ ka bugum yi saazugu n‑sigi tingbaŋŋu ni na, ka daadam nya.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Ka makkri seesi ŋɔn nya yiiko n‑tumni n‑tiri daŋŋni bunkɔbgu la, ana n daa yɔmsiri duniya dima. Ka u yɛli ba, ni, ba maalim bunni ka ka ŋmanni bunkɔbgu la, bunkɔbsɔ ŋɔn daa nya takoobi daŋŋa ka laa n‑neaai maa.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Ka ba daa ti u yiiko ni u kyɛ ka ban daa maali bunseaa la ti nɛ ka vuusira, ka laa n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asira. Ka kyɛ ka ba ku ninvugseeba ban ku sakki n‑gyeem bummaannigu kan ŋmanni bunkɔbgu la zaa.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Ka laa n‑kyɛ ka sɔkam deaai la daalimni u nudirigu ni, bee, u gbeoo ni; ni, ba zaa deaama - tarimma ni birikyindima, bundaandima ni faradima, ban su ba mansi ni daaba zaa.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑daari neema, bee, n‑koosira, na'ala u mari la daalimni maa, di nyɛ la bunkɔbbeoo yuuri la, bee, u yuuri maa kaaligu namba la.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Kpa wa n nyɛ yam yɛlli; kyɛ ka ŋɔn mari hankali baŋŋi bunkɔbgu kaaligu namba gbinni: u kaaligu kɔbsiyoobu ni pisiyoobu ni ayoobu - din wulisiri la ninsaalisɔa yɛla.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Tɔ: ka bunni kan mari alahazibu pam daa bɛ arizana puuni n‑yi na; ka di mari di gbinni, bunsɔ'arigu#:- pɔ'a n daa bɛ ni, ka u sɔ la wuntaŋŋa neesim, ka u gbaya tɔmni namboaa zugu, ka saŋmariwuusi pii ni ayi n daa gyiligi u zugu n‑ŋmanni hoo kubibɔbga.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Pɔ'a maa daa mari la puuwa, ka u bɛ la dɔ'am wahala ni - dɔ'am beerim n daa mari u, ka u kaasiri di zugu.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Ka alahazibu yɛliseaa yaasa daa bɛ arizana puuni n‑yi na, ka bunseelli kan ŋmanni Nyɛbga m bɛ ni, ka u daa galisi pam; bunzeoo n daa nyɛ u, ka u mari zugri ayɔpɔi ni yiila piiya; ka ba daa zaŋŋi bunveela ayɔpɔi n‑vili u zugu hoo kubibɔbsi la.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Ka u zuuri daa daari saŋmariwuusi purigri yimmu buta zugu m‑basiri tingbaŋŋu maa na. Ka Nyɛbga wa zɛ pɔ'a maa tooni u dɔ'am maa saha, ni, u yi ti dɔ'ai bii maa sahanseaa, ŋɔn nin ŋɔbi u.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Pɔ'a maa daa dɔ'aya: u dɔ'ai la doowa, ka u bii maa n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn ti ya n‑su duniya tinsi zaa naam, ka zaŋŋi u kutti damni n‑kari ba sariya. Ka ba daa fa u ya'abi n‑taasi Naawunni saani, ŋɔn Naawunni naam zizii maa ya'ari sa.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Ka pɔ'a maa mun daa zɔ n‑kpɛ yoo ni, seela Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun maali u beebu ziiya siri n‑ti u. Bɛni ka ba nin guri u ka diri dabsa tusiri ni kɔbsiyi ni pisiyoobu la.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Di saha ka tɔppu daa doaai arizana ni, ka Maikyɛli ni u malaakadima tuusiri Nyɛbkarigu wa, ka ŋɔna ni u malaakadima gba daa tuusi ba.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Amaa Nyɛbga dima maa daa bu nyaŋŋi, halli ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya beebu ziiya arizana ni.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Ba daa tɔ Nyɛbkarigu wa m‑basi tiŋŋa; ŋɔna n nyɛ kurilim ni sa Waafu la, ban boonni sɔ Yibrisi, bee Sintaani la, ka u yɔmsiri la duniya wa zaa. Ba daa tɔ u m‑basi na, ŋɔna ni u malaakadima zaa.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Ka n daa wum, ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri arizana puuni, n‑yɛtti, ni#:- Pampaŋŋɔ tiliginsim nyɛ la Naawunni dinni - Yiiko ni naam nyɛ la u dinni, Fukumsi nyɛ la u Masiiya gba dinni! Dama sɔ ŋɔn daa galim ti mabiisi la luya, U daa galimni ba Naawunni saani, Wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa ka u daa galimni ba!.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Pɛbila ziim la zugu ka ba nyaŋŋi u - Ba nyaŋŋi u Naawunni yɛla la zugu; Dama ba daa moonni Naawunni yɛla - ban daa nya ka wum u saani yɛttɔ'aseesi la. Ba pa la seeba ban boori ba nyɔvuya Halli ni ba kum ni.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Yan Arizana maa, ya mari sufupeelli, Ya mari sufupeelli, yaseeba ban bɛ bɛni! Ya mun tingbaŋŋu, ni teeku, ya nya zugbeoo, Dama Sintaani sigi ya saani na; U suuri yi yisigi pampam; Dama u mi, ni, u saha ku laa n‑yuui.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Tɔ; ka Nyɛbga wa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, ba zaŋŋi u m‑basi la duniya ni na, u daa kari pɔ'a maa nɔba, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn dɔ'ai bidibga maa.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Amaa ba daa zaŋŋi wubga kpunkpanyigra ayi n‑ti pɔ'a maa, ni u ti yikki saazugu n‑kpɛ yoo ni m‑paai u maŋtoori ziiya; ka ba ti guri u bɛni n‑niŋi saha yinni, saha buyi ni purigri, ka Wa'abeoo maa ku ti paai u na.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Ka Nyɛbga wa daa tusiri koom pam n‑yiiri u noori ni na hoo mɔ'ari, ka koom maa daa pɔbri n‑dɔli pɔ'a maa nyaaŋŋa ni bu ti di u.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Di saha ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa bɛ ni, n‑zɛya Zayɔn zoori zugu. Niriba tusa kɔbga ni tusa pisinaasi ni anaasi daa dɔli u, ka u yuuri daa sɔbi ba gbeeri ni, ni u Ba yuuri gba.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Amaa tingbaŋŋu daa suŋŋi pɔ'a maa, ka daa yaai ka noori n‑nyu koom maa, koom bun pɔbri n‑yiiri Nyɛbga la noori ni na maa.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Ka n daa wum kukɔri ŋɔn yi arizana ni na, din ŋmanni kɔpɔbrim pam damni, bee saa ŋɔn tasi pampam; halli man daa wum kukɔseaa la daa ŋmanni la hoo goongyidima ban ŋmeeri ba bunŋmeera.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Di saha ka Nyɛbga maa daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi pɔ'a maa ka daa kyaŋŋi ni u ti lɔ tɔppu n‑tuusi u biseeba ban kpalim la zaa, ban dɔli Naawunni zaaligu zaa deede, ka zɛ yummu m‑moonni Yisa yɛla maa.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Ka ba daa yuumni yuumpaalli naam zizii maa tooni, ni bunvuya anaasi ni kpambaya maa tooni. Sɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑bamsi lala yuumni maa, na'ala niriba tusa kɔbga ni pisinaasi ni anaasi la, ban daa pun yɔ seeba samni n‑tiligi ba duniya wa ni la.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Ka u daa zɛ teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu zugu.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
